   16|       |/// ACP session mode controlling agent capabilities.
   17|       |///
   18|       |/// These modes map to Cursor ACP session modes and control what tools
   19|       |/// the agent can use during the session.
   20|       |///
   21|       |/// **Important:** In Cursor ACP, `Plan` and `Ask` modes are **read-only** and
   22|       |/// cannot call any tools (including MCP tools). Use `Agent` mode for any
   23|       |/// agent that needs to call tools.
   24|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   25|       |pub enum SessionMode {
   26|       |    /// Full tool access - can read/write files, run commands, call MCP tools.
   27|       |    /// Use for all agents that need to call tools (implementers, planners, orchestrators).
   28|       |    #[default]
   29|       |    Agent,
   30|       |    /// Planning mode - read-only behavior, **cannot call tools**.
   31|       |    /// Only use for pure analysis/planning without tool calls.
   32|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   33|       |    Plan,
   34|       |    /// Q&A mode - read-only, for explanations and questions.
   35|       |    /// **Cannot call tools.** Only use for pure Q&A without tool calls.
   36|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   37|       |    Ask,
   38|       |}
   39|       |
   40|       |impl SessionMode {
   41|       |    /// Convert to the string value expected by ACP.
   42|      3|    pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   43|      3|        match self {
   44|      3|            Self::Agent => "agent",
   45|      0|            Self::Plan => "plan",
   46|      0|            Self::Ask => "ask",
   47|       |        }
   48|      3|    }

   51|       |/// Trait defining the interface for an ACP client.
   52|       |///
   53|       |/// This trait abstracts the ACP client operations to enable testing with mock implementations.
   54|       |/// All methods are async and the trait is Send + Sync for use across async boundaries.
   55|       |#[async_trait]
   56|       |pub trait AcpClientTrait: Send + Sync {
   57|       |    /// Initialize the ACP connection
   58|       |    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
   59|       |
   60|       |    /// Create a new session with the specified mode.
   61|       |    ///
   62|       |    /// # Arguments
   63|       |    ///
   64|       |    /// * `model` - The model to use for this session
   65|       |    /// * `mcp_servers` - MCP server configurations
   66|       |    /// * `cwd` - Working directory for the session
   67|       |    /// * `mode` - Session mode controlling agent capabilities:
   68|       |    ///   - `Agent`: Full tool access (for implementers)
   69|       |    ///   - `Plan`: Read-only planning mode (for planners)
   70|       |    ///   - `Ask`: Q&A read-only mode (for explorers)
   71|       |    async fn session_new(
   72|       |        &mut self,
   73|       |        model: &str,
   74|       |        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
   75|       |        cwd: &str,
   76|       |        mode: SessionMode,
   77|       |    ) -> Result<SessionNewResponse>;
   78|       |
   79|       |    /// Send a prompt to a session
   80|       |    async fn session_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()>;
   81|       |
   82|       |    /// Receive next notification from the server
   83|       |    ///
   84|       |    /// Notifications are server-initiated messages (no "id" field) like session/update.
   85|       |    /// Use this to monitor agent progress, receive plans, tool calls, etc.
   86|       |    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value>;
   87|       |
   88|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for external routing.
   89|       |    ///
   90|       |    /// This extracts the internal notification receiver so it can be used by
   91|       |    /// a background routing task. After calling this, `recv()` will return
   92|       |    /// an error immediately since there's no receiver.
   93|       |    ///
   94|       |    /// Returns `None` if the receiver has already been taken.
   95|       |    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>>;
   96|       |
   97|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the ACP client.
   98|       |    ///
   99|       |    /// This closes stdin (signaling EOF to the child), waits for the child process
  100|       |    /// to exit (with a timeout), and cleans up background tasks.
  101|       |    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
  102|       |}

  105|       |type PendingRequests = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, oneshot::Sender<Value>>>>;

  107|       |/// ACP client for managing agent sessions
  108|       |pub struct AcpClient {

  110|       |    tx: mpsc::Sender<String>,

  112|       |    pending_requests: PendingRequests,

  149|      0|            tracing::info!("🔧 Spawning auggie with custom cache dir: {}", dir);
  150|      0|        }

  154|      0|            .stdout(Stdio::piped())

  156|      0|            .kill_on_drop(true) // Kill child when parent process dies
  157|      0|            .spawn()

  159|       |

  161|      0|        let stdout = child.stdout.take().context("Failed to get stdout")?;

  164|      0|        let (tx_notifications, notification_rx) = mpsc::channel::<Value>(100);
  165|       |
  166|       |        // Create shared pending requests map

  168|      0|        let pending_requests_clone = Arc::clone(&pending_requests);

  175|      0|                    tracing::error!("Failed to write to stdin: {}", e);
  176|      0|                    break;
  177|      0|                }

  179|      0|                    tracing::error!("Failed to write newline: {}", e);

  181|      0|                }
  182|       |            }
  183|       |            // Explicitly drop stdin to close it, signaling EOF to the child process
  184|      0|            drop(stdin);
  185|      0|        });
  186|       |
  187|       |        // Spawn task to read from stdout and route messages to appropriate channels
  188|      0|        let stdout_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
  189|      0|            let reader = BufReader::new(stdout);
  190|      0|            let mut lines = reader.lines();

  192|      0|                if let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&line) {
  193|       |                    // Route based on whether message has an "id" field:
  194|       |                    // - Messages with "id" are responses to our requests
  195|       |                    // - Messages without "id" are notifications from the server
  196|      0|                    if let Some(id) = value

  202|      0|                        let mut pending = pending_requests_clone.lock().await;

  204|       |                            // Send response to the waiting request
  205|      0|                            if sender.send(value).is_err() {
  206|      0|                                tracing::debug!("Response receiver dropped for request {}", id);
  207|      0|                            }

  209|       |                            // No pending request found - this is a stale response from a
  210|       |                            // previous session that was already handled or timed out.
  211|       |                            // This is normal during rapid session transitions.
  212|      0|                            tracing::trace!(
  213|       |                                "Ignoring stale response with id {} (no pending request)",
  214|       |                                id
  215|       |                            );
  216|       |                        }
  217|      0|                    } else if tx_notifications.send(value).await.is_err() {
  218|      0|                        break;
  219|      0|                    }
  220|       |                } else {
  221|      0|                    tracing::warn!(
  222|       |                        "Failed to parse ACP response: {}",
  223|      0|                        &line[..line.len().min(100)]
  224|       |                    );

  232|      0|            pending_requests,
  233|      0|            notification_rx: Some(notification_rx),
  234|      0|            stdin_task: Some(stdin_task),
  235|      0|            stdout_task: Some(stdout_task),
  236|      0|            request_timeout,
  237|      0|        })
  238|      0|    }

  240|       |    /// Send a JSON-RPC request and wait for response with timeout.
  241|      0|    async fn request(&mut self, method: &str, params: Value) -> Result<Value> {
  242|      0|        let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
  243|      0|        let request = json!({
  244|      0|            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  245|      0|            "id": id,
  246|      0|            "method": method,
  247|      0|            "params": params,
  248|       |        });
  249|       |
  250|      0|        let request_str = serde_json::to_string(&request)?;
  251|      0|        if method == "session/new" {
  252|      0|            tracing::info!(
  253|       |                "📤 Creating new session: model={}, mcpServers={}",
  254|      0|                params
  255|      0|                    .get("model")
  256|      0|                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
  257|      0|                    .unwrap_or("unknown"),
  258|      0|                params
  259|      0|                    .get("mcpServers")
  260|      0|                    .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
  261|      0|                    .map_or(0, std::vec::Vec::len)
  262|       |            );
  263|       |        } else {
  264|      0|            tracing::debug!("📤 ACP {}: id={}", method, id);
  265|       |        }
  266|       |
  267|       |        // For session/prompt, we don't need to wait for a response since the actual
  268|       |        // results come via notifications. Return immediately.
  269|      0|        if method == "session/prompt" {
  270|      0|            self.tx
  271|      0|                .send(request_str)
  272|      0|                .await
  273|      0|                .context("Failed to send request")?;
  274|      0|            return Ok(json!({}));
  275|      0|        }
  276|       |
  277|       |        // Create a oneshot channel to receive the response for this specific request
  278|      0|        let (response_tx, response_rx) = oneshot::channel();
  279|       |
  280|       |        // Register the pending request before sending to avoid race conditions
  281|       |        {
  282|      0|            let mut pending = self.pending_requests.lock().await;
  283|      0|            pending.insert(id.clone(), response_tx);
  284|       |        }
  285|       |
  286|       |        // Send the request
  287|      0|        if let Err(e) = self.tx.send(request_str).await {
  288|       |            // Remove the pending request if send fails
  289|      0|            let mut pending = self.pending_requests.lock().await;
  290|      0|            pending.remove(&id);
  291|      0|            return Err(e).context("Failed to send request");
  292|      0|        }
  293|       |
  294|       |        // Wait for the response on our dedicated channel with timeout
  295|      0|        let response = match tokio::time::timeout(self.request_timeout, response_rx).await {
  296|      0|            Ok(Ok(response)) => response,
  297|       |            Ok(Err(_)) => {
  298|       |                // Channel closed - receiver dropped
  299|      0|                anyhow::bail!("Response channel closed before receiving response");
  300|       |            }
  301|      0|            Err(_elapsed) => {
  302|       |                // Timeout - remove the pending request so we don't leak memory
  303|      0|                let mut pending = self.pending_requests.lock().await;
  304|      0|                pending.remove(&id);
  305|      0|                tracing::error!(
  306|       |                    "⏰ Timeout waiting for ACP {} response after {:?}",
  307|       |                    method,
  308|       |                    self.request_timeout
  309|       |                );
  310|      0|                anyhow::bail!(
  311|       |                    "Timeout waiting for ACP {} response after {:?}",
  312|       |                    method,
  313|       |                    self.request_timeout
  314|       |                );
  315|       |            }
  316|       |        };
  317|       |
  318|      0|        if method == "session/new" {
  319|      0|            if let Some(result) = response.get("result") {
  320|      0|                let session_id = result
  321|      0|                    .get("sessionId")
  322|      0|                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
  323|      0|                    .unwrap_or("unknown");
  324|      0|                tracing::info!("📥 Session created: {}", session_id);
  325|      0|            }
  326|      0|        }
  327|       |
  328|      0|        if let Some(error) = response.get("error") {
  329|      0|            anyhow::bail!("ACP error: {error}");
  330|      0|        }
  331|       |
  332|      0|        response
  333|      0|            .get("result")
  334|      0|            .cloned()
  335|      0|            .context("No result in response")
  336|      0|    }

  341|       |    /// Initialize the ACP connection
  342|      0|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  343|       |        let params = json!({
  344|       |            "protocolVersion": 1,
  345|       |            "capabilities": {},
  346|       |            "clientInfo": {
  347|       |                "name": "paperboat",
  348|       |                "version": "0.1.0"
  349|       |            }
  350|       |        });
  351|       |
  352|       |        self.request("initialize", params).await?;
  353|       |        Ok(())
  354|      0|    }
  355|       |
  356|       |    /// Create a new session with the specified mode.
  357|       |    ///
  358|       |    /// Note: Auggie (the Augment CLI) currently ignores the mode parameter.
  359|       |    /// Mode-based restrictions are only enforced by Cursor ACP.
  360|       |    async fn session_new(
  361|       |        &mut self,
  362|       |        model: &str,
  363|       |        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  364|       |        cwd: &str,
  365|       |        mode: SessionMode,
  366|      0|    ) -> Result<SessionNewResponse> {
  367|       |        // Note: Auggie doesn't support mode parameter, but we log it for debugging
  368|       |        if mode != SessionMode::Agent {
  369|       |            tracing::debug!(
  370|       |                "📋 Session mode '{}' requested (Auggie ignores mode, using full access)",
  371|       |                mode.as_str()
  372|       |            );
  373|       |        }
  374|       |
  375|       |        let params = json!({
  376|       |            "model": model,
  377|       |            "cwd": cwd,
  378|       |            "mcpServers": mcp_servers
  379|       |        });
  380|       |
  381|       |        let result = self.request("session/new", params).await?;
  382|       |        Ok(serde_json::from_value(result)?)
  383|      0|    }
  384|       |
  385|       |    /// Send a prompt to a session
  386|      0|    async fn session_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {
  387|       |        let params = json!({
  388|       |            "sessionId": session_id,
  389|       |            "prompt": [
  390|       |                {
  391|       |                    "type": "text",
  392|       |                    "text": prompt
  393|       |                }
  394|       |            ],
  395|       |        });
  396|       |
  397|       |        self.request("session/prompt", params).await?;
  398|       |        Ok(())
  399|      0|    }
  400|       |
  401|       |    /// Receive next notification from the server
  402|       |    ///
  403|       |    /// Notifications are server-initiated messages (no "id" field) like session/update.
  404|       |    /// Use this to monitor agent progress, receive plans, tool calls, etc.
  405|       |    ///
  406|       |    /// Returns an error if the notification receiver has been taken via `take_notification_rx()`.
  407|      0|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  408|       |        let rx = self
  409|       |            .notification_rx
  410|       |            .as_mut()
  411|       |            .context("Notification receiver has been taken")?;
  412|       |        rx.recv().await.context("Failed to receive notification")
  413|      0|    }
  414|       |
  415|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for external routing.
  416|      0|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  417|      0|        self.notification_rx.take()
  418|      0|    }
  419|       |
  420|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the ACP client.
  421|       |    ///
  422|       |    /// This closes stdin (signaling EOF to the child), waits for the child process
  423|       |    /// to exit (with a timeout), and cleans up background tasks.
  424|      0|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  425|       |        tracing::debug!("Shutting down ACP client");
  426|       |
  427|       |        // Drop the sender to close the stdin channel, which will cause the stdin
  428|       |        // task to close stdin, signaling EOF to the child process
  429|       |        // Note: We can't actually drop self.tx since we only have &mut self,
  430|       |        // but we can close it by dropping a clone after sending nothing
  431|       |        // Actually, let's just abort the tasks and kill the process
  432|       |
  433|       |        // Abort the stdin and stdout tasks
  434|       |        if let Some(task) = self.stdin_task.take() {
  435|       |            task.abort();
  436|       |        }
  437|       |        if let Some(task) = self.stdout_task.take() {
  438|       |            task.abort();
  439|       |        }
  440|       |
  441|       |        // Try to kill the child process gracefully first, then forcefully
  442|       |        // First, try SIGTERM via start_kill()
  443|       |        if let Err(e) = self.child.start_kill() {
  444|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to send kill signal to child: {}", e);
  445|       |        }
  446|       |
  447|       |        // Wait for the child to exit with a timeout
  448|       |        let wait_result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), self.child.wait()).await;
  449|       |
  450|       |        match wait_result {
  451|       |            Ok(Ok(status)) => {
  452|       |                tracing::debug!("ACP child process exited with status: {}", status);
  453|       |            }
  454|       |            Ok(Err(e)) => {
  455|       |                tracing::warn!("Error waiting for ACP child process: {}", e);
  456|       |            }
  457|       |            Err(_) => {
  458|       |                // Timeout - process didn't exit, try to kill it more forcefully
  459|       |                tracing::warn!("ACP child process did not exit within timeout, killing forcefully");
  460|       |                if let Err(e) = self.child.kill().await {
  461|       |                    tracing::error!("Failed to forcefully kill ACP child: {}", e);
  462|       |                }
  463|       |            }
  464|       |        }
  465|       |
  466|       |        Ok(())
  467|      0|    }
  468|       |}
  469|       |
  470|       |impl Drop for AcpClient {
  471|      0|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  472|       |        // Abort background tasks to prevent them from running after drop
  473|      0|        if let Some(task) = self.stdin_task.take() {
  474|      0|            task.abort();
  475|      0|        }
  476|      0|        if let Some(task) = self.stdout_task.take() {
  477|      0|            task.abort();
  478|      0|        }
  479|       |
  480|       |        // Initiate process termination (can't wait in Drop since it's not async)
  481|      0|        if let Err(e) = self.child.start_kill() {
  482|      0|            tracing::warn!("Failed to initiate kill of ACP child process: {}", e);
  483|      0|        }
  484|      0|    }
  485|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/agents/config.rs:
    1|       |//! Centralized agent tool configuration.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module defines which tools each agent type has access to, for both:
    4|       |//! - **MCP tools**: Our orchestration tools (`spawn_agents`, complete, `create_task`, etc.)
    5|       |//! - **Backend tools**: The agent's built-in tools (str-replace-editor, save-file, etc.)
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! Tool access is controlled via:
    8|       |//! - `PAPERBOAT_AGENT_TYPE` env var: Controls which MCP tools are exposed
    9|       |//! - `PAPERBOAT_REMOVED_TOOLS` env var: Controls which backend tools are removed
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! Note: The `removed_auggie_tools` field name is historical but applies to all backends.
   12|       |
   13|       |/// All auggie tools that can be filtered (used in tests).
   14|       |/// These are the tools provided by the Augment platform.

    1|       |//! Agent templates and registry.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Agent roles are auto-discovered from the `prompts/` directory at compile time.
    4|       |//! To add a new agent type, create `prompts/newrole.txt` and rebuild.
    5|       |
    6|       |pub mod config;
    7|       |mod templates;
    8|       |
    9|       |pub use config::{
   10|       |    get_tool_config, AgentToolConfig, IMPLEMENTER_CONFIG, ORCHESTRATOR_CONFIG, PLANNER_CONFIG,
   11|       |};
   12|       |pub use templates::AgentRegistry;
   13|       |
   14|       |// Include the auto-generated roles module
   15|       |mod generated {
   16|       |    include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/generated_roles.rs"));
   17|       |}
   18|       |
   19|       |pub use generated::{get_prompt, SPAWNABLE_ROLES};
   20|       |
   21|       |/// Agent roles that can be spawned.
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// Note: The enum variants are kept for type safety in internal code,
   24|       |/// but the actual list of spawnable roles is auto-discovered from `prompts/`.
   25|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
   26|       |pub enum AgentRole {
   27|       |    /// Standard implementer - can edit files and run processes
   28|       |    Implementer,
   29|       |    /// Verifier - read-only but can run tests
   30|       |    Verifier,
   31|       |    /// Explorer - read-only, for context gathering
   32|       |    Explorer,
   33|       |    /// Custom agent with user-defined prompt and tools
   34|       |    Custom,
   35|       |    /// Dynamic role loaded from prompts/ directory
   36|       |    Dynamic(String),
   37|       |}
   38|       |
   39|       |impl AgentRole {
   40|       |    /// Parse a role from string. Returns Dynamic for unknown roles that have prompts.
   41|     71|    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
   42|     71|        let lower = s.to_lowercase();
   43|     71|        match lower.as_str() {
   44|     71|            "implementer" => Some(Self::Implementer),
                                           ^67
   45|      4|            "verifier" => Some(Self::Verifier),
                                        ^1
   46|      3|            "explorer" => Some(Self::Explorer),
                                        ^1
   47|      2|            "custom" => Some(Self::Custom),
                                      ^1
   48|       |            _ => {
   49|       |                // Check if it's a valid dynamic role (has a prompt file)
   50|      1|                if SPAWNABLE_ROLES.contains(&lower.as_str()) {
   51|      1|                    Some(Self::Dynamic(lower))
   52|       |                } else {
   53|      0|                    None
   54|       |                }
   55|       |            }
   56|       |        }
   57|     71|    }
   58|       |
   59|       |    /// Get the string representation of this role.
   60|     72|    pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
   61|     72|        match self {
   62|     68|            Self::Implementer => "implementer",
   63|      2|            Self::Verifier => "verifier",
   64|      1|            Self::Explorer => "explorer",
   65|      1|            Self::Custom => "custom",
   66|      0|            Self::Dynamic(name) => name.as_str(),
   67|       |        }
   68|     72|    }
   69|       |}
   70|       |
   71|       |#[cfg(test)]
   72|       |mod tests {
   73|       |    use super::*;
   74|       |
   75|       |    #[test]
   76|      1|    fn test_agent_role_from_str() {
   77|      1|        assert_eq!(
   78|      1|            AgentRole::from_str("implementer"),
   79|       |            Some(AgentRole::Implementer)
   80|       |        );
   81|      1|        assert_eq!(AgentRole::from_str("VERIFIER"), Some(AgentRole::Verifier));
   82|      1|        assert_eq!(AgentRole::from_str("Explorer"), Some(AgentRole::Explorer));
   83|      1|        assert_eq!(AgentRole::from_str("custom"), Some(AgentRole::Custom));
   84|      1|    }

   95|      1|        assert!(SPAWNABLE_ROLES.contains(&"implementer"));

   97|      1|        assert!(SPAWNABLE_ROLES.contains(&"explorer"));

   99|       |        // orchestrator and planner should NOT be in spawnable roles

  101|      1|        assert!(!SPAWNABLE_ROLES.contains(&"planner"));
  102|      1|    }

  104|       |    #[test]
  105|      1|    fn test_get_prompt_returns_content() {
  106|       |        // Should be able to get prompts for discovered roles
  107|      1|        assert!(get_prompt("implementer").is_some());
  108|      1|        assert!(get_prompt("verifier").is_some());

   14|       |/// Strip MCP server prefixes from tool names for cleaner logging.
   15|       |///

   21|       |    // Pattern: toolname_servername (e.g., "complete_paperboat-implementer-abc123")
   22|       |    // We want to extract just "toolname"

   24|      0|        let potential_prefix = &title[..underscore_pos];
   25|      0|        let potential_suffix = &title[underscore_pos + 1..];
   26|       |        // Check if suffix looks like our MCP server name
   27|      0|        if potential_suffix.starts_with("paperboat-") {
   28|      0|            return potential_prefix;
   29|      0|        }
   30|      0|    }
   31|      0|    title

   67|       |///
   68|       |/// This handles the agent's dedicated socket, responding to tool calls
   69|       |/// (especially the `Complete` call that signals the agent is done).
   70|      2|pub async fn run_agent_handler(
   71|      2|    mut socket_handle: AgentSocketHandle,
   72|      2|    mut session_rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<serde_json::Value>,
   73|      2|    timeout: std::time::Duration,
   74|      2|    role: &str,
   75|      2|    task: &str,

   80|       |        loop {
   81|      2|            tokio::select! {
   82|       |                // Handle tool calls from the agent's socket
   83|      2|                Some(tool_msg) = socket_handle.tool_rx.recv() => {
                                   ^0

   85|       |
   86|      0|                    let depth = writer.depth();
   87|      0|                    match &request.tool_call {
   88|      0|                        ToolCall::Complete { success, message, notes, add_tasks } => {
   89|      0|                            tracing::info!(
   90|       |                                "[L{}] ✅ [{}] Agent {} complete: success={}, message={:?}",
   91|       |                                depth, agent_name, role, success, message
   92|       |                            );
   93|       |
   94|      0|                            if let Some(msg) = message {
   95|      0|                                let _ = writer.write_result(msg).await;
   96|      0|                            }
   97|       |
   98|       |                            // Log notes if provided
   99|      0|                            if let Some(n) = notes {
  100|      0|                                tracing::info!("[L{}] 📝 [{}] Agent notes: {}", depth, agent_name, n);
  101|      0|                            }
  102|       |
  103|       |                            // Log add_tasks if provided
  104|      0|                            if let Some(tasks) = add_tasks {
  105|      0|                                tracing::info!("[L{}] 📋 [{}] Agent suggested {} task(s)", depth, agent_name, tasks.len());
  106|      0|                            }
  107|       |
  108|       |                            // Send success response
  109|      0|                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  110|      0|                                request.request_id,
  111|      0|                                message.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "Done".to_string()),
  112|       |                            );
  113|      0|                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  114|       |
  115|       |                            // Clean up socket before returning
  116|      0|                            socket_handle.cleanup();

  119|      0|                                success: *success,
  120|      0|                                message: message.clone(),

  122|      0|                                add_tasks: add_tasks.clone(),
  123|      0|                            };
  124|       |                        }

  126|       |                            // Worker agents should only call complete()
  127|       |                            // Log warning and return error

  130|      0|                                agent_name, role, other.tool_type()

  135|       |                                    "Worker agents can only call complete(). \
  136|       |                                     Tool '{}' is not available.",
  137|      0|                                    other.tool_type()
  138|       |                                ),
  139|       |                            );
  140|      0|                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  141|       |                        }
  142|       |                    }
  143|       |                }
  144|       |
  145|       |                // Handle ACP session messages (for logging and completion detection)
  146|      2|                Some(msg) = session_rx.recv() => {
  147|      2|                    if let Some(params) = msg.get("params") {
  148|      2|                        if let Some(update) = params.get("update") {
  149|       |                            // Support both ACP format (sessionUpdate) and CLI format (type)
  150|      2|                            if let Some(session_update) = update

  166|       |                                        // socket connection issues. The agent may have finished its work

  217|      0|                                    }

  222|       |                }

  274|      0|    timeout: std::time::Duration,
  275|      0|) -> bool {

  277|      0|        while let Some(msg) = session_rx.recv().await {

  279|      0|            if let Some(params) = msg.get("params") {

  281|       |                    // Support both ACP format (sessionUpdate) and CLI format (type)
  282|      0|                    if let Some(session_update) = update

  289|      0|                            return true;

  291|      0|                    }
  292|      0|                }
  293|      0|            }
  294|       |        }
  295|      0|        false
  296|      0|    })

   18|       |/// Spawns a notification routing task for an agent transport.
   19|       |///
   20|       |/// Routes messages from the agent's transport to the shared session router.
   21|       |/// Returns true if the router was started successfully.
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// This works with any `AgentTransport` implementation (ACP or CLI),
   24|       |/// using the `take_notification_rx()` method which all transports implement.
   25|      0|pub fn spawn_notification_router(
   26|      0|    agent_transport: &mut dyn AgentTransport,
   27|      0|    session_router: Arc<RwLock<SessionRouter>>,
   28|      0|    agent_name: &str,
   29|      0|) -> bool {
   30|      0|    let agent_notification_rx = agent_transport.take_notification_rx();
   31|      0|    if let Some(notification_rx) = agent_notification_rx {
   32|      0|        let agent_id_for_log = agent_name.to_string();
   33|      0|        tokio::spawn(async move {
   34|      0|            let mut rx: mpsc::Receiver<serde_json::Value> = notification_rx;
   35|      0|            while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {
   36|      0|                let routed = {
   37|      0|                    let router = session_router.read().await;
   38|      0|                    router.route(msg.clone())
   39|       |                };
   40|      0|                if !routed {
   41|      0|                    tracing::trace!(
   42|       |                        "[{}] Agent message not routed (session may have ended)",
   43|       |                        agent_id_for_log
   44|       |                    );
   45|      0|                }
   46|       |            }
   47|      0|            tracing::debug!("[{}] Agent notification channel closed", agent_id_for_log);
   48|      0|        });
   49|      0|        true
   50|       |    } else {
   51|      0|        tracing::warn!(
   52|       |            "[{}] Could not take notification channel from agent transport",
   53|       |            agent_name
   54|       |        );
   55|      0|        false
   56|       |    }

   96|      1|        let _agent_transport = agent_transport;

  137|      1|            role,

  139|      1|            success: completion.success,
  140|      1|            message: completion.message,
  141|      1|            suggested_task_ids,
  142|      1|        };
  143|       |
  144|       |        // Send result (ignore if receiver dropped)
  145|      1|        let _ = result_tx.send(result);

  149|       |/// Processes agent completion: stores notes, creates suggested tasks, updates status.

  152|      1|async fn process_agent_completion(
  153|      1|    completion: &AgentCompletionData,
  154|      1|    role: &str,
  155|      1|    task_id: Option<&String>,
  156|      1|    task_manager: &Arc<RwLock<TaskManager>>,
  157|      1|) -> Vec<String> {

                              ^0

  161|      0|        tm.add_note(role, task_id.cloned(), notes.clone());
  162|      1|    }
  163|       |
  164|       |    // Create suggested tasks if provided, tracking the IDs

                              ^0

  173|       |        }
  174|      1|    }
  175|       |
  176|       |    // Update task status if linked to a tracked task

  178|      1|        update_task_completion(
  179|      1|            task_manager,
  180|      1|            tid,
  181|      1|            completion.success,
  182|      1|            completion.message.as_deref(),
  183|      1|        )

  185|      0|    }
  186|       |
  187|      1|    suggested_task_ids

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/app/agent_spawner.rs:
    1|       |//! Generic agent spawning.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides a generalized interface for spawning worker agents
    4|       |//! (implementers, verifiers, etc.) with support for concurrent execution.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Retry Logic
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Agent spawning includes automatic retry logic for transient errors like
    9|       |//! MCP server startup failures. The retry configuration can be customized
   10|       |//! via environment variables:
   11|       |//! - `PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRIES`: Max retry attempts (default: 3)
   12|       |//! - `PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRY_DELAY_MS`: Initial delay in ms (default: 500)
   13|       |
   14|       |use super::agent_session_handler::{
   15|       |    spawn_agent_handler_task, spawn_notification_router, AgentHandlerParams,
   16|       |};
   17|       |use super::concurrent_spawner::{
   18|       |    create_spawn_error, extract_role_and_task, handle_fire_and_forget, wait_for_all, wait_for_any,
   19|       |};
   20|       |use super::retry::{retry_async, RetryConfig};
   21|       |use super::socket::{setup_agent_socket, AgentSocketHandle};
   22|       |use super::spawn_config::{build_custom_prompt, validate_no_unreplaced_placeholders, AgentResult};
   23|       |use super::tool_filtering::{build_mcp_server_config, compute_removed_tools};
   24|       |use super::types::truncate_for_log;
   25|       |use super::App;
   26|       |use crate::acp::SessionMode;
   27|       |use crate::backend::transport::{
   28|       |    AgentTransport, AgentType, SessionConfig, SessionInfo, TransportKind,
   29|       |};
   30|       |use crate::backend::{Backend, TransportConfig};
   31|       |use crate::mcp_server::{AgentSpec, ResolvedAgentSpec, WaitMode};
   32|       |use crate::tasks::TaskStatus;
   33|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   34|       |use serde_json::Value;
   35|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
   36|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   37|       |use std::time::Duration;
   38|       |use tokio::sync::oneshot;
   39|       |
   40|       |/// Result of spawning an agent via sequential mode.
   41|       |pub struct AgentSession {
   42|       |    /// The session ID for the agent session
   43|       |    pub session_id: String,
   44|       |    /// The model used for this session
   45|       |    pub model: String,
   46|       |    /// The prompt sent to the agent
   47|       |    pub prompt: String,
   48|       |    /// Socket handle for CLI transport (must be kept alive during the session).
   49|       |    /// This field is intentionally not read directly - its presence keeps the socket listener
   50|       |    /// alive until the AgentSession is dropped.
   51|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   52|       |    socket_handle: Option<AgentSocketHandle>,
   53|       |    /// Tool receiver extracted from the socket handle (for passing to wait functions)
   54|       |    tool_rx: Option<super::types::ToolReceiver>,
   55|       |}
   56|       |
   57|       |impl AgentSession {
   58|       |    /// Take the tool receiver for use in wait_for_session_output.
   59|       |    /// Returns None if there's no CLI socket handle (e.g., ACP transport).
   60|     65|    pub fn take_tool_rx(&mut self) -> Option<super::types::ToolReceiver> {
   61|     65|        self.tool_rx.take()
   62|     65|    }
   63|       |}
   64|       |
   65|       |/// Spawn and initialize an agent transport using the backend with model fallback.
   66|       |///
   67|       |/// This function handles the pattern of creating a transport via the backend,
   68|       |/// initializing it, and creating a session. It tries each model in the chain,
   69|       |/// with automatic retry for transient errors.
   70|       |///
   71|       |/// Returns (Box<dyn AgentTransport>, SessionInfo, actual_model_used) on success.
   72|      3|async fn spawn_transport_with_retry(
   73|      3|    backend: &dyn Backend,
   74|      3|    transport_kind: TransportKind,
   75|      3|    request_timeout: Duration,
   76|      3|    model_chain: &[String],
   77|      3|    mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
   78|      3|    cwd: &str,
   79|      3|    agent_id: &str,
   80|      3|) -> Result<(Box<dyn AgentTransport>, SessionInfo, String)> {
   81|       |    use super::retry::is_model_not_available_error;
   82|       |
   83|      3|    let retry_config = RetryConfig::from_env();
   84|       |
   85|       |    // Track the last error for reporting if all models fail
   86|      3|    let mut last_error: Option<anyhow::Error> = None;
   87|       |
   88|      4|    for model in model_chain {
                               ^3
   89|      4|        let operation_name = format!(
   90|       |            "spawn {} session for {agent_id} with model {model}",
   91|      4|            transport_kind.as_str()
   92|       |        );
   93|       |
   94|       |        // Create transport config for this model
   95|      4|        let config = TransportConfig::new(PathBuf::from(cwd))
   96|      4|            .with_model(model.clone())
   97|      4|            .with_request_timeout(request_timeout)
   98|      4|            .with_mcp_servers(mcp_servers.clone());
   99|       |
  100|       |        // Try to create and initialize the transport
  101|      5|        let result = retry_async(&retry_config, &operation_name, || {
                          ^4       ^4          ^4             ^4
  102|      5|            let config = config.clone();
  103|      5|            let model = model.clone();
  104|      5|            let agent_id = agent_id.to_string();
  105|       |
  106|      5|            async move {
  107|       |                // Create a fresh transport instance via the backend
  108|      5|                let mut transport = backend
                                  ^4
  109|      5|                    .create_transport(transport_kind, AgentType::Implementer, config.clone())
  110|      5|                    .await
  111|      5|                    .with_context(|| {
                                                   ^1
  112|      1|                        format!("Failed to create transport for agent_id={agent_id}")
  113|      1|                    })?;
  114|       |
  115|       |                // Initialize the transport connection
  116|      4|                transport.initialize().await.with_context(|| {
                                                                           ^0
  117|      0|                    format!("Failed to initialize transport for agent_id={agent_id}")
  118|      0|                })?;
  119|       |
  120|       |                // Create the session with MCP servers (Agent mode for full tool access)
  121|      4|                let session_config =
  122|      4|                    SessionConfig::new(model, config.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string())
  123|      4|                        .with_mcp_servers(config.mcp_servers)
  124|      4|                        .with_mode(SessionMode::Agent);
  125|       |
  126|      4|                let session_info = transport
                                  ^2
  127|      4|                    .create_session(session_config)
  128|      4|                    .await
  129|      4|                    .with_context(|| format!("Failed to create session for agent_id={agent_id}"))?;
                                                   ^2                                                          ^2
  130|       |
  131|      2|                Ok((transport, session_info))
  132|      5|            }
  133|      5|        })
  134|      4|        .await;
  135|       |
  136|      4|        match result {
  137|      2|            Ok((transport, session_info)) => return Ok((transport, session_info, model.clone())),
  138|      2|            Err(e) => {
  139|      2|                if is_model_not_available_error(&e) {
  140|      1|                    tracing::debug!(
  141|       |                        "Model '{}' not available for agent {}, trying next in chain...",
  142|       |                        model,
  143|       |                        agent_id
  144|       |                    );
  145|      1|                    last_error = Some(e);
  146|      1|                    continue; // Try next model
  147|      1|                }
  148|       |                // Non-model error - propagate immediately
  149|      1|                return Err(e);
  150|       |            }
  151|       |        }
  152|       |    }
  153|       |
  154|       |    // All models in the chain failed
  155|      0|    Err(last_error
  156|      0|        .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No models in fallback chain"))
  157|      0|        .context(format!(
  158|       |            "All models in fallback chain failed for agent {}: {:?}",
  159|       |            agent_id, model_chain
  160|       |        )))
  161|      3|}
  162|       |
  163|       |impl App {
  164|       |    /// Spawn an implementer agent (convenience wrapper for `spawn_agent_with_resolved_spec`).
  165|       |    /// Returns an `AgentSession` containing session info and socket handle.
  166|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self), fields(agent_type = "implementer"))]
  167|     65|    pub(crate) async fn spawn_implementer(&mut self, task: &str) -> Result<AgentSession> {
  168|       |        let spec = ResolvedAgentSpec {
  169|       |            role: "implementer".to_string(),
  170|       |            task: task.to_string(),
  171|       |            task_id: None,
  172|       |            prompt: None,
  173|       |            tools: None,
  174|       |            model_complexity: None,
  175|       |        };
  176|       |        self.spawn_agent_with_resolved_spec(&spec).await
  177|     65|    }
  178|       |
  179|       |    /// Spawn an agent based on a `ResolvedAgentSpec`.
  180|       |    ///
  181|       |    /// Determines the prompt and `removed_tools` based on the role:
  182|       |    /// - Custom: requires prompt and tools from spec
  183|       |    /// - Template roles (implementer, verifier, explorer): uses registry templates
  184|       |    /// - Unknown roles: falls back to implementer behavior with warning
  185|       |    ///
  186|       |    /// Returns an `AgentSession` containing session info and socket handle.
  187|       |    /// The socket handle must be kept alive until the agent session completes.
  188|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, spec), fields(agent_type = %spec.role, task_id = ?spec.task_id, session_id))]
  189|     65|    pub(crate) async fn spawn_agent_with_resolved_spec(
  190|     65|        &mut self,
  191|     65|        spec: &ResolvedAgentSpec,
  192|     65|    ) -> Result<AgentSession> {
  193|       |        let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?.to_string_lossy().to_string();
  194|       |
  195|       |        // Get the path to the current binary
  196|       |        let binary_path =
  197|       |            std::env::current_exe().context("Failed to get current executable path")?;
  198|       |
  199|       |        // For CLI transport, create a unique socket to prevent MCP server caching.
  200|       |        // This ensures each agent session gets its own MCP server process with the
  201|       |        // correct agent type and tools.
  202|       |        let (socket_address_str, cli_socket_handle) =
  203|       |            if self.acp_worker.kind() == TransportKind::Cli {
  204|       |                let agent_id = format!("cli-{}", &uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8]);
  205|      0|                let socket_handle = setup_agent_socket(&agent_id).await.with_context(|| {
  206|      0|                    format!("Failed to create unique socket for CLI agent: {agent_id}")
  207|      0|                })?;
  208|       |                let addr = socket_handle.socket_address.as_str().to_string();
  209|       |                tracing::debug!(
  210|       |                    "🔌 Created unique socket for CLI worker: {} -> {}",
  211|       |                    agent_id,
  212|       |                    addr
  213|       |                );
  214|       |                (addr, Some(socket_handle))
  215|       |            } else {
  216|       |                let addr = self
  217|       |                    .socket_address
  218|       |                    .as_ref()
  219|       |                    .context("Socket not set up")?
  220|       |                    .as_str()
  221|       |                    .to_string();
  222|       |                (addr, None)
  223|       |            };
  224|       |
  225|       |        // Determine prompt and removed_tools based on role
  226|       |        // Note: context is empty string in sequential path (only used in concurrent spawning)
  227|       |        let (prompt, removed_tools) = self.resolve_prompt_and_tools(spec, "")?;
  228|       |
  229|       |        // Validate that all placeholders have been replaced
  230|       |        validate_no_unreplaced_placeholders(&prompt, &spec.role)?;
  231|       |
  232|       |        // Configure MCP server with agent type
  233|       |        let mcp_server = build_mcp_server_config(
  234|       |            &binary_path,
  235|       |            &socket_address_str,
  236|       |            &spec.role,
  237|       |            &removed_tools,
  238|       |            None,
  239|       |        );
  240|       |        let mcp_servers = vec![mcp_server];
  241|       |
  242|       |        // Resolve model: fallback chain -> tier -> auto resolution -> backend model chain
  243|       |        let model_chain = self.resolve_implementer_model(spec.model_complexity)?;
  244|       |
  245|       |        // Create session with retry logic and model fallback
  246|       |        let (response, actual_model) = self
  247|       |            .create_worker_session_with_retry(&model_chain, mcp_servers, &cwd)
  248|       |            .await?;
  249|       |
  250|       |        // Record session_id in the current span for tracing correlation
  251|       |        tracing::Span::current().record("session_id", &response.session_id);
  252|       |        tracing::debug!("🔨 {} prompt:\n{}", spec.role, prompt);
  253|       |
  254|       |        self.acp_worker
  255|       |            .send_prompt(&response.session_id, &prompt)
  256|       |            .await?;
  257|       |
  258|       |        // Extract tool_rx from the socket handle if present (for CLI transport)
  259|       |        let (socket_handle, tool_rx) = match cli_socket_handle {
  260|       |            Some(mut handle) => {
  261|       |                // Take the tool_rx out of the handle so we can pass it to wait_for_session_output
  262|       |                // We need to keep the handle alive (it has the listener task), but use its tool_rx
  263|       |                let rx = std::mem::replace(
  264|       |                    &mut handle.tool_rx,
  265|       |                    tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1).1, // Replace with dummy receiver
  266|       |                );
  267|       |                (Some(handle), Some(rx))
  268|       |            }
  269|       |            None => (None, None),
  270|       |        };
  271|       |
  272|       |        Ok(AgentSession {
  273|       |            session_id: response.session_id,
  274|       |            model: actual_model,
  275|       |            prompt,
  276|       |            socket_handle,
  277|       |            tool_rx,
  278|       |        })
  279|     65|    }
  280|       |
  281|       |    /// Create a worker session with model fallback chain and retry logic.
  282|       |    ///
  283|       |    /// This tries each model in the chain, and for each model:
  284|       |    /// - Handles transient MCP server startup errors with exponential backoff
  285|       |    /// - Falls back to the next model if "model not available" error occurs
  286|       |    ///
  287|       |    /// Returns (SessionInfo, actual_model_used) on success.
  288|     65|    async fn create_worker_session_with_retry(
  289|     65|        &mut self,
  290|     65|        model_chain: &[String],
  291|     65|        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  292|     65|        cwd: &str,
  293|     65|    ) -> Result<(SessionInfo, String)> {
  294|       |        use super::retry::{is_model_not_available_error, is_transient_error};
  295|       |
  296|     65|        let retry_config = RetryConfig::from_env();
  297|       |
  298|       |        // Track the last error for reporting if all models fail
  299|     65|        let mut last_error: Option<anyhow::Error> = None;
  300|       |
  301|     65|        for model in model_chain {
  302|     65|            let mut attempt = 0;
  303|     65|            let mut delay = retry_config.initial_delay;
  304|       |
  305|       |            loop {
  306|     65|                attempt += 1;
  307|       |
  308|       |                // Create session config with Agent mode for full tool access
  309|     65|                let config = SessionConfig::new(model, cwd)
  310|     65|                    .with_mcp_servers(mcp_servers.clone())
  311|     65|                    .with_mode(SessionMode::Agent);
  312|       |
  313|     65|                match self.acp_worker.create_session(config).await {
  314|     65|                    Ok(response) => {
  315|     65|                        if attempt > 1 {
  316|      0|                            tracing::info!(
  317|       |                                "🔄 Worker create_session succeeded on attempt {}/{} with model {}",
  318|       |                                attempt,
  319|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  320|       |                                model
  321|       |                            );
  322|     65|                        }
  323|     65|                        return Ok((response, model.clone()));
  324|       |                    }
  325|      0|                    Err(e) => {
  326|       |                        // Check if model is not available - try next model
  327|      0|                        if is_model_not_available_error(&e) {
  328|      0|                            tracing::debug!(
  329|       |                                "Model '{}' not available, trying next in chain...",
  330|       |                                model
  331|       |                            );
  332|      0|                            last_error = Some(e);
  333|      0|                            break; // Move to next model in chain
  334|      0|                        }
  335|       |
  336|      0|                        let is_transient = is_transient_error(&e);
  337|      0|                        let can_retry = attempt <= retry_config.max_retries && is_transient;
  338|       |
  339|      0|                        if can_retry {
  340|      0|                            tracing::warn!(
  341|       |                                "⚠️ Worker create_session failed (attempt {}/{}, model {}): {}. Retrying in {:?}...",
  342|       |                                attempt,
  343|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  344|       |                                model,
  345|       |                                e,
  346|       |                                delay
  347|       |                            );
  348|      0|                            tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
  349|       |
  350|       |                            // Exponential backoff with cap
  351|      0|                            delay = Duration::from_secs_f64(
  352|      0|                                (delay.as_secs_f64() * retry_config.backoff_multiplier)
  353|      0|                                    .min(retry_config.max_delay.as_secs_f64()),
  354|      0|                            );
  355|       |                        } else {
  356|       |                            // Non-transient, non-model error - fail immediately
  357|      0|                            let reason = if is_transient {
  358|      0|                                "exhausted retries"
  359|       |                            } else {
  360|      0|                                "non-transient error"
  361|       |                            };
  362|      0|                            tracing::error!(
  363|       |                                "❌ Worker create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s) ({reason}): {e:#}",
  364|       |                            );
  365|      0|                            return Err(e).context(format!(
  366|       |                                "Worker create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s)"
  367|       |                            ));
  368|       |                        }
  369|       |                    }
  370|       |                }
  371|       |            }
  372|       |        }
  373|       |
  374|       |        // All models in the chain failed
  375|      0|        Err(last_error
  376|      0|            .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No models in fallback chain"))
  377|      0|            .context(format!(
  378|       |                "All models in fallback chain failed: {:?}",
  379|       |                model_chain
  380|       |            )))
  381|     65|    }
  382|       |
  383|       |    /// Spawn an agent with its own dedicated IPC endpoint.
  384|       |    ///
  385|       |    /// This is used for concurrent agent execution where each agent needs
  386|       |    /// its own IPC endpoint to receive tool call responses.
  387|       |    ///
  388|       |    /// Returns (`session_id`, `model`, prompt, `AgentSocketHandle`, `Box<dyn AgentTransport>`) so the caller can:
  389|       |    /// - Log the model and prompt
  390|       |    /// - Receive tool calls on the agent's dedicated socket
  391|       |    /// - Clean up the socket when done
  392|       |    /// - Keep the transport alive until the agent completes
  393|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, spec, context), fields(agent_type = %spec.role, task_id = ?spec.task_id, session_id))]
  394|      1|    pub(crate) async fn spawn_agent_with_own_socket(
  395|      1|        &mut self,
  396|      1|        spec: &ResolvedAgentSpec,
  397|      1|        context: &str,
  398|      1|    ) -> Result<(
  399|      1|        String,

  402|      1|        AgentSocketHandle,

  404|      1|    )> {
  405|       |        let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?.to_string_lossy().to_string();
  406|       |

  409|       |            std::env::current_exe().context("Failed to get current executable path")?;
  410|       |

  413|       |        tracing::debug!("Creating socket for agent_id={}", &agent_id[..8]);
  414|      0|        let socket_handle = setup_agent_socket(&agent_id).await.with_context(|| {
  415|      0|            format!(
  416|       |                "Failed to create agent socket for agent_id={}",
  417|      0|                &agent_id[..8]
  418|       |            )
  419|      0|        })?;
  420|       |        tracing::debug!(

  422|       |            &agent_id[..8]
  423|       |        );

  426|       |
  427|       |        // Determine prompt and removed_tools based on role
  428|       |        let (prompt, removed_tools) = self.resolve_prompt_and_tools(spec, context)?;
  429|       |
  430|       |        // Validate that all placeholders have been replaced

  440|       |            );
  441|       |        }
  442|       |

  444|       |        let mcp_server = build_mcp_server_config(
  445|       |            &binary_path,
  446|       |            &socket_address_str,
  447|       |            &spec.role,
  448|       |            &removed_tools,
  449|       |            Some(&agent_id[..8]),
  450|       |        );
  451|       |        let mcp_servers = vec![mcp_server.clone()];
  452|       |
  453|       |        tracing::info!(
  454|       |            "🔧 MCP server config for agent_id={}: name={}, socket={}, socket_exists=true",
  455|       |            &agent_id[..8],
  456|       |            mcp_server["name"],
  457|       |            socket_address_str
  458|       |        );
  459|       |
  460|       |        // Resolve model: fallback chain -> tier -> auto resolution -> backend model chain
  461|       |        let model_chain = self.resolve_implementer_model(spec.model_complexity)?;
  462|       |
  463|       |        // Spawn transport using the configured backend (respects cursor:cli vs auggie:acp)
  464|       |        let (mut agent_transport, session_info, actual_model) = spawn_transport_with_retry(
  465|       |            self.backend.as_ref(),
  466|       |            self.transport_kind,
  467|       |            self.timeout_config.request_timeout,
  468|       |            &model_chain,
  469|       |            mcp_servers,
  470|       |            &cwd,
  471|       |            &agent_id[..8],
  472|       |        )
  473|       |        .await?;
  474|       |
  475|       |        // Record session_id in the current span for tracing correlation
  476|       |        tracing::Span::current().record("session_id", &session_info.session_id);
  477|       |        tracing::debug!(
  478|       |            "🔨 {} prompt (with own socket, agent_id={}):\n{}",
  479|       |            spec.role,
  480|       |            &agent_id[..8],
  481|       |            prompt
  482|       |        );
  483|       |
  484|       |        agent_transport
  485|       |            .send_prompt(&session_info.session_id, &prompt)
  486|       |            .await
  487|      0|            .with_context(|| {
  488|      0|                format!(
  489|       |                    "Failed to send prompt for agent_id={}, session_id={}",
  490|      0|                    &agent_id[..8],
  491|       |                    session_info.session_id
  492|       |                )
  493|      0|            })?;
  494|       |
  495|       |        // Return the transport so it stays alive while the agent runs
  496|       |        Ok((

  498|       |            actual_model,
