  500|       |            socket_handle,
  501|       |            agent_transport,
  502|       |        ))
  503|      1|    }
  504|       |
  505|       |    /// Resolves the prompt and removed tools for an agent based on its role.
  506|       |    ///
  507|       |    /// Handles three cases:
  508|       |    /// - **Custom role**: Uses the custom prompt from spec with completion instructions appended.
  509|       |    ///   Removed tools are computed from the optional tools whitelist.
  510|       |    /// - **Template roles** (implementer, verifier, explorer): Uses registry templates with
  511|       |    ///   placeholder substitution for `{task}`, `{user_goal}`, and `{context}`.
  512|       |    /// - **Unknown roles**: Falls back to implementer behavior with a warning.
  513|       |    ///
  514|       |    /// # Arguments
  515|       |    /// * `spec` - The resolved agent specification containing role, task, and optional prompt/tools
  516|       |    /// * `context` - Context string to substitute for `{context}` placeholder (empty for sequential)
  517|       |    ///
  518|       |    /// # Returns
  519|       |    /// A tuple of (prompt, removed tools) ready for use in agent spawning.
  520|     66|    fn resolve_prompt_and_tools(
  521|     66|        &self,
  522|     66|        spec: &ResolvedAgentSpec,
  523|     66|        context: &str,
  524|     66|    ) -> Result<(String, Vec<String>)> {
  525|       |        use crate::agents::AgentRole;
  526|       |
  527|     66|        match AgentRole::from_str(&spec.role) {
  528|       |            Some(AgentRole::Custom) => {
  529|       |                // Custom agents require an explicit prompt
  530|      0|                let custom_prompt = spec
  531|      0|                    .prompt
  532|      0|                    .as_ref()
  533|      0|                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Custom agent requires 'prompt'"))?;
  534|       |
  535|      0|                let full_prompt = build_custom_prompt(custom_prompt);
  536|      0|                let removed = compute_removed_tools(spec.tools.as_ref());
  537|       |
  538|      0|                Ok((full_prompt, removed))
  539|       |            }
  540|     66|            Some(role) => {
  541|       |                // Template role: get from registry and substitute placeholders
  542|     66|                let template = self
  543|     66|                    .agent_registry
  544|     66|                    .get(&role)
  545|     66|                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No template for role: {role:?}"))?;
                                                 ^0                                                ^0
  546|       |
  547|     66|                let prompt = template
  548|     66|                    .prompt_template
  549|     66|                    .replace("{task}", &spec.task)
  550|     66|                    .replace("{user_goal}", &self.original_goal)
  551|     66|                    .replace("{context}", context);
  552|       |
  553|     66|                let removed = template
  554|     66|                    .removed_tools
  555|     66|                    .iter()
  556|     66|                    .map(std::string::ToString::to_string)
  557|     66|                    .collect();
  558|       |
  559|     66|                Ok((prompt, removed))
  560|       |            }
  561|       |            None => {
  562|       |                // Unknown role - treat as implementer for backward compatibility
  563|      0|                tracing::warn!(
  564|       |                    "Unknown agent role '{}', treating as implementer",
  565|       |                    spec.role
  566|       |                );
  567|      0|                let template = self.agent_registry.get(&AgentRole::Implementer).expect(
  568|      0|                    "implementer template must exist - it's a core role with a compile-time prompt",
  569|       |                );
  570|      0|                let prompt = template
  571|      0|                    .prompt_template
  572|      0|                    .replace("{task}", &spec.task)
  573|      0|                    .replace("{user_goal}", &self.original_goal)
  574|      0|                    .replace("{context}", context);
  575|       |
  576|      0|                Ok((prompt, vec![]))
  577|       |            }
  578|       |        }
  579|     66|    }
  580|       |
  581|       |    /// Resolve an [`AgentSpec`] using the `TaskManager` for `task_id` lookups.
  582|       |    ///
  583|       |    /// The lookup supports both exact task IDs (e.g., "task001") and task names
  584|       |    /// (e.g., "Setup database") as a fallback.
  585|       |    ///
  586|       |    /// If `task_id` is not provided but `task` description matches an existing
  587|       |    /// task in the `TaskManager`, this method automatically infers the `task_id`
  588|       |    /// to enable proper task status synchronization.
  589|      1|    async fn resolve_agent_spec(&self, spec: &AgentSpec) -> Result<ResolvedAgentSpec> {
  590|      1|        let task_manager = self.task_manager.read().await;
  591|      1|        let available_ids = task_manager.list_task_ids();
  592|       |
  593|       |        // Try to infer task_id from task description if not explicitly provided
  594|      1|        let inferred_task_id = if spec.task_id.is_none() {
  595|      0|            if let Some(ref task_desc) = spec.task {
  596|       |                // Try to find a matching task by name or description
  597|      0|                if let Some(found_id) = task_manager.find_by_name_or_description(task_desc) {
  598|      0|                    tracing::info!(
  599|       |                        "📋 Inferred task_id '{}' from task description: {}",
  600|       |                        found_id,
  601|      0|                        crate::app::types::truncate_for_log(task_desc, 60)
  602|       |                    );
  603|      0|                    Some(found_id)
  604|       |                } else {
  605|      0|                    None
  606|       |                }
  607|       |            } else {
  608|      0|                None
  609|       |            }
  610|       |        } else {
  611|      1|            None
  612|       |        };
  613|       |
  614|      1|        let mut resolved = spec
  615|      1|            .resolve(|tid| {
  616|       |                // Use get_by_id_or_name for flexible lookup
  617|      1|                let result = task_manager
  618|      1|                    .get_by_id_or_name(tid)
  619|      1|                    .map(|t| t.description.clone());
  620|       |
  621|       |                // Log helpful debug info if lookup fails
  622|      1|                if result.is_none() {
  623|      0|                    tracing::warn!(
  624|       |                        "Task lookup failed for '{}'. Available task IDs: {:?}",
  625|       |                        tid,
  626|       |                        available_ids
  627|       |                    );
  628|      1|                }
  629|      1|                result
  630|      1|            })
  631|      1|            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;
                                       ^0              ^0 ^0
  632|       |
  633|       |        // Apply inferred task_id if we found one
  634|      1|        if resolved.task_id.is_none() {
  635|      0|            if let Some(inferred_id) = inferred_task_id {
  636|      0|                resolved.task_id = Some(inferred_id);
  637|      0|            }
  638|      1|        }
  639|       |
  640|      1|        Ok(resolved)
  641|      1|    }
  642|       |
  643|       |    /// Spawn an agent asynchronously with its own dedicated socket and context.
  644|       |    ///
  645|       |    /// Each agent gets its own IPC endpoint and tool handler task, enabling
  646|       |    /// concurrent execution of multiple agents without routing conflicts.
  647|       |    ///
  648|       |    /// # Arguments
  649|       |    ///
  650|       |    /// * `spec` - The agent specification describing the role and task
  651|       |    /// * `context` - Additional context about neighboring tasks
  652|       |    ///
  653|       |    /// # Returns
  654|       |    ///
  655|       |    /// A tuple containing the session ID and a oneshot receiver for the result.
  656|       |    #[tracing::instrument(
  657|       |        skip(self, spec, context),
  658|       |        fields(
  659|       |            agent_type = spec.role.as_deref().unwrap_or("implementer"),
  660|       |            task_id = ?spec.task_id,
  661|       |            agent_name,
  662|       |            session_id
  663|       |        )
  664|       |    )]
  665|      1|    pub(crate) async fn spawn_agent_async_with_context(
  666|      1|        &mut self,
  667|      1|        spec: &AgentSpec,
  668|      1|        context: &str,
  669|      1|    ) -> Result<(String, oneshot::Receiver<AgentResult>)> {
  670|       |        // Resolve the spec (look up task_id if needed, apply defaults)
  671|       |        let resolved = self.resolve_agent_spec(spec).await?;
  672|       |
  673|       |        // Create implementer writer
  674|       |        let mut impl_writer = self
  675|       |            .current_scope
  676|       |            .implementer_writer()
  677|       |            .await
  678|       |            .context("Failed to create implementer writer")?;
  679|       |
  680|       |        let impl_name = impl_writer.agent_name().clone();
  681|       |        let task = resolved.task.clone();
  682|       |        let role = resolved.role.clone();
  683|       |        let task_id = resolved.task_id.clone();
  684|       |
  685|       |        // Record agent_name in the current span for tracing correlation
  686|       |        tracing::Span::current().record("agent_name", &impl_name);
  687|       |        tracing::info!(
  688|       |            "🔨 [{}] Starting concurrent agent spawn (role={}, task_id={:?}): {}",
  689|       |            impl_name,
  690|       |            role,
  691|       |            task_id,
  692|       |            truncate_for_log(&task, 100)
  693|       |        );
  694|       |
  695|       |        // Record agent spawn metric
  696|       |        crate::metrics::record_agent_spawned(&role);
  697|       |
  698|       |        // If this agent is linked to a tracked task, mark it as InProgress
  699|       |        if let Some(ref tid) = task_id {
  700|       |            let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  701|       |            tm.update_status(
  702|       |                tid,
  703|       |                &TaskStatus::InProgress {
  704|       |                    agent_session: Some(impl_name.clone()),
  705|       |                },
  706|       |            );
  707|       |            tracing::info!(
  708|       |                "📋 Task {} marked as InProgress (agent: {})",
  709|       |                tid,
  710|       |                impl_name
  711|       |            );
  712|       |        }
  713|       |
  714|       |        // Write a preliminary header so we have context in the log even if spawn fails
  715|       |        if let Err(e) = impl_writer.write_header(&task).await {
  716|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to write preliminary header: {}", e);
  717|       |        }
  718|       |
  719|       |        // Spawn the agent with its own dedicated socket and transport
  720|       |        let spawn_result = self.spawn_agent_with_own_socket(&resolved, context).await;
  721|       |
  722|       |        // If spawn failed, log the error to the implementer log file before propagating
  723|       |        let (session_id, model, agent_prompt, socket_handle, mut agent_transport) =
  724|       |            match spawn_result {
  725|       |                Ok(result) => result,
  726|       |                Err(e) => {
  727|       |                    // Mark task as failed if spawn failed
  728|       |                    if let Some(ref tid) = task_id {
  729|       |                        let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  730|       |                        tm.update_status(
  731|       |                            tid,
  732|       |                            &TaskStatus::Failed {
  733|       |                                // Use {:?} to include the full error chain
  734|       |                                error: format!("Agent spawn failed: {e:?}"),
  735|       |                            },
  736|       |                        );
  737|       |                    }
  738|       |                    // Write spawn error to the log file for debugging
  739|       |                    let _ = impl_writer.write_spawn_error(&e).await;
  740|       |                    let _ = impl_writer.finalize(false).await;
  741|       |                    return Err(e);
  742|       |                }
  743|       |            };
  744|       |        impl_writer.set_session_id(session_id.clone());
  745|       |        impl_writer.set_model(model);
  746|       |        // Record session_id in the current span for tracing correlation
  747|       |        tracing::Span::current().record("session_id", &session_id);
  748|       |        if let Err(e) = impl_writer
  749|       |            .write_header_with_prompt(&task, &agent_prompt)
  750|       |            .await
  751|       |        {
  752|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to write agent header: {}", e);
  753|       |        }
  754|       |        // Emit AgentStarted event for TUI
  755|       |        impl_writer.emit_agent_started(&task);
  756|       |
  757|       |        // Create the completion channel
  758|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = oneshot::channel();
  759|       |
  760|       |        // Register session with router for ACP messages
  761|       |        let session_rx = self.register_session(&session_id).await;
  762|       |
  763|       |        // Route agent notifications to the session router
  764|       |        spawn_notification_router(
  765|       |            agent_transport.as_mut(),
  766|       |            Arc::clone(&self.session_router),
  767|       |            &impl_name,
  768|       |        );
  769|       |
  770|       |        // Spawn handler task for this agent
  771|       |        let handler_params = AgentHandlerParams {
  772|       |            agent_transport,
  773|       |            socket_handle,
  774|       |            session_rx,
  775|       |            timeout_duration: self.timeout_config.session_timeout,
  776|       |            role,
  777|       |            task,
  778|       |            impl_name,
  779|       |            task_id,
  780|       |            session_id: session_id.clone(),
  781|       |            session_router: Arc::clone(&self.session_router),
  782|       |            task_manager: Arc::clone(&self.task_manager),
  783|       |            result_tx,
  784|       |        };
  785|       |
  786|       |        spawn_agent_handler_task(handler_params, impl_writer);
  787|       |
  788|       |        Ok((session_id, result_rx))
  789|      1|    }
  790|       |
  791|       |    /// Spawn multiple agents concurrently with the specified wait mode.
  792|       |    ///
  793|       |    /// Each agent gets its own dedicated IPC endpoint for tool call handling,
  794|       |    /// enabling true concurrent execution without routing conflicts.
  795|       |    ///
  796|       |    /// # Arguments
  797|       |    ///
  798|       |    /// * `agents` - Vector of agent specifications to spawn
  799|       |    /// * `wait` - How to wait for the agents:
  800|       |    ///   - `WaitMode::All`: Wait for all agents to complete, return all results
  801|       |    ///   - `WaitMode::Any`: Wait for the first agent to complete, return that result
  802|       |    ///   - `WaitMode::None`: Return immediately with empty results (fire and forget)
  803|       |    ///
  804|       |    /// # Returns
  805|       |    ///
  806|       |    /// A vector of `AgentResult` for completed agents (may be empty for `WaitMode::None`).
  807|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, agents), fields(agent_count = agents.len(), wait_mode = ?wait))]
  808|      0|    pub(crate) async fn spawn_agents_concurrent(
  809|      0|        &mut self,
  810|      0|        agents: Vec<AgentSpec>,
  811|      0|        wait: WaitMode,
  812|      0|    ) -> Vec<AgentResult> {
  813|       |        if agents.is_empty() {
  814|       |            return Vec::new();
  815|       |        }
  816|       |
  817|       |        let agent_count = agents.len();
  818|       |        tracing::info!(
  819|       |            "🚀 Spawning {} agents concurrently (wait={:?})",
  820|       |            agent_count,
  821|       |            wait
  822|       |        );
  823|       |
  824|       |        // Spawn all agents and collect their receivers
  825|       |        let mut receivers = Vec::with_capacity(agent_count);
  826|       |        let mut spawn_errors = Vec::new();
  827|       |
  828|       |        for (index, spec) in agents.iter().enumerate() {
  829|       |            let context = self.generate_task_context(&agents, index, spec).await;
  830|       |            match self.spawn_agent_async_with_context(spec, &context).await {
  831|       |                Ok((_session_id, rx)) => {
  832|       |                    let (role, task) = extract_role_and_task(spec);
  833|       |                    receivers.push((role, task, rx));
  834|       |                }
  835|       |                Err(e) => {
  836|       |                    spawn_errors.push(create_spawn_error(spec, &e));
  837|       |                }
  838|       |            }
  839|       |        }
  840|       |
  841|      0|        match wait {
  842|       |            WaitMode::None => handle_fire_and_forget(receivers.len(), spawn_errors),
  843|       |            WaitMode::Any => wait_for_any(receivers, spawn_errors).await,
  844|       |            WaitMode::All => wait_for_all(receivers, spawn_errors).await,
  845|       |        }
  846|      0|    }
  847|       |
  848|       |    /// Resolve the implementer model configuration to a fallback chain of model strings.
  849|       |    ///
  850|       |    /// This method:
  851|       |    /// 1. Resolves the fallback chain to a tier using `available_tiers`
  852|       |    /// 2. If the tier is Auto, resolves it based on complexity
  853|       |    /// 3. Uses the backend to convert the tier to a list of model IDs to try
  854|       |    ///
  855|       |    /// Returns a vector of model strings to try in order (first is preferred).
  856|     66|    fn resolve_implementer_model(
  857|     66|        &self,
  858|     66|        complexity: Option<crate::mcp_server::ModelComplexity>,
  859|     66|    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
  860|       |        // Step 1: Resolve fallback chain to a tier
  861|     66|        let tier = self
  862|     66|            .model_config
  863|     66|            .implementer_model
  864|     66|            .resolve(&self.model_config.available_tiers)?;
                                                                      ^0
  865|       |
  866|       |        // Step 2: If Auto, resolve based on complexity
  867|     66|        let resolved_tier = if tier.is_auto() {
  868|      0|            let concrete = tier.resolve_auto(complexity);
  869|      0|            tracing::info!(
  870|       |                "🤖 Auto model resolved: {:?} → {} for implementer",
  871|       |                complexity,
  872|       |                concrete
  873|       |            );
  874|      0|            concrete
  875|       |        } else {
  876|     66|            tier
  877|       |        };
  878|       |
  879|       |        // Step 3: Convert tier to backend-specific model fallback chain with effort level
  880|     66|        self.backend
  881|     66|            .resolve_tier(resolved_tier, Some(self.model_config.implementer_effort))
  882|     66|    }
  883|       |}

 1100|      4|            let previous = std::env::var(key).ok();
 1101|      4|            std::env::set_var(key, value);
 1102|      4|            Self { key, previous }
 1103|      4|        }
 1104|       |    }
 1105|       |

 1108|      4|            match &self.previous {
 1109|      0|                Some(value) => std::env::set_var(self.key, value),
 1110|      4|                None => std::env::remove_var(self.key),
 1111|       |            }
 1112|      4|        }
 1113|       |    }

 1123|      1|        let _delay_guard = EnvGuard::set("PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRY_DELAY_MS", "0");
 1124|       |
 1125|      1|        let backend = RecordingBackend::new(vec![
 1126|      1|            AttemptOutcome::Transport(TransportOutcome {
 1127|      1|                initialize_error: None,

 1132|      1|                initialize_error: None,

 1134|      1|                session_id: "worker-session-002",
 1135|      1|            }),
 1136|       |        ]);
 1137|       |
 1138|      1|        let (_transport, session, actual_model) = spawn_transport_with_retry(
 1139|      1|            &backend,
 1140|      1|            TransportKind::Acp,
 1141|      1|            Duration::from_secs(1),

 1145|      1|            "agent-1234",

 1149|       |
 1150|      1|        assert_eq!(actual_model, "sonnet");
 1151|      1|        assert_eq!(session.session_id, "worker-session-002");
 1152|      1|        assert_eq!(

 1156|      1|                    model: "sonnet".to_string(),
 1157|      1|                },

 1159|      1|                    model: "sonnet".to_string(),
 1160|      1|                },
 1161|      1|                RecordedEvent::CreateSession {
 1162|      1|                    model: "sonnet".to_string(),
 1163|      1|                },
 1164|      1|                RecordedEvent::CreateTransport {
 1165|      1|                    model: "sonnet".to_string(),

   14|      0|pub fn create_spawn_error(spec: &AgentSpec, error: &anyhow::Error) -> AgentResult {
   15|      0|    let role = spec
   16|      0|        .role
   17|      0|        .clone()

   19|      0|    let task = spec

   21|      0|        .clone()
   22|      0|        .or_else(|| spec.task_id.clone())

   27|       |
   28|       |    // Analyze the error and provide actionable guidance

   31|      0|        "The task_id you provided does not exist. This often happens when using a fabricated ID \
   32|      0|         instead of actual task IDs from the plan. Call list_tasks() to see available task IDs \
   33|      0|         (e.g., 'task001', 'task002'), then retry spawn_agents with the correct IDs."
   34|      0|    } else if error_str.contains("mcp server startup") {
   35|      0|        "This may be a transient MCP server startup issue. The system attempted automatic retries. \
   36|      0|         Consider: (1) waiting a moment and retrying with spawn_agents, \
   37|      0|         (2) checking system resources, or (3) reducing concurrent agent count."
   38|      0|    } else if error_str.contains("timeout") {
   39|      0|        "The operation timed out. This could indicate system overload or network issues. \
   40|      0|         Consider retrying with fewer concurrent agents or after a brief wait."
   41|      0|    } else if error_str.contains("socket") {
   42|      0|        "Socket communication error. This may indicate the main process is overloaded. \
   43|      0|         Consider retrying the task."
   44|       |    } else {
   45|      0|        "An unexpected error occurred. Review the error details and consider retrying \
   46|      0|         or creating a recovery task."
   47|       |    };

   49|      0|    let message = format!(
   50|       |        "SPAWN FAILED: {}\n\nError details: {:#}\n\nRecovery suggestion: {}{}",

   52|       |        error,
   53|       |        recovery_hint,
   54|      0|        task_id
   55|      0|            .as_ref()

   57|      0|            .unwrap_or_default()
   58|       |    );

   61|      0|        role,
   62|      0|        task,
   63|      0|        success: false,
   64|      0|        message: Some(message),
   65|      0|        suggested_task_ids: task_id.into_iter().collect(),
   66|      0|    }

   83|       |/// Waits for all agents to complete and collects their results.
   84|      1|pub async fn wait_for_all(
   85|      1|    receivers: Vec<(String, String, oneshot::Receiver<AgentResult>)>,
   86|      1|    spawn_errors: Vec<AgentResult>,
   87|      1|) -> Vec<AgentResult> {
   88|      1|    let mut results = spawn_errors;
   89|       |
   90|      2|    for (role, task, receiver) in receivers {
                                                ^1
   91|      2|        match receiver.await {
   92|      2|            Ok(result) => {
   93|      2|                results.push(result);
   94|      2|            }
   95|      0|            Err(_) => {
   96|      0|                results.push(AgentResult {
   97|      0|                    role,
   98|      0|                    task,
   99|      0|                    success: false,
  100|      0|                    message: Some("Agent channel closed".to_string()),
  101|      0|                    suggested_task_ids: vec![],
  102|      0|                });
  103|      0|            }
  104|       |        }
  105|       |    }
  106|       |
  107|      1|    let success_count = results.iter().filter(|r| r.success).count();
  108|      1|    tracing::info!(
  109|       |        "✅ All {} agents completed: {}/{} successful",
  110|      0|        results.len(),
  111|       |        success_count,
  112|      0|        results.len()
  113|       |    );
  114|       |
  115|      1|    results
  116|      1|}

  118|       |/// Waits for the first agent to complete and returns that result.
  119|      1|pub async fn wait_for_any(
  120|      1|    receivers: Vec<(String, String, oneshot::Receiver<AgentResult>)>,
  121|      1|    spawn_errors: Vec<AgentResult>,
  122|      1|) -> Vec<AgentResult> {
  123|      1|    if receivers.is_empty() {
  124|      0|        return spawn_errors;
  125|      1|    }
  126|       |
  127|      1|    let (tx, mut rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1);
  128|       |
  129|      2|    for (role, task, receiver) in receivers {
                                                ^1
  130|      2|        let tx = tx.clone();
  131|      2|        tokio::spawn(async move {
  132|      2|            match receiver.await {
  133|      1|                Ok(result) => {
  134|      1|                    let _ = tx.send(result).await;
  135|       |                }
  136|       |                Err(_) => {
  137|      0|                    let _ = tx
  138|      0|                        .send(AgentResult {
  139|      0|                            role,
  140|      0|                            task,
  141|      0|                            success: false,
  142|      0|                            message: Some("Agent channel closed".to_string()),
  143|      0|                            suggested_task_ids: vec![],
  144|      0|                        })
  145|      0|                        .await;
  146|       |                }
  147|       |            }
  148|      1|        });
  149|       |    }
  150|      1|    drop(tx); // Drop our copy so channel closes when all senders done
  151|       |
  152|      1|    if let Some(first_result) = rx.recv().await {
  153|      1|        tracing::info!(
  154|       |            "⚡ First agent completed: [{}] success={}",
  155|       |            first_result.role,
  156|       |            first_result.success
  157|       |        );
  158|      1|        let mut results = spawn_errors;
  159|      1|        results.push(first_result);
  160|      1|        results
  161|       |    } else {
  162|      0|        spawn_errors
  163|       |    }
  164|      1|}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/app/context_generator.rs:
    1|       |//! Context generation for implementer agents.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Generates task context including neighboring tasks and dependency summaries.
    4|       |
    5|       |use super::types::truncate_for_log;
    6|       |use super::App;
    7|       |use crate::mcp_server::AgentSpec;
    8|       |use crate::tasks::Task;
    9|       |
   10|       |impl App {
   11|       |    /// Generate context about neighboring tasks and dependency summaries for an implementer.
   12|      0|    pub(crate) async fn generate_task_context(
   13|      0|        &self,
   14|      0|        agents: &[AgentSpec],
   15|      0|        index: usize,
   16|      0|        spec: &AgentSpec,
   17|      0|    ) -> String {
   18|      0|        let mut sections = Vec::new();
   19|       |
   20|       |        // Acquire TaskManager lock once for all lookups
   21|      0|        let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
   22|       |
   23|       |        // 1. Dependency summaries from TaskManager (if task_id is provided)
   24|      0|        if let Some(ref task_id) = spec.task_id {
   25|      0|            if let Some(dep_summary) = tm.format_dependency_summaries(task_id) {
   26|      0|                sections.push(dep_summary);
   27|      0|            }
   28|      0|        }
   29|       |
   30|       |        // 2. Neighboring tasks context
   31|      0|        if agents.len() > 1 {
   32|      0|            let mut neighbor_lines = Vec::new();
   33|       |
   34|       |            // Helper to format task label from spec, looking up name from TaskManager if needed
   35|      0|            let format_task_label = |spec: &AgentSpec, task: Option<&Task>| -> String {
   36|      0|                match (&spec.task_id, &spec.task, task) {
   37|       |                    // If we have a task_id and found the task, show "[task001] Name"
   38|      0|                    (Some(id), _, Some(t)) => format!("[{id}] {}", t.name),
   39|       |                    // If we have a task_id but no task found, and no task text, just show the id
   40|      0|                    (Some(id), None, None) => format!("[{id}]"),
   41|       |                    // If we have a task_id but no task found, show id with fallback text
   42|      0|                    (Some(id), Some(task_text), None) => format!("[{id}] {task_text}"),
   43|       |                    // If we only have task text (no task_id), show it directly
   44|      0|                    (None, Some(task_text), _) => task_text.clone(),
   45|       |                    // No information available
   46|      0|                    (None, None, _) => "(unknown task)".to_string(),
   47|       |                }
   48|      0|            };
   49|       |
   50|       |            // Previous tasks (up to 2)
   51|      0|            let prev_start = index.saturating_sub(2);

   63|       |                    ));
   64|       |                }
   65|      0|            }

    1|       |//! Decompose task handling - creates child orchestrators for subtasks.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! When a task is too complex for a single agent, the orchestrator can decompose it
    4|       |//! into subtasks using a nested planner-orchestrator pair. This module handles:
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! 1. Snapshot/restore of parent task state to prevent ID conflicts
    7|       |//! 2. Creation of child scope (subdirectory) for logging isolation
    8|       |//! 3. Running nested planner to create subtask plan
    9|       |//! 4. Running nested orchestrator to execute subtasks
   10|       |//! 5. Finalizing child tasks before restoring parent state
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! # Child Task Finalization
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! When the nested orchestrator completes, any child tasks still in `NotStarted`
   15|       |//! status are automatically marked as `Skipped` with a reason indicating they
   16|       |//! were not addressed by the nested orchestrator. This ensures:
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! - All child tasks have a definitive final status
   19|       |//! - `TaskStateChanged` events are emitted before tasks are removed
   20|       |//! - The TUI can display accurate completion status
   21|       |
   22|       |use super::types::{format_duration_human, truncate_for_log};
   23|       |use super::App;
   24|       |use crate::mcp_server::{TaskStateInfo, ToolResponse};
   25|       |use crate::tasks::{TaskManager, TaskManagerSnapshot, TaskStatus};
   26|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Finalize all child tasks by marking `NotStarted` tasks as `Skipped`.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Called before restoring the parent's task snapshot to ensure all child tasks
   31|       |/// have a terminal status. This emits `TaskStateChanged` events that update the
   32|       |/// TUI before the child tasks are removed from the `TaskManager`.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Arguments
   35|       |/// * `tm` - The task manager containing child tasks
   36|       |/// * `reason` - The reason to record for skipped tasks
   37|       |///
   38|       |/// # Returns
   39|       |/// The number of tasks that were finalized (marked as Skipped).
   40|      4|fn finalize_child_tasks(tm: &mut TaskManager, reason: &str) -> usize {
   41|       |    // Collect IDs of tasks that need to be skipped (to avoid borrow issues)
   42|      4|    let not_started_ids: Vec<String> = tm
   43|      4|        .all_tasks()
   44|      4|        .iter()
   45|      8|        .filter(|task| matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::NotStarted))
                       ^4
   46|      4|        .map(|task| task.id.clone())
                                  ^0      ^0
   47|      4|        .collect();
   48|       |
   49|      4|    let count = not_started_ids.len();
   50|      4|    for task_id in not_started_ids {
                      ^0
   51|      0|        tm.update_status(
   52|      0|            &task_id,
   53|      0|            &TaskStatus::Skipped {
   54|      0|                reason: reason.to_string(),
   55|      0|            },
   56|      0|        );
   57|      0|    }
   58|      4|    count
   59|      4|}

   76|       |            }
   77|      0|            Err(e) => ToolResponse::failure(request_id.to_string(), e.to_string())
   78|      0|                .with_task_state(task_state),
   79|       |        }
   80|      4|    }

   92|      4|            blocked_tasks: blocked,
   93|      4|        }

    1|       |//! Main orchestrator application module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module contains the core [`App`] struct and orchestration logic, split into
    4|       |//! focused sub-modules for maintainability:
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Module Structure
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! ## Core Types & Configuration
    9|       |//! - [`types`]: Constants, type aliases, and utility functions
   10|       |//! - [`router`]: Session routing for ACP messages to per-session channels
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! ## Agent Lifecycle
   13|       |//! - [`agent_spawner`]: Generic agent spawning with concurrent execution support
   14|       |//! - [`agent_handler`]: Agent tool call handling and completion processing
   15|       |//! - [`context_generator`]: Task context generation for implementer agents
   16|       |//!
   17|       |//! ## Agent Types
   18|       |//! - [`planner`]: Planner agent spawning for task decomposition
   19|       |//! - [`orchestrator`]: Orchestrator agent spawning and execution
   20|       |//! - [`orchestrator_acp`]: ACP message handling for orchestrator
   21|       |//! - [`decompose`]: Decomposition logic for subtasks
   22|       |//!
   23|       |//! ## Communication
   24|       |//! - [`socket`]: IPC socket setup and MCP connection handling
   25|       |//! - [`session`]: Session waiting and output collection
   26|       |//! - [`session_drain`]: Message draining after session completion
   27|       |//!
   28|       |//! ## Entry Point
   29|       |//! - [`run`]: Main execution entry point for the orchestrator
   30|       |

   66|       |/// The main application struct that orchestrates multi-agent workflows.
   67|       |pub struct App {
   68|       |    /// The backend provider for agent communication (Auggie, Cursor, etc.)
   69|       |    pub(crate) backend: Box<dyn Backend>,
   70|       |    /// Transport kind used for all agents (stored for creating new transports)
   71|       |    pub(crate) transport_kind: TransportKind,
   72|       |    /// Transport for the orchestrator agent (coordinates task execution)
   73|       |    pub(crate) acp_orchestrator: Box<dyn AgentTransport>,
   74|       |    /// Transport for the planner agent (decomposes tasks into subtasks)
   75|       |    pub(crate) acp_planner: Box<dyn AgentTransport>,
   76|       |    /// Transport for worker agents (implements tasks)

   78|       |    pub(crate) socket_address: Option<IpcAddress>,

   81|       |    pub(crate) model_config: ModelConfig,

   88|       |    /// Handle to the message routing task for cleanup

   90|       |    /// Whether the message router is active (controls session message routing behavior)
   91|       |    pub(crate) router_active: bool,

   93|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]

  322|       |    /// Set up IPC socket for MCP server communication
  323|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  324|      0|    async fn setup_socket(&mut self) -> Result<IpcAddress> {
  325|      0|        let (socket_address, tool_rx, listener_task) = socket::setup_socket().await?;
  326|       |
  327|      0|        self.socket_address = Some(socket_address.clone());

  337|       |    /// Start the background message routing tasks for agent sessions.
  338|       |    ///
  339|       |    /// This starts router tasks that continuously read from ACP clients' notification channels
  340|       |    /// and route messages to per-session channels via the `session_router`.
  341|     43|    fn start_worker_router(&mut self) {
  342|     43|        let mut any_router_started = false;
  343|       |
  344|       |        // Start router for worker client
  345|     43|        if let Some(notification_rx) = self.acp_worker.take_notification_rx() {
                                  ^0
  346|      0|            let session_router = Arc::clone(&self.session_router);
  347|      0|            let router_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
  348|      0|                let mut rx = notification_rx;
  349|      0|                while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {
  350|      0|                    let routed = {
  351|      0|                        let router = session_router.read().await;
  352|      0|                        router.route(msg.clone())
  353|       |                    };
  354|      0|                    if !routed {
  355|      0|                        if let Some(session_id) = router::SessionRouter::extract_session_id(&msg) {
  356|      0|                            tracing::trace!(
  357|       |                                "Message for unregistered session {}: {:?}",
  358|       |                                session_id,
  359|      0|                                msg.get("method")
  360|       |                            );
  361|      0|                        }
  362|      0|                    }
  363|       |                }
  364|      0|                tracing::debug!("Worker notification channel closed, router task exiting");
  365|      0|            });
  366|      0|            self.router_task = Some(router_task);
  367|      0|            any_router_started = true;
  368|      0|            tracing::debug!("Started worker message router task");
  369|       |        } else {
  370|       |            // This is expected when using CLI transport - the CLI handles messages differently
  371|     43|            tracing::debug!("Worker notification receiver already taken, skipping router setup");
  372|       |        }
  373|       |
  374|       |        // Start router for planner client
  375|     43|        if let Some(notification_rx) = self.acp_planner.take_notification_rx() {
                                  ^0
  376|      0|            let session_router = Arc::clone(&self.session_router);
  377|      0|            tokio::spawn(async move {
  378|      0|                let mut rx = notification_rx;
  379|      0|                while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {
  380|      0|                    let routed = {
  381|      0|                        let router = session_router.read().await;
  382|      0|                        router.route(msg.clone())
  383|       |                    };
  384|      0|                    if !routed {
  385|      0|                        if let Some(session_id) = router::SessionRouter::extract_session_id(&msg) {
  386|      0|                            tracing::trace!(
  387|       |                                "Planner message for unregistered session {}: {:?}",
  388|       |                                session_id,
  389|      0|                                msg.get("method")
  390|       |                            );
  391|      0|                        }
  392|      0|                    }
  393|       |                }
  394|      0|                tracing::debug!("Planner notification channel closed, router task exiting");
  395|      0|            });
  396|      0|            any_router_started = true;
  397|      0|            tracing::debug!("Started planner message router task");
  398|       |        } else {
  399|       |            // This is expected when using CLI transport - the CLI handles messages differently
  400|     43|            tracing::debug!(
  401|       |                "Planner notification receiver already taken, skipping planner router setup"
  402|       |            );
  403|       |        }
  404|       |
  405|       |        // Only mark router as active if at least one router was started
  406|       |        // Mock clients don't have notification receivers, so tests use direct mode
  407|     43|        self.router_active = any_router_started;
  408|     43|    }

  438|       |    /// Applies a model configuration update from the TUI.
  439|       |    ///
  440|       |    /// This method applies partial updates to the model configuration.
  441|       |    /// Only fields that are `Some` in the update will be changed.
  442|       |    /// Changes take effect for newly spawned agents.
  443|       |    /// The changes are also persisted to TOML config files.
  444|       |    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  445|      0|    pub fn apply_model_config_update(&mut self, update: &crate::tui::ModelConfigUpdate) {
  446|       |        use crate::config::save_agent_config;
  447|       |        use crate::models::ModelFallbackChain;
  448|       |
  449|      0|        if let Some(tier) = update.orchestrator_model {
  450|      0|            tracing::info!("📝 Model config updated: orchestrator -> {}", tier);
  451|      0|            self.model_config.orchestrator_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  452|      0|            if let Err(e) = save_agent_config("orchestrator", tier) {
  453|      0|                tracing::warn!("Failed to persist orchestrator config: {}", e);
  454|      0|            }
  455|      0|        }
  456|      0|        if let Some(tier) = update.planner_model {
  457|      0|            tracing::info!("📝 Model config updated: planner -> {}", tier);
  458|      0|            self.model_config.planner_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  459|      0|            if let Err(e) = save_agent_config("planner", tier) {
  460|      0|                tracing::warn!("Failed to persist planner config: {}", e);
  461|      0|            }
  462|      0|        }
  463|      0|        if let Some(tier) = update.implementer_model {
  464|      0|            tracing::info!("📝 Model config updated: implementer -> {}", tier);
  465|      0|            self.model_config.implementer_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  466|      0|            if let Err(e) = save_agent_config("implementer", tier) {
  467|      0|                tracing::warn!("Failed to persist implementer config: {}", e);
  468|      0|            }
  469|      0|        }
  470|      0|    }

  472|       |    /// Polls for and applies any pending model configuration updates from the TUI.
  473|       |    ///
  474|       |    /// This method is non-blocking - it returns immediately if no updates are available.
  475|       |    /// It should be called periodically in the main event loop.
  476|       |    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  477|       |    #[allow(dead_code)] // Public API for alternative polling patterns
  478|      0|    pub fn poll_config_updates(&mut self) {
  479|       |        // Collect all pending updates first to avoid borrow issues
  480|      0|        let updates: Vec<_> = if let Some(ref mut rx) = self.config_update_rx {
  481|      0|            let mut collected = Vec::new();
  482|      0|            while let Ok(update) = rx.try_recv() {
  483|      0|                collected.push(update);
  484|      0|            }
  485|      0|            collected
  486|       |        } else {
  487|      0|            return;
  488|       |        };
  489|       |
  490|       |        // Now apply all collected updates
  491|      0|        for update in &updates {
  492|      0|            self.apply_model_config_update(update);
  493|      0|        }
  494|      0|    }

  503|       |
  504|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the application and all child processes.
  505|       |    ///
  506|       |    /// This should be called before the App is dropped to ensure clean termination
  507|       |    /// of all agent processes and background tasks.
  508|     39|    pub async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  509|     39|        tracing::info!("🛑 Shutting down application...");
  510|       |
  511|       |        // Clean up socket if it exists
  512|     39|        if let Some(socket_address) = self.socket_address.take() {
                                  ^0
  513|      0|            self.cleanup_socket(&socket_address);
  514|     39|        }
  515|       |
  516|       |        // Abort the router task if running
  517|     39|        if let Some(router_task) = self.router_task.take() {
                                  ^0
  518|      0|            router_task.abort();
  519|      0|            tracing::debug!("Aborted message router task");
  520|     39|        }
  521|       |
  522|       |        // Shutdown all ACP clients
  523|       |        // Run them concurrently since they're independent
  524|     39|        let (orchestrator_result, planner_result, worker_result) = tokio::join!(
  525|     39|            self.acp_orchestrator.shutdown(),
  526|     39|            self.acp_planner.shutdown(),
  527|     39|            self.acp_worker.shutdown()
  528|       |        );
  529|       |
  530|     39|        if let Err(e) = orchestrator_result {
                                 ^0
  531|      0|            tracing::warn!("Error shutting down orchestrator ACP client: {}", e);
  532|     39|        }
  533|     39|        if let Err(e) = planner_result {
                                 ^0
  534|      0|            tracing::warn!("Error shutting down planner ACP client: {}", e);
  535|     39|        }
  536|     39|        if let Err(e) = worker_result {
                                 ^0
  537|      0|            tracing::warn!("Error shutting down worker ACP client: {}", e);
  538|     39|        }
  539|       |
  540|     39|        tracing::info!("✅ Shutdown complete");
  541|     39|        Ok(())
  542|     39|    }

  558|       |    #[allow(clippy::items_after_statements)] // use statement close to usage

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/app/orchestrator.rs:
    1|       |//! Orchestrator agent handling.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! The orchestrator is responsible for coordinating task execution by delegating
    4|       |//! work to specialized agents. It has access to the following MCP tools:
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! - **`decompose`**: Break down a task into smaller subtasks with a nested orchestrator
    7|       |//! - **`spawn_agents`**: Spawn one or more worker agents to execute tasks
    8|       |//! - **`complete`**: Signal that orchestration is finished
    9|       |//! - **`create_task`**: Dynamically add new tasks to the plan
   10|       |//! - **`skip_tasks`**: Mark tasks as skipped (not needed)
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! # Task Reconciliation
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! When the orchestrator calls `complete(success=true)`, the system performs
   15|       |//! reconciliation to ensure all tasks have a definitive final status:
   16|       |//!
   17|       |//! 1. Any tasks still in `NotStarted` status are flagged as pending
   18|       |//! 2. If pending tasks exist, completion is rejected with a message asking the
   19|       |//!    orchestrator to either spawn agents or skip the remaining tasks
   20|       |//! 3. Only after all tasks are in a terminal state (Complete, Failed, or Skipped)
   21|       |//!    will the completion be accepted
   22|       |//!
   23|       |//! This prevents the orchestrator from finishing with unaddressed tasks, ensuring
   24|       |//! an accurate audit trail in the task list.
   25|       |
   26|       |use super::retry::{is_transient_error, RetryConfig};
   27|       |use super::socket::{setup_agent_socket, AgentSocketHandle};
   28|       |use super::types::{truncate_for_log, ToolMessage, ORCHESTRATOR_PROMPT};
   29|       |use super::App;
   30|       |use crate::acp::SessionMode;
   31|       |use crate::backend::transport::{SessionConfig, SessionInfo, TransportKind};
   32|       |use crate::logging::AgentWriter;
   33|       |use crate::mcp_server::{AgentSpec, TaskStateInfo, ToolCall, ToolResponse};
   34|       |use crate::tasks::TaskStatus;
   35|       |use crate::types::TaskResult;
   36|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   37|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   38|       |use std::future::Future;
   39|       |use std::pin::Pin;
   40|       |use std::time::Duration;
   41|       |
   42|       |/// Result of spawning an orchestrator agent.
   43|       |pub struct OrchestratorSession {
   44|       |    /// The session ID for the orchestrator session
   45|       |    pub session_id: String,
   46|       |    /// The model used for this session
   47|       |    pub model: String,
   48|       |    /// The full prompt sent to the orchestrator
   49|       |    pub full_prompt: String,
   50|       |    /// Socket handle for CLI transport (must be kept alive during the session).
   51|       |    /// This field is intentionally not read directly - its presence keeps the socket listener
   52|       |    /// alive until the OrchestratorSession is dropped.
   53|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   54|       |    socket_handle: Option<AgentSocketHandle>,
   55|       |    /// Tool receiver extracted from the socket handle (for passing to the orchestrator loop)
   56|       |    tool_rx: Option<super::types::ToolReceiver>,
   57|       |}
   58|       |

   60|       |    /// Take the tool receiver for use in the orchestrator loop.
   61|       |    /// Returns None if there's no CLI socket handle (e.g., ACP transport).
   62|     46|    pub fn take_tool_rx(&mut self) -> Option<super::types::ToolReceiver> {
   63|     46|        self.tool_rx.take()
   64|     46|    }

  166|       |    /// Create an orchestrator session with model fallback chain and retry logic.
  167|       |    ///
  168|       |    /// This tries each model in the chain, and for each model:
  169|       |    /// - Handles transient MCP server startup errors with exponential backoff
  170|       |    /// - Falls back to the next model if "model not available" error occurs
  171|       |    ///
  172|       |    /// Returns (`SessionInfo`, `actual_model`) so the model can be recorded in logs.
  173|     46|    async fn create_orchestrator_session_with_retry(
  174|     46|        &mut self,
  175|     46|        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  176|     46|        cwd: &str,
  177|     46|    ) -> Result<(SessionInfo, String)> {
  178|       |        use super::retry::is_model_not_available_error;
  179|       |
  180|     46|        let retry_config = RetryConfig::from_env();
  181|     46|        let model_chain = self.resolve_orchestrator_model()?;
                                                                         ^0
  182|       |
  183|       |        // Track the last error for reporting if all models fail
  184|     46|        let mut last_error: Option<anyhow::Error> = None;
  185|       |
  186|     46|        for model in &model_chain {
  187|     46|            let mut attempt = 0;
  188|     46|            let mut delay = retry_config.initial_delay;
  189|       |
  190|       |            loop {
  191|     46|                attempt += 1;
  192|       |
  193|       |                // Create session config with Agent mode - orchestrator needs to call MCP tools
  194|       |                // Note: Cursor's "plan" mode is read-only and can't call tools
  195|     46|                let config = SessionConfig::new(model, cwd)
  196|     46|                    .with_mcp_servers(mcp_servers.clone())
  197|     46|                    .with_mode(SessionMode::Agent);
  198|       |
  199|     46|                match self.acp_orchestrator.create_session(config).await {
  200|     46|                    Ok(response) => {
  201|     46|                        if attempt > 1 {
  202|      0|                            tracing::info!(
  203|       |                                "🔄 Orchestrator create_session succeeded on attempt {}/{} with model {}",
  204|       |                                attempt,
  205|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  206|       |                                model
  207|       |                            );
  208|     46|                        }
  209|     46|                        return Ok((response, model.clone()));
  210|       |                    }
  211|      0|                    Err(e) => {
  212|       |                        // Check if model is not available - try next model
  213|      0|                        if is_model_not_available_error(&e) {
  214|      0|                            tracing::debug!(
  215|       |                                "Model '{}' not available for orchestrator, trying next in chain...",
  216|       |                                model
  217|       |                            );
  218|      0|                            last_error = Some(e);
  219|      0|                            break; // Move to next model in chain
  220|      0|                        }
  221|       |
  222|      0|                        let is_transient = is_transient_error(&e);
  223|      0|                        let can_retry = attempt <= retry_config.max_retries && is_transient;
  224|       |
  225|      0|                        if can_retry {
  226|      0|                            tracing::warn!(
  227|       |                                "⚠️ Orchestrator create_session failed (attempt {}/{}, model {}): {}. Retrying in {:?}...",
  228|       |                                attempt,
  229|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  230|       |                                model,
  231|       |                                e,
  232|       |                                delay
  233|       |                            );
  234|      0|                            tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
  235|       |
  236|       |                            // Exponential backoff with cap
  237|      0|                            delay = Duration::from_secs_f64(
  238|      0|                                (delay.as_secs_f64() * retry_config.backoff_multiplier)
  239|      0|                                    .min(retry_config.max_delay.as_secs_f64()),
  240|      0|                            );
  241|       |                        } else {
  242|       |                            // Non-transient, non-model error - fail immediately
  243|      0|                            let reason = if is_transient {
  244|      0|                                "exhausted retries"
  245|       |                            } else {
  246|      0|                                "non-transient error"
  247|       |                            };
  248|      0|                            tracing::error!(
  249|       |                                "❌ Orchestrator create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s) ({reason}): {e:#}",
  250|       |                            );
  251|      0|                            return Err(e).context(format!(
  252|       |                                "Orchestrator create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s)"
  253|       |                            ));
  254|       |                        }
  255|       |                    }
  256|       |                }
  257|       |            }
  258|       |        }
  259|       |
  260|       |        // All models in the chain failed
  261|      0|        Err(last_error
  262|      0|            .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No models in fallback chain"))
  263|      0|            .context(format!(
  264|       |                "All models in fallback chain failed for orchestrator: {:?}",
  265|       |                model_chain
  266|       |            )))
  267|     46|    }

  269|       |    /// Spawn and run an orchestrator agent with logging.
  270|     46|    pub(crate) fn run_orchestrator_with_writer<'a>(
  271|     46|        &'a mut self,
  272|     46|        prompt: &'a str,
  273|     46|        writer: &'a mut AgentWriter,
  274|     46|    ) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Result<TaskResult>> + 'a>> {

                                                                                                         ^43
