  277|       |
  278|       |    #[allow(clippy::ignored_unit_patterns)] // `update` pattern is () when tui feature disabled
  279|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, prompt, writer), fields(agent_type = "orchestrator"))]
  280|     46|    async fn run_orchestrator_with_writer_impl(
  281|     46|        &mut self,
  282|     46|        prompt: &str,
  283|     46|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  284|     46|    ) -> Result<TaskResult> {
  285|       |        let mut orchestrator_session = match self.spawn_orchestrator(prompt).await {
  286|       |            Ok(result) => result,
  287|       |            Err(e) => {
  288|       |                // Write error to orchestrator log so it's not empty
  289|       |                let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  290|       |                tracing::error!("[L{}] ❌ Failed to spawn orchestrator: {:#}", depth, e);
  291|       |                if let Err(write_err) = writer.write_spawn_error(&e).await {
  292|       |                    tracing::warn!(
  293|       |                        "Failed to write spawn error to orchestrator log: {}",
  294|       |                        write_err
  295|       |                    );
  296|       |                }
  297|       |                // Note: finalize is called by the caller (run_execution_phase)
  298|       |                return Err(e);
  299|       |            }
  300|       |        };
  301|       |
  302|       |        let session_id = orchestrator_session.session_id.clone();
  303|       |        let full_prompt = orchestrator_session.full_prompt.clone();
  304|       |
  305|       |        writer.set_session_id(session_id.clone());
  306|       |        writer.set_model(orchestrator_session.model.clone());
  307|       |        // Use the plan as the task description, but log the full prompt for debugging
  308|       |        if let Err(e) = writer.write_header_with_prompt(prompt, &full_prompt).await {
  309|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to write orchestrator header: {}", e);
  310|       |        }
  311|       |        // Emit AgentStarted event for TUI
  312|       |        writer.emit_agent_started(prompt);
  313|       |
  314|       |        // Use the CLI-specific tool_rx if available, otherwise use self.tool_rx
  315|       |        // For CLI transport, the orchestrator has its own socket with its own tool_rx
  316|       |        // Track whether we used CLI's tool_rx so we know whether to restore self.tool_rx later
  317|       |        let (mut tool_rx, _used_cli_tool_rx) = match orchestrator_session.take_tool_rx() {
  318|       |            Some(rx) => (rx, true),
  319|       |            None => (
  320|       |                self.tool_rx.take().context("Tool receiver not set up")?,
  321|       |                false,
  322|       |            ),
  323|       |        };
  324|       |
  325|       |        // Keep the orchestrator session alive until the loop completes.
  326|       |        // This is critical for CLI transport - the socket_handle inside orchestrator_session
  327|       |        // contains the listener task that processes MCP connections. If it's dropped,
  328|       |        // the listener is aborted and tool calls won't be received.
  329|       |        let _keep_session_alive = orchestrator_session;
  330|       |
  331|       |        // Take the config update receiver out of self so we don't hold a mutable borrow
  332|       |        #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  333|       |        let mut config_update_rx = self.config_update_rx.take();
  334|       |
  335|       |        // Handle tool calls from MCP server
  336|       |        tracing::info!(
  337|       |            "🎭 Orchestrator loop started for session {}, waiting for tool calls and ACP messages",
  338|       |            session_id
  339|       |        );
  340|       |        let result = loop {
  341|       |            // Config update future - receives from TUI channel when available
  342|       |            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  343|    329|            let config_update_fut = async {
  344|    329|                match config_update_rx.as_mut() {
  345|      0|                    Some(rx) => rx.recv().await,
  346|    329|                    None => std::future::pending().await,
  347|       |                }
  348|      0|            };
  349|       |            #[cfg(not(feature = "tui"))]
  350|       |            let config_update_fut = std::future::pending::<Option<()>>();
  351|       |
  352|       |            tokio::select! {
  353|       |                // Use biased selection to ensure tool_rx is checked first.
  354|       |                // This is critical for tests: when mock ACP recv() injects a tool call,
  355|       |                // we must process it before polling recv() again, or tool calls may
  356|       |                // be interleaved (e.g., orchestrator's complete received by implementer).
  357|       |                biased;
  358|       |
  359|       |                // Handle config updates from TUI (update is only used with tui feature)
  360|       |                Some(update) = config_update_fut => {
  361|       |                    let _ = &update; // Suppress unused variable warning when tui feature is disabled
  362|       |                    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  363|       |                    self.apply_model_config_update(&update);
  364|       |                }
  365|       |
  366|       |                Some(tool_msg) = tool_rx.recv() => {
  367|       |                    let ToolMessage::Request { request, response_tx } = tool_msg;
  368|       |                    tracing::info!(
  369|       |                        "📨 Orchestrator MCP tool call received: {:?}, request_id={}",
  370|       |                        request.tool_call.tool_type(),
  371|       |                        request.request_id
  372|       |                    );
  373|       |
  374|       |                    match &request.tool_call {
  375|       |                        ToolCall::Decompose { task_id, task } => {
  376|       |                            let resolved_task =
  377|       |                                self.resolve_task_description(task_id.as_ref(), task.as_ref()).await;
  378|       |
  379|       |                            let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  380|       |                            let _ = writer
  381|       |                                .write_mcp_tool_call(
  382|       |                                    "decompose",
  383|       |                                    &truncate_for_log(&resolved_task, 100),
  384|       |                                )
  385|       |                                .await;
  386|       |                            tracing::info!(
  387|       |                                "[L{}] 🔄 decompose({})",
  388|       |                                depth,
  389|       |                                truncate_for_log(&resolved_task, 80)
  390|       |                            );
  391|       |
  392|       |                            tracing::debug!(
  393|       |                                "[L{}] Entering nested decompose handler",
  394|       |                                depth
  395|       |                            );
  396|       |                            self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  397|       |                            let response = self
  398|       |                                .handle_decompose_with_response(&resolved_task, &request.request_id)
  399|       |                                .await;
  400|       |                            tracing::debug!(
  401|       |                                "[L{}] Nested decompose handler finished: success={}",
  402|       |                                depth,
  403|       |                                response.success
  404|       |                            );
  405|       |                            tool_rx = self
  406|       |                                .tool_rx
  407|       |                                .take()
  408|       |                                .context("Tool receiver lost during decompose")?;
  409|       |
  410|       |                            // Use error message for failed responses, summary for success
  411|       |                            let log_message = if response.success {
  412|       |                                &response.summary
  413|       |                            } else {
  414|       |                                response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error")
  415|       |                            };
  416|       |                            let _ = writer
  417|       |                                .write_mcp_tool_result(
  418|       |                                    "decompose",
  419|       |                                    response.success,
  420|       |                                    &truncate_for_log(log_message, 100),
  421|       |                                )
  422|       |                                .await;
  423|       |
  424|       |                            tracing::debug!("[L{}] Decompose complete, continuing loop", depth);
  425|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  426|       |                        }
  427|       |                        ToolCall::SpawnAgents { ref agents, ref wait } => {
  428|       |                            // Log the MCP tool call to orchestrator log
  429|       |                            let agent_count = agents.len();
  430|       |                            let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  431|       |                            let desc = format!("{agent_count} agent(s), wait={wait:?}");
  432|       |                            let _ = writer.write_mcp_tool_call("spawn_agents", &desc).await;
  433|       |                            tracing::info!("[L{}] 🚀 spawn_agents({agent_count} agents, wait={wait:?})", depth);
  434|       |
  435|       |                            // Log each agent being spawned
  436|       |                            for (i, agent) in agents.iter().enumerate() {
  437|       |                                let role = agent.role.as_deref().unwrap_or("implementer");
  438|       |                                let task_desc = agent
  439|       |                                    .task
  440|       |                                    .as_deref()
  441|       |                                    .or(agent.task_id.as_deref())
  442|       |                                    .unwrap_or("(no task)");
  443|       |                                tracing::info!(
  444|       |                                    "[L{}]   📋 Agent {}/{}: [{}] {}",
  445|       |                                    depth,
  446|       |                                    i + 1,
  447|       |                                    agent_count,
  448|       |                                    role,
  449|       |                                    truncate_for_log(task_desc, 60)
  450|       |                                );
  451|       |                            }
  452|       |
  453|       |                            // Validate agent specifications before spawning
  454|       |                            if let Some(error_msg) = Self::validate_agent_specs(agents) {
  455|       |                                let response =
  456|       |                                    ToolResponse::failure(request.request_id.clone(), error_msg);
  457|       |                                let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  458|       |                                continue;
  459|       |                            }
  460|       |
  461|       |                            // Choose execution mode based on router_active:
  462|       |                            // - Concurrent mode (router_active=true): Each agent gets its own socket
  463|       |                            //   and tool handler, enabling true parallel execution
  464|       |                            // - Sequential mode (router_active=false): Fallback for mock tests where
  465|       |                            //   agents share the orchestrator's tool_rx channel
  466|       |                            let (all_success, combined_summary) = if self.router_active {
  467|       |                                // Concurrent mode: use per-agent sockets
  468|       |                                tracing::info!(
  469|       |                                    "🚀 Running {} agents in CONCURRENT mode",
  470|       |                                    agents.len()
  471|       |                                );
  472|       |
  473|       |                                let results =
  474|       |                                    self.spawn_agents_concurrent(agents.clone(), *wait).await;
  475|       |                                self.process_concurrent_results(&results).await
  476|       |                            } else {
  477|       |                                // Sequential mode: spawn agents one at a time
  478|       |                                // This uses handle_implement_with_response which works with mock clients
  479|       |                                tracing::debug!(
  480|       |                                    "Running {} agents in SEQUENTIAL mode (mock/test)",
  481|       |                                    agents.len()
  482|       |                                );
  483|       |
  484|       |                                // Snapshot task IDs before spawning to detect new tasks
  485|       |                                let task_ids_before: std::collections::HashSet<String> = {
  486|       |                                    let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  487|       |                                    tm.list_task_ids().into_iter().collect()
  488|       |                                };
  489|       |
  490|       |                                let mut summaries = Vec::new();
  491|       |
  492|       |                                for agent in agents {
  493|       |                                    let role =
  494|       |                                        agent.role.as_deref().unwrap_or("implementer").to_string();
  495|       |
  496|       |                                    // Resolve task_id (with inference like concurrent mode)
  497|       |                                    let resolved_task_id = self.resolve_task_id_for_agent(agent).await;
  498|       |
  499|       |                                    let task = self
  500|       |                                        .resolve_task_description(agent.task_id.as_ref(), agent.task.as_ref())
  501|       |                                        .await;
  502|       |
  503|       |                                    // Mark task as InProgress before spawning (if we have a task_id)
  504|       |                                    if let Some(ref tid) = resolved_task_id {
  505|       |                                        let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  506|       |                                        tm.update_status(
  507|       |                                            tid,
  508|       |                                            &TaskStatus::InProgress {
  509|       |                                                agent_session: None,
  510|       |                                            },
  511|       |                                        );
  512|       |                                        tracing::info!(
  513|       |                                            "📋 Task {} marked as InProgress (sequential mode)",
  514|       |                                            tid
  515|       |                                        );
  516|       |                                    }
  517|       |
  518|       |                                    // Restore tool_rx before spawning (needs to receive complete signal)
  519|       |                                    self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  520|       |                                    let impl_response = self
  521|       |                                        .handle_implement_with_response(&task, &request.request_id)
  522|       |                                        .await;
  523|       |                                    tool_rx = self
  524|       |                                        .tool_rx
  525|       |                                        .take()
  526|       |                                        .context("Tool receiver lost during spawn_agents")?;
  527|       |
  528|       |                                    // Update task status based on result (if we have a task_id)
  529|       |                                    if let Some(ref tid) = resolved_task_id {
  530|       |                                        let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  531|       |                                        let status = if impl_response.success {
  532|       |                                            tracing::info!("📋 Task {} marked as Complete", tid);
  533|       |                                            TaskStatus::Complete {
  534|       |                                                success: true,
  535|       |                                                summary: impl_response.summary.clone(),
  536|       |                                            }
  537|       |                                        } else {
  538|       |                                            tracing::info!("📋 Task {} marked as Failed", tid);
  539|       |                                            TaskStatus::Failed {
  540|       |                                                error: impl_response.summary.clone(),
  541|       |                                            }
  542|       |                                        };
  543|       |                                        tm.update_status(tid, &status);
  544|       |                                    }
  545|       |
  546|       |                                    let status = if impl_response.success { "✓" } else { "✗" };
  547|       |                                    summaries.push(format!(
  548|       |                                        "[{}] {} {}",
  549|       |                                        role,
  550|       |                                        status,
  551|       |                                        truncate_for_log(&impl_response.summary, 80)
  552|       |                                    ));
  553|       |                                }
  554|       |
  555|       |                                self.process_sequential_results(summaries, &task_ids_before)
  556|       |                                    .await
  557|       |                            };
  558|       |
  559|       |                            // Fetch task state for context-aware response
  560|       |                            let task_state = self.get_task_state().await;
  561|       |
  562|       |                            let response = if all_success {
  563|       |                                ToolResponse::success(request.request_id.clone(), combined_summary)
  564|       |                                    .with_task_state(task_state)
  565|       |                            } else {
  566|       |                                ToolResponse::failure(request.request_id.clone(), combined_summary)
  567|       |                                    .with_task_state(task_state)
  568|       |                            };
  569|       |
  570|       |                            // Log the result to orchestrator log
  571|       |                            // Use error message for failed responses, summary for success
  572|       |                            let log_message = if response.success {
  573|       |                                &response.summary
  574|       |                            } else {
  575|       |                                response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error")
  576|       |                            };
  577|       |                            let _ = writer.write_mcp_tool_result(
  578|       |                                "spawn_agents",
  579|       |                                response.success,
  580|       |                                &truncate_for_log(log_message, 100)
  581|       |                            ).await;
  582|       |
  583|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  584|       |                        }
  585|       |                        ToolCall::Complete { success, message, .. } => {
  586|       |                            let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  587|       |                            let success_str =
  588|       |                                if *success { "success=true" } else { "success=false" };
  589|       |                            let msg_preview = message
  590|       |                                .as_deref()
  591|     45|                                .map(|m| truncate_for_log(m, 50))
  592|       |                                .unwrap_or_default();
  593|       |                            let _ = writer
  594|       |                                .write_mcp_tool_call(
  595|       |                                    "complete",
  596|       |                                    &format!("{success_str}, {msg_preview}"),
  597|       |                                )
  598|       |                                .await;
  599|       |                            tracing::info!("[L{}] 🏁 complete({success_str})", depth);
  600|       |
  601|       |                            // Task reconciliation: check for pending tasks before allowing success
  602|       |                            if *success {
  603|       |                                if let Some(pending_msg) = self.check_completion_blockers().await {
  604|       |                                    let response = ToolResponse::success(
  605|       |                                        request.request_id.clone(),
  606|       |                                        pending_msg,
  607|       |                                    );
  608|       |                                    let _ = writer
  609|       |                                        .write_mcp_tool_result("complete", false, "Rejected: pending tasks remain")
  610|       |                                        .await;
  611|       |                                    let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  612|       |                                    continue;
  613|       |                                }
  614|       |                            }
  615|       |
  616|       |                            // No pending tasks or success=false - proceed with completion
  617|       |                            if let Some(msg) = &message {
  618|       |                                let _ = writer.write_result(msg).await;
  619|       |                            }
  620|       |
  621|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  622|       |                                request.request_id.clone(),
  623|       |                                message
  624|       |                                    .clone()
  625|      0|                                    .unwrap_or_else(|| "Orchestration complete".to_string()),
  626|       |                            );
  627|       |                            let _ = writer
  628|       |                                .write_mcp_tool_result(
  629|       |                                    "complete",
  630|       |                                    *success,
  631|       |                                    if *success {
  632|       |                                        "Accepted"
  633|       |                                    } else {
  634|       |                                        "Task marked as failed"
  635|       |                                    },
  636|       |                                )
  637|       |                                .await;
  638|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  639|       |
  640|       |                            // Brief drain for any final messages (500ms max)
  641|       |                            let drain_result = tokio::time::timeout(
  642|       |                                std::time::Duration::from_millis(500),
  643|       |                                self.drain_orchestrator_messages(&session_id, writer),
  644|       |                            )
  645|       |                            .await;
  646|       |
  647|       |                            if drain_result.is_err() {
  648|       |                                tracing::trace!("Drain timeout, proceeding");
  649|       |                            }
  650|       |
  651|       |                            break TaskResult {
  652|       |                                success: *success,
  653|       |                                message: message.clone(),
  654|       |                            };
  655|       |                        }
  656|       |                        ToolCall::CreateTask { name, description, dependencies } => {
  657|       |                            let response = self
  658|       |                                .handle_create_task(
  659|       |                                    name,
  660|       |                                    description,
  661|       |                                    dependencies,
  662|       |                                    &request.request_id,
  663|       |                                    writer,
  664|       |                                )
  665|       |                                .await;
  666|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  667|       |                        }
  668|       |                        ToolCall::SetGoal { .. } => {
  669|       |                            // SetGoal is only for planner agents, not orchestrator
  670|       |                            tracing::warn!("Orchestrator received unexpected SetGoal call");
  671|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::failure(
  672|       |                                request.request_id.clone(),
  673|       |                                "set_goal is not available to orchestrator agents".to_string(),
  674|       |                            );
  675|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  676|       |                        }
  677|       |                        ToolCall::SkipTasks { ref task_ids, ref reason } => {
  678|       |                            let response = self
  679|       |                                .handle_skip_tasks(task_ids, reason.as_ref(), &request.request_id, writer)
  680|       |                                .await;
  681|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  682|       |                        }
  683|       |                        ToolCall::ListTasks { ref status_filter } => {
  684|       |                            let response = self
  685|       |                                .handle_list_tasks(status_filter.as_ref(), &request.request_id, writer)
  686|       |                                .await;
  687|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  688|       |                        }
  689|       |                        ToolCall::ReportHumanAction { ref description, ref task_id } => {
  690|       |                            let response = self
  691|       |                                .handle_report_human_action(description, task_id.as_ref(), &request.request_id, writer)
  692|       |                                .await;
  693|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  694|       |                        }
  695|       |                    }
  696|       |                }
  697|       |
  698|       |                Ok(msg) = self.acp_orchestrator.recv() => {
  699|       |                    tracing::trace!("📨 Orchestrator received ACP message");
  700|       |                    self.handle_acp_message_with_writer(&msg, writer).await;
  701|       |                }
  702|       |
  703|       |                Ok(msg) = self.acp_worker.recv() => {
  704|       |                    self.handle_acp_message(&msg, "worker").await;
  705|       |                }
  706|       |            }
  707|       |        };
  708|       |
  709|       |        self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  710|       |        #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  711|       |        {
  712|       |            self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  713|       |        }
  714|       |        Ok(result)
  715|     43|    }

  717|       |    /// Fetch current task state for context-aware responses.
  718|       |    ///
  719|       |    /// Creates a [`TaskStateInfo`] snapshot using the `TaskManager` helper methods.
  720|       |    /// This is efficient because it only acquires a read lock and collects
  721|       |    /// the minimal information needed for response building.
  722|     95|    async fn get_task_state(&self) -> TaskStateInfo {
  723|     95|        let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  724|     95|        let pending = tm.get_pending_tasks();
  725|     95|        let parallel = tm.get_parallel_tasks();
  726|     95|        let blocked = tm.get_blocked_tasks();
  727|       |
  728|     95|        TaskStateInfo {
  729|     95|            pending_count: pending.len(),
  730|     95|            parallel_tasks: parallel,
  731|     95|            blocked_tasks: blocked,
  732|     95|        }
  733|     95|    }
  734|       |
  735|       |    /// Resolve a task description from `task_id` or task field.
  736|       |    ///
  737|       |    /// Returns the task description if found, or a fallback message.
  738|     69|    async fn resolve_task_description(
  739|     69|        &self,
  740|     69|        task_id: Option<&String>,
  741|     69|        task: Option<&String>,
  742|     69|    ) -> String {
  743|     69|        if let Some(tid) = task_id {
                                  ^6
  744|      6|            let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  745|      6|            tm.get_by_id_or_name(tid).map_or_else(
  746|      0|                || {
  747|      0|                    tracing::warn!(
  748|       |                        "Task '{}' not found in TaskManager. Available: {:?}",
  749|       |                        tid,
  750|      0|                        tm.list_task_ids()
  751|       |                    );
  752|      0|                    format!("(task {tid} not found)")
  753|      0|                },
  754|      6|                |t| t.description.clone(),
  755|       |            )
  756|       |        } else {
  757|     63|            task.cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| "(no task)".to_string())
                                                          ^0          ^0
  758|       |        }
  759|     69|    }
  760|       |
  761|       |    /// Resolve the `task_id` for an agent spec, with inference support.
  762|       |    ///
  763|       |    /// If `task_id` is provided, uses it directly (with validation).
  764|       |    /// If not, tries to infer the `task_id` from the task description by matching
  765|       |    /// against existing tasks in the `TaskManager`.
  766|       |    ///
  767|       |    /// This mirrors the behavior in concurrent mode (`resolve_agent_spec`).
  768|     65|    async fn resolve_task_id_for_agent(&self, spec: &AgentSpec) -> Option<String> {
  769|       |        // If task_id is explicitly provided, validate and return it
  770|     65|        if let Some(ref tid) = spec.task_id {
                                  ^6
  771|      6|            let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  772|      6|            if tm.get_by_id_or_name(tid).is_some() {
  773|      6|                return Some(tid.clone());
  774|      0|            }
  775|      0|            tracing::warn!(
  776|       |                "Task '{}' not found in TaskManager. Available: {:?}",
  777|       |                tid,
  778|      0|                tm.list_task_ids()
  779|       |            );
  780|      0|            return None;
  781|     59|        }
  782|       |
  783|       |        // Try to infer task_id from task description
  784|     59|        if let Some(ref task_desc) = spec.task {
  785|     59|            let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  786|     59|            if let Some(found_id) = tm.find_by_name_or_description(task_desc) {
                                      ^18
  787|     18|                tracing::info!(
  788|       |                    "📋 Inferred task_id '{}' from task description: {}",
  789|       |                    found_id,
  790|      0|                    truncate_for_log(task_desc, 60)
  791|       |                );
  792|     18|                return Some(found_id);
  793|     41|            }
  794|      0|        }
  795|       |
  796|     41|        None
  797|     65|    }

  843|       |    /// Handle the `list_tasks` tool call.
  844|       |    ///
  845|       |    /// Returns a formatted list of tasks, optionally filtered by status.
  846|      0|    async fn handle_list_tasks(
  847|      0|        &self,
  848|      0|        status_filter: Option<&String>,
  849|      0|        request_id: &str,
  850|      0|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  851|      0|    ) -> ToolResponse {
  852|       |        use crate::tasks::TaskStatus;
  853|       |
  854|      0|        let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  855|      0|        let filter = status_filter.map_or("all", String::as_str);
  856|      0|        let _ = writer

  858|      0|            .await;
  859|      0|        tracing::info!("[L{}] 📋 list_tasks(filter={filter})", depth);
  860|       |
  861|      0|        let task_list = {
  862|      0|            let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  863|      0|            let tasks = tm.all_tasks();
  864|       |
  865|       |            // Filter by status if specified
  866|      0|            let filtered: Vec<_> = tasks
  867|      0|                .into_iter()
  868|      0|                .filter(|t| match filter {
  869|      0|                    "pending" => matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::NotStarted),
  870|      0|                    "in_progress" => matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::InProgress { .. }),
  871|      0|                    "completed" => matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Complete { .. }),
  872|      0|                    "failed" => matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Failed { .. }),
  873|      0|                    "skipped" => matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Skipped { .. }),
  874|      0|                    _ => true,
  875|      0|                })
  876|      0|                .collect();
  877|       |
  878|       |            // Format task list
  879|      0|            let mut lines = Vec::new();

  881|       |                "## Tasks ({} total, filter={})\n",
  882|      0|                filtered.len(),

  886|      0|                let status_str = match &task.status {
  887|      0|                    TaskStatus::NotStarted => "pending",
  888|      0|                    TaskStatus::InProgress { .. } => "in_progress",
  889|      0|                    TaskStatus::Complete { .. } => "completed",
  890|      0|                    TaskStatus::Failed { .. } => "failed",
  891|      0|                    TaskStatus::Skipped { .. } => "skipped",
  892|       |                };

  894|       |                    "- **[{}]** {} ({}): {}",
  895|       |                    task.id, task.name, status_str, task.description
  896|       |                ));
  897|       |            }
  898|      0|            lines.join("\n")

  900|       |
  901|      0|        let response = ToolResponse::success(request_id.to_string(), task_list.clone());
  902|      0|        let _ = writer
  903|      0|            .write_mcp_tool_result(
  904|      0|                "list_tasks",
  905|       |                true,
  906|      0|                &format!("{} tasks returned", task_list.lines().count() - 1),
  907|       |            )
  908|      0|            .await;
  909|       |
  910|      0|        response
  911|      0|    }

  913|       |    /// Handle the `report_human_action` tool call.
  914|       |    ///
  915|       |    /// Records an action that requires manual user intervention.
  916|      0|    async fn handle_report_human_action(
  917|      0|        &self,
  918|      0|        description: &str,
  919|      0|        task_id: Option<&String>,
  920|      0|        request_id: &str,
  921|      0|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  922|      0|    ) -> ToolResponse {
  923|      0|        let depth = self.current_scope.depth();
  924|      0|        let preview = truncate_for_log(description, 50);

  112|       |    /// Drain remaining messages for an orchestrator session after `complete()` is called.
  113|       |    /// Similar to `drain_session_messages` but uses `acp_orchestrator` channel.
  114|       |    ///
  115|       |    /// This is critical for nested orchestrator scenarios: we must stop draining
  116|       |    /// when we see a message for a DIFFERENT session, as that indicates our
  117|       |    /// session's updates are exhausted and we'd be consuming another session's
  118|       |    /// updates (e.g., the parent orchestrator's updates).
  119|     44|    pub(crate) async fn drain_orchestrator_messages(
  120|     44|        &mut self,
  121|     44|        session_id: &str,
  122|     44|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  123|     44|    ) {
  124|       |        loop {
  125|     45|            match self.acp_orchestrator.recv().await {
  126|     45|                Ok(msg) => {
  127|     45|                    let method = msg.get("method").and_then(|v| v.as_str());
  128|     45|                    if method != Some("session/update") {
  129|       |                        // Non-update message, ignore and continue
  130|      0|                        tracing::trace!("Drain ignoring non-update message: {:?}", method);
  131|      0|                        continue;
  132|     45|                    }
  133|       |
  134|     45|                    let Some(params) = msg.get("params") else {
  135|      0|                        tracing::trace!("Drain: session/update missing params, continuing");
  136|      0|                        continue;
  137|       |                    };
  138|       |
  139|       |                    // Support both ACP format (sessionId) and CLI format (session_id)
  140|     45|                    let msg_session_id = params
  141|     45|                        .get("sessionId")
  142|     45|                        .or_else(|| params.get("session_id"))
                                                  ^0     ^0
  143|     45|                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
  144|       |
  145|       |                    // Check if this message is for our session
  146|     45|                    if msg_session_id != Some(session_id) {
  147|       |                        // Message for a different session - our session is done.
  148|       |                        // This happens in nested orchestrator scenarios where after
  149|       |                        // the child orchestrator finishes, recv() returns the parent's
  150|       |                        // pending updates. We must NOT consume those.
  151|      1|                        tracing::debug!(
  152|       |                            "Drain received message for different session {} (expected {}), stopping",
  153|      0|                            msg_session_id.unwrap_or("unknown"),
  154|       |                            session_id
  155|       |                        );
  156|      1|                        return;
  157|     44|                    }
  158|       |
  159|       |                    // Message is for our session - process it
  160|     44|                    let Some(update) = params.get("update") else {
  161|      0|                        tracing::trace!("Drain: update field missing, continuing");
  162|      0|                        continue;
  163|       |                    };
  164|       |
  165|       |                    // Support both ACP format (sessionUpdate) and CLI format (type)
  166|     44|                    let session_update = update
  167|     44|                        .get("sessionUpdate")
  168|     44|                        .or_else(|| update.get("type"))
                                                  ^0     ^0
  169|     44|                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
  170|       |
  171|     44|                    match session_update {
  172|       |                        // ACP: "session_finished", "agent_turn_finished", CLI: "complete"
  173|     44|                        Some("session_finished" | "agent_turn_finished" | "complete") => {
                                                                                        ^1
  174|     43|                            tracing::debug!(
  175|       |                                "✅ Orchestrator session {} properly finished",
  176|       |                                session_id
  177|       |                            );
  178|     43|                            return;
  179|       |                        }
  180|      1|                        Some("agent_message_chunk" | "agent_thought_chunk") => {
                                                                   ^0
  181|       |                            // Continue logging any remaining output
  182|      1|                            if let Some(text) = update
  183|      1|                                .get("content")
  184|      1|                                .and_then(|c| c.get("text"))
  185|      1|                                .and_then(|t| t.as_str())
  186|       |                            {
  187|      1|                                let _ = writer.write_message_chunk(text).await;
  188|      0|                            }
  189|       |                        }
  190|      0|                        _ => {
  191|      0|                            // Silently ignore other update types during drain
  192|      0|                        }
  193|       |                    }
  194|       |                }
  195|      0|                Err(e) => {
  196|      0|                    tracing::debug!("Orchestrator channel closed during drain: {}", e);
  197|      0|                    return;
  198|       |                }
  199|       |            }
  200|       |        }
  201|     44|    }

  130|       |    /// Create a planner session with model fallback chain and retry logic.
  131|       |    ///
  132|       |    /// This tries each model in the chain, and for each model:
  133|       |    /// - Handles transient MCP server startup errors with exponential backoff
  134|       |    /// - Falls back to the next model if "model not available" error occurs
  135|       |    ///
  136|       |    /// Returns (`SessionInfo`, `actual_model`) so the model can be recorded in logs.
  137|     47|    async fn create_planner_session_with_retry(
  138|     47|        &mut self,
  139|     47|        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  140|     47|        cwd: &str,
  141|     47|    ) -> Result<(SessionInfo, String)> {
  142|       |        use super::retry::is_model_not_available_error;
  143|       |
  144|     47|        let retry_config = RetryConfig::from_env();
  145|     47|        let model_chain = self.resolve_planner_model()?;
                                                                    ^0
  146|       |
  147|       |        // Track the last error for reporting if all models fail
  148|     47|        let mut last_error: Option<anyhow::Error> = None;
  149|       |
  150|     47|        for model in &model_chain {
  151|     47|            let mut attempt = 0;
  152|     47|            let mut delay = retry_config.initial_delay;
  153|       |
  154|       |            loop {
  155|     47|                attempt += 1;
  156|       |
  157|       |                // Create session config with Agent mode - planner needs to call MCP tools
  158|       |                // Note: Cursor's "plan" mode is read-only and can't call tools
  159|     47|                let config = SessionConfig::new(model, cwd)
  160|     47|                    .with_mcp_servers(mcp_servers.clone())
  161|     47|                    .with_mode(SessionMode::Agent);
  162|       |
  163|     47|                match self.acp_planner.create_session(config).await {
  164|     47|                    Ok(response) => {
  165|     47|                        if attempt > 1 {
  166|      0|                            tracing::info!(
  167|       |                                "🔄 Planner create_session succeeded on attempt {}/{} with model {}",
  168|       |                                attempt,
  169|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  170|       |                                model
  171|       |                            );
  172|     47|                        }
  173|     47|                        return Ok((response, model.clone()));
  174|       |                    }
  175|      0|                    Err(e) => {
  176|       |                        // Check if model is not available - try next model
  177|      0|                        if is_model_not_available_error(&e) {
  178|      0|                            tracing::debug!(
  179|       |                                "Model '{}' not available for planner, trying next in chain...",
  180|       |                                model
  181|       |                            );
  182|      0|                            last_error = Some(e);
  183|      0|                            break; // Move to next model in chain
  184|      0|                        }
  185|       |
  186|      0|                        let is_transient = is_transient_error(&e);
  187|      0|                        let can_retry = attempt <= retry_config.max_retries && is_transient;
  188|       |
  189|      0|                        if can_retry {
  190|      0|                            tracing::warn!(
  191|       |                                "⚠️ Planner create_session failed (attempt {}/{}, model {}): {}. Retrying in {:?}...",
  192|       |                                attempt,
  193|      0|                                retry_config.max_retries + 1,
  194|       |                                model,
  195|       |                                e,
  196|       |                                delay
  197|       |                            );
  198|      0|                            tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
  199|       |
  200|       |                            // Exponential backoff with cap
  201|      0|                            delay = Duration::from_secs_f64(
  202|      0|                                (delay.as_secs_f64() * retry_config.backoff_multiplier)
  203|      0|                                    .min(retry_config.max_delay.as_secs_f64()),
  204|      0|                            );
  205|       |                        } else {
  206|       |                            // Non-transient, non-model error - fail immediately
  207|      0|                            let reason = if is_transient {
  208|      0|                                "exhausted retries"
  209|       |                            } else {
  210|      0|                                "non-transient error"
  211|       |                            };
  212|      0|                            tracing::error!(
  213|       |                                "❌ Planner create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s) ({reason}): {e:#}",
  214|       |                            );
  215|      0|                            return Err(e).context(format!(
  216|       |                                "Planner create_session failed after {attempt} attempt(s)"
  217|       |                            ));
  218|       |                        }
  219|       |                    }
  220|       |                }
  221|       |            }
  222|       |        }
  223|       |
  224|       |        // All models in the chain failed
  225|      0|        Err(last_error
  226|      0|            .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No models in fallback chain"))
  227|      0|            .context(format!(
  228|       |                "All models in fallback chain failed for planner: {:?}",
  229|       |                model_chain
  230|       |            )))
  231|     47|    }
  232|       |
  233|       |    /// Resolve the planner model configuration to a fallback chain of model strings.
  234|       |    ///
  235|       |    /// Returns a vector of model strings to try in order (first is preferred).
  236|     47|    fn resolve_planner_model(&self) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>> {
  237|       |        // Resolve fallback chain to a tier
  238|     47|        let tier = self
  239|     47|            .model_config

  245|     47|        self.backend
  246|     47|            .resolve_tier(tier, Some(self.model_config.planner_effort))
  247|     47|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/app/retry.rs:
    1|       |//! Retry utilities for resilient agent spawning.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides retry logic with exponential backoff for operations
    4|       |//! that may fail transiently, such as MCP server startup or ACP session creation.
    5|       |
    6|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
    7|       |use std::future::Future;
    8|       |use std::time::Duration;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Configuration for retry behavior.
   11|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   12|       |pub struct RetryConfig {
   13|       |    /// Maximum number of retry attempts (not counting the initial attempt).
   14|       |    pub max_retries: u32,
   15|       |    /// Initial delay between retries.
   16|       |    pub initial_delay: Duration,
   17|       |    /// Maximum delay between retries (caps exponential backoff).
   18|       |    pub max_delay: Duration,
   19|       |    /// Multiplier for exponential backoff (e.g., 2.0 doubles the delay each retry).
   20|       |    pub backoff_multiplier: f64,
   21|       |}
   22|       |
   23|       |impl Default for RetryConfig {
   24|    163|    fn default() -> Self {
   25|    163|        Self {
   26|    163|            max_retries: 3,
   27|    163|            initial_delay: Duration::from_millis(500),
   28|    163|            max_delay: Duration::from_secs(10),
   29|    163|            backoff_multiplier: 2.0,
   30|    163|        }
   31|    163|    }
   32|       |}
   33|       |
   34|       |impl RetryConfig {
   35|       |    /// Create a retry config from environment variables.
   36|       |    ///
   37|       |    /// Environment variables:
   38|       |    /// - `PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRIES`: Max retry attempts (default: 3)
   39|       |    /// - `PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRY_DELAY_MS`: Initial delay in ms (default: 500)
   40|    161|    pub fn from_env() -> Self {
   41|    161|        let max_retries = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRIES")
   42|    161|            .ok()
   43|    161|            .and_then(|s| s.parse().ok())
                                        ^2        ^2
   44|    161|            .unwrap_or(3);
   45|       |
   46|    161|        let initial_delay_ms = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_SPAWN_RETRY_DELAY_MS")
   47|    161|            .ok()
   48|    161|            .and_then(|s| s.parse().ok())
                                        ^2        ^2
   49|    161|            .unwrap_or(500);
   50|       |
   51|    161|        Self {
   52|    161|            max_retries,
   53|    161|            initial_delay: Duration::from_millis(initial_delay_ms),
   54|    161|            ..Default::default()
   55|    161|        }
   56|    161|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Create a config with no retries (useful for testing).
   59|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   60|      0|    pub const fn no_retry() -> Self {
   61|      0|        Self {
   62|      0|            max_retries: 0,
   63|      0|            initial_delay: Duration::from_millis(0),
   64|      0|            max_delay: Duration::from_millis(0),
   65|      0|            backoff_multiplier: 1.0,
   66|      0|        }
   67|      0|    }
   68|       |}
   69|       |
   70|       |/// Check if an error is likely transient and worth retrying.
   71|       |///
   72|       |/// We consider errors transient if they relate to:
   73|       |/// - MCP server startup failures
   74|       |/// - Connection/timeout issues
   75|       |/// - Process spawning issues
   76|     12|pub fn is_transient_error(error: &anyhow::Error) -> bool {
   77|     12|    let error_str = format!("{error:#}").to_lowercase();
   78|       |
   79|       |    // MCP server startup errors are the primary target for retries
   80|     12|    if error_str.contains("mcp server startup")
   81|      5|        || error_str.contains("mcp")
   82|      5|        || error_str.contains("failed to spawn")
   83|      4|        || error_str.contains("failed to initialize")
   84|      4|        || error_str.contains("failed to create acp session")
   85|      4|        || error_str.contains("timeout")
   86|      4|        || error_str.contains("connection refused")
   87|      4|        || error_str.contains("broken pipe")
   88|      4|        || error_str.contains("resource temporarily unavailable")
   89|       |    {
   90|      8|        return true;
   91|      4|    }
   92|       |
   93|      4|    false
   94|     12|}
   95|       |
   96|       |/// Check if an error indicates a model is not available.
   97|       |///
   98|       |/// This is used to trigger fallback to the next model in the chain.
   99|       |/// We detect phrases like "Cannot use this model" from Cursor.
  100|      2|pub fn is_model_not_available_error(error: &anyhow::Error) -> bool {
  101|      2|    let error_str = format!("{error:#}").to_lowercase();
  102|       |
  103|      2|    error_str.contains("cannot use this model")
  104|      1|        || error_str.contains("model not found")
  105|      1|        || error_str.contains("model not available")
  106|      1|        || error_str.contains("invalid model")
  107|      1|        || error_str.contains("unknown model")
  108|      1|        || error_str.contains("unsupported model")
  109|      2|}
  110|       |
  111|       |/// Execute an async operation with retry logic.
  112|       |///
  113|       |/// # Arguments
  114|       |/// * `config` - Retry configuration
  115|       |/// * `operation_name` - Human-readable name for logging
  116|       |/// * `operation` - Async closure that produces the operation's result
  117|       |///
  118|       |/// # Returns
  119|       |/// The result of the operation, or the last error if all retries fail.
  120|      8|pub async fn retry_async<T, F, Fut>(
  121|      8|    config: &RetryConfig,
  122|      8|    operation_name: &str,
  123|      8|    mut operation: F,
  124|      8|) -> Result<T>
  125|      8|where
  126|      8|    F: FnMut() -> Fut,
  127|      8|    Fut: Future<Output = Result<T>>,
  128|      8|{
  129|      8|    let mut attempt = 0;
  130|      8|    let mut delay = config.initial_delay;
  131|       |
  132|       |    loop {
  133|     13|        attempt += 1;
  134|       |
  135|     13|        match operation().await {
  136|      4|            Ok(result) => {
  137|      4|                if attempt > 1 {
  138|      2|                    tracing::info!(
  139|       |                        "🔄 {} succeeded on attempt {}/{}",
  140|       |                        operation_name,
  141|       |                        attempt,
  142|      0|                        config.max_retries + 1
  143|       |                    );
  144|      2|                }
  145|      4|                return Ok(result);
  146|       |            }
  147|      9|            Err(e) => {
  148|      9|                let is_transient = is_transient_error(&e);
  149|      9|                let can_retry = attempt <= config.max_retries && is_transient;
                                                                               ^7
  150|       |
  151|      9|                if can_retry {
  152|      5|                    tracing::warn!(
  153|       |                        "⚠️ {} failed (attempt {}/{}): {}. Retrying in {:?}...",
  154|       |                        operation_name,
  155|       |                        attempt,
  156|      0|                        config.max_retries + 1,
  157|       |                        e,
  158|       |                        delay
  159|       |                    );
  160|      5|                    tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
  161|       |
  162|       |                    // Exponential backoff with cap
  163|      5|                    delay = Duration::from_secs_f64(
  164|      5|                        (delay.as_secs_f64() * config.backoff_multiplier)
  165|      5|                            .min(config.max_delay.as_secs_f64()),
  166|      5|                    );
  167|       |                } else {
  168|       |                    // Either exhausted retries or error is not transient
  169|      4|                    let reason = if is_transient {
  170|      1|                        "exhausted retries"
  171|       |                    } else {
  172|      3|                        "non-transient error"
  173|       |                    };
  174|      4|                    tracing::error!(
  175|       |                        "❌ {operation_name} failed after {attempt} attempt(s) ({reason}): {e:#}",
  176|       |                    );
  177|      4|                    return Err(e).with_context(|| {
  178|      4|                        format!("{operation_name} failed after {attempt} attempt(s)")
  179|      4|                    });
  180|       |                }
  181|       |            }
  182|       |        }
  183|       |    }
  184|      8|}

  204|       |

  208|       |

    1|       |//! Session routing for ACP messages.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the `SessionRouter` struct for routing ACP messages
    4|       |//! to per-session channels based on the session ID in the message params.
    5|       |
    6|       |use serde_json::Value;
    7|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
    8|       |use tokio::sync::mpsc;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Buffer size for per-session message channels.
   11|       |const CHANNEL_BUFFER_SIZE: usize = 100;
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Routes ACP messages to per-session channels.
   14|       |///
   15|       |/// The `SessionRouter` maintains a mapping of session IDs to message channels,
   16|       |/// allowing incoming ACP messages to be delivered to the appropriate session handler.
   17|       |pub struct SessionRouter {
   18|       |    routes: HashMap<String, mpsc::Sender<Value>>,
   19|       |}
   20|       |
   21|       |impl SessionRouter {
   22|       |    /// Create a new empty session router.
   23|     51|    pub fn new() -> Self {
   24|     51|        Self {
   25|     51|            routes: HashMap::new(),
   26|     51|        }
   27|     51|    }
   28|       |
   29|       |    /// Register a new session and return a receiver for its messages.
   30|       |    ///
   31|       |    /// Creates a new channel for the session and returns the receiver end.
   32|       |    /// The router keeps the sender end for routing messages.
   33|       |    ///
   34|       |    /// # Arguments
   35|       |    ///
   36|       |    /// * `session_id` - The unique identifier for the session
   37|       |    ///
   38|       |    /// # Returns
   39|       |    ///
   40|       |    /// A receiver that will receive ACP messages for this session.
   41|      4|    pub fn register(&mut self, session_id: &str) -> mpsc::Receiver<Value> {
   42|      4|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(CHANNEL_BUFFER_SIZE);
   43|      4|        self.routes.insert(session_id.to_string(), tx);
   44|      4|        rx
   45|      4|    }
   46|       |
   47|       |    /// Unregister a session, removing its route.
   48|       |    ///
   49|       |    /// After unregistering, messages for this session will no longer be routed.
   50|       |    ///
   51|       |    /// # Arguments
   52|       |    ///
   53|       |    /// * `session_id` - The unique identifier for the session to unregister
   54|      3|    pub fn unregister(&mut self, session_id: &str) {
   55|      3|        self.routes.remove(session_id);
   56|      3|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Route an ACP message to the appropriate session.
   59|       |    ///
   60|       |    /// Extracts the session ID from the message and sends it to the registered
   61|       |    /// session's channel.
   62|       |    ///
   63|       |    /// # Arguments
   64|       |    ///
   65|       |    /// * `msg` - The ACP message to route
   66|       |    ///
   67|       |    /// # Returns
   68|       |    ///
   69|       |    /// `true` if the message was successfully routed, `false` if no session
   70|       |    /// was registered for the message's session ID or if the session ID
   71|       |    /// could not be extracted.
   72|      9|    pub fn route(&self, msg: Value) -> bool {
   73|      9|        let Some(session_id) = Self::extract_session_id(&msg) else {
   74|      0|            return false;
   75|       |        };
   76|       |
   77|      9|        let Some(sender) = self.routes.get(session_id) else {
                               ^4
   78|      5|            return false;
   79|       |        };
   80|       |
   81|       |        // Use try_send to avoid blocking; if buffer is full, message is dropped
   82|      4|        sender.try_send(msg).is_ok()
   83|      9|    }
   84|       |
   85|       |    /// Extract the session ID from an ACP message.
   86|       |    ///
   87|       |    /// Supports both ACP format (`params.sessionId`) and CLI format (`params.session_id`).
   88|       |    ///
   89|       |    /// # Arguments
   90|       |    ///
   91|       |    /// * `msg` - The ACP message to extract from
   92|       |    ///
   93|       |    /// # Returns
   94|       |    ///
   95|       |    /// The session ID if present, or `None` if not found.
   96|     11|    pub fn extract_session_id(msg: &Value) -> Option<&str> {
   97|     11|        msg.get("params").and_then(|params| {
   98|       |            // Support both ACP format (sessionId) and CLI format (session_id)
   99|     11|            params
  100|     11|                .get("sessionId")
  101|     11|                .or_else(|| params.get("session_id"))
                                          ^1     ^1
  102|     11|                .and_then(|id| id.as_str())
                                             ^10^10
  103|     11|        })
  104|     11|    }
  105|       |}
  106|       |
  107|       |impl Default for SessionRouter {
  108|      0|    fn default() -> Self {
  109|      0|        Self::new()
  110|      0|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/app/run.rs:
    1|       |//! Main run logic for the orchestrator.
    2|       |
    3|       |use super::App;
    4|       |use crate::types::TaskResult;
    5|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
    6|       |
    7|       |impl App {
    8|       |    /// Run the orchestrator with a goal
    9|       |    ///
   10|       |    /// This first spawns a Planner to create a high-level plan, then spawns
   11|       |    /// an Orchestrator to execute the plan by delegating to implementers.
   12|       |    ///
   13|       |    /// Socket setup and MCP configuration happens in the App constructor.
   14|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self))]
   15|     43|    pub async fn run(&mut self, goal: &str) -> Result<TaskResult> {
   16|       |        tracing::info!("Starting with goal: {}", goal);
   17|       |
   18|       |        // Store the original goal for context passing to implementers
   19|       |        self.original_goal = goal.to_string();
   20|       |
   21|       |        // Start the worker router for message routing
   22|       |        self.start_worker_router();
   23|       |
   24|       |        // Run the planning phase
   25|       |        let plan_to_execute = match self.run_planning_phase(goal).await {
   26|       |            Ok(plan) => plan,
   27|       |            Err(e) => {
   28|       |                self.cleanup();
   29|       |                return Err(e);
   30|       |            }
   31|       |        };
   32|       |
   33|       |        // Run the execution phase
   34|       |        let result = self.run_execution_phase(&plan_to_execute).await;
   35|       |
   36|       |        // Always clean up, even if orchestrator failed
   37|       |        self.cleanup();
   38|       |
   39|       |        result
   40|     40|    }
   41|       |
   42|       |    /// Clean up resources (socket and MCP configuration).
   43|     40|    fn cleanup(&mut self) {
   44|       |        // Clean up socket - take ownership to avoid borrow issues
   45|     40|        if let Some(address) = self.socket_address.take() {
   46|     40|            self.cleanup_socket(&address);
   47|     40|        }
                      ^0
   48|       |        // Clean up MCP configuration
   49|     40|        if let Err(e) = self.backend.cleanup_mcp() {
                                 ^0
   50|      0|            tracing::warn!("Failed to cleanup MCP: {}", e);
   51|     40|        }
   52|     40|    }
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// Run the planning phase: spawn a planner and collect its plan.
   55|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self), fields(phase = "planning"))]
   56|     43|    async fn run_planning_phase(&mut self, goal: &str) -> Result<String> {
   57|       |        // Create planner writer at root scope
   58|       |        let mut planner_writer = self
   59|       |            .current_scope
   60|       |            .planner_writer()
   61|       |            .await
   62|       |            .context("Failed to create planner writer")?;
   63|       |
   64|       |        // First, spawn a Planner to create a plan from the goal
   65|       |        tracing::info!("📝 Planning phase: spawning planner agent");
   66|       |        let mut planner_spawn_result = match self.spawn_planner(goal).await {
   67|       |            Ok(result) => {
   68|       |                planner_writer.set_session_id(result.session_id.clone());
   69|       |                planner_writer.set_model(result.model.clone());
   70|       |                if let Err(e) = planner_writer
   71|       |                    .write_header_with_prompt(goal, &result.prompt)
   72|       |                    .await
   73|       |                {
   74|       |                    tracing::warn!("Failed to write planner header: {}", e);
   75|       |                }
   76|       |                // Emit AgentStarted event for TUI
   77|       |                planner_writer.emit_agent_started(goal);
   78|       |                result
   79|       |            }
   80|       |            Err(e) => {
   81|       |                // Write error to planner log so it's not empty
   82|       |                tracing::error!("❌ Failed to spawn planner: {:#}", e);
   83|       |                if let Err(write_err) = planner_writer.write_spawn_error(&e).await {
   84|       |                    tracing::warn!("Failed to write spawn error to planner log: {}", write_err);
   85|       |                }
   86|       |                if let Err(finalize_err) = planner_writer.finalize(false).await {
   87|       |                    tracing::warn!(
   88|       |                        "Failed to finalize planner log after spawn error: {}",
   89|       |                        finalize_err
   90|       |                    );
   91|       |                }
   92|       |                return Err(e);
   93|       |            }
   94|       |        };
   95|       |
   96|       |        // Extract session ID and tool_rx from the planner result
   97|       |        let planner_session = planner_spawn_result.session_id.clone();
   98|       |        let planner_tool_rx = planner_spawn_result.take_tool_rx();
   99|       |
  100|       |        // IMPORTANT: Keep planner_spawn_result alive until wait completes!
  101|       |        // The socket_handle inside it contains the listener task that processes
  102|       |        // MCP connections. If dropped, the socket listener is aborted and the
  103|       |        // MCP server can't connect. We use an explicit binding to prevent NLL
  104|       |        // from dropping it early.
  105|       |        let _keep_planner_alive = planner_spawn_result;
  106|       |
  107|       |        // Wait for planner to complete and collect its output (with timeout)
  108|       |        // Pass the planner's tool_rx so MCP tool calls are received from the correct socket
  109|       |        let planner_output = match self
  110|       |            .wait_for_session_output_with_tool_rx(
  111|       |                &planner_session,
  112|       |                &mut planner_writer,
  113|       |                planner_tool_rx,
  114|       |            )
  115|       |            .await
  116|       |        {
  117|       |            Ok(output) => output,
  118|       |            Err(e) => {
  119|       |                // Finalize planner log with failure status before returning
  120|       |                tracing::error!("❌ Planner session failed: {}", e);
  121|       |                if let Err(finalize_err) = planner_writer.finalize(false).await {
  122|       |                    tracing::warn!(
  123|       |                        "Failed to finalize planner log after session error: {}",
  124|       |                        finalize_err
  125|       |                    );
  126|       |                }
  127|       |                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"));
  128|       |            }
  129|       |        };
  130|       |
  131|       |        // Finalize planner log
  132|       |        if let Err(e) = planner_writer.finalize(true).await {
  133|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to finalize planner log: {}", e);
  134|       |        }
  135|       |
  136|       |        // Renumber tasks by execution order so IDs match execution sequence
  137|       |        {
  138|       |            let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  139|       |            tm.renumber_by_execution_order();
  140|       |        }
  141|       |
  142|       |        // Use structured tasks from TaskManager
  143|       |        let formatted_tasks = {
  144|       |            let tm = self.task_manager.read().await;
  145|       |            tm.format_for_orchestrator()
  146|       |        };
  147|       |
  148|       |        if let Some((count, plan)) = formatted_tasks {
  149|       |            tracing::info!("📋 Using {} structured tasks from create_task calls", count);
  150|       |            Ok(plan)
  151|       |        } else if !planner_output.is_empty() {
  152|       |            // Fallback: raw planner output (not recommended)
  153|       |            tracing::warn!("⚠️  Planner did not create tasks, falling back to raw output");
  154|       |            Ok(planner_output.text.clone())
  155|       |        } else {
  156|       |            Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
  157|       |                "Planner produced no plan (no create_task calls, no text output)"
  158|       |            ))
  159|       |        }
  160|     43|    }
  161|       |
  162|       |    /// Run the execution phase: spawn an orchestrator to execute the plan.
  163|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, plan_to_execute), fields(phase = "execution"))]
  164|     42|    async fn run_execution_phase(&mut self, plan_to_execute: &str) -> Result<TaskResult> {
  165|       |        // Create orchestrator writer
  166|       |        let mut orchestrator_writer = self
  167|       |            .current_scope
  168|       |            .orchestrator_writer()
  169|       |            .await
  170|       |            .context("Failed to create orchestrator writer")?;
  171|       |
  172|       |        // Now spawn the orchestrator to execute the plan
  173|       |        // Pass the clean plan (not the planner's full stream-of-consciousness)
  174|       |        tracing::info!("🎭 Execution phase: spawning orchestrator agent");
  175|       |        let result = self
  176|       |            .run_orchestrator_with_writer(plan_to_execute, &mut orchestrator_writer)
  177|       |            .await;
  178|       |
  179|       |        // Finalize orchestrator log
  180|       |        let success = result.as_ref().map(|r| r.success).unwrap_or(false);
  181|       |        if let Err(e) = orchestrator_writer.finalize(success).await {
  182|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to finalize orchestrator log: {}", e);
  183|       |        }
  184|       |
  185|       |        // Save the final task list to the logs folder
  186|       |        self.save_task_list().await;
  187|       |
  188|       |        result
  189|     39|    }
  190|       |
  191|       |    /// Save the task list to a tasks.json file in the logs folder.
  192|     39|    async fn save_task_list(&self) {
  193|     39|        let task_list_path = self.current_scope.dir().join("tasks.json");
  194|     39|        let task_manager = self.task_manager.read().await;
  195|       |

  203|      0|                        task_manager.all_tasks().len(),
  204|       |                        task_list_path
  205|       |                    );
  206|       |                }
  207|       |            }
  208|      0|            Err(e) => {

   67|       |    /// Register a session with the router and get its per-session receiver.
   68|       |    ///
   69|       |    /// This should be called before waiting for session output.

   71|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self), fields(session_id = %session_id))]

   73|       |        let mut router = self.session_router.write().await;
   74|       |        let rx = router.register(session_id);

   93|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, writer), fields(session_id = %session_id))]
   94|      0|    pub(crate) async fn wait_for_session_output(
   95|      0|        &mut self,

  100|       |            .await
