  102|       |
  103|       |    /// Wait for a session to complete with an optional custom tool_rx.
  104|       |    ///
  105|       |    /// For CLI planner sessions, a separate socket is created with its own tool_rx.
  106|       |    /// Pass that tool_rx here to ensure MCP tool calls are received from the correct socket.
  107|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, writer, tool_rx_override), fields(session_id = %session_id))]
  108|    114|    pub(crate) async fn wait_for_session_output_with_tool_rx(
  109|    114|        &mut self,
  110|    114|        session_id: &str,
  111|    114|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  112|    114|        tool_rx_override: Option<super::types::ToolReceiver>,
  113|    114|    ) -> Result<SessionOutput, OrchestratorError> {
  114|    114|        let timeout_duration = self.timeout_config.session_timeout;
  115|       |
  116|       |        // If router is active, use routed mode with per-session receiver
  117|       |        // Otherwise, use direct mode (for tests with mock clients)
  118|       |        if self.router_active {
  119|       |            // Register the session with the router to get a per-session receiver
  120|       |            let session_rx = self.register_session(session_id).await;
  121|       |
  122|       |            let result = tokio::time::timeout(
  123|       |                timeout_duration,
  124|       |                self.wait_for_session_output_routed(
  125|       |                    session_id,
  126|       |                    writer,
  127|       |                    session_rx,
  128|       |                    tool_rx_override,
  129|       |                ),
  130|       |            )
  131|       |            .await;
  132|       |
  133|       |            // Always unregister the session after completion (success or failure)
  134|       |            self.unregister_session(session_id).await;
  135|       |
  136|       |            match result {
  137|       |                Ok(inner_result) => inner_result.map_err(OrchestratorError::from),
  138|       |                Err(_elapsed) => {
  139|       |                    tracing::error!(
  140|       |                        "⏰ Timeout waiting for session after {:?} (session: {})",
  141|       |                        timeout_duration,
  142|       |                        session_id
  143|       |                    );
  144|       |                    Err(OrchestratorError::Timeout {
  145|       |                        operation: TimeoutOperation::WaitForSession,
  146|       |                        duration: timeout_duration,
  147|       |                        context: Some(format!("session_id: {session_id}")),
  148|       |                    })
  149|       |                }
  150|       |            }
  151|       |        } else {
  152|       |            // Direct mode: call acp_worker.recv() directly (for tests)
  153|       |            match tokio::time::timeout(
  154|       |                timeout_duration,
  155|       |                self.wait_for_session_output_direct(session_id, writer, tool_rx_override),
  156|       |            )
  157|       |            .await
  158|       |            {
  159|       |                Ok(result) => result.map_err(OrchestratorError::from),
  160|       |                Err(_elapsed) => {
  161|       |                    tracing::error!(
  162|       |                        "⏰ Timeout waiting for session after {:?} (session: {})",
  163|       |                        timeout_duration,
  164|       |                        session_id
  165|       |                    );
  166|       |                    Err(OrchestratorError::Timeout {
  167|       |                        operation: TimeoutOperation::WaitForSession,
  168|       |                        duration: timeout_duration,
  169|       |                        context: Some(format!("session_id: {session_id}")),
  170|       |                    })
  171|       |                }
  172|       |            }
  173|       |        }
  174|    114|    }
  175|       |
  176|       |    /// Wait for session output using routed mode (per-session receiver from router).
  177|       |    #[allow(clippy::ignored_unit_patterns)] // `update` pattern is () when tui feature disabled
  178|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, writer, session_rx, tool_rx_override), fields(session_id = %session_id, mode = "routed"))]
  179|      1|    async fn wait_for_session_output_routed(
  180|      1|        &mut self,
  181|      1|        session_id: &str,
  182|      1|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  183|      1|        mut session_rx: mpsc::Receiver<Value>,
  184|      1|        tool_rx_override: Option<super::types::ToolReceiver>,
  185|      1|    ) -> Result<SessionOutput> {
  186|       |        tracing::debug!("⏳ Waiting for session (routed): {}", session_id);
  187|       |        let mut output = SessionOutput::new();
  188|       |        let mut seen_unhandled: std::collections::HashSet<String> =
  189|       |            std::collections::HashSet::new();
  190|       |
  191|       |        // Track whether we're using an override tool_rx (from a dedicated socket)
  192|       |        // vs the main self.tool_rx. We only restore self.tool_rx when we took it.
  193|       |        let using_override = tool_rx_override.is_some();
  194|       |
  195|       |        // Use the override tool_rx if provided (for CLI planner with separate socket),
  196|       |        // otherwise use self.tool_rx
  197|      1|        let mut tool_rx = match tool_rx_override {
  198|       |            Some(rx) => rx,
  199|       |            None => self.tool_rx.take().context("Tool receiver not set up")?,
  200|       |        };
  201|       |
  202|       |        // Take the config update receiver out of self so we don't hold a mutable borrow
  203|       |        #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  204|       |        let mut config_update_rx = self.config_update_rx.take();
  205|       |
  206|       |        loop {
  207|       |            // Config update future - receives from TUI channel when available
  208|       |            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  209|      2|            let config_update_fut = async {
  210|      2|                match config_update_rx.as_mut() {
  211|      0|                    Some(rx) => rx.recv().await,
  212|      2|                    None => std::future::pending().await,
  213|       |                }
  214|      0|            };
  215|       |            #[cfg(not(feature = "tui"))]
  216|       |            let config_update_fut = std::future::pending::<Option<()>>();
  217|       |
  218|       |            tokio::select! {
  219|       |                // Use biased selection to ensure tool_rx is checked first.
  220|       |                // This is critical for tests: when mock ACP recv() injects a tool call,
  221|       |                // we must process it before polling recv() again.
  222|       |                biased;
  223|       |
  224|       |                // Handle config updates from TUI (update is only used with tui feature)
  225|       |                Some(update) = config_update_fut => {
  226|       |                    let _ = &update; // Suppress unused variable warning when tui feature is disabled
  227|       |                    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  228|       |                    self.apply_model_config_update(&update);
  229|       |                }
  230|       |
  231|       |                Some(tool_msg) = tool_rx.recv() => {
  232|       |                    let ToolMessage::Request { request, response_tx } = tool_msg;
  233|       |
  234|       |                    match &request.tool_call {
  235|       |                        ToolCall::Complete { success, message, .. } => {
  236|       |                            tracing::info!(
  237|       |                                "✅ Session {} signaled complete: success={}, message={:?}",
  238|       |                                session_id,
  239|       |                                success,
  240|       |                                message
  241|       |                            );
  242|       |
  243|       |                            if let Some(msg) = message {
  244|       |                                let _ = writer.write_result(msg).await;
  245|       |                            }
  246|       |
  247|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  248|       |                                request.request_id,
  249|      0|                                message.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "Done".to_string()),
  250|       |                            );
  251|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  252|       |
  253|       |                            // Brief drain to capture any final messages (1 second max)
  254|       |                            let drain_result = tokio::time::timeout(
  255|       |                                std::time::Duration::from_millis(500),
  256|       |                                Self::drain_session_messages_from_rx(&mut session_rx, session_id, writer),
  257|       |                            ).await;
  258|       |
  259|       |                            if drain_result.is_err() {
  260|       |                                tracing::trace!("Drain timeout, proceeding");
  261|       |                            }
  262|       |
  263|       |                            // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override).
  264|       |                            // When using override (e.g., implementer's socket), we let the
  265|       |                            // implementer's tool_rx drop here, preserving the caller's tool_rx.
  266|       |                            if !using_override {
  267|       |                                self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  268|       |                            }
  269|       |                            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  270|       |                            {
  271|       |                                self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  272|       |                            }
  273|       |                            return Ok(output);
  274|       |                        }
  275|       |                        ToolCall::CreateTask { name, description, dependencies } => {
  276|       |                            let task_id = {
  277|       |                                let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  278|       |                                tm.create(name, description, dependencies.clone())
  279|       |                            };
  280|       |
  281|       |                            tracing::info!(
  282|       |                                "📋 Session {} created task '{}' (id: {})",
  283|       |                                session_id, name, task_id
  284|       |                            );
  285|       |
  286|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  287|       |                                request.request_id,
  288|       |                                format!("Task '{name}' created with id {task_id}"),
  289|       |                            );
  290|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  291|       |                        }
  292|       |                        ToolCall::SetGoal { summary, acceptance_criteria } => {
  293|       |                            {
  294|       |                                let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  295|       |                                tm.set_goal(summary.clone(), acceptance_criteria.clone());
  296|       |                            }
  297|       |
  298|       |                            tracing::info!(
  299|       |                                "📎 Session {} set goal: {}",
  300|       |                                session_id, summary
  301|       |                            );
  302|       |
  303|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  304|       |                                request.request_id,
  305|       |                                format!("Goal set: {summary}"),
  306|       |                            );
  307|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  308|       |                        }
  309|       |                        other => {
  310|       |                            tracing::warn!("Unexpected tool call from session {}: {:?}", session_id, other);
  311|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::failure(
  312|       |                                request.request_id,
  313|       |                                "This tool is not available to implementer agents. You have access only to complete(). Call complete(success=true, message=\"...\") when your task is done, or complete(success=false, message=\"...\") if you encountered an error.".to_string(),
  314|       |                            );
  315|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  316|       |                        }
  317|       |                    }
  318|       |                }
  319|       |
  320|       |                Some(msg) = session_rx.recv() => {
  321|       |                    let finished = self.handle_worker_session_message(&msg, session_id, writer, &mut output, &mut seen_unhandled).await?;
  322|       |                    if finished {
  323|       |                        // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override)
  324|       |                        if !using_override {
  325|       |                            self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  326|       |                        }
  327|       |                        #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  328|       |                        {
  329|       |                            self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  330|       |                        }
  331|       |                        return Ok(output);
  332|       |                    }
  333|       |                }
  334|       |            }
  335|       |        }
  336|      1|    }
  337|       |
  338|       |    /// Wait for session output using direct mode (polling ACP clients directly).
  339|       |    /// Used for tests with mock clients that don't support the routing infrastructure.
  340|       |    /// Polls both planner and worker clients to handle all session types.
  341|       |    #[allow(clippy::ignored_unit_patterns)] // `update` pattern is () when tui feature disabled
  342|       |    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, writer, tool_rx_override), fields(session_id = %session_id, mode = "direct"))]
  343|    113|    async fn wait_for_session_output_direct(
  344|    113|        &mut self,
  345|    113|        session_id: &str,
  346|    113|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  347|    113|        tool_rx_override: Option<super::types::ToolReceiver>,
  348|    113|    ) -> Result<SessionOutput> {
  349|       |        tracing::debug!("⏳ Waiting for session (direct): {}", session_id);
  350|       |        let mut output = SessionOutput::new();
  351|       |        let mut seen_unhandled: std::collections::HashSet<String> =
  352|       |            std::collections::HashSet::new();
  353|       |
  354|       |        // Track whether we're using an override tool_rx (from a dedicated socket)
  355|       |        // vs the main self.tool_rx. We only restore self.tool_rx when we took it.
  356|       |        let using_override = tool_rx_override.is_some();
  357|       |
  358|       |        // Use the override tool_rx if provided (for CLI planner with separate socket),
  359|       |        // otherwise use self.tool_rx
  360|    113|        let mut tool_rx = match tool_rx_override {
  361|       |            Some(rx) => rx,
  362|       |            None => self.tool_rx.take().context("Tool receiver not set up")?,
  363|       |        };
  364|       |
  365|       |        // Take the config update receiver out of self so we don't hold a mutable borrow
  366|       |        #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  367|       |        let mut config_update_rx = self.config_update_rx.take();
  368|       |
  369|       |        // Track which clients have been exhausted (for mock clients)
  370|       |        let mut worker_exhausted = false;
  371|       |        let mut planner_exhausted = false;
  372|       |
  373|       |        loop {
  374|       |            // If both clients are exhausted and no tool messages, we're stuck
  375|       |            if worker_exhausted && planner_exhausted {
  376|       |                // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override)
  377|       |                if !using_override {
  378|       |                    self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  379|       |                }
  380|       |                #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  381|       |                {
  382|       |                    self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  383|       |                }
  384|       |                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("No more mock updates available"));
  385|       |            }
  386|       |
  387|       |            // Config update future - receives from TUI channel when available
  388|       |            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  389|    619|            let config_update_fut = async {
  390|    619|                match config_update_rx.as_mut() {
  391|      0|                    Some(rx) => rx.recv().await,
  392|    619|                    None => std::future::pending().await,
  393|       |                }
  394|      0|            };
  395|       |            #[cfg(not(feature = "tui"))]
  396|       |            let config_update_fut = std::future::pending::<Option<()>>();
  397|       |
  398|       |            tokio::select! {
  399|       |                // Use biased selection to ensure tool_rx is checked first.
  400|       |                // This is critical for tests: when mock ACP recv() injects a tool call,
  401|       |                // we must process it before polling recv() again, or tool calls may
  402|       |                // be interleaved.
  403|       |                biased;
  404|       |
  405|       |                // Handle config updates from TUI (update is only used with tui feature)
  406|       |                Some(update) = config_update_fut => {
  407|       |                    let _ = &update; // Suppress unused variable warning when tui feature is disabled
  408|       |                    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  409|       |                    self.apply_model_config_update(&update);
  410|       |                }
  411|       |
  412|       |                Some(tool_msg) = tool_rx.recv() => {
  413|       |                    let ToolMessage::Request { request, response_tx } = tool_msg;
  414|       |
  415|       |                    match &request.tool_call {
  416|       |                        ToolCall::Complete { success, message, .. } => {
  417|       |                            tracing::info!(
  418|       |                                "✅ Session {} signaled complete: success={}, message={:?}",
  419|       |                                session_id,
  420|       |                                success,
  421|       |                                message
  422|       |                            );
  423|       |
  424|       |                            if let Some(msg) = message {
  425|       |                                let _ = writer.write_result(msg).await;
  426|       |                            }
  427|       |
  428|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  429|       |                                request.request_id,
  430|      0|                                message.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "Done".to_string()),
  431|       |                            );
  432|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  433|       |
  434|       |                            // Brief drain to capture any final messages (500ms max)
  435|       |                            let drain_result = tokio::time::timeout(
  436|       |                                std::time::Duration::from_millis(500),
  437|       |                                self.drain_session_messages_direct(session_id, writer),
  438|       |                            ).await;
  439|       |
  440|       |                            if drain_result.is_err() {
  441|       |                                tracing::trace!("Drain timeout, proceeding");
  442|       |                            }
  443|       |
  444|       |                            // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override)
  445|       |                            if !using_override {
  446|       |                                self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  447|       |                            }
  448|       |                            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  449|       |                            {
  450|       |                                self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  451|       |                            }
  452|       |                            return Ok(output);
  453|       |                        }
  454|       |                        ToolCall::CreateTask { name, description, dependencies } => {
  455|       |                            let task_id = {
  456|       |                                let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  457|       |                                tm.create(name, description, dependencies.clone())
  458|       |                            };
  459|       |
  460|       |                            tracing::info!(
  461|       |                                "📋 Session {} created task '{}' (id: {})",
  462|       |                                session_id, name, task_id
  463|       |                            );
  464|       |
  465|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  466|       |                                request.request_id,
  467|       |                                format!("Task '{name}' created with id {task_id}"),
  468|       |                            );
  469|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  470|       |                        }
  471|       |                        ToolCall::SetGoal { summary, acceptance_criteria } => {
  472|       |                            {
  473|       |                                let mut tm = self.task_manager.write().await;
  474|       |                                tm.set_goal(summary.clone(), acceptance_criteria.clone());
  475|       |                            }
  476|       |
  477|       |                            tracing::info!(
  478|       |                                "📎 Session {} set goal: {}",
  479|       |                                session_id, summary
  480|       |                            );
  481|       |
  482|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::success(
  483|       |                                request.request_id,
  484|       |                                format!("Goal set: {summary}"),
  485|       |                            );
  486|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  487|       |                        }
  488|       |                        other => {
  489|       |                            tracing::warn!("Unexpected tool call from session {}: {:?}", session_id, other);
  490|       |                            let response = ToolResponse::failure(
  491|       |                                request.request_id,
  492|       |                                "This tool is not available to implementer agents. You have access only to complete(). Call complete(success=true, message=\"...\") when your task is done, or complete(success=false, message=\"...\") if you encountered an error.".to_string(),
  493|       |                            );
  494|       |                            let _ = response_tx.send(response);
  495|       |                        }
  496|       |                    }
  497|       |                }
  498|       |
  499|       |                // Poll worker client (handles implementer sessions)
  500|       |                msg_result = self.acp_worker.recv(), if !worker_exhausted => {
  501|       |                    if let Ok(msg) = msg_result {
  502|       |                        let finished = self.handle_worker_session_message(&msg, session_id, writer, &mut output, &mut seen_unhandled).await?;
  503|       |                        if finished {
  504|       |                            // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override)
  505|       |                            if !using_override {
  506|       |                                self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  507|       |                            }
  508|       |                            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  509|       |                            {
  510|       |                                self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  511|       |                            }
  512|       |                            return Ok(output);
  513|       |                        }
  514|       |                    } else {
  515|       |                        // Worker client exhausted (mock client)
  516|       |                        worker_exhausted = true;
  517|       |                        tracing::trace!("Worker client exhausted");
  518|       |                    }
  519|       |                }
  520|       |
  521|       |                // Poll planner client (handles planner sessions)
  522|       |                msg_result = self.acp_planner.recv(), if !planner_exhausted => {
  523|       |                    if let Ok(msg) = msg_result {
  524|       |                        let finished = self.handle_worker_session_message(&msg, session_id, writer, &mut output, &mut seen_unhandled).await?;
  525|       |                        if finished {
  526|       |                            // Only restore tool_rx to self if we took it (not using override)
  527|       |                            if !using_override {
  528|       |                                self.tool_rx = Some(tool_rx);
  529|       |                            }
  530|       |                            #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  531|       |                            {
  532|       |                                self.config_update_rx = config_update_rx;
  533|       |                            }
  534|       |                            return Ok(output);
  535|       |                        }
  536|       |                    } else {
  537|       |                        // Planner client exhausted (mock client)
  538|       |                        planner_exhausted = true;
  539|       |                        tracing::trace!("Planner client exhausted");
  540|       |                    }
  541|       |                }
  542|       |            }
  543|       |        }
  544|    113|    }

  682|       |    #[tokio::test]
  683|      1|    async fn test_wait_for_session_output_routed_drains_target_messages_and_unregisters_session() {
  684|      1|        let (tool_tx, tool_rx) = mpsc::channel(8);
  685|      1|        let (mut app, _temp_dir) = make_test_app(tool_rx, Box::new(MockTransport::empty()));
  686|      1|        app.router_active = true;
  687|       |
  688|      1|        let mut writer = app.current_scope.planner_writer().await.expect("planner writer");
  689|      1|        let log_path = writer.path().clone();
  690|       |
  691|      1|        let create_task_response = send_tool_request(
  692|      1|            &tool_tx,
  693|      1|            "req-create",
  694|      1|            ToolCall::CreateTask {
  695|      1|                name: "race task".to_string(),
  696|      1|                description: "created before completion wins".to_string(),
  697|      1|                dependencies: vec![],
  698|      1|            },
  699|       |        );
  700|      1|        let complete_response = send_tool_request(
  701|      1|            &tool_tx,
  702|      1|            "req-complete",
  703|      1|            ToolCall::Complete {
  704|      1|                success: true,
  705|      1|                message: Some("completed first".to_string()),
  706|      1|                notes: None,
  707|      1|                add_tasks: None,
  708|      1|            },
  709|       |        );
  710|       |
  711|      1|        let session_router = Arc::clone(&app.session_router);
  712|      1|        let notifier = tokio::spawn(async move {
  713|      1|            let target_msg = session_update("session-routed", "agent_message_chunk", Some("drained target"));
  714|      1|            let other_msg = session_update("session-other", "agent_message_chunk", Some("ignored other"));

  153|       |    /// Drain remaining messages from a per-session receiver after `complete()` is called.
  154|       |    /// This ensures we don't leave stale messages in the notification channel.
  155|       |    /// Waits until we receive `session_finished` or the caller times out.
  156|       |    ///
  157|       |    /// This is a static method that takes the receiver as a parameter to avoid
  158|       |    /// holding any locks on `self` during the drain loop.
  159|      1|    pub(crate) async fn drain_session_messages_from_rx(
  160|      1|        session_rx: &mut mpsc::Receiver<Value>,
  161|      1|        session_id: &str,
  162|      1|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  163|      1|    ) {
  164|      2|        while let Some(msg) = session_rx.recv().await {
  165|      2|            let method = msg.get("method").and_then(|v| v.as_str());

  168|       |                    // Support both ACP format (sessionId) and CLI format (session_id)
  169|      2|                    let msg_session_id = params
  170|      2|                        .get("sessionId")
  171|      2|                        .or_else(|| params.get("session_id"))
                                                  ^0     ^0
  172|      2|                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
  173|       |
  174|       |                    // Only process messages for this session (should always match
  175|       |                    // since the router sends only this session's messages)

  177|      2|                        if let Some(update) = params.get("update") {

  179|      2|                            let session_update = update
  180|      2|                                .get("sessionUpdate")
  181|      2|                                .or_else(|| update.get("type"))
                                                          ^0     ^0
  182|      2|                                .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
  183|       |

  185|      2|                                match update_type {
  186|       |                                    // ACP: "session_finished", "agent_turn_finished"
  187|       |                                    // CLI: "complete"
  188|      2|                                    "session_finished" | "agent_turn_finished" | "complete" => {
                                                                       ^1                      ^1

  218|       |    /// Drain remaining messages directly from ACP clients after `complete()` is called.

  220|       |    /// Polls both planner and worker clients to handle all session types.
  221|       |    /// Waits until we receive `session_finished` or the caller times out.
  222|    110|    pub(crate) async fn drain_session_messages_direct(
  223|    110|        &mut self,
  224|    110|        session_id: &str,
  225|    110|        writer: &mut AgentWriter,

                                                 ^23

  249|     23|                            worker_exhausted = true;
  250|       |                        }
  251|       |                    }

  335|       |    // strip_mcp_prefix Tests

  337|       |
  338|       |    #[test]
  339|      1|    fn test_strip_mcp_prefix_with_paperboat_prefix() {
  340|       |        // The function finds the FIRST underscore and checks if suffix starts with "paperboat-"
  341|       |        // Pattern: toolname_paperboat-servername -> toolname
  342|       |        //
  343|       |        // "complete_paperboat-planner":
  344|       |        //   - first underscore at position 8
  345|       |        //   - prefix = "complete", suffix = "paperboat-planner"
  346|       |        //   - suffix starts with "paperboat-", so returns "complete"
  347|      1|        assert_eq!(strip_mcp_prefix("complete_paperboat-planner"), "complete");
  348|       |
  349|       |        // Tools with underscores in their name won't be stripped because the first
  350|       |        // underscore doesn't have "paperboat-" as the suffix:
  351|       |        //
  352|       |        // "spawn_agents_paperboat-orchestrator":
  353|       |        //   - first underscore at position 5
  354|       |        //   - prefix = "spawn", suffix = "agents_paperboat-orchestrator"
  355|       |        //   - suffix doesn't start with "paperboat-", so returns original
  356|      1|        assert_eq!(
  357|      1|            strip_mcp_prefix("spawn_agents_paperboat-orchestrator"),
  358|       |            "spawn_agents_paperboat-orchestrator"
  359|       |        );
  360|       |
  361|       |        // "create_task_paperboat-planner":
  362|       |        //   - first underscore at position 6
  363|       |        //   - prefix = "create", suffix = "task_paperboat-planner"
  364|       |        //   - suffix doesn't start with "paperboat-", so returns original
  365|      1|        assert_eq!(
  366|      1|            strip_mcp_prefix("create_task_paperboat-planner"),
  367|       |            "create_task_paperboat-planner"
  368|       |        );
  369|      1|    }
  370|       |
  371|       |    #[test]
  372|      1|    fn test_strip_mcp_prefix_without_prefix() {
  373|      1|        assert_eq!(strip_mcp_prefix("complete"), "complete");
  374|      1|        assert_eq!(strip_mcp_prefix("create_task"), "create_task");
  375|      1|        assert_eq!(strip_mcp_prefix("str-replace-editor"), "str-replace-editor");
  376|      1|    }
  377|       |

  394|       |        // Suffix starts with "paperboat-", so returns "" (empty prefix)

    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides cross-platform IPC communication between the main app

   15|       |/// Handle for a per-agent socket, including cleanup resources.
   16|       |pub struct AgentSocketHandle {
   17|       |    /// The IPC address for this agent's socket
   18|       |    pub socket_address: IpcAddress,
   19|       |    /// Receiver for tool messages from this agent
   20|       |    pub tool_rx: ToolReceiver,
   21|       |    /// Handle to the listener task (for cleanup)
   22|       |    listener_task: JoinHandle<()>,
   23|       |}
   24|       |
   25|       |impl AgentSocketHandle {
   26|       |    /// Clean up the socket, removing the endpoint and aborting the listener task.
   27|      2|    pub fn cleanup(self) {
   28|       |        // Abort the listener task
   29|      2|        self.listener_task.abort();
   30|       |
   31|       |        // Clean up the socket endpoint (removes file on Unix, no-op on Windows)
   32|      2|        self.socket_address.cleanup();
   33|      2|    }
   34|       |}
   35|       |
   36|       |impl Drop for AgentSocketHandle {
   37|      3|    fn drop(&mut self) {
   38|       |        // Log when this handle is dropped - this helps debug socket lifecycle issues
   39|      3|        tracing::warn!(
   40|       |            "⚠️ AgentSocketHandle DROPPED: {} - listener will be aborted!",
   41|       |            self.socket_address
   42|       |        );
   43|       |
   44|       |        // Abort the listener task on drop
   45|      3|        self.listener_task.abort();
   46|       |
   47|       |        // Clean up the socket endpoint
   48|      3|        self.socket_address.cleanup();
   49|      3|    }

   52|       |/// Set up an IPC socket for a single agent.
   53|       |///
   54|       |/// Creates a unique socket address for the agent and starts a listener task.
   55|       |/// Returns an `AgentSocketHandle` that can be used to receive tool messages
   56|       |/// and clean up resources when done.
   57|       |///
   58|       |/// # Arguments
   59|       |///
   60|       |/// * `agent_id` - A unique identifier for the agent (used in socket address).
   61|      3|pub async fn setup_agent_socket(agent_id: &str) -> Result<AgentSocketHandle> {
   62|       |    // Generate a unique IPC address for this agent
   63|       |    // The IpcAddress::generate handles platform-specific addressing and path length limits
   64|      3|    let socket_address = IpcAddress::generate(agent_id);
   65|       |
   66|      3|    let listener = IpcListener::bind(&socket_address).await.with_context(|| {
                                                                                          ^0
   67|      0|        format!("Failed to bind agent socket at {socket_address} (agent_id={agent_id})")
   68|      0|    })?;
   69|       |
   70|       |    // Verify the socket endpoint exists before proceeding (on Unix)
   71|       |    // This ensures the socket is ready for MCP server connections
   72|      3|    if !socket_address.exists() {
   73|      0|        anyhow::bail!(
   74|       |            "Socket was not created at {socket_address} after bind (agent_id={agent_id})"
   75|       |        );
   76|      3|    }
   77|       |
   78|      3|    tracing::debug!(
   79|       |        "Agent socket listening at: {} (agent_id={})",
   80|       |        socket_address,
   81|       |        agent_id
   82|       |    );
   83|       |
   84|       |    // Create channel for tool messages
   85|      3|    let (tool_tx, tool_rx) = mpsc::channel::<ToolMessage>(100);
   86|       |
   87|      3|    let socket_addr_str = socket_address.as_str().to_string();
   88|      3|    let listener_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
   89|      3|        tracing::debug!("🔌 Socket listener task started for {}", socket_addr_str);
   90|       |        loop {
   91|      3|            match listener.accept().await {
   92|      0|                Ok(stream) => {
   93|      0|                    tracing::debug!("🔌 Socket accepted connection on {}", socket_addr_str);
   94|      0|                    let tool_tx = tool_tx.clone();
   95|      0|                    let socket_addr = socket_addr_str.clone();
   96|      0|                    tokio::spawn(async move {
   97|      0|                        if let Err(e) = handle_mcp_connection(stream, tool_tx).await {
   98|      0|                            tracing::error!("🔌 MCP connection error on {}: {}", socket_addr, e);
   99|       |                        } else {
  100|      0|                            tracing::debug!(
  101|       |                                "🔌 MCP connection completed successfully on {}",
  102|       |                                socket_addr
  103|       |                            );
  104|       |                        }
  105|      0|                    });
  106|       |                }
  107|      0|                Err(e) => {
  108|       |                    // This error is expected when the listener is dropped during cleanup
  109|      0|                    tracing::debug!("🔌 Socket listener stopped for {}: {}", socket_addr_str, e);
  110|      0|                    break;
  111|       |                }
  112|       |            }
  113|       |        }
  114|      0|        tracing::debug!("🔌 Socket listener task exiting for {}", socket_addr_str);
  115|      0|    });
  116|       |
  117|       |    // Yield to let the listener task start running
  118|       |    // This helps prevent race conditions where the MCP server tries to connect
  119|       |    // before the listener is ready to accept connections
  120|      3|    tokio::task::yield_now().await;
  121|       |
  122|      3|    Ok(AgentSocketHandle {
  123|      3|        socket_address,
  124|      3|        tool_rx,
  125|      3|        listener_task,
  126|      3|    })
  127|      3|}

  169|       |/// Clean up an IPC socket endpoint and abort the listener task.
  170|     40|pub fn cleanup_socket(socket_address: &IpcAddress, listener_task: Option<JoinHandle<()>>) {

  172|     40|    if let Some(task) = listener_task {
                              ^0
  173|      0|        task.abort();
  174|     40|    }
  175|       |
  176|       |    // Clean up the socket endpoint (removes file on Unix, no-op on Windows)
  177|     40|    socket_address.cleanup();

  179|       |
  180|       |/// Handle an MCP connection from the MCP server.
  181|       |///
  182|       |/// Each connection represents a single tool call. The connection stays open
  183|       |/// until the tool call completes, allowing the response to be sent back.
  184|      0|pub async fn handle_mcp_connection(stream: IpcStream, tool_tx: ToolSender) -> Result<()> {
  185|      0|    let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
  186|      0|    let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
  187|      0|    let mut line = String::new();
  188|       |
  189|       |    // Read the tool request (one line of JSON)
  190|      0|    if reader.read_line(&mut line).await? == 0 {
  191|      0|        tracing::debug!("🔌 Socket connection closed (0 bytes read)");
  192|      0|        return Ok(()); // Connection closed
  193|      0|    }
  194|       |
  195|      0|    let request: ToolRequest =
  196|      0|        serde_json::from_str(&line).context("Failed to parse tool request")?;
  197|       |
  198|      0|    tracing::debug!(
  199|       |        "🔌 Socket received tool call: {:?}, request_id={}",
  200|      0|        request.tool_call.tool_type(),
  201|       |        request.request_id
  202|       |    );
  203|       |
  204|       |    // Create a oneshot channel for the response
  205|      0|    let (response_tx, response_rx) = tokio::sync::oneshot::channel();
  206|       |
  207|       |    // Send the request to the app for processing
  208|      0|    let tool_type = format!("{:?}", request.tool_call.tool_type());
  209|      0|    if let Err(e) = tool_tx
  210|      0|        .send(ToolMessage::Request {
  211|      0|            request,
  212|      0|            response_tx,
  213|      0|        })
  214|      0|        .await
  215|       |    {
  216|      0|        tracing::error!(
  217|       |            "🔌 Failed to send {} to app: {} - receiver may be dropped!",
  218|       |            tool_type,
  219|       |            e
  220|       |        );
  221|      0|        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
  222|      0|            "Failed to send tool request to app: {} - is tool_rx receiver alive?",
  223|      0|            e
  224|      0|        ));
  225|      0|    }
  226|      0|    tracing::debug!(
  227|       |        "🔌 Tool request {} sent to app, waiting for response...",
  228|       |        tool_type
  229|       |    );
  230|       |
  231|       |    // Wait for the response
  232|      0|    let response: ToolResponse = match response_rx.await {
  233|      0|        Ok(resp) => resp,
  234|      0|        Err(e) => {
  235|      0|            tracing::error!(
  236|       |                "🔌 Failed to receive response for {}: {} - response_tx was dropped!",
  237|       |                tool_type,
  238|       |                e
  239|       |            );
  240|      0|            return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
  241|      0|                "Failed to receive response from app: {} - did the handler drop response_tx?",
  242|      0|                e
  243|      0|            ));
  244|       |        }
  245|       |    };
  246|       |
  247|      0|    tracing::debug!(
  248|       |        "🔌 Got response for {}: success={}",
  249|       |        tool_type,
  250|       |        response.success
  251|       |    );
  252|       |
  253|       |    // Send the response back to the MCP server
  254|      0|    let response_json = serde_json::to_string(&response)?;
  255|      0|    writer.write_all(response_json.as_bytes()).await?;
  256|      0|    writer.write_all(b"\n").await?;
  257|      0|    writer.flush().await?;
  258|       |
  259|      0|    tracing::debug!("🔌 Response for {} sent to MCP server", tool_type);
  260|       |
  261|      0|    Ok(())
  262|      0|}

   26|       |///
   27|       |/// If `allowed_tools` is provided, returns tools NOT in the whitelist.
   28|       |/// If `allowed_tools` is `None`, returns an empty vec (all tools enabled).

                           ^2
   34|      2|            .map(std::string::ToString::to_string)
   35|      2|            .collect(),
   36|      1|        None => vec![],
   37|       |    }
   38|      3|}

    9|       |/// without going through IPC (Unix sockets on macOS/Linux, named pipes on Windows).
   10|       |#[derive(Debug)]

   48|      0|        let mins = secs / 60;

   52|      0|        format!("{secs}s")
   53|       |    } else {

   55|       |    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/auggie/acp.rs:
    1|       |//! Auggie ACP transport implementation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `AuggieAcpTransport`, a transport that wraps the
    4|       |//! existing `AcpClient` and implements the `AgentTransport` trait.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Design
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! This is an adapter that bridges the `AcpClientTrait` interface used by
    9|       |//! the legacy code with the new `AgentTransport` interface used by the
   10|       |//! transport abstraction layer.
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! The wrapper delegates all operations to the underlying `AcpClient` and
   13|       |//! converts ACP notifications to `SessionUpdate` events.
   14|       |
   15|       |use anyhow::Result;
   16|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   17|       |use serde_json::Value;
   18|       |use std::time::Duration;
   19|       |use tokio::sync::mpsc;
   20|       |use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
   21|       |
   22|       |use crate::acp::{AcpClient, AcpClientTrait};
   23|       |use crate::backend::transport::{
   24|       |    AgentTransport, SessionConfig, SessionInfo, SessionUpdate, ToolResult,
   25|       |};
   26|       |use serde_json::json;
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Auggie ACP transport wrapping the existing `AcpClient`.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// This transport provides the `AgentTransport` interface while delegating
   31|       |/// actual ACP communication to the wrapped `AcpClient`.
   32|       |///
   33|       |/// # Lifecycle
   34|       |///
   35|       |/// 1. `initialize()` - Delegates to `AcpClient::initialize()`
   36|       |/// 2. `create_session()` - Delegates to `AcpClient::session_new()`
   37|       |/// 3. `send_prompt()` - Delegates to `AcpClient::session_prompt()`
   38|       |/// 4. `take_notifications()` - Returns receiver with converted `SessionUpdate` events
   39|       |/// 5. `shutdown()` - Delegates to `AcpClient::shutdown()`
   40|       |pub struct AuggieAcpTransport {
   41|       |    /// The wrapped ACP client
   42|       |    client: AcpClient,
   43|       |    /// Sender for converted session updates
   44|       |    update_tx: mpsc::Sender<SessionUpdate>,
   45|       |    /// Receiver for session updates (taken once by caller)
   46|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   47|       |    update_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>>,
   48|       |    /// Background task converting notifications to session updates
   49|       |    converter_task: Option<JoinHandle<()>>,
   50|       |    /// Current session ID (set after `create_session`)
   51|       |    current_session_id: Option<String>,
   52|       |}
   53|       |
   54|       |impl AuggieAcpTransport {
   55|       |    /// Create a new Auggie ACP transport.
   56|       |    ///
   57|       |    /// # Arguments
   58|       |    ///
   59|       |    /// * `cache_dir` - Optional cache directory for tool filtering
   60|       |    /// * `request_timeout` - Timeout for ACP requests
   61|       |    ///
   62|       |    /// # Errors
   63|       |    ///
   64|       |    /// Returns an error if the ACP client cannot be spawned.
   65|      0|    pub async fn new(cache_dir: Option<&str>, request_timeout: Duration) -> Result<Self> {
   66|      0|        let client = AcpClient::spawn_with_timeout(cache_dir, request_timeout).await?;
   67|      0|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(100);
   68|       |
   69|      0|        Ok(Self {
   70|      0|            client,
   71|      0|            update_tx: tx,
   72|      0|            update_rx: Some(rx),
   73|      0|            converter_task: None,
   74|      0|            current_session_id: None,
   75|      0|        })
   76|      0|    }
   77|       |
   78|       |    /// Start the background task that converts ACP notifications to `SessionUpdate`s.
   79|      0|    fn start_notification_converter(&mut self) {
   80|       |        // Take the notification receiver from the client
   81|      0|        let Some(mut notification_rx) = self.client.take_notification_rx() else {
   82|      0|            tracing::warn!("Notification receiver already taken");
   83|      0|            return;
   84|       |        };
   85|       |
   86|      0|        let update_tx = self.update_tx.clone();
   87|      0|        let session_id = self.current_session_id.clone().unwrap_or_default();
   88|       |
   89|      0|        self.converter_task = Some(tokio::spawn(async move {
   90|      0|            while let Some(notification) = notification_rx.recv().await {
   91|      0|                if let Some(update) = convert_notification_to_update(&notification, &session_id) {
   92|      0|                    if update_tx.send(update).await.is_err() {

   96|      0|                }

  175|       |/// Convert an ACP notification to a `SessionUpdate`.
  176|       |///
  177|       |/// ACP notifications have the format:
  178|       |/// ```json
  179|       |/// {
  180|       |///   "method": "session/update",
  181|       |///   "params": {
  182|       |///     "sessionId": "...",
  183|       |///     "type": "text|tool_use|tool_result|completion|end",
  184|       |///     ...
  185|       |///   }
  186|       |/// }
  187|       |/// ```
  188|      7|fn convert_notification_to_update(
  189|      7|    notification: &Value,
  190|      7|    default_session_id: &str,
  191|      7|) -> Option<SessionUpdate> {
  192|      7|    let method = notification.get("method")?.as_str()?;
                                                         ^0        ^0
  193|      7|    if method != "session/update" {
  194|       |        // Non-update notifications are returned as raw
  195|      1|        return Some(SessionUpdate::Raw {
  196|      1|            session_id: None,
  197|      1|            data: notification.clone(),
  198|      1|        });
  199|      6|    }
  200|       |
  201|      6|    let params = notification.get("params")?;
                                                         ^0
  202|      6|    let session_id = params
  203|      6|        .get("sessionId")
  204|      6|        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                                    ^5^5
  205|      6|        .unwrap_or(default_session_id)
  206|      6|        .to_string();
  207|      6|    let update_type = params.get("type")?.as_str()?;
                                                      ^0        ^0
  208|       |
  209|      6|    match update_type {
  210|      6|        "text" => {
  211|      2|            let content = params.get("content")?.as_str()?.to_string();
                                                             ^0        ^0
  212|      2|            Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  213|      2|                session_id,
  214|      2|                content,
  215|      2|            })
  216|       |        }
  217|      4|        "tool_use" => {
  218|      1|            let tool_use_id = params.get("toolUseId")?.as_str()?.to_string();
                                                                   ^0        ^0
  219|      1|            let tool_name = params.get("toolName")?.as_str()?.to_string();
                                                                ^0        ^0
  220|      1|            let input = params.get("input").cloned().unwrap_or(Value::Null);
  221|      1|            Some(SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  222|      1|                session_id,
  223|      1|                tool_use_id,
  224|      1|                tool_name,
  225|      1|                input,
  226|      1|            })
  227|       |        }
  228|      3|        "tool_result" => {
  229|      0|            let tool_use_id = params.get("toolUseId")?.as_str()?.to_string();
  230|      0|            let content = params.get("content")?.as_str()?.to_string();
  231|      0|            let is_success = params
  232|      0|                .get("isSuccess")
  233|      0|                .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_bool)
  234|      0|                .unwrap_or(true);
  235|      0|            Some(SessionUpdate::ToolResult {
  236|      0|                session_id,
  237|      0|                tool_use_id,
  238|      0|                content,
  239|      0|                is_success,
  240|      0|            })
  241|       |        }
  242|      3|        "completion" | "end" => {
                                     ^2
  243|      2|            let result = params
  244|      2|                .get("result")
  245|      2|                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                                            ^1^1
  246|      2|                .map(String::from);
  247|      2|            let success = params
  248|      2|                .get("success")
  249|      2|                .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_bool)
  250|      2|                .unwrap_or(true);
  251|      2|            Some(SessionUpdate::Completion {
  252|      2|                session_id,
  253|      2|                result,
  254|      2|                success,
  255|      2|            })
  256|       |        }
  257|       |        _ => {
  258|       |            // Unknown type - return as raw for forward compatibility
  259|      1|            Some(SessionUpdate::Raw {
  260|      1|                session_id: Some(session_id),
  261|      1|                data: notification.clone(),
  262|      1|            })
  263|       |        }
  264|       |    }

  332|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the transport.
  333|       |    ///
  334|       |    /// Aborts the converter task and delegates to `AcpClient::shutdown()`.
  335|      0|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  336|       |        // Abort the converter task
  337|       |        if let Some(task) = self.converter_task.take() {
  338|       |            task.abort();
  339|       |        }
  340|       |
  341|       |        self.client.shutdown().await
  342|      0|    }
  343|       |
  344|       |    /// Receive the next raw notification (legacy compatibility).
  345|       |    ///
  346|       |    /// This delegates to `AcpClient::recv()` for backward compatibility
  347|       |    /// with code that polls for notifications.
  348|      0|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  349|       |        self.client.recv().await
  350|      0|    }
  351|       |
  352|       |    /// Take the raw notification receiver (legacy compatibility).
  353|       |    ///
  354|       |    /// This bridges the typed `SessionUpdate` channel into the legacy JSON
  355|       |    /// notification format used by the existing session router. We must not
  356|       |    /// delegate directly to the wrapped ACP client here because the converter
  357|       |    /// task takes that receiver when a session starts.
  358|      0|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  359|      0|        let typed_rx = self.update_rx.take()?;
  360|      0|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel::<Value>(100);
  361|       |
  362|      0|        tokio::spawn(async move {
  363|      0|            let mut typed_rx = typed_rx;
  364|      0|            while let Some(update) = typed_rx.recv().await {
  365|      0|                let json = Self::session_update_to_json(update);
  366|      0|                if tx.send(json).await.is_err() {
  367|      0|                    break;
  368|      0|                }
  369|       |            }
  370|      0|            tracing::debug!("Auggie ACP notification bridge task ended");
  371|      0|        });
  372|       |
  373|      0|        Some(rx)
  374|      0|    }
  375|       |}
  376|       |
  377|       |impl Drop for AuggieAcpTransport {
  378|      0|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  379|       |        // Abort the converter task to prevent it from running after drop
  380|      0|        if let Some(task) = self.converter_task.take() {
  381|      0|            task.abort();
  382|      0|        }
  383|      0|    }

    1|       |//! Auggie authentication utilities.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides authentication checking for the Auggie backend.
    4|       |//! It verifies that the user has logged in via `auggie login` by checking
    5|       |//! for the existence of the session file.
    6|       |
    7|       |use anyhow::{bail, Result};
    8|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Path to the Augment configuration directory.
   11|       |const AUGMENT_CONFIG_DIR: &str = "~/.augment";
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Name of the session file.
   14|       |const SESSION_FILE_NAME: &str = "session.json";
   15|       |
   16|       |/// Get the path to the main Augment session file.
   17|       |///
   18|       |/// The session file is located at `~/.augment/session.json` and is created
   19|       |/// when the user runs `auggie login`.
   20|       |///
   21|       |/// # Returns
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// The expanded path to the session file.
   24|       |#[must_use]
   25|      3|pub fn session_file_path() -> PathBuf {
   26|      3|    let augment_dir = shellexpand::tilde(AUGMENT_CONFIG_DIR).to_string();
   27|      3|    PathBuf::from(&augment_dir).join(SESSION_FILE_NAME)
   28|      3|}
   29|       |
   30|       |/// Check if Auggie CLI is authenticated.
   31|       |///
   32|       |/// This function verifies that the user has authenticated with the Auggie CLI
   33|       |/// by checking for the existence of `~/.augment/session.json`.
   34|       |///
   35|       |/// # Errors
   36|       |///
   37|       |/// Returns an error if the session file does not exist, indicating that
   38|       |/// the user needs to run `auggie login` first.
   39|       |///
   40|       |/// # Example
   41|       |///
   42|       |/// ```ignore
   43|       |/// use paperboat::backend::auggie::auth::check_auggie_auth;
   44|       |///
   45|       |/// fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   46|       |///     check_auggie_auth()?;
   47|       |///     println!("Authenticated!");
   48|       |///     Ok(())
   49|       |/// }
   50|       |/// ```
   51|      1|pub fn check_auggie_auth() -> Result<()> {
   52|      1|    let session_path = session_file_path();
   53|       |
   54|      1|    if !session_path.exists() {
   55|      0|        bail!(
   56|       |            "Augment CLI is not authenticated.\n\n\
   57|       |            Please run 'auggie login' first to authenticate, then try again."
   58|       |        );
   59|      1|    }
   60|       |
   61|      1|    Ok(())
   62|      1|}
   63|       |
   64|       |#[cfg(test)]
   65|       |mod tests {
   66|       |    use super::*;
   67|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/auggie/cache.rs:
    1|       |//! Auggie cache directory management.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles setting up Auggie-specific cache directories with
    4|       |//! the appropriate `settings.json` configuration for tool removal.
    5|       |
    6|       |use crate::backend::AgentCacheType;
    7|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
    8|       |use serde_json::json;
    9|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   10|       |
   11|       |use super::auth;
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Base path for paperboat's cache directories.
   14|       |const CACHE_BASE: &str = "~/.paperboat";
   15|       |
   16|       |/// Set up an Auggie cache directory for a specific agent type.
   17|       |///
   18|       |/// This function ensures the agent has the correct tool restrictions by:
   19|       |/// 1. Verifying Auggie authentication (session.json exists)
   20|       |/// 2. Creating the cache directory if it doesn't exist
   21|       |/// 3. Copying session.json for authentication
   22|       |/// 4. Writing settings.json with the removedTools configuration
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// # Arguments
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// * `agent_type` - The type of agent (Orchestrator, Planner, or Worker)
   27|       |/// * `removed_tools` - List of tool names to remove for this agent
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// # Returns
   30|       |///
   31|       |/// The path to the configured cache directory. For Worker type, returns
   32|       |/// an empty PathBuf since workers use the default cache.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Config Format
   35|       |///
   36|       |/// Auggie uses `settings.json` with a `removedTools` array:
   37|       |/// ```json
   38|       |/// {
   39|       |///     "removedTools": ["tool1", "tool2"]
   40|       |/// }
   41|       |/// ```
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// # Errors
   44|       |///
   45|       |/// Returns an error if:
   46|       |/// - Auggie is not authenticated (session.json doesn't exist)
   47|       |/// - The cache directory cannot be created
   48|       |/// - session.json cannot be copied
   49|       |/// - settings.json cannot be written
   50|      0|pub fn setup_auggie_cache(agent_type: AgentCacheType, removed_tools: &[&str]) -> Result<PathBuf> {
   51|       |    // First, check if auggie is authenticated
   52|      0|    auth::check_auggie_auth()?;
   53|       |
   54|      0|    let cache_name = match agent_type {
   55|      0|        AgentCacheType::Orchestrator => "augment-orchestrator",
   56|      0|        AgentCacheType::Planner => "augment-planner",
   57|      0|        AgentCacheType::Worker => return Ok(PathBuf::new()),
   58|       |    };
   59|       |
   60|      0|    let cache_dir = shellexpand::tilde(&format!("{}/{}", CACHE_BASE, cache_name)).to_string();
   61|      0|    let cache_path = Path::new(&cache_dir);
   62|       |
   63|       |    // Create directory if it doesn't exist
   64|      0|    if !cache_path.exists() {
   65|      0|        std::fs::create_dir_all(cache_path)
   66|      0|            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to create {} cache directory", agent_type))?;
   67|      0|        tracing::info!("Created {} cache directory: {}", agent_type, cache_dir);
   68|      0|    }
   69|       |
   70|       |    // Copy session.json from main augment directory for authentication
   71|      0|    let main_session = auth::session_file_path();
   72|      0|    let agent_session = cache_path.join("session.json");
   73|       |
   74|      0|    if !agent_session.exists() {
   75|      0|        std::fs::copy(&main_session, &agent_session)
   76|      0|            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to copy session.json to {} cache", agent_type))?;
   77|      0|        tracing::info!("Copied session.json to {} cache", agent_type);
   78|      0|    }
   79|       |
   80|       |    // Always write settings.json to ensure removedTools is current
   81|      0|    let settings = json!({
   82|      0|        "removedTools": removed_tools
   83|       |    });
   84|      0|    let settings_path = cache_path.join("settings.json");
   85|      0|    std::fs::write(&settings_path, serde_json::to_string_pretty(&settings)?)
   86|      0|        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to write {} settings.json", agent_type))?;
   87|      0|    tracing::debug!(
   88|       |        "Wrote {} settings.json with {} removed tools",
   89|       |        agent_type,
   90|      0|        removed_tools.len()
   91|       |    );
   92|       |
   93|      0|    Ok(cache_path.to_path_buf())
   94|      0|}
   95|       |

    1|       |// Allow some clippy lints for this new module - can be cleaned up later
    2|       |#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
    3|       |#![allow(clippy::uninlined_format_args)]
    4|       |
    5|       |//! Auggie backend implementation.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! This module implements the [`Backend`] trait for Augment's Auggie CLI.
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! # Authentication
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! Auggie requires authentication via `auggie login`, which stores credentials
   12|       |//! in `~/.augment/session.json`.
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! # Features
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! - Model discovery via `auggie model list`
   17|       |//! - Cache configuration uses `settings.json` with `removedTools` format
   18|       |//! - No additional ACP calls required after `initialize` (unlike Cursor)
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! # Example
   21|       |//!
   22|       |//! ```ignore
   23|       |//! use paperboat::backend::auggie::AuggieBackend;
   24|       |//! use paperboat::backend::Backend;
   25|       |//!
   26|       |//! let backend = AuggieBackend::new();
   27|       |//! backend.check_auth()?;
   28|       |//! let models = backend.discover_models().await?;
   29|       |//! ```
   30|       |
   31|       |pub mod acp;
   32|       |pub mod auth;
   33|       |pub mod cache;
   34|       |mod models;
   35|       |
   36|       |use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
   37|       |use async_trait::async_trait;

   47|       |/// Auggie CLI backend implementation.
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// This is a zero-sized type that implements the [`Backend`] trait for
   50|       |/// Augment's Auggie CLI. Auggie uses the `auggie` command-line tool
   51|       |/// with ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) for AI interactions.
   52|       |///
   53|       |/// # Key Features
   54|       |///
   55|       |/// - Simple authentication: just requires `auggie login`
   56|       |/// - Model discovery: supports `auggie model list` command
   57|       |/// - Cache configuration: uses `settings.json` with `removedTools` array
   58|       |///
   59|       |/// # Example
   60|       |///
   61|       |/// ```ignore
   62|       |/// use paperboat::backend::auggie::AuggieBackend;
   63|       |/// use paperboat::backend::Backend;
   64|       |///
   65|       |/// let backend = AuggieBackend::new();
   66|       |/// backend.check_auth()?;
   67|       |/// let models = backend.discover_models().await?;
   68|       |/// let client = backend.create_client(None, Duration::from_secs(60)).await?;
   69|       |/// ```
   70|       |pub struct AuggieBackend;
   71|       |

   96|      0|    async fn available_tiers(&self) -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>> {
   97|       |        models::discover_auggie_tiers().await
   98|      0|    }
   99|       |
  100|      0|    fn resolve_tier(
  101|      0|        &self,
  102|      0|        tier: ModelTier,
  103|      0|        _effort: Option<crate::models::EffortLevel>,
  104|      0|    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
  105|       |        // Auggie uses format like "haiku4.5", "sonnet4.5", "opus4.5"
  106|       |        // Returns a fallback chain of versions to try (newest first)
  107|       |        // Note: Auggie doesn't support effort levels - the parameter is ignored
  108|      0|        match tier {
  109|      0|            ModelTier::Auto => Ok(vec!["auto".to_string()]),
  110|       |            // Opus: try 4.5 (latest known)
  111|      0|            ModelTier::Opus => Ok(vec!["opus4.5".to_string()]),
  112|       |            // Sonnet: try 4.5 (latest known)
  113|      0|            ModelTier::Sonnet => Ok(vec!["sonnet4.5".to_string()]),
  114|       |            // Haiku: try 4.5 (latest known)
  115|      0|            ModelTier::Haiku => Ok(vec!["haiku4.5".to_string()]),
  116|       |            // Auggie doesn't have these tiers
  117|       |            ModelTier::Gpt
  118|       |            | ModelTier::OpenAI
  119|       |            | ModelTier::Codex
  120|       |            | ModelTier::CodexMini
  121|       |            | ModelTier::Gemini
  122|       |            | ModelTier::GeminiFlash
  123|       |            | ModelTier::Grok
  124|      0|            | ModelTier::Composer => Err(anyhow!(
  125|      0|                "Model tier '{}' is not available in Auggie backend",
  126|      0|                tier
  127|      0|            )),
  128|       |        }
  129|      0|    }
  130|       |
  131|      0|    async fn setup_mcp(&self, _socket_path: &str) -> Result<()> {
  132|       |        // Auggie passes mcpServers dynamically in session/new, no pre-setup needed
  133|       |        Ok(())
  134|      0|    }
  135|       |

  137|       |        // Auggie doesn't need cleanup - MCP config is per-session
  138|      0|        Ok(())
  139|      0|    }
  140|       |
  141|       |    async fn create_client(
  142|       |        &self,
  143|       |        _agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  144|       |        cache_dir: Option<&str>,
  145|       |        request_timeout: Duration,
  146|      0|    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AcpClientTrait + Send>> {
  147|       |        // Auggie uses cache_dir with settings.json to control tool access
  148|       |        // The permission policy is enforced via the cache directory configuration
  149|       |        let client = AcpClient::spawn_with_timeout(cache_dir, request_timeout).await?;
  150|       |        Ok(Box::new(client))
  151|      0|    }
  152|       |
  153|      0|    fn setup_agent_cache(
  154|      0|        &self,
  155|      0|        agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  156|      0|        removed_tools: &[&str],
  157|      0|    ) -> Result<PathBuf> {
  158|      0|        cache::setup_auggie_cache(agent_type, removed_tools)
  159|      0|    }
  160|       |
  161|       |    // ========================================================================
  162|       |    // Transport Methods
  163|       |    // ========================================================================
  164|       |
  165|       |    /// Returns the list of transport protocols Auggie supports.
  166|       |    ///
  167|       |    /// Auggie only supports ACP transport. CLI transport is not available.
  168|      2|    fn supported_transports(&self) -> Vec<TransportKind> {
  169|      2|        vec![TransportKind::Acp]
  170|      2|    }
  171|       |
  172|       |    /// Create a transport instance for the specified protocol.
  173|       |    ///
  174|       |    /// # Arguments
  175|       |    ///
  176|       |    /// * `kind` - The transport protocol (only `TransportKind::Acp` is supported)
  177|       |    /// * `agent_type` - The type of agent (Orchestrator, Planner, Implementer)
  178|       |    /// * `config` - Transport configuration including workspace, model, timeout
  179|       |    ///
  180|       |    /// # Errors
  181|       |    ///
  182|       |    /// Returns an error if:
  183|       |    /// - `kind` is not `TransportKind::Acp` (CLI not supported)
  184|       |    /// - The transport cannot be created (e.g., auggie binary not found)
  185|       |    async fn create_transport(
  186|       |        &self,
  187|       |        kind: TransportKind,
  188|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
  189|       |        config: TransportConfig,
  190|      1|    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AgentTransport>> {
  191|       |        // Check if the requested transport is supported
  192|       |        if kind != TransportKind::Acp {
  193|       |            return Err(anyhow!(
  194|       |                "Transport '{}' is not supported by Auggie backend. \
  195|       |                Only ACP transport is available. \
  196|       |                Use TransportKind::Acp instead.",
  197|       |                kind
  198|       |            ));
  199|       |        }
  200|       |
  201|       |        // Determine cache directory based on agent type
  202|       |        // This controls tool filtering via settings.json
  203|       |        let cache_dir = match agent_type {
  204|       |            AgentType::Orchestrator => {
  205|       |                let path = self.setup_agent_cache(AgentCacheType::Orchestrator, &[])?;
  206|       |                if path.as_os_str().is_empty() {
  207|       |                    None
  208|       |                } else {
  209|       |                    Some(path.to_string_lossy().to_string())
  210|       |                }
  211|       |            }
  212|       |            AgentType::Planner => {
  213|       |                let path = self.setup_agent_cache(AgentCacheType::Planner, &[])?;
  214|       |                if path.as_os_str().is_empty() {
  215|       |                    None
  216|       |                } else {
  217|       |                    Some(path.to_string_lossy().to_string())
  218|       |                }
  219|       |            }
  220|       |            AgentType::Implementer => {
  221|       |                // Implementers use default cache (full tool access)
  222|       |                None
  223|       |            }
  224|       |        };
  225|       |
  226|       |        let transport =
  227|       |            acp::AuggieAcpTransport::new(cache_dir.as_deref(), config.request_timeout).await?;
  228|       |
  229|       |        Ok(Box::new(transport))
  230|      1|    }
  231|       |
  232|      6|    fn login_hint(&self) -> &'static str {
  233|      6|        "auggie login"
  234|      6|    }
  235|       |}

   48|       |            }
   49|      0|        })?;

    1|       |//! Cursor-specific ACP client implementation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `CursorAcpClient`, an ACP client that communicates
    4|       |//! with Cursor's agent CLI over stdin/stdout using JSON-RPC 2.0.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! Key differences from the standard `AcpClient`:
    7|       |//! - Spawns `agent acp` instead of `auggie --acp`
    8|       |//! - Supports session modes (`agent`, `plan`, `ask`) to control tool access
    9|       |//! - Requires an `authenticate` call with methodId: "cursor_login" after initialize
   10|       |//! - Handles `session/request_permission` to control tool access per agent
   11|       |
   12|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   13|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   14|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   15|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   16|       |use std::process::Stdio;
   17|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   18|       |use std::time::Duration;
   19|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
   20|       |use tokio::process::{Child, Command};
   21|       |use tokio::sync::{mpsc, oneshot, Mutex};
   22|       |use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
   23|       |
   24|       |use crate::acp::{AcpClientTrait, SessionMode, SessionNewResponse};
   25|       |
   26|       |// Re-export PermissionPolicy from the shared module
   27|       |pub use super::permission::PermissionPolicy;
   28|       |
   29|       |/// Pending request tracker - maps request IDs to their response channels
   30|       |type PendingRequests = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, oneshot::Sender<Value>>>>;
   31|       |
   32|       |/// ACP client for Cursor's agent CLI.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// This client spawns `agent acp` and communicates via JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout.
   35|       |/// Unlike the standard AcpClient, it requires an additional `authenticate` call
   36|       |/// after initialization.
   37|       |///
   38|       |/// # Permission Handling
   39|       |///
   40|       |/// When Cursor sends `session/request_permission` for a tool, this client
   41|       |/// automatically responds based on its `PermissionPolicy`. This allows
   42|       |/// planners/orchestrators to be restricted from file editing while still
   43|       |/// having access to MCP tools.
   44|       |pub struct CursorAcpClient {
   45|       |    child: Child,
   46|       |    tx: mpsc::Sender<String>,
   47|       |    /// Map of pending request IDs to their response channels
   48|       |    pending_requests: PendingRequests,
   49|       |    /// Channel for JSON-RPC notifications (messages without an "id" field).
   50|       |    notification_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>>,
   51|       |    /// Handle to the stdin writer task
   52|       |    stdin_task: Option<JoinHandle<()>>,
   53|       |    /// Handle to the stdout reader task
   54|       |    stdout_task: Option<JoinHandle<()>>,
   55|       |    /// Timeout for request/response operations
   56|       |    request_timeout: Duration,
   57|       |    /// Permission policy for auto-responding to tool permission requests
   58|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   59|       |    permission_policy: PermissionPolicy,
   60|       |}
   61|       |

  226|     19|    async fn request(&mut self, method: &str, params: Value) -> Result<Value> {
  227|     19|        let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
  228|     19|        let request = json!({
  229|     19|            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  230|     19|            "id": id,
  231|     19|            "method": method,
  232|     19|            "params": params,
  233|       |        });
  234|       |
  235|     19|        let request_str = serde_json::to_string(&request)?;
                                                                       ^0
  236|     19|        if method == "session/new" {
  237|      3|            tracing::info!(

  239|      0|                params
  240|      0|                    .get("model")
  241|      0|                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())

  253|       |        // results come via notifications. Return immediately.

  266|       |        {
