  338|       |#[async_trait]
  339|       |impl AcpClientTrait for CursorAcpClient {
  340|       |    /// Initialize the ACP connection and perform Cursor-specific authentication.
  341|       |    ///
  342|       |    /// This implementation sends the standard initialize request, then automatically
  343|       |    /// calls authenticate with methodId: "cursor_login" as required by Cursor's ACP.
  344|      6|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  345|       |        // Standard ACP initialize
  346|       |        let params = json!({
  347|       |            "protocolVersion": 1,
  348|       |            "capabilities": {},
  349|       |            "clientInfo": {
  350|       |                "name": "paperboat",
  351|       |                "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
  352|       |            }
  353|       |        });
  354|       |
  355|       |        self.request("initialize", params).await?;
  356|       |
  357|       |        // Cursor-specific: authenticate after initialize
  358|       |        self.authenticate().await?;
  359|       |
  360|       |        Ok(())
  361|      6|    }
  362|       |
  363|       |    /// Create a new session with the specified mode.
  364|       |    ///
  365|       |    /// Cursor ACP supports session modes to control agent capabilities:
  366|       |    /// - `agent`: Full tool access (for implementers)
  367|       |    /// - `plan`: Read-only planning mode (for planners)
  368|       |    /// - `ask`: Q&A read-only mode (for explorers)
  369|       |    async fn session_new(
  370|       |        &mut self,
  371|       |        model: &str,
  372|       |        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  373|       |        cwd: &str,
  374|       |        mode: SessionMode,
  375|      3|    ) -> Result<SessionNewResponse> {

  377|       |            "model": model,
  378|       |            "cwd": cwd,
  379|       |            "mcpServers": mcp_servers,
  380|       |            "mode": mode.as_str()
  381|       |        });
  382|       |
  383|       |        let result = self.request("session/new", params).await?;
  384|       |        Ok(serde_json::from_value(result)?)
  385|      3|    }
  386|       |
  387|       |    /// Send a prompt to a session
  388|      1|    async fn session_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {
  389|       |        let params = json!({
  390|       |            "sessionId": session_id,
  391|       |            "prompt": [
  392|       |                {
  393|       |                    "type": "text",
  394|       |                    "text": prompt
  395|       |                }
  396|       |            ],
  397|       |        });
  398|       |
  399|       |        self.request("session/prompt", params).await?;
  400|       |        Ok(())
  401|      1|    }
  402|       |
  403|       |    /// Receive next notification from the server
  404|       |    ///
  405|       |    /// Notifications are server-initiated messages (no "id" field) like session/update.
  406|       |    /// Use this to monitor agent progress, receive plans, tool calls, etc.
  407|       |    ///
  408|       |    /// Returns an error if the notification receiver has been taken via `take_notification_rx()`.
  409|      3|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  410|       |        let rx = self
  411|       |            .notification_rx
  412|       |            .as_mut()
  413|       |            .context("Notification receiver has been taken")?;
  414|       |        rx.recv().await.context("Failed to receive notification")
  415|      3|    }
  416|       |
  417|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for external routing.
  418|      3|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  419|      3|        self.notification_rx.take()
  420|      3|    }
  421|       |
  422|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the ACP client.
  423|       |    ///
  424|       |    /// This closes stdin (signaling EOF to the child), waits for the child process
  425|       |    /// to exit (with a timeout), and cleans up background tasks.
  426|      3|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  427|       |        tracing::debug!("Shutting down Cursor ACP client");
  428|       |
  429|       |        // Abort the stdin and stdout tasks
  430|       |        if let Some(task) = self.stdin_task.take() {
  431|       |            task.abort();
  432|       |        }
  433|       |        if let Some(task) = self.stdout_task.take() {
  434|       |            task.abort();
  435|       |        }
  436|       |
  437|       |        // Try to kill the child process gracefully first, then forcefully
  438|       |        if let Err(e) = self.child.start_kill() {
  439|       |            tracing::warn!("Failed to send kill signal to child: {}", e);
  440|       |        }
  441|       |
  442|       |        // Wait for the child to exit with a timeout
  443|       |        let wait_result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), self.child.wait()).await;
  444|       |
  445|       |        match wait_result {
  446|       |            Ok(Ok(status)) => {
  447|       |                tracing::debug!("Cursor ACP child process exited with status: {}", status);
  448|       |            }
  449|       |            Ok(Err(e)) => {
  450|       |                tracing::warn!("Error waiting for Cursor ACP child process: {}", e);
  451|       |            }
  452|       |            Err(_) => {
  453|       |                // Timeout - process didn't exit, try to kill it more forcefully
  454|       |                tracing::warn!(
  455|       |                    "Cursor ACP child process did not exit within timeout, killing forcefully"
  456|       |                );
  457|       |                if let Err(e) = self.child.kill().await {
  458|       |                    tracing::error!("Failed to forcefully kill Cursor ACP child: {}", e);
  459|       |                }
  460|       |            }
  461|       |        }
  462|       |
  463|       |        Ok(())
  464|      3|    }
  465|       |}
  466|       |
  467|       |impl Drop for CursorAcpClient {
  468|     12|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  469|       |        // Abort background tasks to prevent them from running after drop
  470|     12|        if let Some(task) = self.stdin_task.take() {
                                  ^0
  471|      0|            task.abort();
  472|     12|        }
  473|     12|        if let Some(task) = self.stdout_task.take() {
                                  ^0
  474|      0|            task.abort();
  475|     12|        }
  476|       |
  477|       |        // Initiate process termination (can't wait in Drop since it's not async)
  478|     12|        if let Err(e) = self.child.start_kill() {
                                 ^0
  479|      0|            tracing::warn!("Failed to initiate kill of Cursor ACP child process: {}", e);
  480|     12|        }
  481|     12|    }
  482|       |}
  483|       |
  484|       |#[cfg(test)]
  485|       |mod tests {
  486|       |    use super::*;
  487|       |    use serial_test::serial;
  488|       |    use std::env;
  489|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  490|       |    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
  491|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  492|       |    use std::path::Path;
  493|       |
  494|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  495|       |        key: &'static str,
  496|       |        original: Option<String>,
  497|       |    }
  498|       |
  499|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  500|      4|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  501|      4|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  502|      4|            env::set_var(key, value);
  503|      4|            Self { key, original }
  504|      4|        }
  505|       |    }
  506|       |
  507|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  508|      4|        fn drop(&mut self) {
  509|      4|            match &self.original {
  510|      4|                Some(value) => env::set_var(self.key, value),
  511|      0|                None => env::remove_var(self.key),
  512|       |            }
  513|      4|        }
  514|       |    }
  515|       |
  516|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  517|      2|    fn prepend_path(dir: &Path) -> EnvGuard {
  518|      2|        let mut path_entries = vec![dir.display().to_string()];
  519|      2|        if let Ok(existing) = env::var("PATH") {
  520|      2|            path_entries.push(existing);
  521|      2|        }
                      ^0
  522|      2|        EnvGuard::set("PATH", &path_entries.join(":"))
  523|      2|    }
  524|       |
  525|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  526|      2|    fn write_fake_agent_acp(
  527|      2|        dir: &Path,
  528|      2|        permission_log: &Path,
  529|      2|        requested_tool: &str,
  530|      2|        notification_text: &str,
  531|      2|    ) {
  532|      2|        let script_path = dir.join("agent");
  533|      2|        std::fs::write(
  534|      2|            &script_path,
  535|      2|            format!(
  536|       |                r#"#!/usr/bin/env python3
  537|       |import json
  538|       |import pathlib
  539|       |import sys
  540|       |
  541|       |permission_log = pathlib.Path({permission_log:?})
  542|       |requested_tool = {requested_tool:?}
  543|       |notification_text = {notification_text:?}
  544|       |
  545|       |def read_request():
  546|       |    line = sys.stdin.readline()
  547|       |    if not line:
  548|       |        sys.exit(1)
  549|       |    return json.loads(line)
  550|       |
  551|       |init_request = read_request()
  552|       |print(json.dumps({{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": init_request["id"], "result": {{}}}}), flush=True)
  553|       |
  554|       |auth_request = read_request()
  555|       |print(json.dumps({{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": auth_request["id"], "result": {{}}}}), flush=True)
  556|       |
  557|       |print(
  558|       |    json.dumps(
  559|       |        {{
  560|       |            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  561|       |            "id": "perm-1",
  562|       |            "method": "session/request_permission",
  563|       |            "params": {{"tool": requested_tool}},
  564|       |        }}
  565|       |    ),
  566|       |    flush=True,
  567|       |)
  568|       |
  569|       |permission_response = read_request()
  570|       |permission_log.write_text(json.dumps(permission_response))
  571|       |
  572|       |print(
  573|       |    json.dumps(
  574|       |        {{
  575|       |            "method": "session/update",
  576|       |            "params": {{"type": "text", "content": notification_text}},
  577|       |        }}
  578|       |    ),
  579|       |    flush=True,
  580|       |)
  581|       |"#,
  582|       |            ),
  583|       |        )
  584|      2|        .unwrap();
  585|       |
  586|      2|        let mut permissions = std::fs::metadata(&script_path).unwrap().permissions();
  587|      2|        permissions.set_mode(0o755);
  588|      2|        std::fs::set_permissions(&script_path, permissions).unwrap();
  589|      2|    }
  590|       |
  591|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  592|     10|    async fn make_test_client(
  593|     10|        request_timeout: Duration,
  594|     10|    ) -> (CursorAcpClient, mpsc::Receiver<String>, PendingRequests) {
  595|     10|        let child = Command::new("/bin/sh")
  596|     10|            .args(["-c", "sleep 60"])
  597|     10|            .stdin(Stdio::null())
  598|     10|            .stdout(Stdio::null())
  599|     10|            .stderr(Stdio::null())
  600|     10|            .spawn()
  601|     10|            .unwrap();
  602|     10|        let (tx, rx_commands) = mpsc::channel::<String>(10);
  603|     10|        let (_notification_tx, notification_rx) = mpsc::channel::<Value>(10);
  604|     10|        let pending_requests: PendingRequests = Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
  605|       |
  606|     10|        (
  607|     10|            CursorAcpClient {
  608|     10|                child,
  609|     10|                tx,
  610|     10|                pending_requests: Arc::clone(&pending_requests),
  611|     10|                notification_rx: Some(notification_rx),
  612|     10|                stdin_task: None,
  613|     10|                stdout_task: None,
  614|     10|                request_timeout,
  615|     10|                permission_policy: PermissionPolicy::allow_all(),
  616|     10|            },
  617|     10|            rx_commands,
  618|     10|            pending_requests,
  619|     10|        )
  620|     10|    }

  623|       |    #[tokio::test]
  624|      1|    async fn test_initialize_sends_authenticate_after_initialize() {
  625|      1|        let (mut client, mut rx_commands, pending_requests) =
  626|      1|            make_test_client(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;
  627|      1|        let (methods_tx, methods_rx) = oneshot::channel();
  628|       |
  629|      1|        tokio::spawn(async move {
  630|      1|            let mut methods = Vec::new();
  631|      3|            while methods.len() < 2 {
  632|      2|                let request = rx_commands.recv().await.unwrap();
  633|      2|                let value: Value = serde_json::from_str(&request).unwrap();
  634|      2|                let id = value["id"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();
  635|      2|                methods.push(value["method"].as_str().unwrap().to_string());
  636|       |
  637|      2|                let sender = pending_requests.lock().await.remove(&id).unwrap();
  638|      2|                sender
  639|      2|                    .send(json!({
  640|      2|                        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  641|      2|                        "id": id,
  642|      2|                        "result": {}
  643|       |                    }))
  644|      2|                    .unwrap();
  645|       |            }
  646|      1|            let _ = methods_tx.send(methods);
  647|      1|        });
  648|       |
  649|      1|        client.initialize().await.unwrap();
  650|       |
  651|      1|        assert_eq!(
  652|      1|            methods_rx.await.unwrap(),
  653|      1|            vec!["initialize".to_string(), "authenticate".to_string()]
  654|      1|        );
  655|      1|    }

  880|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  881|       |    #[tokio::test]
  882|      1|    async fn test_session_new_with_mcp_servers() {
  883|       |        use crate::acp::SessionMode;
  884|       |
  885|      1|        let (mut client, mut rx_commands, pending_requests) =
  886|      1|            make_test_client(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;
  887|       |
  888|      1|        let (mcp_tx, mcp_rx) = tokio::sync::oneshot::channel();
  889|       |
  890|      1|        tokio::spawn(async move {
  891|       |            // Skip initialize and authenticate
  892|      1|            for _ in 0..2 {
  893|      2|                let req = rx_commands.recv().await.unwrap();
  894|      2|                let value: Value = serde_json::from_str(&req).unwrap();
  895|      2|                let id = value["id"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();
  896|      2|                let sender = pending_requests.lock().await.remove(&id).unwrap();
  897|      2|                sender
  898|      2|                    .send(json!({
  899|      2|                        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  900|      2|                        "id": id,
  901|      2|                        "result": {}
  902|       |                    }))
  903|      2|                    .unwrap();
  904|       |            }
  905|       |
  906|       |            // Capture session/new request
  907|      1|            let req = rx_commands.recv().await.unwrap();
  908|      1|            let value: Value = serde_json::from_str(&req).unwrap();

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/acp_transport.rs:
    1|       |//! Cursor ACP transport wrapper.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `CursorAcpTransport`, an adapter that wraps the existing
    4|       |//! `CursorAcpClient` to implement the `AgentTransport` trait. This allows the
    5|       |//! ACP protocol to be used through the unified transport interface.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # Note on MCP Support
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! **Warning:** Cursor's ACP mode has broken MCP tool support. Use `CursorCliTransport`
   10|       |//! (via `TransportKind::Cli`) for proper MCP server integration. This transport is
   11|       |//! provided for compatibility and for when Cursor fixes the ACP MCP issues.
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! # Usage
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! Typically created via `CursorBackend::create_transport()`:
   16|       |//!
   17|       |//! ```ignore
   18|       |//! let backend = CursorBackend::new();
   19|       |//! let config = TransportConfig::new("/workspace").with_model("sonnet-4.6");
   20|       |//! let transport = backend.create_transport(
   21|       |//!     TransportKind::Acp,
   22|       |//!     AgentType::Implementer,
   23|       |//!     config,
   24|       |//! ).await?;
   25|       |//! ```
   26|       |
   27|       |use anyhow::Result;
   28|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   29|       |use serde_json::Value;
   30|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
   31|       |use std::time::Duration;
   32|       |use tokio::sync::mpsc;
   33|       |
   34|       |use super::acp::CursorAcpClient;
   35|       |use super::permission::PermissionPolicy;
   36|       |use crate::acp::AcpClientTrait;
   37|       |use crate::backend::transport::{
   38|       |    AgentTransport, SessionConfig, SessionInfo, SessionUpdate, ToolResult,
   39|       |};
   40|       |
   41|       |/// Cursor ACP transport implementing the `AgentTransport` trait.
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// This is an adapter that wraps `CursorAcpClient` to provide a unified
   44|       |/// transport interface. It converts between the ACP notification format
   45|       |/// and `SessionUpdate` events.
   46|       |///
   47|       |/// # Permission Handling
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// The transport uses a `PermissionPolicy` to control which tools are
   50|       |/// allowed for each agent type. This is applied during ACP permission
   51|       |/// requests from the agent.
   52|       |pub struct CursorAcpTransport {
   53|       |    /// The wrapped ACP client (created on initialize)
   54|       |    client: Option<CursorAcpClient>,
   55|       |    /// Permission policy for tool access control
   56|       |    permission_policy: PermissionPolicy,
   57|       |    /// Request timeout for ACP operations
   58|       |    request_timeout: Duration,
   59|       |    /// Workspace path (stored for session creation)
   60|       |    workspace: PathBuf,
   61|       |    /// Model to use (stored for session creation)
   62|       |    model: Option<String>,
   63|       |    /// MCP servers to configure
   64|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   65|       |    mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
   66|       |    /// Sender for converted session updates
   67|       |    notification_tx: mpsc::Sender<SessionUpdate>,
   68|       |    /// Receiver for converted session updates (taken once by caller)
   69|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   70|       |    notification_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>>,
   71|       |}
   72|       |
   73|       |impl CursorAcpTransport {
   74|       |    /// Create a new ACP transport with the given permission policy.
   75|       |    ///
   76|       |    /// The transport is not yet connected; call `initialize()` to spawn
   77|       |    /// the ACP client.
   78|       |    #[must_use]
   79|     13|    pub fn new(permission_policy: PermissionPolicy, request_timeout: Duration) -> Self {
   80|     13|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(100);
   81|     13|        Self {
   82|     13|            client: None,
   83|     13|            permission_policy,
   84|     13|            request_timeout,
   85|     13|            workspace: PathBuf::new(),
   86|     13|            model: None,
   87|     13|            mcp_servers: Vec::new(),
   88|     13|            notification_tx: tx,
   89|     13|            notification_rx: Some(rx),
   90|     13|        }
   91|     13|    }
   92|       |

   97|      1|        Self::new(PermissionPolicy::for_orchestrator(), timeout)
   98|      1|    }

  129|       |    /// Convert an ACP notification to a SessionUpdate.
  130|     14|    fn convert_notification(notification: &Value, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionUpdate> {
  131|       |        // ACP notifications have method: "session/update" and params with the update data
  132|     14|        let params = notification.get("params")?;
                          ^13                                ^1
  133|     13|        let update_type = params.get("type")?.as_str()?;

  134|       |

  136|     11|            "text" => {

                                                                 ^0        ^0
  138|      2|                Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  139|      2|                    session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  140|      2|                    content: content.to_string(),

  142|       |            }
  143|      9|            "tool_use" => {
  144|      2|                let id = params.get("id")?.as_str()?;

  146|      2|                let input = params.get("input")?.clone();

  147|      2|                Some(SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  148|      2|                    session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  149|      2|                    tool_use_id: id.to_string(),
  150|      2|                    tool_name: name.to_string(),
  151|      2|                    input,
  152|      2|                })
  153|       |            }

  183|       |            _ => {
  184|       |                // Unknown type - return as raw

  188|      1|                })
  189|       |            }
  190|       |        }

  194|       |#[async_trait]
  195|       |impl AgentTransport for CursorAcpTransport {
  196|       |    /// Initialize the ACP connection.
  197|       |    ///
  198|       |    /// Spawns the Cursor ACP client and performs authentication.
  199|      0|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  200|       |        tracing::debug!("Initializing Cursor ACP transport");
  201|       |
  202|       |        // Spawn the ACP client with the configured permission policy
  203|       |        let mut client = CursorAcpClient::spawn_with_policy(
  204|       |            None, // cache_dir is ignored by Cursor
  205|       |            self.request_timeout,
  206|       |            self.permission_policy.clone(),
  207|       |        )
  208|       |        .await?;
  209|       |
  210|       |        // Initialize the ACP connection (includes Cursor-specific authentication)
  211|       |        client.initialize().await?;
  212|       |
  213|       |        self.client = Some(client);
  214|       |        tracing::info!("✅ Cursor ACP transport initialized");
  215|       |        Ok(())
  216|      0|    }
  217|       |
  218|       |    /// Create a new agent session.
  219|       |    ///
  220|       |    /// Uses the stored workspace, model, and MCP server configuration.
  221|      2|    async fn create_session(&mut self, config: SessionConfig) -> Result<SessionInfo> {
  222|      2|        let client = self.client.as_mut().ok_or_else(|| {
  223|      2|            anyhow::anyhow!("Transport not initialized. Call initialize() first.")
  224|      2|        })?;
  225|       |
  226|       |        // Create session with the provided configuration
  227|       |        let response = client
  228|       |            .session_new(
  229|       |                &config.model,
  230|       |                config.mcp_servers,
  231|       |                &config.workspace,
  232|       |                config.mode,
  233|       |            )
  234|       |            .await?;
  235|       |
  236|       |        let session_id = response.session_id;
  237|       |
  238|       |        // Start background task to convert ACP notifications to SessionUpdates
  239|       |        if let Some(mut acp_rx) = client.take_notification_rx() {
  240|       |            let tx = self.notification_tx.clone();
  241|       |            let session_id_clone = session_id.clone();
  242|       |
  243|      0|            tokio::spawn(async move {
  244|      0|                while let Some(notification) = acp_rx.recv().await {
  245|       |                    // Convert ACP notification to SessionUpdate
  246|      0|                    if let Some(update) =
  247|      0|                        CursorAcpTransport::convert_notification(&notification, &session_id_clone)
  248|       |                    {
  249|      0|                        if tx.send(update).await.is_err() {
  250|      0|                            tracing::debug!(
  251|       |                                "Notification receiver dropped, stopping ACP notification reader"
  252|       |                            );
  253|      0|                            break;
  254|      0|                        }
  255|      0|                    }
  256|       |                }
  257|      0|            });
  258|       |        }
  259|       |
  260|       |        Ok(SessionInfo::new(session_id))
  261|      2|    }
  262|       |
  263|       |    /// Send a prompt to the agent.
  264|      1|    async fn send_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {
  265|      1|        let client = self.client.as_mut().ok_or_else(|| {
  266|      1|            anyhow::anyhow!("Transport not initialized. Call initialize() first.")
  267|      1|        })?;
  268|       |
  269|       |        client.session_prompt(session_id, prompt).await
  270|      1|    }
  271|       |
  272|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for streaming updates.
  273|      2|    fn take_notifications(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>> {
  274|      2|        self.notification_rx.take()
  275|      2|    }
  276|       |
  277|       |    /// Respond to a tool call with the result.
  278|       |    ///
  279|       |    /// For ACP transport, this sends a tool response via the ACP protocol.
  280|       |    /// Note: Currently uses recv() to handle tool responses inline.
  281|       |    ///
  282|       |    /// Tool responses are handled internally by the ACP client via its permission system.
  283|       |    /// This method exists for trait compatibility but does not need to send responses
  284|       |    /// externally since the ACP protocol manages tool execution permissions directly.
  285|       |    async fn respond_to_tool(
  286|       |        &mut self,
  287|       |        _session_id: &str,
  288|       |        _tool_use_id: &str,
  289|       |        _result: ToolResult,
  290|      1|    ) -> Result<()> {
  291|       |        // ACP client handles tool responses internally via its permission system.
  292|       |        // This no-op implementation is intentional for trait compatibility.
  293|       |        tracing::trace!("respond_to_tool called on ACP transport (handled internally)");
  294|       |        Ok(())
  295|      1|    }
  296|       |
  297|       |    /// Shutdown the transport.
  298|      1|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  299|       |        tracing::debug!("Shutting down Cursor ACP transport");
  300|       |        if let Some(mut client) = self.client.take() {
  301|       |            client.shutdown().await?;
  302|       |        }
  303|       |        Ok(())
  304|      1|    }
  305|       |
  306|       |    /// Receive the next raw notification (legacy compatibility).
  307|       |    ///
  308|       |    /// This delegates to `CursorAcpClient::recv()` for backward compatibility
  309|       |    /// with code that polls for notifications.
  310|      0|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  311|      0|        let client = self.client.as_mut().ok_or_else(|| {
  312|      0|            anyhow::anyhow!("Transport not initialized. Call initialize() first.")
  313|      0|        })?;
  314|       |        client.recv().await
  315|      0|    }
  316|       |
  317|       |    /// Take the raw notification receiver (legacy compatibility).
  318|       |    ///
  319|       |    /// This delegates to `CursorAcpClient::take_notification_rx()` for backward
  320|       |    /// compatibility with the existing notification routing pattern.
  321|      0|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  322|      0|        self.client.as_mut().and_then(|c| c.take_notification_rx())
  323|      0|    }
  324|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/auth.rs:
    1|       |//! Cursor CLI authentication checking.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides authentication verification for the Cursor CLI.
    4|       |//! Authentication can be done via:
    5|       |//! - `CURSOR_API_KEY` environment variable
    6|       |//! - `CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable
    7|       |//! - Interactive login (`agent login` which stores credentials in `~/.cursor/`)
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! Note: This is a sanity check only. The actual authentication happens via
   10|       |//! the ACP `authenticate` call during client initialization.
   11|       |
   12|       |use anyhow::{bail, Result};
   13|       |use std::env;
   14|       |use std::path::Path;
   15|       |
   16|       |/// Check if Cursor CLI is authenticated.
   17|       |///
   18|       |/// This function checks for authentication in the following order:
   19|       |/// 1. `CURSOR_API_KEY` environment variable
   20|       |/// 2. `CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable  
   21|       |/// 3. `~/.cursor/` directory existence (indicates `agent login` was run)
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// Returns `Ok(())` if any authentication method is available, or an error
   24|       |/// with a helpful message if not authenticated.
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// # Errors
   27|       |///
   28|       |/// Returns an error if no authentication method is detected, with guidance
   29|       |/// on how to authenticate.
   30|       |///
   31|       |/// # Example
   32|       |///
   33|       |/// ```ignore
   34|       |/// use paperboat::backend::cursor::auth::check_cursor_auth;
   35|       |///
   36|       |/// fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   37|       |///     check_cursor_auth()?;
   38|       |///     println!("Cursor authentication is available");
   39|       |///     Ok(())
   40|       |/// }
   41|       |/// ```
   42|      2|pub fn check_cursor_auth() -> Result<()> {
   43|       |    // Check for API key or auth token in environment (highest priority)
   44|      2|    if env::var("CURSOR_API_KEY").is_ok() {
   45|      1|        return Ok(());
   46|      1|    }
   47|       |
   48|      1|    if env::var("CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN").is_ok() {
   49|      0|        return Ok(());
   50|      1|    }
   51|       |
   52|       |    // Check for ~/.cursor/ directory as fallback (indicates interactive login)
   53|      1|    if cursor_config_dir_exists() {
   54|      1|        return Ok(());
   55|      0|    }
   56|       |
   57|       |    // No authentication method found
   58|      0|    bail!(
   59|       |        "Cursor CLI is not authenticated.\n\n\
   60|       |        Please authenticate using one of these methods:\n\
   61|       |        • Set the CURSOR_API_KEY environment variable\n\
   62|       |        • Set the CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable\n\
   63|       |        • Run 'agent login' to authenticate interactively"
   64|       |    );
   65|      2|}
   66|       |
   67|       |/// Check if the Cursor config directory exists.
   68|       |///
   69|       |/// On Unix systems, this is `~/.cursor/`.
   70|       |/// On Windows, this is `$env:USERPROFILE\.cursor` or `%USERPROFILE%\.cursor`.
   71|       |///
   72|       |/// The presence of this directory indicates that `agent login` has been run
   73|       |/// at some point.
   74|      1|fn cursor_config_dir_exists() -> bool {
   75|      1|    let config_dir = get_cursor_config_dir();
   76|      1|    Path::new(&config_dir).exists()
   77|      1|}
   78|       |
   79|       |/// Get the path to the Cursor config directory.
   80|       |///
   81|       |/// Uses shellexpand for tilde expansion on Unix.
   82|       |/// On Windows, uses USERPROFILE environment variable.
   83|      2|fn get_cursor_config_dir() -> String {
   84|       |    // On Windows, shellexpand's tilde expansion uses USERPROFILE,
   85|       |    // so this works cross-platform
   86|      2|    shellexpand::tilde("~/.cursor").to_string()
   87|      2|}
   88|       |
   89|       |#[cfg(test)]
   90|       |mod tests {
   91|       |    use super::*;
   92|       |    use std::env;
   93|       |
   94|       |    // Helper to temporarily set an environment variable for a test
   95|       |    struct EnvGuard {
   96|       |        key: &'static str,
   97|       |        original: Option<String>,
   98|       |    }
   99|       |
  100|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  101|      2|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  102|      2|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  103|      2|            env::set_var(key, value);
  104|      2|            Self { key, original }
  105|      2|        }
  106|       |
  107|      2|        fn remove(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  108|      2|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  109|      2|            env::remove_var(key);
  110|      2|            Self { key, original }
  111|      2|        }
  112|       |    }
  113|       |
  114|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  115|      4|        fn drop(&mut self) {
  116|      4|            match &self.original {
  117|      0|                Some(val) => env::set_var(self.key, val),
  118|      4|                None => env::remove_var(self.key),
  119|       |            }
  120|      4|        }
  121|       |    }

    1|       |//! Cursor cache directory management.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles setting up Cursor-specific cache directories with
    4|       |//! the appropriate `cli-config.json` configuration for tool permissions.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! **Note:** Currently, `agent acp` does not support a `--config-dir` flag,
    7|       |//! so the cache directories are set up but not actually used. The config
    8|       |//! files are written to preserve the structure for future Cursor CLI versions
    9|       |//! that may support this feature.
   10|       |
   11|       |use crate::backend::AgentCacheType;
   12|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   13|       |use serde_json::json;
   14|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   15|       |
   16|       |/// Base path for paperboat's cache directories.
   17|       |const CACHE_BASE: &str = "~/.paperboat";
   18|       |
   19|       |/// Set up a Cursor cache directory for a specific agent type.
   20|       |///
   21|       |/// Creates the cache directory if it doesn't exist and writes the
   22|       |/// `cli-config.json` file with the appropriate permissions configuration.
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// # Arguments
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// * `agent_type` - The type of agent (Orchestrator, Planner, or Worker)
   27|       |/// * `denied_tools` - List of tool names to deny for this agent
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// # Returns
   30|       |///
   31|       |/// The path to the configured cache directory. For Worker type, returns
   32|       |/// an empty PathBuf since workers use the default cache.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Config Format
   35|       |///
   36|       |/// Cursor uses `cli-config.json` with a `permissions` object:
   37|       |/// ```json
   38|       |/// {
   39|       |///     "version": 1,
   40|       |///     "permissions": {
   41|       |///         "allow": [],
   42|       |///         "deny": ["tool1", "tool2"]
   43|       |///     }
   44|       |/// }
   45|       |/// ```
   46|       |///
   47|       |/// # Errors
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// Returns an error if:
   50|       |/// - The cache directory cannot be created
   51|       |/// - The `cli-config.json` file cannot be written
   52|      1|pub fn setup_cursor_cache(agent_type: AgentCacheType, denied_tools: &[&str]) -> Result<PathBuf> {
   53|      1|    let cache_name = match agent_type {
                      ^0
   54|      0|        AgentCacheType::Orchestrator => "cursor-orchestrator",
   55|      0|        AgentCacheType::Planner => "cursor-planner",
   56|      1|        AgentCacheType::Worker => return Ok(PathBuf::new()),
   57|       |    };
   58|       |
   59|      0|    let cache_dir = shellexpand::tilde(&format!("{}/{}", CACHE_BASE, cache_name)).to_string();
   60|      0|    let cache_path = Path::new(&cache_dir);
   61|       |
   62|       |    // Create directory if needed
   63|      0|    if !cache_path.exists() {
   64|      0|        std::fs::create_dir_all(cache_path)
   65|      0|            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to create cache directory: {}", cache_dir))?;
   66|      0|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    // Write cli-config.json with permissions (Cursor format)
   69|      0|    let config = json!({
   70|      0|        "version": 1,
   71|      0|        "permissions": {
   72|      0|            "allow": [],
   73|      0|            "deny": denied_tools
   74|       |        }
   75|       |    });
   76|       |
   77|      0|    let config_path = cache_path.join("cli-config.json");
   78|      0|    std::fs::write(&config_path, serde_json::to_string_pretty(&config)?).with_context(|| {
   79|      0|        format!(
   80|       |            "Failed to write cli-config.json to {}",
   81|      0|            config_path.display()
   82|       |        )
   83|      0|    })?;
   84|       |
   85|      0|    Ok(cache_path.to_path_buf())
   86|      1|}
   87|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/cli.rs:
    1|       |//! Cursor CLI transport implementation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `CursorCliTransport`, a transport that uses Cursor's
    4|       |//! non-interactive CLI mode (`agent --print`) instead of ACP. This is the
    5|       |//! preferred transport for Cursor because ACP mode has broken MCP support.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # CLI Mode vs ACP Mode
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! - **CLI Mode** (`agent --print`): Properly loads MCP servers from `~/.cursor/mcp.json`
   10|       |//! - **ACP Mode** (`agent acp`): MCP tools don't work (Cursor bug, no ETA for fix)
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! # Output Format
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! The CLI outputs newline-delimited JSON when using `--output-format stream-json`:
   15|       |//! ```json
   16|       |//! {"type":"text","content":"I'll help you..."}
   17|       |//! {"type":"tool_use","id":"call_123","name":"paperboat-create_task","input":{...}}
   18|       |//! {"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"call_123","content":"Task created"}
   19|       |//! {"type":"result","subtype":"success","session_id":"abc-123",...}
   20|       |//! ```
   21|       |//!
   22|       |//! # Session Resumption
   23|       |//!
   24|       |//! Multi-turn conversations use the `--resume <session_id>` flag to continue
   25|       |//! a previous session. The session_id comes from the `result` message at the
   26|       |//! end of each turn.
   27|       |
   28|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   29|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   30|       |use serde::Deserialize;
   31|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   32|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
   33|       |use std::process::Stdio;
   34|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
   35|       |use tokio::process::{Child, Command};
   36|       |use tokio::sync::mpsc;
   37|       |use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
   38|       |
   39|       |use super::permission::PermissionPolicy;
   40|       |use crate::backend::transport::{
   41|       |    AgentTransport, SessionConfig, SessionInfo, SessionUpdate, ToolResult,
   42|       |};
   43|       |
   44|       |/// Cursor CLI transport using `agent --print` mode.
   45|       |///
   46|       |/// This transport spawns separate `agent` processes for each prompt, using
   47|       |/// the `--resume` flag for multi-turn conversations. It properly loads MCP
   48|       |/// servers from `~/.cursor/mcp.json`, unlike the ACP transport.
   49|       |///
   50|       |/// # Lifecycle
   51|       |///
   52|       |/// 1. `initialize()` - No-op (CLI doesn't need initialization)
   53|       |/// 2. `create_session()` - Stores config, returns placeholder session
   54|       |/// 3. `send_prompt()` - Spawns `agent --print` process, streams output
   55|       |/// 4. `take_notifications()` - Returns receiver for streaming updates
   56|       |/// 5. `shutdown()` - Kills any running process
   57|       |pub struct CursorCliTransport {
   58|       |    /// Workspace directory for the agent
   59|       |    workspace: PathBuf,
   60|       |    /// Model to use for prompts
   61|       |    model: String,
   62|       |    /// Agent type for MCP server selection
   63|       |    agent_type: String,
   64|       |    /// Socket path for MCP communication (set from SessionConfig)
   65|       |    socket_path: Option<String>,
   66|       |    /// Unique ID for this session to prevent MCP server caching.
   67|       |    /// Each session gets a unique ID that's used in the MCP server name.
   68|       |    session_unique_id: String,
   69|       |    /// Permission policy for tool filtering
   70|       |    permission_policy: PermissionPolicy,
   71|       |    /// Current session ID (from last successful prompt)
   72|       |    current_session_id: Option<String>,
   73|       |    /// Sender for streaming updates
   74|       |    notification_tx: mpsc::Sender<SessionUpdate>,
   75|       |    /// Receiver for streaming updates.
   76|       |    /// Used by `recv()` for legacy polling, or can be taken once by `take_notifications()`.
   77|       |    notification_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>>,
   78|       |    /// Currently running agent process (if any)
   79|       |    current_process: Option<Child>,
   80|       |    /// Background task reading stdout
   81|       |    reader_task: Option<JoinHandle<()>>,
   82|       |}
   83|       |
   84|       |/// Parsed output line from CLI stream-json format.
   85|       |///
   86|       |/// Cursor CLI outputs various message types:
   87|       |/// - `thinking` with `subtype: "delta"` for streaming thought tokens
   88|       |/// - `thinking` with `subtype: "completed"` when thinking is done
   89|       |/// - `assistant` with the final response message
   90|       |/// - `tool_use` for tool invocations
   91|       |/// - `tool_result` for tool outputs
   92|       |/// - `result` for completion status
   93|       |/// - `system`, `user` for metadata (ignored)
   94|       |///
   95|       |/// Note: We use `#[serde(flatten)]` to capture any extra fields as `Value` since the
   96|       |/// Cursor CLI may add new fields like `timestamp_ms`, `apiKeySource`, etc.
   97|       |#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   98|       |#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   99|       |enum CliOutputLine {
  100|       |    /// Text content from the agent (legacy format, may not be used by Cursor)
  101|       |    Text {
  102|       |        content: String,
  103|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  104|       |        _extra: Value,
  105|       |    },
  106|       |    /// Thinking/reasoning tokens (streaming delta or completed)
  107|       |    Thinking {
  108|       |        #[serde(default)]
  109|       |        subtype: Option<String>,
  110|       |        #[serde(default)]
  111|       |        text: Option<String>,
  112|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  113|       |        _extra: Value,
  114|       |    },
  115|       |    /// Final assistant response message
  116|       |    Assistant {
  117|       |        message: AssistantMessage,
  118|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  119|       |        _extra: Value,
  120|       |    },
  121|       |    /// Tool use request
  122|       |    ToolUse {
  123|       |        id: String,
  124|       |        name: String,
  125|       |        input: Value,
  126|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  127|       |        _extra: Value,
  128|       |    },
  129|       |    /// Tool result
  130|       |    ToolResult {
  131|       |        tool_use_id: String,
  132|       |        content: String,
  133|       |        #[serde(default)]
  134|       |        is_error: bool,
  135|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  136|       |        _extra: Value,
  137|       |    },
  138|       |    /// Final result with session info
  139|       |    Result {
  140|       |        #[serde(default)]
  141|       |        subtype: Option<String>,
  142|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
  143|       |        #[serde(default)]
  144|       |        result: Option<String>,
  145|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  146|       |        _extra: Value,
  147|       |    },
  148|       |    /// System initialization message (ignored)
  149|       |    System {
  150|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  151|       |        _extra: Value,
  152|       |    },
  153|       |    /// User message echo (ignored)
  154|       |    User {
  155|       |        #[serde(flatten)]
  156|       |        _extra: Value,
  157|       |    },
  158|       |}
  159|       |
  160|       |/// Assistant message structure from Cursor CLI
  161|       |#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
  162|       |struct AssistantMessage {
  163|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  164|       |    role: String,
  165|       |    content: Vec<ContentBlock>,
  166|       |}
  167|       |
  168|       |/// Content block within a message
  169|       |#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
  170|       |#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
  171|       |enum ContentBlock {
  172|       |    Text { text: String },
  173|       |    // Tool use blocks are handled separately via ToolUse type
  174|       |    #[serde(other)]
  175|       |    Other,
  176|       |}
  177|       |
  178|       |impl CursorCliTransport {
  179|       |    /// Create a new CLI transport with the given agent type and permission policy.
  180|       |    ///
  181|       |    /// # Arguments
  182|       |    ///
  183|       |    /// * `agent_type` - The type of agent ("planner", "orchestrator", "implementer")
  184|       |    /// * `permission_policy` - Policy for filtering tool calls
  185|       |    #[must_use]
  186|     61|    pub fn new(agent_type: impl Into<String>, permission_policy: PermissionPolicy) -> Self {
  187|     61|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(100);
  188|       |        // Generate a unique ID for this transport instance to prevent MCP server caching.
  189|       |        // Use first 8 chars of UUID for readability while maintaining sufficient uniqueness.
  190|     61|        let session_unique_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string();
  191|     61|        Self {
  192|     61|            workspace: PathBuf::new(),
  193|     61|            model: String::new(),
  194|     61|            agent_type: agent_type.into(),
  195|     61|            socket_path: None,
  196|     61|            session_unique_id,
  197|     61|            permission_policy,
  198|     61|            current_session_id: None,
  199|     61|            notification_tx: tx,
  200|     61|            notification_rx: Some(rx),
  201|     61|            current_process: None,
  202|     61|            reader_task: None,
  203|     61|        }
  204|     61|    }
  205|       |
  206|       |    /// Create a transport for orchestrator agents.
  207|       |    #[must_use]
  208|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  209|      8|    pub fn for_orchestrator() -> Self {
  210|      8|        Self::new("orchestrator", PermissionPolicy::for_orchestrator())
  211|      8|    }
  212|       |
  213|       |    /// Create a transport for planner agents.
  214|       |    #[must_use]
  215|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  216|      4|    pub fn for_planner() -> Self {
  217|      4|        Self::new("planner", PermissionPolicy::for_planner())
  218|      4|    }
  219|       |
  220|       |    /// Create a transport for implementer agents.
  221|       |    #[must_use]
  222|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  223|     41|    pub fn for_implementer() -> Self {
  224|     41|        Self::new("implementer", PermissionPolicy::for_implementer())
  225|     41|    }
  226|       |
  227|       |    /// Parse a line of stream-json output and convert to SessionUpdate.
  228|     43|    fn parse_output_line(&self, line: &str, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionUpdate> {
  229|     43|        let parsed: Result<CliOutputLine, _> = serde_json::from_str(line);
  230|       |
  231|     36|        match parsed {
  232|      6|            Ok(CliOutputLine::Text { content, .. }) => Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  233|      6|                session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  234|      6|                content,
  235|      6|            }),
  236|      2|            Ok(CliOutputLine::Thinking { subtype, text, .. }) => {
  237|       |                // Only emit text for delta subtypes (streaming tokens)
  238|       |                // Ignore "completed" subtypes
  239|      2|                if subtype.as_deref() == Some("delta") {
  240|      1|                    if let Some(text) = text {
  241|      1|                        return Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  242|      1|                            session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  243|      1|                            content: text,
  244|      1|                        });
  245|      0|                    }
  246|      1|                }
  247|      1|                None
  248|       |            }
  249|      1|            Ok(CliOutputLine::Assistant { message, .. }) => {
  250|       |                // Extract text from the message content blocks
  251|      1|                let text: String = message
  252|      1|                    .content
  253|      1|                    .iter()
  254|      1|                    .filter_map(|block| match block {
  255|      1|                        ContentBlock::Text { text } => Some(text.as_str()),
  256|      0|                        ContentBlock::Other => None,
  257|      1|                    })
  258|      1|                    .collect::<Vec<_>>()
  259|      1|                    .join("");
  260|       |
  261|      1|                if !text.is_empty() {
  262|      1|                    Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  263|      1|                        session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  264|      1|                        content: format!("\n\n**Response:**\n{}", text),
  265|      1|                    })
  266|       |                } else {
  267|      0|                    None
  268|       |                }
  269|       |            }

  271|     16|                id, name, input, ..

  273|       |                // Apply permission policy filtering
  274|     16|                if !self.permission_policy.should_allow(&name) {
  275|      4|                    tracing::warn!(
  276|       |                        "🚫 CLI transport filtering tool call: {} (denied by policy)",
  277|       |                        name
  278|       |                    );
  279|      4|                    return None;
  280|     12|                }
  281|     12|                Some(SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  282|     12|                    session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  283|     12|                    tool_use_id: id,
  284|     12|                    tool_name: name,
  285|     12|                    input,
  286|     12|                })

  289|      4|                tool_use_id,
  290|      4|                content,
  291|      4|                is_error,

  294|      4|                session_id: session_id.to_string(),
  295|      4|                tool_use_id,
  296|      4|                content,
  297|      4|                is_success: !is_error,
  298|      4|            }),

  320|      3|                        session_id: Some(session_id.to_string()),

  330|       |    /// Convert a SessionUpdate to the JSON format expected by the legacy notification router.
  331|       |    ///
  332|       |    /// This mimics the ACP notification format so the existing routing code can handle
  333|       |    /// messages from CLI transport the same way it handles ACP transport messages.
  334|       |    ///
  335|       |    /// The session handler (`handle_worker_session_message`) expects:
  336|       |    /// - Text: `update.content.text` for the message content
  337|       |    /// - ToolCall: `update.title` for the tool name
  338|       |    /// - ToolResult: `update.title`, `update.isError`, `update.content.text`
  339|      1|    fn session_update_to_json(update: SessionUpdate) -> Value {
  340|      1|        match update {
  341|       |            SessionUpdate::Text {
  342|      0|                session_id,
  343|      0|                content,
  344|       |            } => {
  345|       |                // Session handler expects: update.content.text
  346|      0|                json!({
  347|      0|                    "method": "session/update",
  348|      0|                    "params": {
  349|      0|                        "session_id": session_id,
  350|      0|                        "update": {
  351|      0|                            "type": "agent_message_chunk",
  352|      0|                            "content": {
  353|      0|                                "text": content
  354|       |                            }
  355|       |                        }
  356|       |                    }
  357|       |                })
  358|       |            }
  359|       |            SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  360|      0|                session_id,
  361|      0|                tool_use_id,
  362|      0|                tool_name,
  363|      0|                input,
  364|       |            } => {
  365|       |                // Session handler expects: update.title for the tool name
  366|      0|                json!({
  367|      0|                    "method": "session/update",
  368|      0|                    "params": {
  369|      0|                        "session_id": session_id,
  370|      0|                        "update": {
  371|      0|                            "type": "tool_call",
  372|      0|                            "title": tool_name,
  373|      0|                            "tool_use_id": tool_use_id,
  374|      0|                            "input": input
  375|       |                        }
  376|       |                    }
  377|       |                })
  378|       |            }
  379|       |            SessionUpdate::ToolResult {
  380|      1|                session_id,
  381|      1|                tool_use_id,
  382|      1|                content,
  383|      1|                is_success,
  384|       |            } => {
  385|       |                // Session handler expects: update.title, update.isError, update.content.text
  386|      1|                json!({
  387|      1|                    "method": "session/update",
  388|      1|                    "params": {
  389|      1|                        "session_id": session_id,
  390|      1|                        "update": {
  391|      1|                            "type": "tool_result",
  392|      1|                            "title": tool_use_id,
  393|      1|                            "isError": !is_success,
  394|      1|                            "content": {
  395|      1|                                "text": content
  396|       |                            }
  397|       |                        }
  398|       |                    }
  399|       |                })
  400|       |            }
  401|       |            SessionUpdate::Completion {
  402|      0|                session_id,
  403|      0|                result,
  404|      0|                success,
  405|       |            } => {
  406|       |                // Session handler expects: type "complete" to signal session end
  407|      0|                json!({
  408|      0|                    "method": "session/update",
  409|      0|                    "params": {
  410|      0|                        "session_id": session_id,
  411|      0|                        "update": {
  412|      0|                            "type": "complete",
  413|      0|                            "result": result,
  414|      0|                            "success": success
  415|       |                        }
  416|       |                    }
  417|       |                })
  418|       |            }
  419|      0|            SessionUpdate::Raw { session_id, data } => {
  420|      0|                json!({
  421|      0|                    "method": "session/update",
  422|      0|                    "params": {
  423|      0|                        "session_id": session_id.unwrap_or_default(),
  424|      0|                        "update": data
  425|       |                    }
  426|       |                })
  427|       |            }
  428|       |        }
  429|      1|    }

  431|       |    /// Spawn the agent process and start reading output.
  432|      2|    async fn spawn_agent(&mut self, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {

  435|       |        // We use a unique suffix to prevent Cursor from caching MCP server processes
  436|       |        // across different agent types/sessions

                                  ^1

  439|      1|                &self.agent_type,

  446|       |            );

  454|      1|            "--approve-mcps",

  457|      1|            "stream-json",

                          ^0

  496|      0|        }
  497|       |
  498|      0|        let stdout = child.stdout.take().context("Failed to get stdout")?;
  499|      0|        let stderr = child.stderr.take();

  505|      0|                let mut lines = reader.lines();

  509|      0|                    }
  510|       |                }
  511|      0|            });
  512|      0|        }

  604|       |    /// Kill the current process if running.

  622|       |    /// Initialize the transport.
  623|       |    ///

  625|       |    /// to establish. The actual agent process is spawned per-prompt.
  626|      3|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  627|       |        tracing::debug!("CLI transport initialized (no-op)");
  628|       |        Ok(())
  629|      3|    }
  630|       |
  631|       |    /// Create a new session with the given configuration.
  632|       |    ///
  633|       |    /// For CLI transport, this stores the configuration for later use and
  634|       |    /// returns a placeholder session ID. The actual session is created
  635|       |    /// when the first prompt is sent.
  636|      8|    async fn create_session(&mut self, config: SessionConfig) -> Result<SessionInfo> {
  637|       |        self.model = config.model;
  638|       |        self.workspace = PathBuf::from(&config.workspace);
  639|       |
  640|       |        // Extract socket path from MCP server config
  641|       |        // The MCP servers config contains: {"args": ["--mcp-server", "--socket", "<path>"], ...}
  642|       |        for server in &config.mcp_servers {
  643|      4|            if let Some(args) = server.get("args").and_then(|a| a.as_array()) {
  644|       |                for (i, arg) in args.iter().enumerate() {
  645|       |                    if arg.as_str() == Some("--socket") {
  646|      3|                        if let Some(socket) = args.get(i + 1).and_then(|s| s.as_str()) {
  647|       |                            self.socket_path = Some(socket.to_string());
  648|       |                            tracing::debug!("📦 Extracted socket path from MCP config: {}", socket);
  649|       |                            break;
  650|       |                        }
  651|       |                    }
  652|       |                }
  653|       |            }
  654|       |        }
  655|       |
  656|       |        // Generate an initial session ID (may be replaced by CLI's session ID)
  657|       |        let session_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
  658|       |        self.current_session_id = Some(session_id.clone());
  659|       |

  665|       |
  666|       |        Ok(SessionInfo::new(session_id))

  668|       |
  669|       |    /// Send a prompt to the agent.
  670|       |    ///
  671|       |    /// For CLI transport, this spawns a new `agent --print` process with
  672|       |    /// the prompt. For subsequent prompts in the same session, the
  673|       |    /// `--resume` flag is used to continue the conversation.
  674|      2|    async fn send_prompt(&mut self, _session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {

  676|       |        self.kill_process().await;

  678|       |        // Spawn new process
  679|       |        self.spawn_agent(prompt).await?;
  680|       |

  683|       |
  684|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for streaming updates.

  688|      3|    fn take_notifications(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>> {

  708|       |
  709|       |    /// Shutdown the transport.
  710|       |    ///
  711|       |    /// Kills any running agent process and cleans up resources.
  712|      1|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  713|       |        tracing::debug!("Shutting down Cursor CLI transport");
  714|       |        self.kill_process().await;
  715|       |        Ok(())
  716|      1|    }
  717|       |
  718|       |    /// Receive the next raw notification (legacy compatibility).
  719|       |    ///
  720|       |    /// Receives a `SessionUpdate` from the notification channel and converts
  721|       |    /// it to the raw JSON format expected by the legacy polling code.
  722|      1|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  723|       |        // Get the receiver - if it's been taken, return an error
  724|      1|        let rx = self.notification_rx.as_mut().ok_or_else(|| {
  725|      1|            anyhow::anyhow!(
  726|       |                "Notification receiver has been taken. Use take_notifications() only once."
  727|       |            )
  728|      1|        })?;
  729|       |
  730|       |        // Wait for the next update
  731|      0|        let update = rx.recv().await.ok_or_else(|| {
  732|      0|            anyhow::anyhow!("CLI transport channel closed - agent process may have finished")
  733|      0|        })?;
  734|       |
  735|       |        // Convert SessionUpdate to the JSON format expected by legacy code
  736|       |        Ok(Self::session_update_to_json(update))
  737|      1|    }
  738|       |
  739|       |    /// Returns the transport kind for this transport.
  740|       |    ///
  741|       |    /// CLI transport returns `TransportKind::Cli` to enable transport-specific
  742|       |    /// behavior like creating unique sockets to prevent MCP server caching.
  743|      3|    fn kind(&self) -> crate::backend::transport::TransportKind {
  744|      3|        crate::backend::transport::TransportKind::Cli
  745|      3|    }
  746|       |
  747|       |    /// Take the raw notification receiver (legacy compatibility).
  748|       |    ///
  749|       |    /// This converts the typed `SessionUpdate` channel to a raw `Value` channel
  750|       |    /// for compatibility with the existing notification routing system.
  751|       |    ///
  752|       |    /// A bridge task is spawned that converts each `SessionUpdate` to the JSON
  753|       |    /// format expected by the legacy code and forwards it to a new channel.
  754|      1|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  755|       |        // Take the typed receiver
  756|      1|        let typed_rx = self.notification_rx.take()?;
                                                                ^0
  757|       |
  758|       |        // Create a new channel for raw JSON values
  759|      1|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel::<Value>(100);
  760|       |
  761|       |        // Spawn a bridge task that converts SessionUpdate to Value
  762|      1|        tokio::spawn(async move {
  763|      1|            let mut typed_rx = typed_rx;
  764|      2|            while let Some(update) = typed_rx.recv().await {
                                         ^1
  765|       |                // Convert SessionUpdate to the JSON format expected by legacy code
  766|      1|                let json = Self::session_update_to_json(update);
  767|      1|                if tx.send(json).await.is_err() {
  768|       |                    // Receiver dropped, stop bridging
  769|      0|                    break;
  770|      1|                }
  771|       |            }
  772|      0|            tracing::debug!("CLI transport notification bridge task ended");
  773|      0|        });
  774|       |
  775|      1|        Some(rx)
  776|      1|    }
  777|       |}
  778|       |
  779|       |impl Drop for CursorCliTransport {
  780|     61|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  781|       |        // Abort reader task to prevent it from running after drop
  782|     61|        if let Some(task) = self.reader_task.take() {
                                  ^0
  783|      0|            task.abort();
  784|     61|        }
  785|       |
  786|       |        // Initiate process termination (can't wait in Drop since it's not async)
  787|     61|        if let Some(ref mut child) = self.current_process {
                                  ^0
  788|      0|            if let Err(e) = child.start_kill() {
  789|      0|                tracing::warn!("Failed to initiate kill of Cursor CLI process: {}", e);
  790|      0|            }
  791|     61|        }
  792|     61|    }
  793|       |}
  794|       |
  795|       |// ============================================================================
  796|       |// Tests
  797|       |// ============================================================================
  798|       |
  799|       |#[cfg(test)]
  800|       |mod tests {
  801|       |    use super::*;
  802|       |    use serial_test::serial;
  803|       |    use std::env;
  804|       |
  805|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  806|       |        key: &'static str,
  807|       |        original: Option<String>,
  808|       |    }
  809|       |
  810|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  811|      3|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  812|      3|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  813|      3|            env::set_var(key, value);
  814|      3|            Self { key, original }
  815|      3|        }
  816|       |    }
  817|       |
  818|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  819|      3|        fn drop(&mut self) {
  820|      3|            match &self.original {
  821|      3|                Some(value) => env::set_var(self.key, value),
  822|      0|                None => env::remove_var(self.key),
  823|       |            }
  824|      3|        }
  825|       |    }
  826|       |
  827|       |    // ========================================================================

  832|      1|    fn test_parse_text_output() {
  833|      1|        let transport = CursorCliTransport::for_implementer();
  834|      1|        let line = r#"{"type":"text","content":"Hello, world!"}"#;
  835|      1|        let update = transport.parse_output_line(line, "test-session");
  836|       |
  837|      1|        assert!(update.is_some());
  838|       |        if let Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  839|      1|            session_id,
  840|      1|            content,
  841|      1|        }) = update
  842|       |        {
  843|      1|            assert_eq!(session_id, "test-session");
  844|      1|            assert_eq!(content, "Hello, world!");
  845|       |        } else {
  846|      0|            panic!("Expected Text update");
  847|       |        }
  848|      1|    }
  849|       |
