/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/mcp_config.rs:
    1|       |//! Cursor MCP configuration management.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles writing Paperboat's MCP server configuration to
    4|       |//! Cursor's `~/.cursor/mcp.json` file so that Cursor agents can access
    5|       |//! our orchestration tools.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # Multi-Server Architecture
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! We register multiple MCP servers, one per agent type:
   10|       |//! - `paperboat-planner`: Exposes planner tools (set_goal, create_task, complete)
   11|       |//! - `paperboat-orchestrator`: Exposes orchestrator tools (spawn_agents, decompose, etc.)
   12|       |//! - `paperboat-implementer`: Exposes implementer tools (complete)
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! Before spawning an agent, we enable the appropriate server and disable others.
   15|       |//! This ensures each agent only sees the tools it should use.
   16|       |
   17|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   18|       |use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   19|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   20|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Agent types for MCP server registration.
   23|       |pub const AGENT_TYPES: &[&str] = &["planner", "orchestrator", "implementer"];
   24|       |
   25|       |/// Get the MCP server name for a given agent type.
   26|     12|pub fn mcp_server_name(agent_type: &str) -> String {
   27|     12|    format!("paperboat-{agent_type}")
   28|     12|}
   29|       |
   30|       |/// Get the path to Cursor's mcp.json config file.
   31|     12|pub fn cursor_mcp_config_path() -> Result<PathBuf> {
   32|     12|    let home = dirs::home_dir().context("Could not determine home directory")?;
                                                                                           ^0
   33|     12|    Ok(home.join(".cursor").join("mcp.json"))
   34|     12|}
   35|       |
   36|       |/// Cursor's MCP configuration file format.
   37|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   38|       |pub struct CursorMcpConfig {
   39|       |    /// Map of MCP server name to configuration.
   40|       |    #[serde(rename = "mcpServers", default)]
   41|       |    pub mcp_servers: HashMap<String, McpServerConfig>,
   42|       |}
   43|       |
   44|       |/// Configuration for a single MCP server.
   45|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   46|       |pub struct McpServerConfig {
   47|       |    /// Command to run the MCP server.
   48|       |    pub command: String,
   49|       |    /// Arguments to pass to the command.
   50|       |    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   51|       |    pub args: Vec<String>,
   52|       |    /// Environment variables to set.
   53|       |    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "HashMap::is_empty")]
   54|       |    pub env: HashMap<String, String>,
   55|       |}
   56|       |
   57|     17|fn read_or_create_mcp_config(config_path: &Path) -> Result<CursorMcpConfig> {
   58|     17|    if config_path.exists() {
   59|      8|        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(config_path)
   60|      8|            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read {}", config_path.display()))?;
                                           ^0                           ^0          ^0         ^0
   61|      8|        Ok(serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default())
   62|       |    } else {
   63|      9|        if let Some(parent) = config_path.parent() {
   64|      9|            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)
   65|      9|                .with_context(|| format!("Failed to create {}", parent.display()))?;
                                               ^1                             ^1     ^1         ^1
   66|      0|        }
   67|      8|        Ok(CursorMcpConfig::default())
   68|       |    }
   69|     17|}
   70|       |
   71|     14|fn write_mcp_config(config_path: &Path, config: &CursorMcpConfig) -> Result<()> {
   72|     14|    let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(config).context("Failed to serialize MCP config")?;
                                                                                                              ^0
   73|     14|    std::fs::write(config_path, content)
   74|     14|        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to write {}", config_path.display()))?;
                                       ^1                            ^1          ^1         ^1
   75|     13|    Ok(())
   76|     14|}
   77|       |
   78|      7|fn current_binary_path() -> Result<String> {
   79|      7|    Ok(std::env::current_exe()
   80|      7|        .context("Failed to get current executable path")?
                                                                       ^0
   81|      7|        .to_string_lossy()
   82|      7|        .to_string())
   83|      7|}
   84|       |
   85|     15|fn build_paperboat_server_config(
   86|     15|    binary_path: &str,
   87|     15|    agent_type: &str,
   88|     15|    socket_path: &str,
   89|     15|) -> McpServerConfig {
   90|     15|    let mut env = HashMap::new();
   91|     15|    env.insert("PAPERBOAT_AGENT_TYPE".to_string(), agent_type.to_string());
   92|       |
   93|     15|    McpServerConfig {
   94|     15|        command: binary_path.to_string(),
   95|     15|        args: vec![
   96|     15|            "--mcp-server".to_string(),
   97|     15|            "--socket".to_string(),
   98|     15|            socket_path.to_string(),
   99|     15|        ],
  100|     15|        env,
  101|     15|    }
  102|     15|}
  103|       |
  104|     14|fn agent_server_name(agent_type: &str, unique_suffix: Option<&str>) -> String {
  105|     14|    unique_suffix.map_or_else(
  106|      3|        || mcp_server_name(agent_type),
  107|     11|        |suffix| format!("paperboat-{agent_type}-{suffix}"),
  108|       |    )
  109|     14|}
  110|       |
  111|      4|fn remove_paperboat_servers(config: &mut CursorMcpConfig) {
  112|      4|    config
  113|      4|        .mcp_servers
  114|     11|        .retain(|name, _| !name.starts_with("paperboat-"));
                       ^4
  115|      4|}
  116|       |
  117|     12|fn configure_mcp_for_agent_at_path(
  118|     12|    config_path: &Path,
  119|     12|    binary_path: &str,
  120|     12|    agent_type: &str,
  121|     12|    socket_path: &str,
  122|     12|    unique_suffix: Option<&str>,
  123|     12|) -> Result<String> {
  124|     12|    let mut config = read_or_create_mcp_config(config_path)?;
                      ^11                                                ^1
  125|       |
  126|     11|    let server_name = agent_server_name(agent_type, unique_suffix);
  127|     11|    let mcp_config = build_paperboat_server_config(binary_path, agent_type, socket_path);
  128|       |
  129|       |    // Keep other Paperboat entries intact so concurrently running agents retain
  130|       |    // their own socket-backed MCP servers. Replacing the exact server name is
  131|       |    // enough to refresh stale config for retries of the same session.
  132|     11|    config.mcp_servers.insert(server_name.clone(), mcp_config);
  133|       |
  134|     11|    write_mcp_config(config_path, &config)?;
                                                        ^1
  135|     10|    Ok(server_name)
  136|     12|}
  137|       |
  138|      6|fn run_agent_mcp_enable(server_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
  139|      6|    let output = std::process::Command::new("agent")
                      ^4
  140|      6|        .args(["mcp", "enable", server_name])
  141|      6|        .output()
  142|      6|        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to run 'agent mcp enable': {}", e))?;
                                   ^2                                                         ^2
  143|       |
  144|      4|    if !output.status.success() {
  145|      0|        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
  146|      0|        tracing::warn!("Failed to enable {}: {}", server_name, stderr);
  147|      4|    }
  148|       |
  149|      4|    Ok(())
  150|      6|}
  151|       |
  152|       |/// Register all Paperboat MCP servers in Cursor's config.
  153|       |///
  154|       |/// This adds/updates entries for each agent type in `~/.cursor/mcp.json`:
  155|       |/// - `paperboat-planner`: For planner agents
  156|       |/// - `paperboat-orchestrator`: For orchestrator agents
  157|       |/// - `paperboat-implementer`: For implementer agents
  158|       |///
  159|       |/// Each server is configured with the appropriate `PAPERBOAT_AGENT_TYPE` env var
  160|       |/// so the MCP server exposes only the tools for that agent type.
  161|       |///
  162|       |/// Note: This function is currently unused as we use `enable_mcp_for_agent`
  163|       |/// for just-in-time MCP registration per agent type. Keeping for potential
  164|       |/// future use cases.
  165|       |///
  166|       |/// # Arguments
  167|       |///
  168|       |/// * `socket_path` - IPC address for MCP communication
  169|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
  170|      1|pub fn register_paperboat_mcp(socket_path: &str) -> Result<()> {
  171|      1|    let config_path = cursor_mcp_config_path()?;
                                                            ^0
  172|      1|    let binary_path = current_binary_path()?;
                                                         ^0
  173|      1|    let mut config = read_or_create_mcp_config(&config_path)?;
                                                                          ^0
  174|       |
  175|       |    // Register an MCP server for each agent type
  176|      3|    for agent_type in AGENT_TYPES {
  177|      3|        config.mcp_servers.insert(
  178|      3|            mcp_server_name(agent_type),
  179|      3|            build_paperboat_server_config(&binary_path, agent_type, socket_path),
  180|      3|        );
  181|      3|    }
  182|       |
  183|      1|    write_mcp_config(&config_path, &config)?;
                                                         ^0
  184|       |
  185|      1|    tracing::info!(
  186|       |        "📝 Registered Paperboat MCP servers in {}",
  187|      0|        config_path.display()
  188|       |    );
  189|       |
  190|      1|    Ok(())
  191|      1|}
  192|       |
  193|       |/// Remove all Paperboat MCP servers from Cursor's config.
  194|       |///
  195|       |/// Call this when Paperboat exits to clean up.
  196|      4|pub fn unregister_paperboat_mcp() -> Result<()> {
  197|      4|    let config_path = cursor_mcp_config_path()?;
                                                            ^0
  198|       |
  199|      4|    if !config_path.exists() {
  200|      1|        return Ok(());
  201|      3|    }
  202|       |
  203|      3|    let mut config = read_or_create_mcp_config(&config_path)?;
                                                                          ^0
  204|      3|    let original_len = config.mcp_servers.len();
  205|      3|    remove_paperboat_servers(&mut config);
  206|      3|    let removed = config.mcp_servers.len() != original_len;
  207|       |
  208|      3|    if removed {
  209|      2|        write_mcp_config(&config_path, &config)?;
                                                             ^0
  210|       |
  211|      2|        tracing::info!(
  212|       |            "🗑️ Removed Paperboat MCP servers from {}",
  213|      0|            config_path.display()
  214|       |        );
  215|      1|    }
  216|       |
  217|      3|    Ok(())
  218|      4|}
  219|       |
  220|       |/// Configure MCP for a specific agent type.
  221|       |///
  222|       |/// Each session can register its own uniquely named Paperboat MCP server. We
  223|       |/// intentionally preserve other Paperboat entries here so concurrently running
  224|       |/// agents do not lose the socket mapping for their already-approved MCP tool.
  225|       |/// Cleanup still removes all Paperboat entries when Paperboat exits.
  226|       |///
  227|       |/// # Arguments
  228|       |///
  229|       |/// * `agent_type` - The agent type ("planner", "orchestrator", "implementer")
  230|       |/// * `socket_path` - IPC address for MCP communication
  231|       |/// * `unique_suffix` - Optional unique suffix to prevent MCP server caching.
  232|       |///   When provided, creates a server name like `paperboat-implementer-abc123`.
  233|       |///   This forces Cursor to start a new MCP server process instead of reusing
  234|       |///   a cached one from a previous agent.
  235|      6|pub fn enable_mcp_for_agent(
  236|      6|    agent_type: &str,
  237|      6|    socket_path: &str,
  238|      6|    unique_suffix: Option<&str>,
  239|      6|) -> Result<()> {
  240|      6|    let config_path = cursor_mcp_config_path()?;
                                                            ^0
  241|      6|    let binary_path = current_binary_path()?;
                                                         ^0
  242|      6|    let server_name = configure_mcp_for_agent_at_path(
  243|      6|        &config_path,
  244|      6|        &binary_path,
  245|      6|        agent_type,
  246|      6|        socket_path,
  247|      6|        unique_suffix,
  248|      0|    )?;
  249|      6|    run_agent_mcp_enable(&server_name)?;
                                                    ^2
  250|       |
  251|      4|    tracing::debug!(
  252|       |        "✅ Configured MCP for {} agent: {}",
  253|       |        agent_type,
  254|       |        server_name
  255|       |    );
  256|      4|    Ok(())
  257|      6|}
  258|       |
  259|       |#[cfg(test)]
  260|       |mod tests {
  261|       |    use super::*;
  262|       |    use serial_test::serial;
  263|       |    use std::env;
  264|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  265|       |    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
  266|       |    use std::path::Path;
  267|       |    use tempfile::tempdir;
  268|       |
  269|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  270|       |        key: &'static str,
  271|       |        original: Option<String>,
  272|       |    }
  273|       |
  274|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  275|     14|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  276|     14|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  277|     14|            env::set_var(key, value);
  278|     14|            Self { key, original }
  279|     14|        }
  280|       |    }
  281|       |
  282|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  283|     14|        fn drop(&mut self) {
  284|     14|            match &self.original {
  285|     14|                Some(value) => env::set_var(self.key, value),
  286|      0|                None => env::remove_var(self.key),
  287|       |            }
  288|     14|        }
  289|       |    }
  290|       |
  291|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  292|      4|    fn prepend_path(dir: &Path) -> EnvGuard {
  293|      4|        let mut path_entries = vec![dir.display().to_string()];
  294|      4|        if let Ok(existing) = env::var("PATH") {
  295|      4|            path_entries.push(existing);
  296|      4|        }
                      ^0
  297|      4|        EnvGuard::set("PATH", &path_entries.join(":"))
  298|      4|    }
  299|       |
  300|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  301|      4|    fn write_agent_stub(dir: &Path, log_path: &Path) {
  302|      4|        let script_path = dir.join("agent");
  303|      4|        std::fs::write(
  304|      4|            &script_path,
  305|      4|            format!(
  306|       |                "#!/bin/sh\nprintf '%s\\n' \"$*\" >> \"{}\"\nexit 0\n",
  307|      4|                log_path.display()
  308|       |            ),
  309|       |        )
  310|      4|        .unwrap();
  311|       |
  312|      4|        let mut permissions = std::fs::metadata(&script_path).unwrap().permissions();
  313|      4|        permissions.set_mode(0o755);
  314|      4|        std::fs::set_permissions(&script_path, permissions).unwrap();
  315|      4|    }

  489|      1|    fn test_configure_mcp_propagates_write_failures() {
  490|      1|        let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
  491|      1|        let cursor_dir = temp.path().join(".cursor");
  492|      1|        std::fs::create_dir_all(&cursor_dir).unwrap();
  493|      1|        let config_path = cursor_dir.join("mcp.json");
  494|      1|        std::fs::write(&config_path, r#"{"mcpServers":{}}"#).unwrap();
  495|      1|        let mut permissions = std::fs::metadata(&config_path).unwrap().permissions();
  496|      1|        permissions.set_readonly(true);
  497|      1|        std::fs::set_permissions(&config_path, permissions.clone()).unwrap();
  498|       |
  499|      1|        let result = configure_mcp_for_agent_at_path(
  500|      1|            &config_path,
  501|      1|            "/paperboat",
  502|      1|            "planner",
  503|      1|            "/tmp/planner.sock",
  504|      1|            None,
  505|       |        );
  506|       |
  507|      1|        let mut restore = permissions;
  508|      1|        restore.set_readonly(false);
  509|      1|        std::fs::set_permissions(&config_path, restore).unwrap();
  510|       |
  511|      1|        assert!(result.is_err());
  512|      1|        assert!(result.unwrap_err().to_string().contains("Failed to write"));
  513|      1|    }

  567|       |    #[test]
  568|       |    #[serial]
  569|      1|    fn test_unregister_paperboat_mcp_removes_entries_and_preserves_unrelated() {
  570|      1|        let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
  571|      1|        let _home = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp.path().to_str().unwrap());
  572|      1|        let config_path = temp.path().join(".cursor/mcp.json");
  573|      1|        std::fs::create_dir_all(config_path.parent().unwrap()).unwrap();
  574|       |
  575|      1|        std::fs::write(
  576|      1|            &config_path,
  577|      1|            serde_json::json!({
  578|      1|                "mcpServers": {
  579|      1|                    "github": {
  580|      1|                        "command": "gh",
  581|      1|                        "args": ["mcp", "serve"]
  582|       |                    },
  583|      1|                    "paperboat-planner": {
  584|      1|                        "command": "/old/paperboat",
  585|      1|                        "args": ["--mcp-server"]
  586|       |                    },
  587|      1|                    "paperboat-implementer-session456": {
  588|      1|                        "command": "/old/paperboat",
  589|      1|                        "args": ["--mcp-server"]
  590|       |                    },
  591|      1|                    "another-tool": {
  592|      1|                        "command": "/usr/bin/tool",
  593|      1|                        "args": []
  594|       |                    }
  595|       |                }
  596|       |            })
  597|      1|            .to_string(),
  598|       |        )
  599|      1|        .unwrap();
  600|       |
  601|      1|        unregister_paperboat_mcp().unwrap();
  602|       |
  603|      1|        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path).unwrap();
  604|      1|        let config: CursorMcpConfig = serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap();
  605|       |
  606|       |        // Verify paperboat entries removed
  607|      2|        assert!(!config.mcp_servers.keys().any(|k| k.starts_with("paperboat-")));
                      ^1       ^1                        ^1
  608|       |        // Verify unrelated entries preserved
  609|      1|        assert!(config.mcp_servers.contains_key("github"));
  610|      1|        assert!(config.mcp_servers.contains_key("another-tool"));

    1|       |// Allow some clippy lints for this new module - can be cleaned up later
    2|       |#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
    3|       |#![allow(clippy::len_zero)]
    4|       |#![allow(clippy::uninlined_format_args)]

    6|       |
    7|       |//! Cursor CLI backend implementation.
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This module implements the [`Backend`] trait for Cursor's agent CLI.
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! # Authentication
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! Cursor supports multiple authentication methods:
   14|       |//! - `CURSOR_API_KEY` environment variable
   15|       |//! - `CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable
   16|       |//! - Interactive login via `agent login`
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! # Differences from Auggie
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! - Cursor requires an `authenticate` ACP call after `initialize`
   21|       |//! - Model discovery via `cursor-agent --list-models` with different ID format
   22|       |//! - Cache configuration uses `cli-config.json` with `permissions.deny` format
   23|       |
   24|       |pub mod acp;
   25|       |pub mod acp_transport;
   26|       |pub mod auth;
   27|       |pub mod cache;
   28|       |pub mod cli;
   29|       |pub mod mcp_config;
   30|       |mod models;
   31|       |pub mod permission;
   32|       |
   33|       |// Re-export commonly used items for convenience
   34|       |pub use acp_transport::CursorAcpTransport;
   35|       |pub use cli::CursorCliTransport;
   36|       |pub use permission::PermissionPolicy;
   37|       |

   47|       |use crate::models::ModelTier;
   48|       |
   49|       |/// Cursor CLI backend implementation.
   50|       |///
   51|       |/// This is a zero-sized type that implements the [`Backend`] trait for
   52|       |/// Cursor's agent infrastructure. Cursor uses the `agent` command-line tool
   53|       |/// with ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) for AI interactions.
   54|       |///
   55|       |/// # Key Differences from Auggie
   56|       |///
   57|       |/// - Requires an `authenticate` ACP call after `initialize` (handled by `CursorAcpClient`)
   58|       |/// - Does not support `agent model list` - uses hardcoded model defaults
   59|       |/// - Cache configuration uses `cli-config.json` with `permissions.deny` format
   60|       |///
   61|       |/// # Example
   62|       |///
   63|       |/// ```ignore
   64|       |/// use paperboat::backend::cursor::CursorBackend;
   65|       |/// use paperboat::backend::Backend;
   66|       |///
   67|       |/// let backend = CursorBackend::new();
   68|       |/// backend.check_auth()?;
   69|       |/// let models = backend.discover_models().await?;
   70|       |/// ```
   71|       |pub struct CursorBackend;
   72|       |
   73|       |impl CursorBackend {
   74|       |    /// Create a new Cursor backend instance.
   75|       |    #[must_use]
   76|     22|    pub const fn new() -> Self {
   77|     22|        Self
   78|     22|    }
   79|       |}
   80|       |
   81|       |impl Default for CursorBackend {
   82|      1|    fn default() -> Self {
   83|      1|        Self::new()
   84|      1|    }
   85|       |}
   86|       |
   87|       |#[async_trait]
   88|       |impl Backend for CursorBackend {
   89|      3|    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
   90|      3|        "cursor"
   91|      3|    }
   92|       |
   93|      0|    fn check_auth(&self) -> Result<()> {
   94|      0|        auth::check_cursor_auth()
   95|      0|    }
   96|       |
   97|       |    /// Discover available model tiers for Cursor.
   98|       |    ///
   99|       |    /// Runs `cursor-agent --list-models` and parses the output to discover
  100|       |    /// available tiers. Maps Cursor model IDs to ModelTier values.
  101|      0|    async fn available_tiers(&self) -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>> {
  102|       |        models::discover_cursor_tiers().await
  103|      0|    }
  104|       |
  105|      4|    fn resolve_tier(
  106|      4|        &self,
  107|      4|        tier: ModelTier,
  108|      4|        effort: Option<crate::models::EffortLevel>,
  109|      4|    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
  110|       |        use crate::models::EffortLevel;
  111|       |
  112|       |        // Cursor uses model IDs like "sonnet-4.6", "opus-4.6", "gpt-5.1-codex-mini"
  113|       |        //
  114|       |        // This returns a fallback chain of models to try in order.
  115|       |        // The chain includes multiple versions and effort variants to maximize
  116|       |        // the chance of finding an available model.
  117|       |
  118|      4|        let effort = effort.unwrap_or_default();
  119|       |
  120|       |        // Helper to build Claude fallback chain (only base and -thinking exist)
  121|      4|        let claude_chain = |versions: &[&str]| -> Vec<String> {
                                                                            ^2
  122|      2|            let mut chain = Vec::new();
  123|      4|            for v in versions {
                                   ^2
  124|      4|                match effort {
  125|      0|                    EffortLevel::High | EffortLevel::XHigh => {
  126|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-thinking"));
  127|      0|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  128|      0|                    }
  129|      4|                    EffortLevel::Low | EffortLevel::Medium => {
  130|      4|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  131|      4|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-thinking"));
  132|      4|                    }
  133|       |                }
  134|       |            }
  135|      2|            chain
  136|      2|        };
  137|       |
  138|       |        // Helper to build GPT/Codex fallback chain (full effort spectrum)
  139|      4|        let gpt_chain = |versions: &[&str]| -> Vec<String> {
                                                                         ^0
  140|      0|            let mut chain = Vec::new();
  141|      0|            for v in versions {
  142|      0|                match effort {
  143|      0|                    EffortLevel::XHigh => {
  144|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-xhigh"));
  145|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-high"));
  146|      0|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  147|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-low"));
  148|      0|                    }
  149|      0|                    EffortLevel::High => {
  150|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-high"));
  151|      0|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  152|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-xhigh"));
  153|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-low"));
  154|      0|                    }
  155|      0|                    EffortLevel::Medium => {
  156|      0|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  157|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-high"));
  158|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-low"));
  159|      0|                    }
  160|      0|                    EffortLevel::Low => {
  161|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-low"));
  162|      0|                        chain.push(v.to_string());
  163|      0|                        chain.push(format!("{v}-high"));
  164|      0|                    }
  165|       |                }
  166|       |            }
  167|      0|            chain
  168|      0|        };
  169|       |
  170|      4|        match tier {
  171|      1|            ModelTier::Auto => Ok(vec!["auto".to_string()]),
  172|       |
  173|       |            // Opus: try 4.6, then 4.5
  174|      1|            ModelTier::Opus => Ok(claude_chain(&["opus-4.6", "opus-4.5"])),
  175|       |
  176|       |            // Sonnet: try 4.6, then 4.5
  177|      1|            ModelTier::Sonnet => Ok(claude_chain(&["sonnet-4.6", "sonnet-4.5"])),
  178|       |
  179|       |            // GPT: try 5.4, 5.3, 5.2, 5.1
  180|      0|            ModelTier::Gpt => Ok(gpt_chain(&["gpt-5.4", "gpt-5.3", "gpt-5.2", "gpt-5.1"])),
  181|       |
  182|       |            // OpenAI: comprehensive chain of all OpenAI models
  183|       |            // Includes GPT, Codex variants - try to find ANY working OpenAI model
  184|       |            ModelTier::OpenAI => {
  185|      0|                let mut chain = gpt_chain(&["gpt-5.4", "gpt-5.3", "gpt-5.2", "gpt-5.1"]);
  186|       |                // Also include codex variants as fallback
  187|      0|                chain.extend(gpt_chain(&[
  188|      0|                    "gpt-5.3-codex",
  189|      0|                    "gpt-5.2-codex",
  190|      0|                    "gpt-5.1-codex-max",
  191|      0|                ]));
  192|      0|                Ok(chain)
  193|       |            }
  194|       |
  195|       |            // Codex: specialized coding models
  196|      0|            ModelTier::Codex => Ok(gpt_chain(&[
  197|      0|                "gpt-5.3-codex",
  198|      0|                "gpt-5.2-codex",
  199|      0|                "gpt-5.1-codex-max",
  200|      0|            ])),
  201|       |
  202|       |            // CodexMini: cheap/fast coding model
  203|      0|            ModelTier::CodexMini => Ok(vec!["gpt-5.1-codex-mini".to_string()]),
  204|       |
  205|       |            // Gemini: try 3.1, then 3
  206|      0|            ModelTier::Gemini => Ok(vec![
  207|      0|                "gemini-3.1-pro".to_string(),
  208|      0|                "gemini-3-pro".to_string(),
  209|      0|            ]),
  210|       |
  211|      0|            ModelTier::GeminiFlash => Ok(vec!["gemini-3-flash".to_string()]),
  212|       |
  213|      0|            ModelTier::Grok => Ok(vec!["grok".to_string()]),
  214|       |
  215|       |            // Composer: try 1.5, then 1
  216|      0|            ModelTier::Composer => Ok(vec!["composer-1.5".to_string(), "composer-1".to_string()]),
  217|       |
  218|       |            // Cursor doesn't have Haiku
  219|      1|            ModelTier::Haiku => Err(anyhow!(
  220|      1|                "Model tier 'haiku' is not available in Cursor backend. \
  221|      1|                Use 'codex-mini' or 'grok' for cheap models."
  222|      1|            )),
  223|       |        }
  224|      4|    }
  225|       |
  226|      1|    async fn setup_mcp(&self, socket_path: &str) -> Result<()> {
  227|       |        // For CLI transport, we don't pre-register MCP servers.
  228|       |        // Instead, enable_mcp_for_agent() is called just-in-time before spawning
  229|       |        // each agent, which registers ONLY that agent type's MCP server.
  230|       |        // This ensures each agent sees only its own tools.
  231|       |        tracing::debug!(
  232|       |            "CLI transport will configure MCP per-agent (socket: {})",
  233|       |            socket_path
  234|       |        );
  235|       |        Ok(())
  236|      1|    }
  237|       |
  238|      1|    fn cleanup_mcp(&self) -> Result<()> {
  239|      1|        mcp_config::unregister_paperboat_mcp()
  240|      1|    }
  241|       |
  242|       |    async fn create_client(
  243|       |        &self,
  244|       |        agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  245|       |        cache_dir: Option<&str>,
  246|       |        request_timeout: Duration,
  247|      0|    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AcpClientTrait + Send>> {
  248|       |        // Determine permission policy based on agent type
  249|       |        // This controls which tools each agent can use via permission requests
  250|       |        let permission_policy = match agent_type {
  251|       |            AgentCacheType::Orchestrator => acp::PermissionPolicy::for_orchestrator(),
  252|       |            AgentCacheType::Planner => acp::PermissionPolicy::for_planner(),
  253|       |            AgentCacheType::Worker => acp::PermissionPolicy::for_implementer(),
  254|       |        };
  255|       |
  256|       |        let client =
  257|       |            acp::CursorAcpClient::spawn_with_policy(cache_dir, request_timeout, permission_policy)
  258|       |                .await?;
  259|       |        Ok(Box::new(client))
  260|      0|    }
  261|       |
  262|      0|    fn setup_agent_cache(
  263|      0|        &self,
  264|      0|        agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  265|      0|        removed_tools: &[&str],
  266|      0|    ) -> Result<PathBuf> {
  267|      0|        cache::setup_cursor_cache(agent_type, removed_tools)
  268|      0|    }
  269|       |
  270|      4|    fn login_hint(&self) -> &'static str {
  271|      4|        "agent login"
  272|      4|    }
  273|       |
  274|      2|    fn auth_error_message(&self) -> String {
  275|      2|        "Cursor CLI is not authenticated.\n\n\
  276|      2|        Please run 'agent login' first to authenticate, then try again.\n\
  277|      2|        Alternatively, set CURSOR_API_KEY or CURSOR_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable."
  278|      2|            .to_string()
  279|      2|    }
  280|       |
  281|       |    // ========================================================================
  282|       |    // Transport Methods
  283|       |    // ========================================================================
  284|       |
  285|       |    /// Returns the list of transport protocols Cursor supports.
  286|       |    ///
  287|       |    /// Cursor supports both ACP and CLI transports:
  288|       |    /// - **CLI** (recommended): Properly loads MCP servers from `~/.cursor/mcp.json`
  289|       |    /// - **ACP**: Has broken MCP support (Cursor bug, no ETA for fix)
  290|       |    ///
  291|       |    /// Use CLI transport for production workloads until Cursor fixes ACP MCP support.
  292|     10|    fn supported_transports(&self) -> Vec<TransportKind> {
  293|     10|        vec![TransportKind::Acp, TransportKind::Cli]
  294|     10|    }
  295|       |
  296|       |    /// Create a transport instance for the specified protocol.
  297|       |    ///
  298|       |    /// Creates either a CLI or ACP transport based on the requested kind,
  299|       |    /// configured with appropriate permission policy for the agent type.
  300|       |    ///
  301|       |    /// # Arguments
  302|       |    ///
  303|       |    /// * `kind` - `TransportKind::Cli` (recommended) or `TransportKind::Acp`
  304|       |    /// * `agent_type` - Controls which tools are allowed (Orchestrator, Planner, Implementer)
  305|       |    /// * `config` - Transport configuration including workspace, model, timeout
  306|       |    ///
  307|       |    /// # Recommended Usage
  308|       |    ///
  309|       |    /// ```ignore
  310|       |    /// let backend = CursorBackend::new();
  311|       |    /// let config = TransportConfig::new("/workspace")
  312|       |    ///     .with_model("sonnet-4.6")
  313|       |    ///     .with_request_timeout(Duration::from_secs(300));
  314|       |    ///
  315|       |    /// // Use CLI transport for proper MCP support
  316|       |    /// let transport = backend.create_transport(
  317|       |    ///     TransportKind::Cli,  // Recommended over Acp
  318|       |    ///     AgentType::Implementer,
  319|       |    ///     config,
  320|       |    /// ).await?;
  321|       |    /// ```
  322|       |    async fn create_transport(
  323|       |        &self,
  324|       |        kind: TransportKind,
  325|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
  326|       |        config: TransportConfig,
  327|      8|    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AgentTransport>> {
  328|       |        // Validate transport kind is supported
  329|       |        if !self.supported_transports().contains(&kind) {
  330|       |            return Err(anyhow!(
  331|       |                "Transport '{}' is not supported by Cursor backend. Supported: acp, cli",
  332|       |                kind
  333|       |            ));
  334|       |        }
  335|       |
  336|       |        // Determine permission policy based on agent type
  337|       |        let permission_policy = match agent_type {
  338|       |            AgentType::Orchestrator => PermissionPolicy::for_orchestrator(),
  339|       |            AgentType::Planner => PermissionPolicy::for_planner(),
  340|       |            AgentType::Implementer => PermissionPolicy::for_implementer(),
  341|       |        };
  342|       |
  343|       |        match kind {
  344|       |            TransportKind::Cli => {
  345|       |                tracing::debug!(
  346|       |                    "Creating Cursor CLI transport for {} agent",
  347|       |                    agent_type.as_str()
  348|       |                );
  349|       |
  350|       |                // Create CLI transport with agent type for MCP server selection
  351|       |                let transport = CursorCliTransport::new(agent_type.as_str(), permission_policy);
  352|       |
  353|       |                // Configure the transport with workspace and model
  354|       |                // Note: CLI transport gets these from create_session, but we can
  355|       |                // pre-configure them here for consistency
  356|       |                if let Some(model) = &config.model {
  357|       |                    // The CLI transport will use the model from SessionConfig
  358|       |                    tracing::debug!("CLI transport will use model: {}", model);
  359|       |                }
  360|       |
  361|       |                Ok(Box::new(transport))
  362|       |            }
  363|       |            TransportKind::Acp => {
  364|       |                tracing::debug!(
  365|       |                    "Creating Cursor ACP transport for {} agent",
  366|       |                    agent_type.as_str()
  367|       |                );
  368|       |                tracing::warn!(
  369|       |                    "⚠️ Using ACP transport - note that Cursor ACP has broken MCP support. \
  370|       |                    Consider using CLI transport instead."
  371|       |                );
  372|       |
  373|       |                // Create ACP transport with permission policy and timeout
  374|       |                let mut transport =
  375|       |                    CursorAcpTransport::new(permission_policy, config.request_timeout);
  376|       |
  377|       |                // Pre-configure workspace, model, and MCP servers
  378|       |                transport.set_workspace(config.workspace);
  379|       |                if let Some(model) = config.model {
  380|       |                    transport.set_model(model);
  381|       |                }
  382|       |                transport.set_mcp_servers(config.mcp_servers);
  383|       |
  384|       |                Ok(Box::new(transport))
  385|       |            }
  386|       |        }
  387|      8|    }
  388|       |}

  398|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  399|       |        key: &'static str,
  400|       |        original: Option<String>,
  401|       |    }
  402|       |
  403|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  404|      2|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  405|      2|            let original = env::var(key).ok();
  406|      2|            env::set_var(key, value);
  407|      2|            Self { key, original }
  408|      2|        }
  409|       |    }
  410|       |
  411|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  412|      2|        fn drop(&mut self) {
  413|      2|            match &self.original {
  414|      2|                Some(value) => env::set_var(self.key, value),
  415|      0|                None => env::remove_var(self.key),
  416|       |            }
  417|      2|        }
  418|       |    }

  548|      1|        let _home = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp.path().to_str().unwrap());
  549|      1|        let config_path = temp.path().join(".cursor/mcp.json");
  550|      1|        std::fs::create_dir_all(config_path.parent().unwrap()).unwrap();

  556|      1|                        "command": "gh",
  557|      1|                        "args": ["mcp", "serve"]
  558|       |                    },
  559|      1|                    "paperboat-planner": {
  560|      1|                        "command": "/old/paperboat",

  568|       |            })
  569|      1|            .to_string(),
  570|       |        )
  571|      1|        .unwrap();
  572|       |

  574|       |
  575|      1|        let config: mcp_config::CursorMcpConfig =

  577|      1|        assert_eq!(config.mcp_servers.len(), 1);
  578|      1|        assert!(config.mcp_servers.contains_key("github"));
  579|      1|        assert!(!config.mcp_servers.keys().any(|name| name.starts_with("paperboat-")));

  661|      1|    async fn test_create_transport_cli_session_creates_unique_session_id() {
  662|      1|        let backend = CursorBackend::new();
  663|      1|        let config1 = TransportConfig::new("/tmp/workspace").with_model("sonnet-4.6");
  664|      1|        let config2 = TransportConfig::new("/tmp/workspace").with_model("sonnet-4.6");
  665|       |
  666|      1|        let mut transport1 = backend
  667|      1|            .create_transport(TransportKind::Cli, AgentType::Implementer, config1)
  668|      1|            .await
  669|      1|            .unwrap();
  670|       |
  671|      1|        let mut transport2 = backend
  672|      1|            .create_transport(TransportKind::Cli, AgentType::Implementer, config2)
  673|      1|            .await
  674|      1|            .unwrap();
  675|       |
  676|       |        // Each transport session should have unique IDs
  677|      1|        let session1 = transport1
  678|      1|            .create_session(SessionConfig::new("sonnet-4.6", "/tmp/workspace"))
  679|      1|            .await
  680|      1|            .unwrap();
  681|      1|        let session2 = transport2
  682|      1|            .create_session(SessionConfig::new("sonnet-4.6", "/tmp/workspace"))
  683|      1|            .await
  684|      1|            .unwrap();
  685|       |
  686|      1|        assert_ne!(session1.session_id, session2.session_id);
  687|      1|    }

  717|       |    // Note: discover_models() tests are in the models module since they
  718|       |    // require mocking the cursor-agent command or running against real CLI
  719|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/models.rs:
    1|       |//! Model tier discovery for Cursor backend.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module discovers available model tiers by running `cursor-agent --list-models`.
    4|       |//! The output format is:
    5|       |//! ```text
    6|       |//! sonnet-4.6 - Claude 4.6 Sonnet
    7|       |//! opus-4.6 - Claude 4.6 Opus  (current, default)
    8|       |//! gpt-5.1-codex-mini - GPT-5.1 Codex Mini
    9|       |//! ```
   10|       |
   11|       |use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
   12|       |use regex::Regex;
   13|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
   14|       |use tokio::process::Command;
   15|       |
   16|       |use crate::models::ModelTier;
   17|       |
   18|       |/// Discover available model tiers by running `cursor-agent --list-models`.
   19|       |///
   20|       |/// Parses the output and maps Cursor model IDs to ModelTier values.
   21|      0|pub async fn discover_cursor_tiers() -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>> {
   22|      0|    let output = Command::new("cursor-agent")
   23|      0|        .arg("--list-models")
   24|      0|        .output()
   25|      0|        .await
   26|      0|        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to run 'cursor-agent --list-models': {e}"))?;
   27|       |
   28|      0|    if !output.status.success() {
   29|      0|        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
   30|      0|        return Err(anyhow!(
   31|      0|            "cursor-agent --list-models failed with status {}: {}",
   32|      0|            output.status,
   33|      0|            stderr
   34|      0|        ));
   35|      0|    }
   36|       |
   37|      0|    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
   38|      0|    parse_cursor_tiers(&stdout)
   39|      0|}
   40|       |
   41|       |/// Parse the output of `cursor-agent --list-models` into a set of ModelTiers.
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// The format is:
   44|       |/// ```text
   45|       |/// model-id - Display Name
   46|       |/// model-id - Display Name  (current, default)
   47|       |/// ```
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// Maps cursor model IDs to tiers:
   50|       |/// - `sonnet-*` → Sonnet
   51|       |/// - `opus-*` → Opus
   52|       |/// - `gpt-*-codex-mini` → CodexMini
   53|       |/// - `gpt-*-codex` → Codex
   54|       |/// - `gemini-*-pro` → Gemini
   55|       |/// - `gemini-*-flash` → GeminiFlash
   56|       |/// - `grok*` → Grok
   57|       |/// - `composer-*` → Composer
   58|       |///
   59|       |/// Also adds meta-tiers:
   60|       |/// - If any GPT model is available → adds OpenAI meta-tier
   61|      4|pub fn parse_cursor_tiers(output: &str) -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>> {
   62|      4|    let mut tiers = HashSet::new();
   63|       |
   64|       |    // Pattern: "model-id - Display Name"
   65|      4|    let line_re = Regex::new(r"^([a-z0-9.-]+)\s+-\s+")?;
                                                                    ^0
   66|       |
   67|     18|    for line in output.lines() {
                              ^4     ^4
   68|     18|        let line = line.trim();
   69|     18|        if line.is_empty() || line.starts_with("Loading") || line.starts_with("Available") {
                                            ^14  ^14                       ^14  ^14
   70|      4|            continue;
   71|     14|        }
   72|       |
   73|     14|        if let Some(caps) = line_re.captures(line) {
   74|     14|            let cursor_id = caps.get(1).map_or("", |m| m.as_str());
   75|     14|            if let Some(tier) = extract_tier_from_cursor_id(cursor_id) {
   76|     14|                tiers.insert(tier);
   77|     14|            }
                          ^0
   78|      0|        }
   79|       |    }
   80|       |
   81|       |    // Add meta-tiers: OpenAI is available if any GPT model is available
   82|      4|    if tiers.contains(&ModelTier::Gpt)
   83|      3|        || tiers.contains(&ModelTier::Codex)
   84|      2|        || tiers.contains(&ModelTier::CodexMini)
   85|      3|    {
   86|      3|        tiers.insert(ModelTier::OpenAI);
   87|      3|    }
                  ^1
   88|       |
   89|      4|    Ok(tiers)
   90|      4|}
   91|       |
   92|       |/// Extract a ModelTier from a Cursor model ID string.
   93|       |///
   94|       |/// Examples:
   95|       |/// - "sonnet-4.6" → Sonnet
   96|       |/// - "opus-4.5-thinking" → Opus
   97|       |/// - "gpt-5.4-high" → Gpt
   98|       |/// - "gpt-5.1-codex-mini" → CodexMini
   99|       |/// - "gpt-5.3-codex" → Codex
  100|       |/// - "gemini-3.1-pro" → Gemini
  101|       |/// - "gemini-3-flash" → GeminiFlash
  102|       |/// - "grok" → Grok
  103|       |/// - "composer-1.5" → Composer
  104|     23|fn extract_tier_from_cursor_id(id: &str) -> Option<ModelTier> {
  105|     23|    let lower = id.to_lowercase();
  106|       |
  107|       |    // Claude models
  108|     23|    if lower.starts_with("sonnet") {
  109|      4|        return Some(ModelTier::Sonnet);
  110|     19|    }
  111|     19|    if lower.starts_with("opus") {
  112|      4|        return Some(ModelTier::Opus);
  113|     15|    }
  114|       |
  115|       |    // GPT models - check for specific tiers first (most specific match)
  116|     15|    if lower.contains("codex-mini") {
  117|      3|        return Some(ModelTier::CodexMini);
  118|     12|    }
  119|     12|    if lower.contains("codex") {
  120|      2|        return Some(ModelTier::Codex);
  121|     10|    }
  122|       |    // Generic GPT (not codex)
  123|     10|    if lower.starts_with("gpt") {
  124|      1|        return Some(ModelTier::Gpt);
  125|      9|    }
  126|       |
  127|       |    // Gemini models
  128|      9|    if lower.contains("gemini") && lower.contains("flash") {
                                                 ^4
  129|      2|        return Some(ModelTier::GeminiFlash);
  130|      7|    }
  131|      7|    if lower.starts_with("gemini") {
  132|      2|        return Some(ModelTier::Gemini);
  133|      5|    }
  134|       |
  135|       |    // Other models
  136|      5|    if lower.starts_with("grok") {
  137|      2|        return Some(ModelTier::Grok);
  138|      3|    }
  139|      3|    if lower.starts_with("composer") {
  140|      2|        return Some(ModelTier::Composer);
  141|      1|    }
  142|       |
  143|      1|    if lower == "auto" {
  144|      0|        return Some(ModelTier::Auto);
  145|      1|    }
  146|       |
  147|      1|    None
  148|     23|}
  149|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/cursor/permission.rs:
    1|       |//! Permission policy for tool access control.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `PermissionPolicy`, which controls which tools an agent
    4|       |//! can use. It is used by both ACP and CLI transports to enforce consistent
    5|       |//! tool access rules across different agent types.
    6|       |
    7|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
    8|       |
    9|       |use crate::agents::{AgentToolConfig, IMPLEMENTER_CONFIG, ORCHESTRATOR_CONFIG, PLANNER_CONFIG};
   10|       |
   11|       |/// All MCP tool names that Paperboat exposes.
   12|       |const ALL_MCP_TOOLS: &[&str] = &[
   13|       |    "set_goal",
   14|       |    "create_task",
   15|       |    "complete",
   16|       |    "decompose",
   17|       |    "spawn_agents",
   18|       |    "skip_tasks",
   19|       |    "list_tasks",
   20|       |];
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Permission policy for tool access control.
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// This controls which tools an agent can use. When the backend receives a
   25|       |/// permission request for a tool, it checks this policy to decide whether
   26|       |/// to allow or deny the tool call.
   27|       |///
   28|       |/// # Design
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// - The policy is immutable after creation
   31|       |/// - Tool names are matched exactly (case-sensitive)
   32|       |/// - MCP tools (Paperboat's own tools) are checked against `allowed_mcp_tools`
   33|       |/// - Backend/built-in tools are checked against `denied_tools`
   34|       |///
   35|       |/// # Example
   36|       |///
   37|       |/// ```ignore
   38|       |/// let policy = PermissionPolicy::for_orchestrator();
   39|       |/// assert!(!policy.should_allow("edit_file")); // File editing denied
   40|       |/// assert!(policy.should_allow("list_tasks")); // MCP tools allowed
   41|       |/// ```
   42|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   43|       |pub struct PermissionPolicy {
   44|       |    /// Tools that should always be rejected (backend/built-in tools).
   45|       |    pub denied_tools: HashSet<String>,
   46|       |    /// MCP tools that are allowed (all others are denied).
   47|       |    pub allowed_mcp_tools: HashSet<String>,
   48|       |}
   49|       |
   50|       |impl PermissionPolicy {
   51|       |    /// Create a policy that allows all tools.
   52|     28|    pub fn allow_all() -> Self {
   53|       |        Self {
   54|     28|            denied_tools: HashSet::new(),
   55|    196|            allowed_mcp_tools: ALL_MCP_TOOLS.iter().map(|s| (*s).to_string()).collect(),
                                             ^28           ^28    ^28                       ^28
   56|       |        }
   57|     28|    }
   58|       |
   59|       |    /// Create a policy from an agent tool configuration.
   60|       |    ///
   61|       |    /// This uses the centralized config from `src/agents/config.rs` to determine
   62|       |    /// which tools are allowed/denied for each agent type.
   63|     93|    pub fn from_agent_config(config: &AgentToolConfig) -> Self {
   64|       |        Self {
   65|       |            // Deny the tools listed in removed_auggie_tools
   66|     93|            denied_tools: config
   67|     93|                .removed_auggie_tools
   68|     93|                .iter()
   69|    711|                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())
                               ^93
   70|     93|                .collect(),
   71|       |            // Only allow the MCP tools listed for this agent type
   72|    255|            allowed_mcp_tools: config.mcp_tools.iter().map(|s| (*s).to_string()).collect(),
                                             ^93              ^93    ^93                       ^93
   73|       |        }
   74|     93|    }
   75|       |
   76|       |    /// Create a policy for planner agents.
   77|       |    ///
   78|       |    /// Denies file editing tools but allows MCP tools for planning.
   79|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   80|     15|    pub fn for_planner() -> Self {
   81|     15|        Self::from_agent_config(&PLANNER_CONFIG)
   82|     15|    }
   83|       |
   84|       |    /// Create a policy for orchestrator agents.
   85|       |    ///
   86|       |    /// Denies file editing tools but allows MCP tools for orchestration.
   87|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   88|     22|    pub fn for_orchestrator() -> Self {
   89|     22|        Self::from_agent_config(&ORCHESTRATOR_CONFIG)
   90|     22|    }
   91|       |
   92|       |    /// Create a policy for implementer agents.
   93|       |    ///
   94|       |    /// Allows all tools including file editing.
   95|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   96|     56|    pub fn for_implementer() -> Self {
   97|     56|        Self::from_agent_config(&IMPLEMENTER_CONFIG)
   98|     56|    }
   99|       |
  100|       |    /// Check if a tool should be allowed.
  101|       |    ///
  102|       |    /// # Arguments
  103|       |    ///
  104|       |    /// * `tool_name` - The name of the tool to check
  105|       |    ///
  106|       |    /// # Returns
  107|       |    ///
  108|       |    /// `true` if the tool should be allowed, `false` otherwise.
  109|     67|    pub fn should_allow(&self, tool_name: &str) -> bool {
  110|       |        // Check if it's an MCP tool
  111|     67|        if ALL_MCP_TOOLS.contains(&tool_name) {
  112|     36|            return self.allowed_mcp_tools.contains(tool_name);
  113|     31|        }
  114|       |
  115|       |        // For backend tools, check if it's denied
  116|     31|        !self.denied_tools.contains(tool_name)
  117|     67|    }
  118|       |}

  360|      1|        assert!(policy.should_allow("spawn_agents"));

  363|       |        // the denied_tools check. Since "Spawn_Agents" isn't in denied_tools, it's allowed.

  366|      1|        assert!(!policy.allowed_mcp_tools.contains("Spawn_Agents"));
  367|      1|        assert!(!policy.allowed_mcp_tools.contains("SPAWN_AGENTS"));

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/mod.rs:
    1|       |// Allow some clippy lints for this new module - can be cleaned up later
    2|       |#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
    3|       |#![allow(clippy::uninlined_format_args)]
    4|       |#![allow(clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls)]
    5|       |#![allow(clippy::use_self)]
    6|       |
    7|       |//! Backend abstraction layer for agent providers.
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This module provides the core abstraction for supporting multiple agent backends
   10|       |//! (Auggie, Cursor, etc.) while maintaining a unified interface for the application.
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! # Architecture: Backend × Transport = Client
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! The backend system separates concerns into three layers:
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! ```text
   17|       |//! Backend (vendor)     × Transport (protocol)  = Client (implementation)
   18|       |//! ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   19|       |//! Auggie               × ACP                   = AuggieAcpClient ✓
   20|       |//! Cursor               × ACP                   = CursorAcpClient (MCP broken)
   21|       |//! Cursor               × CLI                   = CursorCliClient (MCP works) ✓
   22|       |//! ```
   23|       |//!
   24|       |//! - **Backend**: Vendor-specific configuration (auth, models, MCP setup)
   25|       |//! - **Transport**: Communication protocol (ACP vs CLI)
   26|       |//! - **`AgentType`**: Permission control (what tools each agent type can use)
   27|       |//!
   28|       |//! # Core Types
   29|       |//!
   30|       |//! - [`BackendKind`] - An enum representing available backend providers
   31|       |//! - [`BackendConfig`] - Configuration combining backend + optional transport
   32|       |//! - [`Backend`] - Trait for vendor-specific configuration
   33|       |//! - [`TransportKind`] - Available communication protocols (ACP, CLI)
   34|       |//! - `AgentTransport` - Trait for communication implementations (in `transport` module)
   35|       |//!
   36|       |//! # Backend Selection
   37|       |//!
   38|       |//! Backends can be selected via (in priority order):
   39|       |//! 1. CLI flag: `--backend cursor` or `--backend cursor:cli`
   40|       |//! 2. Environment variable: `PAPERBOAT_BACKEND=cursor:cli`
   41|       |//! 3. Project config: `.paperboat/config.toml` with `backend = "cursor:cli"`
   42|       |//! 4. User config: `~/.paperboat/config.toml`
   43|       |//! 5. Default: Auggie with ACP
   44|       |//!
   45|       |//! # Backend:Transport Syntax
   46|       |//!
   47|       |//! The `--backend` flag supports an optional transport suffix:
   48|       |//!
   49|       |//! ```text
   50|       |//! --backend cursor        # Uses default transport (CLI for Cursor)
   51|       |//! --backend cursor:cli    # Explicitly use CLI transport
   52|       |//! --backend cursor:acp    # Explicitly use ACP transport
   53|       |//! --backend auggie        # Uses default transport (ACP for Auggie)
   54|       |//! --backend auggie:acp    # Explicitly use ACP transport (redundant but allowed)
   55|       |//! ```
   56|       |//!
   57|       |//! # Default Transports
   58|       |//!
   59|       |//! | Backend | Default Transport | Reason |
   60|       |//! |---------|-------------------|--------|
   61|       |//! | Auggie  | ACP              | Only supported transport |
   62|       |//! | Cursor  | CLI              | Better MCP tool support |
   63|       |//!
   64|       |//! # Usage
   65|       |//!
   66|       |//! ```ignore
   67|       |//! use paperboat::backend::{BackendKind, BackendConfig, Backend, TransportKind};
   68|       |//!
   69|       |//! // Parse from string (e.g., from CLI or config)
   70|       |//! let config = BackendConfig::parse("cursor:cli").unwrap();
   71|       |//!
   72|       |//! // Create the backend instance
   73|       |//! let backend = config.kind.create();
   74|       |//!
   75|       |//! // Get the effective transport (explicit or default)
   76|       |//! let transport = config.effective_transport();
   77|       |//! assert_eq!(transport, TransportKind::Cli);
   78|       |//! ```
   79|       |//!
   80|       |//! # Adding a New Backend
   81|       |//!
   82|       |//! To add a new backend (e.g., "newbackend"):
   83|       |//!
   84|       |//! 1. **Create the module**: Add `src/backend/newbackend/` with:
   85|       |//!    - `mod.rs` - Main module with struct implementing [`Backend`]
   86|       |//!    - `auth.rs` - Authentication checking
   87|       |//!    - `cache.rs` - Cache directory setup
   88|       |//!    - Transport implementations (e.g., `acp.rs`, `cli.rs`)
   89|       |//!
   90|       |//! 2. **Implement the [`Backend`] trait** for your new backend struct.
   91|       |//!
   92|       |//! 3. **Add the variant to [`BackendKind`]**:
   93|       |//!    - Add `NewBackend` variant to the enum
   94|       |//!    - Update `BackendKind::ALL`
   95|       |//!    - Update `as_str()`, `from_str()`, `create()`, `default_transport()`,
   96|       |//!      and `supported_transports()`
   97|       |//!
   98|       |//! 4. **Update the string parsing** in `FromStr for BackendKind`
   99|       |//!
  100|       |//! See [`auggie`] and [`cursor`] modules for reference implementations.
  101|       |
  102|       |pub mod auggie;
  103|       |pub mod cursor;
  104|       |mod r#trait;
  105|       |pub mod transport;
  106|       |
  107|       |pub use r#trait::{AgentCacheType, Backend, TransportConfig};
  108|       |pub use transport::TransportKind;
  109|       |
  110|       |use std::str::FromStr;

  120|       |///
  121|       |/// # Examples
  122|       |///
  123|       |/// ```ignore
  124|       |/// let config = BackendConfig::parse("cursor:cli")?;
  125|       |/// assert_eq!(config.kind, BackendKind::Cursor);
  126|       |/// assert_eq!(config.transport, Some(TransportKind::Cli));
  127|       |///
  128|       |/// let config = BackendConfig::parse("auggie")?;
  129|       |/// assert_eq!(config.kind, BackendKind::Auggie);
  130|       |/// assert_eq!(config.transport, None); // Uses default
  131|       |/// ```

  272|       |// ============================================================================
  273|       |// Backend Kind

  275|       |
  276|       |/// Available backend providers for agent communication.
  277|       |///
  278|       |/// This enum represents the different agent backends that paperboat can use.
  279|       |/// Each variant corresponds to a specific implementation of the [`Backend`] trait.
  280|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
  281|       |pub enum BackendKind {
  282|       |    /// Augment's Auggie CLI (default).
  283|       |    ///
  284|       |    /// Uses the `auggie` command-line tool for ACP communication.
  285|       |    /// Authenticate with `auggie login`.
  286|       |    ///
  287|       |    /// **Supported transports:** ACP only
  288|       |    #[default]
  289|       |    Auggie,
  290|       |
  291|       |    /// Cursor's agent CLI.
  292|       |    ///
  293|       |    /// Uses Cursor's agent infrastructure for agent communication.
  294|       |    /// Authenticate with `cursor login` or set `CURSOR_API_KEY`.
  295|       |    ///
  296|       |    /// **Supported transports:** CLI (default, better MCP), ACP
  297|       |    Cursor,
  298|       |}

  304|       |

  319|     10|        match self {
  320|      5|            Self::Auggie => Box::new(auggie::AuggieBackend::new()),

  352|       |    /// Validate that a transport is supported by this backend.
  353|       |    ///
  354|       |    /// Returns `Ok(())` if supported, or an error message if not.
  355|     19|    pub fn validate_transport(&self, transport: TransportKind) -> Result<(), String> {
  356|     19|        if self.supports_transport(transport) {
  357|     15|            Ok(())
  358|       |        } else {

  360|      4|                .supported_transports()

  363|      4|                .collect::<Vec<_>>()

  365|       |
  366|      4|            Err(format!(
  367|      4|                "transport '{}' is not supported by {} backend. Supported: {}",
  368|      4|                transport.as_str(),
  369|      4|                self.as_str(),
  370|      4|                supported_list
  371|      4|            ))
  372|       |        }
  373|     19|    }

  417|       |impl FromStr for BackendKind {

  420|       |    /// Parse backend kind from string.
  421|       |    ///
  422|       |    /// This parses only the backend name, not the transport suffix.
  423|       |    /// For parsing with transport, use [`BackendConfig::parse`] or
  424|       |    /// [`BackendKind::parse_with_transport`].
  425|     46|    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
  426|     46|        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
  427|     46|            "auggie" | "augment" => Ok(Self::Auggie),
                                     ^34          ^18
  428|     28|            "cursor" => Ok(Self::Cursor),

  436|       |// ============================================================================
  437|       |// Backend Availability Detection
  438|       |// ============================================================================
  439|       |
  440|       |/// Check which backends are available by attempting to list models.
  441|       |///
  442|       |/// This function runs the model list command for each backend and returns
  443|       |/// which ones successfully respond. This is useful for auto-detecting
  444|       |/// which backend to use when the user hasn't specified one.
  445|       |///
  446|       |/// # Returns
  447|       |///
  448|       |/// A vector of available backend kinds (those that successfully list models).
  449|       |///
  450|       |/// # Example
  451|       |///
  452|       |/// ```ignore
  453|       |/// let available = discover_available_backends().await;
  454|       |/// match available.len() {
  455|       |///     0 => panic!("No backends available"),
  456|       |///     1 => use_backend(available[0]),
  457|       |///     _ => prompt_user_to_select(available),
  458|       |/// }
  459|       |/// ```
  460|      0|pub async fn discover_available_backends() -> Vec<BackendKind> {
  461|       |    use tokio::process::Command;
  462|       |
  463|      0|    let mut available = Vec::new();
  464|       |
  465|       |    // Check Auggie availability by running `auggie model list`
  466|      0|    let auggie_result = Command::new("auggie")
  467|      0|        .args(["model", "list"])
  468|      0|        .output()
  469|      0|        .await;
  470|       |
  471|      0|    if let Ok(output) = auggie_result {
  472|      0|        if output.status.success() {
  473|      0|            tracing::debug!("Auggie backend is available");
  474|      0|            available.push(BackendKind::Auggie);
  475|       |        } else {
  476|      0|            tracing::debug!(
  477|       |                "Auggie backend not available: command failed with status {}",
  478|       |                output.status
  479|       |            );
  480|       |        }
  481|       |    } else {
  482|      0|        tracing::debug!("Auggie backend not available: auggie command not found");
  483|       |    }
  484|       |
  485|       |    // Check Cursor availability by running `cursor-agent --list-models`
  486|      0|    let cursor_result = Command::new("cursor-agent")
  487|      0|        .arg("--list-models")
  488|      0|        .output()
  489|      0|        .await;
  490|       |
  491|      0|    if let Ok(output) = cursor_result {
  492|      0|        if output.status.success() {
  493|      0|            tracing::debug!("Cursor backend is available");
  494|      0|            available.push(BackendKind::Cursor);
  495|       |        } else {
  496|      0|            tracing::debug!(
  497|       |                "Cursor backend not available: command failed with status {}",
  498|       |                output.status
  499|       |            );
  500|       |        }
  501|       |    } else {
  502|      0|        tracing::debug!("Cursor backend not available: cursor-agent command not found");
  503|       |    }
  504|       |
  505|      0|    available
  506|      0|}
  507|       |
  508|       |/// Prompt the user to select a backend interactively with timeout.
  509|       |///
  510|       |/// Displays available backends and waits for user input with a 10-second timeout.
  511|       |/// If no input is received within the timeout, returns the first available backend.
  512|       |/// Returns `None` if stdin is not a terminal.
  513|       |///
  514|       |/// # Ctrl+C Handling
  515|       |///
  516|       |/// If the user presses Ctrl+C during the prompt, this function will exit the process.
  517|       |///
  518|       |/// # Arguments
  519|       |///
  520|       |/// * `available` - List of available backends to choose from
  521|       |///
  522|       |/// # Returns
  523|       |///
  524|       |/// The selected `BackendKind`, or `None` if selection failed.
  525|      0|pub fn prompt_backend_selection(available: &[BackendKind]) -> Option<BackendKind> {
  526|       |    use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
  527|       |    use std::sync::mpsc;
  528|       |    use std::time::Duration;
  529|       |
  530|       |    // Don't prompt if stdin is not a terminal
  531|      0|    if !std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal(&io::stdin()) {
  532|      0|        return None;
  533|      0|    }
  534|       |
  535|      0|    println!("\n🔍 Multiple AI backends are available:\n");
  536|       |
  537|      0|    for (i, kind) in available.iter().enumerate() {
  538|      0|        let description = match kind {
  539|      0|            BackendKind::Auggie => "Augment CLI (auggie)",
  540|      0|            BackendKind::Cursor => "Cursor Agent CLI",
  541|       |        };
  542|      0|        println!("  {}. {} - {}", i + 1, kind.as_str(), description);
  543|       |    }
  544|       |
  545|      0|    println!();
  546|      0|    print!(
  547|       |        "Select a backend [1-{}] (10s timeout, default=1): ",
  548|      0|        available.len()
  549|       |    );
  550|      0|    io::stdout().flush().ok()?;
  551|       |
  552|       |    // Use a channel with timeout for reading input
  553|      0|    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();
  554|      0|    let stdin_thread = std::thread::spawn(move || {
  555|      0|        let stdin = io::stdin();
  556|      0|        let mut line = String::new();
  557|      0|        let read_result = stdin.lock().read_line(&mut line);
  558|      0|        match read_result {
  559|      0|            Ok(0) => {
  560|      0|                // EOF (Ctrl+D on Unix)
  561|      0|                let _ = tx.send(None);
  562|      0|            }
  563|      0|            Ok(_) => {
  564|      0|                let _ = tx.send(Some(line));
  565|      0|            }
  566|      0|            Err(_) => {
  567|      0|                // Read error (possibly Ctrl+C)
  568|      0|                let _ = tx.send(None);
  569|      0|            }
  570|       |        }
  571|      0|    });
  572|       |
  573|       |    // Wait for input with a 10-second timeout
  574|      0|    let result = match rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)) {
  575|      0|        Ok(Some(line)) => {
  576|       |            // Check if line is empty (just Enter pressed)
  577|      0|            let trimmed = line.trim();
  578|      0|            if trimmed.is_empty() {
  579|       |                // Default to first option
  580|      0|                println!();
  581|      0|                println!("✓ Selected: {} (default)\n", available[0].as_str());
  582|      0|                Some(available[0])
  583|       |            } else {
  584|       |                // Try to parse the selection
  585|      0|                match trimmed.parse::<usize>() {
  586|      0|                    Ok(selection) if selection >= 1 && selection <= available.len() => {
  587|      0|                        let selected = available[selection - 1];
  588|      0|                        println!("✓ Selected: {}\n", selected.as_str());
  589|      0|                        Some(selected)
  590|       |                    }
  591|       |                    _ => {
  592|      0|                        eprintln!(
  593|       |                            "Invalid selection. Using default: {}",
  594|      0|                            available[0].as_str()
  595|       |                        );
  596|      0|                        Some(available[0])
  597|       |                    }
  598|       |                }
  599|       |            }
  600|       |        }
  601|       |        Ok(None) => {
  602|       |            // EOF or error - user pressed Ctrl+C or Ctrl+D
  603|      0|            println!();
  604|      0|            eprintln!("\nCancelled.");
  605|      0|            std::process::exit(130); // Standard exit code for Ctrl+C
  606|       |        }
  607|       |        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => {
  608|       |            // Timeout - use default
  609|      0|            println!();
  610|      0|            println!("⏱ Timeout. Using default: {}\n", available[0].as_str());
  611|      0|            Some(available[0])
  612|       |        }
  613|       |        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => {
  614|       |            // Thread panicked or disconnected - use default
  615|      0|            println!();
  616|      0|            println!("✓ Using default: {}\n", available[0].as_str());
  617|      0|            Some(available[0])
  618|       |        }
  619|       |    };
  620|       |
  621|       |    // Wait for the stdin thread to finish (it may be blocked on stdin)
  622|       |    // We don't want to leave dangling threads, but also don't want to block forever
  623|      0|    let _ = stdin_thread.join();
  624|       |
  625|      0|    result
  626|      0|}

  871|       |    #[test]
  872|      1|    fn test_backend_config_parse_simple() {
  873|       |        // Simple backend names (no transport suffix)
  874|      1|        let config = BackendConfig::parse("cursor").unwrap();
  875|      1|        assert_eq!(config.kind, BackendKind::Cursor);
  876|      1|        assert_eq!(config.transport, None);
  877|       |
  878|      1|        let config = BackendConfig::parse("auggie").unwrap();
  879|      1|        assert_eq!(config.kind, BackendKind::Auggie);
  880|      1|        assert_eq!(config.transport, None);
  881|       |
  882|      1|        let config = BackendConfig::parse("augment").unwrap();
  883|      1|        assert_eq!(config.kind, BackendKind::Auggie);
  884|      1|        assert_eq!(config.transport, None);

    2|       |#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]

    4|       |#![allow(clippy::uninlined_format_args)]

    6|       |
    7|       |//! Backend trait definition for agent providers.
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This module defines the `Backend` trait that all backend providers must implement.
   10|       |//! The trait abstracts authentication, model discovery, and client/transport creation
   11|       |//! to support multiple agent backends (Auggie, Cursor, etc.).
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! # Transport Support
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! Backends can support multiple transport protocols (ACP, CLI, etc.):
   16|       |//! - Use `supported_transports()` to check which protocols a backend supports
   17|       |//! - Use `create_transport()` to create a transport instance
   18|       |//! - The legacy `create_client()` method remains for backward compatibility
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! See [`TransportConfig`] for configuration options and [`super::transport`] for
   21|       |//! transport protocol details.
   22|       |
   23|       |use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
   24|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   25|       |use serde_json::Value;
   26|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
   27|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
   28|       |use std::time::Duration;
   29|       |
   30|       |use crate::acp::AcpClientTrait;
   31|       |use crate::models::{EffortLevel, ModelTier};
   32|       |
   33|       |use super::transport::{AgentTransport, AgentType, TransportKind};

   68|       |// ============================================================================
   69|       |// Transport Configuration
   70|       |// ============================================================================
   71|       |
   72|       |/// Configuration for creating an agent transport.
   73|       |///
   74|       |/// Contains all parameters needed to create and initialize a transport connection.
   75|       |/// This struct is designed to be extensible - new fields can be added without
   76|       |/// breaking existing code.
   77|       |///
   78|       |/// # Example
   79|       |///
   80|       |/// ```ignore
   81|       |/// use paperboat::backend::{TransportConfig, AgentType};
   82|       |/// use std::time::Duration;
   83|       |///
   84|       |/// let config = TransportConfig::new("/path/to/workspace")
   85|       |///     .with_model("sonnet-4.6")
   86|       |///     .with_request_timeout(Duration::from_secs(300))
   87|       |///     .with_mcp_servers(vec![/* ... */]);
   88|       |///
   89|       |/// let transport = backend.create_transport(
   90|       |///     TransportKind::Acp,
   91|       |///     AgentType::Implementer,
   92|       |///     config,
   93|       |/// ).await?;
   94|       |/// ```
   95|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   96|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   97|       |pub struct TransportConfig {
   98|       |    /// Working directory for the agent session.
   99|       |    ///
  100|       |    /// The agent will operate relative to this directory.
  101|       |    pub workspace: PathBuf,
  102|       |
  103|       |    /// Model identifier (e.g., "sonnet-4.6", "opus-4.6").
  104|       |    ///
  105|       |    /// This should be the backend-specific model ID returned by
  106|       |    /// `Backend::resolve_tier()`.
  107|       |    pub model: Option<String>,
  108|       |
  109|       |    /// Timeout duration for requests.
  110|       |    ///
  111|       |    /// How long to wait for responses from the agent.
  112|       |    /// Different operations may have different timeout requirements.
  113|       |    pub request_timeout: Duration,
  114|       |
  115|       |    /// MCP server configurations to enable for this session.
  116|       |    ///
  117|       |    /// Each entry is a JSON object with server configuration
  118|       |    /// (typically name, command, args, env).
  119|       |    pub mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  120|       |}
  121|       |
  122|       |impl TransportConfig {
  123|       |    /// Create a new transport configuration with required workspace.

  129|       |    #[must_use]
