  182|       |
  183|       |/// Trait defining a backend provider for agent communication.
  184|       |///
  185|       |/// This trait abstracts the differences between various agent backends
  186|       |/// (e.g., Auggie, Cursor) while maintaining a unified interface for
  187|       |/// the rest of the application.
  188|       |///
  189|       |/// Backends are responsible for:
  190|       |/// - Authentication management
  191|       |/// - Model discovery
  192|       |/// - ACP client creation and configuration
  193|       |/// - Cache directory setup
  194|       |///
  195|       |/// # Example
  196|       |///
  197|       |/// ```ignore
  198|       |/// use paperboat::backend::{Backend, BackendKind, AgentCacheType};
  199|       |///
  200|       |/// async fn run_with_backend() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
  201|       |///     let backend = BackendKind::default().create();
  202|       |///     backend.check_auth()?;
  203|       |///
  204|       |///     let models = backend.discover_models().await?;
  205|       |///     let client = backend.create_client(
  206|       |///         AgentCacheType::Worker,
  207|       |///         None,
  208|       |///         Duration::from_secs(60)
  209|       |///     ).await?;
  210|       |///
  211|       |///     Ok(())
  212|       |/// }
  213|       |/// ```
  214|       |#[async_trait]
  215|       |pub trait Backend: Send + Sync {
  216|       |    /// Human-readable name of the backend (e.g., "auggie", "cursor").
  217|       |    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
  218|       |
  219|       |    /// Check if the backend is authenticated and ready to use.
  220|       |    ///
  221|       |    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authentication is valid, or an error with
  222|       |    /// a helpful message if not authenticated.
  223|       |    fn check_auth(&self) -> Result<()>;
  224|       |
  225|       |    /// Discover available model tiers from this backend.
  226|       |    ///
  227|       |    /// Returns a set of model tiers that can be used with this backend.
  228|       |    /// Each tier (e.g., `Sonnet`, `Opus`) maps to the best available
  229|       |    /// model version for that tier in this backend.
  230|       |    async fn available_tiers(&self) -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>>;
  231|       |
  232|       |    /// Resolve a model tier to a fallback chain of model IDs for this backend.
  233|       |    ///
  234|       |    /// Returns a list of model ID strings to try in order. The spawning code
  235|       |    /// should try each model until one succeeds or all fail.
  236|       |    ///
  237|       |    /// Each backend may use different model ID formats:
  238|       |    /// - Auggie: "sonnet4.5", "opus4.5", "haiku4.5"
  239|       |    /// - Cursor: "sonnet-4.6", "opus-4.6", "gpt-5.1-codex-mini"
  240|       |    ///
  241|       |    /// # Effort Level
  242|       |    ///
  243|       |    /// The optional `effort` parameter affects which models are preferred in
  244|       |    /// the fallback chain. For example, with `High` effort:
  245|       |    /// - First try models with high effort variants (e.g., `gpt-5.4-high`)
  246|       |    /// - Then fall back to base models (e.g., `gpt-5.4`)
  247|       |    /// - Then try older versions (e.g., `gpt-5.3-high`, `gpt-5.3`)
  248|       |    ///
  249|       |    /// # Returns
  250|       |    ///
  251|       |    /// A vector of model ID strings to try, ordered by preference (best first).
  252|       |    /// Returns an error only if the tier is completely unavailable on this backend.
  253|       |    fn resolve_tier(&self, tier: ModelTier, effort: Option<EffortLevel>) -> Result<Vec<String>>;
  254|       |
  255|       |    /// Set up MCP server configuration for this backend.
  256|       |    ///
  257|       |    /// Called before creating ACP clients to ensure the MCP server is
  258|       |    /// properly configured and enabled for the backend.
  259|       |    ///
  260|       |    /// # Arguments
  261|       |    ///
  262|       |    /// * `socket_path` - IPC address for MCP communication (socket path on Unix, pipe name on Windows)
  263|       |    ///
  264|       |    /// # Backend-specific behavior
  265|       |    ///
  266|       |    /// - **Auggie**: No-op (mcpServers are passed in session/new)
  267|       |    /// - **Cursor**: Writes to ~/.cursor/mcp.json and runs `agent mcp enable`
  268|       |    async fn setup_mcp(&self, socket_path: &str) -> Result<()>;
  269|       |
  270|       |    /// Clean up MCP server configuration for this backend.
  271|       |    ///
  272|       |    /// Called after the run completes to remove any MCP configuration
  273|       |    /// that was set up for this session.
  274|       |    fn cleanup_mcp(&self) -> Result<()>;
  275|       |
  276|       |    /// Create an ACP client for the given agent type and timeout.
  277|       |    ///
  278|       |    /// # Arguments
  279|       |    ///
  280|       |    /// * `agent_type` - The type of agent (orchestrator, planner, worker)
  281|       |    /// * `cache_dir` - Optional path to the cache directory for this session.
  282|       |    /// * `request_timeout` - Timeout duration for ACP requests.
  283|       |    ///
  284|       |    /// # Returns
  285|       |    ///
  286|       |    /// A boxed trait object implementing `AcpClientTrait`.
  287|       |    ///
  288|       |    /// # Permission Handling
  289|       |    ///
  290|       |    /// For Cursor backend, the agent_type determines tool permissions:
  291|       |    /// - `Orchestrator`/`Planner`: File editing tools are denied
  292|       |    /// - `Worker`: All tools are allowed
  293|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  294|       |    async fn create_client(
  295|       |        &self,
  296|       |        agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  297|       |        cache_dir: Option<&str>,
  298|       |        request_timeout: Duration,
  299|       |    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AcpClientTrait + Send>>;
  300|       |
  301|       |    /// Set up the cache directory for a specific agent type.
  302|       |    ///
  303|       |    /// Creates and configures the cache directory structure needed for
  304|       |    /// the specified agent type, including any tool filtering configuration.
  305|       |    ///
  306|       |    /// # Arguments
  307|       |    ///
  308|       |    /// * `agent_type` - The type of agent to set up the cache for.
  309|       |    /// * `removed_tools` - List of tool names to remove/deny for this agent.
  310|       |    ///
  311|       |    /// # Returns
  312|       |    ///
  313|       |    /// The path to the configured cache directory.
  314|       |    fn setup_agent_cache(
  315|       |        &self,
  316|       |        agent_type: AgentCacheType,
  317|       |        removed_tools: &[&str],
  318|       |    ) -> Result<PathBuf>;
  319|       |
  320|       |    // ========================================================================
  321|       |    // Transport Methods (new transport abstraction layer)
  322|       |    // ========================================================================
  323|       |
  324|       |    /// Returns the list of transport protocols this backend supports.
  325|       |    ///
  326|       |    /// Each backend may support different communication protocols:
  327|       |    /// - **ACP**: JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout, supported by both Auggie and Cursor
  328|       |    /// - **CLI**: Non-interactive CLI mode (currently Cursor only)
  329|       |    ///
  330|       |    /// Use this method to check if a transport kind is available before calling
  331|       |    /// `create_transport()`.
  332|       |    ///
  333|       |    /// # Example
  334|       |    ///
  335|       |    /// ```ignore
  336|       |    /// let backend = BackendKind::Cursor.create();
  337|       |    /// let transports = backend.supported_transports();
  338|       |    ///
  339|       |    /// if transports.contains(&TransportKind::Cli) {
  340|       |    ///     // Use CLI transport for better MCP support
  341|       |    ///     let transport = backend.create_transport(
  342|       |    ///         TransportKind::Cli,
  343|       |    ///         AgentType::Implementer,
  344|       |    ///         config,
  345|       |    ///     ).await?;
  346|       |    /// }
  347|       |    /// ```
  348|      0|    fn supported_transports(&self) -> Vec<TransportKind> {
  349|       |        // Default: only ACP is supported (both Auggie and Cursor support this)
  350|      0|        vec![TransportKind::Acp]
  351|      0|    }
  352|       |
  353|       |    /// Create a transport instance for the specified protocol.
  354|       |    ///
  355|       |    /// Creates and returns a transport configured for the given protocol,
  356|       |    /// agent type, and configuration. The transport is ready to be initialized
  357|       |    /// via `transport.initialize()`.
  358|       |    ///
  359|       |    /// # Arguments
  360|       |    ///
  361|       |    /// * `kind` - The transport protocol to use (ACP, CLI, etc.)
  362|       |    /// * `agent_type` - The type of agent (Orchestrator, Planner, Implementer)
  363|       |    /// * `config` - Transport configuration including workspace, model, timeout
  364|       |    ///
  365|       |    /// # Returns
  366|       |    ///
  367|       |    /// A boxed transport implementing [`AgentTransport`].
  368|       |    ///
  369|       |    /// # Errors
  370|       |    ///
  371|       |    /// Returns an error if:
  372|       |    /// - The transport kind is not in `supported_transports()`
  373|       |    /// - Transport creation fails (e.g., binary not found, configuration error)
  374|       |    ///
  375|       |    /// # Example
  376|       |    ///
  377|       |    /// ```ignore
  378|       |    /// let config = TransportConfig::new("/path/to/workspace")
  379|       |    ///     .with_model("sonnet-4.6")
  380|       |    ///     .with_request_timeout(Duration::from_secs(300));
  381|       |    ///
  382|       |    /// let mut transport = backend.create_transport(
  383|       |    ///     TransportKind::Acp,
  384|       |    ///     AgentType::Implementer,
  385|       |    ///     config,
  386|       |    /// ).await?;
  387|       |    ///
  388|       |    /// transport.initialize().await?;
  389|       |    /// ```
  390|       |    ///
  391|       |    /// # Note
  392|       |    ///
  393|       |    /// This is the new transport-based API. The legacy `create_client()` method
  394|       |    /// is still available for backward compatibility but will be deprecated.
  395|       |    async fn create_transport(
  396|       |        &self,
  397|       |        kind: TransportKind,
  398|       |        _agent_type: AgentType,
  399|       |        _config: TransportConfig,
  400|      0|    ) -> Result<Box<dyn AgentTransport>> {
  401|       |        // Default implementation: check if transport is supported and return error
  402|       |        // Individual backends should override this to provide actual implementations
  403|       |        let supported = self.supported_transports();
  404|       |        if !supported.contains(&kind) {
  405|       |            return Err(anyhow!(
  406|       |                "Transport '{}' is not supported by {} backend. Supported transports: {}",
  407|       |                kind,
  408|       |                self.name(),
  409|       |                supported
  410|       |                    .iter()
  411|      0|                    .map(|t| t.as_str())
  412|       |                    .collect::<Vec<_>>()
  413|       |                    .join(", ")
  414|       |            ));
  415|       |        }
  416|       |
  417|       |        // If we get here, the transport is "supported" but not implemented
  418|       |        Err(anyhow!(
  419|       |            "Transport '{}' is listed as supported by {} backend but not yet implemented. \
  420|       |            This is a bug - please report it.",
  421|       |            kind,
  422|       |            self.name()
  423|       |        ))
  424|      0|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/backend/transport.rs:
    1|       |// Allow some clippy lints for this new module - can be cleaned up later
    2|       |#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
    3|       |#![allow(clippy::len_zero)]
    4|       |#![allow(clippy::missing_const_for_fn)]
    5|       |#![allow(clippy::uninlined_format_args)]
    6|       |#![allow(clippy::use_self)]
    7|       |
    8|       |//! Transport abstraction for agent communication.
    9|       |//!
   10|       |//! This module provides the core transport layer that separates communication
   11|       |//! protocol from backend vendor. It enables support for multiple connection modes
   12|       |//! (ACP, CLI, future protocols) while maintaining a unified interface.
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! # Architecture
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! The transport layer sits between the application and backend-specific implementations:
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! ```text
   19|       |//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   20|       |//! │                      Application                            │
   21|       |//! │  (src/app/orchestrator.rs, session.rs, agent_handler.rs)   │
   22|       |//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   23|       |//!                              │
   24|       |//!                              ▼
   25|       |//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   26|       |//! │                    AgentTransport Trait                     │
   27|       |//! │  (protocol-agnostic interface for agent communication)      │
   28|       |//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   29|       |//!                              │
   30|       |//!           ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
   31|       |//!           ▼                  ▼                  ▼
   32|       |//!   ┌───────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐
   33|       |//!   │ AuggieAcp     │  │ CursorAcp     │  │ CursorCli     │
   34|       |//!   │ Transport     │  │ Transport     │  │ Transport     │
   35|       |//!   └───────────────┘  └───────────────┘  └───────────────┘
   36|       |//! ```
   37|       |//!
   38|       |//! # Separation of Concerns
   39|       |//!
   40|       |//! - **Backend** (`trait.rs`): Vendor-specific configuration (auth, models, MCP setup)
   41|       |//! - **Transport** (this module): Communication protocol (how to talk to the agent)
   42|       |//! - **AgentType**: Permission control (what tools each agent type can use)
   43|       |//!
   44|       |//! # Transport Kinds
   45|       |//!
   46|       |//! - **ACP** (Agent Communication Protocol): JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout, request/response model
   47|       |//! - **CLI**: Non-interactive CLI mode with streaming JSON output (`agent --print`)
   48|       |//!
   49|       |//! # Usage Example
   50|       |//!
   51|       |//! ```ignore
   52|       |//! // Create a transport via the backend
   53|       |//! let backend = BackendKind::Cursor.create();
   54|       |//! let transport = backend.create_transport(
   55|       |//!     TransportKind::Cli,
   56|       |//!     AgentType::Implementer,
   57|       |//!     config,
   58|       |//! ).await?;
   59|       |//!
   60|       |//! // Initialize and create session
   61|       |//! transport.initialize().await?;
   62|       |//! let session = transport.create_session(SessionConfig {
   63|       |//!     model: "sonnet-4.6".into(),
   64|       |//!     workspace: "/path/to/workspace".into(),
   65|       |//!     mcp_servers: vec![],
   66|       |//!     mode: SessionMode::Agent,
   67|       |//! }).await?;
   68|       |//!
   69|       |//! // Send prompts and receive updates
   70|       |//! transport.send_prompt(&session.session_id, "Hello").await?;
   71|       |//! let updates = transport.take_notifications();
   72|       |//! ```
   73|       |
   74|       |use anyhow::Result;
   75|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   76|       |use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   77|       |use serde_json::Value;
   78|       |use tokio::sync::mpsc;
   79|       |
   80|       |use crate::acp::SessionMode;

   82|       |// ============================================================================
   83|       |// Transport Kind
   84|       |// ============================================================================
   85|       |
   86|       |/// Available transport protocols for agent communication.
   87|       |///
   88|       |/// Each transport kind represents a different communication mechanism
   89|       |/// with its own trade-offs:
   90|       |///
   91|       |/// - **ACP**: Full duplex, supports tool permission requests, but Cursor's
   92|       |///   implementation has broken MCP tool support.
   93|       |/// - **CLI**: Streaming JSON output, properly loads MCP servers from config,
   94|       |///   but runs as separate processes per prompt.
   95|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Default)]
   96|       |pub enum TransportKind {
   97|       |    /// ACP protocol (Agent Communication Protocol).
   98|       |    ///
   99|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout with bidirectional communication.
  100|       |    /// Supports request/response patterns and server-initiated notifications.
  101|       |    ///
  102|       |    /// Used by: `auggie --acp`, `agent acp`
  103|       |    #[default]
  104|       |    Acp,
  105|       |
  106|       |    /// Non-interactive CLI mode.
  107|       |    ///
  108|       |    /// Spawns the agent CLI with `--print` flag for each prompt, receiving
  109|       |    /// streaming JSON output. Better MCP support but less interactive.
  110|       |    ///
  111|       |    /// Used by: `agent --print --output-format stream-json`
  112|       |    Cli,
  113|       |}
  114|       |
  115|       |impl TransportKind {
  116|       |    /// Returns the string identifier for this transport kind.
  117|       |    #[must_use]
  118|     19|    pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
  119|     19|        match self {
  120|      9|            Self::Acp => "acp",
  121|     10|            Self::Cli => "cli",
  122|       |        }
  123|     19|    }
  124|       |}
  125|       |
  126|       |impl std::fmt::Display for TransportKind {
  127|      5|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
  128|      5|        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
  129|      5|    }
  130|       |}
  131|       |
  132|       |// ============================================================================
  133|       |// Agent Type (for permission control)
  134|       |// ============================================================================
  135|       |
  136|       |/// Type of agent for permission and capability control.
  137|       |///
  138|       |/// Different agent types have different tool access permissions:
  139|       |///
  140|       |/// | Agent Type   | File Editing | MCP Tools                        |
  141|       |/// |-------------|--------------|----------------------------------|
  142|       |/// | Orchestrator | ❌ Denied    | spawn_agents, decompose, skip_tasks, list_tasks, complete |
  143|       |/// | Planner      | ❌ Denied    | set_goal, create_task, complete  |
  144|       |/// | Implementer  | ✅ Allowed   | complete only                    |
  145|       |///
  146|       |/// This enum is used by transports to configure permission policies
  147|       |/// that control which tools are allowed or denied for each agent type.
  148|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
  149|       |pub enum AgentType {
  150|       |    /// Orchestrator agent that coordinates task execution.
  151|       |    ///
  152|       |    /// Has access to spawn_agents, decompose, skip_tasks, list_tasks.
  153|       |    /// Cannot edit files directly.
  154|       |    Orchestrator,
  155|       |
  156|       |    /// Planner agent that creates task plans.
  157|       |    ///
  158|       |    /// Has access to set_goal, create_task.
  159|       |    /// Cannot edit files directly.
  160|       |    Planner,
  161|       |
  162|       |    /// Implementer agent that executes tasks.
  163|       |    ///
  164|       |    /// Full file system access.
  165|       |    /// Only has the complete MCP tool.
  166|       |    Implementer,
  167|       |}
  168|       |

  170|       |    /// Returns the string identifier for this agent type.

  173|     11|        match self {
  174|      3|            Self::Orchestrator => "orchestrator",
  175|      3|            Self::Planner => "planner",
  176|      5|            Self::Implementer => "implementer",
  177|       |        }
  178|     11|    }
  179|       |}

  266|       |// ============================================================================
  267|       |// Session Updates (streaming responses)
  268|       |// ============================================================================
  269|       |
  270|       |/// Streaming updates from an agent session.
  271|       |///
  272|       |/// These represent the different types of messages that can be received
  273|       |/// from an agent during execution. Both ACP notifications and CLI streaming
  274|       |/// JSON are normalized to these variants.
  275|       |///
  276|       |/// # ACP Mapping
  277|       |///
  278|       |/// ACP `session/update` notifications with different `type` fields map to:
  279|       |/// - `"text"` → `SessionUpdate::Text`
  280|       |/// - `"tool_use"` → `SessionUpdate::ToolUse`
  281|       |/// - `"tool_result"` → `SessionUpdate::ToolResult`
  282|       |/// - `"completion"` / `"end"` → `SessionUpdate::Completion`
  283|       |///
  284|       |/// # CLI Mapping
  285|       |///
  286|       |/// CLI `--output-format stream-json` lines map similarly:
  287|       |/// - `{"type":"text",...}` → `SessionUpdate::Text`
  288|       |/// - `{"type":"tool_use",...}` → `SessionUpdate::ToolUse`
  289|       |/// - `{"type":"tool_result",...}` → `SessionUpdate::ToolResult`
  290|       |/// - `{"type":"result",...}` → `SessionUpdate::Completion`
  291|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  292|       |#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
  293|       |pub enum SessionUpdate {
  294|       |    /// Text content from the agent.
  295|       |    ///
  296|       |    /// This is the agent's natural language output, delivered incrementally
  297|       |    /// as the agent generates it.
  298|       |    Text {
  299|       |        /// The session this update belongs to.
  300|       |        session_id: String,
  301|       |        /// The text content.
  302|       |        content: String,
  303|       |    },
  304|       |
  305|       |    /// Agent is calling a tool.
  306|       |    ///
  307|       |    /// Indicates the agent wants to use a tool. For MCP tools, the
  308|       |    /// application should execute the tool and respond with `respond_to_tool()`.
  309|       |    ToolUse {
  310|       |        /// The session this update belongs to.
  311|       |        session_id: String,
  312|       |        /// Unique identifier for this tool use (for responding).
  313|       |        tool_use_id: String,
  314|       |        /// Name of the tool being called.
  315|       |        tool_name: String,
  316|       |        /// Tool input parameters as JSON.
  317|       |        input: Value,
  318|       |    },
  319|       |
  320|       |    /// Result from a tool call.
  321|       |    ///
  322|       |    /// Contains the output from a tool that was executed. This is typically
  323|       |    /// sent by the agent/backend after it processes a tool call internally,
  324|       |    /// or after we respond to an MCP tool call.
  325|       |    ToolResult {
  326|       |        /// The session this update belongs to.
  327|       |        session_id: String,
  328|       |        /// ID of the tool use this result corresponds to.
  329|       |        tool_use_id: String,
  330|       |        /// The tool output content.
  331|       |        content: String,
  332|       |        /// Whether the tool execution was successful.
  333|       |        #[serde(default = "default_true")]
  334|       |        is_success: bool,
  335|       |    },
  336|       |
  337|       |    /// Agent has finished processing.
  338|       |    ///
  339|       |    /// Indicates the agent completed its turn. May include a final result
  340|       |    /// or summary message.
  341|       |    Completion {
  342|       |        /// The session this update belongs to.
  343|       |        session_id: String,
  344|       |        /// Final result or summary (optional).
  345|       |        result: Option<String>,
  346|       |        /// Whether the completion was successful.
  347|       |        #[serde(default = "default_true")]
  348|       |        success: bool,
  349|       |    },
  350|       |
  351|       |    /// Raw/unknown update type (for forward compatibility).
  352|       |    ///
  353|       |    /// Used for update types not yet supported by this enum.
  354|       |    /// Contains the raw JSON for inspection.
  355|       |    Raw {
  356|       |        /// The session this update belongs to (if available).
  357|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
  358|       |        /// The raw JSON data.
  359|       |        data: Value,
  360|       |    },
  361|       |}
  362|       |
  363|       |/// Default value function for serde.
  364|      2|const fn default_true() -> bool {
  365|      2|    true
  366|      2|}
  367|       |
  368|       |impl SessionUpdate {
  369|       |    /// Get the session ID if available.
  370|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  371|     17|    pub fn session_id(&self) -> Option<&str> {
  372|     17|        match self {
  373|      4|            Self::Text { session_id, .. }
  374|      3|            | Self::ToolUse { session_id, .. }
  375|      3|            | Self::ToolResult { session_id, .. }
  376|     13|            | Self::Completion { session_id, .. } => Some(session_id),
                                               ^3
  377|      4|            Self::Raw { session_id, .. } => session_id.as_deref(),
  378|       |        }
  379|     17|    }
  380|       |
  381|       |    /// Check if this is a completion update.
  382|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]

  384|      7|        matches!(self, Self::Completion { .. })

  386|       |

  390|      7|        matches!(self, Self::ToolUse { .. })
                      ^5
  391|      7|    }
  392|       |}

  432|       |
  433|       |// ============================================================================
  434|       |// Agent Transport Trait
  435|       |// ============================================================================
  436|       |
  437|       |/// Trait defining the transport interface for agent communication.
  438|       |///
  439|       |/// This trait abstracts the communication protocol (ACP, CLI, etc.) from
  440|       |/// the application logic, enabling different backends to use different
  441|       |/// transport mechanisms while presenting a unified interface.
  442|       |///
  443|       |/// # Lifecycle
  444|       |///
  445|       |/// A typical transport lifecycle:
  446|       |///
  447|       |/// 1. **Create**: Backend creates transport via `create_transport()`
  448|       |/// 2. **Initialize**: Call `initialize()` to establish connection
  449|       |/// 3. **Create Session**: Call `create_session()` to start a session
  450|       |/// 4. **Take Notifications**: Call `take_notifications()` to get the update receiver
  451|       |/// 5. **Send Prompts**: Call `send_prompt()` to send messages
  452|       |/// 6. **Handle Tool Calls**: Call `respond_to_tool()` for MCP tool results
  453|       |/// 7. **Shutdown**: Call `shutdown()` when done
  454|       |///
  455|       |/// # Thread Safety
  456|       |///
  457|       |/// All implementations must be `Send + Sync` to support async contexts
  458|       |/// and potential sharing across tasks.
  459|       |///
  460|       |/// # Error Handling
  461|       |///
  462|       |/// Methods return `anyhow::Result` for flexibility in error types.
  463|       |/// Implementations should provide descriptive error messages.
  464|       |#[async_trait]
  465|       |pub trait AgentTransport: Send + Sync {
  466|       |    /// Initialize the transport connection.
  467|       |    ///
  468|       |    /// This establishes the connection to the agent process.
  469|       |    /// For ACP, this sends the initialize JSON-RPC request.
  470|       |    /// For CLI, this may be a no-op or verify the CLI is available.
  471|       |    ///
  472|       |    /// # Errors
  473|       |    ///
  474|       |    /// Returns an error if the connection cannot be established,
  475|       |    /// authentication fails, or the agent process cannot be started.
  476|       |    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
  477|       |
  478|       |    /// Create a new agent session.
  479|       |    ///
  480|       |    /// Initializes a session with the given configuration. The returned
  481|       |    /// `SessionInfo` contains the session ID needed for subsequent calls.
  482|       |    ///
  483|       |    /// # Arguments
  484|       |    ///
  485|       |    /// * `config` - Session configuration including model, workspace, etc.
  486|       |    ///
  487|       |    /// # Returns
  488|       |    ///
  489|       |    /// Session information including the session ID.
  490|       |    ///
  491|       |    /// # Errors
  492|       |    ///
  493|       |    /// Returns an error if session creation fails (invalid model,
  494|       |    /// workspace access denied, etc.).
  495|       |    async fn create_session(&mut self, config: SessionConfig) -> Result<SessionInfo>;
  496|       |
  497|       |    /// Send a prompt to the agent session.
  498|       |    ///
  499|       |    /// Sends a message to the specified session. Responses come via
  500|       |    /// the notification channel obtained from `take_notifications()`.
  501|       |    ///
  502|       |    /// # Arguments
  503|       |    ///
  504|       |    /// * `session_id` - The session to send to (from `SessionInfo`)
  505|       |    /// * `prompt` - The prompt text to send
  506|       |    ///
  507|       |    /// # Errors
  508|       |    ///
  509|       |    /// Returns an error if the session doesn't exist or if sending fails.
  510|       |    async fn send_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()>;
  511|       |
  512|       |    /// Take the notification receiver for streaming updates.
  513|       |    ///
  514|       |    /// Returns the receiver end of the channel that receives `SessionUpdate`
  515|       |    /// messages. This can only be called once; subsequent calls return `None`.
  516|       |    ///
  517|       |    /// The receiver should be consumed by a background task that processes
  518|       |    /// updates (text, tool calls, completions) as they arrive.
  519|       |    ///
  520|       |    /// # Returns
  521|       |    ///
  522|       |    /// `Some(receiver)` on first call, `None` on subsequent calls.
  523|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  524|       |    fn take_notifications(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>>;
  525|       |
  526|       |    /// Respond to a tool call with the result.
  527|       |    ///
  528|       |    /// After receiving a `SessionUpdate::ToolUse`, the application should
  529|       |    /// execute the tool and send the result back via this method.
  530|       |    ///
  531|       |    /// # Arguments
  532|       |    ///
  533|       |    /// * `session_id` - The session the tool call belongs to
  534|       |    /// * `tool_use_id` - The tool use ID from `SessionUpdate::ToolUse`
  535|       |    /// * `result` - The tool execution result
  536|       |    ///
  537|       |    /// # Errors
  538|       |    ///
  539|       |    /// Returns an error if the session doesn't exist, the tool use ID is
  540|       |    /// invalid, or if sending the response fails.
  541|       |    ///
  542|       |    /// # Note
  543|       |    ///
  544|       |    /// Not all transports support this. CLI transport may ignore this call
  545|       |    /// since tool results are handled differently in non-interactive mode.
  546|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  547|       |    async fn respond_to_tool(
  548|       |        &mut self,
  549|       |        session_id: &str,
  550|       |        tool_use_id: &str,
  551|       |        result: ToolResult,
  552|       |    ) -> Result<()>;
  553|       |
  554|       |    /// Gracefully shutdown the transport.
  555|       |    ///
  556|       |    /// Cleans up resources, terminates child processes, and closes channels.
  557|       |    /// This should be called when the session is complete.
  558|       |    ///
  559|       |    /// # Errors
  560|       |    ///
  561|       |    /// Returns an error if shutdown fails (e.g., process won't terminate).
  562|       |    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
  563|       |
  564|       |    // ========================================================================
  565|       |    // Legacy compatibility methods (for gradual migration from AcpClientTrait)
  566|       |    // ========================================================================
  567|       |
  568|       |    /// Receive the next raw notification from the transport.
  569|       |    ///
  570|       |    /// This is a legacy compatibility method that enables gradual migration
  571|       |    /// from `AcpClientTrait::recv()`. New code should use `take_notifications()`
  572|       |    /// instead.
  573|       |    ///
  574|       |    /// The default implementation returns an error indicating this transport
  575|       |    /// doesn't support polling. Transports that wrap ACP clients can override
  576|       |    /// this to provide backward compatibility.
  577|       |    ///
  578|       |    /// # Errors
  579|       |    ///
  580|       |    /// Returns an error if the transport doesn't support polling or if no
  581|       |    /// more notifications are available.
  582|      0|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  583|       |        Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
  584|       |            "This transport does not support polling via recv(). Use take_notifications() instead."
  585|       |        ))
  586|      0|    }
  587|       |
  588|       |    /// Take the raw notification receiver for legacy routing.
  589|       |    ///
  590|       |    /// This is a legacy compatibility method that enables gradual migration
  591|       |    /// from `AcpClientTrait::take_notification_rx()`. New code should use
  592|       |    /// `take_notifications()` instead.
  593|       |    ///
  594|       |    /// The default implementation returns `None`. Transports that wrap ACP
  595|       |    /// clients can override this to provide backward compatibility.
  596|       |    ///
  597|       |    /// # Returns
  598|       |    ///
  599|       |    /// `Some(receiver)` if the transport supports raw notifications,
  600|       |    /// `None` otherwise.
  601|      0|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  602|      0|        None
  603|      0|    }
  604|       |
  605|       |    /// Returns the transport kind for this transport.
  606|       |    ///
  607|       |    /// This is used to detect which transport type is being used, allowing
  608|       |    /// the application to make transport-specific decisions (e.g., using
  609|       |    /// unique sockets for CLI transports to prevent MCP server caching).
  610|       |    ///
  611|       |    /// # Returns
  612|       |    ///
  613|       |    /// The `TransportKind` for this transport. Defaults to `Acp` for
  614|       |    /// backward compatibility.
  615|    161|    fn kind(&self) -> TransportKind {
  616|    161|        TransportKind::Acp
  617|    161|    }
  618|       |}
  619|       |
  620|       |// ============================================================================
  621|       |// Tests
  622|       |// ============================================================================
  623|       |

  634|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_as_str() {
  635|      1|        assert_eq!(TransportKind::Acp.as_str(), "acp");
  636|      1|        assert_eq!(TransportKind::Cli.as_str(), "cli");
  637|      1|    }
  638|       |
  639|       |    #[test]
  640|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_display() {
  641|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{}", TransportKind::Acp), "acp");
  642|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{}", TransportKind::Cli), "cli");
  643|      1|    }
  644|       |
  645|       |    #[test]
  646|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_default() {
  647|      1|        assert_eq!(TransportKind::default(), TransportKind::Acp);
  648|      1|    }
  649|       |
  650|       |    #[test]
  651|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_equality() {
  652|      1|        assert_eq!(TransportKind::Acp, TransportKind::Acp);
  653|      1|        assert_eq!(TransportKind::Cli, TransportKind::Cli);
  654|      1|        assert_ne!(TransportKind::Acp, TransportKind::Cli);
  655|      1|    }
  656|       |
  657|       |    #[test]
  658|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_debug() {
  659|      1|        let acp = TransportKind::Acp;
  660|      1|        let cli = TransportKind::Cli;
  661|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{:?}", acp), "Acp");
  662|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{:?}", cli), "Cli");
  663|      1|    }
  664|       |
  665|       |    #[test]
  666|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_clone() {
  667|      1|        let original = TransportKind::Cli;
  668|      1|        let cloned = original;
  669|      1|        assert_eq!(original, cloned);
  670|      1|    }
  671|       |
  672|       |    #[test]
  673|      1|    fn test_transport_kind_hash() {
  674|       |        use std::collections::HashSet;
  675|      1|        let mut set = HashSet::new();
  676|      1|        set.insert(TransportKind::Acp);
  677|      1|        set.insert(TransportKind::Cli);
  678|      1|        assert_eq!(set.len(), 2);
  679|      1|        assert!(set.contains(&TransportKind::Acp));
  680|      1|        assert!(set.contains(&TransportKind::Cli));
  681|      1|    }

  880|       |
  881|       |    #[test]
  882|      1|    fn test_session_update_session_id() {
  883|      1|        let text = SessionUpdate::Text {
  884|      1|            session_id: "sess-1".into(),
  885|      1|            content: "Hello".into(),
  886|      1|        };
  887|      1|        assert_eq!(text.session_id(), Some("sess-1"));
  888|       |
  889|      1|        let raw = SessionUpdate::Raw {
  890|      1|            session_id: None,
  891|      1|            data: json!({}),
  892|      1|        };
  893|      1|        assert_eq!(raw.session_id(), None);
  894|      1|    }
  895|       |
  896|       |    #[test]
  897|      1|    fn test_session_update_session_id_all_variants() {
  898|       |        // Text
  899|      1|        let text = SessionUpdate::Text {
  900|      1|            session_id: "s1".into(),
  901|      1|            content: "".into(),
  902|      1|        };
  903|      1|        assert_eq!(text.session_id(), Some("s1"));
  904|       |
  905|       |        // ToolUse
  906|      1|        let tool_use = SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  907|      1|            session_id: "s2".into(),
  908|      1|            tool_use_id: "t1".into(),
  909|      1|            tool_name: "test".into(),
  910|      1|            input: json!({}),
  911|      1|        };
  912|      1|        assert_eq!(tool_use.session_id(), Some("s2"));
  913|       |
  914|       |        // ToolResult
  915|      1|        let tool_result = SessionUpdate::ToolResult {
  916|      1|            session_id: "s3".into(),
  917|      1|            tool_use_id: "t1".into(),
  918|      1|            content: "".into(),
  919|      1|            is_success: true,
  920|      1|        };
  921|      1|        assert_eq!(tool_result.session_id(), Some("s3"));
  922|       |
  923|       |        // Completion
  924|      1|        let completion = SessionUpdate::Completion {
  925|      1|            session_id: "s4".into(),
  926|      1|            result: None,
  927|      1|            success: true,
  928|      1|        };
  929|      1|        assert_eq!(completion.session_id(), Some("s4"));
  930|       |
  931|       |        // Raw with session_id
  932|      1|        let raw_with_id = SessionUpdate::Raw {
  933|      1|            session_id: Some("s5".into()),
  934|      1|            data: json!({}),
  935|      1|        };
  936|      1|        assert_eq!(raw_with_id.session_id(), Some("s5"));
  937|      1|    }
  938|       |
  939|       |    #[test]
  940|      1|    fn test_session_update_is_completion() {
  941|      1|        let completion = SessionUpdate::Completion {
  942|      1|            session_id: "sess-1".into(),
  943|      1|            result: None,
  944|      1|            success: true,
  945|      1|        };
  946|      1|        assert!(completion.is_completion());
  947|       |

    1|       |//! Configuration file loading for Paperboat
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles loading agent configuration from TOML files.
    4|       |//! Configuration is loaded from two locations, with project-level
    5|       |//! settings taking priority over user-level settings:
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! 1. User-level: `~/.paperboat/agents/`
    8|       |//! 2. Project-level: `.paperboat/agents/`
    9|       |//!
   10|       |//! Each agent type has its own configuration file:
   11|       |//! - `orchestrator.toml`
   12|       |//! - `planner.toml`
   13|       |//! - `implementer.toml`
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! # Backend Selection
   16|       |//!
   17|       |//! Backend selection follows a priority order:
   18|       |//! 1. `PAPERBOAT_BACKEND` environment variable
   19|       |//! 2. Project config file (`.paperboat/config.toml`)
   20|       |//! 3. User config file (`~/.paperboat/config.toml`)
   21|       |//! 4. Default (Auggie)
   22|       |
   23|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   24|       |use serde::Deserialize;
   25|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
   26|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   27|       |
   28|       |use crate::backend::BackendKind;
   29|       |use crate::error::{suggest_model_alias, ConfigError, KNOWN_MODEL_ALIASES};
   30|       |use crate::models::{EffortLevel, ModelConfig, ModelFallbackChain, ModelTier};
   31|       |
   32|       |/// Configuration for a single agent loaded from TOML
   33|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]
   34|       |pub struct AgentFileConfig {
   35|       |    /// Model to use for this agent (e.g., "opus", "sonnet4.5", "openai, opus, gemini")
   36|       |    pub model: Option<String>,
   37|       |    /// Effort/thinking level for this agent (e.g., "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh")
   38|       |    pub effort: Option<String>,
   39|       |}

   85|       |    /// Validates a single model alias (not a chain).
   86|     29|    fn validate_single_model(
   87|     29|        &self,

   92|       |
   93|       |        // Check if it's a known alias
   94|     29|        if KNOWN_MODEL_ALIASES.contains(&model_lower.as_str()) {
   95|     22|            return Ok(());
   96|      7|        }
   97|       |
   98|       |        // Check if it looks like a versioned model (family + version number)

  108|       |            // The actual resolution will validate against available models later
  109|      1|            return Ok(());
  110|      6|        }
  111|       |
  112|       |        // Invalid model - try to suggest a correction
  113|      6|        let suggestion = suggest_model_alias(&model_lower);
  114|       |

  122|      3|            Err(ConfigError::invalid_model(model, suggestion.as_deref()))

  176|       |/// Loads all agent configurations from both user and project directories.
  177|       |/// Project-level settings override user-level settings.

  261|       |// ============================================================================
  262|       |// Backend Configuration

  264|       |
  265|       |use crate::backend::BackendConfig;
  266|       |
  267|       |/// Configuration loaded from TOML config files for backend selection.
  268|       |///
  269|       |/// This struct is used for deserializing config files, not for runtime configuration.
  270|       |/// The `backend` field can contain a backend name with optional transport suffix:
  271|       |/// - `"cursor"` - Cursor with default transport (CLI)
  272|       |/// - `"cursor:cli"` - Cursor with CLI transport (explicit)
  273|       |/// - `"cursor:acp"` - Cursor with ACP transport
  274|       |/// - `"auggie"` - Auggie with default transport (ACP)
  275|       |///
  276|       |/// See [`BackendConfig`] for the parsed runtime type.
  277|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]
  278|       |pub struct FileBackendConfig {
  279|       |    /// Backend to use with optional transport (e.g., "auggie", "cursor", "cursor:cli").
  280|       |    /// If not specified, defaults to the system default (Auggie with ACP).
  281|       |    pub backend: Option<String>,
  282|       |}
  283|       |
  284|       |/// Environment variable name for backend selection.
  285|       |const PAPERBOAT_BACKEND_ENV: &str = "PAPERBOAT_BACKEND";
  286|       |
  287|       |/// Loads the backend configuration from environment and config files.
  288|       |///
  289|       |/// # Backend:Transport Syntax
  290|       |///
  291|       |/// Both environment variables and config files support the `backend:transport` syntax:
  292|       |///
  293|       |/// ```toml
  294|       |/// # .paperboat/config.toml
  295|       |/// backend = "cursor"       # Uses default transport (CLI for Cursor)
  296|       |/// backend = "cursor:cli"   # Explicitly use CLI transport
  297|       |/// backend = "cursor:acp"   # Explicitly use ACP transport
  298|       |/// backend = "auggie:acp"   # Auggie with ACP (only option)
  299|       |/// ```
  300|       |///
  301|       |/// ```bash
  302|       |/// # Environment variable
  303|       |/// PAPERBOAT_BACKEND=cursor:cli cargo run -- "task"
  304|       |/// ```
  305|       |///
  306|       |/// # Priority Order
  307|       |///
  308|       |/// Priority order (highest to lowest):
  309|       |/// 1. `PAPERBOAT_BACKEND` environment variable
  310|       |/// 2. Project config file (`.paperboat/config.toml`)
  311|       |/// 3. User config file (`~/.paperboat/config.toml`)
  312|       |/// 4. Default (`BackendKind::Auggie` with ACP transport)
  313|       |///
  314|       |/// Invalid backend names are logged as warnings and fall back to the default.
  315|       |///
  316|       |/// # Returns
  317|       |///
  318|       |/// A [`BackendConfig`] containing the selected backend kind and optional transport.
  319|       |///
  320|       |/// # Example
  321|       |///
  322|       |/// ```ignore
  323|       |/// use paperboat::config::load_backend_config;
  324|       |///
  325|       |/// // Respects PAPERBOAT_BACKEND=cursor:cli if set
  326|       |/// let config = load_backend_config();
  327|       |/// let backend = config.kind.create();
  328|       |/// let transport = config.effective_transport();
  329|       |/// ```
  330|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
  331|     13|pub fn load_backend_config() -> BackendConfig {
  332|       |    // Priority 1: Environment variable
  333|     13|    if let Ok(backend_str) = std::env::var(PAPERBOAT_BACKEND_ENV) {
                            ^12
  334|     12|        let backend_str = backend_str.trim();
  335|     12|        if !backend_str.is_empty() {
  336|     10|            match BackendConfig::parse(backend_str) {
  337|      7|                Ok(config) => {
  338|      7|                    tracing::debug!(
  339|       |                        "Backend '{}' with transport '{}' selected from {} environment variable",
  340|       |                        config.kind,
  341|      0|                        config.effective_transport(),
  342|       |                        PAPERBOAT_BACKEND_ENV
  343|       |                    );
  344|      7|                    return config;
  345|       |                }
  346|      3|                Err(err) => {
  347|      3|                    tracing::warn!(
  348|       |                        "Invalid {} value '{}': {}. Using default backend.",
  349|       |                        PAPERBOAT_BACKEND_ENV,
  350|       |                        backend_str,
  351|       |                        err
  352|       |                    );
  353|       |                }
  354|       |            }
  355|      2|        }
  356|      1|    }
  357|       |
  358|       |    // Priority 2: Project config file (.paperboat/config.toml)
  359|      6|    if let Some(config) = load_backend_from_config(&project_backend_config_path()) {
                              ^0
  360|      0|        tracing::debug!(
  361|       |            "Backend '{}' with transport '{}' selected from project config file",
  362|       |            config.kind,
  363|      0|            config.effective_transport()
  364|       |        );
  365|      0|        return config;
  366|      6|    }
  367|       |
  368|       |    // Priority 3: User config file (~/.paperboat/config.toml)
  369|      6|    if let Some(config) = load_backend_from_config(&user_backend_config_path()) {
                              ^0
  370|      0|        tracing::debug!(
  371|       |            "Backend '{}' with transport '{}' selected from user config file",
  372|       |            config.kind,
  373|      0|            config.effective_transport()
  374|       |        );
  375|      0|        return config;
  376|      6|    }
  377|       |
  378|       |    // Priority 4: Default (Auggie with ACP transport)
  379|      6|    let default_config = BackendConfig::default();
  380|      6|    tracing::debug!(
  381|       |        "Using default backend '{}' with transport '{}'",
  382|       |        default_config.kind,
  383|      0|        default_config.effective_transport()
  384|       |    );
  385|      6|    default_config
  386|     13|}
  387|       |
  388|       |/// Loads the backend configuration from environment and config files.
  389|       |///
  390|       |/// This is a convenience function that returns only the [`BackendKind`],
  391|       |/// discarding transport information. For full configuration including
  392|       |/// transport, use [`load_backend_config`] instead.
  393|       |///
  394|       |/// # Deprecation Note
  395|       |///
  396|       |/// This function is kept for backwards compatibility. New code should use
  397|       |/// [`load_backend_config`] which returns the full [`BackendConfig`].
  398|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
  399|      7|pub fn load_backend_kind() -> BackendKind {
  400|      7|    load_backend_config().kind
  401|      7|}
  402|       |
  403|       |/// Check if a backend was explicitly configured via environment variable or config files.
  404|       |///
  405|       |/// This function checks for explicit configuration without falling back to defaults.
  406|       |/// Returns `Some(BackendConfig)` if explicitly configured, `None` if no explicit config exists.
  407|       |///
  408|       |/// # Use Case
  409|       |///
  410|       |/// This is useful for determining whether to prompt the user to select a backend
  411|       |/// when multiple are available. If the user has explicitly configured a backend,
  412|       |/// we should use it. If not, we can offer a choice.
  413|       |///
  414|       |/// # Priority Order
  415|       |///
  416|       |/// Same as [`load_backend_config`]:
  417|       |/// 1. `PAPERBOAT_BACKEND` environment variable
  418|       |/// 2. Project config file (`.paperboat/config.toml`)
  419|       |/// 3. User config file (`~/.paperboat/config.toml`)
  420|       |///
  421|       |/// Returns `None` if none of these are configured (would have fallen back to default).
  422|      0|pub fn get_explicit_backend_config() -> Option<BackendConfig> {
  423|       |    // Check environment variable
  424|      0|    if let Ok(backend_str) = std::env::var(PAPERBOAT_BACKEND_ENV) {
  425|      0|        let backend_str = backend_str.trim();
  426|      0|        if !backend_str.is_empty() {
  427|      0|            if let Ok(config) = BackendConfig::parse(backend_str) {
  428|      0|                return Some(config);
  429|      0|            }
  430|      0|        }
  431|      0|    }
  432|       |
  433|       |    // Check project config file
  434|      0|    if let Some(config) = load_backend_from_config(&project_backend_config_path()) {
  435|      0|        return Some(config);
  436|      0|    }
  437|       |
  438|       |    // Check user config file
  439|      0|    if let Some(config) = load_backend_from_config(&user_backend_config_path()) {
  440|      0|        return Some(config);
  441|      0|    }
  442|       |
  443|       |    // No explicit config found
  444|      0|    None
  445|      0|}
  446|       |
  447|       |/// Returns the user-level backend config file path (~/.paperboat/config.toml)
  448|      6|fn user_backend_config_path() -> PathBuf {
  449|      6|    let home = shellexpand::tilde("~/.paperboat/config.toml").to_string();
  450|      6|    PathBuf::from(home)
  451|      6|}
  452|       |
  453|       |/// Returns the project-level backend config file path (.paperboat/config.toml)
  454|      6|fn project_backend_config_path() -> PathBuf {
  455|      6|    PathBuf::from(".paperboat/config.toml")
  456|      6|}
  457|       |
  458|       |/// Attempts to load backend configuration from a config file.
  459|       |///
  460|       |/// Returns `Some(BackendConfig)` if the file exists, is valid, and contains
  461|       |/// a valid backend setting with optional transport. Returns `None` otherwise
  462|       |/// (file doesn't exist, parse error, or no backend specified).
  463|       |///
  464|       |/// # Supported Syntax
  465|       |///
  466|       |/// The `backend` field in config files can be:
  467|       |/// - `"cursor"` - Cursor with default transport (CLI)
  468|       |/// - `"cursor:cli"` - Cursor with CLI transport (explicit)
  469|       |/// - `"cursor:acp"` - Cursor with ACP transport
  470|       |/// - `"auggie"` or `"augment"` - Auggie with default transport (ACP)
  471|     27|fn load_backend_from_config(path: &Path) -> Option<BackendConfig> {
  472|     27|    if !path.exists() {
  473|     13|        return None;
  474|     14|    }
  475|       |
  476|     14|    let contents = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {
  477|     14|        Ok(c) => c,
  478|      0|        Err(e) => {
  479|      0|            tracing::warn!(
  480|       |                "Failed to read backend config file '{}': {}",
  481|      0|                path.display(),
  482|       |                e
  483|       |            );
  484|      0|            return None;
  485|       |        }
  486|       |    };
  487|       |
  488|     14|    let file_config: FileBackendConfig = match toml::from_str(&contents) {
                      ^13          ^13
  489|     13|        Ok(c) => c,
  490|      1|        Err(e) => {
  491|      1|            tracing::warn!(
  492|       |                "Failed to parse backend config file '{}': {}",
  493|      0|                path.display(),
  494|       |                e
  495|       |            );
  496|      1|            return None;
  497|       |        }
  498|       |    };
  499|       |
  500|     13|    let backend_str = file_config.backend.as_ref()?;
                      ^12                                       ^1
  501|     12|    let backend_str = backend_str.trim();
  502|     12|    if backend_str.is_empty() {
  503|      2|        return None;
  504|     10|    }
  505|       |
  506|     10|    match BackendConfig::parse(backend_str) {
  507|      7|        Ok(config) => Some(config),
  508|      3|        Err(err) => {
  509|      3|            tracing::warn!(
  510|       |                "Invalid backend '{}' in config file '{}': {}. Ignoring.",
  511|       |                backend_str,
  512|      0|                path.display(),
  513|       |                err
  514|       |            );
  515|      3|            None
  516|       |        }
  517|       |    }

  679|      1|            model: Some("sonnett".to_string()),
  680|      1|            ..Default::default()
  681|      1|        };

  683|      1|        assert!(result.is_err());
  684|       |

  686|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  687|      1|        assert!(
  688|      1|            display.contains("Did you mean 'sonnet'?"),
  689|       |            "Expected suggestion in: {display}"
  690|       |        );
  691|      1|    }

  846|       |    #[test]
  847|      1|    fn test_build_model_config_default() {
  848|      1|        let loaded = LoadedAgentConfigs::default();
  849|      1|        let available_tiers: HashSet<ModelTier> =
  850|      1|            [ModelTier::Opus, ModelTier::Sonnet].into_iter().collect();
  851|       |
  852|      1|        let config = build_model_config(&loaded, available_tiers).unwrap();
  853|       |
  854|       |        // Should use ModelConfig::new() defaults when no model specified
  855|      1|        let default_config = crate::models::ModelConfig::default();
  856|      1|        assert_eq!(config.orchestrator_model, default_config.orchestrator_model);
  857|      1|        assert_eq!(config.planner_model, default_config.planner_model);
  858|      1|        assert_eq!(config.implementer_model, default_config.implementer_model);
  859|      1|    }
  860|       |
  861|       |    #[test]
  862|      1|    fn test_build_model_config_with_model_strings() {
  863|      1|        let loaded = LoadedAgentConfigs {
  864|      1|            orchestrator: AgentFileConfig {
  865|      1|                model: Some("opus".to_string()),
  866|      1|                ..Default::default()
  867|      1|            },
  868|      1|            planner: AgentFileConfig {
  869|      1|                model: Some("sonnet".to_string()),
  870|      1|                ..Default::default()
  871|      1|            },
  872|      1|            implementer: AgentFileConfig {
  873|      1|                model: Some("haiku".to_string()),
  874|      1|                ..Default::default()
  875|      1|            },
  876|      1|        };
  877|       |
  878|      1|        let available_tiers: HashSet<ModelTier> =
  879|      1|            [ModelTier::Opus, ModelTier::Sonnet, ModelTier::Haiku]
  880|      1|                .into_iter()
  881|      1|                .collect();
  882|       |
  883|      1|        let config = build_model_config(&loaded, available_tiers).unwrap();
  884|       |
  885|      1|        assert_eq!(config.orchestrator_model.primary(), Some(ModelTier::Opus));
  886|      1|        assert_eq!(config.planner_model.primary(), Some(ModelTier::Sonnet));
  887|      1|        assert_eq!(config.implementer_model.primary(), Some(ModelTier::Haiku));
  888|      1|    }
  889|       |
  890|       |    #[test]
  891|      1|    fn test_build_model_config_with_fallback_chain() {
  892|      1|        let loaded = LoadedAgentConfigs {
  893|      1|            orchestrator: AgentFileConfig {
  894|      1|                model: Some("gemini, codex, opus".to_string()),
  895|      1|                ..Default::default()
  896|      1|            },
  897|      1|            planner: AgentFileConfig::default(),
  898|      1|            implementer: AgentFileConfig::default(),
  899|      1|        };
  900|       |
  901|      1|        let available_tiers: HashSet<ModelTier> =
  902|      1|            [ModelTier::Opus, ModelTier::Sonnet].into_iter().collect();
  903|       |
  904|      1|        let config = build_model_config(&loaded, available_tiers).unwrap();
  905|       |
  906|       |        // Should parse as a fallback chain
  907|      1|        assert_eq!(
  908|       |            config.orchestrator_model.0,
  909|      1|            vec![ModelTier::Gemini, ModelTier::Codex, ModelTier::Opus]
  910|       |        );
  911|      1|    }

  916|      1|            orchestrator: AgentFileConfig {
  917|      1|                model: Some("opus".to_string()),
  918|      1|                ..Default::default()
  919|      1|            },
  920|      1|            planner: AgentFileConfig::default(),
  921|      1|            implementer: AgentFileConfig::default(),
  922|      1|        };
  923|       |
  924|      1|        let available_tiers: HashSet<ModelTier> = [ModelTier::Opus].into_iter().collect();
  925|       |
  926|      1|        let config = build_model_config(&loaded, available_tiers).unwrap();
  927|       |

  930|       |        // Others should use defaults
  931|      1|        assert_eq!(
  932|       |            config.planner_model,
  933|      1|            crate::models::ModelConfig::default().planner_model
  934|       |        );
  935|      1|    }

 1202|       |    // load_backend_kind Tests (Priority Order)
 1203|       |    // ========================================================================
 1204|       |    //
 1205|       |    // These tests use the serial_test crate because they modify environment
 1206|       |    // variables which are process-global state.
 1207|       |
 1208|       |    use serial_test::serial;
 1209|       |
 1210|       |    // Helper to temporarily set an environment variable for a test
 1211|       |    struct EnvGuard {
 1212|       |        key: &'static str,
 1213|       |        original: Option<String>,
 1214|       |    }
 1215|       |
 1216|       |    impl EnvGuard {
 1217|     12|        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
 1218|     12|            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
 1219|     12|            std::env::set_var(key, value);
 1220|     12|            Self { key, original }
 1221|     12|        }
 1222|       |
 1223|      1|        fn remove(key: &'static str) -> Self {
 1224|      1|            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
 1225|      1|            std::env::remove_var(key);
 1226|      1|            Self { key, original }
 1227|      1|        }
 1228|       |    }
 1229|       |
 1230|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
 1231|     13|        fn drop(&mut self) {
 1232|     13|            match &self.original {
 1233|      0|                Some(val) => std::env::set_var(self.key, val),
 1234|     13|                None => std::env::remove_var(self.key),
 1235|       |            }
 1236|     13|        }
 1237|       |    }
 1238|       |
 1239|       |    #[test]

 1243|      1|        let _guard = EnvGuard::set("PAPERBOAT_BACKEND", "cursor");
 1244|       |

    2|       |//!

   54|       |/// Saves an agent configuration to its TOML file.
   55|       |///
   56|       |/// This function:
   57|       |/// 1. Creates the config directory if it doesn't exist
   58|       |/// 2. Writes the model configuration to a TOML file
   59|       |/// 3. Uses atomic writes (write to temp file, then rename) to avoid corruption
   60|       |/// 4. Preserves existing comments if the file already exists
   61|       |///
   62|       |/// # Arguments
   63|       |///
   64|       |/// * `agent_type` - The type of agent ("orchestrator", "planner", or "implementer")
   65|       |/// * `model_id` - The model ID to save
   66|       |///
   67|       |/// # Returns
   68|       |///
   69|       |/// `Ok(())` on success, or `Err` if the operation fails.
   70|       |///
   71|       |/// # File Format
   72|       |///
   73|       |/// The file is written in TOML format:
   74|       |/// ```toml
   75|       |/// # Orchestrator agent configuration
   76|       |/// model = "opus"
   77|       |/// ```
   78|       |#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "tui"), allow(dead_code))]
   79|      0|pub fn save_agent_config(agent_type: &str, tier: ModelTier) -> Result<()> {
   80|      0|    save_agent_config_to_dir(&project_config_dir(), agent_type, tier)
   81|      0|}
   82|       |

                                                                   ^0

   91|       |

                                                                                                ^0

                                       ^0                                        ^0                   ^0
  101|       |

                                       ^0                                          ^0                   ^0
  105|       |

  107|      5|        .sync_all()
  108|      5|        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to sync temp file: {}", temp_path.display()))?;
                                       ^0                                      ^0                   ^0
  109|       |
  110|       |    // Rename temp file to target (atomic on most filesystems)
  111|      5|    fs::rename(&temp_path, &file_path).with_context(|| {
                                                                     ^0
  112|      0|        format!(
  113|       |            "Failed to rename temp file {} to {}",
  114|      0|            temp_path.display(),
  115|      0|            file_path.display()

  155|       |/// Updates the model line in existing content, preserving comments and other lines.
  156|     10|fn update_model_in_content(existing: &str, model_family: &str, agent_type: &str) -> String {
  157|     10|    let mut lines: Vec<String> = existing.lines().map(ToString::to_string).collect();
  158|     10|    let mut found_model_line = false;
  159|       |
  160|     16|    for line in &mut lines {
                              ^10
  161|     16|        let trimmed = line.trim();
  162|       |        // Match lines like: model = "xxx" or model = 'xxx' or model = xxx
  163|     16|        if trimmed.starts_with("model") {
  164|      8|            if let Some(_eq_pos) = trimmed.find('=') {
  165|      8|                *line = format!("model = \"{model_family}\"");
  166|      8|                found_model_line = true;
  167|      8|                break;
  168|      0|            }
  169|      8|        }
  170|       |    }
  171|       |
  172|     10|    if !found_model_line {
  173|       |        // If no model line found, add it at the end
  174|       |        // But first check if there's a header comment, if not add one
  175|      2|        let has_header = lines.iter().any(|l| l.trim().starts_with('#'));
                                                            ^1       ^1
  176|      2|        if !has_header {
  177|      1|            let agent_title = capitalize_first(agent_type);
  178|      1|            lines.insert(0, format!("# {agent_title} agent configuration"));
  179|      1|        }
  180|      2|        lines.push(format!("model = \"{model_family}\""));
  181|      8|    }
  182|       |
  183|       |    // Ensure file ends with newline
  184|     10|    let mut result = lines.join("\n");
  185|     10|    if !result.ends_with('\n') {
  186|     10|        result.push('\n');
  187|     10|    }
                  ^0
  188|     10|    result
  189|     10|}
  190|       |
  191|       |/// Capitalizes the first letter of a string.
  192|      8|fn capitalize_first(s: &str) -> String {
  193|      8|    let mut chars = s.chars();
  194|      8|    match chars.next() {
  195|      1|        None => String::new(),
  196|      7|        Some(first) => first.to_uppercase().collect::<String>() + chars.as_str(),
  197|       |    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/error/acp.rs:
    1|       |//! ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) error types.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Errors related to communication with the agent backend via ACP.
    4|       |//! This module is backend-agnostic - it works with any backend (Auggie, Cursor, etc.).
    5|       |
    6|       |use std::time::Duration;
    7|       |use thiserror::Error;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Errors that can occur during ACP operations.
   10|       |#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   11|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   12|       |pub enum AcpError {
   13|       |    /// Failed to establish connection with the agent CLI.
   14|       |    #[error("Failed to connect to agent: {message}")]
   15|       |    ConnectionFailed {
   16|       |        /// Description of what went wrong.
   17|       |        message: String,
   18|       |        /// Optional suggestion for fixing the issue.
   19|       |        #[source]
   20|       |        suggestion: Option<AcpSuggestion>,
   21|       |    },
   22|       |
   23|       |    /// Request timed out waiting for response.
   24|       |    #[error("Request timed out after {duration:?} while {context}")]
   25|       |    RequestTimeout {
   26|       |        /// How long we waited before timing out.
   27|       |        duration: Duration,
   28|       |        /// What operation was being attempted.
   29|       |        context: String,
   30|       |    },
   31|       |
   32|       |    /// Received an invalid response from ACP.
   33|       |    #[error("Invalid response from ACP: {message}")]
   34|       |    InvalidResponse {
   35|       |        /// Description of what was wrong with the response.
   36|       |        message: String,
   37|       |        /// The raw response that was invalid, if available.
   38|       |        raw_response: Option<String>,
   39|       |    },
   40|       |
   41|       |    /// Error during session operations.
   42|       |    #[error("Session error: {message}")]
   43|       |    SessionError {
   44|       |        /// Session ID if known.
   45|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   46|       |        /// Description of what went wrong.
   47|       |        message: String,
   48|       |    },
   49|       |
   50|       |    /// Response channel was closed unexpectedly.
   51|       |    #[error("Response channel closed before receiving response")]
   52|       |    ChannelClosed,
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// Failed to serialize or send request.
   55|       |    #[error("Failed to send request: {0}")]
   56|       |    SendFailed(String),
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// ACP returned an error in the response.
   59|       |    #[error("ACP error: {message}")]
   60|       |    ProtocolError {
   61|       |        /// The error code from ACP, if any.
   62|       |        code: Option<i32>,
   63|       |        /// The error message from ACP.
   64|       |        message: String,
   65|       |    },
   66|       |
   67|       |    /// Failed to parse response data.
   68|       |    #[error("Failed to parse ACP response: {message}")]
   69|       |    ParseError {
   70|       |        /// What went wrong during parsing.
   71|       |        message: String,
   72|       |        /// The raw data that couldn't be parsed.
   73|       |        raw_data: Option<String>,
   74|       |    },
   75|       |}
   76|       |
   77|       |/// Suggestions for resolving ACP errors.
   78|       |///
   79|       |/// These suggestions are backend-agnostic and can include backend-specific
   80|       |/// information via their string parameters.
   81|       |#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   82|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   83|       |pub enum AcpSuggestion {
   84|       |    /// The CLI is not installed or not in PATH.
   85|       |    /// Parameter: backend name for context (e.g., "auggie", "cursor").
   86|       |    #[error("Is the {0} CLI installed and in your PATH?")]
   87|       |    InstallCli(String),
   88|       |
   89|       |    /// Authentication is required to use this backend.
   90|       |    /// Parameter: backend-specific auth error message from `backend.auth_error_message()`.
   91|       |    #[error("{0}")]
   92|       |    AuthRequired(String),
   93|       |
   94|       |    /// The agent process may not be running.
   95|       |    /// Parameter: backend name for context.
   96|       |    #[error("Check that the {0} process is running")]
   97|       |    CheckProcess(String),
   98|       |
   99|       |    /// Custom backend-specific error message.
  100|       |    #[error("{0}")]
  101|       |    Custom(String),
  102|       |}
  103|       |
  104|       |#[cfg(test)]
  105|       |mod tests {
  106|       |    use super::*;
  107|       |
  108|       |    #[test]
  109|      1|    fn test_connection_failed_display() {
  110|      1|        let err = AcpError::ConnectionFailed {
  111|      1|            message: "spawn failed".to_string(),
  112|      1|            suggestion: Some(AcpSuggestion::InstallCli("auggie".to_string())),
  113|      1|        };
  114|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  115|      1|        assert!(display.contains("spawn failed"));
  116|      1|    }
  117|       |
  118|       |    #[test]
  119|      1|    fn test_request_timeout_display() {
  120|      1|        let err = AcpError::RequestTimeout {
  121|      1|            duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
  122|      1|            context: "session/new".to_string(),
  123|      1|        };
  124|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  125|      1|        assert!(display.contains("60"));
  126|      1|        assert!(display.contains("session/new"));
  127|      1|    }
  128|       |
  129|       |    #[test]
  130|      1|    fn test_invalid_response_display() {

  132|      1|            message: "missing sessionId field".to_string(),
  133|      1|            raw_response: Some("{\"result\": {}}".to_string()),
  134|      1|        };
  135|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  136|      1|        assert!(display.contains("missing sessionId"));
  137|      1|    }
  138|       |
  139|       |    #[test]
  140|      1|    fn test_session_error_display() {
  141|      1|        let err = AcpError::SessionError {
  142|      1|            session_id: Some("session-123".to_string()),
  143|      1|            message: "session terminated unexpectedly".to_string(),
  144|      1|        };
  145|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  146|      1|        assert!(display.contains("terminated unexpectedly"));
  147|      1|    }
  148|       |
  149|       |    #[test]
  150|      1|    fn test_protocol_error_display() {

  152|      1|            code: Some(-32600),
  153|      1|            message: "Invalid request".to_string(),
  154|      1|        };
  155|      1|        let display = format!("{err}");
  156|      1|        assert!(display.contains("Invalid request"));
  157|      1|    }
  158|       |
  159|       |    #[test]
  160|      1|    fn test_install_cli_suggestion_display() {
  161|      1|        let suggestion = AcpSuggestion::InstallCli("cursor".to_string());
  162|      1|        let display = format!("{suggestion}");
  163|      1|        assert!(display.contains("cursor"));
  164|      1|        assert!(display.contains("CLI"));
  165|      1|        assert!(display.contains("PATH"));
  166|      1|    }
  167|       |
  168|       |    #[test]
  169|      1|    fn test_auth_required_suggestion_display() {
  170|      1|        let auth_message = "Please run 'auggie login' to authenticate.";
  171|      1|        let suggestion = AcpSuggestion::AuthRequired(auth_message.to_string());
  172|      1|        let display = format!("{suggestion}");
  173|      1|        assert_eq!(display, auth_message);
  174|      1|    }
  175|       |
  176|       |    #[test]
  177|      1|    fn test_check_process_suggestion_display() {
  178|      1|        let suggestion = AcpSuggestion::CheckProcess("agent".to_string());
  179|      1|        let display = format!("{suggestion}");
  180|      1|        assert!(display.contains("agent"));
  181|      1|        assert!(display.contains("process"));
  182|      1|    }
  183|       |
  184|       |    #[test]
  185|      1|    fn test_custom_suggestion_display() {
  186|      1|        let suggestion = AcpSuggestion::Custom("Try restarting the service".to_string());
  187|      1|        let display = format!("{suggestion}");
  188|      1|        assert_eq!(display, "Try restarting the service");
  189|      1|    }
