/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/error/config.rs:
    1|       |//! Configuration error types.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Errors related to loading, parsing, and validating configuration files.
    4|       |
    5|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    6|       |use thiserror::Error;
    7|       |
    8|       |/// Known model aliases that can be used in configuration files.
    9|       |/// These are case-insensitive and will be resolved to actual model IDs.
   10|       |pub const KNOWN_MODEL_ALIASES: &[&str] = &[
   11|       |    "auto",         // Let the system choose
   12|       |    "opus",         // Highest capability
   13|       |    "sonnet",       // Balanced
   14|       |    "haiku",        // Fastest/cheapest
   15|       |    "gpt",          // OpenAI GPT (general)
   16|       |    "openai",       // Meta-tier for all OpenAI models
   17|       |    "codex",        // GPT Codex (coding)
   18|       |    "codex-mini",   // GPT Codex Mini
   19|       |    "gemini",       // Google Gemini Pro
   20|       |    "gemini-flash", // Google Gemini Flash
   21|       |    "grok",         // xAI Grok
   22|       |    "composer",     // Cursor Composer
   23|       |    // Version-specific aliases
   24|       |    "opus4",
   25|       |    "opus4.5",
   26|       |    "sonnet4",
   27|       |    "sonnet4.5",
   28|       |    "haiku4",
   29|       |    "haiku4.5",
   30|       |];
   31|       |
   32|       |/// Suggests a similar model alias for typos.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// Uses simple edit distance heuristics to find the closest match.
   35|      6|pub fn suggest_model_alias(typo: &str) -> Option<String> {
   36|      6|    let typo_lower = typo.to_lowercase();
   37|       |
   38|       |    // Check for common typos and near-matches
   39|      6|    let suggestions: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
   40|      6|        ("opis", "opus"),
   41|      6|        ("opuse", "opus"),
   42|      6|        ("opus5", "opus4.5"),
   43|      6|        ("sonet", "sonnet"),
   44|      6|        ("sonnett", "sonnet"),
   45|      6|        ("sonett", "sonnet"),
   46|      6|        ("sonnet5", "sonnet4.5"),
   47|      6|        ("hakai", "haiku"),
   48|      6|        ("hiku", "haiku"),
   49|      6|        ("haiku5", "haiku4.5"),
   50|      6|        ("automatic", "auto"),
   51|      6|    ];
   52|       |
   53|     54|    for (mistake, correction) in suggestions {
                                               ^6
   54|     54|        if typo_lower == *mistake {
   55|      2|            return Some((*correction).to_string());
   56|     52|        }
   57|       |    }
   58|       |
   59|       |    // Check if it's a prefix match
   60|     72|    for alias in KNOWN_MODEL_ALIASES {
   61|     72|        if alias.starts_with(&typo_lower) && alias.len() > typo_lower.len() {
                                                           ^0    ^0
   62|      0|            return Some((*alias).to_string());
   63|     72|        }
   64|       |    }
   65|       |
   66|       |    // Check for partial matches with known families
   67|      4|    let families = ["opus", "sonnet", "haiku"];
   68|     12|    for family in families {
                                ^4
   69|     12|        if typo_lower.contains(family) || family.contains(&typo_lower) {
   70|      0|            return Some(family.to_string());
   71|     12|        }
   72|       |    }
   73|       |
   74|      4|    None
   75|      6|}

   79|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   80|       |pub enum ConfigError {
   81|       |    /// Configuration file was not found.
   82|       |    #[error("Configuration file not found: {path}")]
   83|       |    FileNotFound {
   84|       |        /// Path to the missing file.
   85|       |        path: PathBuf,
   86|       |    },
   87|       |
   88|       |    /// Failed to read configuration file.
   89|       |    #[error("Failed to read configuration file '{path}': {message}")]
   90|       |    ReadError {
   91|       |        /// Path to the file that couldn't be read.
   92|       |        path: PathBuf,
   93|       |        /// Description of the error.
   94|       |        message: String,
   95|       |    },
   96|       |
   97|       |    /// Failed to parse configuration file.
   98|       |    #[error("Failed to parse configuration file '{path}': {message}")]

  100|       |        /// Path to the file that couldn't be parsed.
  101|       |        path: PathBuf,
  102|       |        /// Description of the parse error.
  103|       |        message: String,
  104|       |        /// Line number where the error occurred, if known.
  105|       |        line: Option<usize>,
  106|       |    },
  107|       |
  108|       |    /// Invalid model specified in configuration.
  109|       |    #[error("Invalid model '{model}': {reason}. Available models: {}", .available.join(", "))]
  110|       |    InvalidModel {
  111|       |        /// The invalid model string.
  112|       |        model: String,
  113|       |        /// Why the model is invalid.
  114|       |        reason: String,
  115|       |        /// List of available models.
  116|       |        available: Vec<String>,
  117|       |    },
  118|       |
  119|       |    /// Model is not available (not authenticated or no access).
  120|       |    #[error("Model '{model}' is not available: {reason}")]
  121|       |    ModelNotAvailable {
  122|       |        /// The unavailable model.
  123|       |        model: String,
  124|       |        /// Why the model is not available.
  125|       |        reason: String,
  126|       |    },
  127|       |

  130|       |    MergeConflict {
  131|       |        /// The configuration key that has a conflict.
  132|       |        key: String,
  133|       |        /// Description of the conflict.
  134|       |        message: String,
  135|       |    },
  136|       |
  137|       |    /// Required field is missing from configuration.
  138|       |    #[error("Missing required field '{field}' in configuration")]
  139|       |    MissingField {
  140|       |        /// The missing field name.
  141|       |        field: String,
  142|       |        /// Path to the config file, if applicable.
  143|       |        path: Option<PathBuf>,
  144|       |    },
  145|       |
  146|       |    /// Configuration validation failed.
  147|       |    #[error("Configuration validation failed: {message}")]
  148|       |    ValidationFailed {
  149|       |        /// What validation check failed.
  150|       |        message: String,
  151|       |    },
  152|       |
  153|       |    /// Failed to write configuration file.
  154|       |    #[error("Failed to write configuration file '{path}': {message}")]
  155|       |    WriteError {
  156|       |        /// Path to the file that couldn't be written.
  157|       |        path: PathBuf,
  158|       |        /// Description of the error.
  159|       |        message: String,
  160|       |    },
  161|       |}

    7|       |/// Errors that can occur during MCP operations.
    8|       |#[derive(Debug, Error)]
    9|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   10|       |pub enum McpError {
   11|       |    /// Requested tool was not found.
   12|       |    #[error("Tool not found: '{tool_name}'. Available tools: {}", .available_tools.join(", "))]
   13|       |    ToolNotFound {
   14|       |        /// The name of the tool that was requested.
   15|       |        tool_name: String,
   16|       |        /// List of available tool names.
   17|       |        available_tools: Vec<String>,
   18|       |    },
   19|       |
   20|       |    /// Invalid arguments were passed to a tool.
   21|       |    #[error("Invalid arguments for tool '{tool_name}': {reason}")]
   22|       |    InvalidArguments {
   23|       |        /// The name of the tool.
   24|       |        tool_name: String,
   25|       |        /// Why the arguments were invalid.
   26|       |        reason: String,
   27|       |        /// The invalid arguments as JSON string, if available.
   28|       |        arguments: Option<String>,
   29|       |    },
   30|       |
   31|       |    /// Tool handler failed during execution.
   32|       |    #[error("Handler failed for tool '{tool_name}': {message}")]
   33|       |    HandlerFailed {
   34|       |        /// The name of the tool that failed.
   35|       |        tool_name: String,
   36|       |        /// Description of what went wrong.
   37|       |        message: String,
   38|       |    },
   39|       |
   40|       |    /// Protocol-level error (invalid JSON-RPC, etc).
   41|       |    #[error("MCP protocol error: {message}")]
   42|       |    ProtocolError {
   43|       |        /// The JSON-RPC error code, if applicable.
   44|       |        code: i32,
   45|       |        /// Description of the protocol error.
   46|       |        message: String,
   47|       |    },
   48|       |
   49|       |    /// Parse error while processing request.
   50|       |    #[error("Failed to parse MCP request: {message}")]
   51|       |    ParseError {
   52|       |        /// What went wrong during parsing.
   53|       |        message: String,
   54|       |        /// Preview of the input that couldn't be parsed.
   55|       |        input_preview: Option<String>,
   56|       |    },
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Invalid request structure.
   59|       |    #[error("Invalid MCP request: {reason}")]
   60|       |    InvalidRequest {
   61|       |        /// Why the request was invalid.
   62|       |        reason: String,
   63|       |    },
   64|       |
   65|       |    /// Socket communication error.
   66|       |    #[error("MCP socket error: {message}")]
   67|       |    SocketError {
   68|       |        /// Description of the socket error.
   69|       |        message: String,
   70|       |    },
   71|       |
   72|       |    /// Response channel error.
   73|       |    #[error("Failed to send tool response: {0}")]
   74|       |    ResponseFailed(String),
   75|       |}
   76|       |
   77|       |// JSON-RPC 2.0 standard error codes (re-exported for convenience)
   78|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   79|       |impl McpError {
   80|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0: Parse error
   81|       |    pub const CODE_PARSE_ERROR: i32 = -32700;
   82|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0: Invalid request
   83|       |    pub const CODE_INVALID_REQUEST: i32 = -32600;
   84|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0: Method not found
   85|       |    pub const CODE_METHOD_NOT_FOUND: i32 = -32601;
   86|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0: Invalid params
   87|       |    pub const CODE_INVALID_PARAMS: i32 = -32602;
   88|       |    /// JSON-RPC 2.0: Internal error
   89|       |    pub const CODE_INTERNAL_ERROR: i32 = -32603;
   90|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/error/mod.rs:
    1|       |//! Error types for the Paperboat orchestrator.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides a granular error hierarchy for better error handling
    4|       |//! and debugging. Each subsystem has its own error type with clear, actionable
    5|       |//! error messages.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # Error Hierarchy
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! ```text
   10|       |//! PaperboatError (top-level)
   11|       |//! ├── AcpError (connection, timeout, invalid response, session errors)
   12|       |//! ├── McpError (tool not found, invalid arguments, handler failed, protocol errors)
   13|       |//! ├── TaskError (task not found, invalid status, dependency failed, validation failed)
   14|       |//! ├── ConfigError (file not found, parse error, invalid model, merge conflict)
   15|       |//! └── OrchestratorError (timeout, internal errors)
   16|       |//! ```
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! # Example
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! ```ignore
   21|       |//! use paperboat::error::{AcpError, AcpSuggestion, PaperboatError};
   22|       |//!
   23|       |//! fn connect_to_agent(backend_name: &str) -> Result<(), PaperboatError> {
   24|       |//!     Err(AcpError::ConnectionFailed {
   25|       |//!         message: format!("Failed to spawn {} process", backend_name),
   26|       |//!         suggestion: Some(AcpSuggestion::InstallCli(backend_name.to_string())),
   27|       |//!     }.into())
   28|       |//! }
   29|       |//! ```
   30|       |
   31|       |mod acp;
   32|       |mod config;
   33|       |mod mcp;
   34|       |mod orchestrator;
   35|       |mod task;
   36|       |
   37|       |// Re-export all error types for convenient access
   38|       |pub use acp::AcpError;
   39|       |pub use config::{suggest_model_alias, ConfigError, KNOWN_MODEL_ALIASES};
   40|       |pub use mcp::McpError;
   41|       |pub use orchestrator::{OrchestratorError, TimeoutConfig, TimeoutOperation};
   42|       |pub use task::TaskError;
   43|       |
   44|       |use thiserror::Error;
   45|       |
   46|       |/// Top-level error type for all Paperboat operations.
   47|       |///
   48|       |/// This enum encompasses all error types in the Paperboat system,
   49|       |/// allowing for unified error handling at the application level.
   50|       |#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   51|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   52|       |pub enum PaperboatError {
   53|       |    /// ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) errors
   54|       |    #[error(transparent)]
   55|       |    Acp(#[from] AcpError),
   56|       |
   57|       |    /// MCP (Model Context Protocol) errors
   58|       |    #[error(transparent)]
   59|       |    Mcp(#[from] McpError),
   60|       |
   61|       |    /// Task management errors
   62|       |    #[error(transparent)]
   63|       |    Task(#[from] TaskError),
   64|       |
   65|       |    /// Configuration errors
   66|       |    #[error(transparent)]
   67|       |    Config(#[from] ConfigError),
   68|       |
   69|       |    /// Orchestrator-level errors
   70|       |    #[error(transparent)]
   71|       |    Orchestrator(#[from] OrchestratorError),
   72|       |
   73|       |    /// Catch-all for other errors (wraps `anyhow::Error`)
   74|       |    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
   75|       |    Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   76|       |}
   77|       |

   79|       |mod tests {
   80|       |    use super::*;

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/error/orchestrator.rs:
    1|       |//! Orchestrator-level error types.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Errors related to orchestrator operations, including timeout handling
    4|       |//! for planning and session completion.
    5|       |
    6|       |use std::time::Duration;
    7|       |use thiserror::Error;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Errors that can occur during orchestrator operations.
   10|       |#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   11|       |pub enum OrchestratorError {
   12|       |    /// A timeout occurred while waiting for an operation to complete.
   13|       |    #[error("Timeout after {duration:?} while {operation}{}", .context.as_ref().map(|c| format!(" ({c})")).unwrap_or_default())]
   14|       |    Timeout {
   15|       |        /// Description of the operation that timed out.
   16|       |        operation: TimeoutOperation,
   17|       |        /// The duration after which the timeout occurred.
   18|       |        duration: Duration,
   19|       |        /// Optional additional context (e.g., session ID).
   20|       |        context: Option<String>,
   21|       |    },
   22|       |
   23|       |    /// An internal error occurred (wraps `anyhow::Error` for compatibility).
   24|       |    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
   25|       |    Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Types of operations that can time out.
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   30|       |pub enum TimeoutOperation {
   31|       |    /// Waiting for a session to complete (any agent type).
   32|       |    WaitForSession,
   33|       |    /// Waiting for an ACP request/response (e.g., session/new, initialize).
   34|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   35|       |    AcpRequest,
   36|       |}
   37|       |
   38|       |impl std::fmt::Display for TimeoutOperation {
   39|      2|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   40|      2|        match self {
   41|      2|            Self::WaitForSession => write!(f, "waiting for session"),
   42|      0|            Self::AcpRequest => write!(f, "waiting for ACP response"),
   43|       |        }
   44|      2|    }
   45|       |}
   46|       |
   47|       |/// Configuration for timeout durations.
   48|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   49|       |pub struct TimeoutConfig {
   50|       |    /// Timeout for waiting for any session to complete.
   51|       |    /// Default: 30 minutes. Env: `PAPERBOAT_SESSION_TIMEOUT`
   52|       |    pub session_timeout: Duration,
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// Timeout for ACP request/response (e.g., session/new, initialize).
   55|       |    /// Default: 60 seconds. Env: `PAPERBOAT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT`
   56|       |    pub request_timeout: Duration,
   57|       |}
   58|       |
   59|       |impl Default for TimeoutConfig {
   60|     48|    fn default() -> Self {
   61|     48|        Self::from_env()
   62|     48|    }
   63|       |}
   64|       |
   65|       |impl TimeoutConfig {
   66|       |    /// Default session timeout in seconds (30 minutes)
   67|       |    const DEFAULT_SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 30 * 60;
   68|       |
   69|       |    /// Default request timeout in seconds (60 seconds)
   70|       |    const DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 60;
   71|       |
   72|       |    /// Create a new `TimeoutConfig` with custom durations.
   73|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   74|      1|    pub const fn new(session_timeout: Duration, request_timeout: Duration) -> Self {
   75|      1|        Self {
   76|      1|            session_timeout,
   77|      1|            request_timeout,
   78|      1|        }
   79|      1|    }
   80|       |
   81|       |    /// Create a `TimeoutConfig` from environment variables.
   82|       |    ///
   83|       |    /// Supported environment variables:
   84|       |    /// - `PAPERBOAT_SESSION_TIMEOUT`: Session timeout in seconds (default: 1800)
   85|       |    /// - `PAPERBOAT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT`: ACP request timeout in seconds (default: 60)
   86|     48|    pub fn from_env() -> Self {
   87|     48|        let session_timeout = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_SESSION_TIMEOUT")
   88|     48|            .ok()
   89|     48|            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok())
                                        ^0               ^0
   90|     48|            .map_or_else(
   91|     48|                || Duration::from_secs(Self::DEFAULT_SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECS),
   92|       |                Duration::from_secs,
   93|       |            );
   94|       |
   95|     48|        let request_timeout = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT")
   96|     48|            .ok()
   97|     48|            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok())
                                        ^0               ^0
   98|     48|            .map_or_else(
   99|     48|                || Duration::from_secs(Self::DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECS),
  100|       |                Duration::from_secs,
  101|       |            );
  102|       |
  103|     48|        Self {
  104|     48|            session_timeout,
  105|     48|            request_timeout,
  106|     48|        }
  107|     48|    }
  108|       |
  109|       |    /// Create a `TimeoutConfig` with no timeouts (infinite wait).
  110|       |    /// Useful for debugging or long-running tasks.
  111|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  112|      1|    pub const fn no_timeout() -> Self {
  113|      1|        Self {
  114|      1|            session_timeout: Duration::MAX,
  115|      1|            request_timeout: Duration::MAX,
  116|      1|        }
  117|      1|    }
  118|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/ipc/address.rs:
    1|       |//! Platform-agnostic IPC address type.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `IpcAddress`, a unified way to identify IPC endpoints
    4|       |//! across different platforms.
    5|       |
    6|       |use std::fmt;
    7|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Platform-agnostic IPC endpoint address.
   10|       |///
   11|       |/// Encapsulates the platform-specific addressing scheme:
   12|       |/// - **Unix**: File system path (e.g., `/tmp/vl-abc12345.sock`)
   13|       |/// - **Windows**: Named pipe path (e.g., `\\.\pipe\vl-abc12345`)
   14|       |///
   15|       |/// # Generation
   16|       |///
   17|       |/// Use `IpcAddress::generate()` to create a unique address with a prefix:
   18|       |///
   19|       |/// ```ignore
   20|       |/// let addr = IpcAddress::generate("agent-abc123");
   21|       |/// // Unix:   /tmp/vl-agent-ab.sock (truncated for SUN_LEN limit)
   22|       |/// // Windows: \\.\pipe\vl-agent-abc123
   23|       |/// ```
   24|       |///
   25|       |/// # String Representation
   26|       |///
   27|       |/// The address can be converted to a string for passing to child processes
   28|       |/// via environment variables or command line arguments.
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   30|       |pub struct IpcAddress {
   31|       |    /// The underlying path/name representation.
   32|       |    ///
   33|       |    /// - Unix: PathBuf pointing to socket file
   34|       |    /// - Windows: PathBuf containing the pipe name (\\.\pipe\...)
   35|       |    inner: PathBuf,
   36|       |}
   37|       |
   38|       |impl IpcAddress {
   39|       |    /// Generate a new unique IPC address with the given prefix.
   40|       |    ///
   41|       |    /// The prefix is used to identify the purpose (e.g., "agent-", "selfimprove-").
   42|       |    /// A short UUID segment is appended to ensure uniqueness.
   43|       |    ///
   44|       |    /// # Platform Behavior
   45|       |    ///
   46|       |    /// - **Unix**: Creates a path in the temp directory. The prefix is truncated
   47|       |    ///   to 8 characters to stay within macOS's ~104 byte socket path limit.
   48|       |    /// - **Windows**: Creates a named pipe path. No truncation needed.
   49|       |    ///
   50|       |    /// # Example
   51|       |    ///
   52|       |    /// ```ignore
   53|       |    /// let addr = IpcAddress::generate("agent-abc12345");
   54|       |    /// println!("{}", addr);  // /tmp/vl-agent-ab.sock (Unix)
   55|       |    /// ```
   56|       |    #[must_use]
   57|     24|    pub fn generate(prefix: &str) -> Self {
   58|     24|        let short_uuid = &uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8];
   59|       |
   60|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
   61|       |        {
   62|       |            // Truncate prefix to 8 chars to avoid exceeding macOS SUN_LEN limit (~104 bytes)
   63|       |            // Format: /tmp/vl-{prefix}-{uuid}.sock
   64|     24|            let short_prefix = &prefix[..8.min(prefix.len())];
   65|     24|            let path = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("vl-{short_prefix}-{short_uuid}.sock"));
   66|     24|            Self { inner: path }
   67|       |        }
   68|       |
   69|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
   70|       |        {
   71|       |            // Windows named pipes don't have the same length restrictions
   72|       |            // Format: \\.\pipe\vl-{prefix}-{uuid}
   73|       |            let path = PathBuf::from(format!(r"\\.\pipe\vl-{prefix}-{short_uuid}"));
   74|       |            Self { inner: path }
   75|       |        }
   76|     24|    }
   77|       |
   78|       |    /// Create an IPC address from a string representation.
   79|       |    ///
   80|       |    /// Used when receiving an address from a child process or command line.
   81|       |    /// The string is interpreted according to the current platform.
   82|       |    #[must_use]
   83|     55|    pub fn from_string(s: &str) -> Self {
   84|     55|        Self {
   85|     55|            inner: PathBuf::from(s),
   86|     55|        }
   87|     55|    }
   88|       |
   89|       |    /// Get the address as a string for serialization.
   90|       |    ///
   91|       |    /// This is used to pass the address to child processes via environment
   92|       |    /// variables or command line arguments.
   93|       |    #[must_use]
   94|    162|    pub fn as_str(&self) -> std::borrow::Cow<'_, str> {
   95|    162|        self.inner.to_string_lossy()
   96|    162|    }
   97|       |
   98|       |    /// Get the underlying path representation.
   99|       |    ///
  100|       |    /// On Unix, this is the socket file path.
  101|       |    /// On Windows, this is the named pipe path.
  102|       |    #[must_use]
  103|     13|    pub fn as_path(&self) -> &PathBuf {
  104|     13|        &self.inner
  105|     13|    }
  106|       |
  107|       |    /// Check if the address endpoint exists.
  108|       |    ///
  109|       |    /// On Unix, checks if the socket file exists.
  110|       |    /// On Windows, this always returns true (named pipes don't have file presence).
  111|       |    #[must_use]
  112|      7|    pub fn exists(&self) -> bool {
  113|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  114|       |        {
  115|      7|            self.inner.exists()
  116|       |        }
  117|       |
  118|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  119|       |        {
  120|       |            // Named pipes don't have a file system presence to check
  121|       |            // The existence is determined by whether a server is listening
  122|       |            true
  123|       |        }
  124|      7|    }
  125|       |
  126|       |    /// Clean up the address endpoint if applicable.
  127|       |    ///
  128|       |    /// On Unix, removes the socket file.
  129|       |    /// On Windows, this is a no-op (named pipes are cleaned up automatically).
  130|     60|    pub fn cleanup(&self) {
  131|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  132|     60|        {
  133|     60|            let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&self.inner);
  134|     60|        }
  135|       |
  136|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  137|       |        {
  138|       |            // Named pipes are automatically cleaned up when the server closes
  139|       |        }
  140|     60|    }
  141|       |}
  142|       |
  143|       |impl fmt::Display for IpcAddress {
  144|      3|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
  145|      3|        write!(f, "{}", self.inner.display())
  146|      3|    }
  147|       |}
  148|       |
  149|       |impl From<PathBuf> for IpcAddress {
  150|      0|    fn from(path: PathBuf) -> Self {
  151|      0|        Self { inner: path }
  152|      0|    }
  153|       |}
  154|       |
  155|       |impl From<&str> for IpcAddress {
  156|      0|    fn from(s: &str) -> Self {
  157|      0|        Self::from_string(s)
  158|      0|    }
  159|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/ipc/error.rs:
    1|       |//! IPC error types.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides structured error handling for IPC operations while maintaining
    4|       |//! compatibility with `anyhow::Error` for the rest of the codebase.
    5|       |
    6|       |use std::fmt;
    7|       |use std::io;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Structured error type for IPC operations.
   10|       |///
   11|       |/// This enum provides semantic error variants for common IPC failure modes,
   12|       |/// making it easier to handle specific errors programmatically while still
   13|       |/// being convertible to `anyhow::Error` for propagation.
   14|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   15|       |pub enum IpcError {
   16|       |    /// Failed to bind to the address (server-side).
   17|       |    ///
   18|       |    /// Common causes:
   19|       |    /// - Address already in use
   20|       |    /// - Permission denied
   21|       |    /// - Invalid path (Unix) or pipe name (Windows)
   22|       |    BindFailed {
   23|       |        /// The address that failed to bind
   24|       |        address: String,
   25|       |        /// The underlying IO error
   26|       |        source: io::Error,
   27|       |    },
   28|       |
   29|       |    /// Failed to connect to the address (client-side).
   30|       |    ///
   31|       |    /// Common causes:
   32|       |    /// - Server not listening
   33|       |    /// - Connection refused
   34|       |    /// - Timeout
   35|       |    ConnectionFailed {
   36|       |        /// The address that failed to connect
   37|       |        address: String,
   38|       |        /// The underlying IO error
   39|       |        source: io::Error,
   40|       |    },
   41|       |
   42|       |    /// Failed to accept a connection (server-side).
   43|       |    ///
   44|       |    /// This usually indicates the listener was closed or an OS-level error.
   45|       |    AcceptFailed {
   46|       |        /// The underlying IO error
   47|       |        source: io::Error,
   48|       |    },
   49|       |}
   50|       |
   51|       |impl fmt::Display for IpcError {
   52|      0|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   53|      0|        match self {
   54|      0|            Self::BindFailed { address, source } => {
   55|      0|                write!(f, "Failed to bind IPC listener at '{address}': {source}")
   56|       |            }
   57|      0|            Self::ConnectionFailed { address, source } => {
   58|      0|                write!(f, "Failed to connect to IPC endpoint '{address}': {source}")
   59|       |            }
   60|      0|            Self::AcceptFailed { source } => {
   61|      0|                write!(f, "Failed to accept IPC connection: {source}")
   62|       |            }
   63|       |        }
   64|      0|    }
   65|       |}
   66|       |
   67|       |impl std::error::Error for IpcError {
   68|      0|    fn source(&self) -> Option<&(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)> {
   69|      0|        match self {
   70|      0|            Self::BindFailed { source, .. }
   71|      0|            | Self::ConnectionFailed { source, .. }
   72|      0|            | Self::AcceptFailed { source } => Some(source),
   73|       |        }
   74|      0|    }
   75|       |}
   76|       |
   77|       |// Note: IpcError automatically converts to anyhow::Error via the blanket
   78|       |// impl<E: std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static> From<E> for anyhow::Error.
   79|       |// Use `?` operator or `anyhow::Error::from(ipc_error)` for conversion.

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/ipc/stream.rs:
    1|       |//! IPC stream and listener types.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the core abstractions for bidirectional IPC communication:
    4|       |//! - `IpcListener`: Accepts incoming connections (server-side)
    5|       |//! - `IpcStream`: A bidirectional async stream for reading/writing
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! Both types wrap platform-specific implementations and expose a unified API.
    8|       |
    9|       |use super::address::IpcAddress;
   10|       |use super::error::IpcError;
   11|       |use anyhow::Result;
   12|       |use std::io;
   13|       |use std::pin::Pin;
   14|       |use std::task::{Context, Poll};
   15|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncRead, AsyncWrite, ReadBuf};
   16|       |
   17|       |// ============================================================================
   18|       |// IpcStream - Bidirectional async stream
   19|       |// ============================================================================
   20|       |
   21|       |/// A bidirectional async stream for IPC communication.
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// This type wraps platform-specific stream implementations:
   24|       |/// - **Unix**: `tokio::net::UnixStream`
   25|       |/// - **Windows**: `tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeClient/Server`
   26|       |///
   27|       |/// It implements `AsyncRead` and `AsyncWrite`, making it compatible with
   28|       |/// tokio's async I/O ecosystem (e.g., `BufReader`, `BufWriter`).
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// # Protocol
   31|       |///
   32|       |/// The stream is used for newline-delimited JSON messages:
   33|       |/// 1. Read a line (JSON request)
   34|       |/// 2. Parse and process the request
   35|       |/// 3. Write a line (JSON response)
   36|       |///
   37|       |/// # Example
   38|       |///
   39|       |/// ```ignore
   40|       |/// use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
   41|       |/// use paperboat::ipc::IpcStream;
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// async fn handle_connection(stream: IpcStream) -> Result<()> {
   44|       |///     let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
   45|       |///     let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
   46|       |///     
   47|       |///     let mut line = String::new();
   48|       |///     reader.read_line(&mut line).await?;
   49|       |///     
   50|       |///     let response = process_request(&line)?;
   51|       |///     writer.write_all(response.as_bytes()).await?;
   52|       |///     writer.write_all(b"\n").await?;
   53|       |///     writer.flush().await?;
   54|       |///     
   55|       |///     Ok(())
   56|       |/// }
   57|       |/// ```
   58|       |pub struct IpcStream {
   59|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
   60|       |    pub(crate) inner: tokio::net::UnixStream,
   61|       |
   62|       |    #[cfg(windows)]
   63|       |    pub(crate) inner: WindowsStream,
   64|       |}
   65|       |
   66|       |/// Windows stream type - either client or server half of a named pipe.
   67|       |#[cfg(windows)]
   68|       |pub enum WindowsStream {
   69|       |    Client(tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeClient),
   70|       |    Server(tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeServer),
   71|       |}
   72|       |
   73|       |impl IpcStream {
   74|       |    /// Connect to an IPC endpoint as a client.
   75|       |    ///
   76|       |    /// This is used by the MCP server to connect back to the main app.
   77|       |    ///
   78|       |    /// # Errors
   79|       |    ///
   80|       |    /// Returns `IpcError::ConnectionFailed` if the connection cannot be established.
   81|      4|    pub async fn connect(address: &IpcAddress) -> Result<Self, IpcError> {
   82|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
   83|       |        {
   84|      4|            tokio::net::UnixStream::connect(address.as_path())
   85|      4|                .await
   86|      4|                .map(|stream| Self { inner: stream })
   87|      4|                .map_err(|e| IpcError::ConnectionFailed {
   88|      0|                    address: address.to_string(),
   89|      0|                    source: e,
   90|      0|                })
   91|       |        }
   92|       |
   93|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
   94|       |        {
   95|       |            super::windows::connect(address).await
   96|       |        }
   97|      4|    }
   98|       |
   99|       |    /// Split the stream into read and write halves.
  100|       |    ///
  101|       |    /// This allows concurrent reading and writing on the same connection.
  102|       |    /// Uses `tokio::io::split()` which works with any `AsyncRead + AsyncWrite`.
  103|      7|    pub fn into_split(self) -> (IpcReadHalf, IpcWriteHalf) {
  104|      7|        let (read, write) = tokio::io::split(self);
  105|      7|        (IpcReadHalf { inner: read }, IpcWriteHalf { inner: write })
  106|      7|    }
  107|       |}
  108|       |
  109|       |// ============================================================================
  110|       |// IpcReadHalf / IpcWriteHalf - Split halves of an IpcStream
  111|       |// ============================================================================
  112|       |
  113|       |/// The read half of an `IpcStream`, created by `IpcStream::into_split()`.
  114|       |///
  115|       |/// This type implements `AsyncRead` and can be used with buffered readers.
  116|       |pub struct IpcReadHalf {
  117|       |    inner: tokio::io::ReadHalf<IpcStream>,
  118|       |}
  119|       |
  120|       |/// The write half of an `IpcStream`, created by `IpcStream::into_split()`.
  121|       |///
  122|       |/// This type implements `AsyncWrite` and can be used with buffered writers.
  123|       |pub struct IpcWriteHalf {
  124|       |    inner: tokio::io::WriteHalf<IpcStream>,
  125|       |}
  126|       |
  127|       |impl AsyncRead for IpcReadHalf {
  128|     12|    fn poll_read(
  129|     12|        mut self: Pin<&mut Self>,
  130|     12|        cx: &mut Context<'_>,
  131|     12|        buf: &mut ReadBuf<'_>,
  132|     12|    ) -> Poll<io::Result<()>> {
  133|     12|        Pin::new(&mut self.inner).poll_read(cx, buf)
  134|     12|    }
  135|       |}
  136|       |
  137|       |impl AsyncWrite for IpcWriteHalf {
  138|      7|    fn poll_write(
  139|      7|        mut self: Pin<&mut Self>,
  140|      7|        cx: &mut Context<'_>,
  141|      7|        buf: &[u8],
  142|      7|    ) -> Poll<io::Result<usize>> {
  143|      7|        Pin::new(&mut self.inner).poll_write(cx, buf)
  144|      7|    }
  145|       |
  146|      5|    fn poll_flush(mut self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<io::Result<()>> {
  147|      5|        Pin::new(&mut self.inner).poll_flush(cx)
  148|      5|    }
  149|       |
  150|      0|    fn poll_shutdown(mut self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<io::Result<()>> {
  151|      0|        Pin::new(&mut self.inner).poll_shutdown(cx)
  152|      0|    }
  153|       |}
  154|       |
  155|       |// Implement AsyncRead for IpcStream
  156|       |impl AsyncRead for IpcStream {
  157|     12|    fn poll_read(
  158|     12|        mut self: Pin<&mut Self>,
  159|     12|        cx: &mut Context<'_>,
  160|     12|        buf: &mut ReadBuf<'_>,

  228|       |
  229|       |// ============================================================================
  230|       |// IpcListener - Server-side connection acceptor
  231|       |// ============================================================================
  232|       |
  233|       |/// A listener that accepts incoming IPC connections.
  234|       |///
  235|       |/// This type wraps platform-specific listener implementations:
  236|       |/// - **Unix**: `tokio::net::UnixListener`
  237|       |/// - **Windows**: `tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeServer` (recreated per accept)
  238|       |///
  239|       |/// # Server Pattern
  240|       |///
  241|       |/// The listener follows the standard server pattern:
  242|       |/// 1. Bind to an address
  243|       |/// 2. Accept connections in a loop
  244|       |/// 3. Spawn a task for each connection
  245|       |///
  246|       |/// # Example
  247|       |///
  248|       |/// ```ignore
  249|       |/// use paperboat::ipc::{IpcAddress, IpcListener};
  250|       |///
  251|       |/// async fn run_server() -> Result<()> {
  252|       |///     let addr = IpcAddress::generate("my-server");
  253|       |///     let listener = IpcListener::bind(&addr).await?;
  254|       |///
  255|       |///     loop {
  256|       |///         match listener.accept().await {
  257|       |///             Ok(stream) => {
  258|       |///                 tokio::spawn(handle_connection(stream));
  259|       |///             }
  260|       |///             Err(e) => {
  261|       |///                 tracing::error!("Accept failed: {}", e);
  262|       |///                 break;
  263|       |///             }
  264|       |///         }
  265|       |///     }
  266|       |///
  267|       |///     Ok(())
  268|       |/// }
  269|       |/// ```
  270|       |pub struct IpcListener {
  271|       |    #[cfg(unix)]
  272|       |    pub(crate) inner: tokio::net::UnixListener,
  273|       |
  274|       |    #[cfg(windows)]
  275|       |    pub(crate) inner: WindowsListener,
  276|       |}
  277|       |
  278|       |/// Windows listener state - manages named pipe server instances.
  279|       |///
  280|       |/// Uses interior mutability because accept() needs to create new pipe instances.
  281|       |#[cfg(windows)]
  282|       |pub struct WindowsListener {
  283|       |    pub(crate) address: IpcAddress,
  284|       |    pub(crate) current_server:
  285|       |        std::sync::Mutex<Option<tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeServer>>,
  286|       |}
  287|       |
  288|       |impl IpcListener {
  289|       |    /// Bind to an IPC address and start listening.
  290|       |    ///
  291|       |    /// On Unix, this creates a socket file at the specified path.
  292|       |    /// On Windows, this creates a named pipe server.
  293|       |    ///
  294|       |    /// # Cleanup
  295|       |    ///
  296|       |    /// On Unix, any existing socket file at the path is removed before binding.
  297|       |    /// This handles the case where a previous process crashed without cleanup.
  298|       |    ///
  299|       |    /// # Errors
  300|       |    ///
  301|       |    /// Returns `IpcError::BindFailed` if the listener cannot be created.
  302|      9|    pub async fn bind(address: &IpcAddress) -> Result<Self, IpcError> {
  303|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  304|       |        {
  305|       |            // Remove existing socket file (handles previous crash)
  306|      9|            address.cleanup();
  307|       |
  308|      9|            tokio::net::UnixListener::bind(address.as_path())
  309|      9|                .map(|listener| Self { inner: listener })
  310|      9|                .map_err(|e| IpcError::BindFailed {
  311|      0|                    address: address.to_string(),
  312|      0|                    source: e,
  313|      0|                })
  314|       |        }
  315|       |
  316|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  317|       |        {
  318|       |            super::windows::bind(address).await
  319|       |        }
  320|      9|    }
  321|       |
  322|       |    /// Accept a new connection.
  323|       |    ///
  324|       |    /// This method waits for an incoming connection and returns the stream.
  325|       |    /// The returned `IpcStream` can be used for bidirectional communication.
  326|       |    ///
  327|       |    /// # Errors
  328|       |    ///
  329|       |    /// Returns `IpcError::AcceptFailed` if accepting fails. This typically
  330|       |    /// indicates the listener has been closed or an OS-level error occurred.
  331|     12|    pub async fn accept(&self) -> Result<IpcStream, IpcError> {
  332|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  333|       |        {
  334|     12|            self.inner
  335|     12|                .accept()
  336|     12|                .await
  337|      4|                .map(|(stream, _addr)| IpcStream { inner: stream })
  338|      4|                .map_err(|e| IpcError::AcceptFailed { source: e })
                                                                            ^0^0
  339|       |        }
  340|       |
  341|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  342|       |        {
  343|       |            super::windows::accept(&self.inner).await
  344|       |        }
  345|      4|    }
  346|       |
  347|       |    /// Get the address this listener is bound to.
  348|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  349|      0|    pub fn address(&self) -> Option<IpcAddress> {
  350|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  351|       |        {
  352|       |            // Unix sockets don't easily expose their path after binding
  353|      0|            None
  354|       |        }
  355|       |
  356|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  357|       |        {
  358|       |            Some(self.inner.address.clone())
  359|       |        }
  360|      0|    }
  361|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/ipc/unix.rs:
    1|       |//! Unix-specific IPC implementation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides Unix socket implementation details.
    4|       |//! It is only compiled on Unix platforms (macOS, Linux, etc.).
    5|       |
    6|       |// The Unix implementation is straightforward since tokio::net::UnixStream
    7|       |// and tokio::net::UnixListener directly implement AsyncRead/AsyncWrite.
    8|       |//
    9|       |// Most of the implementation is in stream.rs using cfg(unix) blocks.
   10|       |// This module exists for any Unix-specific utilities if needed.
   11|       |
   12|       |use super::address::IpcAddress;
   13|       |use super::stream::IpcStream;
   14|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   15|       |use std::time::Duration;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Connect to an IPC endpoint with retry logic.
   18|       |///
   19|       |/// Uses exponential backoff to handle timing issues when the
   20|       |/// main app's IPC listener might not be ready yet.
   21|       |///
   22|       |/// This wraps `IpcStream::connect` with retry behavior.
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// # Arguments
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// * `address` - The IPC address to connect to
   27|       |///
   28|       |/// # Returns
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// An `IpcStream` connected to the address, or an error after all retries fail.
   31|      0|pub async fn connect_with_retry(address: &IpcAddress) -> Result<IpcStream> {
   32|       |    // Delays in ms: 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000 (total ~4s max wait)
   33|      0|    let delays = [50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000];
   34|      0|    let mut last_error = None;
   35|       |
   36|       |    // First, verify the socket file exists
   37|      0|    if !address.exists() {
   38|      0|        eprintln!("⚠️  Socket file does not exist yet: {address}");
   39|      0|    }
   40|       |
   41|      0|    for (attempt, delay_ms) in delays.iter().enumerate() {
   42|      0|        match IpcStream::connect(address).await {
   43|      0|            Ok(stream) => {
   44|      0|                if attempt > 0 {
   45|      0|                    let attempt_num = attempt + 1;
   46|      0|                    eprintln!("✅ Socket connection succeeded on attempt {attempt_num}");
   47|      0|                }
   48|      0|                return Ok(stream);
   49|       |            }
   50|      0|            Err(e) => {
   51|      0|                let attempt_num = attempt + 1;
   52|      0|                eprintln!(
   53|       |                    "⚠️  Socket connection attempt {attempt_num} failed: {e}. Retrying in {delay_ms}ms..."
   54|       |                );
   55|      0|                last_error = Some(e);
   56|      0|                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(*delay_ms)).await;
   57|       |            }
   58|       |        }
   59|       |    }
   60|       |
   61|       |    // Final attempt with detailed error message
   62|      0|    eprintln!("🔌 Final socket connection attempt to {address}");
   63|      0|    IpcStream::connect(address).await.with_context(|| {
   64|      0|        format!(
   65|       |            "Failed to connect to app socket at {} after {} retries. Last error: {:?}",
   66|       |            address,
   67|      0|            delays.len(),
   68|       |            last_error
   69|       |        )
   70|      0|    })
   71|      0|}
   72|       |
   73|       |#[cfg(test)]
   74|       |mod tests {
   75|       |    use super::*;
   76|       |    use crate::ipc::IpcListener;
   77|       |    use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
   78|       |
   79|       |    #[tokio::test]
   80|      1|    async fn test_ipc_round_trip() {
   81|       |        // Create a unique address
   82|      1|        let addr = IpcAddress::generate("test");
   83|       |
   84|       |        // Set up listener
   85|      1|        let listener = IpcListener::bind(&addr).await.unwrap();
   86|       |
   87|       |        // Spawn server task
   88|      1|        let server_addr = addr.clone();
   89|      1|        let server = tokio::spawn(async move {
   90|      1|            let stream = listener.accept().await.unwrap();
   91|      1|            let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
   92|      1|            let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
   93|       |
   94|      1|            let mut line = String::new();
   95|      1|            reader.read_line(&mut line).await.unwrap();
   96|      1|            assert_eq!(line.trim(), "hello");
   97|       |
   98|      1|            writer.write_all(b"world\n").await.unwrap();
   99|      1|            writer.flush().await.unwrap();
  100|      1|        });
  101|       |
  102|       |        // Give server time to start
  103|      1|        tokio::task::yield_now().await;
  104|       |
  105|       |        // Connect as client
  106|      1|        let stream = IpcStream::connect(&addr).await.unwrap();
  107|      1|        let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
  108|      1|        let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
  109|       |
  110|       |        // Send request
  111|      1|        writer.write_all(b"hello\n").await.unwrap();
  112|      1|        writer.flush().await.unwrap();
  113|       |
  114|       |        // Read response
  115|      1|        let mut response = String::new();
  116|      1|        reader.read_line(&mut response).await.unwrap();
  117|      1|        assert_eq!(response.trim(), "world");
  118|       |
  119|       |        // Wait for server
  120|      1|        server.await.unwrap();
  121|       |
  122|       |        // Cleanup
  123|      1|        addr.cleanup();
  124|      1|    }
  125|       |}

    1|       |//! Run log manager for coordinating logging.
    2|       |
    3|       |use super::scope::LogScope;
    4|       |use super::stream::LogEvent;
    5|       |use chrono::Local;
    6|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
    7|       |use tokio::sync::broadcast;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Manages the log directory for a single application run.
   10|       |///
   11|       |/// Creates the run folder on initialization and provides factory methods
   12|       |/// for creating scoped loggers for the agent hierarchy.
   13|       |pub struct RunLogManager {
   14|       |    /// Root directory for this run (e.g., logs/2026-03-05_14-32-15_abc123/)
   15|       |    run_dir: PathBuf,
   16|       |    /// Broadcast channel for streaming log events to observers (e.g., UI)
   17|       |    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<LogEvent>,
   18|       |}
   19|       |
   20|       |impl RunLogManager {
   21|       |    /// Create a new run log manager.
   22|       |    ///
   23|       |    /// Creates the run directory with format: `{base_dir}/{timestamp}_{short_uuid}/`
   24|     48|    pub fn new(base_dir: &str) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
   25|     48|        let timestamp = Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S");
   26|     48|        let short_uuid = &uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8];
   27|     48|        let run_dir = PathBuf::from(base_dir).join(format!("{timestamp}_{short_uuid}"));
   28|       |
   29|     48|        std::fs::create_dir_all(&run_dir)?;
                                                       ^0
   30|       |
   31|     48|        let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);
   32|       |
   33|     48|        Ok(Self { run_dir, event_tx })
   34|     48|    }
   35|       |
   36|       |    /// Create a `RunLogManager` with a specific run directory (for testing).
   37|       |    #[cfg(test)]
   38|      3|    pub fn with_run_dir(run_dir: PathBuf) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
   39|      3|        std::fs::create_dir_all(&run_dir)?;
                                                       ^0
   40|      3|        let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);
   41|      3|        Ok(Self { run_dir, event_tx })
   42|      3|    }
   43|       |
   44|       |    /// Get the run directory path.
   45|     46|    pub const fn run_dir(&self) -> &PathBuf {
   46|     46|        &self.run_dir
   47|     46|    }
   48|       |
   49|       |    /// Create the root `LogScope` for this run.
   50|       |    ///
   51|       |    /// The root scope is where the app and root orchestrator logs live.
   52|     50|    pub fn root_scope(&self) -> LogScope {
   53|     50|        LogScope::new(self.run_dir.clone(), self.event_tx.clone(), 0)
   54|     50|    }
   55|       |
   56|       |    /// Subscribe to log events for streaming to UI.
   57|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   58|      1|    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<LogEvent> {
   59|      1|        self.event_tx.subscribe()
   60|      1|    }
   61|       |
   62|       |    /// Get a clone of the event sender for task management integration.
   63|      0|    pub fn event_sender(&self) -> broadcast::Sender<LogEvent> {
   64|      0|        self.event_tx.clone()
   65|      0|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/logging/scope.rs:
    1|       |//! Logging scope for hierarchy management.
    2|       |
    3|       |use super::stream::LogEvent;
    4|       |use super::writer::{AgentType, AgentWriter};
    5|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
    6|       |use std::sync::Arc;
    7|       |use tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};
    8|       |
    9|       |/// A logging scope representing one level in the agent hierarchy.
   10|       |///
   11|       |/// Each scope can create writers for agents at this level and
   12|       |/// child scopes for nested decomposition (subtasks).
   13|       |#[derive(Clone)]
   14|       |pub struct LogScope {
   15|       |    /// Directory for this scope's logs
   16|       |    dir: PathBuf,
   17|       |    /// Broadcast channel for log events
   18|       |    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<LogEvent>,
   19|       |    /// Depth in the hierarchy (0 = root)
   20|       |    depth: u32,
   21|       |    /// Counter for implementers at this level
   22|       |    implementer_count: Arc<RwLock<u32>>,
   23|       |    /// Counter for subtasks (decomposed work) at this level
   24|       |    subtask_count: Arc<RwLock<u32>>,
   25|       |}
   26|       |
   27|       |impl LogScope {
   28|       |    /// Create a new logging scope.
   29|     66|    pub fn new(dir: PathBuf, event_tx: broadcast::Sender<LogEvent>, depth: u32) -> Self {
   30|     66|        Self {
   31|     66|            dir,
   32|     66|            event_tx,
   33|     66|            depth,
   34|     66|            implementer_count: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),
   35|     66|            subtask_count: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),
   36|     66|        }
   37|     66|    }
   38|       |
   39|       |    /// Create a writer for the planner at this scope level.
   40|       |    /// In the "always planner first" architecture, this is called first.
   41|     52|    pub async fn planner_writer(&self) -> std::io::Result<AgentWriter> {
   42|     52|        AgentWriter::new(
   43|     52|            self.dir.join("planner.log"),
   44|     52|            AgentType::Planner,
   45|     52|            self.event_tx.clone(),
   46|     52|            self.depth,
   47|     52|        )
   48|     52|        .await
   49|     52|    }
   50|       |
   51|       |    /// Create a writer for the orchestrator at this scope level.
   52|       |    /// Called after planner creates the plan.
   53|     50|    pub async fn orchestrator_writer(&self) -> std::io::Result<AgentWriter> {
   54|     50|        AgentWriter::new(
   55|     50|            self.dir.join("orchestrator.log"),
   56|     50|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
   57|     50|            self.event_tx.clone(),
   58|     50|            self.depth,
   59|     50|        )
   60|     50|        .await
   61|     50|    }
   62|       |
   63|       |    /// Create a writer for a new implementer at this scope level.
   64|       |    /// Returns a numbered writer (implementer-001.log, etc.)
   65|     74|    pub async fn implementer_writer(&self) -> std::io::Result<AgentWriter> {
   66|     74|        let mut count = self.implementer_count.write().await;
   67|     74|        *count += 1;
   68|     74|        let filename = format!("implementer-{:03}.log", *count);
   69|       |
   70|     74|        AgentWriter::new(
   71|     74|            self.dir.join(&filename),
   72|     74|            AgentType::Implementer { index: *count },
   73|     74|            self.event_tx.clone(),
   74|     74|            self.depth,
   75|     74|        )
   76|     74|        .await
   77|     74|    }
   78|       |
   79|       |    /// Create a writer for the self-improver agent.
   80|       |    /// Creates a self-improver.log file in the scope directory.
   81|      2|    pub async fn self_improver_writer(&self) -> std::io::Result<AgentWriter> {
   82|      2|        AgentWriter::new(
   83|      2|            self.dir.join("self-improver.log"),
   84|      2|            AgentType::SelfImprover,
   85|      2|            self.event_tx.clone(),
   86|      2|            self.depth,
   87|      2|        )
   88|      2|        .await
   89|      2|    }
   90|       |
   91|       |    /// Create a child scope for a decompose operation (subtask).
   92|       |    /// Creates a new subdirectory and returns a `LogScope` for it.
   93|     10|    pub async fn child_scope(&self, task_description: &str) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
   94|     10|        let mut count = self.subtask_count.write().await;
   95|     10|        *count += 1;
   96|     10|        let subdir = self.dir.join(format!("subtask-{:03}", *count));
   97|       |
   98|     10|        std::fs::create_dir_all(&subdir)?;
                                                      ^0
   99|       |
  100|       |        // Broadcast subtask creation event
  101|     10|        let _ = self.event_tx.send(LogEvent::SubtaskCreated {
  102|     10|            parent_depth: self.depth,
  103|     10|            new_depth: self.depth + 1,
  104|     10|            path: subdir.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  105|     10|            task_description: task_description.to_string(),
  106|     10|        });
  107|       |
  108|     10|        Ok(Self::new(subdir, self.event_tx.clone(), self.depth + 1))
  109|     10|    }
  110|       |
  111|       |    /// Get the directory path for this scope.
  112|     44|    pub const fn dir(&self) -> &PathBuf {
  113|     44|        &self.dir
  114|     44|    }
  115|       |
  116|       |    /// Get the depth of this scope in the hierarchy.
  117|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  118|    225|    pub const fn depth(&self) -> u32 {
  119|    225|        self.depth
  120|    225|    }
  121|       |}
  122|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/logging/stream.rs:
    1|       |//! Log event types for streaming and observation.
    2|       |
    3|       |use super::writer::AgentType;
    4|       |
    5|       |/// Events broadcast for UI streaming and observation.
    6|       |///
    7|       |/// The fields in each variant are intentionally kept for the streaming API,
    8|       |/// even though they may not currently be used outside of tests.
    9|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   10|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   11|       |pub enum LogEvent {
   12|       |    /// Agent started a new session.
   13|       |    AgentStarted {
   14|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   15|       |        session_id: String,
   16|       |        depth: u32,
   17|       |        task: String,
   18|       |    },
   19|       |
   20|       |    /// Agent sent a message chunk (streaming text).
   21|       |    AgentMessage {
   22|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   23|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   24|       |        depth: u32,
   25|       |        content: String,
   26|       |    },
   27|       |
   28|       |    /// Agent made a tool call.
   29|       |    ToolCall {
   30|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   31|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   32|       |        depth: u32,
   33|       |        tool_name: String,
   34|       |    },
   35|       |
   36|       |    /// Tool execution progress update (streaming).
   37|       |    ToolProgress {
   38|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   39|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   40|       |        depth: u32,
   41|       |        tool_name: String,
   42|       |        progress_text: String,
   43|       |    },
   44|       |
   45|       |    /// Tool call completed.
   46|       |    ToolResult {
   47|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   48|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   49|       |        depth: u32,
   50|       |        tool_name: String,
   51|       |        is_error: bool,
   52|       |    },
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// Agent completed its work.
   55|       |    AgentComplete {
   56|       |        agent_type: AgentType,
   57|       |        session_id: Option<String>,
   58|       |        depth: u32,
   59|       |        success: bool,
   60|       |    },
   61|       |
   62|       |    /// A new child scope was created (subtask from decompose).
   63|       |    SubtaskCreated {
   64|       |        parent_depth: u32,
   65|       |        new_depth: u32,
   66|       |        path: String,
   67|       |        task_description: String,
   68|       |    },
   69|       |
   70|       |    /// A task was created in the task manager.
   71|       |    TaskCreated {
   72|       |        task_id: String,
   73|       |        name: String,
   74|       |        description: String,
   75|       |        dependencies: Vec<String>,
   76|       |        depth: u32,
   77|       |    },
   78|       |
   79|       |    /// A task's state changed (e.g., `pending` -> `in_progress` -> `completed`).
   80|       |    TaskStateChanged {
   81|       |        task_id: String,
   82|       |        name: String,
   83|       |        old_status: String,
   84|       |        new_status: String,
   85|       |        depth: u32,
   86|       |    },
   87|       |}
   88|       |
   89|       |#[cfg(test)]
   90|       |mod tests {
   91|       |    use super::*;
   92|       |
   93|       |    #[test]
   94|      1|    fn test_log_event_clone_and_debug() {
   95|      1|        let event = LogEvent::AgentMessage {
   96|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Planner,
   97|      1|            session_id: Some("test-session".to_string()),
   98|      1|            depth: 0,
   99|      1|            content: "Hello world".to_string(),
  100|      1|        };
  101|       |
  102|       |        // Test Debug
  103|      1|        let debug_str = format!("{event:?}");
  104|      1|        assert!(debug_str.contains("AgentMessage"));
  105|      1|        assert!(debug_str.contains("Planner"));
  106|      1|    }
  107|       |
  108|       |    #[test]
  109|      1|    fn test_all_event_variants() {
  110|      1|        let events = vec![
  111|      1|            LogEvent::AgentStarted {
  112|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  113|      1|                session_id: "sess-1".to_string(),
  114|      1|                depth: 0,
  115|      1|                task: "Test task".to_string(),
  116|      1|            },
  117|      1|            LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  118|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Planner,
  119|      1|                session_id: None,
  120|      1|                depth: 1,
  121|      1|                content: "Planning...".to_string(),
  122|      1|            },
  123|      1|            LogEvent::ToolCall {
  124|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  125|      1|                session_id: Some("sess-2".to_string()),
  126|      1|                depth: 2,
  127|      1|                tool_name: "view".to_string(),
  128|      1|            },
  129|      1|            LogEvent::ToolProgress {
  130|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  131|      1|                session_id: Some("sess-2".to_string()),
  132|      1|                depth: 2,
  133|      1|                tool_name: "view".to_string(),
  134|      1|                progress_text: "Reading file...".to_string(),
  135|      1|            },
  136|      1|            LogEvent::ToolResult {
  137|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Implementer { index: 2 },
  138|      1|                session_id: None,
  139|      1|                depth: 1,
  140|      1|                tool_name: "save-file".to_string(),
  141|      1|                is_error: false,
  142|      1|            },
  143|      1|            LogEvent::AgentComplete {
  144|      1|                agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  145|      1|                session_id: Some("sess-1".to_string()),
  146|      1|                depth: 0,
  147|      1|                success: true,
  148|      1|            },
  149|      1|            LogEvent::SubtaskCreated {

  321|       |    /// Write completion marker and flush.
  322|    162|    pub async fn finalize(&mut self, success: bool) -> std::io::Result<()> {
  323|    162|        let timestamp = Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S");
  324|    162|        let status = if success { "SUCCESS" } else { "FAILURE" };
                                                ^155               ^7
  325|    162|        let duration = self.start_time.elapsed();
  326|    162|        let duration_str = format_duration(duration);
  327|    162|        let footer = format!(
  328|       |            "\n{}\nCompleted: {} - {} ({})\n",
  329|    162|            "=".repeat(60),
  330|       |            timestamp,
  331|       |            status,
  332|       |            duration_str
  333|       |        );
  334|    162|        self.file.write_all(footer.as_bytes()).await?;
                                                                  ^0
  335|    162|        self.file.flush().await?;
                                             ^0
  336|       |
  337|    162|        let _ = self.event_tx.send(LogEvent::AgentComplete {
  338|    162|            agent_type: self.agent_type.clone(),
  339|    162|            session_id: self.session_id.clone(),
  340|    162|            depth: self.depth,
  341|    162|            success,
  342|    162|        });
  343|       |
  344|    162|        Ok(())
  345|    162|    }

  347|       |
  348|       |/// Format a duration as a human-readable string.
  349|    162|fn format_duration(duration: std::time::Duration) -> String {
  350|    162|    let secs = duration.as_secs();
  351|    162|    let millis = duration.subsec_millis();
  352|    162|    if secs >= 60 {
  353|      0|        let mins = secs / 60;
  354|      0|        let secs = secs % 60;
  355|      0|        format!("{mins}m {secs}s")
  356|    162|    } else if secs > 0 {
  357|      0|        format!("{secs}.{millis:03}s")
  358|       |    } else {
  359|    162|        format!("{millis}ms")
  360|       |    }

   38|       |    /// Run in MCP server mode

   45|       |    metrics_enabled: bool,
   46|       |    /// Backend configuration (backend and optional transport).
   47|       |    ///
   48|       |    /// Parsed from `--backend` flag in format "backend:transport".
   49|       |    /// Examples: "cursor", "cursor:cli", "cursor:acp", "auggie:acp"
   50|       |    backend_config: Option<BackendConfig>,
   51|       |    /// The goal/task to accomplish
   52|       |    goal: Option<String>,
