   54|       |
   55|       |/// Parse command-line arguments and extract flags and the goal.
   56|       |///
   57|       |/// # Goal Resolution
   58|       |///
   59|       |/// The goal argument is resolved in this priority order:
   60|       |/// 1. If argument is a file path that exists → read file contents as goal
   61|       |/// 2. Otherwise → use argument as direct prompt string
   62|       |/// 3. No argument → `None` (handled later with interactive prompt)
   63|       |///
   64|       |/// # Backend Selection
   65|       |///
   66|       |/// The `--backend` flag supports an optional transport suffix:
   67|       |/// - `--backend cursor` - Cursor with default transport (CLI)
   68|       |/// - `--backend cursor:cli` - Cursor with CLI transport (explicit)
   69|       |/// - `--backend cursor:acp` - Cursor with ACP transport
   70|       |/// - `--backend auggie` - Auggie with default transport (ACP)
   71|       |/// - `--backend auggie:acp` - Auggie with ACP transport (explicit)
   72|      0|fn parse_args(args: &[String]) -> CliArgs {
   73|       |    // --help or -h shows help and exits
   74|      0|    let help_mode = args.contains(&"--help".to_string()) || args.contains(&"-h".to_string());
   75|       |    // --headless disables TUI mode (TUI is enabled by default)
   76|      0|    let headless_mode = args.contains(&"--headless".to_string());
   77|      0|    let mcp_server_mode = args.get(1).is_some_and(|a| a == "--mcp-server");
   78|      0|    let validate_config_mode = args.contains(&"--validate-config".to_string());
   79|       |    // --json-logs enables JSON formatted logs (can also be set via PAPERBOAT_JSON_LOGS env var)
   80|      0|    let json_logs = args.contains(&"--json-logs".to_string())
   81|      0|        || std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_JSON_LOGS").is_ok_and(|v| v == "1" || v == "true");
   82|       |    // --metrics enables metrics collection (can also be set via PAPERBOAT_METRICS env var)
   83|      0|    let metrics_enabled = args.contains(&"--metrics".to_string())
   84|      0|        || std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_METRICS").is_ok_and(|v| v == "1" || v == "true");
   85|       |
   86|       |    // --backend <name[:transport]> selects the backend and optional transport
   87|       |    // Examples: cursor, cursor:cli, cursor:acp, auggie, auggie:acp
   88|      0|    let backend_config = args
   89|      0|        .iter()
   90|      0|        .position(|a| a == "--backend")
   91|      0|        .and_then(|i| args.get(i + 1))
   92|      0|        .map(|backend_str| {
   93|      0|            match BackendConfig::parse(backend_str) {
   94|      0|                Ok(config) => config,
   95|      0|                Err(e) => {
   96|       |                    // Print error and exit immediately for invalid backend config
   97|      0|                    eprintln!(
   98|       |                        "❌ Invalid --backend value '{backend_str}': {e}\n\n\
   99|       |                        Valid backends:\n  \
  100|       |                          auggie        Augment's Auggie CLI (default transport: acp)\n  \
  101|       |                          cursor        Cursor's agent CLI (default transport: cli)\n\n\
  102|       |                        Transport options (optional suffix):\n  \
  103|       |                          cursor:cli    CLI transport (better MCP support, default for Cursor)\n  \
  104|       |                          cursor:acp    ACP transport (JSON-RPC protocol)\n  \
  105|       |                          auggie:acp    ACP transport (only option for Auggie)\n\n\
  106|       |                        Examples:\n  \
  107|       |                          --backend cursor\n  \
  108|       |                          --backend cursor:cli\n  \
  109|       |                          --backend cursor:acp\n  \
  110|       |                          --backend auggie"
  111|       |                    );
  112|      0|                    std::process::exit(1);
  113|       |                }
  114|       |            }
  115|      0|        });
  116|       |
  117|       |    // Goal is the first non-flag argument after the program name
  118|       |    // Skip arguments that are values for flags (like --backend value)
  119|      0|    let flags_with_values = ["--backend", "--socket"];
  120|      0|    let goal_arg = args
  121|      0|        .iter()
  122|      0|        .enumerate()
  123|      0|        .skip(1)
  124|      0|        .filter(|(i, arg)| {
  125|       |            // Skip flag arguments
  126|      0|            if arg.starts_with("--") || arg.starts_with('-') {
  127|      0|                return false;
  128|      0|            }
  129|       |            // Skip values of flags that take arguments
  130|      0|            if *i > 0 {
  131|      0|                if let Some(prev) = args.get(i - 1) {
  132|      0|                    if flags_with_values.contains(&prev.as_str()) {
  133|      0|                        return false;
  134|      0|                    }
  135|      0|                }
  136|      0|            }
  137|      0|            true
  138|      0|        })
  139|      0|        .map(|(_, arg)| arg.clone())
  140|      0|        .next();
  141|       |
  142|       |    // Resolve goal: check if argument is a file path or direct prompt
  143|      0|    let goal = goal_arg.and_then(|arg| resolve_goal_argument(&arg));
  144|       |
  145|      0|    CliArgs {
  146|      0|        help_mode,
  147|      0|        headless_mode,
  148|      0|        mcp_server_mode,
  149|      0|        validate_config_mode,
  150|      0|        json_logs,
  151|      0|        metrics_enabled,
  152|      0|        backend_config,
  153|      0|        goal,
  154|      0|    }
  155|      0|}
  156|       |
  157|       |/// Resolves a goal argument, checking if it's a file path or a direct prompt.
  158|       |///
  159|       |/// Returns:
  160|       |/// - `Some(content)` if argument is a file that exists (reads file content)
  161|       |/// - `Some(arg)` if argument is not a file (uses as direct prompt)
  162|       |/// - Exits with error if file exists but cannot be read
  163|      0|fn resolve_goal_argument(arg: &str) -> Option<String> {
  164|      0|    let path = PathBuf::from(arg);
  165|       |
  166|       |    // Check if the argument looks like a file path and exists
  167|      0|    if path.exists() {
  168|      0|        match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
  169|      0|            Ok(content) => {
  170|      0|                let trimmed = content.trim();
  171|      0|                if trimmed.is_empty() {
  172|      0|                    eprintln!("❌ Goal file is empty: {}", path.display());
  173|      0|                    std::process::exit(1);
  174|      0|                }
  175|      0|                eprintln!("📄 Reading goal from file: {}", path.display());
  176|      0|                Some(trimmed.to_string())
  177|       |            }
  178|      0|            Err(e) => {
  179|      0|                eprintln!("❌ Failed to read goal file '{}': {}", path.display(), e);
  180|      0|                std::process::exit(1);
  181|       |            }
  182|       |        }
  183|       |    } else {
  184|       |        // Not a file, use as direct prompt
  185|      0|        let trimmed = arg.trim();
  186|      0|        if trimmed.is_empty() {
  187|      0|            None
  188|       |        } else {
  189|      0|            Some(trimmed.to_string())
  190|       |        }
  191|       |    }
  192|      0|}
  193|       |
  194|       |/// Prompts the user for a goal interactively via stdin.
  195|       |///
  196|       |/// This function handles:
  197|       |/// - Checking if stdin is a terminal (returns None if not)
  198|       |/// - Timeout handling (60 seconds)
  199|       |/// - Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D handling (exits gracefully)
  200|       |/// - Empty input handling
  201|       |///
  202|       |/// Returns `Some(goal)` if user enters valid input, `None` if stdin is not a terminal.
  203|      0|fn prompt_goal_interactively() -> Option<String> {
  204|       |    // Don't prompt if stdin is not a terminal
  205|      0|    if !std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal(&io::stdin()) {
  206|      0|        return None;
  207|      0|    }
  208|       |
  209|      0|    println!("\n🎯 No goal provided. Please enter your task:");
  210|      0|    println!("(Enter your task/goal. Press Enter twice or Ctrl+D when done)\n");
  211|      0|    print!("> ");
  212|      0|    io::stdout().flush().ok()?;
  213|       |
  214|       |    // Use a channel with timeout for reading input
  215|      0|    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();
  216|      0|    let stdin_thread = std::thread::spawn(move || {
  217|      0|        let stdin = io::stdin();
  218|      0|        let mut lines = Vec::new();
  219|       |
  220|       |        // Read multiple lines until empty line or EOF
  221|       |        loop {
  222|      0|            let mut line = String::new();
  223|      0|            let read_result = stdin.lock().read_line(&mut line);
  224|      0|            match read_result {
  225|       |                Ok(0) => {
  226|       |                    // EOF (Ctrl+D on Unix)
  227|      0|                    break;
  228|       |                }
  229|       |                Ok(_) => {
  230|       |                    // Check for empty line (just Enter pressed) to end input
  231|      0|                    if line.trim().is_empty() && !lines.is_empty() {
  232|      0|                        break;
  233|      0|                    }
  234|      0|                    lines.push(line);
  235|       |                }
  236|       |                Err(_) => {
  237|       |                    // Read error (possibly Ctrl+C)
  238|      0|                    let _ = tx.send(None);
  239|      0|                    return;
  240|       |                }
  241|       |            }
  242|       |        }
  243|       |
  244|      0|        let combined = lines.concat();
  245|      0|        let trimmed = combined.trim();
  246|      0|        if trimmed.is_empty() {
  247|      0|            let _ = tx.send(None);
  248|      0|        } else {
  249|      0|            let _ = tx.send(Some(trimmed.to_string()));
  250|      0|        }
  251|      0|    });
  252|       |
  253|       |    // Wait for input with a 60-second timeout
  254|      0|    let result = match rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60)) {
  255|      0|        Ok(Some(goal)) => {
  256|      0|            println!("\n✓ Goal received\n");
  257|      0|            Some(goal)
  258|       |        }
  259|       |        Ok(None) => {
  260|       |            // Empty input or error
  261|      0|            println!();
  262|      0|            eprintln!("\n❌ No goal provided. Exiting.");
  263|      0|            std::process::exit(1);
  264|       |        }
  265|       |        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => {
  266|      0|            println!();
  267|      0|            eprintln!("\n⏱ Input timeout. Exiting.");
  268|      0|            std::process::exit(1);
  269|       |        }
  270|       |        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => {
  271|       |            // Thread panicked or disconnected
  272|      0|            println!();
  273|      0|            eprintln!("\n❌ Input error. Exiting.");
  274|      0|            std::process::exit(1);
  275|       |        }
  276|       |    };
  277|       |
  278|       |    // Wait for the stdin thread to finish
  279|      0|    let _ = stdin_thread.join();
  280|       |
  281|      0|    result
  282|      0|}
  283|       |

  417|       |

  421|       |

  723|      0|                signal(SignalKind::terminate()).expect("Failed to set up SIGTERM handler");

  731|       |                }

  737|       |                }

  742|      0|            if let Some(tx) = tx_opt {
  743|      0|                let _ = tx.send(());
  744|      0|            }
  745|       |        }

  749|       |            // On Windows, we use Ctrl+C handler which is cross-platform in tokio
  750|       |            if let Err(e) = tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await {
  751|       |                tracing::error!("Failed to listen for Ctrl+C: {}", e);
  752|       |                return;
  753|       |            }
  754|       |            tracing::info!("📴 Received Ctrl+C, initiating shutdown...");
  755|       |
  756|       |            // Signal main task to shutdown
  757|       |            let tx_opt = signal_shutdown_tx.lock().unwrap().take();
  758|       |            if let Some(tx) = tx_opt {
  759|       |                let _ = tx.send(());
  760|       |            }
  761|       |        }
  762|      0|    });
  763|       |
  764|       |    // Run the main task with ability to be interrupted

  766|      0|        result = app.run(&goal) => result?,

  771|       |        }

  773|       |

  775|      0|    app.shutdown().await?;
  776|       |
  777|       |    // Self-improvement phase (only in paperboat's own repository)

  779|       |    // Errors are non-fatal - they're logged but don't affect the main task's result.
  780|      0|    {

  783|      0|            Ok(Some(outcome)) => {
  784|      0|                if outcome.success {
  785|      0|                    tracing::info!("🔄 Self-improvement completed successfully");

  787|      0|                        tracing::debug!("Self-improvement message: {}", msg);
  788|      0|                    }
  789|      0|                } else {
  790|      0|                    tracing::warn!("🔄 Self-improvement completed with issues");
  791|      0|                    if let Some(msg) = outcome.message {
  792|      0|                        tracing::warn!("Self-improvement message: {}", msg);
  793|      0|                    }
  794|      0|                }
  795|      0|            }
  796|      0|            Ok(None) => {
  797|      0|                // Self-improvement was skipped (disabled, not in paperboat repo, etc.)
  798|      0|                tracing::debug!("Self-improvement skipped");
  799|      0|            }
  800|      0|            Err(e) => {
  801|      0|                // Self-improvement failed, but this is non-fatal
  802|      0|                tracing::warn!("Self-improvement failed (non-fatal): {}", e);
  803|      0|            }
  804|      0|        }
  805|      0|    }
  806|      0|
  807|      0|    // Wait for TUI thread to finish if it was started
  808|      0|    #[cfg(feature = "tui")]
  809|      0|    if let Some(handle) = tui_handle {
  810|      0|        // The TUI thread will exit when it receives the quit command or the channel closes
  811|      0|        match handle.join() {
  812|      0|            Ok(Ok(())) => {

  814|      0|            }
  815|      0|            Ok(Err(e)) => {

  817|      0|                tracing::warn!("TUI thread exited with error: {}", e);
  818|      0|            }
  819|      0|            Err(_) => {
  820|      0|                // Thread panicked, but we handled it with the panic hook
  821|      0|                tracing::warn!("TUI thread panicked");
  822|      0|            }
  823|      0|        }
  824|      0|    }
  825|      0|
  826|      0|    // Print human action items if any exist

  830|      0|            println!("{human_actions}");
  831|      0|        }
  832|      0|    }
  833|      0|
  834|      0|    if result.success {
  835|      0|        println!("\n✅ Task completed successfully!");
  836|      0|    } else {
  837|      0|        println!("\n❌ Task failed");
  838|      0|    }
  839|      0|
  840|      0|    Ok(())

  885|       |/// Run config validation mode.
  886|       |///
  887|       |/// Loads and validates all configuration files without running the app.
  888|       |/// Prints validation results and exits with code 0 on success, 1 on failure.
  889|       |#[allow(clippy::unnecessary_wraps)]
  890|      0|fn run_validate_config_mode() -> Result<()> {
  891|      0|    println!("🔍 Validating Paperboat configuration files...\n");
  892|       |
  893|       |    // Try to load agent configs - this will validate them during loading
  894|      0|    match load_agent_configs() {
  895|      0|        Ok(loaded) => {
  896|      0|            println!("✅ Configuration files are valid!");
  897|      0|            println!();
  898|      0|            println!("Loaded configurations:");
  899|       |
  900|       |            // Report orchestrator config
  901|      0|            if let Some(ref model) = loaded.orchestrator.model {
  902|      0|                println!("  Orchestrator: model = \"{model}\"");
  903|      0|            } else {
  904|      0|                println!("  Orchestrator: (using defaults)");
  905|      0|            }
  906|       |
  907|       |            // Report planner config
  908|      0|            if let Some(ref model) = loaded.planner.model {
  909|      0|                println!("  Planner: model = \"{model}\"");
  910|      0|            } else {
  911|      0|                println!("  Planner: (using defaults)");
  912|      0|            }
  913|       |
  914|       |            // Report implementer config
  915|      0|            if let Some(ref model) = loaded.implementer.model {
  916|      0|                println!("  Implementer: model = \"{model}\"");
  917|      0|            } else {
  918|      0|                println!("  Implementer: (using defaults)");
  919|      0|            }
  920|       |
  921|      0|            println!();
  922|      0|            println!(
  923|       |                "Config locations searched:\n  User-level: ~/.paperboat/agents/\n  Project-level: .paperboat/agents/"
  924|       |            );
  925|       |
  926|      0|            Ok(())
  927|       |        }
  928|      0|        Err(e) => {
  929|      0|            eprintln!("❌ Configuration validation failed!\n");
  930|      0|            eprintln!("Error: {e}");
  931|       |
  932|       |            // Print chain of errors for context
  933|      0|            let mut source = e.source();
  934|      0|            while let Some(cause) = source {
  935|      0|                eprintln!("Caused by: {cause}");
  936|      0|                source = cause.source();
  937|      0|            }
  938|       |
  939|      0|            std::process::exit(1);
  940|       |        }
  941|       |    }

    2|       |//!

    8|       |// JSON-RPC 2.0 standard error codes
    9|       |pub const PARSE_ERROR: i32 = -32700;
   10|       |pub const INVALID_REQUEST: i32 = -32600;
   11|       |pub const METHOD_NOT_FOUND: i32 = -32601;
   12|       |pub const INVALID_PARAMS: i32 = -32602;
   13|       |pub const INTERNAL_ERROR: i32 = -32603;
   14|       |
   15|       |/// Create a JSON-RPC 2.0 error response without additional data.
   16|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   17|      2|pub fn json_rpc_error(id: Option<&Value>, code: i32, message: &str) -> Value {
   18|      2|    json!({
   19|      2|        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
   20|      2|        "id": id,
   21|      2|        "error": { "code": code, "message": message }
   22|       |    })
   23|      2|}
   24|       |
   25|       |/// Create a JSON-RPC 2.0 error response with additional structured data.
   26|     16|pub fn json_rpc_error_with_data(
   27|     16|    id: Option<&Value>,
   28|     16|    code: i32,
   29|     16|    message: &str,
   30|     16|    data: &Value,
   31|     16|) -> Value {
   32|     16|    json!({
   33|     16|        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
   34|     16|        "id": id,
   35|     16|        "error": { "code": code, "message": message, "data": data }
   36|       |    })
   37|     16|}

   39|       |/// Create a parse error response (-32700).
   40|       |///
   41|       |/// Used when the server receives invalid JSON or cannot parse the request.
   42|      2|pub fn parse_error(parse_err: &str, input_preview: Option<&str>) -> Value {
   43|      2|    let data = match input_preview {
   44|      1|        Some(preview) => json!({ "parse_error": parse_err, "input_preview": preview }),
   45|      1|        None => json!({ "parse_error": parse_err }),
   46|       |    };
   47|      2|    json_rpc_error_with_data(
   48|      2|        None,
   49|       |        PARSE_ERROR,
   50|      2|        &format!("Parse error: {parse_err}"),
   51|      2|        &data,
   52|       |    )

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/mcp_server/handlers/mod.rs:
    1|       |//! JSON-RPC request handlers for the MCP server.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module is organized into:
    4|       |//! - `tool_schemas`: JSON schema definitions for each tool
    5|       |//! - `tool_parsing`: Functions to parse tool call arguments
    6|       |//! - `response`: Functions to build response text
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! The main entry point is `handle_request` which dispatches JSON-RPC requests.
    9|       |
   10|       |mod response;
   11|       |mod tool_parsing;
   12|       |mod tool_schemas;
   13|       |
   14|       |use super::error::{
   15|       |    internal_error, invalid_params_error, invalid_request_error, method_not_found_error,
   16|       |};
   17|       |use super::socket::send_request_and_wait;
   18|       |use super::types::ToolRequest;
   19|       |use crate::ipc::IpcAddress;
   20|       |use anyhow::Result;
   21|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   22|       |
   23|       |/// Handle a JSON-RPC request
   24|     23|pub async fn handle_request(request: &Value, socket_address: &IpcAddress) -> Result<Option<Value>> {
   25|     23|    let id = request.get("id");
   26|       |
   27|       |    // Extract method, returning error response if missing
   28|     23|    let method = if let Some(m) = request["method"].as_str() {
                      ^22                  ^22
   29|     22|        m
   30|       |    } else {
   31|      1|        tracing::warn!("⚠️  Request missing 'method' field: {:?}", request);
   32|      1|        return Ok(Some(invalid_request_error(id, "missing 'method' field")));
   33|       |    };
   34|       |
   35|      2|    match method {
   36|     22|        "initialize" => Ok(Some(handle_initialize(id))),
                                      ^1
   37|     21|        "tools/list" => Ok(Some(handle_tools_list(id))),
                                      ^12
   38|      9|        "tools/call" => handle_tool_call(request, id.cloned(), socket_address).await,
                                      ^7               ^7       ^7 ^7
   39|       |        // Handle notifications (no id) - just log and ignore
   40|      2|        _ if id.is_none() => {
                                      ^1
   41|      1|            tracing::debug!("Ignoring notification with method: {}", method);
   42|      1|            Ok(None)
   43|       |        }
   44|       |        // Unknown method with id - return proper error
   45|       |        _ => {
   46|      1|            tracing::warn!("⚠️  Unknown method: {}", method);
   47|      1|            Ok(Some(method_not_found_error(
   48|      1|                id,
   49|      1|                method,
   50|      1|                &["initialize", "tools/list", "tools/call"],
   51|      1|            )))
   52|       |        }
   53|       |    }
   54|     23|}

  202|      0|            Ok(tc) => tc,
  203|      0|            Err(msg) => {
  204|      0|                return Ok(Some(invalid_params_error(
  205|      0|                    id.as_ref(),
  206|      0|                    "report_human_action",
  207|      0|                    msg,
  208|      0|                )))
  209|       |            }
  210|       |        },
  211|       |        _ => {
  212|      1|            tracing::warn!("⚠️  Unknown tool requested: {}", name);
  213|      1|            return Ok(Some(method_not_found_error(
  214|      1|                id.as_ref(),
  215|      1|                name,
  216|      1|                &[
  217|      1|                    "decompose",
  218|      1|                    "spawn_agents",
  219|      1|                    "complete",
  220|      1|                    "create_task",
  221|      1|                    "set_goal",
  222|      1|                    "skip_tasks",
  223|      1|                    "list_tasks",
  224|      1|                    "report_human_action",
  225|      1|                ],
  226|      1|            )));
  227|       |        }
  228|       |    };
  229|       |
  230|      0|    eprintln!("🔧 MCP Tool call: {tool_call:?}");
  231|       |
  232|       |    // Create request with unique ID for correlation
  233|      0|    let request_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
  234|      0|    let tool_request = ToolRequest {
  235|      0|        request_id: request_id.clone(),
  236|      0|        tool_call: tool_call.clone(),
  237|      0|    };
  238|       |
  239|       |    // Send request and wait for response from the app
  240|      0|    let tool_response = match send_request_and_wait(socket_address, &tool_request).await {
  241|      0|        Ok(resp) => resp,
  242|      0|        Err(e) => {
  243|      0|            tracing::error!("Failed to get response from app: {}", e);
  244|      0|            return Ok(Some(internal_error(
  245|      0|                id.as_ref(),
  246|      0|                "socket communication",
  247|      0|                &e.to_string(),
  248|      0|            )));
  249|       |        }
  250|       |    };
  251|       |
  252|       |    // Build response text based on actual result from the app
  253|       |    // Pass task state from response for context-aware "What's Next" guidance
  254|      0|    let response_text = response::build_response_text_with_state(
  255|      0|        &tool_call,
  256|      0|        &tool_response,
  257|      0|        tool_response.task_state.as_ref(),
  258|       |    );
  259|       |
  260|      0|    let result = json!({
  261|      0|        "content": [
  262|       |            {
  263|      0|                "type": "text",
  264|      0|                "text": response_text
  265|       |            }
  266|       |        ]
  267|       |    });
  268|       |
  269|      0|    Ok(Some(json!({
  270|      0|        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  271|      0|        "id": id,
  272|      0|        "result": result
  273|      0|    })))
  274|      7|}

  287|       |    /// Helper to extract tool names from a tools/list response.
  288|      9|    fn extract_tool_names(response: &Value) -> HashSet<String> {
  289|      9|        response["result"]["tools"]
  290|      9|            .as_array()
  291|      9|            .map(|tools| {
  292|      9|                tools
  293|      9|                    .iter()
  294|     39|                    .filter_map(|t| t["name"].as_str().map(String::from))
                                   ^9
  295|      9|                    .collect()
  296|      9|            })
  297|      9|            .unwrap_or_default()
  298|      9|    }

  302|       |    #[tokio::test]

  304|      2|    async fn test_planner_tool_access() {
  305|       |        // Set agent type to planner
  306|      1|        std::env::set_var("PAPERBOAT_AGENT_TYPE", "planner");
  307|       |
  308|      1|        let request = json!({
  309|      1|            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  310|      1|            "id": 1,
  311|      1|            "method": "tools/list"
  312|       |        });
  313|       |
  314|      1|        let socket_address = IpcAddress::from_string("/tmp/test-socket");
  315|      1|        let response = handle_request(&request, &socket_address)
  316|      1|            .await
  317|      1|            .unwrap()
  318|      1|            .unwrap();
  319|       |

  321|       |

  325|       |            "Planner should have create_task tool"

  329|       |            "Planner should have complete tool"
  330|       |        );
  331|       |

  333|      1|        assert!(

  335|       |            "Planner should NOT have spawn_agents tool"
  336|       |        );
  337|      1|        assert!(
  338|      1|            !tool_names.contains("decompose"),
  339|       |            "Planner should NOT have decompose tool"
  340|       |        );

  342|      1|            !tool_names.contains("skip_tasks"),

  349|       |            "Planner should have set_goal tool"
  350|       |        );
  351|       |

  607|       |    /// Test that skip_tasks tool definition is available to orchestrators.
  608|       |    #[tokio::test]
  609|       |    #[serial]
  610|      2|    async fn test_skip_tasks_tool_definition() {
  611|      1|        std::env::set_var("PAPERBOAT_AGENT_TYPE", "orchestrator");
  612|       |
  613|      1|        let request = json!({
  614|      1|            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  615|      1|            "id": 1,
  616|      1|            "method": "tools/list"
  617|       |        });
  618|       |
  619|      1|        let socket_address = IpcAddress::from_string("/tmp/test-socket");
  620|      1|        let response = handle_request(&request, &socket_address)
  621|      1|            .await
  622|      1|            .unwrap()
  623|      1|            .unwrap();
  624|       |
  625|       |        // Find the skip_tasks tool definition
  626|      1|        let tools = response["result"]["tools"].as_array().unwrap();
  627|      1|        let skip_tasks_tool = tools
  628|      1|            .iter()
  629|      5|            .find(|t| t["name"] == "skip_tasks")
                           ^1
  630|      1|            .expect("skip_tasks tool should exist for orchestrator");
  631|       |
  632|       |        // Verify schema structure
  633|      1|        let schema = &skip_tasks_tool["inputSchema"];
  634|      1|        assert_eq!(schema["type"], "object");
  635|       |
  636|       |        // Check task_ids property
  637|      1|        let task_ids_prop = &schema["properties"]["task_ids"];
  638|      1|        assert_eq!(task_ids_prop["type"], "array");
  639|      1|        assert_eq!(task_ids_prop["items"]["type"], "string");
  640|       |
  641|       |        // Check reason property
  642|      1|        let reason_prop = &schema["properties"]["reason"];
  643|      1|        assert_eq!(reason_prop["type"], "string");
  644|       |
  645|       |        // Check required fields
  646|      1|        let required = schema["required"].as_array().unwrap();
  647|      1|        assert!(required.contains(&json!("task_ids")));
  648|      1|        assert!(
  649|      1|            !required.contains(&json!("reason")),
  650|      1|            "reason should be optional"
  651|      1|        );
  652|       |    }

  679|       |    /// Test parsing of skip_tasks tool call with valid arguments.
  680|       |    #[test]
  681|      1|    fn test_parse_skip_tasks_valid() {
  682|       |        // This test validates the parsing logic by checking the ToolCall variant construction
  683|      1|        let task_ids = vec!["task001".to_string(), "task002".to_string()];
  684|      1|        let reason = Some("Already completed by previous task".to_string());
  685|       |
  686|      1|        let tool_call = ToolCall::SkipTasks {
  687|      1|            task_ids: task_ids.clone(),
  688|      1|            reason: reason.clone(),
  689|      1|        };
  690|       |
  691|      1|        assert_eq!(tool_call.tool_type(), "skip_tasks");
  692|       |        if let ToolCall::SkipTasks {
  693|      1|            task_ids: ids,
  694|      1|            reason: r,
  695|      1|        } = tool_call
  696|       |        {
  697|      1|            assert_eq!(ids, task_ids);
  698|      1|            assert_eq!(r, reason);
  699|       |        } else {
  700|      0|            panic!("Expected SkipTasks variant");
  701|       |        }
  702|      1|    }
  703|       |
  704|       |    /// Test parsing of skip_tasks tool call without reason (optional field).
  705|       |    #[test]
  706|      1|    fn test_parse_skip_tasks_without_reason() {
  707|      1|        let task_ids = vec!["task001".to_string()];
  708|       |
  709|      1|        let tool_call = ToolCall::SkipTasks {
  710|      1|            task_ids: task_ids.clone(),
  711|      1|            reason: None,
  712|      1|        };
  713|       |
  714|      1|        assert_eq!(tool_call.tool_type(), "skip_tasks");
  715|       |        if let ToolCall::SkipTasks {
  716|      1|            task_ids: ids,
  717|      1|            reason,
  718|      1|        } = tool_call
  719|       |        {
  720|      1|            assert_eq!(ids, task_ids);
  721|      1|            assert!(reason.is_none());
  722|       |        } else {
  723|      0|            panic!("Expected SkipTasks variant");
  724|       |        }
  725|      1|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/mcp_server/handlers/response.rs:
    1|       |//! Response text builders for tool calls.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Contains functions to build user-friendly response messages from tool call results.
    4|       |//!
    5|       |//! ## Context-Aware Responses
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! Response builders support an optional `TaskStateInfo` parameter that enables
    8|       |//! dynamic "What's Next" guidance based on actual task state. When provided,
    9|       |//! responses include:
   10|       |//! - Count of remaining tasks
   11|       |//! - Tasks that can run in parallel (no dependencies)
   12|       |//! - Blocked tasks and what they're waiting for
   13|       |//! - Concrete next action suggestions
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! See `docs/INTENT_BASED_MCP_DESIGN.md` for the design rationale.
   16|       |
   17|       |use crate::mcp_server::types::{TaskStateInfo, ToolCall, ToolResponse, WaitMode};
   18|       |
   19|       |impl TaskStateInfo {
   20|       |    /// Check if there are any remaining tasks to work on.
   21|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   22|      4|    pub const fn has_remaining_work(&self) -> bool {
   23|      4|        self.pending_count > 0 || !self.blocked_tasks.is_empty()
                                                ^2
   24|      4|    }
   25|       |
   26|       |    /// Format the "What's Next" section based on task state.
   27|       |    ///
   28|       |    /// Returns a formatted string with actionable guidance, or None if
   29|       |    /// there's no meaningful guidance to provide (e.g., all tasks complete).
   30|      9|    pub fn format_whats_next(&self) -> Option<String> {
   31|      9|        let mut lines = Vec::new();
   32|       |
   33|       |        // Show count of remaining tasks
   34|      9|        if self.pending_count > 0 {
   35|      7|            let task_word = if self.pending_count == 1 {
   36|      2|                "task"
   37|       |            } else {
   38|      5|                "tasks"
   39|       |            };
   40|      7|            lines.push(format!(
   41|       |                "- **{} {} remaining**",
   42|       |                self.pending_count, task_word
   43|       |            ));
   44|      2|        }
   45|       |
   46|       |        // Show parallel execution opportunities
   47|      9|        if self.parallel_tasks.len() > 1 {
   48|      3|            lines.push(format!(
   49|       |                "- {} and {} have no dependencies—spawn them together for parallel execution",
   50|      3|                self.parallel_tasks[0],
   51|      3|                if self.parallel_tasks.len() == 2 {
   52|      2|                    self.parallel_tasks[1].clone()
   53|       |                } else {
   54|      1|                    format!("{} others", self.parallel_tasks.len() - 1)
   55|       |                }
   56|       |            ));
   57|      6|        } else if self.parallel_tasks.len() == 1 {
   58|      3|            lines.push(format!(
   59|      3|                "- {} is ready to execute (no dependencies)",
   60|      3|                self.parallel_tasks[0]
   61|      3|            ));
   62|      3|        }
   63|       |
   64|       |        // Show blocked tasks
   65|      9|        for (task_id, blockers) in &self.blocked_tasks {
                           ^4       ^4
   66|      4|            let blocker_list = blockers.join(", ");
   67|      4|            lines.push(format!(
   68|       |                "- {} depends on {}; wait for {} first",
   69|       |                task_id,
   70|       |                blocker_list,
   71|      4|                if blockers.len() == 1 { "it" } else { "them" }
                                                       ^2            ^2
   72|       |            ));
   73|       |        }
   74|       |
   75|       |        // Provide completion hint if no work remains
   76|      9|        if self.pending_count == 0 && self.blocked_tasks.is_empty() {
                                                    ^2                 ^2
   77|      2|            lines.push("- All tasks resolved! Call complete(success=true) to finish.".to_string());
   78|      7|        }
   79|       |
   80|      9|        if lines.is_empty() {
   81|      0|            None
   82|       |        } else {
   83|      9|            Some(lines.join("\n"))
   84|       |        }
   85|      9|    }
   86|       |}
   87|       |
   88|       |/// Build a helpful response message based on the tool call and app response.
   89|       |///
   90|       |/// This is the backward-compatible version without task state. Use
   91|       |/// `build_response_text_with_state` for context-aware responses.
   92|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
   93|      1|pub fn build_response_text(tool_call: &ToolCall, response: &ToolResponse) -> String {
   94|      1|    build_response_text_with_state(tool_call, response, None)
   95|      1|}
   96|       |
   97|       |/// Build a helpful response message with optional task state for context-aware guidance.
   98|       |///
   99|       |/// When `task_state` is provided, success responses include dynamic "What's Next"
  100|       |/// sections showing remaining tasks, parallel execution opportunities, and blocked tasks.
  101|       |///
  102|       |/// # Arguments
  103|       |///
  104|       |/// * `tool_call` - The tool call that was executed
  105|       |/// * `response` - The response from executing the tool call
  106|       |/// * `task_state` - Optional snapshot of current task state for context-aware guidance
  107|       |///
  108|       |/// # Example
  109|       |///
  110|       |/// ```ignore
  111|       |/// let state = TaskStateInfo {
  112|       |///     pending_count: 2,
  113|       |///     parallel_tasks: vec!["task004".into()],
  114|       |///     blocked_tasks: vec![("task005".into(), vec!["task004".into()])],
  115|       |/// };
  116|       |/// let text = build_response_text_with_state(&tool_call, &response, Some(&state));
  117|       |/// ```
  118|     33|pub fn build_response_text_with_state(
  119|     33|    tool_call: &ToolCall,
  120|     33|    response: &ToolResponse,
  121|     33|    task_state: Option<&TaskStateInfo>,
  122|     33|) -> String {
  123|     33|    match tool_call {
  124|      5|        ToolCall::Decompose { task_id, task } => {
  125|      5|            build_decompose_response(task_id.as_ref(), task.as_ref(), response, task_state)
  126|       |        }
  127|      7|        ToolCall::SpawnAgents { agents, wait } => {
  128|      7|            build_spawn_agents_response(agents.len(), agents, *wait, response, task_state)
  129|       |        }
  130|       |        ToolCall::Complete {
  131|      5|            success, message, ..
  132|      5|        } => build_complete_response(*success, message.as_deref()),
  133|      3|        ToolCall::CreateTask { name, .. } => build_create_task_response(name, response),
  134|      2|        ToolCall::SetGoal { summary, .. } => build_set_goal_response(summary, response),
  135|      4|        ToolCall::SkipTasks { task_ids, reason } => {
  136|      4|            build_skip_tasks_response(task_ids, reason.as_deref(), response, task_state)
  137|       |        }
  138|      4|        ToolCall::ListTasks { status_filter } => {
  139|      4|            build_list_tasks_response(status_filter.as_deref(), response)
  140|       |        }
  141|      3|        ToolCall::ReportHumanAction { description, .. } => {
  142|      3|            build_report_human_action_response(description, response)
  143|       |        }
  144|       |    }
  145|     33|}
  146|       |
  147|      4|fn build_list_tasks_response(status_filter: Option<&str>, response: &ToolResponse) -> String {
  148|      4|    let filter = status_filter.unwrap_or("all");
  149|      4|    if response.success {
  150|       |        // The response.summary contains the formatted task list
  151|      2|        response.summary.clone()
  152|       |    } else {
  153|      2|        let error_msg = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or(&response.summary);
  154|      2|        format!(
  155|       |            "❌ Failed to list tasks (filter={filter}): {error_msg}\n\n\
  156|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  157|       |             The task list could not be retrieved. This may occur if:\n\
  158|       |             - No goal has been set yet (tasks require a goal context)\n\
  159|       |             - The task database is in an inconsistent state\n\n\
  160|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  161|       |             - Call set_goal() first to establish the goal context\n\
  162|       |             - Try again with a different status_filter (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', or omit for 'all')\n\
  163|       |             - If the problem persists, the orchestrator may need to be restarted"
  164|       |        )
  165|       |    }

  168|      3|fn build_report_human_action_response(description: &str, response: &ToolResponse) -> String {
  169|      3|    if response.success {
  170|      2|        let preview = if description.len() > 60 {
  171|      1|            format!("{}...", &description[..57])
  172|       |        } else {
  173|      1|            description.to_string()
  174|       |        };
  175|      2|        format!(
  176|       |            "✅ Human action recorded: \"{preview}\"\n\n\
  177|       |             This will be displayed prominently at the end of the run."
  178|       |        )
  179|       |    } else {
  180|      1|        let error_msg = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or(&response.summary);
  181|      1|        format!(
  182|       |            "❌ Failed to record human action: {error_msg}\n\n\
  183|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  184|       |             - Ensure the description is a non-empty string\n\
  185|       |             - Try calling the tool again with a valid description"
  186|       |        )
  187|       |    }

  189|       |
  190|      5|fn build_decompose_response(
  191|      5|    task_id: Option<&String>,
  192|      5|    task: Option<&String>,
  193|      5|    response: &ToolResponse,
  194|      5|    task_state: Option<&TaskStateInfo>,
  195|      5|) -> String {
  196|      5|    let task_desc = task
  197|      5|        .map(String::as_str)
  198|      5|        .or_else(|| task_id.map(String::as_str))
                                  ^4      ^4
  199|      5|        .unwrap_or("(unknown task)");
  200|      5|    if response.success {
  201|       |        // Build dynamic "What's Next" section if task state is available
  202|      4|        let next_steps = task_state
  203|      4|            .and_then(TaskStateInfo::format_whats_next)
  204|      4|            .map_or_else(
  205|      3|                || {
  206|      3|                    "## Next Steps\n\
  207|      3|                 The subtasks have been planned and executed. \
  208|      3|                 Continue with any remaining tasks or call complete() when done."
  209|      3|                        .to_string()
  210|      3|                },
  211|      1|                |guidance| format!("## What's Next\n{guidance}"),
  212|       |            );
  213|       |
  214|      4|        format!(
  215|       |            "✅ Decomposition complete for: \"{}\"\n\n\
  216|       |             ## Summary\n\
  217|       |             {}\n\n\
  218|       |             {}",
  219|       |            task_desc, response.summary, next_steps
  220|       |        )
  221|       |    } else {
  222|      1|        let error = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error");
  223|      1|        format!(
  224|       |            "❌ Decomposition failed for: \"{task_desc}\"\n\n\
  225|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  226|       |             {error}\n\n\
  227|       |             The decomposition could not create or execute subtasks. Common causes:\n\
  228|       |             - The task_id does not exist or was already completed\n\
  229|       |             - The task description is unclear or too vague to decompose\n\
  230|       |             - Sub-agent execution encountered errors\n\n\
  231|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  232|       |             - Use list_tasks() to verify the task exists and check its current status\n\
  233|       |             - Provide a clearer task description with specific, actionable requirements\n\
  234|       |             - Try decomposing into fewer, simpler subtasks\n\
  235|       |             - If the task cannot be completed, call complete(success=false) with an explanation"
  236|       |        )
  237|       |    }

  240|      7|fn build_spawn_agents_response(
  241|      7|    agent_count: usize,
  242|      7|    agents: &[crate::mcp_server::types::AgentSpec],
  243|      7|    wait: WaitMode,
  244|      7|    response: &ToolResponse,
  245|      7|    task_state: Option<&TaskStateInfo>,
  246|      7|) -> String {
  247|      7|    let roles: Vec<String> = agents
  248|      7|        .iter()
  249|      7|        .map(|a| a.role.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "implementer".to_string()))
                                                                ^5            ^5
  250|      7|        .collect();
  251|       |
  252|      7|    if response.success {
  253|      4|        let files_section = response
  254|      4|            .files_modified
  255|      4|            .as_ref()
  256|      4|            .filter(|f| !f.is_empty())
                                       ^2^2
  257|      4|            .map(|files| {
                                       ^1
  258|      1|                format!(
  259|       |                    "\n\n## Files Modified\n{}",
  260|      1|                    files
  261|      1|                        .iter()
  262|      2|                        .map(|f| format!("- {f}"))
                                       ^1
  263|      1|                        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
  264|      1|                        .join("\n")
  265|       |                )
  266|      1|            })
  267|      4|            .unwrap_or_default();
  268|       |
  269|       |        // Build dynamic "What's Next" section if task state is available
  270|      4|        let next_steps = task_state
  271|      4|            .and_then(TaskStateInfo::format_whats_next)
  272|      4|            .map_or_else(
  273|      3|                || {
  274|      3|                    "## Next Steps\n\
  275|      3|                 If you have more independent tasks, call spawn_agents() for each batch. \
  276|      3|                 When all work is done, call complete(success=true)."
  277|      3|                        .to_string()
  278|      3|                },
  279|      1|                |guidance| format!("## What's Next\n{guidance}"),
  280|       |            );
  281|       |
  282|      4|        format!(
  283|       |            "✅ Spawned {} agent(s) [{:?}] (wait={:?}) completed successfully.\n\n\
  284|       |             ## Summary\n\
  285|       |             {}{}\n\n\
  286|       |             {}",
  287|       |            agent_count, roles, wait, response.summary, files_section, next_steps
  288|       |        )
  289|       |    } else {
  290|      3|        let error = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error");
  291|      3|        let error_lower = error.to_lowercase();
  292|       |
  293|       |        // Detect timeout-specific failures for targeted guidance
  294|      3|        let is_timeout = error_lower.contains("timed out") || error_lower.contains("timeout");
                                                                            ^2
  295|       |
  296|       |        // Detect socket/MCP connection failures - agent may have completed work but failed to signal
  297|      3|        let is_socket_error = error_lower.contains("socket")
  298|      2|            || error_lower.contains("without calling complete")
  299|      2|            || error_lower.contains("mcp socket");
  300|       |
  301|      3|        let how_to_fix = if is_timeout {
  302|      1|            "## How to Fix (Timeout)\n\
  303|      1|             - The agent ran out of time. This often happens with long-running tests or complex tasks.\n\
  304|      1|             - Consider creating smaller, more focused tasks with create_task()\n\
  305|      1|             - For verification tasks, run quick sanity checks instead of full test suites\n\
  306|      1|             - If the task completed meaningful work before timeout, check its notes/results\n\
  307|      1|             - You can retry by spawning another agent for the same task_id"
  308|      2|        } else if is_socket_error {
  309|      1|            "## How to Fix (Socket/Communication Error)\n\
  310|      1|             - The agent likely completed its work but failed to signal completion via MCP socket.\n\
  311|      1|             - Check if the expected files/changes were created - the work may actually be done.\n\
  312|      1|             - Verify the task status with list_tasks() - it may show failure even though work succeeded.\n\
  313|      1|             - Retry the task with spawn_agents() - the retry should succeed quickly if work is already done.\n\
  314|      1|             - If retries keep failing, reduce concurrent agent count to avoid socket resource exhaustion."
  315|       |        } else {
  316|      1|            "## How to Fix\n\
  317|      1|             - Use list_tasks() to verify the task_id exists and is in 'pending' or 'in_progress' state\n\
  318|      1|             - Review the error message for specific issues in agent execution\n\
  319|      1|             - Try breaking the work into smaller pieces with decompose()\n\
  320|      1|             - If the task requirements are unclear, create clearer subtasks with create_task()\n\
  321|      1|             - If unrecoverable, call complete(success=false) with details about what failed"
  322|       |        };
  323|       |
  324|       |        // Add socket-specific cause if detected
  325|      3|        let causes = if is_socket_error {
  326|      1|            "Agent spawning can fail due to:\n\
  327|      1|             - MCP socket connection issues (agent finished work but couldn't signal completion)\n\
  328|      1|             - Invalid task_id reference (task does not exist or is already completed)\n\
  329|      1|             - Resource constraints or timeout"
  330|       |        } else {
  331|      2|            "Agent spawning can fail due to:\n\
  332|      2|             - Invalid task_id reference (task does not exist or is already completed)\n\
  333|      2|             - Agent execution errors (compilation failures, test failures, etc.)\n\
  334|      2|             - Resource constraints or timeout"
  335|       |        };
  336|       |
  337|      3|        format!(
  338|       |            "❌ Spawned {agent_count} agent(s) [{roles:?}] failed.\n\n\
  339|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  340|       |             {error}\n\n\
  341|       |             {causes}\n\n\
  342|       |             {how_to_fix}"
  343|       |        )
  344|       |    }
  345|      7|}

  353|      3|            message.unwrap_or("Work finished")
  354|       |        )
  355|       |    } else {
  356|      2|        format!(
  357|       |            "⚠️ Tasks completed with issues.\n\n\
  358|       |             ## Details\n\

  360|      2|            message.unwrap_or("Some tasks encountered problems")
  361|       |        )

  365|      3|fn build_create_task_response(name: &str, response: &ToolResponse) -> String {
  366|      3|    if response.success {
  367|      1|        format!("✅ Task '{name}' created successfully.")
  368|       |    } else {
  369|      2|        let error = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error");
  370|      2|        format!(
  371|       |            "❌ Failed to create task '{name}': {error}\n\n\
  372|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  373|       |             The task could not be created. Common causes:\n\
  374|       |             - A task with this name may already exist\n\
  375|       |             - No goal has been set (tasks must belong to a goal)\n\
  376|       |             - Invalid task parameters (empty name, invalid parent_id, etc.)\n\n\
  377|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  378|       |             - Use list_tasks() to see existing tasks and avoid duplicate names\n\
  379|       |             - If no goal exists, call set_goal() first to establish context\n\
  380|       |             - Verify the parent_id (if provided) exists using list_tasks()\n\
  381|       |             - Try a different, more descriptive task name"
  382|       |        )
  383|       |    }

  386|      2|fn build_set_goal_response(summary: &str, response: &ToolResponse) -> String {
  387|      2|    if response.success {
  388|      1|        format!("✅ Goal set: {summary}\n\nNow create tasks to achieve this goal.",)
  389|       |    } else {
  390|      1|        let error = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error");
  391|      1|        format!(
  392|       |            "❌ Failed to set goal: {error}\n\n\
  393|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  394|       |             The goal could not be set. Possible reasons:\n\
  395|       |             - A goal may already be active (only one goal at a time)\n\
  396|       |             - The goal summary may be empty or invalid\n\
  397|       |             - Internal state error\n\n\
  398|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  399|       |             - Use list_tasks() to check if a goal already exists\n\
  400|       |             - Ensure the goal summary is non-empty and descriptive\n\
  401|       |             - If a goal exists and you need to change it, complete the current work first with complete()"
  402|       |        )
  403|       |    }

  406|      4|fn build_skip_tasks_response(
  407|      4|    task_ids: &[String],
  408|      4|    reason: Option<&str>,
  409|      4|    response: &ToolResponse,
  410|      4|    task_state: Option<&TaskStateInfo>,
  411|      4|) -> String {
  412|      4|    let task_count = task_ids.len();
  413|      4|    let reason_str = reason.unwrap_or("No reason provided");
  414|      4|    if response.success {
  415|       |        // Build dynamic "What's Next" section if task state is available
  416|      2|        let next_steps = task_state
  417|      2|            .and_then(TaskStateInfo::format_whats_next)
  418|      2|            .map_or_else(
  419|      1|                || {
  420|      1|                    "## Next Steps\n\
  421|      1|                     Continue with remaining tasks or call complete() when done."
  422|      1|                        .to_string()
  423|      1|                },
  424|      1|                |guidance| format!("## What's Next\n{guidance}"),
  425|       |            );
  426|       |
  427|      2|        format!(
  428|       |            "✅ Skipped {task_count} task(s): {task_ids:?}\n\n\
  429|       |             ## Reason\n\
  430|       |             {reason_str}\n\n\
  431|       |             {next_steps}"
  432|       |        )
  433|       |    } else {
  434|      2|        let error = response.error.as_deref().unwrap_or("Unknown error");
  435|      2|        format!(
  436|       |            "❌ Failed to skip tasks: {error}\n\n\
  437|       |             ## Why This Happened\n\
  438|       |             One or more tasks could not be skipped. Common causes:\n\
  439|       |             - Task ID(s) do not exist: {task_ids:?}\n\
  440|       |             - Task(s) are already completed or already skipped\n\
  441|       |             - Task(s) have dependent tasks that would be orphaned\n\n\
  442|       |             ## How to Fix\n\
  443|       |             - Use list_tasks() to verify the task_ids exist and check their current status\n\
  444|       |             - Only pending or in_progress tasks can be skipped\n\
  445|       |             - Ensure you're using the correct task_id format (UUID)\n\
  446|       |             - Reason provided: {reason_str}"
  447|       |        )
  448|       |    }

  458|     19|    }
  459|       |

  462|     14|    }
  463|       |
  464|       |    #[test]
  465|      1|    fn format_whats_next_covers_pending_parallel_blocked_and_complete_states() {
  466|      1|        let cases = [
  467|      1|            (
  468|      1|                "pending with parallel and blocked work",
  469|      1|                TaskStateInfo {
  470|      1|                    pending_count: 3,
  471|      1|                    parallel_tasks: vec!["task004".into(), "task005".into(), "task006".into()],
  472|      1|                    blocked_tasks: vec![(
  473|      1|                        "task007".into(),

                                          ^2

  962|      1|    fn build_report_human_action_response_success_and_failure() {
  963|      1|        let cases = [
  964|      1|            (

  204|      4|        .map(String::from);
  205|       |
  206|      4|    Ok(ToolCall::ReportHumanAction {
  207|      4|        description,
  208|      4|        task_id,
  209|      4|    })

  243|      1|            ),

  264|      1|    fn parse_spawn_agents_validates_agents_payload_and_wait_mode() {
  265|      1|        let cases = [
  266|      1|            (
  267|      1|                "task_id based agent with explicit wait",
  268|      1|                json!({
  269|      1|                    "agents": [
  270|      1|                        { "task_id": "task001", "role": "implementer" }
  271|      1|                    ],
  272|      1|                    "wait": "Any"
  273|      1|                }),
  274|      1|                Ok((
  275|      1|                    1usize,
  276|      1|                    WaitMode::Any,
  277|      1|                    Some("task001"),
  278|      1|                    None,
  279|      1|                    Some("implementer"),
  280|      1|                )),
  281|      1|            ),
  282|      1|            (
  283|      1|                "deprecated task fallback is still accepted",
  284|      1|                json!({
  285|      1|                    "agents": [
  286|      1|                        { "task": "Write focused tests" }
  287|      1|                    ]
  288|      1|                }),
  289|      1|                Ok((
  290|      1|                    1usize,
  291|      1|                    WaitMode::All,
  292|      1|                    None,
  293|      1|                    Some("Write focused tests"),
  294|      1|                    None,
  295|      1|                )),
  296|      1|            ),
  297|      1|            (
  298|      1|                "invalid wait falls back to default",
  299|      1|                json!({
  300|      1|                    "agents": [
  301|      1|                        { "task_id": "task002" }
  302|      1|                    ],
  303|      1|                    "wait": "later"
  304|      1|                }),
  305|      1|                Ok((1usize, WaitMode::All, Some("task002"), None, None)),
  306|      1|            ),
  307|      1|            (
  308|      1|                "missing agents",
  309|      1|                json!({ "wait": "none" }),
  310|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array argument"),
  311|      1|            ),
  312|      1|            (
  313|      1|                "agents must deserialize cleanly",
  314|      1|                json!({
  315|      1|                    "agents": [
  316|      1|                        { "task_id": "task003" },
  317|      1|                        { "task_id": 99, "role": "implementer" }
  318|      1|                    ]
  319|      1|                }),
  320|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  321|      1|            ),
  322|      1|        ];
  323|       |
  324|      5|        for (name, arguments, expected) in cases {
                                                         ^1
  325|      5|            let parsed = parse_spawn_agents(&object(arguments));
  326|      5|            match (parsed, expected) {
  327|       |                (
  328|      3|                    Ok(ToolCall::SpawnAgents { agents, wait }),
  329|       |                    Ok((
  330|      3|                        agent_count,
  331|      3|                        expected_wait,
  332|      3|                        expected_task_id,
  333|      3|                        expected_task,
  334|      3|                        expected_role,
  335|       |                    )),
  336|       |                ) => {
  337|      3|                    assert_eq!(agents.len(), agent_count, "{name}");
  338|      3|                    assert_eq!(wait, expected_wait, "{name}");
  339|      3|                    let first = &agents[0];
  340|      3|                    assert_eq!(first.task_id.as_deref(), expected_task_id, "{name}");
  341|      3|                    assert_eq!(first.task.as_deref(), expected_task, "{name}");
  342|      3|                    assert_eq!(first.role.as_deref(), expected_role, "{name}");
  343|       |                }
  344|      2|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  345|      2|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  346|       |                }
  347|      0|                (other, expected) => {
  348|      0|                    panic!("unexpected result for {name}: got {other:?}, expected {expected:?}");
  349|       |                }
  350|       |            }
  351|       |        }

  355|      1|    fn parse_skip_tasks_requires_non_empty_string_ids() {
  356|      1|        let cases = [
  357|      1|            (
  358|      1|                "valid ids with reason",
  359|      1|                json!({
  360|      1|                    "task_ids": ["task001", "task002"],
  361|      1|                    "reason": "covered by upstream refactor"
  362|      1|                }),
  363|      1|                Ok((
  364|      1|                    vec!["task001", "task002"],
  365|      1|                    Some("covered by upstream refactor"),
  366|      1|                )),
  367|      1|            ),
  368|      1|            (
  369|      1|                "mixed id types keep valid task ids",
  370|      1|                json!({
  371|      1|                    "task_ids": ["task003", 12, true],
  372|      1|                    "reason": "manual follow-up"
  373|      1|                }),
  374|      1|                Ok((vec!["task003"], Some("manual follow-up"))),
  375|      1|            ),
  376|      1|            (
  377|      1|                "empty array is rejected",
  378|      1|                json!({ "task_ids": [] }),
  379|      1|                Err("'task_ids' array must contain at least one task ID"),
  380|      1|            ),
  381|      1|            (
  382|      1|                "non-string ids only are rejected after filtering",
  383|      1|                json!({ "task_ids": [1, 2, 3] }),
  384|      1|                Err("'task_ids' array must contain at least one task ID"),
  385|      1|            ),
  386|      1|            (
  387|      1|                "missing argument is rejected",
  388|      1|                json!({ "reason": "not needed" }),
  389|      1|                Err("requires 'task_ids' array argument"),
  390|      1|            ),
  391|      1|        ];
  392|       |
  393|      5|        for (name, arguments, expected) in cases {
                                                         ^1
  394|      5|            let parsed = parse_skip_tasks(&object(arguments));
  395|      5|            match (parsed, expected) {
  396|       |                (
  397|      2|                    Ok(ToolCall::SkipTasks { task_ids, reason }),
  398|      2|                    Ok((expected_ids, expected_reason)),
  399|       |                ) => {
  400|      2|                    assert_eq!(task_ids, expected_ids, "{name}");
  401|      2|                    assert_eq!(reason.as_deref(), expected_reason, "{name}");
  402|       |                }
  403|      3|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  404|      3|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  405|       |                }
  406|      0|                (other, expected) => {
  407|      0|                    panic!("unexpected result for {name}: got {other:?}, expected {expected:?}");
  408|       |                }
  409|       |            }
  410|       |        }
  411|      1|    }

  414|      1|    fn parse_complete_requires_boolean_success() {
  415|      1|        let cases = [
  416|      1|            (
  417|      1|                "full payload",
  418|      1|                json!({
  419|      1|                    "success": true,
  420|      1|                    "message": "Implemented the feature",
  421|      1|                    "notes": "Left follow-up details",
  422|      1|                    "add_tasks": [
  423|      1|                        {
  424|      1|                            "name": "task_follow_up",
  425|      1|                            "description": "Handle the leftover edge case",
  426|      1|                            "depends_on": ["task001"]
  427|      1|                        }
  428|      1|                    ]
  429|      1|                }),
  430|      1|                Ok((
  431|      1|                    true,
  432|      1|                    Some("Implemented the feature"),
  433|      1|                    Some("Left follow-up details"),
  434|      1|                    1usize,
  435|      1|                )),
  436|      1|            ),
  437|      1|            (
  438|      1|                "missing success",
  439|      1|                json!({ "message": "done" }),
  440|      1|                Err("requires 'success' boolean argument"),
  441|      1|            ),
  442|      1|            (
  443|      1|                "invalid success type",
  444|      1|                json!({ "success": "yes" }),
  445|      1|                Err("requires 'success' boolean argument"),
  446|      1|            ),
  447|      1|        ];
  448|       |
  449|      3|        for (name, arguments, expected) in cases {
                                                         ^1
  450|      3|            let parsed = parse_complete(&object(arguments));
  451|      3|            match (parsed, expected) {
  452|       |                (
  453|       |                    Ok(ToolCall::Complete {
  454|      1|                        success,
  455|      1|                        message,
  456|      1|                        notes,
  457|      1|                        add_tasks,
  458|       |                    }),
  459|      1|                    Ok((expected_success, expected_message, expected_notes, expected_task_count)),
  460|       |                ) => {
  461|      1|                    assert_eq!(success, expected_success, "{name}");
  462|      1|                    assert_eq!(message.as_deref(), expected_message, "{name}");
  463|      1|                    assert_eq!(notes.as_deref(), expected_notes, "{name}");
  464|      1|                    assert_eq!(
  465|      1|                        add_tasks.as_ref().map_or(0, std::vec::Vec::len),
  466|       |                        expected_task_count,
  467|       |                        "{name}"
  468|       |                    );
  469|       |                }
  470|      2|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  471|      2|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  472|       |                }
  473|      0|                (other, expected) => {
  474|      0|                    panic!("unexpected result for {name}: got {other:?}, expected {expected:?}");
  475|       |                }
  476|       |            }
  477|       |        }
  478|      1|    }

  647|      1|            ),

  652|      1|            ),

  654|      1|                "both provided prefers task_id",

  656|      1|                Ok((Some("task001"), Some("inline task"))),
  657|      1|            ),
  658|      1|        ];

  663|      2|                (Ok(ToolCall::Decompose { task_id, task }), Ok((expected_id, expected_task))) => {
  664|      2|                    assert_eq!(task_id.as_deref(), expected_id, "{name}");
  665|      2|                    assert_eq!(task.as_deref(), expected_task, "{name}");
  666|       |                }
  667|      5|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  668|      5|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  669|       |                }
  670|      0|                (other, expected) => {
  671|      0|                    panic!("unexpected result for {name}: got {other:?}, expected {expected:?}");
  672|       |                }

  677|       |    #[test]
  678|      1|    fn parse_spawn_agents_validates_mixed_valid_invalid_specs() {
  679|       |        // Test that parsing fails when any agent has invalid types
  680|      1|        let cases = [
  681|      1|            (
  682|      1|                "first agent valid, second has non-string task_id",
  683|      1|                json!({
  684|      1|                    "agents": [
  685|      1|                        { "task_id": "task001", "role": "implementer" },
  686|      1|                        { "task_id": 42, "role": "verifier" }
  687|      1|                    ]
  688|      1|                }),
  689|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  690|      1|            ),
  691|      1|            (
  692|      1|                "all agents have non-string types",
  693|      1|                json!({
  694|      1|                    "agents": [
  695|      1|                        { "task_id": 1, "role": "implementer" },
  696|      1|                        { "task_id": 2, "role": "verifier" }
  697|      1|                    ]
  698|      1|                }),
  699|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  700|      1|            ),
  701|      1|            (
  702|      1|                "agent with numeric role",
  703|      1|                json!({
  704|      1|                    "agents": [
  705|      1|                        { "task_id": "task001", "role": 42 }
  706|      1|                    ]
  707|      1|                }),
  708|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  709|      1|            ),
  710|      1|            (
  711|      1|                "agents is not an array",
  712|      1|                json!({ "agents": "single-agent" }),
  713|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  714|      1|            ),
  715|      1|            (
  716|      1|                "agents is null",
  717|      1|                json!({ "agents": null }),
  718|      1|                Err("requires 'agents' array of {task_id, role} objects"),
  719|      1|            ),
  720|      1|            (
  721|      1|                "empty agents array is valid",
  722|      1|                json!({ "agents": [] }),
  723|      1|                Ok((0usize, WaitMode::All)),
  724|      1|            ),
  725|      1|            (
  726|      1|                "multiple valid agents with None wait mode",
  727|      1|                json!({
  728|      1|                    "agents": [
  729|      1|                        { "task_id": "task001" },
  730|      1|                        { "task_id": "task002", "role": "verifier" },
  731|      1|                        { "task": "inline task without task_id" }
  732|      1|                    ],
  733|      1|                    "wait": "None"
  734|      1|                }),
  735|      1|                Ok((3usize, WaitMode::None)),
  736|      1|            ),
  737|      1|        ];

  739|      7|        for (name, arguments, expected) in cases {
                                                         ^1
  740|      7|            let parsed = parse_spawn_agents(&object(arguments));
  741|      7|            match (parsed, expected) {
  742|      2|                (Ok(ToolCall::SpawnAgents { agents, wait }), Ok((count, expected_wait))) => {
  743|      2|                    assert_eq!(agents.len(), count, "{name}");
  744|      2|                    assert_eq!(wait, expected_wait, "{name}");
  745|       |                }
  746|      5|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  747|      5|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  748|       |                }
  749|      0|                (other, expected) => {
  750|      0|                    panic!("unexpected result for {name}: got {other:?}, expected {expected:?}");
  751|       |                }
  752|       |            }
  753|       |        }

  873|       |                    );
  874|       |                }

  937|       |                (
  938|       |                    Ok(ToolCall::CreateTask {
  939|      3|                        name,
  940|      3|                        description,
  941|      3|                        dependencies,
  942|       |                    }),
  943|      3|                    Ok((expected_name, expected_desc, expected_deps)),
  944|       |                ) => {
  945|      3|                    assert_eq!(name, expected_name);
  946|      3|                    assert_eq!(description, expected_desc);
  947|      3|                    assert_eq!(dependencies, expected_deps);
  948|       |                }
  949|      5|                (Err(actual), Err(expected_error)) => {
  950|      5|                    assert_eq!(actual, expected_error, "{name}");
  951|       |                }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/mcp_server/handlers/tool_schemas.rs:
    1|       |//! Tool schema definitions for MCP tools.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Contains the JSON schema definitions for each tool exposed by the MCP server.
    4|       |
    5|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
    6|       |
    7|       |/// Generate the complete tool definition for the `set_goal` tool.
    8|      3|pub fn set_goal_schema() -> Value {
    9|      3|    json!({
   10|      3|        "name": "set_goal",
   11|      3|        "description": "<usecase>Define the goal and success criteria before creating tasks.</usecase>\n<instructions>Call this FIRST to establish what success looks like. This helps the orchestrator verify that the work achieves the user's actual goal, not just completes tasks.</instructions>\n<on_error>If the goal is rejected as too vague, add specific acceptance_criteria that describe measurable outcomes. If already set, the goal cannot be changed - proceed with task creation instead.</on_error>",
   12|      3|        "inputSchema": {
   13|      3|            "type": "object",
   14|      3|            "properties": {
   15|      3|                "summary": {
   16|      3|                    "type": "string",
   17|      3|                    "description": "A concise summary of the user's goal (1-2 sentences)"
   18|       |                },
   19|      3|                "acceptance_criteria": {
   20|      3|                    "type": "string",
   21|      3|                    "description": "What must be true for the goal to be considered achieved (success conditions)"
   22|       |                }
   23|       |            },
   24|      3|            "required": ["summary"]
   25|       |        }
   26|       |    })
   27|      3|}
   28|       |
   29|       |/// Generate the planner's version of the `create_task` tool definition.
   30|      3|pub fn create_task_schema_planner() -> Value {
   31|      3|    json!({
   32|      3|        "name": "create_task",
   33|      3|        "description": "<usecase>Add a task to the plan.</usecase>\n<instructions>Call once per task. Each task will be executed by a separate agent.</instructions>\n<on_error>If a dependency task_id is not found, use list_tasks() to see existing task IDs. If the description is rejected as too vague, include specific requirements, files to modify, and expected outcomes. If planning has already completed, you cannot add more tasks - ask the orchestrator to use create_task() instead.</on_error>",
   34|      3|        "inputSchema": {
   35|      3|            "type": "object",
   36|      3|            "properties": {
   37|      3|                "name": {
   38|      3|                    "type": "string",
   39|      3|                    "description": "Short task name (e.g., 'Create user model', 'Add login endpoint')"
   40|       |                },
   41|      3|                "description": {
   42|      3|                    "type": "string",
   43|      3|                    "description": "What the implementer agent should do. Include requirements, decisions and contracts. Avoid implementation details."
   44|       |                },
   45|      3|                "dependencies": {
   46|      3|                    "type": "array",
