/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/mcp_server/server.rs:
    1|       |//! Stdio MCP server for orchestrator tools.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides decompose, implement, and complete tools via JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout.
    4|       |//! Communicates tool calls back to the main app via IPC (Unix sockets or Windows named pipes).
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! The server supports concurrent tool calls: each tool call opens its own IPC
    7|       |//! connection to the app, sends the request, and waits for a response. This allows
    8|       |//! the orchestrator to make multiple parallel tool calls.
    9|       |
   10|       |use super::error::{internal_error, parse_error};
   11|       |use super::handlers::handle_request;
   12|       |use super::socket::send_response;
   13|       |use crate::ipc::IpcAddress;
   14|       |use anyhow::Result;
   15|       |use serde_json::Value;
   16|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   17|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
   18|       |use tokio::sync::Mutex;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Run the MCP server in stdio mode
   21|       |///
   22|       |/// This is called when the binary is invoked with --mcp-server flag.
   23|       |/// It reads JSON-RPC requests from stdin, writes responses to stdout,
   24|       |/// and sends tool calls to the app via IPC.
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// Tool calls are handled concurrently - each spawns a task that opens its own
   27|       |/// IPC connection, sends the request, waits for the response, and sends
   28|       |/// the MCP response back to stdout.
   29|      0|pub async fn run_stdio_server(socket_address: IpcAddress) -> Result<()> {
   30|       |    // Log to stderr which auggie captures
   31|      0|    eprintln!(
   32|       |        "🔌 MCP server PID={} starting with socket address: {socket_address}",
   33|      0|        std::process::id()
   34|       |    );
   35|       |
   36|       |    // Log whether socket endpoint exists at startup (for debugging connection issues)
   37|      0|    if socket_address.exists() {
   38|      0|        eprintln!("✅ Socket endpoint exists at startup: {socket_address}");
   39|      0|    } else {
   40|      0|        eprintln!(
   41|      0|            "⚠️  Socket endpoint does NOT exist at startup: {socket_address}. \
   42|      0|            Will retry connections when making tool calls."
   43|      0|        );
   44|      0|    }
   45|       |
   46|      0|    let stdin = tokio::io::stdin();
   47|      0|    let stdout = Arc::new(Mutex::new(tokio::io::stdout()));
   48|      0|    let reader = BufReader::new(stdin);
   49|      0|    let mut lines = reader.lines();
   50|      0|    let socket_address = Arc::new(socket_address);
   51|       |
   52|      0|    eprintln!("✅ MCP server started (waiting for JSON-RPC messages)");
   53|       |
   54|       |    loop {
   55|       |        // Read the next line, handling stdin errors gracefully
   56|      0|        let line = match lines.next_line().await {
   57|      0|            Ok(Some(line)) => line,
   58|       |            Ok(None) => {
   59|       |                // EOF reached, clean shutdown
   60|      0|                eprintln!("🔌 MCP server shutting down (stdin closed)");
   61|      0|                break;
   62|       |            }
   63|      0|            Err(e) => {
   64|      0|                tracing::error!("❌ Error reading from stdin: {}. Shutting down.", e);
   65|      0|                return Err(e.into());
   66|       |            }
   67|       |        };
   68|       |
   69|      0|        eprintln!("📥 MCP server received: {line}");
   70|       |
   71|       |        // Parse the JSON request, sending error response on failure
   72|      0|        let request: Value = match serde_json::from_str(&line) {
   73|      0|            Ok(req) => req,
   74|      0|            Err(e) => {
   75|      0|                let input_preview = if line.len() > 200 {
   76|      0|                    &line[..200]
   77|       |                } else {
   78|      0|                    &line
   79|       |                };
   80|      0|                tracing::warn!(
   81|       |                    "⚠️  Failed to parse JSON-RPC request: {}. Input preview: {}",
   82|       |                    e,
   83|       |                    input_preview
   84|       |                );
   85|      0|                let error_response = parse_error(&e.to_string(), Some(input_preview));
   86|      0|                let mut stdout = stdout.lock().await;
   87|      0|                if let Err(send_err) = send_response(&mut stdout, &error_response).await {
   88|      0|                    tracing::error!("❌ Failed to send parse error response: {}", send_err);
   89|      0|                }
   90|      0|                continue;
   91|       |            }
   92|       |        };
   93|       |
   94|       |        // Spawn a task to handle the request concurrently
   95|       |        // This allows multiple tool calls to be processed in parallel
   96|      0|        let stdout_clone = Arc::clone(&stdout);
   97|      0|        let socket_address_clone = Arc::clone(&socket_address);
   98|      0|        tokio::spawn(async move {
   99|       |            // Handle the request, catching any errors
  100|      0|            let response = match handle_request(&request, &socket_address_clone).await {
  101|      0|                Ok(resp) => resp,
  102|      0|                Err(e) => {
  103|      0|                    let id = request.get("id");
  104|      0|                    let method = request
  105|      0|                        .get("method")
  106|      0|                        .and_then(|m| m.as_str())
  107|      0|                        .unwrap_or("unknown");
  108|      0|                    tracing::error!("❌ Error handling request: {}. Method: {}", e, method);
  109|      0|                    Some(internal_error(
  110|      0|                        id,
  111|      0|                        &format!("handling {method} request"),
  112|      0|                        &e.to_string(),
  113|      0|                    ))
  114|       |                }
  115|       |            };
  116|       |
  117|       |            // Send the response if there is one
  118|      0|            if let Some(resp) = response {
  119|      0|                let mut stdout = stdout_clone.lock().await;
  120|      0|                if let Err(e) = send_response(&mut stdout, &resp).await {
  121|      0|                    tracing::error!("❌ Failed to send response: {}. Continuing...", e);
  122|      0|                }
  123|      0|            }
  124|      0|        });
  125|       |    }
  126|       |
  127|      0|    Ok(())
  128|      0|}
  129|       |
  130|       |#[cfg(test)]
  131|       |mod tests {
  132|       |    use super::*;
  133|       |    use crate::mcp_server::error::{INVALID_PARAMS, INVALID_REQUEST, METHOD_NOT_FOUND};
  134|       |    use crate::mcp_server::handlers::handle_request;
  135|       |    use crate::mcp_server::types::ToolCall;
  136|       |    use serde_json::json;
  137|       |
  138|       |    // ========================================================================
  139|       |    // ToolCall Serialization Tests
  140|       |    // ========================================================================
  141|       |
  142|       |    #[test]
  143|      1|    fn test_tool_call_decompose_serialization() {
  144|      1|        let tool_call = ToolCall::Decompose {
  145|      1|            task_id: None,
  146|      1|            task: Some("Build a feature".to_string()),
  147|      1|        };
  148|      1|        let json = serde_json::to_string(&tool_call).unwrap();
  149|      1|        assert!(json.contains("Decompose"));
  150|      1|        assert!(json.contains("Build a feature"));
  151|      1|    }
  152|       |
  153|       |    #[test]
  154|      1|    fn test_tool_call_spawn_agents_serialization() {
  155|      1|        let tool_call = ToolCall::SpawnAgents {
  156|      1|            agents: vec![super::super::types::AgentSpec {
  157|      1|                role: Some("implementer".to_string()),
  158|      1|                task: Some("Fix the bug".to_string()),
  159|      1|                task_id: None,

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/mcp_server/socket.rs:
    1|       |//! IPC socket communication utilities for the MCP server.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides cross-platform IPC communication for MCP tool calls,
    4|       |//! using the IPC abstraction layer for Unix sockets and Windows named pipes.
    5|       |
    6|       |use super::types::{ToolRequest, ToolResponse};
    7|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
    8|       |use serde_json::Value;
    9|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
   10|       |
   11|       |use crate::ipc::{connect_with_retry, IpcAddress};
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Send a response to stdout, handling errors gracefully
   14|      0|pub async fn send_response(stdout: &mut tokio::io::Stdout, response: &Value) -> Result<()> {
   15|      0|    let resp_str = serde_json::to_string(response)?;
   16|      0|    eprintln!("📤 MCP server sending: {resp_str}");
   17|      0|    stdout.write_all(resp_str.as_bytes()).await?;
   18|      0|    stdout.write_all(b"\n").await?;
   19|      0|    stdout.flush().await?;
   20|      0|    Ok(())
   21|      0|}
   22|       |
   23|       |/// Send a tool request to the app and wait for the response.
   24|       |///
   25|       |/// This opens a new IPC connection for each request to allow concurrent tool calls.
   26|       |/// Uses Unix sockets on macOS/Linux and named pipes on Windows.
   27|       |/// The app will process the request and send back a `ToolResponse`.
   28|      0|pub async fn send_request_and_wait(
   29|      0|    socket_address: &IpcAddress,
   30|      0|    request: &ToolRequest,
   31|      0|) -> Result<ToolResponse> {
   32|       |    // Connect to app socket using the IPC abstraction layer
   33|      0|    let stream = connect_with_retry(socket_address).await?;
   34|      0|    let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
   35|       |
   36|       |    // Send the request
   37|      0|    let request_json = serde_json::to_string(request)?;
   38|      0|    eprintln!("📨 Sending to app: {request_json}");
   39|      0|    writer.write_all(request_json.as_bytes()).await?;
   40|      0|    writer.write_all(b"\n").await?;
   41|      0|    writer.flush().await?;
   42|       |
   43|       |    // Wait for response
   44|      0|    let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
   45|      0|    let mut response_line = String::new();
   46|      0|    let bytes_read = reader
   47|      0|        .read_line(&mut response_line)
   48|      0|        .await

    6|       |/// Model complexity hint for auto mode.
    7|       |///
    8|       |/// When the model is set to "auto", this tells the system what level of capability
    9|       |/// is needed for the task. The system will select an appropriate model based on
   10|       |/// this complexity level.
   11|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
   12|       |#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
   13|       |pub enum ModelComplexity {
   14|       |    /// Simple tasks: straightforward edits, small changes, well-defined work.
   15|       |    /// Maps to faster, more cost-effective models (e.g., Haiku).
   16|       |    Simple,
   17|       |    /// Medium complexity: typical development tasks, moderate reasoning required.
   18|       |    /// Maps to balanced models (e.g., Sonnet).
   19|       |    #[default]
   20|       |    Medium,
   21|       |    /// Complex tasks: architectural decisions, complex refactoring, nuanced judgment.
   22|       |    /// Maps to the most capable models (e.g., Opus).
   23|       |    Complex,
   24|       |}
   25|       |
   26|       |impl ModelComplexity {
   27|       |    /// Returns the string representation of this complexity level.
   28|      6|    pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   29|      6|        match self {
   30|      2|            Self::Simple => "simple",
   31|      2|            Self::Medium => "medium",
   32|      2|            Self::Complex => "complex",
   33|       |        }
   34|      6|    }
   35|       |}
   36|       |
   37|       |impl std::fmt::Display for ModelComplexity {
   38|      3|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   39|      3|        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
   40|      3|    }

   42|       |
   43|       |/// Specification for an agent to be spawned.
   44|       |///
   45|       |/// Used by the orchestrator to describe worker agents when making
   46|       |/// `spawn_agents` tool calls.
   47|       |///
   48|       |/// There are two ways to specify an agent:
   49|       |/// 1. **By `task_id`**: Just provide `task_id` (e.g., "task001"). The task description
   50|       |///    is looked up from `TaskManager`, and role defaults to "implementer".
   51|       |/// 2. **Explicitly**: Provide `role` and `task` directly. Use this for custom agents
   52|       |///    or when not using the task tracking system.
   53|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
   54|       |pub struct AgentSpec {
   55|       |    /// The role of the agent (e.g., "implementer", "verifier", "explorer", "custom").
   56|       |    /// Defaults to "implementer" when `task_id` is provided.
   57|       |    #[serde(default)]
   58|       |    pub role: Option<String>,
   59|       |    /// The task to be performed by this agent.
   60|       |    /// Optional when `task_id` is provided (looked up from `TaskManager`).
   61|       |    #[serde(default)]
   62|       |    pub task: Option<String>,
   63|       |    /// Task ID linking this agent to a tracked task (e.g., "task001").
   64|       |    /// When provided:
   65|       |    /// - Task description is looked up automatically
   66|       |    /// - Role defaults to "implementer"
   67|       |    /// - Status is auto-updated: `InProgress` → Complete/Failed
   68|       |    #[serde(default)]
   69|       |    pub task_id: Option<String>,
   70|       |    /// Custom prompt (required for role="custom", optional for others)
   71|       |    #[serde(default)]
   72|       |    pub prompt: Option<String>,
   73|       |    /// Explicit tool whitelist (required for role="custom")
   74|       |    #[serde(default)]
   75|       |    pub tools: Option<Vec<String>>,
   76|       |    /// Model complexity hint for auto mode.
   77|       |    /// Required when the implementer model is set to "auto".
   78|       |    /// Tells the system what capability level is needed for this task.
   79|       |    #[serde(default)]
   80|       |    pub model_complexity: Option<ModelComplexity>,
   81|       |}
   82|       |
   83|       |/// A fully resolved agent specification with all required fields populated.
   84|       |///
   85|       |/// Created from an [`AgentSpec`] by resolving `task_id` lookups and applying defaults.
   86|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   87|       |pub struct ResolvedAgentSpec {
   88|       |    /// The role of the agent (resolved from spec or defaulted to "implementer")
   89|       |    pub role: String,
   90|       |    /// The task description (resolved from `TaskManager` if `task_id` was provided)
   91|       |    pub task: String,
   92|       |    /// The task ID if this agent is linked to a tracked task
   93|       |    pub task_id: Option<String>,
   94|       |    /// Custom prompt (for role="custom")
   95|       |    pub prompt: Option<String>,
   96|       |    /// Explicit tool whitelist (for role="custom")
   97|       |    pub tools: Option<Vec<String>>,
   98|       |    /// Model complexity hint for auto mode.
   99|       |    /// Used to select an appropriate model when `implementer_model` is set to "auto".
  100|       |    pub model_complexity: Option<ModelComplexity>,
  101|       |}
  102|       |
  103|       |impl AgentSpec {
  104|       |    /// Resolve this spec into a fully-populated `ResolvedAgentSpec`.
  105|       |    ///
  106|       |    /// If `task_id` is provided, looks up the task description from the provided lookup function.
  107|       |    /// Returns an error if:
  108|       |    /// - `task_id` is provided but the task is not found
  109|       |    /// - Neither `task_id` nor task is provided
  110|      3|    pub fn resolve<F>(&self, task_lookup: F) -> Result<ResolvedAgentSpec, String>
  111|      3|    where
  112|      3|        F: Fn(&str) -> Option<String>,
  113|       |    {
  114|       |        // Resolve task: either from task_id lookup or explicit task field
  115|      3|        let task = if let Some(ref tid) = self.task_id {
                                             ^1
  116|      1|            task_lookup(tid).ok_or_else(|| format!("Task '{tid}' not found"))?
                                                         ^0                                ^0
  117|      2|        } else if let Some(ref t) = self.task {
  118|      2|            t.clone()
  119|       |        } else {
  120|      0|            return Err("Either 'task_id' or 'task' must be provided".to_string());
  121|       |        };
  122|       |
  123|       |        // Resolve role: explicit or default to "implementer"
  124|      3|        let role = self
  125|      3|            .role
  126|      3|            .clone()
  127|      3|            .unwrap_or_else(|| "implementer".to_string());
                                             ^0            ^0
  128|       |
  129|      3|        Ok(ResolvedAgentSpec {
  130|      3|            role,
  131|      3|            task,
  132|      3|            task_id: self.task_id.clone(),
  133|      3|            prompt: self.prompt.clone(),
  134|      3|            tools: self.tools.clone(),
  135|      3|            model_complexity: self.model_complexity,
  136|      3|        })
  137|      3|    }

  140|       |/// Wait mode for spawned agents.
  141|       |///
  142|       |/// Controls how the orchestrator waits for spawned agents to complete.
  143|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
  144|       |pub enum WaitMode {
  145|       |    /// Wait for all agents to complete before continuing
  146|       |    #[default]
  147|       |    All,
  148|       |    /// Wait for any one agent to complete before continuing
  149|       |    Any,
  150|       |    /// Don't wait; fire and forget
  151|       |    None,

  165|       |/// Task state information for context-aware response building.
  166|       |///
  167|       |/// This struct captures the current task state snapshot that response builders
  168|       |/// use to generate dynamic "What's Next" guidance. It's created from `TaskManager`
  169|       |/// helper methods and passed through the response chain.
  170|       |///
  171|       |/// # Example
  172|       |///
  173|       |/// ```ignore
  174|       |/// let state = TaskStateInfo {
  175|       |///     pending_count: 3,
  176|       |///     parallel_tasks: vec!["task004".into(), "task005".into()],
  177|       |///     blocked_tasks: vec![("task006".into(), vec!["task005".into()])],
  178|       |/// };
  179|       |/// ```

  181|       |pub struct TaskStateInfo {

  184|       |    /// Task IDs that can be executed now (no unmet dependencies).
  185|       |    pub parallel_tasks: Vec<String>,
  186|       |    /// Blocked tasks as (`task_id`, `blocking_task_ids`) pairs.
  187|       |    pub blocked_tasks: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,
  188|       |}
  189|       |
  190|       |/// Response sent from the app back to the MCP server via IPC.
  191|       |///
  192|       |/// Contains the result of executing a tool call, correlated by request ID.
  193|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
  194|       |pub struct ToolResponse {

  196|       |    pub request_id: String,
  197|       |    /// Whether the operation succeeded
  198|       |    pub success: bool,

  200|       |    pub summary: String,

  202|       |    pub files_modified: Option<Vec<String>>,
  203|       |    /// Optional error message if the operation failed

  205|       |    /// Optional task state for context-aware responses.
  206|       |    /// Contains information about pending, parallel-ready, and blocked tasks.
  207|       |    /// Used by response builders to provide actionable "What's Next" guidance.
  208|       |    #[serde(default)]
  209|       |    pub task_state: Option<TaskStateInfo>,
  210|       |}

  265|       |/// A task suggested by an agent during completion.
  266|       |///
  267|       |/// Agents can suggest tasks they discovered were needed but are outside their scope.
  268|       |/// These are added to the `TaskManager` and the orchestrator can act on them.
  269|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
  270|       |pub struct SuggestedTask {
  271|       |    /// The name of the suggested task.
  272|       |    pub name: String,
  273|       |    /// What needs to be done.
  274|       |    pub description: String,
  275|       |    /// Optional dependencies (by task name or ID).
  276|       |    #[serde(default)]
  277|       |    pub depends_on: Option<Vec<String>>,
  278|       |}
  279|       |
  280|       |/// Tool call from an agent.
  281|       |///
  282|       |/// Represents the different operations that can be requested by agents
  283|       |/// via the MCP protocol. These calls are sent from the MCP server to the main
  284|       |/// application via IPC (Unix sockets on macOS/Linux, named pipes on Windows).
  285|       |///
  286|       |/// # Variants
  287|       |///
  288|       |/// - `Decompose` - Break down a task into smaller subtasks (orchestrator only)
  289|       |/// - `SpawnAgents` - Spawn one or more worker agents (orchestrator only)
  290|       |/// - `Complete` - Signal that the agent has finished processing
  291|       |/// - `CreateTask` - Create a task in the plan (planner and orchestrator)
  292|       |/// - `SetGoal` - Set the goal summary (planner only)
  293|       |/// - `SkipTasks` - Mark tasks as skipped/not needed (orchestrator only)
  294|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
  295|       |pub enum ToolCall {
  296|       |    /// Request to decompose a task into subtasks.
  297|       |    /// Either `task_id` or `task` must be provided.
  298|       |    Decompose {
  299|       |        /// Task ID to decompose (e.g., "task001"). Looked up from `TaskManager`.
  300|       |        #[serde(default)]
  301|       |        task_id: Option<String>,
  302|       |        /// Explicit task description to decompose.
  303|       |        #[serde(default)]
  304|       |        task: Option<String>,
  305|       |    },
  306|       |    /// Request to spawn one or more worker agents.
  307|       |    SpawnAgents {
  308|       |        /// The agents to spawn with their roles and tasks.
  309|       |        agents: Vec<AgentSpec>,
  310|       |        /// How to wait for the spawned agents.
  311|       |        #[serde(default)]
  312|       |        wait: WaitMode,
  313|       |    },
  314|       |    /// Signal completion of an agent's work.
  315|       |    Complete {
  316|       |        /// Whether the work was successful.
  317|       |        success: bool,
  318|       |        /// Optional message providing details about the completion.
  319|       |        message: Option<String>,
  320|       |        /// Optional notes for future agents/orchestrator (insights, decisions, warnings).
  321|       |        /// Use this to leave context that will help other agents or inform the orchestrator.
  322|       |        #[serde(default)]
  323|       |        notes: Option<String>,
  324|       |        /// Optional tasks to add to the plan.
  325|       |        /// Use this for work you discovered was needed but is outside your current scope.
  326|       |        #[serde(default)]
  327|       |        add_tasks: Option<Vec<SuggestedTask>>,
  328|       |    },
  329|       |    /// Create a task (used by planner agents).
  330|       |    /// This creates a task for the orchestrator to track and execute.
  331|       |    CreateTask {
  332|       |        /// The name of the task.
  333|       |        name: String,
  334|       |        /// The description of the task.
  335|       |        description: String,
  336|       |        /// Names of tasks that this task depends on.
  337|       |        dependencies: Vec<String>,
  338|       |    },
  339|       |    /// Set the goal summary (used by planner agents).
  340|       |    /// Called first to define what success looks like before creating tasks.
  341|       |    SetGoal {
  342|       |        /// A concise summary of the user's goal.
  343|       |        summary: String,
  344|       |        /// Success criteria / acceptance conditions.
  345|       |        #[serde(default)]
  346|       |        acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,
  347|       |    },
  348|       |    /// Skip one or more tasks (used by orchestrator agents).
  349|       |    /// Marks tasks as not needed rather than leaving them pending.
  350|       |    SkipTasks {
  351|       |        /// IDs of tasks to skip (e.g., `["task001", "task002"]`).
  352|       |        task_ids: Vec<String>,
  353|       |        /// Optional explanation for why tasks are being skipped.
  354|       |        #[serde(default)]
  355|       |        reason: Option<String>,
  356|       |    },
  357|       |    /// List tasks with their current status (used by orchestrator agents).
  358|       |    /// Useful for checking progress and finding tasks that need attention.
  359|       |    ListTasks {
  360|       |        /// Optional status filter (e.g., `pending`, `in_progress`, `all`).
  361|       |        #[serde(default)]
  362|       |        status_filter: Option<String>,
  363|       |    },
  364|       |    /// Report that something requires human intervention (used by orchestrator agents).
  365|       |    /// Use this when a task cannot be fully automated and needs manual user action.
  366|       |    ReportHumanAction {
  367|       |        /// Clear description of what the user needs to do.
  368|       |        description: String,
  369|       |        /// Optional task ID this action relates to.
  370|       |        #[serde(default)]
  371|       |        task_id: Option<String>,
  372|       |    },
  373|       |}
  374|       |
  375|       |impl ToolCall {
  376|       |    /// Returns the type of tool call as a string.
  377|      8|    pub const fn tool_type(&self) -> &'static str {
  378|      8|        match self {
  379|      0|            Self::Decompose { .. } => "decompose",
  380|      1|            Self::SpawnAgents { .. } => "spawn_agents",
  381|      3|            Self::Complete { .. } => "complete",
  382|      1|            Self::CreateTask { .. } => "create_task",
  383|      0|            Self::SetGoal { .. } => "set_goal",
  384|      3|            Self::SkipTasks { .. } => "skip_tasks",
  385|      0|            Self::ListTasks { .. } => "list_tasks",
  386|      0|            Self::ReportHumanAction { .. } => "report_human_action",
  387|       |        }
  388|      8|    }
  389|       |}

    1|       |//! Metrics collection for monitoring agent performance and system health.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides metrics collection using the `metrics` crate. Metrics are only
    4|       |//! recorded when the `metrics` feature is enabled and `init_metrics()` has been called.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Available Metrics
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! ## Counters
    9|       |//! - `paperboat_agents_spawned_total` - Total agents spawned (labels: `agent_type`)
   10|       |//! - `paperboat_tasks_total` - Total task status transitions (labels: `status`)
   11|       |//! - `paperboat_tool_calls_total` - Total tool calls made (labels: `tool_name`)
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! ## Histograms
   14|       |//! - `paperboat_agent_duration_seconds` - Agent execution duration (labels: `agent_type`, `success`)
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! # Usage
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! Metrics are only recorded when both conditions are met:
   19|       |//! 1. The crate is compiled with the `metrics` feature
   20|       |//! 2. `init_metrics()` is called at startup (e.g., when `--metrics` flag is passed)
   21|       |//!
   22|       |//! If either condition is not met, all metric operations are no-ops with zero overhead.
   23|       |
   24|       |/// Whether metrics collection is currently enabled.
   25|       |/// This is controlled by calling `init_metrics()` at startup.
   26|       |#[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   27|       |static METRICS_ENABLED: std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool = std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool::new(false);
   28|       |
   29|       |/// Initialize the metrics system.
   30|       |///
   31|       |/// This sets up the metrics recorder. Must be called before any metrics will be recorded.
   32|       |/// If `enable_prometheus` is true, starts a Prometheus HTTP exporter on the specified port.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Arguments
   35|       |/// * `enable_prometheus` - Whether to start the Prometheus HTTP exporter
   36|       |/// * `prometheus_port` - Port for the Prometheus exporter (default: 9090)
   37|       |#[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   38|      0|pub fn init_metrics(enable_prometheus: bool, prometheus_port: u16) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   39|       |    use metrics_exporter_prometheus::PrometheusBuilder;
   40|       |
   41|      0|    if enable_prometheus {
   42|      0|        let addr = std::net::SocketAddr::new(
   43|      0|            std::net::IpAddr::V4(std::net::Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST),
   44|      0|            prometheus_port,
   45|       |        );
   46|       |
   47|      0|        PrometheusBuilder::new()
   48|      0|            .with_http_listener(addr)
   49|      0|            .install()
   50|      0|            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to install Prometheus exporter: {e}"))?;
   51|       |
   52|      0|        tracing::info!("📊 Prometheus metrics exporter started on http://{addr}");
   53|       |    } else {
   54|       |        // Install a no-op recorder that still allows metrics to be recorded
   55|       |        // (they just won't be exported anywhere)
   56|      0|        PrometheusBuilder::new()
   57|      0|            .install()
   58|      0|            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to install metrics recorder: {e}"))?;
   59|       |
   60|      0|        tracing::info!("📊 Metrics collection enabled (no exporter)");
   61|       |    }
   62|       |
   63|      0|    METRICS_ENABLED.store(true, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Release);
   64|      0|    describe_metrics();
   65|      0|    Ok(())
   66|      0|}
   67|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/models.rs:
    1|       |//! Model configuration and discovery for Villalobos
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides types for managing AI model configuration using
    4|       |//! tier-based model selection with fallback chains.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Model Tiers
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Instead of specific model versions (e.g., "sonnet4.5"), this module uses
    9|       |//! model tiers (e.g., "sonnet") that each backend resolves to the best
   10|       |//! available version.
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! # Effort Levels
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! Some backends (like Cursor) support effort/thinking levels that affect
   15|       |//! model behavior. Use [`EffortLevel`] to request higher quality responses
   16|       |//! at the cost of latency/tokens.
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! # Fallback Chains
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! Model configuration supports fallback chains like CSS font-family:
   21|       |//! ```toml
   22|       |//! orchestrator_model = ["gemini", "codex", "opus"]
   23|       |//! ```
   24|       |//! The system picks the first tier available in the current backend.
   25|       |
   26|       |use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
   27|       |use regex::Regex;
   28|       |use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   29|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
   30|       |use std::str::FromStr;
   31|       |
   32|       |// ============================================================================
   33|       |// Effort Level
   34|       |// ============================================================================
   35|       |
   36|       |/// Effort/thinking level for model inference.
   37|       |///
   38|       |/// Some backends support different effort levels that affect model quality
   39|       |/// and latency. Higher effort typically means more "thinking" time and
   40|       |/// better responses, but at the cost of increased latency and tokens.
   41|       |///
   42|       |/// # Backend Support
   43|       |///
   44|       |/// - **Cursor**: Maps to model suffixes like `-low`, `-high`, `-thinking`
   45|       |/// - **Auggie**: Ignored (no effort level support)
   46|       |///
   47|       |/// # Example
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// ```toml
   50|       |/// # .paperboat/agents/planner.toml
   51|       |/// effort = "high"
   52|       |/// model = "openai, opus, gemini, composer"
   53|       |/// ```
   54|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   55|       |#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
   56|       |pub enum EffortLevel {
   57|       |    /// Low effort - fastest, minimal thinking
   58|       |    Low,
   59|       |    /// Medium effort - balanced (default)
   60|       |    #[default]
   61|       |    Medium,
   62|       |    /// High effort - more thinking, better quality
   63|       |    High,
   64|       |    /// Extra high effort - maximum thinking/reasoning
   65|       |    #[serde(rename = "xhigh")]
   66|       |    XHigh,
   67|       |}
   68|       |
   69|       |impl EffortLevel {
   70|       |    /// Returns the string identifier for this effort level.
   71|      8|    pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   72|      8|        match self {
   73|      2|            Self::Low => "low",
   74|      2|            Self::Medium => "medium",
   75|      2|            Self::High => "high",
   76|      2|            Self::XHigh => "xhigh",
   77|       |        }
   78|      8|    }
   79|       |
   80|       |    /// Returns all known effort levels.
   81|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   82|      0|    pub const fn all() -> &'static [Self] {
   83|      0|        &[Self::Low, Self::Medium, Self::High, Self::XHigh]
   84|      0|    }
   85|       |}
   86|       |
   87|       |impl FromStr for EffortLevel {
   88|       |    type Err = anyhow::Error;
   89|       |
   90|     14|    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self> {
   91|     14|        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
   92|     14|            "low" => Ok(Self::Low),
                                   ^2
   93|     12|            "medium" | "med" => Ok(Self::Medium),
                                     ^11      ^2
   94|     10|            "high" => Ok(Self::High),
                                    ^2
   95|      8|            "xhigh" | "x-high" | "extra-high" | "max" => Ok(Self::XHigh),
                                    ^6         ^5             ^4       ^5
   96|      3|            _ => Err(anyhow!("Unknown effort level: {s}")),
   97|       |        }
   98|     14|    }
   99|       |}
  100|       |
  101|       |impl std::fmt::Display for EffortLevel {
  102|      4|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
  103|      4|        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
  104|      4|    }
  105|       |}
  106|       |
  107|       |// ============================================================================
  108|       |// Model Tiers
  109|       |// ============================================================================
  110|       |
  111|       |/// Model tiers representing capability classes across backends.
  112|       |///
  113|       |/// Each tier maps to the best available model version for that tier
  114|       |/// in each backend. For example, `Sonnet` maps to:
  115|       |/// - Auggie: `sonnet4.5` (or latest)
  116|       |/// - Cursor: `sonnet-4.6` (or latest)
  117|       |///
  118|       |/// # Meta-Tiers
  119|       |///
  120|       |/// Some tiers are "meta-tiers" that expand to multiple concrete tiers:
  121|       |/// - `OpenAI`: Expands to `[Gpt, Codex]` - all OpenAI models
  122|       |///
  123|       |/// # Effort Levels
  124|       |///
  125|       |/// When combined with [`EffortLevel`], backends may select different model
  126|       |/// variants. For example, Cursor with `Gpt` + `High` effort resolves to
  127|       |/// `gpt-5.4-high`.
  128|       |///
  129|       |/// Tiers are ordered roughly by capability (Opus > Sonnet > Haiku).
  130|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
  131|       |#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
  132|       |pub enum ModelTier {
  133|       |    /// Auto mode: system chooses based on task complexity
  134|       |    Auto,
  135|       |    /// Anthropic Opus - most capable, best for complex reasoning
  136|       |    Opus,
  137|       |    /// Anthropic Sonnet - balanced capability and speed
  138|       |    #[default]
  139|       |    Sonnet,
  140|       |    /// Anthropic Haiku - fast and cheap (Auggie only)
  141|       |    Haiku,
  142|       |    /// OpenAI GPT - general purpose model (meta-tier for all GPT models)
  143|       |    Gpt,
  144|       |    /// OpenAI models - meta-tier that expands to [Gpt, Codex]
  145|       |    #[serde(rename = "openai")]
  146|       |    OpenAI,
  147|       |    /// GPT Codex - coding-optimized model
  148|       |    Codex,
  149|       |    /// GPT Codex Mini - cheaper coding model
  150|       |    CodexMini,
  151|       |    /// Google Gemini Pro
  152|       |    Gemini,
  153|       |    /// Google Gemini Flash - faster/cheaper
  154|       |    GeminiFlash,
  155|       |    /// Grok by xAI
  156|       |    Grok,
  157|       |    /// Cursor Composer - Cursor's custom model
  158|       |    Composer,
  159|       |}

  273|       |/// A fallback chain of model tiers.
  274|       |///
  275|       |/// When selecting a model, the system tries each tier in order and
  276|       |/// uses the first one available in the current backend.
  277|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  278|       |#[serde(transparent)]
  279|       |pub struct ModelFallbackChain(pub Vec<ModelTier>);

  285|    566|        Self(tiers)
  286|    566|    }

  290|     21|        Self(vec![tier])

  293|       |    /// Resolve this fallback chain to a concrete tier using available tiers.
  294|       |    ///
  295|       |    /// Returns the first tier in the chain that is available in the given set.
  296|       |    /// Returns an error if no tier in the chain is available.
  297|    167|    pub fn resolve(&self, available: &HashSet<ModelTier>) -> Result<ModelTier> {

                                  ^167

  307|      2|        ))
  308|    167|    }

  310|       |    /// Returns `true` if the chain contains only `Auto`.

  312|      0|    pub fn is_auto(&self) -> bool {
  313|      0|        self.0.len() == 1 && self.0[0].is_auto()

  339|       |    /// Parse a fallback chain from a string.

  342|       |    /// - Single tier: "sonnet"
  343|       |    /// - Comma-separated list: "gemini, codex, opus"

                           ^10
  348|     10|            .collect();
  349|     10|        Ok(Self(tiers?))
                                   ^1
  350|     10|    }

  352|       |
  353|       |/// Configuration for which models to use for different roles.
  354|       |///
  355|       |/// Each role has a fallback chain of model tiers. At runtime, the system
  356|       |/// resolves each chain to a concrete model using the backend's available tiers.
  357|       |///
  358|       |/// # Effort Levels
  359|       |///
  360|       |/// Each agent role can have its own effort level. When the backend supports
  361|       |/// effort levels (e.g., Cursor), this affects the model variant selected.
  362|       |/// For example, `Opus` with `High` effort might resolve to `opus-4.6-thinking`.
  363|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  364|       |pub struct ModelConfig {
  365|       |    /// Available model tiers from the backend
  366|       |    pub available_tiers: HashSet<ModelTier>,
  367|       |    /// Model fallback chain for orchestration (default: opus, sonnet)
  368|       |    pub orchestrator_model: ModelFallbackChain,
  369|       |    /// Effort level for orchestrator (default: medium)
  370|       |    pub orchestrator_effort: EffortLevel,
  371|       |    /// Model fallback chain for planning (default: sonnet, opus)
  372|       |    pub planner_model: ModelFallbackChain,
  373|       |    /// Effort level for planner (default: medium)
  374|       |    pub planner_effort: EffortLevel,
  375|       |    /// Model fallback chain for implementation (default: sonnet, codex)
  376|       |    pub implementer_model: ModelFallbackChain,
  377|       |    /// Effort level for implementer (default: medium)
  378|       |    pub implementer_effort: EffortLevel,
  379|       |}
  380|       |
  381|       |/// Default fallback chains for different environments.
  382|       |pub mod defaults {
  383|       |    use super::{ModelFallbackChain, ModelTier};
  384|       |
  385|       |    /// Default orchestrator chain: prefer most capable models
  386|    187|    pub fn orchestrator() -> ModelFallbackChain {
  387|    187|        ModelFallbackChain::new(vec![ModelTier::Opus, ModelTier::Sonnet, ModelTier::Codex])
  388|    187|    }
  389|       |
  390|       |    /// Default planner chain: balanced capability
  391|    187|    pub fn planner() -> ModelFallbackChain {
  392|    187|        ModelFallbackChain::new(vec![ModelTier::Sonnet, ModelTier::Opus, ModelTier::Codex])
  393|    187|    }
  394|       |
  395|       |    /// Default implementer chain: coding-optimized
  396|    187|    pub fn implementer() -> ModelFallbackChain {
  397|    187|        ModelFallbackChain::new(vec![ModelTier::Sonnet, ModelTier::Codex, ModelTier::Opus])
  398|    187|    }
  399|       |}
  400|       |
  401|       |impl Default for ModelConfig {
  402|    131|    fn default() -> Self {
  403|    131|        Self {
  404|    131|            available_tiers: HashSet::new(),
  405|    131|            orchestrator_model: defaults::orchestrator(),
  406|    131|            orchestrator_effort: EffortLevel::default(),
  407|    131|            planner_model: defaults::planner(),
  408|    131|            planner_effort: EffortLevel::default(),
  409|    131|            implementer_model: defaults::implementer(),
  410|    131|            implementer_effort: EffortLevel::default(),
  411|    131|        }
  412|    131|    }
  413|       |}

  429|       |    /// Applies debug build model override.
  430|       |    ///
  431|       |    /// In debug builds, all models use the cheap fallback chain for fast testing.
  432|       |    /// This can be overridden by setting the `PAPERBOAT_MODEL` environment variable.
  433|       |    ///
  434|       |    /// In release builds, this is a no-op (respects user configuration).
  435|       |    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
  436|      2|    pub fn apply_debug_override(&mut self) {
  437|       |        // Check for environment variable override first
  438|      2|        if let Ok(model_str) = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_MODEL") {
                                ^1

  440|      1|                let chain = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);

  442|      1|                self.orchestrator_model = chain.clone();
  443|      1|                self.planner_model = chain.clone();
  444|      1|                self.implementer_model = chain;
  445|      1|                return;
  446|      0|            }
  447|      0|            tracing::warn!(
  448|       |                "⚠️  Invalid PAPERBOAT_MODEL '{}', falling back to debug default",
  449|       |                model_str
  450|       |            );
  451|      1|        }

  455|      1|            ModelTier::CodexMini,

  458|      1|            ModelTier::Haiku,

  476|       |    /// Validates that at least one tier in each fallback chain is available.
  477|      2|    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<()> {

  479|      2|        self.orchestrator_model

                                       ^1                                                 ^1
  482|       |
  483|      1|        self.planner_model

  485|      1|            .map_err(|_| anyhow!("No planner model tier is available"))?;

  486|       |
  487|      1|        self.implementer_model
  488|      1|            .resolve(&self.available_tiers)
  489|      1|            .map_err(|_| anyhow!("No implementer model tier is available"))?;
                                       ^0                                                ^0
  490|       |
  491|      1|        Ok(())
  492|      2|    }

  513|       |/// Parses the output of `auggie model list` and returns available tiers.
  514|       |///
  515|       |/// This function parses the model list output format and extracts
  516|       |/// the tier from model IDs like "haiku4.5", "sonnet4.5", "opus4.5".
  517|       |///
  518|       |/// # Arguments
  519|       |///
  520|       |/// * `output` - The raw output from `auggie model list`
  521|       |///
  522|       |/// # Returns
  523|       |///
  524|       |/// A set of available `ModelTier`s parsed from the output.
  525|      2|pub fn parse_auggie_model_list(output: &str) -> Result<HashSet<ModelTier>> {
  526|      2|    let mut tiers = HashSet::new();
  527|       |
  528|       |    // Pattern to match lines like " - Haiku 4.5 [haiku4.5]"
  529|      2|    let model_re = Regex::new(r"^\s*-\s*.+?\s*\[([^\]]+)\]\s*$")?;
                                                                              ^0
  530|       |
  531|      6|    for line in output.lines() {
                              ^2     ^2
  532|      6|        if let Some(caps) = model_re.captures(line) {
                                  ^3
  533|      3|            let id_str = caps.get(1).map_or("", |m| m.as_str()).trim();
  534|       |            // Extract tier from model ID (e.g., "haiku4.5" -> "haiku")
  535|      3|            if let Some(tier) = extract_tier_from_auggie_id(id_str) {
  536|      3|                tiers.insert(tier);
  537|      3|            }
                          ^0
  538|      3|        }
  539|       |    }
  540|       |
  541|      2|    Ok(tiers)
  542|      2|}
  543|       |
  544|       |/// Extract a `ModelTier` from an Auggie model ID string.
  545|       |///
  546|       |/// Examples:
  547|       |/// - "haiku4.5" -> Haiku
  548|       |/// - "sonnet4.5" -> Sonnet
  549|       |/// - "opus4.5" -> Opus
  550|      3|fn extract_tier_from_auggie_id(id: &str) -> Option<ModelTier> {
  551|      3|    let lower = id.to_lowercase();
  552|      3|    if lower.starts_with("haiku") {
  553|      1|        Some(ModelTier::Haiku)
  554|      2|    } else if lower.starts_with("sonnet") {
  555|      1|        Some(ModelTier::Sonnet)
  556|      1|    } else if lower.starts_with("opus") {
  557|      1|        Some(ModelTier::Opus)
  558|      0|    } else if lower.starts_with("gpt") {
  559|      0|        Some(ModelTier::Codex) // Map GPT models to Codex tier
  560|      0|    } else if lower == "auto" {
  561|      0|        Some(ModelTier::Auto)
  562|       |    } else {
  563|      0|        None
  564|       |    }
  565|      3|}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/self_improve/config.rs:
    1|       |//! Configuration for self-improvement feature.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles loading configuration for the self-improvement feature.
    4|       |//! Configuration is read from (in priority order):
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! 1. Environment variable `PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVE` (`1`, `true`, `0`, `false`)
    7|       |//! 2. Project-level config: `.paperboat/self-improve.toml`
    8|       |//! 3. User-level config: `~/.paperboat/self-improve.toml`
    9|       |//! 4. Default: `true` (opt-out feature - enabled by default)
   10|       |
   11|       |use serde::Deserialize;
   12|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Configuration for self-improvement feature loaded from TOML
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]
   16|       |pub struct SelfImproveConfig {
   17|       |    /// Whether self-improvement is enabled
   18|       |    #[serde(default)]
   19|       |    pub enabled: bool,
   20|       |}
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Returns the user-level self-improve config path (~/.paperboat/self-improve.toml)
   23|      3|fn user_config_path() -> PathBuf {
   24|      3|    let home = shellexpand::tilde("~/.paperboat/self-improve.toml").to_string();
   25|      3|    PathBuf::from(home)
   26|      3|}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Returns the project-level self-improve config path (.paperboat/self-improve.toml)
   29|      3|fn project_config_path() -> PathBuf {
   30|      3|    PathBuf::from(".paperboat/self-improve.toml")
   31|      3|}
   32|       |
   33|       |/// Loads self-improvement configuration from TOML file.
   34|       |///
   35|       |/// Returns None if the file doesn't exist or can't be parsed.
   36|     10|fn load_config_file(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<SelfImproveConfig> {
   37|     10|    if !path.exists() {
   38|      5|        return None;
   39|      5|    }
   40|       |
   41|      5|    let contents = std::fs::read_to_string(path).ok()?;
                                                                   ^0
   42|      5|    toml::from_str(&contents).ok()
   43|     10|}
   44|       |
   45|       |/// Loads self-improvement configuration from config files.
   46|       |///
   47|       |/// Checks project-level config first, then user-level config.
   48|       |/// Returns None if no config file exists.
   49|      2|fn load_self_improve_config() -> Option<SelfImproveConfig> {
   50|       |    // Check project-level config first (higher priority)
   51|      2|    if let Some(config) = load_config_file(&project_config_path()) {
                              ^0
   52|      0|        return Some(config);
   53|      2|    }
   54|       |
   55|       |    // Fall back to user-level config
   56|      2|    load_config_file(&user_config_path())
   57|      2|}
   58|       |
   59|       |/// Checks if self-improvement is enabled.
   60|       |///
   61|       |/// Priority (highest to lowest):
   62|       |/// 1. `PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVE` environment variable (`1`, `true` to enable; `0`, `false` to disable)
   63|       |/// 2. `.paperboat/self-improve.toml` (project-level)
   64|       |/// 3. `~/.paperboat/self-improve.toml` (user-level)
   65|       |/// 4. Default: `true` (opt-out feature - enabled by default)
   66|       |///
   67|       |/// # Examples
   68|       |///
   69|       |/// ```ignore
   70|       |/// // Check if self-improvement should run
   71|       |/// if is_self_improvement_enabled() {
   72|       |///     // Run self-improvement agent
   73|       |/// }
   74|       |/// ```
   75|     14|pub fn is_self_improvement_enabled() -> bool {
   76|       |    // Check env var first (highest priority)
   77|     14|    if let Ok(val) = std::env::var("PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVE") {
                            ^12
   78|     12|        let val_lower = val.to_lowercase();
   79|       |        // Explicit disable values return false
   80|     12|        if matches!(val_lower.as_str(), "0" | "false" | "no" | "off") {
                         ^6                                 ^9        ^7     ^6
   81|      6|            return false;
   82|      6|        }
   83|       |        // Enable values return true
   84|      6|        return matches!(val_lower.as_str(), "1" | "true" | "yes" | "on");
                                                                ^2       ^0      ^0
   85|      2|    }
   86|       |
   87|       |    // Check config files, default to true if no config exists
   88|      2|    load_self_improve_config().is_none_or(|c| c.enabled)
   89|     14|}
   90|       |

   93|       |    use super::*;
   94|       |    use std::sync::Mutex;
   95|       |
   96|       |    // Use a mutex to serialize tests that modify PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVE env var.
   97|       |    // This prevents race conditions when tests run in parallel.
   98|       |    static ENV_VAR_MUTEX: Mutex<()> = Mutex::new(());
   99|       |
  100|       |    // Test the parsing logic directly without relying on environment variables
  101|     15|    fn parse_env_value(val: &str) -> bool {
  102|     15|        let val_lower = val.to_lowercase();
  103|     15|        matches!(val_lower.as_str(), "1" | "true" | "yes" | "on")
                      ^8                                 ^14      ^11     ^9
  104|     15|    }

  285|      1|    }

   45|       |    /// Run completed successfully - focus on optimization
   46|       |    Success,
   47|       |    /// Run partially succeeded - focus on error patterns and recovery

   78|       |            Self::PartialSuccess => {

   98|       |/// Build rich context for the self-improvement agent from a completed run.
   99|       |///
  100|       |/// This function analyzes the run directory, task results, and task manager state
  101|       |/// to produce a structured markdown context that can be appended to the agent's
  102|       |/// task description.
  103|       |///
  104|       |/// # Arguments
  105|       |///
  106|       |/// * `log_dir` - Path to the run's log directory
  107|       |/// * `result` - The final task result from the run
  108|       |/// * `task_manager` - Reference to the `TaskManager` with final state
  109|       |///
  110|       |/// # Returns
  111|       |///
  112|       |/// A markdown-formatted string suitable for inclusion in an agent prompt.
  113|       |///
  114|       |/// # Errors
  115|       |///
  116|       |/// This function is designed to be resilient. It will not fail if logs are
  117|       |/// missing or unreadable - it will simply note what's unavailable.
  118|      7|pub async fn build_self_improvement_context(
  119|      7|    log_dir: &Path,
  120|      7|    result: &TaskResult,
  121|      7|    task_manager: &TaskManager,
  122|      7|) -> Result<String, std::io::Error> {
  123|      7|    let mut sections = Vec::new();
  124|       |
  125|       |    // 1. Extract run statistics from task manager
  126|      7|    let stats = extract_run_stats(task_manager, log_dir).await;
  127|       |

  129|      7|    let outcome = RunOutcome::from_result_and_stats(result, &stats);
  130|       |
  131|       |    // 3. Build run summary section
  132|      7|    sections.push(build_run_summary(log_dir, result, &stats, outcome));
  133|       |
  134|       |    // 4. Build log file inventory
  135|      7|    sections.push(build_log_inventory(log_dir).await);
  136|       |
  137|       |    // 5. Build quick stats section
  138|      7|    sections.push(build_quick_stats(&stats));
  139|       |
  140|       |    // 6. Add focus areas based on outcome
  141|      7|    sections.push(outcome.focus_areas().to_string());
  142|       |

  144|      7|    if let Some(task_section) = build_task_summary(task_manager) {
                              ^3
  145|      3|        sections.push(task_section);
  146|      4|    }
  147|       |
  148|       |    // 8. Add error patterns if found
  149|      7|    if let Some(error_section) = extract_error_patterns(log_dir).await {
                              ^3
  150|      3|        sections.push(error_section);
  151|      4|    }
  152|       |
  153|      7|    Ok(sections.join("\n\n---\n\n"))

  156|       |/// Extract statistics from the task manager and log directory.
  157|      7|async fn extract_run_stats(task_manager: &TaskManager, log_dir: &Path) -> RunStats {
  158|      7|    let mut stats = RunStats::default();
  159|       |
  160|       |    // Count tasks by status
  161|      7|    for task in task_manager.all_tasks() {
                      ^5
  162|      5|        stats.total_tasks += 1;
  163|      5|        match &task.status {
  164|      4|            TaskStatus::Complete { success, .. } => {
  165|      4|                if *success {
  166|      4|                    stats.completed_tasks += 1;
  167|      4|                } else {
  168|      0|                    stats.failed_tasks += 1;
  169|      0|                }
  170|       |            }
  171|      1|            TaskStatus::Failed { .. } => stats.failed_tasks += 1,
  172|      0|            TaskStatus::Skipped { .. } => stats.skipped_tasks += 1,
  173|      0|            _ => {}
  174|       |        }
  175|       |    }
  176|       |
  177|       |    // Count implementer logs to estimate agents spawned
  178|      7|    stats.agents_spawned = count_implementer_logs(log_dir);
  179|       |
  180|       |    // Count errors and warnings in logs
  181|      7|    let (errors, warnings) = count_log_patterns(log_dir).await;
  182|      7|    stats.error_count = errors;
  183|      7|    stats.warning_count = warnings;
  184|       |
  185|      7|    stats
  186|      7|}

  215|       |/// Count error and warning patterns in log files.
  216|       |///
  217|       |/// This function counts actual error/warning patterns while filtering out
  218|       |/// false positives that come from code documentation or discussions about
  219|       |/// error types (e.g., "Implements std::error::Error").
  220|     11|async fn count_log_patterns(dir: &Path) -> (usize, usize) {
  221|     11|    let mut errors = 0;
  222|     11|    let mut warnings = 0;
  223|       |
  224|       |    // Patterns that indicate errors or warnings in our logs
  225|     11|    let error_patterns = ["❌", "ERROR", "error:", "Failed:", "Tool failed:"];
  226|     11|    let warning_patterns = ["⚠", "WARN", "warning:"];
  227|       |
  228|       |    // Patterns to exclude (false positives from code documentation/discussion)
  229|     11|    let false_positive_patterns = [
  230|     11|        "std::error::Error",
  231|     11|        "impl.*Error",
  232|     11|        "anyhow::Error",
  233|     11|        "Result<.*Error",
  234|     11|        "error::.*Error",
  235|     11|        "error handling",
  236|     11|        "error message",
  237|     11|        "ErrorKind",
  238|     11|        "ERROR_PIPE_BUSY",
  239|     11|    ];
  240|       |
  241|     11|    if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(dir) {
  242|     19|        for entry in entries.flatten() {
                                   ^11     ^11
  243|     19|            let path = entry.path();
  244|     19|            if path.is_file()
  245|     15|                && path
  246|     15|                    .extension()
  247|     15|                    .is_some_and(|e| e.eq_ignore_ascii_case("log"))
  248|       |            {
  249|     15|                if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
  250|       |                    // Count line by line to enable false positive filtering
  251|     27|                    for line in content.lines() {
                                              ^15
  252|       |                        // Skip lines with false positive patterns
  253|     27|                        let is_false_positive = false_positive_patterns
  254|     27|                            .iter()
  255|    243|                            .any(|pattern| line.contains(pattern));
                                           ^27
  256|     27|                        if is_false_positive {
  257|      0|                            continue;
  258|     27|                        }
  259|       |
  260|    123|                        for pattern in &error_patterns {
                                                     ^27
  261|    123|                            if line.contains(pattern) {
  262|      3|                                errors += 1;
  263|      3|                                break; // Count each line only once
  264|    120|                            }
  265|       |                        }
  266|     81|                        for pattern in &warning_patterns {
                                                     ^27
  267|     81|                            if line.contains(pattern) {
  268|      0|                                warnings += 1;
  269|      0|                                break; // Count each line only once
  270|     81|                            }
  271|       |                        }
  272|       |                    }
  273|      0|                }
  274|      4|            } else if path.is_dir() {
  275|      4|                let (sub_errors, sub_warnings) = Box::pin(count_log_patterns(&path)).await;
  276|      4|                errors += sub_errors;
  277|      4|                warnings += sub_warnings;
  278|      0|            }
  279|       |        }
  280|      0|    }
  281|       |
  282|     11|    (errors, warnings)

  358|       |    // Standard log files to look for

  368|      6|            let size = std::fs::metadata(&path).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
  369|      6|            (true, size)

  371|     15|            (false, 0)
  372|       |        };

  375|     21|            path: name.to_string(),

  379|     21|        });

  402|     10|                    None
  403|       |                }
  404|     14|            })

  406|       |

  447|       |/// Build task summary section from task manager state.
  448|      9|fn build_task_summary(task_manager: &TaskManager) -> Option<String> {
  449|      9|    let tasks: Vec<_> = task_manager.all_tasks();
  450|      9|    if tasks.is_empty() {
  451|      4|        return None;
  452|      5|    }
  453|       |
  454|      5|    let mut lines = vec!["## Task Summary".to_string()];
  455|      5|    lines.push(String::new());
  456|       |
  457|       |    // Sort tasks by ID
  458|      5|    let mut sorted: Vec<_> = tasks.into_iter().collect();
  459|    288|    sorted.sort_by(|a, b| a.id.cmp(&b.id));
                  ^5     ^5
  460|       |
  461|       |    // Limit to first 20 tasks to keep context size reasonable
  462|      5|    let limit = 20;
  463|      5|    let truncated = sorted.len() > limit;
  464|       |
  465|     28|    for task in sorted.iter().take(limit) {
                              ^5            ^5   ^5
  466|     28|        let status_emoji = match &task.status {
  467|     26|            TaskStatus::Complete { success: true, .. } => "✅",
  468|      0|            TaskStatus::Complete { success: false, .. } => "⚠️",
  469|      2|            TaskStatus::Failed { .. } => "❌",
  470|      0|            TaskStatus::Skipped { .. } => "⏭️",
  471|      0|            TaskStatus::InProgress { .. } => "🔄",
  472|      0|            TaskStatus::NotStarted => "⏸️",
  473|       |        };
  474|       |
  475|     28|        let summary = match &task.status {
  476|     26|            TaskStatus::Complete { summary, .. } => {
  477|       |                // Truncate long summaries
  478|     26|                if summary.len() > 80 {
  479|      0|                    format!("{}...", &summary[..77])
  480|       |                } else {
  481|     26|                    summary.clone()
  482|       |                }
  483|       |            }
  484|      2|            TaskStatus::Failed { error } => {
  485|      2|                let err_display = if error.len() > 60 {
  486|      0|                    &error[..57]
  487|       |                } else {
  488|      2|                    error
  489|       |                };
  490|      2|                format!("FAILED: {err_display}")
  491|       |            }
  492|      0|            TaskStatus::Skipped { reason } => {
  493|      0|                let reason_display = if reason.len() > 60 {
  494|      0|                    &reason[..57]
  495|       |                } else {
  496|      0|                    reason
  497|       |                };
  498|      0|                format!("Skipped: {reason_display}")
  499|       |            }
  500|      0|            _ => task.status.as_display_str().to_string(),
  501|       |        };
  502|       |
  503|     28|        lines.push(format!(
  504|       |            "- {} **[{}]** {}: {}",
  505|       |            status_emoji, task.id, task.name, summary
  506|       |        ));
  507|       |    }

  510|      1|        lines.push(format!("\n... and {} more tasks", sorted.len() - limit));

  516|       |/// Extract error patterns from log files for analysis.
  517|       |///
  518|       |/// Returns a markdown section summarizing notable errors if found.
  519|      7|async fn extract_error_patterns(log_dir: &Path) -> Option<String> {
  520|       |    const MAX_ERRORS: usize = 15;
  521|      7|    let mut error_lines = Vec::new();
  522|       |
  523|       |    // Patterns that indicate errors worth highlighting
  524|      7|    let error_markers = ["❌", "ERROR", "error:", "Tool failed:", "Failed:", "panic"];
  525|       |
  526|       |    // Patterns to exclude (false positives from code documentation/discussion)
  527|       |    // These appear in agent logs when discussing code, not actual errors
  528|      7|    let false_positive_patterns = [
  529|      7|        "std::error::Error", // Rust trait documentation
  530|      7|        "impl.*Error",       // Implementing Error trait discussions
  531|      7|        "anyhow::Error",     // Anyhow error type mentions
  532|      7|        "Result<.*Error",    // Result type discussions
  533|      7|        "error::.*Error",    // Error module paths
  534|      7|        "error handling",    // Documentation about error handling
  535|      7|        "error message",     // Documentation about error messages
  536|      7|        "ErrorKind",         // std::io::ErrorKind discussions
  537|      7|        "ERROR_PIPE_BUSY",   // Windows error constant
  538|      7|    ];
  539|       |
  540|       |    // Read each log file and extract error lines
  541|      7|    if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(log_dir) {
  542|     14|        for entry in entries.flatten() {
                                   ^7      ^7
  543|     14|            let path = entry.path();
  544|     14|            if !path.is_file()
  545|     10|                || path
  546|     10|                    .extension()
  547|     10|                    .is_none_or(|e| !e.eq_ignore_ascii_case("log"))
  548|       |            {
  549|      4|                continue;
  550|     10|            }
  551|       |
  552|     10|            let file_name = path
  553|     10|                .file_name()
  554|     10|                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
  555|     10|                .unwrap_or("unknown");
  556|       |
  557|     10|            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
  558|     22|                for line in content.lines() {
                                          ^10
  559|       |                    // Check for error indicators
  560|    117|                    let is_error = error_markers.iter().any(|marker| line.contains(marker));
                                      ^22        ^22                  ^22
  561|     22|                    if !is_error {
  562|     19|                        continue;
  563|      3|                    }
  564|       |
  565|       |                    // Filter out false positives (code documentation mentioning error types)
  566|      3|                    let is_false_positive = false_positive_patterns
  567|      3|                        .iter()
  568|     27|                        .any(|pattern| line.contains(pattern));
                                       ^3
  569|      3|                    if is_false_positive {
  570|      0|                        continue;
  571|      3|                    }
  572|       |
  573|       |                    // Truncate long lines
  574|      3|                    let display_line = if line.len() > 120 {
  575|      0|                        format!("{}...", &line[..117])
  576|       |                    } else {
  577|      3|                        line.to_string()
  578|       |                    };
  579|       |
  580|      3|                    error_lines.push(format!("[{file_name}] {display_line}"));
  581|       |
  582|      3|                    if error_lines.len() >= MAX_ERRORS {
  583|      0|                        break;
  584|      3|                    }
  585|       |                }
  586|      0|            }
  587|       |
  588|     10|            if error_lines.len() >= MAX_ERRORS {
  589|      0|                break;
  590|     10|            }
  591|       |        }
  592|      0|    }
  593|       |
  594|      7|    if error_lines.is_empty() {
  595|      4|        return None;
  596|      3|    }
  597|       |
  598|      3|    let mut section = String::from("## Error Patterns Detected\n\n");
  599|      3|    section.push_str("The following errors were found in the logs:\n\n");
  600|      3|    section.push_str("```\n");
  601|      3|    for line in &error_lines {
  602|      3|        section.push_str(line);
  603|      3|        section.push('\n');
  604|      3|    }
  605|      3|    section.push_str("```\n");
  606|       |
  607|      3|    Some(section)
  608|      7|}

    1|       |//! Repository detection for self-improvement feature.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides utilities to detect whether the application is running
    4|       |//! in its own repository (paperboat) or in a different repository. This is used
    5|       |//! to enable/disable the self-improvement feature.
    6|       |
    7|       |use std::path::Path;
    8|       |use std::process::Command;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Result of repository detection.
   11|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
   12|       |pub enum RepositoryKind {
   13|       |    /// Running in the paperboat repository (self-improvement enabled).
   14|       |    OwnRepository,
   15|       |    /// Running in a different repository.
   16|       |    DifferentRepository,
   17|       |    /// Could not determine the repository type.
   18|       |    Unknown,
   19|       |}
   20|       |
   21|       |impl RepositoryKind {
   22|       |    /// Returns true if this is the paperboat repository.
   23|       |    #[must_use]
   24|     11|    pub const fn is_own_repository(&self) -> bool {
   25|     11|        matches!(self, Self::OwnRepository)
                      ^5
   26|     11|    }
   27|       |}
   28|       |
   29|       |/// Detects whether the current working directory is the paperboat repository.
   30|       |///
   31|       |/// Uses multiple detection methods:
   32|       |/// 1. Check git remote URL for "paperboat" or "villalobos"
   33|       |/// 2. Check Cargo.toml for `name = "paperboat"`
   34|       |///
   35|       |/// Either method matching is sufficient to confirm we're in the paperboat repo.
   36|       |///
   37|       |/// # Returns
   38|       |/// - `OwnRepository` if detected as the paperboat repository
   39|       |/// - `DifferentRepository` if definitely a different repository
   40|       |/// - `Unknown` if detection failed (no git, no Cargo.toml, etc.)
   41|       |#[must_use]
   42|      4|pub fn detect_repository() -> RepositoryKind {
   43|      4|    detect_repository_at(Path::new("."))
   44|      4|}
   45|       |
   46|       |/// Detects whether the given directory is the paperboat repository.
   47|       |///
   48|       |/// This is the testable version that accepts a path parameter.
   49|       |///
   50|       |/// # Arguments
   51|       |/// * `dir` - The directory to check
   52|       |///
   53|       |/// # Returns
   54|       |/// - `OwnRepository` if detected as the paperboat repository
   55|       |/// - `DifferentRepository` if definitely a different repository
   56|       |/// - `Unknown` if detection failed
   57|       |#[must_use]
   58|     20|pub fn detect_repository_at(dir: &Path) -> RepositoryKind {
   59|       |    // Method 1: Check git remote URL
   60|     20|    if let Some(is_paperboat) = check_git_remote(dir) {
                              ^3
   61|      3|        if is_paperboat {
   62|      3|            return RepositoryKind::OwnRepository;
   63|      0|        }
   64|       |        // Git remote exists but doesn't match - could still check Cargo.toml
   65|       |        // for local development scenarios without proper remote
   66|     17|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    // Method 2: Check Cargo.toml package name
   69|     17|    if let Some(is_paperboat) = check_cargo_toml(dir) {
                              ^13
   70|     13|        if is_paperboat {
   71|      7|            return RepositoryKind::OwnRepository;
   72|      6|        }
   73|       |        // Has Cargo.toml but different package name
   74|      6|        return RepositoryKind::DifferentRepository;
   75|      4|    }
   76|       |
   77|       |    // Neither method could make a determination
   78|      4|    RepositoryKind::Unknown
   79|     20|}
   80|       |
   81|       |/// Check if the git remote URL indicates the paperboat repository.
   82|       |///
   83|       |/// Returns `Some(true)` if the remote matches paperboat/villalobos,
   84|       |/// `Some(false)` if git remote exists but doesn't match,
   85|       |/// `None` if git is not available or not a git repository.
   86|     21|fn check_git_remote(dir: &Path) -> Option<bool> {
   87|     21|    let output = Command::new("git")
                      ^20
   88|     21|        .args(["remote", "get-url", "origin"])
   89|     21|        .current_dir(dir)
   90|     21|        .output()
   91|     21|        .ok()?;
                           ^1
   92|       |
   93|     20|    if !output.status.success() {
   94|     17|        return None;
   95|      3|    }
   96|       |
   97|      3|    let url = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
   98|      3|    let url_lower = url.to_lowercase();
   99|       |
  100|       |    // Check for paperboat or villalobos (the monorepo name)
  101|      3|    let is_paperboat = url_lower.contains("paperboat") || url_lower.contains("villalobos");
                                                                        ^0
  102|       |
  103|      3|    Some(is_paperboat)
  104|     21|}
  105|       |
  106|       |/// Check if the Cargo.toml package name is "paperboat".
  107|       |///
  108|       |/// Returns `Some(true)` if Cargo.toml contains `name = "paperboat"`,
  109|       |/// `Some(false)` if Cargo.toml exists but has different package name,
  110|       |/// `None` if Cargo.toml doesn't exist or can't be read.
  111|     22|fn check_cargo_toml(dir: &Path) -> Option<bool> {
  112|     22|    let cargo_path = dir.join("Cargo.toml");
  113|     22|    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&cargo_path).ok()?;
                      ^17                                                ^5
  114|       |
  115|       |    // Simple check: look for name = "paperboat" in the package section
  116|       |    // This is intentionally simple - a full TOML parse would be overkill
  117|     17|    let is_paperboat = content.contains(r#"name = "paperboat""#);
  118|       |
  119|     17|    Some(is_paperboat)
  120|     22|}

  122|       |/// Async-compatible wrapper for repository detection.
  123|       |///
  124|       |/// This function is async to allow for future enhancements that may
  125|       |/// require async operations (e.g., checking remote repository state).
  126|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
  127|      2|pub async fn detect_repository_async() -> RepositoryKind {
  128|       |    // For now, just wrap the sync version
  129|       |    // Using spawn_blocking for the git command execution
  130|      2|    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(detect_repository)
  131|      2|        .await
  132|      2|        .unwrap_or(RepositoryKind::Unknown)
  133|      2|}
  134|       |
  135|       |/// Async-compatible wrapper for repository detection at a specific path.
  136|       |#[allow(dead_code)]
  137|      4|pub async fn detect_repository_at_async(dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> RepositoryKind {
  138|      4|    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || detect_repository_at(&dir))
  139|      4|        .await
  140|      4|        .unwrap_or(RepositoryKind::Unknown)

  143|       |/// Checks if the current working directory is the paperboat repository.
  144|       |///
  145|       |/// This is a convenience function that returns `true` if the current directory
  146|       |/// is identified as the paperboat repository using git remote URL and Cargo.toml checks.
  147|       |///
  148|       |/// # Returns

  151|       |///

  159|       |/// }
  160|       |/// ```
  161|       |#[must_use]
  162|      2|pub fn is_paperboat_repository() -> bool {

  164|      2|}

  166|       |/// Checks if the given directory is the paperboat repository.

  413|       |        // The actual check_git_remote function uses Command, so we test indirectly
  414|       |        // by checking the URL matching pattern in check_git_remote
  415|       |
  416|       |        // The function looks for "paperboat" or "villalobos" in the remote URL
  417|      1|        let test_urls = [
  418|      1|            ("https://github.com/user/paperboat.git", true),
  419|      1|            ("git@github.com:user/paperboat.git", true),
  420|      1|            ("https://github.com/user/villalobos.git", true),
  421|      1|            ("git@github.com:user/villalobos.git", true),
  422|      1|            ("https://github.com/user/other-project.git", false),
  423|      1|        ];

  425|      5|        for (url, expected_match) in test_urls {

  429|       |                is_match, expected_match,

  433|       |        }
  434|      1|    }
