/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/self_improve/runner.rs:
    1|       |//! Self-improvement runner module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles spawning and running the self-improvement agent
    4|       |//! after a successful paperboat run in its own repository.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! The self-improvement feature only runs when paperboat detects it is
    7|       |//! in its own repository, using the `is_paperboat_repository()` check.
    8|       |//! The agent has full implementer permissions to analyze logs and make
    9|       |//! small, safe code changes.
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! # Configuration
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! Self-improvement is enabled by default (opt-out). Control via:
   14|       |//! - `PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVE=0` environment variable to disable
   15|       |//! - `.paperboat/self-improve.toml` config file with `enabled = false`
   16|       |
   17|       |use crate::acp::{AcpClient, AcpClientTrait, SessionMode};
   18|       |use crate::agents::{get_prompt, get_tool_config};
   19|       |use crate::app::retry::{retry_async, RetryConfig};
   20|       |use crate::ipc::{IpcAddress, IpcListener, IpcStream};
   21|       |use crate::logging::{AgentWriter, LogScope};
   22|       |use crate::tasks::TaskManager;
   23|       |use crate::types::TaskResult;
   24|       |use anyhow::{Context, Result};
   25|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   26|       |use std::path::Path;
   27|       |use std::time::Duration;
   28|       |use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
   29|       |use tokio::sync::{broadcast, mpsc};
   30|       |use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
   31|       |
   32|       |use super::context_builder::build_self_improvement_context;
   33|       |use super::detection::is_paperboat_repository;
   34|       |use super::is_self_improvement_enabled;
   35|       |
   36|       |/// Result of a self-improvement run.
   37|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   38|       |pub struct SelfImprovementOutcome {
   39|       |    /// Whether the self-improvement run was successful.
   40|       |    pub success: bool,
   41|       |    /// Message from the self-improver agent.
   42|       |    pub message: Option<String>,
   43|       |    /// Number of changes made (if any).
   44|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   45|       |    pub changes_made: usize,
   46|       |}
   47|       |
   48|       |/// Configuration for the self-improvement runner.
   49|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   50|       |pub struct SelfImprovementConfig {
   51|       |    /// Timeout for the self-improvement session.
   52|       |    pub session_timeout: Duration,
   53|       |    /// Request timeout for ACP operations.
   54|       |    pub request_timeout: Duration,
   55|       |    /// Model to use for self-improvement.
   56|       |    pub model: String,
   57|       |}
   58|       |
   59|       |impl Default for SelfImprovementConfig {
   60|      4|    fn default() -> Self {
   61|      4|        Self {
   62|      4|            session_timeout: Duration::from_secs(300), // 5 minutes
   63|      4|            request_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
   64|      4|            model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514".to_string(),
   65|      4|        }
   66|      4|    }
   67|       |}
   68|       |
   69|       |/// Run self-improvement if conditions are met.
   70|       |///
   71|       |/// This is the main entry point called from main.rs after a successful run.
   72|       |/// It checks all preconditions and spawns the self-improver agent if appropriate.
   73|       |///
   74|       |/// Self-improvement only runs when:
   75|       |/// 1. The feature is enabled via `PAPERBOAT_SELF_IMPROVEMENT=1`
   76|       |/// 2. The run was successful
   77|       |/// 3. We're running in the paperboat repository
   78|       |///
   79|       |/// # Arguments
   80|       |///
   81|       |/// * `run_dir` - Path to the completed run's log directory
   82|       |/// * `result` - The final task result from the main run
   83|       |/// * `task_manager` - Reference to the `TaskManager` with final state
   84|       |///
   85|       |/// # Returns
   86|       |///
   87|       |/// * `Ok(Some(outcome))` - Self-improvement ran and completed
   88|       |/// * `Ok(None)` - Self-improvement was skipped (disabled, not in paperboat repo, etc.)
   89|       |/// * `Err(e)` - Self-improvement failed
   90|       |///
   91|       |/// # Errors
   92|       |///
   93|       |/// This function is designed to be resilient. Errors are logged but should
   94|       |/// not cause the main application to fail.
   95|      3|pub async fn maybe_run_self_improvement(
   96|      3|    run_dir: &Path,
   97|      3|    result: &TaskResult,
   98|      3|    task_manager: &TaskManager,
   99|      3|) -> Result<Option<SelfImprovementOutcome>> {
  100|       |    // Check if self-improvement is enabled
  101|      3|    if !is_self_improvement_enabled() {
  102|      1|        tracing::debug!("Self-improvement is disabled");
  103|      1|        return Ok(None);
  104|      2|    }
  105|       |
  106|       |    // Check if the run was successful (only improve on success or partial success)
  107|      2|    if !result.success {
  108|      1|        tracing::debug!("Run failed, skipping self-improvement");
  109|      1|        return Ok(None);
  110|      1|    }
  111|       |
  112|       |    // Check if we're in the paperboat repository
  113|      1|    if !is_paperboat_repository() {
  114|      1|        tracing::debug!("Not in paperboat repository, skipping self-improvement");
  115|      1|        return Ok(None);
  116|      0|    }
  117|       |
  118|      0|    tracing::info!("🔄 Starting self-improvement phase...");
  119|       |
  120|       |    // Build context for the self-improver
  121|      0|    let context = build_self_improvement_context(run_dir, result, task_manager)
  122|      0|        .await
  123|      0|        .context("Failed to build self-improvement context")?;
  124|       |
  125|       |    // Run the self-improvement agent with full implementer permissions
  126|      0|    let config = SelfImprovementConfig::default();
  127|      0|    let outcome = run_self_improver(run_dir, &context, &config).await?;
  128|       |
  129|      0|    Ok(Some(outcome))
  130|      3|}
  131|       |
  132|       |/// Run the self-improvement agent with full editing permissions.
  133|      0|async fn run_self_improver(
  134|      0|    run_dir: &Path,
  135|      0|    context: &str,
  136|      0|    config: &SelfImprovementConfig,
  137|      0|) -> Result<SelfImprovementOutcome> {
  138|       |    // Create the self-improvement scope for logging
  139|      0|    let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);
  140|      0|    let self_improve_dir = run_dir.join("self-improve");
  141|      0|    std::fs::create_dir_all(&self_improve_dir)?;
  142|      0|    let scope = LogScope::new(self_improve_dir.clone(), event_tx, 0);
  143|       |
  144|       |    // Create writer for the self-improver agent
  145|      0|    let mut writer = scope
  146|      0|        .self_improver_writer()
  147|      0|        .await
  148|      0|        .context("Failed to create self-improver writer")?;
  149|       |
  150|       |    // Build the task description
  151|      0|    let task = build_self_improvement_task(run_dir);
  152|       |
  153|       |    // Get the self-improver prompt template
  154|      0|    let prompt_template = get_prompt("selfimprover")
  155|      0|        .context("selfimprover prompt not found - ensure prompts/selfimprover.txt exists")?;
  156|       |
  157|       |    // Build the full prompt
  158|      0|    let prompt = prompt_template
  159|      0|        .replace("{task}", &task)
  160|      0|        .replace("{context}", context);
  161|       |
  162|       |    // Get removed tools for selfimprover (defaults to implementer config)
  163|      0|    let tool_config = get_tool_config("selfimprover");
  164|      0|    let removed_tools: Vec<String> = tool_config
  165|      0|        .removed_auggie_tools
  166|      0|        .iter()
  167|      0|        .map(|s| (*s).to_string())
  168|      0|        .collect();
  169|       |
  170|       |    // Log the prompt
  171|      0|    let _ = writer.write_header_with_prompt(&task, &prompt).await;
  172|       |
  173|       |    // Spawn and run the agent with implementer-level tools
  174|      0|    let outcome =
  175|      0|        spawn_and_run_agent(&prompt, &removed_tools, "implementer", config, &mut writer).await?;
  176|       |
  177|       |    // Finalize the log
  178|      0|    let _ = writer.finalize(outcome.success).await;
  179|       |

  275|      0|                .await
  276|      0|                .context("Failed to create self-improver session")?;

  279|      0|        }
  280|      0|    })

  307|       |/// Handle for the self-improver socket, for cleanup.
  308|       |struct SelfImproverSocketHandle {
  309|       |    socket_address: IpcAddress,
  310|       |    listener_task: JoinHandle<()>,
  311|       |}
  312|       |
  313|       |impl SelfImproverSocketHandle {
  314|      5|    fn cleanup(self) {
  315|      5|        self.listener_task.abort();
  316|      5|        self.socket_address.cleanup();
  317|      5|    }
  318|       |}
  319|       |
  320|       |/// Set up a socket listener for the self-improver.
  321|       |///
  322|       |/// This handles MCP tool calls from the self-improver agent.
  323|       |/// The main tool we care about is `complete`, which signals the agent is done.
  324|      5|async fn setup_selfimprover_socket(
  325|      5|    socket_address: &IpcAddress,
  326|      5|    completion_tx: mpsc::Sender<CompletionSignal>,
  327|      5|) -> Result<SelfImproverSocketHandle> {
  328|      5|    let listener = IpcListener::bind(socket_address)
  329|      5|        .await
  330|      5|        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to bind self-improver socket at {socket_address}"))?;
                                       ^0                                                                 ^0
  331|       |
  332|      5|    tracing::debug!("Self-improver socket listening at: {}", socket_address);
  333|       |
  334|      5|    let socket_address_for_handle = socket_address.clone();
  335|       |
  336|      5|    let listener_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
  337|       |        loop {
  338|      8|            match listener.accept().await {
  339|      3|                Ok(stream) => {
  340|      3|                    let completion_tx = completion_tx.clone();
  341|      3|                    tokio::spawn(async move {
  342|      3|                        if let Err(e) = handle_selfimprover_tool_call(stream, completion_tx).await {
                                                 ^1
  343|      1|                            tracing::warn!("Self-improver tool call error: {}", e);
  344|      2|                        }
  345|      3|                    });
  346|       |                }
  347|      0|                Err(e) => {
  348|      0|                    tracing::debug!("Self-improver socket listener stopped: {}", e);
  349|      0|                    break;
  350|       |                }
  351|       |            }
  352|       |        }
  353|      0|    });
  354|       |
  355|       |    // Yield to let the listener task start
  356|      5|    tokio::task::yield_now().await;
  357|       |
  358|      5|    Ok(SelfImproverSocketHandle {
  359|      5|        socket_address: socket_address_for_handle,
  360|      5|        listener_task,
  361|      5|    })

  364|       |/// Handle a single tool call from the self-improver agent.
  365|       |///
  366|       |/// This parses the MCP tool request (in JSON-RPC format), handles it,
  367|       |/// and sends the response back. The main tool we care about is `complete`.
  368|      3|async fn handle_selfimprover_tool_call(
  369|      3|    stream: IpcStream,
  370|      3|    completion_tx: mpsc::Sender<CompletionSignal>,
  371|      3|) -> Result<()> {
  372|      3|    let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
  373|      3|    let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
  374|      3|    let mut line = String::new();
  375|       |
  376|       |    // Read the tool request (JSON-RPC format from MCP server)
  377|      3|    if reader.read_line(&mut line).await? == 0 {
                                                      ^0
  378|      1|        return Ok(()); // Connection closed
  379|      2|    }
  380|       |
  381|      2|    let request: Value = serde_json::from_str(&line).context("Failed to parse tool request")?;
                      ^1       ^1                                                                         ^1
  382|       |
  383|       |    // Extract tool name and arguments from the request
  384|       |    // The MCP server sends requests with request_id and tool_call containing tool info
  385|      1|    let request_id = request["request_id"]
  386|      1|        .as_str()
  387|      1|        .unwrap_or("unknown")
  388|      1|        .to_string();
  389|      1|    let tool_call = &request["tool_call"];
  390|       |
  391|       |    // Tool call contains the tool type as a key with its arguments
  392|      1|    let (tool_name, arguments) = if let Some(obj) = tool_call.as_object() {
  393|       |        // Find the first key (tool type) and its value (arguments)
  394|      1|        obj.iter()
  395|      1|            .next()
  396|      1|            .map_or(("", Value::Null), |(k, v)| (k.as_str(), v.clone()))
  397|       |    } else {
  398|      0|        ("", Value::Null)
  399|       |    };
  400|       |
  401|      1|    tracing::debug!("Self-improver tool call received: {}", tool_name);
  402|       |
  403|       |    // Handle the tool call and build response
  404|      1|    let response =
  405|      1|        handle_selfimprover_request(&request_id, tool_name, &arguments, &completion_tx).await;
  406|       |
  407|       |    // Send response back
  408|      1|    let response_json = serde_json::to_string(&response)?;
                                                                      ^0
  409|      1|    writer.write_all(response_json.as_bytes()).await?;
                                                                  ^0
  410|      1|    writer.write_all(b"\n").await?;
                                               ^0
  411|      1|    writer.flush().await?;
                                      ^0
  412|       |
  413|      1|    Ok(())
  414|      3|}
  415|       |
  416|       |/// Handle a self-improver MCP tool request.
  417|       |///
  418|       |/// The self-improver has access to implementer tools (view, edit, etc.) which
  419|       |/// are handled by Augment, but the `complete` tool is handled by us.
  420|      7|async fn handle_selfimprover_request(
  421|      7|    request_id: &str,
  422|      7|    tool_name: &str,
  423|      7|    arguments: &Value,
  424|      7|    completion_tx: &mpsc::Sender<CompletionSignal>,
  425|      7|) -> Value {
  426|       |    // Check if this is the complete tool
  427|      7|    if tool_name == "complete" || tool_name == "Complete" {
                                                ^3
  428|       |        // Extract success and message from arguments
  429|      5|        let success = arguments
  430|      5|            .get("success")
  431|      5|            .and_then(Value::as_bool)
  432|      5|            .unwrap_or(true);
  433|       |
  434|      5|        let message = arguments
  435|      5|            .get("message")
  436|      5|            .and_then(Value::as_str)
  437|      5|            .map(str::to_string);
  438|       |
  439|       |        // Send completion signal
  440|      5|        let _ = completion_tx
  441|      5|            .send(CompletionSignal { success, message })
  442|      5|            .await;
  443|       |
  444|      5|        return json!({
  445|      5|            "request_id": request_id,
  446|      5|            "success": true,
  447|      5|            "summary": "Self-improvement analysis complete.",
  448|      5|            "files_modified": []
  449|       |        });
  450|      2|    }
  451|       |
  452|       |    // For other tools, return an error - they should be handled by Augment
  453|      2|    json!({
  454|      2|        "request_id": request_id,
  455|      2|        "success": false,
  456|      2|        "summary": format!("Tool '{}' is not handled by the self-improver MCP server.", tool_name),
  457|      2|        "files_modified": [],
  458|      2|        "error": format!("Unknown tool: {tool_name}")
  459|       |    })
  460|      7|}
  461|       |
  462|       |/// Drain messages from the self-improver session until completion.
  463|       |///
  464|       |/// Completion can happen in two ways:
  465|       |/// 1. The agent calls the `complete` MCP tool (signaled via `completion_rx`)
  466|       |/// 2. The ACP session finishes (`session_finished`/`agent_turn_finished`)
  467|      0|async fn drain_self_improver_session(
  468|      0|    acp: &mut AcpClient,
  469|      0|    session_id: &str,
  470|      0|    writer: &mut AgentWriter,
  471|      0|    config: &SelfImprovementConfig,
  472|      0|    mut completion_rx: mpsc::Receiver<CompletionSignal>,
  473|      0|) -> Result<SelfImprovementOutcome> {
  474|      0|    let start = std::time::Instant::now();
  475|      0|    let mut final_message: Option<String> = None;
  476|      0|    let mut success = false;
  477|      0|    let mut changes_made: usize = 0;
  478|       |
  479|       |    loop {
  480|       |        // Check timeout
  481|      0|        if start.elapsed() > config.session_timeout {
  482|      0|            tracing::warn!("Self-improvement session timed out");
  483|      0|            return Ok(SelfImprovementOutcome {
  484|      0|                success: false,
  485|      0|                message: Some("Session timed out".to_string()),
  486|      0|                changes_made,
  487|      0|            });
  488|      0|        }
  489|       |
  490|       |        // Use tokio::select to wait for either:
  491|       |        // 1. ACP message from the agent
  492|       |        // 2. Completion signal from the socket handler (when complete tool is called)
  493|      0|        tokio::select! {
  494|       |            // Check for completion signal from socket handler
  495|      0|            Some(signal) = completion_rx.recv() => {
  496|      0|                tracing::info!("✅ Self-improver signaled completion via MCP tool");
  497|      0|                return Ok(SelfImprovementOutcome {
  498|      0|                    success: signal.success,
  499|      0|                    message: signal.message,
  500|      0|                    changes_made,
  501|      0|                });
  502|       |            }
  503|       |
  504|       |            // Check for ACP messages
  505|      0|            result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), acp.recv()) => {
  506|      0|                match result {
  507|      0|                    Ok(Ok(msg)) => {
  508|      0|                        if let Some(update_type) = extract_session_update(&msg, session_id) {
  509|      0|                            match update_type.as_str() {
  510|      0|                                "session_finished" | "agent_turn_finished" => {
  511|      0|                                    tracing::info!("✅ Self-improver session finished");
  512|      0|                                    success = true;
  513|      0|                                    break;
  514|       |                                }
  515|      0|                                "agent_message_chunk" | "agent_thought_chunk" => {
  516|      0|                                    if let Some(text) = extract_message_text(&msg) {
  517|      0|                                        let _ = writer.write_message_chunk(&text).await;
  518|       |                                        // Accumulate for final message
  519|      0|                                        if let Some(ref mut m) = final_message {
  520|      0|                                            m.push_str(&text);
  521|      0|                                        } else {
  522|      0|                                            final_message = Some(text);
  523|      0|                                        }
  524|      0|                                    }
  525|       |                                }
  526|      0|                                "tool_call" => {
  527|      0|                                    if let Some(title) = extract_tool_title(&msg) {
  528|      0|                                        let _ = writer.write_tool_call(&title).await;
  529|      0|                                        tracing::debug!("🔧 Self-improver tool call: {}", title);
  530|       |                                        // Count file-editing tool calls as changes
  531|      0|                                        if is_editing_tool(&title) {
  532|      0|                                            changes_made += 1;
  533|      0|                                        }
  534|      0|                                    }
  535|       |                                }
  536|       |                                // "tool_result" and other message types don't need processing
  537|      0|                                _ => {}
  538|       |                            }
  539|      0|                        }
  540|       |                    }
  541|      0|                    Ok(Err(e)) => {
  542|      0|                        tracing::warn!("Error receiving from self-improver: {}", e);
  543|      0|                        break;
  544|       |                    }
  545|      0|                    Err(_) => {
  546|      0|                        // Timeout on recv, continue loop (will check completion_rx next iteration)
  547|      0|                    }
  548|       |                }
  549|       |            }
  550|       |        }
  551|       |    }
  552|       |
  553|      0|    Ok(SelfImprovementOutcome {
  554|      0|        success,
  555|      0|        message: final_message,
  556|      0|        changes_made,
  557|      0|    })
  558|      0|}
  559|       |
  560|       |/// Extract the session update type from a message.
  561|     12|fn extract_session_update(msg: &Value, expected_session_id: &str) -> Option<String> {
  562|     12|    let params = msg.get("params")?;
                      ^11                       ^1
  563|       |    // Support both ACP format (sessionId) and CLI format (session_id)
  564|     11|    let session_id = params
                      ^10
  565|     11|        .get("sessionId")
  566|     11|        .or_else(|| params.get("session_id"))?
                                  ^2     ^2                ^1
  567|     10|        .as_str()?;
                               ^0
  568|       |
  569|     10|    if session_id != expected_session_id {
  570|      1|        return None;
  571|      9|    }
  572|       |
  573|      9|    let update = params.get("update")?;
                      ^8                           ^1
  574|       |    // Support both ACP format (sessionUpdate) and CLI format (type)
  575|      8|    let session_update = update
  576|      8|        .get("sessionUpdate")
  577|      8|        .or_else(|| update.get("type"))?
                                  ^1     ^1          ^0
  578|      8|        .as_str()?;
                               ^0
  579|      8|    Some(session_update.to_string())
  580|     12|}
  581|       |
  582|       |/// Extract message text from an `agent_message_chunk` or `agent_thought_chunk`.
  583|      6|fn extract_message_text(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
  584|      6|    let content = msg.get("params")?.get("update")?.get("content")?;
                      ^5                         ^0             ^0              ^1
  585|      5|    let text = content.get("text")?.as_str()?;
                      ^4                        ^1^4      ^0
  586|      4|    Some(text.to_string())
  587|      6|}
  588|       |
  589|       |/// Extract tool title from a `tool_call` message.
  590|      5|fn extract_tool_title(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
  591|      5|    let title = msg.get("params")?.get("update")?.get("title")?.as_str()?;
                      ^3                       ^0             ^1^4          ^1^3      ^0
  592|       |    // Strip MCP server prefix if present
  593|      3|    let title = title
  594|      3|        .strip_prefix("mcp__paperboat-selfimprover__")
  595|      3|        .unwrap_or(title);
  596|      3|    Some(title.to_string())
  597|      5|}
  598|       |
  599|       |/// Check if a tool name represents a file-editing operation.
  600|       |///
  601|       |/// Returns true for tools that modify files in the codebase:
  602|       |/// - `str-replace-editor`: Edit existing files
  603|       |/// - `save-file`: Create new files
  604|       |/// - `remove-files`: Delete files
  605|     12|fn is_editing_tool(tool_name: &str) -> bool {
  606|      3|    matches!(
  607|     12|        tool_name,
  608|     12|        "str-replace-editor" | "save-file" | "remove-files"
                                             ^11           ^10
  609|       |    )
  610|     12|}

  747|       |    #[test]
  748|      1|    fn test_ipc_address_generate_unique() {
  749|      1|        let addr1 = IpcAddress::generate("selfimprove");
  750|      1|        let addr2 = IpcAddress::generate("selfimprove");
  751|       |
  752|       |        // Each generated address should be unique
  753|      1|        assert_ne!(addr1.to_string(), addr2.to_string());
  754|       |
  755|       |        // Platform-specific format checks
  756|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  757|       |        {
  758|       |            // On Unix, should be a .sock file in temp directory
  759|      1|            let addr_str = addr1.to_string();
  760|      1|            assert!(addr_str.contains("vl-"));
  761|      1|            assert!(addr_str.ends_with(".sock"));
  762|       |        }
  763|       |
  764|       |        #[cfg(windows)]
  765|       |        {
  766|       |            // On Windows, should be a named pipe path
  767|       |            let addr_str = addr1.to_string();
  768|       |            assert!(addr_str.starts_with(r"\\.\pipe\vl-"));
  769|       |        }
  770|      1|    }

 1004|       |    #[tokio::test]
 1005|      1|    async fn test_handle_selfimprover_request_complete_tool() {
 1006|      1|        let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel::<CompletionSignal>(1);
 1007|       |
 1008|      1|        let arguments = json!({
 1009|      1|            "success": true,
 1010|      1|            "message": "Self-improvement complete"
 1011|       |        });
 1012|       |
 1013|      1|        let response = handle_selfimprover_request("req-001", "complete", &arguments, &tx).await;
 1014|       |
 1015|       |        // Verify response format
 1016|      1|        assert_eq!(response["request_id"], "req-001");
 1017|      1|        assert!(response["success"].as_bool().unwrap());
 1018|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1019|      1|            response["summary"],
 1020|       |            "Self-improvement analysis complete."
 1021|       |        );
 1022|      1|        assert!(response["files_modified"].as_array().unwrap().is_empty());
 1023|       |
 1024|       |        // Verify completion signal was sent
 1025|      1|        let signal = rx.recv().await.unwrap();
 1026|      1|        assert!(signal.success);
 1027|      1|        assert_eq!(signal.message, Some("Self-improvement complete".to_string()));
 1028|      1|    }
 1029|       |
 1030|       |    #[tokio::test]
 1031|      1|    async fn test_handle_selfimprover_request_complete_uppercase() {
 1032|      1|        let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel::<CompletionSignal>(1);
 1033|       |
 1034|      1|        let arguments = json!({
 1035|      1|            "success": false,
 1036|      1|            "message": "Failed to complete"
 1037|       |        });
 1038|       |
 1039|      1|        let response = handle_selfimprover_request("req-002", "Complete", &arguments, &tx).await;
 1040|       |
 1041|      1|        assert!(response["success"].as_bool().unwrap());
 1042|       |
 1043|      1|        let signal = rx.recv().await.unwrap();
 1044|      1|        assert!(!signal.success);
 1045|      1|        assert_eq!(signal.message, Some("Failed to complete".to_string()));
 1046|      1|    }

 1051|       |

 1054|      1|            "message": "Done"

 1111|       |    // Socket Handler Tests (using real IPC sockets)
 1112|       |    // ========================================================================
 1113|       |
 1114|       |    #[tokio::test]
 1115|      1|    async fn test_setup_selfimprover_socket_binds_successfully() {
 1116|      1|        let socket_address = IpcAddress::generate("test-selfimprove-setup");
 1117|      1|        let (completion_tx, _completion_rx) = mpsc::channel::<CompletionSignal>(1);
 1118|       |
 1119|      1|        let handle = setup_selfimprover_socket(&socket_address, completion_tx)
 1120|      1|            .await
 1121|      1|            .expect("Should bind socket successfully");
 1122|       |
 1123|       |        // Socket should exist on Unix
 1124|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
 1125|      1|        assert!(socket_address.exists(), "Socket file should exist");
 1126|       |
 1127|       |        // Clean up
 1128|      1|        handle.cleanup();
 1129|      1|    }
 1130|       |
 1131|       |    #[tokio::test]
 1132|      1|    async fn test_selfimprover_socket_handle_cleanup() {
 1133|      1|        let socket_address = IpcAddress::generate("test-selfimprove-cleanup");
 1134|      1|        let (completion_tx, _completion_rx) = mpsc::channel::<CompletionSignal>(1);
 1135|       |
 1136|      1|        let handle = setup_selfimprover_socket(&socket_address, completion_tx)
 1137|      1|            .await
 1138|      1|            .expect("Should bind socket");
 1139|       |
 1140|      1|        let addr_clone = handle.socket_address.clone();
 1141|      1|        handle.cleanup();
 1142|       |
 1143|       |        // After cleanup, socket should not exist (on Unix)
 1144|      1|        #[cfg(unix)]
 1145|      1|        {
 1146|      1|            // Give a moment for cleanup to complete
 1147|      1|            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10)).await;
 1148|      1|            assert!(!addr_clone.exists(), "Socket file should be cleaned up");
 1149|      1|        }
 1150|      1|    }
 1151|       |
 1152|       |    #[tokio::test]
 1153|      1|    async fn test_selfimprover_socket_receives_complete_tool() {
 1154|       |        use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader};
 1155|       |
 1156|      1|        let socket_address = IpcAddress::generate("test-selfimprove-complete");
 1157|      1|        let (completion_tx, mut completion_rx) = mpsc::channel::<CompletionSignal>(1);
 1158|       |
 1159|      1|        let handle = setup_selfimprover_socket(&socket_address, completion_tx)
 1160|      1|            .await
 1161|      1|            .expect("Should bind socket");
 1162|       |
 1163|       |        // Give the listener task time to start
 1164|      1|        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;
 1165|       |
 1166|       |        // Connect as a client and send a complete tool call
 1167|      1|        let stream = IpcStream::connect(&socket_address)
 1168|      1|            .await
 1169|      1|            .expect("Should connect to socket");
 1170|       |
 1171|      1|        let (reader, mut writer) = stream.into_split();
 1172|      1|        let mut reader = BufReader::new(reader);
 1173|       |
 1174|       |        // Send complete tool call in the expected format
 1175|      1|        let request = json!({
 1176|      1|            "request_id": "test-req-001",
 1177|      1|            "tool_call": {
 1178|      1|                "complete": {
 1179|      1|                    "success": true,
 1180|      1|                    "message": "Socket test complete"
 1181|       |                }
 1182|       |            }
 1183|       |        });
 1184|       |
 1185|      1|        writer
 1186|      1|            .write_all(serde_json::to_string(&request).unwrap().as_bytes())
 1187|      1|            .await
 1188|      1|            .unwrap();
 1189|      1|        writer.write_all(b"\n").await.unwrap();
 1190|      1|        writer.flush().await.unwrap();
 1191|       |
 1192|       |        // Read response
 1193|      1|        let mut response_line = String::new();
 1194|      1|        reader.read_line(&mut response_line).await.unwrap();
 1195|      1|        let response: Value = serde_json::from_str(&response_line).unwrap();
 1196|       |
 1197|      1|        assert!(response["success"].as_bool().unwrap());
 1198|       |
 1199|       |        // Verify completion signal was received
 1200|      1|        let signal = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), completion_rx.recv())
 1201|      1|            .await
 1202|      1|            .expect("Should receive signal within timeout")
 1203|      1|            .expect("Should have signal");
 1204|       |
 1205|      1|        assert!(signal.success);
 1206|      1|        assert_eq!(signal.message, Some("Socket test complete".to_string()));
 1207|       |
 1208|      1|        handle.cleanup();
 1209|      1|    }

 1359|       |
 1360|       |    #[test]
 1361|      1|    fn test_self_improvement_outcome_type_supports_failure_isolation() {
 1362|       |        // This test verifies the Result<Option<Outcome>> pattern
 1363|       |        // that allows self-improvement failures to be isolated
 1364|       |
 1365|       |        // Successful self-improvement
 1366|      1|        let success: Result<Option<SelfImprovementOutcome>> = Ok(Some(SelfImprovementOutcome {
 1367|      1|            success: true,
 1368|      1|            message: Some("Improved".to_string()),
 1369|      1|            changes_made: 1,
 1370|      1|        }));
 1371|      1|        assert!(success.is_ok());
 1372|       |
 1373|       |        // Skipped self-improvement (not in paperboat repo, etc.)
 1374|      1|        let skipped: Result<Option<SelfImprovementOutcome>> = Ok(None);
 1375|      1|        assert!(skipped.is_ok());
 1376|      1|        assert!(skipped.unwrap().is_none());
 1377|       |
 1378|       |        // Failed self-improvement (error that can be logged but doesn't crash main run)
 1379|      1|        let failed: Result<Option<SelfImprovementOutcome>> =
 1380|      1|            Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Self-improvement error"));
 1381|      1|        assert!(failed.is_err());
 1382|       |
 1383|       |        // The caller can handle the error without affecting the main result
 1384|      1|        let main_result_success = true;
 1385|      1|        let self_improve_result: Result<Option<SelfImprovementOutcome>> =
 1386|      1|            Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Error"));
 1387|       |
 1388|       |        // Main result should remain unaffected
 1389|      1|        if let Err(e) = self_improve_result {
 1390|      1|            // Log the error but don't flip the main result
 1391|      1|            let _ = format!("Self-improvement failed (non-fatal): {e}");
 1392|      1|        }
                      ^0
 1393|      1|        assert!(main_result_success);
 1394|      1|    }
 1395|       |
 1396|       |    #[test]
 1397|      1|    fn test_outcome_failure_does_not_affect_success_field() {
 1398|       |        // Even a failed self-improvement can have success: false in outcome
 1399|       |        // without affecting the main run result
 1400|      1|        let outcome = SelfImprovementOutcome {
 1401|      1|            success: false, // Self-improvement failed
 1402|      1|            message: Some("Something went wrong".to_string()),
 1403|      1|            changes_made: 0,
 1404|      1|        };
 1405|       |
 1406|       |        // The outcome itself indicates failure...
 1407|      1|        assert!(!outcome.success);
 1408|       |
 1409|       |        // ...but this doesn't mean the main run failed
 1410|       |        // (that's a separate concern handled by the caller)
 1411|      1|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tasks/manager.rs:
    1|       |//! Task manager for CRUD operations and queries.
    2|       |
    3|       |use super::{Task, TaskId, TaskStatus};
    4|       |use crate::logging::LogEvent;
    5|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
    6|       |use tokio::sync::broadcast;
    7|       |
    8|       |/// The goal summary set by the planner.
    9|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   10|       |pub struct GoalSummary {
   11|       |    /// A concise summary of the user's goal.
   12|       |    pub summary: String,
   13|       |    /// Success criteria / acceptance conditions.
   14|       |    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,
   15|       |}
   16|       |
   17|       |/// A note left by an agent for context sharing.
   18|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   19|       |pub struct AgentNote {
   20|       |    /// The role of the agent that left the note (e.g., "implementer", "verifier").
   21|       |    pub agent_role: String,
   22|       |    /// Optional task ID this note is associated with.
   23|       |    pub task_id: Option<TaskId>,
   24|       |    /// The note content.
   25|       |    pub content: String,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Represents an action that requires human intervention.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Reported by the orchestrator when it determines that something
   31|       |/// cannot be automated and needs manual user action.
   32|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   33|       |pub struct HumanActionItem {
   34|       |    /// Clear description of what the user needs to do.
   35|       |    pub description: String,
   36|       |    /// Optional task ID this action relates to.
   37|       |    pub task_id: Option<String>,
   38|       |}
   39|       |
   40|       |/// Snapshot of `TaskManager` state for nested orchestration.
   41|       |///
   42|       |/// When decomposing tasks, we need to temporarily clear the task manager
   43|       |/// for the nested planner, then restore the parent's state after.
   44|       |#[derive(Clone)]
   45|       |pub struct TaskManagerSnapshot {
   46|       |    tasks: HashMap<TaskId, Task>,
   47|       |    next_task_num: u32,
   48|       |    goal: Option<GoalSummary>,
   49|       |    /// Notes are saved in the snapshot but not restored - we keep notes
   50|       |    /// accumulated during nested orchestration for context.
   51|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   52|       |    notes: Vec<AgentNote>,
   53|       |    /// Depth level to restore when returning from nested orchestration.
   54|       |    depth: u32,
   55|       |}
   56|       |
   57|       |/// Manages tasks for a structured plan.
   58|       |///
   59|       |/// Provides CRUD operations and queries for tasks, including
   60|       |/// dependency tracking and status updates.
   61|       |pub struct TaskManager {
   62|       |    tasks: HashMap<TaskId, Task>,
   63|       |    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<LogEvent>,
   64|       |    /// Counter for generating sequential task IDs (task001, task002, etc.)
   65|       |    next_task_num: u32,
   66|       |    /// The goal summary set by the planner.
   67|       |    goal: Option<GoalSummary>,
   68|       |    /// Notes left by agents for context sharing.
   69|       |    notes: Vec<AgentNote>,
   70|       |    /// Current depth in the task hierarchy (0 = root level).
   71|       |    /// Used when emitting `TaskCreated` events for nested task visualization.
   72|       |    depth: u32,
   73|       |    /// Actions that require human intervention, reported by the orchestrator.
   74|       |    human_actions: Vec<HumanActionItem>,
   75|       |}
   76|       |
   77|       |impl TaskManager {
   78|       |    /// Creates a new `TaskManager` with the given event sender.
   79|     90|    pub fn new(event_tx: broadcast::Sender<LogEvent>) -> Self {
   80|     90|        Self {
   81|     90|            tasks: HashMap::new(),
   82|     90|            event_tx,
   83|     90|            next_task_num: 1,
   84|     90|            goal: None,
   85|     90|            notes: Vec::new(),
   86|     90|            depth: 0,
   87|     90|            human_actions: Vec::new(),
   88|     90|        }
   89|     90|    }
   90|       |
   91|       |    /// Take a snapshot of the current task state for nested orchestration.
   92|       |    ///
   93|       |    /// This saves the current tasks, goal, notes, and depth so they can be restored

  103|      4|    }

  105|       |    /// Clear tasks for nested orchestration.
  106|       |    ///
  107|       |    /// This clears all tasks and resets the task counter, allowing a nested
  108|       |    /// planner to create fresh tasks without ID conflicts.
  109|       |    /// The goal and notes are preserved for context.

  111|       |    /// # Arguments
  112|       |    /// * `new_depth` - The depth level for the nested scope (for task hierarchy visualization)
  113|      4|    pub fn clear_tasks_for_nested(&mut self, new_depth: u32) {
  114|      4|        self.tasks.clear();
  115|      4|        self.next_task_num = 1;
  116|      4|        self.depth = new_depth;

  118|       |            "🔄 Cleared tasks for nested orchestration (depth={})",
  119|       |            new_depth
  120|       |        );
  121|      4|    }
  122|       |

  137|       |            self.depth
  138|       |        );
  139|      4|    }

  247|       |    /// Find task by name (for dependency resolution).

  255|       |    /// Find task by matching name or description.
  256|       |    ///
  257|       |    /// This method performs flexible matching for task inference when an agent
  258|       |    /// is spawned with a task description but no explicit `task_id`.
  259|       |    /// It tries (in order):
  260|       |    /// 1. Exact description match
  261|       |    /// 2. Exact name match
  262|       |    /// 3. Case-insensitive description match
  263|       |    /// 4. Description contains the search text (or vice versa)
  264|       |    ///
  265|       |    /// Returns the task ID if a unique match is found.
  266|     65|    pub fn find_by_name_or_description(&self, text: &str) -> Option<TaskId> {
  267|     65|        let text_lower = text.to_lowercase();
  268|       |
  269|       |        // 1. Exact description match
  270|    131|        if let Some(task) = self.tasks.values().find(|t| t.description == text) {
                                  ^1      ^65                 ^65
  271|      1|            return Some(task.id.clone());
  272|     64|        }
  273|       |
  274|       |        // 2. Exact name match
  275|    125|        if let Some(task) = self.tasks.values().find(|t| t.name == text) {
                                  ^19     ^64                 ^64
  276|     19|            return Some(task.id.clone());
  277|     45|        }
  278|       |
  279|       |        // 3. Case-insensitive description match
  280|     45|        if let Some(task) = self
                                  ^1
  281|     45|            .tasks
  282|     45|            .values()
  283|     98|            .find(|t| t.description.to_lowercase() == text_lower)
                           ^45
  284|       |        {
  285|      1|            return Some(task.id.clone());
  286|     44|        }
  287|       |
  288|       |        // 4. Description contains the search text or vice versa
  289|       |        // Only return if there's exactly one match to avoid ambiguity
  290|     44|        let partial_matches: Vec<_> = self
  291|     44|            .tasks
  292|     44|            .values()
  293|     96|            .filter(|t| {
                           ^44
  294|     96|                let desc_lower = t.description.to_lowercase();
  295|     96|                desc_lower.contains(&text_lower) || text_lower.contains(&desc_lower)
                                                                  ^94
  296|     96|            })
  297|     44|            .collect();
  298|       |
  299|     44|        if partial_matches.len() == 1 {
  300|      2|            return Some(partial_matches[0].id.clone());
  301|     42|        }
  302|       |
  303|     42|        None
  304|     65|    }

  306|       |    /// Get all tasks ready to execute (dependencies complete or skipped).

  321|      5|                            matches!(
  322|     11|                                dep.status,

  324|       |                            )
  325|     11|                        })
  326|     11|                    })

  328|      9|            .collect()

  337|       |    /// Get all pending tasks (tasks in `NotStarted` status).

  339|       |    /// Returns a list of task IDs that have not yet been started. These are

  363|    108|            .collect();

  365|    108|        pending

  411|       |    /// Get all blocked tasks and what they're waiting for.
  412|       |    ///
  413|       |    /// A task is "blocked" when:
  414|       |    /// - It has `NotStarted` status
  415|       |    /// - At least one of its dependencies is not `Complete` or `Skipped`
  416|       |    ///
  417|       |    /// # Returns
  418|       |    ///
  419|       |    /// A vector of tuples (`task_id`, `blocking_task_ids`) where `blocking_task_ids`
  420|       |    /// are the dependencies that are preventing the task from being ready.
  421|       |    /// Results are sorted by task ID for consistent ordering.
  422|       |    ///
  423|       |    /// # Examples
  424|       |    ///
  425|       |    /// ```ignore
  426|       |    /// let blocked = manager.get_blocked_tasks();
  427|       |    /// for (task_id, blockers) in blocked {
  428|       |    ///     println!("{} is waiting on: {:?}", task_id, blockers);
  429|       |    /// }
  430|       |    /// ```
  431|    108|    pub fn get_blocked_tasks(&self) -> Vec<(TaskId, Vec<TaskId>)> {
  432|    108|        let mut blocked: Vec<(TaskId, Vec<TaskId>)> = self
  433|    108|            .tasks
  434|    108|            .values()
  435|    214|            .filter_map(|t| {
                           ^108
  436|    214|                if !matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::NotStarted) {
                                  ^113
  437|    113|                    return None;
  438|    101|                }
  439|       |
  440|       |                // Find which dependencies are blocking this task
  441|    101|                let blockers: Vec<TaskId> = t
  442|    101|                    .dependencies
  443|    101|                    .iter()
  444|    101|                    .filter(|dep_id| {
                                                   ^47
  445|     47|                        !self.tasks.get(*dep_id).is_some_and(|dep| {
  446|     44|                            matches!(
  447|     47|                                dep.status,
  448|       |                                TaskStatus::Complete { .. } | TaskStatus::Skipped { .. }
  449|       |                            )
  450|     47|                        })
  451|     47|                    })
  452|    101|                    .cloned()
  453|    101|                    .collect();
  454|       |
  455|       |                // Only include if there are blockers (otherwise it's a parallel task)
  456|    101|                if blockers.is_empty() {
  457|     58|                    None
  458|       |                } else {
  459|     43|                    Some((t.id.clone(), blockers))
  460|       |                }
  461|    214|            })
  462|    108|            .collect();

  464|    108|        blocked

  467|       |    /// Generate a formatted summary of task state for responses.
  468|       |    ///
  469|       |    /// This method produces a human-readable summary of the current task state,
  470|       |    /// suitable for inclusion in tool responses to help agents understand what
  471|       |    /// work remains and what can be done next.
  472|       |    ///
  473|       |    /// The summary includes:
  474|       |    /// - Total task count and completion statistics
  475|       |    /// - Tasks available for parallel execution
  476|       |    /// - Blocked tasks and their dependencies
  477|       |    /// - Failed tasks (if any)
  478|       |    ///
  479|       |    /// # Returns
  480|       |    ///
  481|       |    /// A formatted string summarizing the task state. Returns a minimal message
  482|       |    /// if there are no tasks.
  483|       |    ///
  484|       |    /// # Examples
  485|       |    ///
  486|       |    /// ```ignore
  487|       |    /// let summary = manager.get_task_summary();
  488|       |    /// // Example output:
  489|       |    /// // "📊 Task Summary: 3/5 complete, 0 failed
  490|       |    /// //
  491|       |    /// // ▶ Ready to execute (can run in parallel):
  492|       |    /// //   - task003: Implement feature
  493|       |    /// //   - task004: Write tests
  494|       |    /// //
  495|       |    /// // ⏳ Blocked tasks:
  496|       |    /// //   - task005 (waiting on: task003, task004)"
  497|       |    /// ```
  498|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
  499|      6|    pub fn get_task_summary(&self) -> String {
  500|      6|        if self.tasks.is_empty() {
  501|      1|            return "📊 No tasks defined.".to_string();
  502|      5|        }
  503|       |
  504|      5|        let total = self.tasks.len();

  506|      5|            .tasks
  507|      5|            .values()
  508|     11|            .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Complete { .. }))

  509|      5|            .count();

  511|      5|            .tasks
  512|      5|            .values()
  513|     11|            .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Failed { .. }))
                           ^5
  514|      5|            .count();
  515|      5|        let skipped = self
  516|      5|            .tasks
  517|      5|            .values()
  518|     11|            .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Skipped { .. }))
                           ^5
  519|      5|            .count();

  521|      5|            .tasks
  522|      5|            .values()
  523|     11|            .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::InProgress { .. }))
                           ^5
  524|      5|            .count();
  525|       |
  526|      5|        let mut lines = vec![format!(
  527|       |            "📊 Task Summary: {complete}/{total} complete, {failed} failed, {skipped} skipped"
  528|       |        )];
  529|       |
  530|       |        // Add in-progress info if any
  531|      5|        if in_progress > 0 {
  532|      1|            lines.push(format!("   ({in_progress} currently in progress)"));
  533|      4|        }
  534|       |

  542|      3|                    lines.push(format!("  - {}: {}", task_id, task.name));
  543|      3|                }
                              ^0
  544|       |            }
  545|      3|        }
  546|       |
  547|       |        // Show blocked tasks
  548|      5|        let blocked = self.get_blocked_tasks();
  549|      5|        if !blocked.is_empty() {
  550|      1|            lines.push(String::new());
  551|      1|            lines.push("⏳ Blocked tasks:".to_string());

  553|      1|                lines.push(format!(
  554|      1|                    "  - {} (waiting on: {})",
  555|      1|                    task_id,
  556|      1|                    blockers.join(", ")
  557|      1|                ));
  558|      1|            }
  559|      4|        }
  560|       |
  561|       |        // Show failed tasks prominently
  562|      5|        if failed > 0 {

  565|      2|            for task in self.tasks.values() {
                                      ^1         ^1

  568|      1|                }
  569|       |            }
  570|      4|        }
  571|       |
  572|       |        // Check if all tasks are complete
  573|      5|        let pending = self.get_pending_tasks();
  574|      5|        if pending.is_empty() && failed == 0 && in_progress == 0 {
                                               ^2             ^2
  575|      2|            lines.push(String::new());

  577|      3|        }
  578|       |
  579|      5|        lines.join("\n")
  580|      6|    }

  731|     79|        if let Some(task) = self.tasks.get(id_or_name) {
                                  ^76

  735|      3|        self.tasks.values().find(|t| t.name == id_or_name)

  777|       |    /// Renumber tasks by execution order.
  778|       |    ///
  779|       |    /// Sorts tasks by (dependency count, original ID) to establish execution order,
  780|       |    /// then assigns new sequential IDs (task001, task002, ...) and updates all
  781|       |    /// dependency references.
  782|       |    ///
  783|       |    /// This ensures task IDs match execution order, making logs easier to follow.
  784|     46|    pub fn renumber_by_execution_order(&mut self) {
  785|     46|        if self.tasks.is_empty() {
  786|      4|            return;
  787|     42|        }
  788|       |
  789|       |        // Sort tasks by execution order: fewer dependencies first, then by original ID
  790|     42|        let mut sorted_tasks: Vec<_> = self.tasks.values().cloned().collect();
  791|     45|        sorted_tasks.sort_by(|a, b| {
                      ^42          ^42
  792|     45|            let a_deps = a.dependencies.len();
  793|     45|            let b_deps = b.dependencies.len();
  794|     45|            a_deps.cmp(&b_deps).then_with(|| a.id.cmp(&b.id))
                                                           ^28  ^28 ^28
  795|     45|        });
  796|       |
  797|       |        // Create mapping from old IDs to new IDs
  798|     42|        let id_mapping: HashMap<TaskId, TaskId> = sorted_tasks
  799|     42|            .iter()
  800|     42|            .enumerate()
  801|     79|            .map(|(i, task)| (task.id.clone(), format!("task{:03}", i + 1)))
                           ^42
  802|     42|            .collect();
  803|       |
  804|       |        // Rebuild tasks with new IDs and updated dependency references
  805|     42|        let mut new_tasks = HashMap::new();
  806|     79|        for task in sorted_tasks {
                                  ^42
  807|     79|            let new_id = id_mapping
  808|     79|                .get(&task.id)
  809|     79|                .expect("task id must exist in id_mapping since we just built it from sorted_tasks")
  810|     79|                .clone();
  811|     79|            let new_deps: Vec<TaskId> = task
  812|     79|                .dependencies
  813|     79|                .iter()
  814|     79|                .filter_map(|dep_id| id_mapping.get(dep_id).cloned())
                                                   ^20        ^20 ^20     ^20
  815|     79|                .collect();
  816|       |
  817|     79|            let new_task = Task {
  818|     79|                id: new_id.clone(),
  819|     79|                name: task.name,
  820|     79|                description: task.description,
  821|     79|                status: task.status,
  822|     79|                dependencies: new_deps,
  823|     79|                created_at: task.created_at,
  824|     79|            };
  825|     79|            new_tasks.insert(new_id, new_task);
  826|       |        }
  827|       |
  828|     42|        self.tasks = new_tasks;
  829|       |        // Truncation safe: task count won't exceed u32::MAX in practice
  830|       |        #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
  831|     42|        {
  832|     42|            self.next_task_num = self.tasks.len() as u32 + 1;
  833|     42|        }
  834|       |
  835|     42|        tracing::debug!(
  836|       |            "📋 Renumbered {} tasks by execution order",
  837|      0|            self.tasks.len()
  838|       |        );
  839|     46|    }
  840|       |
  841|       |    /// Format tasks as a structured plan for the orchestrator.
  842|       |    /// Returns None if no tasks exist, otherwise (`task_count`, `formatted_plan`).
  843|       |    ///
  844|       |    /// The format includes task IDs so the orchestrator can reference them
  845|       |    /// when spawning agents: `[task001] **Name**: Description`
  846|       |    ///
  847|       |    /// Tasks are sorted by task ID which reflects execution order after
  848|       |    /// `renumber_by_execution_order()` has been called.
  849|       |    ///
  850|       |    /// Each task is separated by a blank line for better readability in logs.
  851|     47|    pub fn format_for_orchestrator(&self) -> Option<(usize, String)> {
  852|     47|        if self.tasks.is_empty() {
  853|      4|            return None;
  854|     43|        }
  855|       |
  856|       |        // Sort tasks by ID (which reflects execution order after renumbering)
  857|     43|        let mut tasks: Vec<_> = self.tasks.values().collect();
  858|     46|        tasks.sort_by(|a, b| a.id.cmp(&b.id));
                      ^43   ^43
  859|       |
  860|     43|        let lines: Vec<String> = tasks
  861|     43|            .iter()
  862|     81|            .map(|task| {
                           ^43
  863|     81|                let deps_str = if task.dependencies.is_empty() {
  864|     60|                    String::new()
  865|       |                } else {
  866|     21|                    format!(" (depends on: {})", task.dependencies.join(", "))
  867|       |                };
  868|     81|                format!(
  869|       |                    "[{}] **{}**: {}{}",
  870|       |                    task.id, task.name, task.description, deps_str
  871|       |                )
  872|     81|            })
  873|     43|            .collect();
  874|       |
  875|       |        // Join with double newlines for clear separation between tasks in logs
  876|     43|        Some((tasks.len(), lines.join("\n\n")))
  877|     47|    }

 1032|       |    #[test]
 1033|      1|    fn test_find_by_name_or_description() {
 1034|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1035|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1036|       |
 1037|      1|        let id1 = manager.create(
 1038|      1|            "Fix button styling",
 1039|      1|            "Update button CSS to use new design system colors",
 1040|      1|            vec![],
 1041|       |        );
 1042|      1|        let id2 = manager.create(
 1043|      1|            "Add tests",
 1044|      1|            "Write unit tests for authentication module",
 1045|      1|            vec![],
 1046|       |        );
 1047|       |
 1048|       |        // 1. Exact description match
 1049|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1050|      1|            manager
 1051|      1|                .find_by_name_or_description("Update button CSS to use new design system colors"),
 1052|      1|            Some(id1.clone())
 1053|       |        );
 1054|       |
 1055|       |        // 2. Exact name match
 1056|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1057|      1|            manager.find_by_name_or_description("Add tests"),
 1058|      1|            Some(id2.clone())
 1059|       |        );
 1060|       |
 1061|       |        // 3. Case-insensitive description match
 1062|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1063|      1|            manager
 1064|      1|                .find_by_name_or_description("UPDATE BUTTON CSS TO USE NEW DESIGN SYSTEM COLORS"),
 1065|      1|            Some(id1.clone())
 1066|       |        );
 1067|       |
 1068|       |        // 4. Partial match (description contains search text)
 1069|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1070|      1|            manager.find_by_name_or_description("authentication module"),
 1071|      1|            Some(id2.clone())
 1072|       |        );
 1073|       |
 1074|       |        // 5. Non-existent returns None
 1075|      1|        assert!(manager
 1076|      1|            .find_by_name_or_description("nonexistent task")
 1077|      1|            .is_none());
 1078|       |
 1079|       |        // 6. Ambiguous partial match returns None
 1080|       |        // Both tasks have "t" in them, so searching for "t" would be ambiguous
 1081|       |        // Actually let's test with something more specific
 1082|      1|        let _id3 = manager.create("Refactor code", "Clean up the codebase", vec![]);
 1083|       |        // "code" appears in both id3's name and description - but only one task has it
 1084|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1085|      1|            manager.find_by_name_or_description("code"),
 1086|      1|            Some(_id3.clone())
 1087|       |        );
 1088|      1|    }
 1089|       |
 1090|       |    #[test]
 1091|      1|    fn test_list_task_ids() {
 1092|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1093|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1094|       |
 1095|       |        // Empty manager should return empty list
 1096|      1|        assert!(manager.list_task_ids().is_empty());
 1097|       |
 1098|       |        // Create some tasks
 1099|      1|        manager.create("Task A", "Desc A", vec![]);
 1100|      1|        manager.create("Task B", "Desc B", vec![]);
 1101|      1|        manager.create("Task C", "Desc C", vec![]);
 1102|       |
 1103|      1|        let ids = manager.list_task_ids();
 1104|      1|        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 3);
 1105|      1|        assert!(ids.contains(&"task001".to_string()));
 1106|      1|        assert!(ids.contains(&"task002".to_string()));
 1107|      1|        assert!(ids.contains(&"task003".to_string()));
 1108|      1|    }

 1110|       |    #[test]
 1111|      1|    fn test_format_for_orchestrator() {
 1112|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1113|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1114|       |
 1115|      1|        manager.create("Task A", "First task", vec![]);
 1116|      1|        manager.create("Task B", "Second task", vec!["Task A".to_string()]);
 1117|       |
 1118|      1|        let (count, formatted) = manager.format_for_orchestrator().unwrap();
 1119|      1|        assert_eq!(count, 2);
 1120|       |
 1121|       |        // Task A has no deps, should come first
 1122|      1|        assert!(formatted.contains("[task001] **Task A**: First task"));
 1123|       |        // Task B has deps, should include them
 1124|      1|        assert!(formatted.contains("[task002] **Task B**: Second task (depends on: task001)"));
 1125|       |        // Tasks should be separated by double newlines for log readability
 1126|      1|        assert!(
 1127|      1|            formatted.contains("\n\n"),
 1128|       |            "Expected double newline between tasks"
 1129|       |        );
 1130|      1|    }
 1131|       |
 1132|       |    #[test]
 1133|      1|    fn test_update_status_emits_task_state_changed_event() {
 1134|       |        use crate::logging::LogEvent;
 1135|       |
 1136|      1|        let (tx, mut rx) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1137|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1138|       |
 1139|       |        // Create a task
 1140|      1|        let id = manager.create("Test Task", "Description", vec![]);
 1141|       |
 1142|       |        // Drain the TaskCreated event
 1143|      1|        let _ = rx.try_recv();
 1144|       |
 1145|       |        // Update status to InProgress
 1146|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1147|      1|            &id,
 1148|      1|            &TaskStatus::InProgress {
 1149|      1|                agent_session: Some("impl-001".to_string()),
 1150|      1|            },
 1151|       |        );
 1152|       |
 1153|       |        // Verify TaskStateChanged event was emitted
 1154|      1|        let event = rx.try_recv().expect("Expected TaskStateChanged event");
 1155|      1|        match event {
 1156|       |            LogEvent::TaskStateChanged {
 1157|      1|                task_id,
 1158|      1|                name,
 1159|      1|                old_status,
 1160|      1|                new_status,
 1161|       |                ..
 1162|       |            } => {
 1163|      1|                assert_eq!(task_id, "task001");
 1164|      1|                assert_eq!(name, "Test Task");
 1165|      1|                assert_eq!(old_status, "pending");
 1166|      1|                assert_eq!(new_status, "in_progress");
 1167|       |            }
 1168|      0|            _ => panic!("Expected TaskStateChanged event"),
 1169|       |        }
 1170|       |
 1171|       |        // Update status to Complete
 1172|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1173|      1|            &id,
 1174|      1|            &TaskStatus::Complete {
 1175|      1|                success: true,
 1176|      1|                summary: "Done".to_string(),
 1177|      1|            },
 1178|       |        );
 1179|       |
 1180|      1|        let event = rx.try_recv().expect("Expected TaskStateChanged event");
 1181|      1|        match event {
 1182|       |            LogEvent::TaskStateChanged {
 1183|      1|                old_status,
 1184|      1|                new_status,
 1185|       |                ..
 1186|       |            } => {
 1187|      1|                assert_eq!(old_status, "in_progress");
 1188|      1|                assert_eq!(new_status, "completed");
 1189|       |            }
 1190|      0|            _ => panic!("Expected TaskStateChanged event"),
 1191|       |        }
 1192|      1|    }
 1193|       |
 1194|       |    #[test]
 1195|      1|    fn test_update_status_emits_failed_state() {
 1196|       |        use crate::logging::LogEvent;
 1197|       |
 1198|      1|        let (tx, mut rx) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1199|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1200|       |
 1201|      1|        let id = manager.create("Failing Task", "Will fail", vec![]);
 1202|       |
 1203|       |        // Drain the TaskCreated event
 1204|      1|        let _ = rx.try_recv();
 1205|       |
 1206|       |        // Go to InProgress first
 1207|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1208|      1|            &id,
 1209|      1|            &TaskStatus::InProgress {
 1210|      1|                agent_session: None,
 1211|      1|            },
 1212|       |        );
 1213|      1|        let _ = rx.try_recv(); // Drain InProgress event
 1214|       |
 1215|       |        // Update to Failed
 1216|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1217|      1|            &id,
 1218|      1|            &TaskStatus::Failed {
 1219|      1|                error: "Something went wrong".to_string(),
 1220|      1|            },
 1221|       |        );
 1222|       |
 1223|      1|        let event = rx.try_recv().expect("Expected TaskStateChanged event");
 1224|      1|        match event {
 1225|       |            LogEvent::TaskStateChanged {
 1226|      1|                old_status,
 1227|      1|                new_status,
 1228|       |                ..
 1229|       |            } => {
 1230|      1|                assert_eq!(old_status, "in_progress");
 1231|      1|                assert_eq!(new_status, "failed");
 1232|       |            }
 1233|      0|            _ => panic!("Expected TaskStateChanged event"),
 1234|       |        }
 1235|      1|    }

 1237|       |    #[test]
 1238|      1|    fn test_update_status_emits_skipped_state() {
 1239|       |        use crate::logging::LogEvent;
 1240|       |
 1241|      1|        let (tx, mut rx) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1242|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1243|       |
 1244|      1|        let id = manager.create("Skipped Task", "Will be skipped", vec![]);
 1245|       |
 1246|       |        // Drain the TaskCreated event
 1247|      1|        let _ = rx.try_recv();
 1248|       |
 1249|       |        // Update to Skipped
 1250|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1251|      1|            &id,
 1252|      1|            &TaskStatus::Skipped {
 1253|      1|                reason: "No longer needed".to_string(),
 1254|      1|            },
 1255|       |        );
 1256|       |
 1257|      1|        let event = rx.try_recv().expect("Expected TaskStateChanged event");
 1258|      1|        match event {
 1259|       |            LogEvent::TaskStateChanged {
 1260|      1|                task_id,
 1261|      1|                name,
 1262|      1|                old_status,
 1263|      1|                new_status,
 1264|       |                ..
 1265|       |            } => {
 1266|      1|                assert_eq!(task_id, "task001");
 1267|      1|                assert_eq!(name, "Skipped Task");
 1268|      1|                assert_eq!(old_status, "pending");
 1269|      1|                assert_eq!(new_status, "skipped");
 1270|       |            }
 1271|      0|            _ => panic!("Expected TaskStateChanged event"),
 1272|       |        }
 1273|      1|    }

 1275|       |    #[test]
 1276|      1|    fn test_get_ready_tasks_with_skipped_dependency() {
 1277|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1278|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1279|       |
 1280|      1|        let id_a = manager.create("Task A", "First task", vec![]);
 1281|      1|        manager.create("Task B", "Depends on A", vec!["Task A".to_string()]);
 1282|       |
 1283|       |        // Initially only Task A is ready (no dependencies)
 1284|      1|        assert_eq!(manager.get_ready_tasks().len(), 1);
 1285|      1|        assert_eq!(manager.get_ready_tasks()[0].name, "Task A");
 1286|       |
 1287|       |        // Skip Task A
 1288|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1289|      1|            &id_a,
 1290|      1|            &TaskStatus::Skipped {
 1291|      1|                reason: "Not needed".to_string(),
 1292|      1|            },
 1293|       |        );
 1294|       |
 1295|       |        // Now Task B should be ready since its dependency is skipped (resolved)
 1296|      1|        let ready = manager.get_ready_tasks();
 1297|      1|        assert_eq!(ready.len(), 1);
 1298|      1|        assert_eq!(ready[0].name, "Task B");
 1299|      1|    }
 1300|       |
 1301|       |    #[test]
 1302|      1|    fn test_get_ready_tasks_with_mixed_resolved_dependencies() {
 1303|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1304|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1305|       |
 1306|      1|        let id_a = manager.create("Task A", "Will be completed", vec![]);
 1307|      1|        let id_b = manager.create("Task B", "Will be skipped", vec![]);
 1308|      1|        manager.create(
 1309|      1|            "Task C",
 1310|      1|            "Depends on A and B",
 1311|      1|            vec!["Task A".to_string(), "Task B".to_string()],
 1312|       |        );
 1313|       |
 1314|       |        // Initially only Task A and B are ready
 1315|      1|        assert_eq!(manager.get_ready_tasks().len(), 2);
 1316|       |
 1317|       |        // Complete Task A
 1318|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1319|      1|            &id_a,
 1320|      1|            &TaskStatus::Complete {
 1321|      1|                success: true,
 1322|      1|                summary: "Done".to_string(),
 1323|      1|            },
 1324|       |        );
 1325|       |
 1326|       |        // Task C still not ready (B is not resolved)
 1327|      1|        let ready = manager.get_ready_tasks();
 1328|      1|        assert_eq!(ready.len(), 1);
 1329|      1|        assert_eq!(ready[0].name, "Task B");
 1330|       |
 1331|       |        // Skip Task B
 1332|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1333|      1|            &id_b,
 1334|      1|            &TaskStatus::Skipped {
 1335|      1|                reason: "Not applicable".to_string(),
 1336|      1|            },
 1337|       |        );
 1338|       |
 1339|       |        // Now Task C should be ready (both dependencies resolved - one complete, one skipped)
 1340|      1|        let ready = manager.get_ready_tasks();
 1341|      1|        assert_eq!(ready.len(), 1);
 1342|      1|        assert_eq!(ready[0].name, "Task C");
 1343|      1|    }

 1355|       |    #[test]
 1356|      1|    fn test_get_pending_tasks() {
 1357|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1358|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1359|       |
 1360|      1|        let id_a = manager.create("Task A", "First task", vec![]);
 1361|      1|        let id_b = manager.create("Task B", "Second task", vec![]);
 1362|      1|        manager.create("Task C", "Third task", vec![]);
 1363|       |
 1364|       |        // All should be pending initially
 1365|      1|        let pending = manager.get_pending_tasks();
 1366|      1|        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 3);
 1367|       |
 1368|       |        // Start Task A
 1369|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1370|      1|            &id_a,
 1371|      1|            &TaskStatus::InProgress {
 1372|      1|                agent_session: None,
 1373|      1|            },
 1374|       |        );
 1375|       |
 1376|       |        // Now 2 pending
 1377|      1|        let pending = manager.get_pending_tasks();
 1378|      1|        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 2);
 1379|      1|        assert!(!pending.contains(&id_a));
 1380|      1|        assert!(pending.contains(&id_b));
 1381|       |
 1382|       |        // Complete Task B
 1383|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1384|      1|            &id_b,
 1385|      1|            &TaskStatus::Complete {
 1386|      1|                success: true,
 1387|      1|                summary: "Done".to_string(),
 1388|      1|            },
 1389|       |        );
 1390|       |
 1391|       |        // Now 1 pending
 1392|      1|        let pending = manager.get_pending_tasks();
 1393|      1|        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 1);
 1394|      1|        assert_eq!(pending[0], "task003");
 1395|      1|    }

 1417|       |    #[test]
 1418|      1|    fn test_get_parallel_tasks_with_deps() {
 1419|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1420|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1421|       |
 1422|      1|        let id_a = manager.create("Task A", "No deps", vec![]);
 1423|      1|        manager.create("Task B", "Depends on A", vec!["Task A".to_string()]);
 1424|      1|        manager.create("Task C", "Also depends on A", vec!["Task A".to_string()]);
 1425|       |
 1426|       |        // Only Task A is parallel-ready initially
 1427|      1|        let parallel = manager.get_parallel_tasks();
 1428|      1|        assert_eq!(parallel.len(), 1);
 1429|      1|        assert_eq!(parallel[0], "task001");
 1430|       |
 1431|       |        // Complete Task A
 1432|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1433|      1|            &id_a,
 1434|      1|            &TaskStatus::Complete {
 1435|      1|                success: true,
 1436|      1|                summary: "Done".to_string(),
 1437|      1|            },
 1438|       |        );
 1439|       |
 1440|       |        // Now B and C are parallel-ready
 1441|      1|        let parallel = manager.get_parallel_tasks();
 1442|      1|        assert_eq!(parallel.len(), 2);

 1453|       |    #[test]
 1454|      1|    fn test_get_blocked_tasks_no_deps() {
 1455|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1456|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1457|       |
 1458|      1|        manager.create("Task A", "First task", vec![]);
 1459|      1|        manager.create("Task B", "Second task", vec![]);
 1460|       |
 1461|       |        // No blocked tasks when there are no dependencies
 1462|      1|        let blocked = manager.get_blocked_tasks();
 1463|      1|        assert!(blocked.is_empty());
 1464|      1|    }
 1465|       |
 1466|       |    #[test]
 1467|      1|    fn test_get_blocked_tasks_with_deps() {
 1468|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1469|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1470|       |
 1471|      1|        let id_a = manager.create("Task A", "No deps", vec![]);
 1472|      1|        let id_b = manager.create("Task B", "Depends on A", vec!["Task A".to_string()]);
 1473|      1|        manager.create(
 1474|      1|            "Task C",
 1475|      1|            "Depends on A and B",
 1476|      1|            vec!["Task A".to_string(), "Task B".to_string()],
 1477|       |        );
 1478|       |
 1479|       |        // B is blocked on A, C is blocked on A and B
 1480|      1|        let blocked = manager.get_blocked_tasks();
 1481|      1|        assert_eq!(blocked.len(), 2);
 1482|       |
 1483|       |        // Find task B's blockers
 1484|      1|        let task_b_entry = blocked.iter().find(|(id, _)| id == &id_b).unwrap();
 1485|      1|        assert_eq!(task_b_entry.1.len(), 1);
 1486|      1|        assert!(task_b_entry.1.contains(&id_a));
 1487|       |
 1488|       |        // Find task C's blockers
 1489|      2|        let task_c_entry = blocked.iter().find(|(id, _)| id == "task003").unwrap();
                          ^1             ^1             ^1                              ^1
 1490|      1|        assert_eq!(task_c_entry.1.len(), 2);
 1491|       |
 1492|       |        // Complete Task A
 1493|      1|        manager.update_status(
 1494|      1|            &id_a,
 1495|      1|            &TaskStatus::Complete {
 1496|      1|                success: true,
 1497|      1|                summary: "Done".to_string(),
 1498|      1|            },
 1499|       |        );
 1500|       |
 1501|       |        // Now only C is blocked (on B)
 1502|      1|        let blocked = manager.get_blocked_tasks();
 1503|      1|        assert_eq!(blocked.len(), 1);
 1504|      1|        assert_eq!(blocked[0].0, "task003");
 1505|      1|        assert_eq!(blocked[0].1.len(), 1);
 1506|      1|        assert!(blocked[0].1.contains(&id_b));
 1507|      1|    }

 1587|       |    #[test]
 1588|      1|    fn test_get_task_summary_all_complete() {
 1589|      1|        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(10);
 1590|      1|        let mut manager = TaskManager::new(tx);
 1591|       |
