/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tasks/mod.rs:
    1|       |//! Task tracking for structured plans.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides types for tracking tasks in a structured plan.
    4|       |//! Tasks have dependencies, statuses, and can be managed through the [`TaskManager`].
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Task Lifecycle
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Tasks progress through the following status transitions:
    9|       |//!
   10|       |//! ```text
   11|       |//! NotStarted ──┬──▶ InProgress ──┬──▶ Complete
   12|       |//!              │                 └──▶ Failed
   13|       |//!              │
   14|       |//!              └──▶ Skipped (via skip_tasks or reconciliation)
   15|       |//! ```
   16|       |//!
   17|       |//! ## Status Descriptions
   18|       |//!
   19|       |//! - **`NotStarted`**: Task has been created but no agent has been assigned to work on it.
   20|       |//! - **`InProgress`**: An agent is actively working on this task. Tracks the agent's session ID.
   21|       |//! - **`Complete`**: Task finished successfully. Includes a summary of what was accomplished.
   22|       |//! - **`Failed`**: Task encountered an error. Includes an error description.
   23|       |//! - **`Skipped`**: Task was explicitly skipped. This can happen in two ways:
   24|       |//!   - **Orchestrator skip**: The orchestrator calls `skip_tasks` to mark tasks as no longer
   25|       |//!     needed (e.g., already done by another task, no longer relevant, or blocked).
   26|       |//!   - **Reconciliation skip**: When a nested orchestrator completes (via `decompose`),
   27|       |//!     any remaining `NotStarted` child tasks are automatically marked as `Skipped`
   28|       |//!     before restoring the parent's task state.
   29|       |//!
   30|       |//! # Reconciliation at Completion
   31|       |//!
   32|       |//! When an orchestrator attempts to complete with `success=true`, the system performs
   33|       |//! reconciliation to ensure all tasks have a definitive final status:
   34|       |//!
   35|       |//! 1. Check for any tasks still in `NotStarted` status
   36|       |//! 2. If pending tasks exist, reject the completion and prompt the orchestrator to either:
   37|       |//!    - Spawn agents to execute the remaining tasks, or
   38|       |//!    - Call `skip_tasks` to explicitly skip tasks that are not needed
   39|       |//! 3. Only allow completion when all tasks are in a terminal state
   40|       |//!
   41|       |//! This ensures the task list provides an accurate audit trail of what was done.
   42|       |
   43|       |mod manager;
   44|       |
   45|       |pub use manager::{TaskManager, TaskManagerSnapshot};
   46|       |
   47|       |use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   48|       |use std::time::Instant;
   49|       |
   50|       |/// Unique identifier for a task.
   51|       |pub type TaskId = String;
   52|       |
   53|       |/// A task in a structured plan.
   54|       |///
   55|       |/// Tasks represent units of work that can be tracked, have dependencies
   56|       |/// on other tasks, and go through various status transitions.
   57|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   58|       |pub struct Task {
   59|       |    /// Unique identifier for this task.
   60|       |    pub id: TaskId,
   61|       |    /// Human-readable name for the task.
   62|       |    pub name: String,
   63|       |    /// Detailed description of what needs to be done.
   64|       |    pub description: String,
   65|       |    /// Current status of the task.
   66|       |    pub status: TaskStatus,
   67|       |    /// IDs of tasks that must complete before this task can start.
   68|       |    pub dependencies: Vec<TaskId>,
   69|       |    /// When this task was created (not serialized).
   70|       |    /// Note: Part of the API for future use, not yet read in production code.
   71|       |    #[serde(skip)]
   72|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   73|       |    pub created_at: Option<Instant>,
   74|       |}
   75|       |
   76|       |/// Status of a task in its lifecycle.
   77|       |///
   78|       |/// Tasks progress through these statuses as work is performed. See the module-level
   79|       |/// documentation for the complete lifecycle diagram and transition rules.
   80|       |///
   81|       |/// The status is serialized with a `type` tag for clean JSON representation.
   82|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   83|       |#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   84|       |pub enum TaskStatus {
   85|       |    /// Task has not been started yet.
   86|       |    ///
   87|       |    /// This is the initial state for all tasks. Tasks remain in this state until
   88|       |    /// an agent is spawned to work on them.
   89|       |    #[default]
   90|       |    NotStarted,
   91|       |    /// Task is currently being worked on.
   92|       |    ///
   93|       |    /// Set when an agent begins working on a task. The `agent_session` field tracks
   94|       |    /// which agent is working on the task for debugging and logging purposes.
   95|       |    InProgress {
   96|       |        /// Session ID of the agent working on this task, if any.
   97|       |        agent_session: Option<String>,
   98|       |    },
   99|       |    /// Task completed successfully.
  100|       |    ///
  101|       |    /// Set when an agent finishes its work. The `success` field indicates whether
  102|       |    /// the work was successful, and `summary` provides details about what was done.
  103|       |    Complete {
  104|       |        /// Whether the task was successful.
  105|       |        success: bool,
  106|       |        /// Summary of what was accomplished.
  107|       |        summary: String,
  108|       |    },
  109|       |    /// Task failed with an error.
  110|       |    ///
  111|       |    /// Set when a task encounters an unrecoverable error during execution.
  112|       |    Failed {
  113|       |        /// Description of what went wrong.
  114|       |        error: String,
  115|       |    },
  116|       |    /// Task was explicitly skipped and will not be executed.
  117|       |    ///
  118|       |    /// This status indicates the task was deliberately not pursued. Common reasons:
  119|       |    /// - Already completed by another task
  120|       |    /// - No longer relevant to the goal
  121|       |    /// - Blocked by external factors
  122|       |    /// - Nested orchestrator completed without addressing all child tasks
  123|       |    ///
  124|       |    /// The `reason` field provides context for why the task was skipped.
  125|       |    Skipped {
  126|       |        /// Reason why the task was skipped.
  127|       |        reason: String,
  128|       |    },
  129|       |}
  130|       |
  131|       |impl TaskStatus {
  132|       |    /// Returns a simple string representation suitable for display in TUI.
  133|       |    ///
  134|       |    /// Returns one of: `pending`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `failed`, or `skipped`.
  135|    626|    pub const fn as_display_str(&self) -> &'static str {
  136|    626|        match self {
  137|    157|            Self::NotStarted => "pending",
  138|     88|            Self::InProgress { .. } => "in_progress",
  139|    201|            Self::Complete { .. } => "completed",
  140|     15|            Self::Failed { .. } => "failed",
  141|    165|            Self::Skipped { .. } => "skipped",
  142|       |        }
  143|    626|    }
  144|       |}

   26|       |
   27|       |/// Test harness that wraps the App with mock clients and tool interceptors.
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// Provides a controlled environment for integration testing where:
   30|       |/// - ACP sessions return scripted updates from the scenario
   31|       |/// - Tool calls (decompose, implement, complete, `write_plan`) are intercepted
   32|       |/// - All interactions are captured for assertions
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Example
   35|       |///
   36|       |/// ```rust,ignore
   37|       |/// use paperboat::testing::{TestHarness, MockScenario};
   38|       |///
   39|       |/// let scenario = MockScenario::from_file("tests/scenarios/simple_implement.toml")?;
   40|       |/// let harness = TestHarness::with_scenario(scenario);
   41|       |/// let result = harness.run_goal("Add a new feature").await?;
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// assert!(result.task_result.success);
   44|       |/// assert!(result.planner_was_spawned());
   45|       |/// assert!(!result.implement_calls().is_empty());
   46|       |/// ```
   47|       |pub struct TestHarness {
   48|       |    /// The mock scenario being used.

   52|       |    /// Timeout for test runs.
   53|       |    timeout: Duration,
   54|       |    /// Temporary directory for test logs (cleaned up on drop).

   56|       |}

  255|       |    };

  261|       |    };

    1|       |//! Mock tool interceptor for testing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides `MockToolInterceptor` which intercepts tool calls
    4|       |//! and returns scripted responses from test scenarios.
    5|       |
    6|       |use super::assertions::CapturedToolCall;
    7|       |use super::{MockScenario, MockToolType};
    8|       |use crate::mcp_server::{ToolCall, ToolResponse};
    9|       |use anyhow::Result;
   10|       |use std::collections::VecDeque;
   11|       |
   12|       |const PENDING_APP_RESPONSE_ERROR: &str = "__pending_app_tool_response__";
   13|       |
   14|       |// ============================================================================
   15|       |// Mock Tool Interceptor
   16|       |// ============================================================================
   17|       |
   18|       |/// Thread-safe interceptor for tool calls that provides scripted responses.
   19|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   20|       |pub struct MockToolInterceptor {
   21|       |    /// Queue of tool responses to return (consumed in order).
   22|       |    pub(crate) response_queue: VecDeque<(MockToolType, crate::testing::MockToolResponseData)>,
   23|       |    /// Legacy captured tool calls based on scripted mock responses.
   24|       |    captured_calls: Vec<CapturedToolCall>,
   25|       |    /// Captured tool calls with the actual responses returned by the app.
   26|       |    app_captured_calls: Vec<CapturedToolCall>,
   27|       |    /// Counter for tracking exhaustion errors.
   28|       |    exhausted_count: usize,
   29|       |}
   30|       |
   31|       |impl MockToolInterceptor {
   32|       |    /// Create a new interceptor from a scenario's `mock_tool_responses`.
   33|     47|    pub fn from_scenario(scenario: &MockScenario) -> Self {
   34|     47|        let response_queue = scenario
   35|     47|            .mock_tool_responses
   36|     47|            .iter()
   37|     64|            .map(|r| (r.tool_type, r.response.clone()))
                           ^47
   38|     47|            .collect();
   39|       |
   40|     47|        Self {
   41|     47|            response_queue,
   42|     47|            captured_calls: Vec::new(),
   43|     47|            app_captured_calls: Vec::new(),
   44|     47|            exhausted_count: 0,
   45|     47|        }
   46|     47|    }
   47|       |
   48|       |    /// Create an empty interceptor.
   49|      5|    pub const fn empty() -> Self {
   50|      5|        Self {
   51|      5|            response_queue: VecDeque::new(),
   52|      5|            captured_calls: Vec::new(),
   53|      5|            app_captured_calls: Vec::new(),
   54|      5|            exhausted_count: 0,
   55|      5|        }
   56|      5|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Check if all responses have been consumed.
   59|      2|    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
   60|      2|        self.response_queue.is_empty()
   61|      2|    }
   62|       |
   63|       |    /// Get the number of times we tried to get a response when exhausted.
   64|      1|    pub const fn exhausted_count(&self) -> usize {
   65|      1|        self.exhausted_count
   66|      1|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    /// Get all captured tool calls.
   69|     42|    pub fn captured_calls(&self) -> &[CapturedToolCall] {
   70|     42|        &self.captured_calls

    1|       |//! Mock ACP client for testing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides a mock implementation of `AcpClientTrait` that returns scripted
    4|       |//! responses from a `MockScenario`, enabling deterministic testing without
    5|       |//! requiring a live agent process.
    6|       |
    7|       |use crate::acp::{AcpClientTrait, SessionMode, SessionNewResponse};
    8|       |use crate::app::ToolMessage;
    9|       |use crate::mcp_server::{AgentSpec, ToolCall, ToolRequest, ToolResponse, WaitMode};
   10|       |use crate::testing::{
   11|       |    AgentType, MockAcpResponse, MockAgentSession, MockMcpToolCall, MockScenario, MockSessionUpdate,
   12|       |};
   13|       |use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
   14|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   15|       |use serde_json::{json, Value};
   16|       |use std::collections::VecDeque;
   17|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   18|       |use tokio::sync::{mpsc, Mutex};
   19|       |
   20|       |/// A session update paired with its optional associated tool call.
   21|       |/// Tool calls are injected when the corresponding update is returned.
   22|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   23|       |struct QueuedUpdate {
   24|       |    /// The ACP notification to return from `recv()`.
   25|       |    notification: Value,
   26|       |    /// Optional tool call to inject when this update is returned.
   27|       |    tool_call: Option<MockMcpToolCall>,
   28|       |}
   29|       |
   30|       |/// Mock ACP client that returns scripted responses.
   31|       |///
   32|       |/// This client simulates ACP behavior using data from a `MockScenario`,
   33|       |/// allowing tests to run without spawning actual agent processes.
   34|       |pub struct MockAcpClient {
   35|       |    /// Session-specific update queues, keyed by session ID.
   36|       |    /// This ensures updates for different sessions don't get interleaved,
   37|       |    /// which is critical for nested orchestrator scenarios (e.g., decompose).
   38|       |    session_queues: std::collections::HashMap<String, VecDeque<QueuedUpdate>>,
   39|       |
   40|       |    /// Stack of active session IDs. The top of the stack is the current session.
   41|       |    /// This tracks which session's updates should be returned by `recv()`.
   42|       |    active_sessions: Vec<String>,
   43|       |
   44|       |    /// Counter for generating unique session IDs.
   45|       |    session_counter: usize,
   46|       |
   47|       |    /// Captured prompts for assertion: (`session_id`, prompt).
   48|       |    captured_prompts: Vec<(String, String)>,
   49|       |
   50|       |    /// Reference sessions from the scenario (by agent type).
   51|       |    planner_sessions: Vec<MockAgentSession>,
   52|       |    orchestrator_sessions: Vec<MockAgentSession>,
   53|       |    implementer_sessions: Vec<MockAgentSession>,
   54|       |
   55|       |    /// Index tracking for each session type.
   56|       |    planner_index: usize,
   57|       |    orchestrator_index: usize,
   58|       |    implementer_index: usize,
   59|       |
   60|       |    /// Track created sessions for test assertions.
   61|       |    sessions_created: Vec<String>,
   62|       |
   63|       |    /// Scripted ACP method responses (for retry/fallback/error-path tests).
   64|       |    acp_responses: VecDeque<MockAcpResponse>,
   65|       |
   66|       |    /// Flag indicating shutdown was called.
   67|       |    shutdown_called: bool,
   68|       |
   69|       |    /// Channel to send tool calls to the App (for test mode).
   70|       |    tool_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<ToolMessage>>,
   71|       |
   72|       |    /// Mock tool interceptor for getting responses (shared with test harness).
   73|       |    tool_interceptor: Option<Arc<Mutex<super::MockToolInterceptor>>>,
   74|       |}
   75|       |
   76|       |impl MockAcpClient {
   77|       |    /// Create a new mock ACP client from a scenario.
   78|    155|    pub fn from_scenario(scenario: &MockScenario) -> Self {
   79|    155|        Self {
   80|    155|            session_queues: std::collections::HashMap::new(),
   81|    155|            active_sessions: Vec::new(),
   82|    155|            session_counter: 0,
   83|    155|            captured_prompts: Vec::new(),
   84|    155|            planner_sessions: scenario.planner_sessions.clone(),
   85|    155|            orchestrator_sessions: scenario.orchestrator_sessions.clone(),
   86|    155|            implementer_sessions: scenario.implementer_sessions.clone(),
   87|    155|            planner_index: 0,
   88|    155|            orchestrator_index: 0,
   89|    155|            implementer_index: 0,
   90|    155|            sessions_created: Vec::new(),
   91|    155|            acp_responses: scenario.mock_acp_responses.clone().into(),
   92|    155|            shutdown_called: false,
   93|    155|            tool_tx: None,
   94|    155|            tool_interceptor: None,
   95|    155|        }
   96|    155|    }
   97|       |
   98|       |    /// Create an empty mock client (no sessions).
   99|      2|    pub fn empty() -> Self {
  100|      2|        Self::from_scenario(&MockScenario::default())
  101|      2|    }
  102|       |
  103|       |    /// Set the tool channel for injecting tool calls.
  104|    129|    pub fn with_tool_channel(
  105|    129|        mut self,
  106|    129|        tool_tx: mpsc::Sender<ToolMessage>,
  107|    129|        tool_interceptor: Arc<Mutex<super::MockToolInterceptor>>,

  117|      2|    }
  118|       |
  119|       |    /// Get the number of sessions created.
  120|      5|    pub const fn sessions_created_count(&self) -> usize {
  121|      5|        self.sessions_created.len()
  122|      5|    }

  191|       |                        }

  193|       |                }
  194|      0|            }

  198|      0|                if name.contains("planner") {
  199|      0|                    return AgentType::Planner;
  200|      0|                } else if name.contains("implementer") {

  220|       |    /// Queue updates from a session for the given `session_id`.
  221|       |    /// Each update is paired with its optional associated tool call.
  222|       |    /// Updates are stored in a session-specific queue to prevent interleaving.
  223|    163|    fn queue_updates(&mut self, session_id: &str, updates: &[MockSessionUpdate]) {
  224|    163|        let queue = self
  225|    163|            .session_queues

                                    ^163

  231|    718|                notification,
  232|    718|                tool_call: update.inject_mcp_tool_call.clone(),
  233|    718|            });
  234|    718|        }
  235|       |        // Push this session to the active sessions stack
  236|    163|        self.active_sessions.push(session_id.to_string());
  237|    163|    }

  380|       |/// Convert a `MockSessionUpdate` to an ACP JSON-RPC notification format.
  381|    718|fn mock_session_update_to_notification(session_id: &str, update: &MockSessionUpdate) -> Value {
  382|    718|    let mut notification = json!({
  383|    718|        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  384|    718|        "method": "session/update",
  385|    718|        "params": {
  386|    718|            "sessionId": session_id,
  387|    718|            "update": {
  388|    718|                "sessionUpdate": update.session_update
  389|       |            }
  390|       |        }
  391|       |    });
  392|       |
  393|       |    // Add content if present (for message chunks)
  394|    718|    if let Some(content) = &update.content {
                              ^479
  395|    479|        notification["params"]["update"]["content"] = json!({
  396|    479|            "type": "text",
  397|    479|            "text": content
  398|    479|        });
  399|    479|    }
                  ^239
  400|       |
  401|       |    // Add tool title if present (for tool_call updates)
  402|    718|    if let Some(tool_title) = &update.tool_title {
                              ^41
  403|     41|        notification["params"]["update"]["title"] = json!(tool_title);
  404|    677|    }
  405|       |
  406|       |    // Add tool result if present (for tool_result updates)
  407|    718|    if let Some(tool_result) = &update.tool_result {
                              ^41
  408|     41|        notification["params"]["update"]["title"] = json!(tool_result.title);
  409|     41|        notification["params"]["update"]["isError"] = json!(tool_result.is_error);
  410|     41|        notification["params"]["update"]["content"] = json!(tool_result.content);
  411|    677|    }
  412|       |
  413|    718|    notification
  414|    718|}

  418|     11|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {

  423|       |        }
  424|       |        Ok(())
  425|     11|    }

  427|       |    async fn session_new(
  428|       |        &mut self,
  429|       |        model: &str,
  430|       |        mcp_servers: Vec<Value>,
  431|       |        _cwd: &str,
  432|       |        _mode: SessionMode,
  433|    168|    ) -> Result<SessionNewResponse> {
  434|       |        if let Some(response) = self.take_acp_response("session/new") {
  435|       |            if let Some(error) = response.error {
  436|       |                return Err(anyhow!("Mock ACP session/new failed: {}", error.message));
  437|       |            }
  438|       |        }
  439|       |
  440|       |        // Determine agent type from model name and MCP server config
  441|       |        let agent_type = self.agent_type_from_config(model, &mcp_servers);
  442|       |
  443|       |        // Try to get the next session for this agent type
  444|       |        if let Some(session) = self.next_session(agent_type) {
  445|       |            let session_id = session.session_id.clone();
  446|       |            self.sessions_created.push(session_id.clone());
  447|       |
  448|       |            // Queue all updates from this session
  449|       |            self.queue_updates(&session_id, &session.updates);
  450|       |
  451|       |            Ok(SessionNewResponse { session_id })
  452|       |        } else {
  453|       |            // Fall back to generating a unique session ID
  454|       |            self.session_counter += 1;
  455|       |            let session_counter = self.session_counter;
  456|       |            let session_id = format!("mock-session-{session_counter}");

  458|       |            Ok(SessionNewResponse { session_id })
  459|       |        }

  462|    160|    async fn session_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {
  463|       |        if let Some(response) = self.take_acp_response("session/prompt") {
  464|       |            if let Some(error) = response.error {
  465|       |                return Err(anyhow!("Mock ACP session/prompt failed: {}", error.message));
  466|       |            }
  467|       |        }
  468|       |
  469|       |        // Capture the prompt for test assertions
  470|       |        self.captured_prompts
  471|       |            .push((session_id.to_string(), prompt.to_string()));
  472|       |
  473|       |        // Optionally validate against expected patterns
  474|       |        // (Find the session that matches this session_id and check expected_prompt_contains)
  475|       |        let all_sessions: Vec<&MockAgentSession> = self
  476|       |            .planner_sessions
  477|       |            .iter()
  478|       |            .chain(self.orchestrator_sessions.iter())
  479|       |            .chain(self.implementer_sessions.iter())
  480|       |            .collect();
  481|       |
  482|       |        for session in all_sessions {
  483|       |            if session.session_id == session_id {
  484|       |                if let Some(patterns) = &session.expected_prompt_contains {
  485|       |                    for pattern in patterns {
  486|       |                        if !prompt.contains(pattern) {
  487|       |                            return Err(anyhow!(
  488|       |                                "Prompt validation failed: expected prompt to contain '{pattern}', got: {prompt}"
  489|       |                            ));
  490|       |                        }
  491|       |                    }
  492|       |                }
  493|       |                break;
  494|       |            }
  495|       |        }
  496|       |
  497|       |        Ok(())
  498|    160|    }
  499|       |
  500|    837|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  501|       |        // Get the current active session (most recently started)
  502|       |        // We use the LAST session in the stack - this is the most recently started session
  503|       |        // which should be the one currently being processed
  504|       |        let Some(current_session) = self.active_sessions.last().cloned() else {
  505|       |            return Err(anyhow!("No active session"));
  506|       |        };
  507|       |
  508|       |        // Get the next update from the current session's queue
  509|       |        let queue = self.session_queues.get_mut(&current_session);
  510|       |        let queued = queue.and_then(VecDeque::pop_front);
  511|       |
  512|       |        if let Some(queued) = queued {
  513|       |            // If this update has an associated tool call, inject it
  514|       |            // The tool call is injected AFTER returning the update notification,
  515|       |            // simulating the real behavior where the agent sends a message chunk
  516|       |            // like "[calling create_task]" followed by the actual tool call.
  517|       |            if let Some(ref tool_call) = queued.tool_call {
  518|       |                if let Err(e) = self.inject_tool_call(tool_call).await {
  519|       |                    tracing::warn!("Failed to inject mock tool call: {}", e);
  520|       |                    // Continue anyway - the test may still work
  521|       |                }
  522|       |            }
  523|       |
  524|       |            // Check if this session's queue is now empty AND it was the last update
  525|       |            // (agent_turn_finished or similar)
  526|       |            let queue_empty = self
  527|       |                .session_queues
  528|       |                .get(&current_session)
  529|       |                .map_or(true, VecDeque::is_empty);
  530|       |
  531|       |            // Extract session_update from notification to check if this is the end
  532|       |            // Path is: params.update.sessionUpdate
  533|       |            let session_update = queued
  534|       |                .notification
  535|       |                .get("params")
  536|    715|                .and_then(|p| p.get("update"))
  537|    715|                .and_then(|u| u.get("sessionUpdate"))
  538|    715|                .and_then(|s| s.as_str())
  539|       |                .unwrap_or("");
  540|       |
  541|       |            if queue_empty
  542|       |                && (session_update == "agent_turn_finished" || session_update == "session_finished")
  543|       |            {
  544|       |                // This session is complete - remove it from active sessions
  545|    158|                self.active_sessions.retain(|s| s != &current_session);
  546|       |            }
  547|       |
  548|       |            Ok(queued.notification)
  549|       |        } else {
  550|       |            // No more updates for this session - remove it from active
  551|      1|            self.active_sessions.retain(|s| s != &current_session);
  552|       |            // Return an error to signal exhaustion of this session
  553|       |            Err(anyhow!(
  554|       |                "No more mock updates available for session {current_session}"
  555|       |            ))
  556|       |        }
  557|    837|    }
  558|       |
  559|     86|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  560|       |        // Mock client doesn't have a real notification receiver to take.
  561|       |        // For testing, we return None since mock clients use the update_queue directly.
  562|     86|        None
  563|     86|    }
  564|       |
  565|    119|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  566|       |        self.shutdown_called = true;
  567|       |        Ok(())
  568|    119|    }
  569|       |}

  571|       |#[cfg(test)]
  572|       |mod tests {
  573|       |    use super::*;
  574|       |    use crate::testing::MockSessionBuilder;
  575|       |
  576|       |    #[tokio::test]
  577|      1|    async fn test_mock_acp_client_basic() {
  578|      1|        let scenario = MockScenario {
  579|      1|            planner_sessions: vec![MockSessionBuilder::new("planner-001")
  580|      1|                .with_message_chunk("Planning...", 100)
  581|      1|                .with_turn_finished(50)
  582|      1|                .build()],
  583|      1|            ..Default::default()
  584|      1|        };
  585|       |
  586|      1|        let mut client = MockAcpClient::from_scenario(&scenario);
  587|       |
  588|       |        // Initialize should succeed
  589|      1|        client.initialize().await.unwrap();
  590|       |
  591|       |        // Create a planner session
  592|      1|        let response = client
  593|      1|            .session_new("planner-model", vec![], "/tmp", SessionMode::Plan)
  594|      1|            .await
  595|      1|            .unwrap();
  596|      1|        assert_eq!(response.session_id, "planner-001");
  597|      1|        assert_eq!(client.sessions_created_count(), 1);
  598|       |
  599|       |        // Should have queued 2 updates
  600|      1|        let update1 = client.recv().await.unwrap();
  601|      1|        assert_eq!(update1["method"], "session/update");
  602|      1|        assert_eq!(
  603|      1|            update1["params"]["update"]["sessionUpdate"],
  604|       |            "agent_message_chunk"
  605|       |        );
  606|       |
  607|      1|        let update2 = client.recv().await.unwrap();

  611|       |        );

  615|      1|        assert!(client.is_exhausted());

    1|       |//! Mock backend for testing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides a mock implementation of the `Backend` trait that can be configured
    4|       |//! for different test scenarios, enabling deterministic testing without requiring
    5|       |//! real authentication or cache setup.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # Configurable Behavior
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! The `MockBackend` supports configuring:
   10|       |//! - Authentication success/failure via `with_auth_failure()`
   11|       |//! - Custom models via `with_models()`
   12|       |//! - Custom backend name via `with_name()`
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! # Example
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! ```ignore
   17|       |//! use paperboat::testing::MockBackend;
   18|       |//!
   19|       |//! // Default mock backend (always succeeds)
   20|       |//! let backend = MockBackend::new();
   21|       |//! backend.check_auth()?; // Always succeeds
   22|       |//!
   23|       |//! // Mock backend that fails auth
   24|       |//! let failing_backend = MockBackend::builder()
   25|       |//!     .auth_fails(true)
   26|       |//!     .build();
   27|       |//! assert!(failing_backend.check_auth().is_err());
   28|       |//! ```
   29|       |
   30|       |use anyhow::{bail, Result};
   31|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   32|       |use std::collections::HashSet;
   33|       |use std::path::PathBuf;

   41|       |/// A mock backend with configurable behavior for testing.
   42|       |///
   43|       |/// This backend is designed for testing scenarios where you need to control:
   44|       |/// - Whether authentication checks pass or fail
   45|       |/// - What models are returned from discovery
   46|       |/// - The backend name for error messages
   47|       |///
   48|       |/// # Default Behavior
   49|       |///
   50|       |/// By default, `MockBackend`:
   51|       |/// - Always passes authentication checks
   52|       |/// - Returns a single mock model (Sonnet 4.5)
   53|       |/// - Uses "mock" as the backend name
   54|       |/// - Returns fake cache paths (no filesystem operations)
   55|       |///
   56|       |/// # Builder Pattern
   57|       |///
   58|       |/// Use `MockBackend::builder()` to create a backend with custom behavior:
   59|       |///
   60|       |/// ```ignore
   61|       |/// let backend = MockBackend::builder()
   62|       |///     .name("test-backend")
   63|       |///     .auth_fails(true)
   64|       |///     .models(vec![...])
   65|       |///     .build();
   66|       |/// ```
   67|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   68|       |pub struct MockBackend {
   69|       |    /// Optional custom name for the backend (defaults to "mock")
   70|       |    name: Option<&'static str>,
   71|       |    /// Whether authentication should fail
   72|       |    auth_fails: bool,
   73|       |    /// Custom tiers to return from `available_tiers()`
   74|       |    custom_tiers: Option<HashSet<ModelTier>>,
   75|       |}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/testing/mock_transport.rs:
    1|       |//! Mock transport for testing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides a mock implementation of `AgentTransport` that returns scripted
    4|       |//! responses from a `MockScenario`, enabling deterministic testing without
    5|       |//! requiring a live agent process.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! This wraps and delegates to `MockAcpClient` to preserve backward compatibility
    8|       |//! while providing the new `AgentTransport` interface.
    9|       |
   10|       |use crate::acp::AcpClientTrait;
   11|       |#[cfg(test)]
   12|       |use crate::acp::SessionMode;
   13|       |use crate::app::ToolMessage;
   14|       |use crate::backend::transport::{
   15|       |    AgentTransport, SessionConfig, SessionInfo, SessionUpdate, ToolResult,
   16|       |};
   17|       |use crate::testing::{MockAcpClient, MockScenario, MockToolInterceptor};
   18|       |use anyhow::Result;
   19|       |use async_trait::async_trait;
   20|       |use serde_json::Value;
   21|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   22|       |use tokio::sync::{mpsc, Mutex};
   23|       |
   24|       |/// Mock transport that implements `AgentTransport` for testing.
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// This wraps `MockAcpClient` and converts between the old and new interfaces,
   27|       |/// allowing tests written for the ACP client interface to continue working
   28|       |/// while the codebase migrates to the transport abstraction.
   29|       |pub struct MockTransport {
   30|       |    /// The underlying mock ACP client
   31|       |    inner: MockAcpClient,
   32|       |    /// Sender for session updates
   33|       |    update_tx: mpsc::Sender<SessionUpdate>,
   34|       |    /// Receiver for session updates (taken once)
   35|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   36|       |    update_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>>,
   37|       |    /// Current session ID
   38|       |    current_session_id: Option<String>,
   39|       |}
   40|       |
   41|       |impl MockTransport {
   42|       |    /// Create a new mock transport from a scenario.
   43|    150|    pub fn from_scenario(scenario: &MockScenario) -> Self {
   44|    150|        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(100);
   45|    150|        Self {
   46|    150|            inner: MockAcpClient::from_scenario(scenario),
   47|    150|            update_tx: tx,
   48|    150|            update_rx: Some(rx),
   49|    150|            current_session_id: None,
   50|    150|        }
   51|    150|    }
   52|       |
   53|       |    /// Create an empty mock transport (no sessions).
   54|     15|    pub fn empty() -> Self {
   55|     15|        Self::from_scenario(&MockScenario::default())
   56|     15|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Set the tool channel for injecting tool calls.
   59|    129|    pub fn with_tool_channel(
   60|    129|        mut self,
   61|    129|        tool_tx: mpsc::Sender<ToolMessage>,
   62|    129|        tool_interceptor: Arc<Mutex<MockToolInterceptor>>,
   63|    129|    ) -> Self {
   64|    129|        self.inner = self.inner.with_tool_channel(tool_tx, tool_interceptor);
   65|    129|        self
   66|    129|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    /// Get all captured prompts as (`session_id`, prompt) pairs.

   70|      1|        self.inner.captured_prompts()

   83|       |    /// Convert ACP notification JSON to `SessionUpdate`.
   84|    721|    fn convert_to_session_update(
   85|    721|        notification: &Value,
   86|    721|        default_session_id: &str,
   87|    721|    ) -> Option<SessionUpdate> {
   88|    721|        let method = notification.get("method")?.as_str()?;
                                                             ^0        ^0
   89|    721|        if method != "session/update" {
   90|      1|            return Some(SessionUpdate::Raw {
   91|      1|                session_id: None,
   92|      1|                data: notification.clone(),
   93|      1|            });
   94|    720|        }
   95|       |
   96|    720|        let params = notification.get("params")?;
                                                             ^0
   97|    720|        let session_id = params
   98|    720|            .get("sessionId")
   99|    720|            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                                        ^719^719
  100|    720|            .unwrap_or(default_session_id)
  101|    720|            .to_string();
  102|       |
  103|       |        // Get update field and sessionUpdate type
  104|    720|        let update = params.get("update")?;
                                                       ^0
  105|    720|        let session_update_type = update.get("sessionUpdate").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;
                                                                                                    ^0
  106|       |
  107|    720|        match session_update_type {
  108|    720|            "agent_message_chunk" | "agent_thought_chunk" => {
                                                  ^240
  109|    481|                let content = update
  110|    481|                    .get("content")
  111|    481|                    .and_then(|c| c.get("text"))
                                                ^479^479
  112|    481|                    .and_then(|t| t.as_str())
                                                ^479^479
  113|    481|                    .unwrap_or("")
  114|    481|                    .to_string();
  115|    481|                Some(SessionUpdate::Text {
  116|    481|                    session_id,
  117|    481|                    content,
  118|    481|                })
  119|       |            }
  120|    239|            "tool_call" => {
  121|     42|                let tool_name = update
  122|     42|                    .get("title")
  123|     42|                    .and_then(|t| t.as_str())
  124|     42|                    .unwrap_or("unknown")
  125|     42|                    .to_string();
  126|     42|                Some(SessionUpdate::ToolUse {
  127|     42|                    session_id,
  128|     42|                    tool_use_id: String::new(),
  129|     42|                    tool_name,
  130|     42|                    input: Value::Null,
  131|     42|                })
  132|       |            }
  133|    197|            "agent_turn_finished" | "session_finished" => Some(SessionUpdate::Completion {
                                                  ^43
  134|    155|                session_id,
  135|    155|                result: None,
  136|    155|                success: true,
  137|    155|            }),

  139|     42|                session_id: Some(session_id),
  140|     42|                data: notification.clone(),
  141|     42|            }),
  142|       |        }
  143|    721|    }

  146|       |#[async_trait]
  147|       |impl AgentTransport for MockTransport {
  148|      8|    async fn initialize(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  149|       |        self.inner.initialize().await
  150|      8|    }
  151|       |
  152|    165|    async fn create_session(&mut self, config: SessionConfig) -> Result<SessionInfo> {
  153|       |        let response = self
  154|       |            .inner
  155|       |            .session_new(
  156|       |                &config.model,
  157|       |                config.mcp_servers,
  158|       |                &config.workspace,
  159|       |                config.mode,
  160|       |            )
  161|       |            .await?;
  162|       |        self.current_session_id = Some(response.session_id.clone());
  163|       |        Ok(SessionInfo::new(response.session_id))
  164|    165|    }
  165|       |
  166|    159|    async fn send_prompt(&mut self, session_id: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<()> {
  167|       |        self.inner.session_prompt(session_id, prompt).await
  168|    159|    }
  169|       |
  170|      2|    fn take_notifications(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<SessionUpdate>> {
  171|      2|        self.update_rx.take()
  172|      2|    }
  173|       |
  174|       |    async fn respond_to_tool(
  175|       |        &mut self,
  176|       |        _session_id: &str,
  177|       |        _tool_use_id: &str,
  178|       |        _result: ToolResult,
  179|      1|    ) -> Result<()> {
  180|       |        // Mock transport doesn't need to handle tool responses -
  181|       |        // they're handled by the MockToolInterceptor
  182|       |        Ok(())
  183|      1|    }
  184|       |
  185|    118|    async fn shutdown(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
  186|       |        self.inner.shutdown().await
  187|    118|    }
  188|       |
  189|       |    /// Receive the next raw notification (legacy compatibility).
  190|       |    ///
  191|       |    /// This method supports the legacy polling pattern used in App code.
  192|       |    /// It gets the next notification from the inner `MockAcpClient` and optionally
  193|       |    /// converts it to a `SessionUpdate` that's sent to the update channel.
  194|  2.28k|    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Value> {
  195|       |        let notification = self.inner.recv().await?;
  196|       |
  197|       |        // Also convert to SessionUpdate and send to channel
  198|       |        if let Some(update) = Self::convert_to_session_update(
  199|       |            &notification,
  200|       |            self.current_session_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(""),
  201|       |        ) {
  202|       |            let _ = self.update_tx.send(update).await;
  203|       |        }
  204|       |
  205|       |        Ok(notification)
  206|  2.28k|    }
  207|       |
  208|       |    /// Take the raw notification receiver from the inner client.
  209|       |    ///
  210|       |    /// This is used for backward compatibility with the existing router pattern.
  211|     86|    fn take_notification_rx(&mut self) -> Option<mpsc::Receiver<Value>> {
  212|     86|        self.inner.take_notification_rx()
  213|     86|    }

  560|      1|            implementer_sessions: vec![MockSessionBuilder::new("impl-001").build()],
  561|      1|            ..Default::default()

  570|      1|        let _session = transport.create_session(config).await.unwrap();
  571|       |

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/testing/scenario.rs:
    1|       |//! Scenario loading for the mock testing system.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the `MockScenario` type and related functionality

   17|       |    /// Agent is currently running

   50|       |}

   65|    110|        }
   66|    110|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    /// Returns a display name for this agent.
   69|       |    ///
   70|       |    /// For running agents, the status icon alternates between "~" and "-" based on
   71|       |    /// the animation frame to create a smooth animated indicator.
   72|       |    #[must_use]
   73|     48|    pub fn display_name(&self, animation_frame: u32) -> String {
   74|     48|        let status_icon = match self.status {
   75|       |            AgentStatus::Running => {
   76|       |                // Alternate between "~" and "-" every ~500ms at 60 FPS
   77|     34|                if animation_frame % 60 < 30 {
   78|     33|                    "~"
   79|       |                } else {
   80|      1|                    "-"
   81|       |                }
   82|       |            }
   83|     12|            AgentStatus::Completed => "✓",
   84|      2|            AgentStatus::Failed => "✗",
   85|       |        };

    1|       |//! Agent tree state management.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module contains [`AgentTreeState`], which manages the hierarchical
    4|       |//! agent tree structure built from `LogEvent`s. It maintains:
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! - A tree of [`AgentNode`]s built from `AgentStarted` and `SubtaskCreated` events
    7|       |//! - Agent status updates from `AgentComplete` events
    8|       |//! - The `tui-tree-widget` [`TreeState`] for navigation
    9|       |//! - Message buffers per agent for the output pane
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! The tree is used by the TUI to display the agent hierarchy in the left panel
   12|       |//! and to track which agent's output to show in the center panel.
   13|       |
   14|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   15|       |
   16|       |use tui_tree_widget::{TreeItem, TreeState};
   17|       |
   18|       |use crate::logging::AgentType;
   19|       |
   20|       |// Re-export from agent_node module
   21|       |pub use super::agent_node::{AgentNode, AgentStatus};
   22|       |
   23|       |// ============================================================================
   24|       |// Agent Tree State
   25|       |// ============================================================================
   26|       |
   27|       |/// Manages the agent hierarchy tree and associated metadata.
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// This struct maintains:
   30|       |/// - A tree structure built from `AgentStarted` and `SubtaskCreated` events
   31|       |/// - Agent status updates from `AgentComplete` events
   32|       |/// - The `tui-tree-widget` [`TreeState`] for navigation
   33|       |/// - Message buffers per agent for the output pane
   34|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   35|       |pub struct AgentTreeState {
   36|       |    /// All agents indexed by `session_id`
   37|       |    agents: HashMap<String, AgentNode>,
   38|       |    /// Root session IDs (depth 0 agents)
   39|       |    roots: Vec<String>,
   40|       |    /// Messages per `session_id` for output display
   41|       |    messages: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>,
   42|       |    /// Tree widget navigation state
   43|       |    pub tree_state: TreeState<String>,
   44|       |    /// Mapping from depth to current "active" `session_id` at that depth.
   45|       |    /// Used for determining parent when a new agent starts.
   46|       |    depth_to_session: HashMap<u32, String>,
   47|       |}
   48|       |
   49|       |impl Default for AgentTreeState {
   50|      0|    fn default() -> Self {
   51|      0|        Self::new()
   52|      0|    }
   53|       |}
   54|       |
   55|       |impl AgentTreeState {
   56|       |    /// Creates a new empty agent tree state.
   57|       |    #[must_use]
   58|    160|    pub fn new() -> Self {
   59|    160|        Self {
   60|    160|            agents: HashMap::new(),
   61|    160|            roots: Vec::new(),
   62|    160|            messages: HashMap::new(),
   63|    160|            tree_state: TreeState::default(),
   64|    160|            depth_to_session: HashMap::new(),
   65|    160|        }
   66|    160|    }
   67|       |
   68|       |    /// Handles an `AgentStarted` event.
   69|    109|    pub fn handle_agent_started(

   71|    109|        agent_type: AgentType,
   72|    109|        session_id: String,
   73|    109|        depth: u32,
   74|    109|        task: String,
   75|    109|    ) {
   76|    109|        let mut node = AgentNode::new(agent_type, session_id.clone(), depth, task);
   77|       |
   78|       |        // Determine parent based on depth
   79|    109|        if depth > 0 {
   80|       |            // Look for parent at depth - 1
   81|     34|            if let Some(parent_id) = self.depth_to_session.get(&(depth - 1)).cloned() {
   82|     34|                node.parent_session_id = Some(parent_id.clone());
   83|       |                // Add as child of parent
   84|     34|                if let Some(parent) = self.agents.get_mut(&parent_id) {
   85|     34|                    parent.children.push(session_id.clone());
   86|     34|                }
                              ^0
   87|      0|            }
   88|     75|        } else {
   89|     75|            // Root level agent
   90|     75|            self.roots.push(session_id.clone());
   91|     75|        }
   92|       |
   93|       |        // Update depth mapping
   94|    109|        self.depth_to_session.insert(depth, session_id.clone());
   95|       |
   96|       |        // Store the agent
   97|    109|        self.agents.insert(session_id.clone(), node);
   98|       |
   99|       |        // Initialize message buffer
  100|    109|        self.messages.insert(session_id, Vec::new());
  101|    109|    }
  102|       |
  103|       |    /// Handles an `AgentComplete` event.
  104|     27|    pub fn handle_agent_complete(&mut self, session_id: Option<&str>, success: bool) {
  105|     27|        if let Some(id) = session_id {
  106|     27|            if let Some(agent) = self.agents.get_mut(id) {
  107|     27|                agent.status = if success {
  108|     21|                    AgentStatus::Completed
  109|       |                } else {
  110|      6|                    AgentStatus::Failed
  111|       |                };
  112|      0|            }
  113|      0|        }
  114|     27|    }
  115|       |
  116|       |    /// Handles a `SubtaskCreated` event.
  117|       |    ///
  118|       |    /// This event signals that a decompose operation is starting at a new depth level.
  119|       |    /// The `parent_depth` indicates the depth of the orchestrator that initiated the decompose,
  120|       |    /// and `new_depth` is where the sub-agents will be created.
  121|       |    ///
  122|       |    /// Note: The actual parent-child relationship is established when `AgentStarted` events
  123|       |    /// are received, using the `depth_to_session` mapping. This method prepares context
  124|       |    /// for better tracking of nested decomposition scenarios.
  125|      2|    pub fn handle_subtask_created(&mut self, parent_depth: u32, _new_depth: u32, _path: &str) {
  126|       |        // The depth_to_session mapping at parent_depth should already contain
  127|       |        // the orchestrator that called decompose. When sub-agents start at new_depth,
  128|       |        // they will correctly look up their parent at parent_depth.
  129|       |        //
  130|       |        // This method serves as a hook for future enhancements like:
  131|       |        // - Tracking decomposition paths for display
  132|       |        // - Adding markers in the tree for decomposition boundaries
  133|       |        // - Collecting metrics on decomposition depth
  134|       |        //
  135|       |        // For now, we verify the parent depth mapping exists (defensive programming)
  136|      2|        if parent_depth > 0 && !self.depth_to_session.contains_key(&parent_depth) {
                                             ^0^0
  137|       |            // This shouldn't happen in normal operation, but log if it does
  138|      0|            tracing::warn!(
  139|       |                "SubtaskCreated at parent_depth {} but no session registered at that depth",
  140|       |                parent_depth
  141|       |            );
  142|      2|        }
  143|      2|    }
  144|       |
  145|       |    /// Handles an `AgentMessage` event (streaming content).
  146|       |    ///
  147|       |    /// Streaming chunks are concatenated together. Only when we encounter a newline
  148|       |    /// do we split into separate messages for display.
  149|       |    ///
  150|       |    /// Note: If the last message is a "standalone" message (starts with `>`, `✓`, `✗`, or `+`),
  151|       |    /// we always start a new message to avoid concatenating streaming content with tool calls/results.
  152|     26|    pub fn handle_agent_message(&mut self, session_id: Option<&str>, content: &str) {
  153|     26|        if let Some(id) = session_id {
  154|     26|            if let Some(messages) = self.messages.get_mut(id) {
  155|       |                // Pre-process content to add line breaks where tool call descriptions
  156|       |                // are immediately followed by regular text (e.g., 'Calling: tool "query"Let me...')
  157|     26|                let processed_content = Self::insert_missing_line_breaks(content);
  158|       |
  159|       |                // Check if we need to start a new message:
  160|       |                // 1. No messages yet
  161|       |                // 2. Last message is a standalone message (tool call, result, etc.)
  162|       |                // 3. This content starts with a standalone prefix (like "Calling:")
  163|     26|                let content_is_standalone = Self::is_standalone_message(&processed_content);
  164|     26|                let should_start_new = messages.is_empty()
  165|     11|                    || messages
  166|     11|                        .last()
  167|     11|                        .is_some_and(|last| Self::is_standalone_message(last))
  168|      6|                    || content_is_standalone;
  169|       |
  170|     26|                if should_start_new {
  171|     21|                    messages.push(String::new());
  172|     21|                }
                              ^5
  173|       |
  174|       |                // Append content to the last message, handling newlines
  175|     33|                for (i, part) in processed_content.split('\n').enumerate() {
                                               ^26                           ^26
  176|     33|                    if i > 0 {
  177|      7|                        // Start a new message for each newline
  178|      7|                        messages.push(String::new());
  179|     26|                    }
  180|     33|                    let idx = messages.len() - 1;
  181|     33|                    messages[idx].push_str(part);
  182|       |                }
  183|      0|            }
  184|      0|        }
  185|     26|    }
  186|       |
  187|       |    /// Insert missing line breaks where tool call descriptions are joined with regular text.
  188|       |    ///
  189|       |    /// Detects patterns where a closing quote is followed by a capital letter,
  190|       |    /// indicating a tool call description ending and regular agent text beginning.
  191|       |    ///
  192|       |    /// This only triggers for the LAST closing quote in a string that contains
  193|       |    /// "Calling:" (indicating it's a tool call description), not for all quotes.
  194|     26|    fn insert_missing_line_breaks(content: &str) -> String {
  195|       |        // Only apply this heuristic if content contains "Calling:" pattern
  196|     26|        if !content.contains("Calling:") {
  197|     24|            return content.to_string();
  198|      2|        }
  199|       |
  200|       |        // Find the last closing quote that's followed by a capital letter
  201|       |        // This is likely the end of a tool call query followed by agent text
  202|      2|        let bytes = content.as_bytes();
  203|     57|        for i in (0..bytes.len().saturating_sub(1)).rev() {
                                ^2 ^2                             ^2
  204|     57|            if bytes[i] == b'"' {
  205|       |                // Check if next char is uppercase
  206|      1|                if let Some(&next_byte) = bytes.get(i + 1) {
  207|      1|                    if next_byte.is_ascii_uppercase() {
  208|       |                        // Split at this position
  209|      1|                        let (before, after) = content.split_at(i + 1);
  210|      1|                        return format!("{before}\n{after}");
  211|      0|                    }
  212|      0|                }
  213|     56|            }
  214|       |        }
  215|       |
  216|      1|        content.to_string()
  217|     26|    }
  218|       |
  219|       |    /// Returns true if the message is a "standalone" message that should not be appended to.
  220|       |    ///
  221|       |    /// Standalone messages are tool calls, tool results, and other formatted outputs that
  222|       |    /// start with specific prefixes:
  223|       |    /// - `>` - Tool call formatted by TUI (e.g., "> Calling: ...")
  224|       |    /// - `✓` - Success result (e.g., "✓ `tool_name` completed")
  225|       |    /// - `✗` - Error result (e.g., "✗ `tool_name` completed")
  226|       |    /// - `+` - Added content (e.g., "+ Added file ...")
  227|       |    /// - `Calling:` - Tool call from agent output (e.g., "Calling: Context engine: ...")
  228|     37|    fn is_standalone_message(msg: &str) -> bool {
  229|     37|        let trimmed = msg.trim_start();
  230|     37|        trimmed.starts_with('>')
  231|     36|            || trimmed.starts_with('✓')
  232|     34|            || trimmed.starts_with('✗')
  233|     33|            || trimmed.starts_with('+')
  234|     32|            || trimmed.starts_with("Calling:")
  235|     37|    }
  236|       |
  237|       |    /// Adds a standalone message (like tool calls) that should always be on its own line.
  238|       |    ///
  239|       |    /// Unlike streaming content, this always starts on a new line.
  240|     93|    pub fn handle_standalone_message(&mut self, session_id: Option<&str>, content: &str) {
  241|     93|        if let Some(id) = session_id {
  242|     93|            if let Some(messages) = self.messages.get_mut(id) {
  243|     93|                // Always add as a new message on its own line
  244|     93|                messages.push(content.to_string());
  245|     93|            }
                          ^0
  246|      0|        }
  247|     93|    }

  275|       |    /// Builds tree items for rendering with `tui-tree-widget`.
  276|       |    ///
  277|       |    /// Returns owned [`TreeItem`]s that can be used for rendering.
  278|       |    ///
  279|       |    /// # Arguments
  280|       |    ///
  281|       |    /// * `animation_frame` - Current animation frame for animated status indicators
  282|       |    #[must_use]
  283|      7|    pub fn build_tree_items(&self, animation_frame: u32) -> Vec<TreeItem<'static, String>> {
  284|      7|        self.roots
  285|      7|            .iter()
  286|      7|            .filter_map(|root_id| self.build_tree_item_recursive(root_id, animation_frame))
  287|      7|            .collect()
  288|      7|    }
  289|       |
  290|       |    /// Recursively builds a tree item and its children.
  291|     26|    fn build_tree_item_recursive(
  292|     26|        &self,
  293|     26|        session_id: &str,
  294|     26|        animation_frame: u32,
  295|     26|    ) -> Option<TreeItem<'static, String>> {
  296|     26|        let agent = self.agents.get(session_id)?;
                                                             ^0
  297|       |
  298|       |        // Build children first
  299|     26|        let children: Vec<TreeItem<'static, String>> = agent
  300|     26|            .children
  301|     26|            .iter()
  302|     26|            .filter_map(|child_id| self.build_tree_item_recursive(child_id, animation_frame))
                                                 ^19  ^19                       ^19       ^19
  303|     26|            .collect();
  304|       |
  305|     26|        let display_name = agent.display_name(animation_frame);
  306|       |
  307|     26|        if children.is_empty() {
  308|     16|            Some(TreeItem::new_leaf(session_id.to_string(), display_name))
  309|       |        } else {
  310|     10|            TreeItem::new(session_id.to_string(), display_name, children).ok()
  311|       |        }
  312|     26|    }

  314|       |    /// Selects an agent by `session_id`.
  315|     35|    pub fn select(&mut self, session_id: &str) {
  316|       |        // Build the path from root to this session
  317|     35|        let path = self.build_path_to_session(session_id);
  318|     35|        self.tree_state.select(path);
  319|     35|    }

  321|       |    /// Builds the identifier path from root to a given session.
  322|     36|    fn build_path_to_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Vec<String> {
  323|     36|        let mut path = Vec::new();
  324|     36|        let mut current = Some(session_id);
  325|       |
  326|     77|        while let Some(id) = current {
                                     ^41
  327|     41|            path.push(id.to_string());
  328|     41|            current = self
  329|     41|                .agents
  330|     41|                .get(id)
  331|     41|                .and_then(|a| a.parent_session_id.as_deref());
                                            ^40                 ^40
  332|       |        }
  333|       |
  334|     36|        path.reverse();
  335|     36|        path
  336|     36|    }

  338|       |    /// Returns the most recently started agent's `session_id`.
  339|       |    #[must_use]
  340|      0|    pub fn most_recent_session_id(&self) -> Option<&str> {
  341|      0|        self.agents

  343|      0|            .filter(|a| a.status == AgentStatus::Running)
  344|      0|            .max_by_key(|a| a.start_time)
  345|      0|            .map(|a| a.session_id.as_str())
  346|      0|            .or_else(|| {
  347|       |                // Fall back to most recently started agent overall
  348|      0|                self.agents
  349|      0|                    .values()
  350|      0|                    .max_by_key(|a| a.start_time)
  351|      0|                    .map(|a| a.session_id.as_str())
  352|      0|            })
  353|      0|    }

  363|       |    /// Returns agent counts by status: `(succeeded, failed, in_progress)`.
  364|       |    #[must_use]
  365|     32|    pub fn count_agents_by_status(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize) {
  366|     32|        let mut succeeded = 0;
  367|     32|        let mut failed = 0;
  368|     32|        let mut in_progress = 0;
  369|       |
  370|     35|        for agent in self.agents.values() {
                                   ^32         ^32
  371|     35|            match agent.status {
  372|     11|                AgentStatus::Completed => succeeded += 1,
  373|      6|                AgentStatus::Failed => failed += 1,
  374|     18|                AgentStatus::Running => in_progress += 1,
  375|       |            }
  376|       |        }
  377|       |
  378|     32|        (succeeded, failed, in_progress)
  379|     32|    }

  381|       |    /// Returns `session_id`s in the order they appear visually (flattened tree).
  382|       |    ///
  383|       |    /// Only includes items that are currently visible (parent nodes must be expanded).
  384|       |    /// This is useful for mapping visual row indices to `session_id`s (e.g., for mouse clicks).
  385|       |    #[must_use]
  386|     10|    pub fn visible_items(&self) -> Vec<String> {
  387|     10|        let mut result = Vec::new();
  388|     10|        for root_id in &self.roots {
  389|     10|            self.collect_visible_recursive(root_id, std::slice::from_ref(root_id), &mut result);
  390|     10|        }
  391|     10|        result
  392|     10|    }
  393|       |
  394|       |    /// Recursively collects visible `session_id`s in depth-first order.
  395|       |    ///
  396|       |    /// # Arguments
  397|       |    ///
  398|       |    /// * `session_id` - The current node to process
  399|       |    /// * `current_path` - The path from root to current node (for checking opened state)
  400|       |    /// * `result` - Accumulator for visible session IDs
  401|     21|    fn collect_visible_recursive(
  402|     21|        &self,
  403|     21|        session_id: &str,
  404|     21|        current_path: &[String],
  405|     21|        result: &mut Vec<String>,
  406|     21|    ) {
  407|     21|        result.push(session_id.to_string());
  408|       |
  409|       |        // Check if this node is expanded in tree_state
  410|       |        // A node's children are visible if the node's path is in the opened set
  411|     21|        if self.tree_state.opened().contains(&current_path.to_vec()) {
  412|      8|            if let Some(agent) = self.agents.get(session_id) {
  413|     11|                for child_id in &agent.children {
                                              ^8
  414|     11|                    // Build the path to this child
  415|     11|                    let mut child_path = current_path.to_owned();
  416|     11|                    child_path.push(child_id.clone());
  417|     11|                    self.collect_visible_recursive(child_id, &child_path, result);
  418|     11|                }
  419|      0|            }
  420|     13|        }
  421|     21|    }

  552|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
  553|       |
  554|       |        // Should produce: "Hello", "" (empty for the trailing newline)
  555|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 2, "Expected 2 messages, got {:?}", messages);
  556|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[0], "Hello");
  557|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[1], ""); // Empty string for the line break
  558|      1|    }
  559|       |
  560|       |    #[test]
  561|      1|    fn test_agent_message_just_newline() {
  562|       |        // Test just a newline: "\n"
  563|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  564|       |
  565|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  566|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  567|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
  568|       |            0,
  569|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
  570|       |        );
  571|       |
  572|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(Some("session-1"), "First");
  573|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(Some("session-1"), "\n");
  574|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(Some("session-1"), "Second");
  575|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
  576|       |
  577|       |        // When "\n" arrives, it creates an empty message slot that "Second" fills
  578|       |        // So we get ["First", "Second"] - this is intentional streaming behavior
  579|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 2);
  580|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[0], "First");
  581|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[1], "Second");
  582|      1|    }

  585|      1|    fn test_full_flow_content_with_newlines() {
  586|       |        // Test the full flow: content "Line 1\nLine 2\n" arriving in one chunk
  587|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  588|       |
  589|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  590|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  591|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
  592|       |            0,
  593|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
  594|       |        );
  595|       |
  596|       |        // Simulate content as it would arrive from the LLM
  597|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(Some("session-1"), "Line 1\nLine 2\n");
  598|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
  599|       |
  600|       |        // Should split into: "Line 1", "Line 2", "" (empty for trailing newline)
  601|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 3, "Expected 3 messages, got {:?}", messages);
  602|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[0], "Line 1");
  603|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[1], "Line 2");
  604|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[2], ""); // Trailing newline becomes empty message
  605|      1|    }

  610|       |
  611|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  612|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,

  614|       |            0,
  615|      1|            "Root task".to_string(),
  616|       |        );
  617|       |
  618|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  619|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  620|      1|            "child".to_string(),
  621|       |            1,

  623|       |        );
  624|       |

  628|      1|    }

  630|       |    #[test]
  631|      1|    fn test_nested_decomposition_hierarchy() {
  632|       |        // Simulates the nested_decompose.toml scenario:
  633|       |        // 1. Planner at depth 0 creates plan
  634|       |        // 2. Orchestrator at depth 0 executes
  635|       |        // 3. Decompose creates child scope, sub-agents at depth 1
  636|       |        // 4. Sub-planner and sub-orchestrator at depth 1
  637|       |        // 5. Implementers at depth 2 (under sub-orchestrator)
  638|       |
  639|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  640|       |
  641|       |        // Main planner starts (depth 0)
  642|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  643|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
  644|      1|            "planner-001".to_string(),
  645|       |            0,
  646|      1|            "Main plan".to_string(),
  647|       |        );
  648|       |
  649|       |        // Planner completes
  650|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("planner-001"), true);
  651|       |
  652|       |        // Main orchestrator starts (depth 0)
  653|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  654|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  655|      1|            "orchestrator-001".to_string(),
  656|       |            0,
  657|      1|            "Execute main plan".to_string(),
  658|       |        );
  659|       |
  660|       |        // Decompose: SubtaskCreated event would be sent here
  661|       |        // Then sub-planner starts at depth 1
  662|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  663|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
  664|      1|            "planner-002".to_string(),
  665|       |            1,
  666|      1|            "Sub plan for auth".to_string(),
  667|       |        );
  668|       |
  669|       |        // Sub-planner should be child of main orchestrator
  670|      1|        let sub_planner = tree.get_agent("planner-002").unwrap();
  671|      1|        assert_eq!(
  672|       |            sub_planner.parent_session_id,
  673|      1|            Some("orchestrator-001".to_string()),
  674|       |            "Sub-planner should be parented to main orchestrator"
  675|       |        );
  676|       |
  677|       |        // Sub-planner completes
  678|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("planner-002"), true);
  679|       |
  680|       |        // Sub-orchestrator starts at depth 1
  681|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  682|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  683|      1|            "orchestrator-002".to_string(),
  684|       |            1,
  685|      1|            "Execute sub plan".to_string(),
  686|       |        );
  687|       |
  688|       |        // Sub-orchestrator should also be child of main orchestrator
  689|      1|        let sub_orch = tree.get_agent("orchestrator-002").unwrap();
  690|      1|        assert_eq!(
  691|       |            sub_orch.parent_session_id,
  692|      1|            Some("orchestrator-001".to_string()),
  693|       |            "Sub-orchestrator should be parented to main orchestrator"
  694|       |        );
  695|       |
  696|       |        // Implementers at depth 2 (children of sub-orchestrator)
  697|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  698|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  699|      1|            "implementer-001".to_string(),
  700|       |            2,
  701|      1|            "Implement login".to_string(),
  702|       |        );
  703|       |
  704|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  705|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 2 },
  706|      1|            "implementer-002".to_string(),
  707|       |            2,
  708|      1|            "Implement register".to_string(),
  709|       |        );
  710|       |
  711|       |        // Implementers should be children of sub-orchestrator
  712|      1|        let impl1 = tree.get_agent("implementer-001").unwrap();
  713|      1|        assert_eq!(
  714|       |            impl1.parent_session_id,
  715|      1|            Some("orchestrator-002".to_string()),
  716|       |            "Implementer 1 should be parented to sub-orchestrator"
  717|       |        );
  718|       |
  719|      1|        let impl2 = tree.get_agent("implementer-002").unwrap();
  720|      1|        assert_eq!(
  721|       |            impl2.parent_session_id,
  722|      1|            Some("orchestrator-002".to_string()),
  723|       |            "Implementer 2 should be parented to sub-orchestrator"
  724|       |        );
  725|       |
  726|       |        // Check tree structure
  727|      1|        let items = tree.build_tree_items(0);
  728|      1|        assert_eq!(
  729|      1|            items.len(),
  730|       |            2,
  731|       |            "Should have 2 roots (planner + orchestrator at depth 0)"
  732|       |        );
  733|       |
  734|       |        // Find the orchestrator root
  735|      1|        let orch_item = items
  736|      1|            .iter()
  737|      2|            .find(|i| i.identifier() == &"orchestrator-001".to_string());
                           ^1
  738|      1|        assert!(orch_item.is_some(), "Should find orchestrator-001 in tree");
  739|       |
  740|      1|        let orch_item = orch_item.unwrap();
  741|      1|        assert_eq!(
  742|      1|            orch_item.children().len(),
  743|       |            2,
  744|       |            "Main orchestrator should have 2 children (sub-planner + sub-orchestrator)"
  745|       |        );
  746|       |
  747|       |        // Find sub-orchestrator
  748|      1|        let sub_orch_item = orch_item
  749|      1|            .children()
  750|      1|            .iter()
  751|      2|            .find(|c| c.identifier() == &"orchestrator-002".to_string());
                           ^1
  752|      1|        assert!(sub_orch_item.is_some(), "Should find sub-orchestrator");
  753|       |
  754|      1|        let sub_orch_item = sub_orch_item.unwrap();
  755|      1|        assert_eq!(
  756|      1|            sub_orch_item.children().len(),
  757|       |            2,
  758|       |            "Sub-orchestrator should have 2 implementer children"
  759|       |        );
  760|      1|    }

  762|       |    #[test]
  763|      1|    fn test_multiple_implementers_same_depth() {
  764|       |        // Test multiple implementers at the same depth getting proper parent
  765|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  766|       |
  767|       |        // Orchestrator at depth 0
  768|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  769|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  770|      1|            "orch".to_string(),
  771|       |            0,
  772|      1|            "Main task".to_string(),
  773|       |        );
  774|       |
  775|       |        // Multiple implementers at depth 1
  776|      5|        for i in 1..=5 {
                               ^1
  777|      5|            tree.handle_agent_started(
  778|      5|                AgentType::Implementer { index: i },
  779|      5|                format!("impl-{i:03}"),
  780|      5|                1,
  781|      5|                format!("Task {i}"),
  782|      5|            );
  783|      5|        }
  784|       |
  785|       |        // All implementers should be children of orchestrator
  786|      1|        let orch = tree.get_agent("orch").unwrap();
  787|      1|        assert_eq!(
  788|      1|            orch.children.len(),
  789|       |            5,
  790|       |            "Orchestrator should have 5 children"
  791|       |        );
  792|       |
  793|      5|        for i in 1..=5 {
                               ^1
  794|      5|            let impl_id = format!("impl-{i:03}");
  795|      5|            let impl_agent = tree.get_agent(&impl_id).unwrap();
  796|      5|            assert_eq!(
  797|       |                impl_agent.parent_session_id,
  798|      5|                Some("orch".to_string()),
  799|       |                "All implementers should be parented to orchestrator"
  800|       |            );
  801|       |        }
  802|       |
  803|       |        // Check tree structure
  804|      1|        let items = tree.build_tree_items(0);
  805|      1|        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
  806|      1|        assert_eq!(items[0].children().len(), 5);
  807|      1|    }
