  809|       |    #[test]
  810|      1|    fn test_deeply_nested_decomposition() {
  811|       |        // Test 3+ levels of nesting
  812|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  813|       |
  814|       |        // Level 0: Main orchestrator
  815|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  816|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  817|      1|            "orch-0".to_string(),
  818|       |            0,
  819|      1|            "Main".to_string(),
  820|       |        );
  821|       |
  822|       |        // Level 1: First decompose
  823|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  824|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  825|      1|            "orch-1".to_string(),
  826|       |            1,
  827|      1|            "Sub-1".to_string(),
  828|       |        );
  829|       |
  830|      1|        let orch1 = tree.get_agent("orch-1").unwrap();
  831|      1|        assert_eq!(orch1.parent_session_id, Some("orch-0".to_string()));
  832|       |
  833|       |        // Level 2: Second decompose
  834|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  835|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  836|      1|            "orch-2".to_string(),
  837|       |            2,
  838|      1|            "Sub-2".to_string(),
  839|       |        );
  840|       |
  841|      1|        let orch2 = tree.get_agent("orch-2").unwrap();
  842|      1|        assert_eq!(orch2.parent_session_id, Some("orch-1".to_string()));
  843|       |
  844|       |        // Level 3: Third decompose
  845|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  846|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  847|      1|            "orch-3".to_string(),
  848|       |            3,
  849|      1|            "Sub-3".to_string(),
  850|       |        );
  851|       |
  852|      1|        let orch3 = tree.get_agent("orch-3").unwrap();
  853|      1|        assert_eq!(orch3.parent_session_id, Some("orch-2".to_string()));
  854|       |
  855|       |        // Level 4: Implementer
  856|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  857|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  858|      1|            "impl-deep".to_string(),
  859|       |            4,
  860|      1|            "Deep impl".to_string(),
  861|       |        );
  862|       |
  863|      1|        let impl_deep = tree.get_agent("impl-deep").unwrap();
  864|      1|        assert_eq!(impl_deep.parent_session_id, Some("orch-3".to_string()));
  865|       |
  866|       |        // Verify tree depth
  867|      1|        let items = tree.build_tree_items(0);
  868|      1|        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
  869|       |
  870|       |        // Navigate down the tree
  871|      1|        let mut current_children = items[0].children();
  872|      1|        assert_eq!(current_children.len(), 1);
  873|       |
  874|      1|        current_children = current_children[0].children();
  875|      1|        assert_eq!(current_children.len(), 1);
  876|       |
  877|      1|        current_children = current_children[0].children();
  878|      1|        assert_eq!(current_children.len(), 1);
  879|       |
  880|       |        // Level 3 orchestrator has implementer child
  881|      1|        current_children = current_children[0].children();
  882|      1|        assert_eq!(current_children.len(), 1);
  883|       |
  884|       |        // Implementer is a leaf
  885|      1|        assert_eq!(current_children[0].children().len(), 0);
  886|      1|    }

  888|       |    #[test]
  889|      1|    fn test_status_indicators_in_display_name() {
  890|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  891|       |
  892|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  893|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  894|      1|            "running".to_string(),
  895|       |            0,
  896|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
  897|       |        );
  898|       |
  899|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  900|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
  901|      1|            "completed".to_string(),
  902|       |            0,
  903|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
  904|       |        );
  905|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("completed"), true);
  906|       |
  907|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  908|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  909|      1|            "failed".to_string(),
  910|       |            0,
  911|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
  912|       |        );
  913|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("failed"), false);
  914|       |
  915|      1|        let running = tree.get_agent("running").unwrap();
  916|       |        // Test animation frames: frame 0 should show "~", frame 30 should show "-"
  917|      1|        assert!(
  918|      1|            running.display_name(0).contains('~'),
  919|       |            "Running agent at frame 0 should have ~ indicator"
  920|       |        );
  921|      1|        assert!(
  922|      1|            running.display_name(30).contains('-'),
  923|       |            "Running agent at frame 30 should have - indicator"
  924|       |        );
  925|       |        // Frame 60 should wrap back to "~"
  926|      1|        assert!(
  927|      1|            running.display_name(60).contains('~'),
  928|       |            "Running agent at frame 60 should have ~ indicator"
  929|       |        );
  930|       |
  931|      1|        let completed = tree.get_agent("completed").unwrap();
  932|      1|        assert!(
  933|      1|            completed.display_name(0).contains('✓'),
  934|       |            "Completed agent should have checkmark"
  935|       |        );
  936|       |
  937|      1|        let failed = tree.get_agent("failed").unwrap();
  938|      1|        assert!(
  939|      1|            failed.display_name(0).contains('✗'),
  940|       |            "Failed agent should have X mark"
  941|       |        );
  942|      1|    }

  945|      1|    fn test_select_nested_agent() {
  946|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  947|       |
  948|       |        // Create a nested structure
  949|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  950|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  951|      1|            "root".to_string(),
  952|       |            0,
  953|      1|            "Root".to_string(),
  954|       |        );
  955|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  956|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  957|      1|            "child".to_string(),
  958|       |            1,
  959|      1|            "Child".to_string(),
  960|       |        );
  961|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  962|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
  963|      1|            "grandchild".to_string(),
  964|       |            2,
  965|      1|            "Grandchild".to_string(),
  966|       |        );
  967|       |

  970|       |
  971|       |        // Verify selection path is correct

  973|      1|        assert_eq!(selected, Some("grandchild"));
  974|       |
  975|       |        // The path should include all ancestors
  976|      1|        let path = tree.build_path_to_session("grandchild");
  977|      1|        assert_eq!(path, vec!["root", "child", "grandchild"]);
  978|      1|    }

  980|       |    #[test]
  981|      1|    fn test_handle_subtask_created() {
  982|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
  983|       |
  984|       |        // Start orchestrator at depth 0
  985|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
  986|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
  987|      1|            "orch-main".to_string(),
  988|       |            0,
  989|      1|            "Main task".to_string(),
  990|       |        );
  991|       |
  992|       |        // SubtaskCreated event fires when decompose is called
  993|       |        // This should not panic and should not change tree structure
  994|      1|        tree.handle_subtask_created(0, 1, "/logs/run/subtask-001");
  995|       |
  996|       |        // Verify state is still valid
  997|      1|        assert_eq!(tree.agent_count(), 1);
  998|       |
  999|       |        // Now sub-agents start at depth 1
 1000|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1001|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
 1002|      1|            "sub-planner".to_string(),
 1003|       |            1,
 1004|      1|            "Sub plan".to_string(),
 1005|       |        );
 1006|       |
 1007|       |        // Sub-planner should be correctly parented
 1008|      1|        let sub_planner = tree.get_agent("sub-planner").unwrap();
 1009|      1|        assert_eq!(sub_planner.parent_session_id, Some("orch-main".to_string()));
 1010|      1|    }
 1011|       |
 1012|       |    #[test]
 1013|      1|    fn test_full_decomposition_flow_with_subtask_created() {
 1014|       |        // This simulates the exact event sequence that occurs during decomposition
 1015|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1016|       |
 1017|       |        // 1. Main planner starts
 1018|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1019|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
 1020|      1|            "planner-main".to_string(),
 1021|       |            0,
 1022|      1|            "Create plan".to_string(),
 1023|       |        );
 1024|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("planner-main"), true);
 1025|       |
 1026|       |        // 2. Main orchestrator starts
 1027|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1028|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1029|      1|            "orch-main".to_string(),
 1030|       |            0,
 1031|      1|            "Execute plan".to_string(),
 1032|       |        );
 1033|       |
 1034|       |        // 3. Orchestrator calls decompose -> SubtaskCreated
 1035|      1|        tree.handle_subtask_created(0, 1, "/logs/run/subtask-001");
 1036|       |
 1037|       |        // 4. Sub-planner starts at depth 1
 1038|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1039|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
 1040|      1|            "planner-sub".to_string(),
 1041|       |            1,
 1042|      1|            "Create sub-plan".to_string(),
 1043|       |        );
 1044|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("planner-sub"), true);
 1045|       |
 1046|       |        // 5. Sub-orchestrator starts at depth 1
 1047|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1048|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1049|      1|            "orch-sub".to_string(),
 1050|       |            1,
 1051|      1|            "Execute sub-plan".to_string(),
 1052|       |        );
 1053|       |
 1054|       |        // 6. Sub-orchestrator spawns implementers at depth 2
 1055|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1056|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
 1057|      1|            "impl-1".to_string(),
 1058|       |            2,
 1059|      1|            "Implement task 1".to_string(),
 1060|       |        );
 1061|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("impl-1"), true);
 1062|       |
 1063|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1064|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 2 },
 1065|      1|            "impl-2".to_string(),
 1066|       |            2,
 1067|      1|            "Implement task 2".to_string(),
 1068|       |        );
 1069|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("impl-2"), true);
 1070|       |
 1071|       |        // 7. Sub-orchestrator completes
 1072|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("orch-sub"), true);
 1073|       |
 1074|       |        // 8. Main orchestrator continues with another task at depth 1
 1075|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1076|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 3 },
 1077|      1|            "impl-3".to_string(),
 1078|       |            1,
 1079|      1|            "Implement simple task".to_string(),
 1080|       |        );
 1081|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("impl-3"), true);
 1082|       |
 1083|       |        // 9. Main orchestrator completes
 1084|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("orch-main"), true);
 1085|       |
 1086|       |        // Verify the final tree structure
 1087|       |        // 7 agents: planner-main, orch-main, planner-sub, orch-sub, impl-1, impl-2, impl-3
 1088|      1|        assert_eq!(tree.agent_count(), 7);
 1089|       |
 1090|       |        // Check main planner is a root
 1091|      1|        let main_planner = tree.get_agent("planner-main").unwrap();
 1092|      1|        assert!(main_planner.parent_session_id.is_none());
 1093|      1|        assert_eq!(main_planner.status, AgentStatus::Completed);
 1094|       |
 1095|       |        // Check main orchestrator is a root with children
 1096|      1|        let main_orch = tree.get_agent("orch-main").unwrap();
 1097|      1|        assert!(main_orch.parent_session_id.is_none());
 1098|      1|        assert_eq!(main_orch.status, AgentStatus::Completed);
 1099|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1100|      1|            main_orch.children.len(),
 1101|       |            3,
 1102|       |            "Main orch should have: sub-planner, sub-orch, impl-3"
 1103|       |        );
 1104|       |
 1105|       |        // Check sub-orchestrator has implementer children
 1106|      1|        let sub_orch = tree.get_agent("orch-sub").unwrap();
 1107|      1|        assert_eq!(sub_orch.parent_session_id, Some("orch-main".to_string()));
 1108|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1109|      1|            sub_orch.children.len(),
 1110|       |            2,
 1111|       |            "Sub-orch should have 2 implementers"
 1112|       |        );
 1113|       |
 1114|       |        // Verify tree builds correctly
 1115|      1|        let items = tree.build_tree_items(0);
 1116|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1117|      1|            items.len(),
 1118|       |            2,
 1119|       |            "Should have 2 roots: main planner and main orch"
 1120|       |        );
 1121|       |
 1122|       |        // Find main orchestrator and verify its structure
 1123|      1|        let main_orch_item = items
 1124|      1|            .iter()
 1125|      2|            .find(|i| i.identifier() == "orch-main")
                           ^1
 1126|      1|            .unwrap();
 1127|      1|        assert_eq!(main_orch_item.children().len(), 3);
 1128|      1|    }
 1129|       |
 1130|       |    #[test]
 1131|      1|    fn test_count_agents_by_status() {
 1132|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1133|       |
 1134|       |        // Initially empty
 1135|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1136|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 0);
 1137|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
 1138|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 0);
 1139|       |
 1140|       |        // Add a running agent
 1141|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1142|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1143|      1|            "running-1".to_string(),
 1144|       |            0,
 1145|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1146|       |        );
 1147|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1148|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 0);
 1149|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
 1150|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 1);
 1151|       |
 1152|       |        // Add another running agent
 1153|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1154|      1|            AgentType::Planner,
 1155|      1|            "running-2".to_string(),
 1156|       |            0,
 1157|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1158|       |        );
 1159|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1160|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 0);
 1161|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
 1162|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 2);
 1163|       |
 1164|       |        // Complete one successfully
 1165|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("running-1"), true);
 1166|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1167|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 1);
 1168|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
 1169|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 1);
 1170|       |
 1171|       |        // Fail one
 1172|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("running-2"), false);
 1173|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1174|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 1);
 1175|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 1);
 1176|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 0);
 1177|       |
 1178|       |        // Add more agents with mixed statuses
 1179|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1180|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
 1181|      1|            "impl-1".to_string(),
 1182|       |            0,
 1183|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1184|       |        );
 1185|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1186|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 2 },
 1187|      1|            "impl-2".to_string(),
 1188|       |            0,
 1189|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1190|       |        );
 1191|      1|        tree.handle_agent_complete(Some("impl-1"), true);
 1192|       |
 1193|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = tree.count_agents_by_status();
 1194|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 2); // running-1, impl-1
 1195|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 1); // running-2
 1196|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 1); // impl-2
 1197|      1|    }
 1198|       |
 1199|       |    #[test]
 1200|      1|    fn test_standalone_message_not_concatenated_with_agent_message() {
 1201|       |        // Regression test for: agent output lines incorrectly joined together
 1202|       |        // Example of the problem:
 1203|       |        // > Calling: Context engine: "Help popup..."Let me gather more information...
 1204|       |        // Should be:
 1205|       |        // > Calling: Context engine: "Help popup..."
 1206|       |        // Let me gather more information...
 1207|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1208|       |
 1209|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1210|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1211|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
 1212|       |            0,
 1213|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1214|       |        );
 1215|       |
 1216|       |        // Simulate a tool call followed by an agent message
 1217|      1|        tree.handle_standalone_message(
 1218|      1|            Some("session-1"),
 1219|      1|            "> Calling: Context engine: \"Help popup implementation\"",
 1220|       |        );
 1221|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(
 1222|      1|            Some("session-1"),
 1223|      1|            "Let me gather more information about the model configuration.",
 1224|       |        );
 1225|       |
 1226|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
 1227|       |
 1228|       |        // Should have 2 separate messages, not concatenated
 1229|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 2, "Expected 2 messages, got {:?}", messages);
 1230|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1231|      1|            messages[0], "> Calling: Context engine: \"Help popup implementation\"",
 1232|       |            "First message should be the tool call"
 1233|       |        );
 1234|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1235|      1|            messages[1], "Let me gather more information about the model configuration.",
 1236|       |            "Second message should be the agent message"
 1237|       |        );
 1238|      1|    }

 1355|       |    #[test]
 1356|      1|    fn test_insert_missing_line_breaks_quote_followed_by_capital() {
 1357|       |        // The exact issue: tool call description followed by regular text in same chunk
 1358|       |        // e.g., 'Calling: Context engine: "Help popup"Let me gather...'
 1359|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1360|       |
 1361|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1362|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1363|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
 1364|       |            0,
 1365|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1366|       |        );
 1367|       |
 1368|       |        // Simulate content arriving as a single chunk without newline
 1369|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(
 1370|      1|            Some("session-1"),
 1371|      1|            "Calling: Context engine: \"Help popup implementation\"Let me gather more information.",
 1372|       |        );
 1373|       |
 1374|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
 1375|       |
 1376|       |        // Should be split into 2 messages (at the LAST closing quote before capital letter)
 1377|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 2, "Expected 2 messages, got {:?}", messages);
 1378|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1379|      1|            messages[0],
 1380|       |            "Calling: Context engine: \"Help popup implementation\""
 1381|       |        );
 1382|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[1], "Let me gather more information.");
 1383|      1|    }

 1385|       |    #[test]
 1386|      1|    fn test_insert_missing_line_breaks_only_affects_calling_pattern() {
 1387|       |        // The heuristic only applies when "Calling:" is present
 1388|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1389|       |
 1390|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1391|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1392|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
 1393|       |            0,
 1394|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1395|       |        );
 1396|       |
 1397|       |        // Without "Calling:", multiple quote-followed-by-capital patterns are NOT split
 1398|       |        // (to avoid breaking normal quoted text in code/prose)
 1399|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(
 1400|      1|            Some("session-1"),
 1401|      1|            "First \"query\"Second \"query\"Third text.",
 1402|       |        );
 1403|       |
 1404|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
 1405|       |
 1406|       |        // Should NOT be split - no "Calling:" pattern
 1407|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 1, "Expected 1 message, got {:?}", messages);
 1408|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[0], "First \"query\"Second \"query\"Third text.");
 1409|      1|    }
 1410|       |
 1411|       |    #[test]
 1412|      1|    fn test_insert_missing_line_breaks_preserves_normal_text() {
 1413|       |        // Normal text with quotes should not be affected
 1414|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1415|       |
 1416|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1417|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1418|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),
 1419|       |            0,
 1420|      1|            "Task".to_string(),
 1421|       |        );
 1422|       |
 1423|       |        // Normal sentence with quotes - no "Calling:" so no splitting
 1424|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(
 1425|      1|            Some("session-1"),
 1426|      1|            "I said \"hello\" to him and he replied \"hi\" back.",
 1427|       |        );
 1428|       |
 1429|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();
 1430|       |
 1431|       |        // Should remain as single message
 1432|      1|        assert_eq!(messages.len(), 1, "Expected 1 message, got {:?}", messages);
 1433|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1434|      1|            messages[0],
 1435|       |            "I said \"hello\" to him and he replied \"hi\" back."
 1436|       |        );
 1437|      1|    }

 1440|      1|    fn test_calling_prefix_is_standalone() {

 1442|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1443|       |
 1444|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1445|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1446|      1|            "session-1".to_string(),

 1449|       |        );

 1453|       |

 1455|      1|        tree.handle_agent_message(Some("session-1"), "Calling: codebase-retrieval");
 1456|       |
 1457|      1|        let messages = tree.get_messages("session-1").unwrap();

 1462|      1|        assert_eq!(messages[1], "Calling: codebase-retrieval");
 1463|      1|    }

 1512|       |    #[test]
 1513|      1|    fn test_visible_items_collapsed_parent() {
 1514|       |        // By default, nodes are collapsed, so children should NOT be visible
 1515|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1516|       |
 1517|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1518|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1519|      1|            "root".to_string(),
 1520|       |            0,
 1521|      1|            "Root".to_string(),
 1522|       |        );
 1523|       |
 1524|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1525|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
 1526|      1|            "child".to_string(),
 1527|       |            1,
 1528|      1|            "Child".to_string(),
 1529|       |        );
 1530|       |
 1531|       |        // Parent is collapsed by default
 1532|      1|        let visible = tree.visible_items();
 1533|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1534|       |            visible,
 1535|      1|            vec!["root"],
 1536|       |            "Collapsed parent should hide children"
 1537|       |        );
 1538|      1|    }

 1540|       |    #[test]
 1541|      1|    fn test_visible_items_expanded_parent() {
 1542|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1543|       |
 1544|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1545|      1|            AgentType::Orchestrator,
 1546|      1|            "root".to_string(),
 1547|       |            0,
 1548|      1|            "Root".to_string(),
 1549|       |        );
 1550|       |
 1551|      1|        tree.handle_agent_started(
 1552|      1|            AgentType::Implementer { index: 1 },
 1553|      1|            "child".to_string(),
 1554|       |            1,
 1555|      1|            "Child".to_string(),
 1556|       |        );
 1557|       |
 1558|       |        // Expand the root node
 1559|      1|        tree.tree_state.open(vec!["root".to_string()]);
 1560|       |
 1561|      1|        let visible = tree.visible_items();
 1562|      1|        assert_eq!(
 1563|       |            visible,

 1566|       |        );

 1569|       |    #[test]
 1570|      1|    fn test_visible_items_deeply_nested_partial_expansion() {
 1571|      1|        let mut tree = AgentTreeState::new();
 1572|       |

   40|       |/// Runs the TUI on a dedicated `std::thread`.
   41|       |///
   42|       |/// This is the main entry point for the TUI. It:
   43|       |/// 1. Sets up a panic hook to restore the terminal on crash
   44|       |/// 2. Initializes the terminal (raw mode, alternate screen)
   45|       |/// 3. Runs the main event loop
   46|       |/// 4. Restores the terminal on exit (normal, error, or panic)
   47|       |///
   48|       |/// # Thread Safety
   49|       |///
   50|       |/// This function is designed to run on a `std::thread` (not a `tokio` task)
   51|       |/// to avoid blocking the async runtime. Communication with the main app
   52|       |/// happens via the provided `event_rx` channel.
   53|       |///
   54|       |/// # Arguments
   55|       |///
   56|       |/// * `event_rx` - Channel receiver for [`LogEvent`]s from the main application
   57|       |///
   58|       |/// # Errors
   59|       |///
   60|       |/// Returns an error if terminal initialization or cleanup fails.
   61|       |#[allow(dead_code)] // Public API for simpler TUI usage without config
   62|      0|pub fn run_tui(event_rx: Receiver<LogEvent>) -> Result<()> {
   63|       |    // Install panic hook to restore terminal on crash
   64|      0|    install_panic_hook();
   65|       |
   66|       |    // Initialize terminal
   67|      0|    let mut terminal = init_terminal().context("Failed to initialize terminal")?;
   68|       |
   69|       |    // Run the main event loop (capturing result)
   70|      0|    let result = run_event_loop(&mut terminal, event_rx, None, None);
   71|       |
   72|       |    // Always restore terminal, regardless of how we exit
   73|      0|    restore_terminal(&mut terminal).context("Failed to restore terminal")?;
   74|       |
   75|      0|    result
   76|      0|}

  161|       |/// Installs a panic hook that restores the terminal before the default panic handler runs.
  162|       |///
  163|       |/// This ensures that even if the application panics, the terminal will be left in a usable state.
  164|      0|fn install_panic_hook() {
  165|      0|    let original_hook = std::panic::take_hook();
  166|       |
  167|      0|    std::panic::set_hook(Box::new(move |panic_info| {
  168|       |        // Attempt to restore terminal - ignore errors since we're already panicking
  169|      0|        let _ = disable_raw_mode();
  170|      0|        let _ = execute!(io::stdout(), DisableMouseCapture, LeaveAlternateScreen);
  171|       |
  172|       |        // Call the original panic hook
  173|      0|        original_hook(panic_info);
  174|      0|    }));
  175|      0|}

  184|      0|        .context("Failed to enter alternate screen and enable mouse capture")?;
  185|      0|    let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);
  186|      0|    Terminal::new(backend).context("Failed to create terminal")

  190|       |///

  194|      0|    execute!(
  195|      0|        terminal.backend_mut(),
  196|       |        DisableMouseCapture,
  197|       |        LeaveAlternateScreen
  198|       |    )
  199|      0|    .context("Failed to disable mouse capture and leave alternate screen")?;
  200|      0|    terminal.show_cursor().context("Failed to show cursor")?;
  201|      0|    Ok(())

  203|       |
  204|       |/// Main event loop for the TUI.
  205|       |///
  206|       |/// Polls for terminal events, processes `LogEvent`s from the channel,
  207|       |/// and renders frames at up to 60 FPS.
  208|       |///
  209|       |/// # Event Processing
  210|       |///
  211|       |/// The loop follows this structure on each iteration:
  212|       |/// 1. **Process `LogEvent`s** - Non-blocking batch processing of all pending events
  213|       |/// 2. **Poll for keyboard events** - With a short timeout to stay responsive
  214|       |/// 3. **Render frame** - Only when enough time has passed since the last frame
  215|       |///
  216|       |/// # Performance
  217|       |///
  218|       |/// - Frame rate is capped at 60 FPS to avoid excessive CPU usage
  219|       |/// - `LogEvent`s are batch processed for efficiency
  220|       |/// - Terminal resize events trigger immediate redraw
  221|       |const MAX_EVENTS_PER_FRAME: usize = 100;
  222|       |
  223|       |#[allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_value)] // Receiver ownership transfer is intentional
  224|      0|fn run_event_loop(
  225|      0|    terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>,
  226|      0|    event_rx: Receiver<LogEvent>,
  227|      0|    model_config: Option<ModelConfig>,
  228|      0|    config_tx: Option<SyncSender<ModelConfigUpdate>>,
  229|      0|) -> Result<()> {
  230|       |    // Initialize TUI state (with model config if provided)
  231|       |    // TuiState now includes logger_state for the app logs panel
  232|      0|    let mut state = match model_config {
  233|      0|        Some(config) => TuiState::with_model_config(config),
  234|      0|        None => TuiState::new(),
  235|       |    };
  236|       |
  237|       |    // Frame timing
  238|      0|    let mut last_frame = Instant::now();
  239|       |
  240|       |    loop {
  241|       |        // =====================================================================
  242|       |        // 1. Process all available LogEvents (non-blocking, batched)
  243|       |        // =====================================================================
  244|      0|        let mut events_processed = 0;
  245|      0|        while let Ok(log_event) = event_rx.try_recv() {
  246|      0|            state.handle_event(log_event);
  247|      0|            events_processed += 1;
  248|       |
  249|       |            // Limit events per frame to prevent blocking on burst
  250|      0|            if events_processed >= MAX_EVENTS_PER_FRAME {
  251|      0|                break;
  252|      0|            }
  253|       |        }
  254|       |
  255|       |        // =====================================================================
  256|       |        // 2. Poll for keyboard events (with short timeout for responsiveness)
  257|       |        // =====================================================================
  258|       |        // Use a short poll timeout to stay responsive while allowing batching
  259|      0|        let poll_timeout = Duration::from_millis(16); // ~60 FPS budget
  260|       |
  261|      0|        if event::poll(poll_timeout).context("Failed to poll for events")? {
  262|      0|            match event::read().context("Failed to read event")? {
  263|      0|                CrosstermEvent::Key(key) => {
  264|       |                    // Only process key press events (not release or repeat)
  265|      0|                    if key.kind == KeyEventKind::Press {
  266|       |                        // Calculate layout from terminal size for accurate panel dimensions
  267|      0|                        let layout = terminal
  268|      0|                            .size()
  269|      0|                            .map(|r| calculate_layout(Rect::new(0, 0, r.width, r.height)))
  270|      0|                            .unwrap_or_default();
  271|       |
  272|       |                        // Handle key event through the event handler
  273|      0|                        match handle_key_event(&mut state, key, &layout) {
  274|      0|                            EventResult::Quit => break,
  275|      0|                            EventResult::Continue => {}
  276|       |                        }
  277|      0|                    }
  278|       |                }
  279|      0|                CrosstermEvent::Resize(_width, _height) => {
  280|      0|                    // Terminal will automatically handle resize on next draw
  281|      0|                    // Force a redraw by resetting frame timing
  282|      0|                    last_frame = Instant::now()
  283|      0|                        .checked_sub(TARGET_FRAME_DURATION)
  284|      0|                        .unwrap_or(last_frame);
  285|      0|                }
  286|      0|                CrosstermEvent::Mouse(mouse_event) => {
  287|       |                    // Get current terminal size to calculate layout
  288|      0|                    if let Ok(size) = terminal.size() {
  289|      0|                        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, size.width, size.height);
  290|      0|                        let layout = calculate_layout(area);
  291|       |
  292|      0|                        match mouse_event.kind {
  293|       |                            // Handle mouse clicks for panel focus switching
  294|      0|                            event::MouseEventKind::Down(_) => {
  295|      0|                                handle_mouse_click(&mut state, mouse_event, &layout);
  296|      0|                            }
  297|       |                            // Handle mouse wheel scroll up
  298|      0|                            event::MouseEventKind::ScrollUp => {
  299|      0|                                handle_mouse_scroll(
  300|      0|                                    &mut state,
  301|      0|                                    mouse_event,
  302|      0|                                    &layout,
  303|      0|                                    ScrollDirection::Up,
  304|      0|                                );
  305|      0|                            }
  306|       |                            // Handle mouse wheel scroll down
  307|      0|                            event::MouseEventKind::ScrollDown => {
  308|      0|                                handle_mouse_scroll(
  309|      0|                                    &mut state,
  310|      0|                                    mouse_event,
  311|      0|                                    &layout,
  312|      0|                                    ScrollDirection::Down,
  313|      0|                                );
  314|      0|                            }
  315|       |                            // Ignore other mouse events (drag, move, etc.)
  316|      0|                            _ => {}
  317|       |                        }
  318|      0|                    }
  319|       |                }
  320|       |                // Ignore other events (FocusGained, FocusLost, Paste)
  321|      0|                _ => {}
  322|       |            }
  323|      0|        }
  324|       |
  325|       |        // =====================================================================
  326|       |        // 3. Send pending config updates to the App (if channel is available)
  327|       |        // =====================================================================
  328|      0|        if let Some(ref tx) = config_tx {
  329|      0|            if let Some(update) = state.take_pending_config_update() {
  330|       |                // Try to send the update; if channel is full or disconnected, log and continue
  331|      0|                if let Err(e) = tx.try_send(update) {
  332|      0|                    tracing::warn!("Failed to send model config update to App: {}", e);
  333|      0|                }
  334|      0|            }
  335|      0|        }
  336|       |
  337|       |        // =====================================================================
  338|       |        // 4. Render frame (respecting frame rate limit)
  339|       |        // =====================================================================
  340|      0|        if last_frame.elapsed() >= TARGET_FRAME_DURATION {
  341|       |            // Increment animation frame counter (wrapping to prevent overflow)
  342|      0|            state.animation_frame = state.animation_frame.wrapping_add(1);
  343|       |
  344|      0|            terminal
  345|      0|                .draw(|frame| {
  346|      0|                    render_ui_frame(frame, &mut state);
  347|      0|                })
  348|      0|                .context("Failed to draw frame")?;

  350|      0|        }
  351|       |    }

  353|      0|    Ok(())

  356|       |/// Main event loop with backend selection support.
  357|       |///
  358|       |/// This version handles the complete startup flow:
  359|       |/// 1. Shows splash screen immediately
  360|       |/// 2. Receives available backends and shows selection popup if multiple
  361|       |/// 3. Sends selected backend back to App
  362|       |/// 4. Receives model config and continues normal operation
  363|       |#[allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_value)]

  366|      0|    terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>,
  367|      0|    event_rx: Receiver<LogEvent>,
  368|      0|    initial_config_rx: Receiver<ModelConfig>,
  369|      0|    config_tx: SyncSender<ModelConfigUpdate>,
  370|      0|    available_backends_rx: Receiver<Vec<BackendKind>>,
  371|      0|    selected_backend_tx: SyncSender<BackendKind>,
  372|      0|) -> Result<()> {
  373|       |    // Initialize TUI state with splash visible
  374|      0|    let mut state = TuiState::new();
  375|       |
  376|       |    // Track whether we've received model config yet
  377|      0|    let mut model_config_received = false;
  378|       |
  379|       |    // Frame timing
  380|      0|    let mut last_frame = Instant::now();
  381|       |
  382|       |    loop {
  383|       |        // =====================================================================
  384|       |        // 1. Check for available backends (non-blocking)
  385|       |        // =====================================================================
  386|       |        // Keep checking until we receive backends, even if splash is dismissed.
  387|       |        // This prevents a race condition where backends arrive right as splash
  388|       |        // auto-dismisses after 5 seconds.
  389|      0|        if !state.backends_received && !state.backend_selection_state.visible {
  390|      0|            if let Ok(backends) = available_backends_rx.try_recv() {
  391|      0|                state.backends_received = true;

  393|       |                    // Multiple backends available - show selection popup
  394|       |                    // Also show splash again if it was dismissed (to show popup on top)
  395|      0|                    if !state.splash_visible {
  396|      0|                        state.splash_visible = true;
  397|      0|                    }
  398|      0|                    state.backend_selection_state = BackendSelectionState::with_backends(backends);
  399|      0|                } else if let Some(&backend) = backends.first() {
  400|      0|                    // Single backend - send it back immediately
  401|      0|                    let _ = selected_backend_tx.try_send(backend);
  402|      0|                }
  403|      0|            }
  404|      0|        }
  405|       |
  406|       |        // =====================================================================
  407|       |        // 2. Check for model config (non-blocking, keeps splash running)
  408|       |        // =====================================================================

  411|      0|                let available_tiers: Vec<_> = config.available_tiers.iter().copied().collect();
  412|      0|                state.model_config = config;
  413|      0|                state.available_tiers = available_tiers;
  414|      0|                model_config_received = true;
  415|      0|            }
  416|      0|        }
  417|       |

  419|       |        // 3. Process all available LogEvents (non-blocking, batched)
  420|       |        // =====================================================================
  421|      0|        let mut events_processed = 0;

  423|      0|            state.handle_event(log_event);
  424|      0|            events_processed += 1;
  425|       |
  426|      0|            if events_processed >= MAX_EVENTS_PER_FRAME {
  427|      0|                break;
  428|      0|            }
  429|       |        }
  430|       |
  431|       |        // =====================================================================
  432|       |        // 4. Poll for keyboard events (with short timeout for responsiveness)

  435|       |

  444|      0|                                KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => {
  445|      0|                                    state.backend_selection_state.select_previous();
  446|      0|                                }
  447|      0|                                KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => {
  448|      0|                                    state.backend_selection_state.select_next();
  449|      0|                                }

  451|      0|                                    if let Some(backend) =
  452|      0|                                        state.backend_selection_state.confirm_selection()

  454|      0|                                        let _ = selected_backend_tx.try_send(backend);
  455|      0|                                    }
  456|       |                                }
  457|      0|                                _ => {}
  458|       |                            }

  460|       |                            // Normal key handling
  461|      0|                            let layout = terminal
  462|      0|                                .size()
  463|      0|                                .map(|r| calculate_layout(Rect::new(0, 0, r.width, r.height)))
  464|      0|                                .unwrap_or_default();
  465|       |

  467|      0|                                EventResult::Quit => break,
  468|      0|                                EventResult::Continue => {}
  469|       |                            }
  470|       |                        }
  471|      0|                    }
  472|       |                }
  473|      0|                CrosstermEvent::Resize(_width, _height) => {
  474|      0|                    last_frame = Instant::now()
  475|      0|                        .checked_sub(TARGET_FRAME_DURATION)
  476|      0|                        .unwrap_or(last_frame);
  477|      0|                }
  478|      0|                CrosstermEvent::Mouse(mouse_event) => {
  479|      0|                    if !state.backend_selection_state.visible {

  487|      0|                                }

  494|      0|                                    );

  502|      0|                                    );

  504|      0|                                _ => {}
  505|       |                            }
  506|      0|                        }
  507|      0|                    }
  508|       |                }
  509|      0|                _ => {}
  510|       |            }
  511|      0|        }

  514|       |        // 5. Send pending config updates to the App

  539|       |
  540|       |/// Renders a complete UI frame with all widgets.
  541|       |///
  542|       |/// This function orchestrates rendering of all panels:
  543|       |/// - Splash screen (if visible, replaces everything)
  544|       |/// - Agent tree (left)
  545|       |/// - Agent output (center)
  546|       |/// - Task list (right)
  547|       |/// - App logs (bottom)
  548|       |/// - Status bar (very bottom)
  549|       |/// - Help overlay (if visible)
  550|      4|fn render_ui_frame(frame: &mut Frame, state: &mut TuiState) {
  551|      4|    let area = frame.area();
  552|       |
  553|       |    // Show splash screen if visible (replaces the entire UI)
  554|       |    // Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds (300 frames at 60fps), but only if backends have been received
  555|      4|    if state.splash_visible {
  556|       |        // Always render the splash screen first
  557|      3|        render_splash_screen(frame, area, state.animation_frame);
  558|       |
  559|       |        // If backend selection popup is visible, render it on top of splash
  560|      3|        if state.backend_selection_state.visible {
  561|      1|            render_backend_selection_popup(frame, area, &state.backend_selection_state);
  562|      1|            return;
  563|      2|        }
  564|       |
  565|       |        // Auto-dismiss splash after 5 seconds, but ONLY if backends have been received.
  566|       |        // This prevents the race condition where splash dismisses before we can show
  567|       |        // the backend selection popup.
  568|      2|        if state.animation_frame >= 300 && state.backends_received {
  569|      1|            state.dismiss_splash();
  570|      1|        } else {
  571|      1|            return;
  572|       |        }
  573|      1|    }
  574|       |
  575|       |    // Calculate layout for all panels
  576|      2|    let layout = calculate_layout(area);
  577|       |
  578|       |    // Check if terminal is too small
  579|      2|    if layout.is_too_small() {
  580|      0|        render_too_small_message(frame, area);
  581|      0|        return;
  582|      2|    }
  583|       |
  584|       |    // Determine focus state for each panel
  585|      2|    let agent_tree_focused = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::AgentTree;
  586|      2|    let agent_output_focused = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::AgentOutput;
  587|      2|    let task_list_focused = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::TaskList;
  588|      2|    let app_logs_focused = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::AppLogs;
  589|       |
  590|       |    // Render left panel (agent tree)
  591|      2|    render_agent_tree(frame, layout.agent_tree, state, agent_tree_focused);
  592|       |
  593|       |    // Render middle panel: task detail when task is selected in focused task list,
  594|       |    // otherwise show agent output
  595|      2|    if let Some(task) = state.selected_task() {
                              ^0
  596|      0|        // Clone the task data to avoid borrow checker issues with state
  597|      0|        let task_clone = task.clone();
  598|      0|        render_task_detail(
  599|      0|            frame,
  600|      0|            layout.agent_output,
  601|      0|            state,
  602|      0|            &task_clone,
  603|      0|            task_list_focused,
  604|      0|        );
  605|      2|    } else {
  606|      2|        render_agent_output(frame, layout.agent_output, state, agent_output_focused);
  607|      2|    }
  608|       |
  609|       |    // Render right panel (task list)
  610|      2|    render_task_list(frame, layout.task_list, state, task_list_focused);
  611|      2|    render_app_logs(frame, layout.app_logs, state, app_logs_focused);
  612|      2|    render_status_bar(frame, layout.status_bar, state);
  613|       |
  614|       |    // Render overlays last (on top of everything)
  615|      2|    if state.help_visible {
  616|      1|        render_help_overlay(frame, area);
  617|      1|    }
  618|       |

  620|      1|        render_settings_overlay(frame, area, state);
  621|      1|    }

    1|       |//! Keyboard event handling.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles keyboard events and routes them to the appropriate
    4|       |//! handlers based on focus state.
    5|       |
    6|       |use crossterm::event::{
    7|       |    KeyCode as CrosstermKeyCode, KeyEvent as CrosstermKeyEvent,
    8|       |    KeyModifiers as CrosstermKeyModifiers,
    9|       |};
   10|       |
   11|       |use crate::tui::layout::PanelLayout;
   12|       |use crate::tui::state::{FocusedPanel, TuiState};
   13|       |use crate::tui::widgets::{handle_agent_output_key, handle_agent_tree_key, handle_task_detail_key};
   14|       |
   15|       |use super::EventResult;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Handles a keyboard event and updates TUI state accordingly.
   18|       |///
   19|       |/// This function processes keyboard input in two phases:
   20|       |///
   21|       |/// 1. **Global keys** - Handled regardless of panel focus
   22|       |/// 2. **Panel-specific keys** - Routed to focused panel's handler
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// # Global Keys
   25|       |///
   26|       |/// | Key | Action |
   27|       |/// |-----|--------|
   28|       |/// | `Tab` | Cycle focus: `AgentTree` → `AgentOutput` → `TaskList` → `AppLogs` → `AgentTree` |
   29|       |/// | `Shift+Tab` | Cycle focus in reverse order |
   30|       |/// | `q` | Quit the TUI |
   31|       |/// | `?` | Toggle help overlay |
   32|       |/// | `Esc` | Close help overlay if visible |
   33|     56|pub fn handle_key_event(
   34|     56|    state: &mut TuiState,
   35|     56|    key: CrosstermKeyEvent,
   36|     56|    layout: &PanelLayout,
   37|     56|) -> EventResult {
   38|       |    // Phase 1: Handle global keys
   39|     56|    if let Some(result) = handle_global_key(state, &key) {
                              ^36
   40|     36|        return result;
   41|     20|    }
   42|       |
   43|       |    // Phase 2: Route to panel-specific handler based on current focus
   44|     20|    handle_panel_key(state, &key, layout);
   45|       |
   46|     20|    EventResult::Continue
   47|     56|}
   48|       |
   49|       |/// Handles global keys that work regardless of panel focus.
   50|       |///
   51|       |/// Returns `Some(EventResult)` if the key was handled, `None` if not a global key.
   52|       |#[allow(clippy::missing_const_for_fn)] // Uses non-const match expressions
   53|     56|fn handle_global_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: &CrosstermKeyEvent) -> Option<EventResult> {
   54|       |    // Handle splash screen dismissal first (any key dismisses it)
   55|     56|    if state.splash_visible {
   56|      0|        state.dismiss_splash();
   57|      0|        return Some(EventResult::Continue);
   58|     56|    }
   59|       |
   60|       |    // Handle settings overlay keys first (modal)
   61|     56|    if state.settings_visible {
   62|     16|        return handle_settings_key(state, key);
   63|     40|    }
   64|       |
   65|      0|    match key.code {
   66|       |        // Tab: cycle focus forward (without Shift)
   67|      8|        CrosstermKeyCode::Tab if !key.modifiers.contains(CrosstermKeyModifiers::SHIFT) => {
   68|      8|            state.cycle_focus();
   69|      8|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
   70|       |        }
   71|       |
   72|       |        // BackTab (Shift+Tab on most terminals): cycle focus backward
   73|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::BackTab => {
   74|      2|            state.cycle_focus_reverse();
   75|      2|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
   76|       |        }
   77|       |
   78|       |        // Tab with Shift modifier (some terminals): cycle focus backward
   79|      0|        CrosstermKeyCode::Tab if key.modifiers.contains(CrosstermKeyModifiers::SHIFT) => {
   80|      0|            state.cycle_focus_reverse();
   81|      0|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
   82|       |        }
   83|       |
   84|       |        // q: quit
   85|      5|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('q') => Some(EventResult::Quit),
   86|       |
   87|       |        // Ctrl+C: quit
   88|      0|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('c') if key.modifiers.contains(CrosstermKeyModifiers::CONTROL) => {
   89|      0|            Some(EventResult::Quit)
   90|       |        }
   91|       |
   92|       |        // Esc: quit (or close help/settings overlay if visible)
   93|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Esc => {
   94|      2|            if state.help_visible {
   95|      1|                state.help_visible = false;
   96|      1|                Some(EventResult::Continue)
   97|       |            } else {
   98|      1|                Some(EventResult::Quit)
   99|       |            }
  100|       |        }
  101|       |
  102|       |        // ?: toggle help overlay
  103|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('?') => {
  104|      2|            state.toggle_help();
  105|      2|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  106|       |        }
  107|       |
  108|       |        // s: toggle settings overlay
  109|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('s') => {
  110|      1|            state.toggle_settings();
  111|      1|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  112|       |        }
  113|       |
  114|     20|        _ => None,
  115|       |    }
  116|     56|}
  117|       |
  118|       |/// Handles keyboard input when the settings overlay is visible.
  119|       |///
  120|       |/// The settings overlay is modal - all keys are captured here.
  121|       |#[allow(clippy::unnecessary_wraps)] // Returns Option for consistency with other key handlers
  122|     16|fn handle_settings_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: &CrosstermKeyEvent) -> Option<EventResult> {
  123|     16|    let available_count = state.available_tiers.len();
  124|       |
  125|      0|    match key.code {
  126|       |        // Esc: close settings
  127|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Esc => {
  128|      1|            state.settings_visible = false;
  129|       |            // Discard pending changes on close
  130|      1|            state.settings_state.clear_pending();
  131|      1|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  132|       |        }
  133|       |
  134|       |        // Tab: switch between agent type tabs
  135|      3|        CrosstermKeyCode::Tab if !key.modifiers.contains(CrosstermKeyModifiers::SHIFT) => {
  136|      3|            state.settings_state.next_agent_type();
  137|      3|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  138|       |        }
  139|       |
  140|       |        // Shift+Tab or BackTab: switch tabs in reverse
  141|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::BackTab | CrosstermKeyCode::Tab
  142|      0|            if key.modifiers.contains(CrosstermKeyModifiers::SHIFT)
  143|      0|                || key.code == CrosstermKeyCode::BackTab =>
  144|       |        {
  145|      0|            state.settings_state.prev_agent_type();
  146|      0|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  147|       |        }
  148|       |
  149|       |        // Left: switch to previous agent type tab
  150|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Left => {
  151|      2|            state.settings_state.prev_agent_type();
  152|      2|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  153|       |        }
  154|       |
  155|       |        // Right: switch to next agent type tab
  156|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Right => {
  157|      2|            state.settings_state.next_agent_type();
  158|      2|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  159|       |        }
  160|       |
  161|       |        // Up: navigate to previous model
  162|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Up => {
  163|      1|            state.settings_state.select_previous_model(available_count);
  164|      1|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  165|       |        }
  166|       |
  167|       |        // Down: navigate to next model
  168|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Down => {
  169|      4|            state.settings_state.select_next_model(available_count);
  170|      4|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  171|       |        }
  172|       |
  173|       |        // Enter: select the highlighted model tier
  174|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Enter => {
  175|      1|            if let Some(&tier) = state
  176|      1|                .available_tiers
  177|      1|                .get(state.settings_state.selected_model_index)
  178|      1|            {
  179|      1|                state.settings_state.set_pending_model(tier);
  180|      1|            }
                          ^0
  181|      1|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  182|       |        }
  183|       |
  184|       |        // s: apply changes and close settings (same key that opens it)
  185|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('s') => {
  186|       |            // Apply any pending changes before closing
  187|      1|            state.apply_settings_changes();
  188|      1|            state.settings_visible = false;
  189|      1|            Some(EventResult::Continue)
  190|       |        }
  191|       |
  192|       |        // Explicitly handle 'q' in settings to not quit (same as default but documented)
  193|       |        // Fall through to default for all other keys
  194|       |        #[allow(clippy::match_same_arms)]
  195|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('q') => Some(EventResult::Continue),
  196|       |
  197|      0|        _ => Some(EventResult::Continue),
  198|       |    }
  199|     16|}
  200|       |
  201|       |/// Routes key events to the appropriate panel handler based on current focus.
  202|     20|fn handle_panel_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: &CrosstermKeyEvent, layout: &PanelLayout) {
  203|       |    // If help or settings is visible, don't process panel keys
  204|     20|    if state.help_visible || state.settings_visible {
                                           ^19
  205|      1|        return;
  206|     19|    }
  207|       |
  208|     19|    match state.current_focus {
  209|      2|        FocusedPanel::AgentTree => handle_agent_tree_panel_key(state, key),
  210|      0|        FocusedPanel::AgentOutput => {
  211|      0|            handle_agent_output_panel_key(
  212|      0|                state,
  213|      0|                key,
  214|      0|                layout.agent_output.height,
  215|      0|                layout.agent_output.width,
  216|      0|            );
  217|      0|        }
  218|      5|        FocusedPanel::TaskList => {
  219|      5|            // Pass both task list height and agent output height (where task detail is shown)
  220|      5|            handle_task_list_panel_key(
  221|      5|                state,
  222|      5|                key,
  223|      5|                layout.task_list.height,
  224|      5|                layout.agent_output.height,
  225|      5|            );
  226|      5|        }
  227|     12|        FocusedPanel::AppLogs => handle_app_logs_panel_key(state, key),
  228|       |    }
  229|     20|}

  235|       |/// Handles keys for the Agent Tree panel.
  236|       |///
  237|       |/// Keys:
  238|       |/// - `↑`/`↓`: Navigate agents in tree
  239|       |/// - `←`/`→`: Collapse/expand tree nodes
  240|       |/// - `Enter`: Select agent for detail view (toggles expand/collapse)
  241|       |/// - `f`: Toggle auto-follow mode
  242|      2|fn handle_agent_tree_panel_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: &CrosstermKeyEvent) {
  243|      2|    match key.code {
  244|      2|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('f') => {
  245|      2|            state.toggle_auto_follow();
  246|      2|        }
  247|       |        // Delegate tree navigation to the widget handler
  248|      0|        _ => {
  249|      0|            handle_agent_tree_key(state, key.code);
  250|      0|        }
  251|       |    }
  252|      2|}
  253|       |
  254|       |/// Handles keys for the Agent Output panel.
  255|       |///
  256|       |/// Keys:
  257|       |/// - `PgUp`/`PgDn`: Scroll output history by page
  258|       |/// - `Home`/`End`: Jump to top/bottom of output
  259|       |/// - `↑`/`↓` or `k`/`j`: Scroll by single line
  260|      0|fn handle_agent_output_panel_key(
  261|      0|    state: &mut TuiState,
  262|      0|    key: &CrosstermKeyEvent,
  263|      0|    visible_height: u16,
  264|      0|    visible_width: u16,
  265|      0|) {
  266|       |    // Delegate to the widget handler which handles all scrolling
  267|      0|    handle_agent_output_key(state, key.code, visible_height, visible_width);
  268|      0|}
  269|       |
  270|       |/// Handles keys for the Task List panel.
  271|       |///
  272|       |/// Keys:
  273|       |/// - `↑`/`↓` or `j`/`k`: Navigate tasks in the task list
  274|       |/// - `PgUp`/`PgDn`: Scroll task detail (when a task is selected), otherwise scroll task list
  275|       |/// - `Home`/`End`: Jump to first/last task
  276|      5|fn handle_task_list_panel_key(
  277|      5|    state: &mut TuiState,
  278|      5|    key: &CrosstermKeyEvent,
  279|      5|    task_list_height: u16,
  280|      5|    task_detail_height: u16,
  281|      5|) {
  282|      5|    let page_size = task_list_height.saturating_sub(2) as usize; // Account for borders
  283|       |
  284|       |    // Check if a task is selected (task detail is showing)
  285|      5|    let task_selected = state.task_list_state.get_selected_task().is_some();
  286|       |
  287|      5|    match key.code {
  288|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Up | CrosstermKeyCode::Char('k') => {
  289|      1|            state.task_list_state.select_previous();
  290|      1|        }
  291|      2|        CrosstermKeyCode::Down | CrosstermKeyCode::Char('j') => {
  292|      2|            state.task_list_state.select_next();
  293|      2|        }
  294|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::PageUp => {
  295|       |            // When a task is selected, scroll the task detail panel
  296|      0|            if task_selected {
  297|      0|                handle_task_detail_key(state, key.code, task_detail_height);
  298|      0|            } else {
  299|       |                // Move selection up by page size in the task list
  300|      0|                for _ in 0..page_size {
  301|      0|                    state.task_list_state.select_previous();
  302|      0|                }
  303|       |            }
  304|       |        }
  305|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::PageDown => {
  306|       |            // When a task is selected, scroll the task detail panel
  307|      0|            if task_selected {
  308|      0|                handle_task_detail_key(state, key.code, task_detail_height);
  309|      0|            } else {
  310|       |                // Move selection down by page size in the task list
  311|      0|                for _ in 0..page_size {
  312|      0|                    state.task_list_state.select_next();
  313|      0|                }
  314|       |            }
  315|       |        }
  316|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::Home => {
  317|       |            // Jump to first task
  318|      1|            if !state.task_list_state.is_empty() {
  319|      1|                state.task_list_state.selected_index = Some(0);
  320|      1|            }
                          ^0
  321|       |        }
  322|       |        CrosstermKeyCode::End => {
  323|       |            // Jump to last task
  324|      1|            let len = state.task_list_state.len();
  325|      1|            if len > 0 {
  326|      1|                state.task_list_state.selected_index = Some(len - 1);
  327|      1|            }
                          ^0
  328|       |        }
  329|      0|        _ => {}
  330|       |    }
  331|      5|}
  332|       |
  333|       |/// Handles keys for the App Logs panel.
  334|       |///
  335|       |/// Keys are delegated to tui-logger's built-in controls:
  336|       |/// - `h`: Toggle target selector (show/hide log targets)
  337|       |/// - `←`/`→`: Filter by log level (decrease/increase minimum level)
  338|       |/// - `PgUp`/`PgDn`: Scroll logs up/down
  339|       |/// - `Space`: Toggle focus between target list and log view (when target selector visible)
  340|       |/// - `↑`/`↓`: Navigate targets (when target selector visible) or scroll logs
  341|     12|fn handle_app_logs_panel_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: &CrosstermKeyEvent) {
  342|       |    use tui_logger::TuiWidgetEvent;
  343|       |
  344|     12|    match key.code {
  345|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::PageUp => {
  346|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::PrevPageKey);
  347|      1|        }
  348|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::PageDown => {
  349|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::NextPageKey);
  350|      1|        }
  351|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Up | CrosstermKeyCode::Char('k') => {
  352|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::UpKey);
  353|      1|        }
  354|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Down | CrosstermKeyCode::Char('j') => {
  355|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::DownKey);
  356|      1|        }
  357|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Home => {
  358|      1|            state.app_logs_scroll = 0;
  359|      1|        }
  360|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('h') => {
  361|      1|            // Toggle target selector visibility
  362|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::HideKey);
  363|      1|        }
  364|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Left => {
  365|      1|            // Decrease log level filter
  366|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::LeftKey);
  367|      1|        }
  368|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Right => {
  369|      1|            // Increase log level filter
  370|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::RightKey);
  371|      1|        }
  372|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char(' ') => {
  373|      1|            // Toggle focus between target list and log view
  374|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::SpaceKey);
  375|      1|        }
  376|      0|        CrosstermKeyCode::Esc => {
  377|      0|            // Exit scroll mode
  378|      0|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::EscapeKey);
  379|      0|        }
  380|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('+') => {
  381|      1|            // Increase capture level filter
  382|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::PlusKey);
  383|      1|        }
  384|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('-') => {
  385|      1|            // Decrease capture level filter
  386|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::MinusKey);
  387|      1|        }
  388|      1|        CrosstermKeyCode::Char('f') => {
  389|      1|            // Toggle focus on selected target only
  390|      1|            state.logger_state.transition(TuiWidgetEvent::FocusKey);
  391|      1|        }
  392|      0|        _ => {}
  393|       |    }
  394|     12|}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tui/events/mod.rs:
    1|       |//! TUI event handling module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles keyboard and mouse events and routes them to the appropriate
    4|       |//! handlers based on focus state.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! # Event Routing
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! ## Keyboard Events
    9|       |//!
   10|       |//! Keyboard events are routed based on two levels:
   11|       |//!
   12|       |//! 1. **Global keys** - Always handled regardless of focus:
   13|       |//!    - `Tab`: Cycle focus between panels
   14|       |//!    - `Shift+Tab`: Cycle focus in reverse
   15|       |//!    - `q`: Quit the TUI
   16|       |//!    - `?`: Toggle help overlay
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! 2. **Panel-specific keys** - Routed based on current focus:
   19|       |//!    - Agent Tree: ↑/↓ navigate, ←/→ collapse/expand, Enter select, f toggle auto-follow
   20|       |//!    - Agent Output: PgUp/PgDn scroll, Home/End jump
   21|       |//!    - Task List: ↑/↓ navigate, PgUp/PgDn scroll
   22|       |//!    - App Logs: h toggle target selector, ←/→ filter level, PgUp/PgDn scroll
   23|       |//!
   24|       |//! ## Mouse Events
   25|       |//!
   26|       |//! Mouse clicks on panels switch focus to the clicked panel. This provides an
   27|       |//! alternative to using Tab/Shift+Tab for panel navigation.
   28|       |
   29|       |mod keyboard;
   30|       |mod mouse;
   31|       |
   32|       |// Re-exports
   33|       |pub use keyboard::handle_key_event;
   34|       |pub use mouse::{handle_mouse_click, handle_mouse_scroll, ScrollDirection};
   35|       |
   36|       |// ============================================================================
   37|       |// Event Result
   38|       |// ============================================================================
   39|       |
   40|       |/// Result of handling a key event.
   41|       |///
   42|       |/// This enum indicates whether the TUI should continue running or quit.
   43|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   44|       |pub enum EventResult {
   45|       |    /// Continue running the TUI
   46|       |    Continue,
   47|       |    /// Quit the TUI
   48|       |    Quit,
   49|       |}
   50|       |
   51|       |// ============================================================================
   52|       |// Tests
   53|       |// ============================================================================
   54|       |
   55|       |#[cfg(test)]

   57|       |    use super::*;

   61|       |        KeyCode as CrosstermKeyCode, KeyEvent as CtKeyEvent, KeyEventKind, KeyEventState,

   63|       |        MouseEventKind,
   64|       |    };

   69|     56|        CtKeyEvent {
   70|     56|            code,
   71|     56|            modifiers: CrosstermKeyModifiers::empty(),
   72|     56|            kind: KeyEventKind::Press,
   73|     56|            state: KeyEventState::empty(),
   74|     56|        }
   75|     56|    }

   86|     42|        state

  237|       |        // Tests that App Logs panel key events are handled without panics.
  238|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  239|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  240|      1|        state.current_focus = FocusedPanel::AppLogs;
  241|       |

  243|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::PageDown), &layout);
  244|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::PageUp), &layout);
  245|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Down), &layout);
  246|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Up), &layout);
  247|       |
  248|       |        // Test level filter events
  249|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Left), &layout);
  250|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Right), &layout);
  251|       |
  252|       |        // Test target selector toggle
  253|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char('h')), &layout);
  254|       |
  255|       |        // Test focus toggle
  256|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char(' ')), &layout);
  257|       |
  258|       |        // Test escape
  259|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Esc), &layout);
  260|       |
  261|       |        // Test capture level adjustments
  262|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char('+')), &layout);
  263|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char('-')), &layout);
  264|       |
  265|       |        // Test focus on target
  266|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char('f')), &layout);
  267|       |
  268|       |        // Verify Home still resets app_logs_scroll
  269|      1|        state.app_logs_scroll = 10;
  270|      1|        handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Home), &layout);
  271|      1|        assert_eq!(state.app_logs_scroll, 0);
  272|      1|    }

  295|       |    #[test]
  296|      1|    fn test_global_keys_work_regardless_of_focus() {
  297|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  298|       |
  299|       |        // Tab should work from any panel
  300|      4|        for panel in [
  301|      1|            FocusedPanel::AgentTree,
  302|      1|            FocusedPanel::AgentOutput,
  303|      1|            FocusedPanel::TaskList,
  304|      1|            FocusedPanel::AppLogs,
  305|      1|        ] {
  306|      4|            let mut state = test_state();
  307|      4|            state.current_focus = panel;
  308|       |
  309|      4|            let result = handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Tab), &layout);
  310|      4|            assert_eq!(result, EventResult::Continue);
  311|      4|            assert_ne!(state.current_focus, panel); // Focus should have changed
  312|       |        }
  313|       |
  314|       |        // q should quit from any panel
  315|      4|        for panel in [
  316|      1|            FocusedPanel::AgentTree,
  317|      1|            FocusedPanel::AgentOutput,
  318|      1|            FocusedPanel::TaskList,
  319|      1|            FocusedPanel::AppLogs,
  320|      1|        ] {
  321|      4|            let mut state = test_state();
  322|      4|            state.current_focus = panel;
  323|       |
  324|      4|            let result = handle_key_event(&mut state, key(CrosstermKeyCode::Char('q')), &layout);
  325|      4|            assert_eq!(result, EventResult::Quit);
  326|       |        }
  327|      1|    }

  332|      1|        assert_ne!(EventResult::Continue, EventResult::Quit);
  333|      1|    }

  340|     11|    fn mouse_click(column: u16, row: u16) -> CrosstermMouseEvent {
  341|     11|        CrosstermMouseEvent {
  342|     11|            kind: MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left),
  343|     11|            column,
  344|     11|            row,
  345|     11|            modifiers: CrosstermKeyModifiers::empty(),
  346|     11|        }
  347|     11|    }

  410|       |
  411|      1|        assert!(mouse::rect_contains(rect, 10, 10));
  412|      1|        assert!(mouse::rect_contains(rect, 29, 24));
  413|      1|        assert!(mouse::rect_contains(rect, 20, 17));
  414|       |
  415|      1|        assert!(!mouse::rect_contains(rect, 9, 10));
  416|      1|        assert!(!mouse::rect_contains(rect, 10, 9));
  417|      1|        assert!(!mouse::rect_contains(rect, 30, 10));
  418|      1|        assert!(!mouse::rect_contains(rect, 10, 25));
