  629|       |    // ========================================================================
  630|       |
  631|       |    /// Helper to create a mouse scroll event at (column, row)
  632|      5|    fn mouse_scroll(column: u16, row: u16, direction: ScrollDirection) -> CrosstermMouseEvent {
  633|       |        CrosstermMouseEvent {
  634|      5|            kind: match direction {
  635|      2|                ScrollDirection::Up => MouseEventKind::ScrollUp,
  636|      3|                ScrollDirection::Down => MouseEventKind::ScrollDown,
  637|       |            },
  638|      5|            column,
  639|      5|            row,
  640|      5|            modifiers: CrosstermKeyModifiers::empty(),
  641|       |        }
  642|      5|    }
  643|       |
  644|       |    #[test]
  645|      1|    fn test_mouse_scroll_agent_output() {
  646|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  647|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  648|       |
  649|       |        // Start at scroll position 0
  650|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 0);
  651|       |
  652|       |        // Scroll up should stay at 0 (can't go negative)
  653|      1|        handle_mouse_scroll(
  654|      1|            &mut state,
  655|      1|            mouse_scroll(50, 10, ScrollDirection::Up),
  656|      1|            &layout,
  657|      1|            ScrollDirection::Up,
  658|       |        );
  659|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 0);
  660|      1|    }
  661|       |
  662|       |    #[test]
  663|      1|    fn test_mouse_scroll_task_list_moves_selection() {
  664|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  665|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  666|       |
  667|       |        // Add some tasks
  668|      5|        for i in 1..=5 {
                               ^1
  669|      5|            state.task_list_state.handle_task_created(
  670|      5|                format!("t{i}"),
  671|      5|                format!("Task {i}"),
  672|      5|                "Desc".to_string(),
  673|      5|                vec![],
  674|      5|                0,
  675|      5|            );
  676|      5|        }
  677|       |
  678|       |        // Initially no selection
  679|      1|        assert!(state.task_list_state.selected_index.is_none());
  680|       |
  681|       |        // Scroll down in task list should move selection
  682|      1|        handle_mouse_scroll(
  683|      1|            &mut state,
  684|      1|            mouse_scroll(100, 10, ScrollDirection::Down),
  685|      1|            &layout,
  686|      1|            ScrollDirection::Down,
  687|       |        );
  688|      1|        assert!(state.task_list_state.selected_index.is_some());
  689|      1|    }
  690|       |
  691|       |    #[test]
  692|      1|    fn test_mouse_scroll_ignored_when_help_visible() {
  693|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  694|      1|        state.help_visible = true;
  695|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  696|       |
  697|       |        // Try to scroll agent output
  698|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 5;
  699|      1|        handle_mouse_scroll(
  700|      1|            &mut state,
  701|      1|            mouse_scroll(50, 10, ScrollDirection::Down),
  702|      1|            &layout,
  703|      1|            ScrollDirection::Down,
  704|       |        );
  705|       |        // Scroll should be unchanged because help is visible
  706|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 5);
  707|      1|    }
  708|       |
  709|       |    #[test]
  710|      1|    fn test_mouse_scroll_ignored_when_settings_visible() {
  711|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  712|      1|        state.settings_visible = true;
  713|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  714|       |
  715|       |        // Try to scroll agent output
  716|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 5;
  717|      1|        handle_mouse_scroll(
  718|      1|            &mut state,
  719|      1|            mouse_scroll(50, 10, ScrollDirection::Down),
  720|      1|            &layout,
  721|      1|            ScrollDirection::Down,
  722|       |        );
  723|       |        // Scroll should be unchanged because settings is visible
  724|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 5);
  725|      1|    }
  726|       |
  727|       |    #[test]
  728|      1|    fn test_mouse_scroll_on_status_bar_no_action() {
  729|      1|        let mut state = test_state();
  730|      1|        let layout = test_layout();
  731|       |
  732|       |        // Set a known scroll position for agent output
  733|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 5;
  734|       |
  735|       |        // Scroll on status bar (row 39 in 40-line terminal)
  736|      1|        handle_mouse_scroll(
  737|      1|            &mut state,
  738|      1|            mouse_scroll(60, 39, ScrollDirection::Up),
  739|      1|            &layout,
  740|      1|            ScrollDirection::Up,
  741|       |        );
  742|       |        // Nothing should change
  743|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 5);
  744|      1|    }

  747|       |    // Mouse Click Task Selection Tests
  748|       |    // ========================================================================

  750|       |    #[test]

    1|       |//! Mouse event handling.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles mouse clicks and scroll events and routes them
    4|       |//! to the appropriate panels based on cursor position.
    5|       |
    6|       |use crossterm::event::MouseEvent as CrosstermMouseEvent;
    7|       |use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    8|       |
    9|       |use crate::tui::layout::PanelLayout;
   10|       |use crate::tui::state::{FocusedPanel, TuiState};

   12|       |
   13|       |// ============================================================================
   14|       |// Mouse Click Handling
   15|       |// ============================================================================
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Handles a mouse click event to switch panel focus.
   18|       |///
   19|       |/// Maps the click coordinates to panel areas and updates the current focus
   20|       |/// if the click occurred within a valid panel area.
   21|       |///
   22|       |/// # Arguments
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// * `state` - Mutable reference to the TUI state
   25|       |/// * `mouse_event` - The crossterm mouse event containing click coordinates
   26|       |/// * `layout` - The current panel layout with rectangle areas
   27|       |///
   28|       |/// # Panel Areas
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Clicks are mapped to the following panels:
   31|       |/// - Agent Tree (left side, top section)
   32|       |/// - Agent Output (center, top section)
   33|       |/// - Task List (right side, top section)
   34|       |/// - App Logs (full width, middle section)
   35|       |///
   36|       |/// Clicks on the status bar are ignored.
   37|     11|pub fn handle_mouse_click(
   38|     11|    state: &mut TuiState,
   39|     11|    mouse_event: CrosstermMouseEvent,
   40|     11|    layout: &PanelLayout,
   41|     11|) {
   42|       |    // Don't process mouse clicks if help overlay is visible
   43|     11|    if state.help_visible {
   44|      1|        return;
   45|     10|    }
   46|       |
   47|     10|    let column = mouse_event.column;
   48|     10|    let row = mouse_event.row;
   49|       |
   50|       |    // Check which panel was clicked and update focus accordingly
   51|     10|    if let Some(panel) = panel_at_position(column, row, layout) {
                              ^9
   52|       |        // Check if task detail is showing in center panel
   53|      9|        let task_detail_visible = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::TaskList
   54|      0|            && state.task_list_state.get_selected_task().is_some();
   55|       |
   56|       |        // Handle panel-specific click behavior
   57|      1|        match panel {
   58|      1|            FocusedPanel::AgentTree => {
   59|      1|                handle_agent_tree_click(state, row, layout);
   60|      1|            }
   61|      6|            FocusedPanel::TaskList => {
   62|      6|                handle_task_list_click(state, row, layout);
   63|      6|            }
   64|      0|            FocusedPanel::AgentOutput if task_detail_visible => {
   65|       |                // When clicking center panel while task detail is shown,
   66|       |                // don't change focus - keep task detail visible
   67|      0|                return;
   68|       |            }
   69|      2|            _ => {}
   70|       |        }
   71|      9|        state.on_focus_changed(panel);
   72|      1|    }
   73|     11|}
   74|       |
   75|       |/// Handles a mouse click inside the task list panel.
   76|       |///
   77|       |/// Calculates which task row was clicked based on the mouse row position,
   78|       |/// accounting for border offset and scroll offset. If a valid task row is
   79|       |/// clicked, selects that task.
   80|       |///
   81|       |/// # Arguments
   82|       |///
   83|       |/// * `state` - Mutable reference to the TUI state
   84|       |/// * `row` - The row position of the mouse click
   85|       |/// * `layout` - The current panel layout with rectangle areas
   86|       |#[allow(clippy::missing_const_for_fn)]
   87|      6|fn handle_task_list_click(state: &mut TuiState, row: u16, layout: &PanelLayout) {
   88|       |    // Account for top border (inner area starts 1 row below panel top)
   89|      6|    let inner_y = layout.task_list.y + 1;
   90|       |    // Inner height excludes top and bottom borders
   91|      6|    let inner_height = layout.task_list.height.saturating_sub(2);
   92|       |
   93|       |    // Check if click is inside the inner content area (not on borders)
   94|      6|    if row >= inner_y && row < inner_y + inner_height {
                                       ^5
   95|      5|        // Calculate which visible row was clicked
   96|      5|        let visible_row = row - inner_y;
   97|      5|        // Calculate actual task index accounting for scroll offset
   98|      5|        let clicked_index = visible_row as usize + state.task_list_state.scroll_offset;
   99|      5|        // select_index has built-in bounds checking
  100|      5|        state.task_list_state.select_index(clicked_index);
  101|      5|    }
                  ^1
  102|      6|}
  103|       |
  104|       |/// Determines which panel (if any) is at the given screen position.
  105|       |///
  106|       |/// Returns `None` if the position is in the status bar or outside all panels.
  107|     18|pub(super) const fn panel_at_position(
  108|     18|    column: u16,
  109|     18|    row: u16,
  110|     18|    layout: &PanelLayout,
  111|     18|) -> Option<FocusedPanel> {
  112|       |    // Check each panel area - order doesn't matter as they don't overlap
  113|     18|    if rect_contains(layout.agent_tree, column, row) {
  114|      2|        Some(FocusedPanel::AgentTree)
  115|     16|    } else if rect_contains(layout.agent_output, column, row) {
  116|      3|        Some(FocusedPanel::AgentOutput)
  117|     13|    } else if rect_contains(layout.task_list, column, row) {
  118|      8|        Some(FocusedPanel::TaskList)
  119|      5|    } else if rect_contains(layout.app_logs, column, row) {
  120|      2|        Some(FocusedPanel::AppLogs)
  121|       |    } else {
  122|       |        // Click was on status bar or outside valid panel areas
  123|      3|        None
  124|       |    }

  133|       |/// Handles a mouse click within the agent tree panel to select an agent.
  134|       |///
  135|       |/// Maps the click row position to a visible tree item and selects it.
  136|       |/// This accounts for:
  137|       |/// - Border offset (inner area starts at `y + 1`)
  138|       |/// - Scroll offset from the tree state
  139|       |/// - Collapsed/expanded node visibility
  140|       |///
  141|       |/// # Arguments
  142|       |///
  143|       |/// * `state` - Mutable reference to the TUI state
  144|       |/// * `row` - The screen row where the click occurred
  145|       |/// * `layout` - The current panel layout with rectangle areas
  146|      1|fn handle_agent_tree_click(state: &mut TuiState, row: u16, layout: &PanelLayout) {
  147|       |    // Calculate inner area (excluding borders)
  148|      1|    let inner_y = layout.agent_tree.y + 1;
  149|      1|    let inner_height = layout.agent_tree.height.saturating_sub(2);
  150|       |
  151|       |    // Check if click is within the inner (non-border) area
  152|      1|    if row >= inner_y && row < inner_y + inner_height {
  153|      1|        let visible_row = (row - inner_y) as usize;
  154|       |
  155|       |        // Get visible items (respects collapsed/expanded state)
  156|      1|        let visible_items = state.agent_tree_state.visible_items();
  157|       |
  158|       |        // Empty tree - nothing to select
  159|      1|        if visible_items.is_empty() {
  160|      1|            return;
  161|      0|        }
  162|       |
  163|       |        // Account for scroll offset in tree_state
  164|      0|        let scroll_offset = state.agent_tree_state.tree_state.get_offset();
  165|      0|        let clicked_index = visible_row + scroll_offset;
  166|       |
  167|       |        // Select the agent if clicked index is valid
  168|      0|        if let Some(session_id) = visible_items.get(clicked_index) {
  169|      0|            state.agent_tree_state.select(session_id);
  170|      0|            state.selected_agent_id = Some(session_id.clone());
  171|      0|            // Disable auto-follow on manual selection
  172|      0|            state.auto_follow_enabled = false;
  173|      0|        }
  174|      0|    }

  177|       |// ============================================================================
  178|       |// Mouse Scroll Handling

  180|       |
  181|       |/// Number of lines to scroll per mouse wheel notch.
  182|       |const MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES: u16 = 3;
  183|       |
  184|       |/// Scroll direction from mouse wheel.
  185|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
  186|       |pub enum ScrollDirection {
  187|       |    /// Scroll up (wheel scrolled up)
  188|       |    Up,
  189|       |    /// Scroll down (wheel scrolled down)
  190|       |    Down,
  191|       |}
  192|       |
  193|       |/// Handles a mouse scroll event to scroll the panel under the cursor.
  194|       |///
  195|       |/// Maps the scroll coordinates to panel areas and scrolls the appropriate
  196|       |/// panel content. Scrolling behavior depends on the panel type:
  197|       |///
  198|       |/// - Agent Tree: Moves tree selection up/down
  199|       |/// - Agent Output: Scrolls output content up/down
  200|       |/// - Task List: Moves task selection up/down (or scrolls task detail if shown)
  201|       |/// - App Logs: Scrolls log content up/down
  202|      5|pub fn handle_mouse_scroll(
  203|      5|    state: &mut TuiState,
  204|      5|    mouse_event: CrosstermMouseEvent,
  205|      5|    layout: &PanelLayout,
  206|      5|    direction: ScrollDirection,
  207|      5|) {
  208|       |    // Don't process mouse scrolls if help or settings overlay is visible
  209|      5|    if state.help_visible || state.settings_visible {
                                           ^4
  210|      2|        return;
  211|      3|    }
  212|       |
  213|      3|    let column = mouse_event.column;
  214|      3|    let row = mouse_event.row;
  215|       |
  216|       |    // Determine which panel the mouse is over and scroll accordingly
  217|      3|    if let Some(panel) = panel_at_position(column, row, layout) {
                              ^2
  218|       |        // Check if task detail is showing in center panel
  219|      2|        let task_detail_visible = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::TaskList
  220|      0|            && state.task_list_state.get_selected_task().is_some();
  221|       |
  222|      2|        match panel {
  223|      0|            FocusedPanel::AgentTree => {
  224|      0|                scroll_agent_tree(state, direction);
  225|      0|            }
  226|       |            FocusedPanel::AgentOutput => {
  227|       |                // If task detail is showing in center panel, scroll that instead
  228|      1|                if task_detail_visible {
  229|      0|                    scroll_task_detail(state, direction, layout.agent_output.height);
  230|      1|                } else {
  231|      1|                    scroll_agent_output(state, direction, &layout.agent_output);
  232|      1|                }
  233|       |            }
  234|       |            FocusedPanel::TaskList => {
  235|       |                // If task detail is showing (task selected), scroll the detail
  236|       |                // Otherwise, scroll the task list selection
  237|      1|                if state.task_list_state.get_selected_task().is_some() {
  238|      0|                    scroll_task_detail(state, direction, layout.agent_output.height);
  239|      1|                } else {
  240|      1|                    scroll_task_list(state, direction);
  241|      1|                }
  242|       |            }
  243|      0|            FocusedPanel::AppLogs => {
  244|      0|                scroll_app_logs(state, direction);
  245|      0|            }
  246|       |        }
  247|      1|    }
  248|      5|}

  250|       |/// Scrolls the agent tree selection up or down.
  251|      0|fn scroll_agent_tree(state: &mut TuiState, direction: ScrollDirection) {
  252|      0|    for _ in 0..MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES {
  253|      0|        match direction {
  254|      0|            ScrollDirection::Up => {
  255|      0|                state.agent_tree_state.tree_state.key_up();
  256|      0|            }
  257|      0|            ScrollDirection::Down => {
  258|      0|                state.agent_tree_state.tree_state.key_down();
  259|      0|            }
  260|       |        }
  261|       |    }
  262|       |    // Update selected agent to match tree selection
  263|      0|    state.selected_agent_id = state
  264|      0|        .agent_tree_state
  265|      0|        .selected_session_id()
  266|      0|        .map(String::from);
  267|      0|}
  268|       |
  269|       |/// Scrolls the agent output panel up or down.
  270|      1|fn scroll_agent_output(state: &mut TuiState, direction: ScrollDirection, area: &Rect) {
  271|       |    // Calculate total lines from selected agent messages, accounting for text wrapping.
  272|       |    // Subtract 2 from width to account for left/right borders.
  273|      1|    let inner_width = area.width.saturating_sub(2);
  274|      1|    let total_lines = state.selected_agent_messages().map_or(0, |messages| {
                                                                                         ^0
  275|      0|        calculate_wrapped_line_count(messages, inner_width)
  276|      0|    });
  277|       |
  278|      1|    let page_size = area.height.saturating_sub(2) as usize; // Account for borders
  279|       |
  280|      1|    match direction {
  281|      1|        ScrollDirection::Up => {
  282|      1|            state.agent_output_scroll =
  283|      1|                state.agent_output_scroll.saturating_sub(MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES);
  284|      1|        }
  285|      0|        ScrollDirection::Down => {
  286|      0|            // Truncation safe: scroll positions are UI coordinates, well under u16::MAX
  287|      0|            #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
  288|      0|            let max_scroll = total_lines.saturating_sub(page_size) as u16;
  289|      0|            state.agent_output_scroll =
  290|      0|                (state.agent_output_scroll + MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES).min(max_scroll);
  291|      0|        }
  292|       |    }
  293|      1|}

  300|      0|                state.task_list_state.select_previous();
  301|      0|            }
  302|      3|            ScrollDirection::Down => {
  303|      3|                state.task_list_state.select_next();
  304|      3|            }
  305|       |        }
  306|       |    }

  309|       |/// Scrolls the task detail panel up or down.
  310|      0|fn scroll_task_detail(state: &mut TuiState, direction: ScrollDirection, visible_height: u16) {
  311|       |    // Get total lines from task detail (approximation - use a conservative estimate)
  312|       |    // The actual line count depends on description length, but for scrolling this is sufficient
  313|      0|    let total_lines = state.task_list_state.get_selected_task().map_or(0, |task| {
  314|       |        // Basic line count: ID + Name + Status + Depth + empty + Description header +
  315|       |        // description lines + empty + Dependencies header + dependency lines
  316|      0|        6 + task.description.lines().count() + 2 + task.dependencies.len().max(1)
  317|      0|    });
  318|       |
  319|      0|    let page_size = visible_height.saturating_sub(2) as usize; // Account for borders
  320|       |
  321|      0|    match direction {
  322|      0|        ScrollDirection::Up => {
  323|      0|            state.task_detail_scroll = state.task_detail_scroll.saturating_sub(MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES);
  324|      0|        }
  325|      0|        ScrollDirection::Down => {
  326|      0|            // Truncation safe: scroll positions are UI coordinates, well under u16::MAX
  327|      0|            #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]

  329|      0|            state.task_detail_scroll =
  330|      0|                (state.task_detail_scroll + MOUSE_SCROLL_LINES).min(max_scroll);
  331|      0|        }
  332|       |    }
  333|      0|}

    1|       |//! TUI layout management module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles the calculation and management of UI component layouts.
    4|       |//!
    5|       |//! # Layout Structure
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! ```text
    8|       |//! ┌─────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬───────────┐
    9|       |//! │  Agent Tree         │  Selected Agent Output           │   Tasks   │
   10|       |//! │  (navigation)       │  (streaming content)             │   List    │
   11|       |//! │  ~20% width         │  ~50% width                      │  ~30% width│
   12|       |//! ├─────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴───────────┤
   13|       |//! │  App Logs (filterable by target/level)                            │
   14|       |//! │  ~30% height                                                       │
   15|       |//! ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   16|       |//! │  Status Bar (1 line)                                               │
   17|       |//! └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   18|       |//! ```
   19|       |
   20|       |use ratatui::layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect};
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Minimum sizes for panels to remain usable.
   23|       |mod min_sizes {
   24|       |    /// Height of the status bar (always 1 line)
   25|       |    pub const STATUS_BAR_HEIGHT: u16 = 1;
   26|       |    /// Minimum total terminal width for reasonable layout
   27|       |    pub const MIN_TERMINAL_WIDTH: u16 = 80;
   28|       |    /// Minimum total terminal height for reasonable layout
   29|       |    pub const MIN_TERMINAL_HEIGHT: u16 = 15;
   30|       |}
   31|       |
   32|       |/// Layout containing rectangles for all UI panels.
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// This struct is returned by [`calculate_layout`] and provides the
   35|       |/// position and size of each panel in the TUI.
   36|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
   37|       |pub struct PanelLayout {
   38|       |    /// Agent tree panel (left side, top section)
   39|       |    /// Shows hierarchical agent navigation
   40|       |    pub agent_tree: Rect,
   41|       |    /// Agent output panel (center, top section)
   42|       |    /// Shows streaming output from selected agent
   43|       |    pub agent_output: Rect,
   44|       |    /// Task list panel (right side, top section)
   45|       |    /// Shows task status overview
   46|       |    pub task_list: Rect,
   47|       |    /// App logs panel (full width, middle section)
   48|       |    /// Shows filterable application logs
   49|       |    pub app_logs: Rect,
   50|       |    /// Status bar (full width, bottom, 1 line)
   51|       |    /// Shows summary info and help hints
   52|       |    pub status_bar: Rect,
   53|       |}
   54|       |
   55|       |impl PanelLayout {
   56|       |    /// Returns true if the layout represents a "too small" terminal.
   57|       |    ///
   58|       |    /// When the terminal is too small, the application should display
   59|       |    /// a message asking the user to resize rather than rendering the
   60|       |    /// full UI.
   61|       |    #[must_use]
   62|      4|    pub const fn is_too_small(&self) -> bool {
   63|      4|        self.agent_output.width == 0 || self.agent_output.height == 0 || self.status_bar.width == 0
                                                      ^3                               ^3
   64|      4|    }

   66|       |
   67|       |/// Calculates the panel layout for the given terminal area.
   68|       |///
   69|       |/// This is the main layout function that divides the terminal into
   70|       |/// the five panels according to the layout specification:
   71|       |///
   72|       |/// - Top section (~70% height): Agent Tree (20%), Agent Output (50%), Task List (30%)
   73|       |/// - Middle section (~30% height): App Logs
   74|       |/// - Bottom section (1 line): Status Bar
   75|       |///
   76|       |/// # Arguments
   77|       |///
   78|       |/// * `area` - The total available terminal area
   79|       |///
   80|       |/// # Returns
   81|       |///
   82|       |/// A [`PanelLayout`] with rectangles for each panel. If the terminal
   83|       |/// is too small, some panels may have zero dimensions.
   84|       |///
   85|       |/// # Example
   86|       |///
   87|       |/// ```ignore
   88|       |/// use ratatui::layout::Rect;
   89|       |/// use paperboat::tui::layout::calculate_layout;
   90|       |///
   91|       |/// let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 120, 40);
   92|       |/// let layout = calculate_layout(area);
   93|       |///
   94|       |/// // Use layout.agent_output to render agent output panel
   95|       |/// // Use layout.task_list to render task list panel
   96|       |/// // etc.
   97|       |/// ```
   98|     43|pub fn calculate_layout(area: Rect) -> PanelLayout {
   99|       |    // Handle degenerate cases
  100|     43|    if area.width < min_sizes::MIN_TERMINAL_WIDTH || area.height < min_sizes::MIN_TERMINAL_HEIGHT {
                                                                   ^41
  101|      2|        return create_minimal_layout(area);
  102|     41|    }
  103|       |
  104|       |    // First, split vertically into: top content, logs, status bar
  105|       |    // Status bar is always 1 line
  106|       |    // Top section gets 70% of remaining height, logs get 30%
  107|       |    // Using Fill constraints ensures stable layout that doesn't shift based on content
  108|     41|    let vertical_chunks = Layout::default()
  109|     41|        .direction(Direction::Vertical)
  110|     41|        .constraints([
  111|     41|            Constraint::Fill(70), // Top section (70% of remaining)
  112|     41|            Constraint::Fill(30), // App logs (30% of remaining)
  113|     41|            Constraint::Length(min_sizes::STATUS_BAR_HEIGHT), // Status bar (1 line)
  114|     41|        ])
  115|     41|        .split(area);
  116|       |
  117|     41|    let top_section = vertical_chunks[0];
  118|     41|    let app_logs = vertical_chunks[1];
  119|     41|    let status_bar = vertical_chunks[2];
  120|       |
  121|       |    // Split the top section horizontally: agent tree (20%), output (50%), tasks (30%)
  122|     41|    let horizontal_chunks = Layout::default()
  123|     41|        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
  124|     41|        .constraints([
  125|     41|            Constraint::Percentage(20), // Agent tree
  126|     41|            Constraint::Percentage(50), // Agent output
  127|     41|            Constraint::Percentage(30), // Task list
  128|     41|        ])
  129|     41|        .split(top_section);
  130|       |
  131|     41|    let agent_tree = horizontal_chunks[0];
  132|     41|    let agent_output = horizontal_chunks[1];
  133|     41|    let task_list = horizontal_chunks[2];
  134|       |
  135|     41|    PanelLayout {
  136|     41|        agent_tree,
  137|     41|        agent_output,
  138|     41|        task_list,
  139|     41|        app_logs,
  140|     41|        status_bar,
  141|     41|    }
  142|     43|}

  144|       |/// Creates a minimal layout for very small terminals.
  145|       |///
  146|       |/// When the terminal is too small for the full layout, this function
  147|       |/// creates a simplified layout that shows at least the status bar
  148|       |/// and as much content as possible.
  149|      2|fn create_minimal_layout(area: Rect) -> PanelLayout {
  150|      2|    if area.height < 2 || area.width < 10 {
  151|       |        // Extremely small - just show status bar if possible
  152|       |        return PanelLayout {
  153|      1|            agent_tree: Rect::default(),
  154|      1|            agent_output: Rect::default(),
  155|      1|            task_list: Rect::default(),
  156|      1|            app_logs: Rect::default(),
  157|      1|            status_bar: if area.height >= 1 {
  158|      1|                Rect::new(area.x, area.y, area.width, 1)
  159|       |            } else {
  160|      0|                Rect::default()
  161|       |            },
  162|       |        };
  163|      1|    }
  164|       |
  165|       |    // For small terminals, simplify to just output + status bar
  166|      1|    let content_height = area.height.saturating_sub(1);
  167|       |
  168|      1|    PanelLayout {
  169|      1|        agent_tree: Rect::default(),
  170|      1|        agent_output: Rect::new(area.x, area.y, area.width, content_height),
  171|      1|        task_list: Rect::default(),
  172|      1|        app_logs: Rect::default(),
  173|      1|        status_bar: Rect::new(area.x, area.y + content_height, area.width, 1),
  174|      1|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tui/mod.rs:
    1|       |//! Terminal User Interface (TUI) module for Villalobos.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides an interactive terminal-based user interface for monitoring
    4|       |//! and interacting with the orchestrator. It displays real-time agent output, task
    5|       |//! progress, and system status.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # Feature Flag
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This module is only compiled when the `tui` feature is enabled:
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! ```toml
   12|       |//! [dependencies]
   13|       |//! paperboat = { version = "0.1", features = ["tui"] }
   14|       |//! ```
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! # Architecture
   17|       |//!
   18|       |//! The TUI is built using ratatui and crossterm, with a reactive architecture:
   19|       |//!
   20|       |//! ## Entry Points
   21|       |//! - [`app::run_tui_with_channels`] - Main entry point that runs the TUI event loop
   22|       |//! - [`spawn_event_bridge`] - Bridges async broadcast channel to sync mpsc for TUI thread
   23|       |//!
   24|       |//! ## State Management
   25|       |//! - [`state`] - Core TUI state including focus management and event routing
   26|       |//! - [`agent_tree_state`] - Agent hierarchy tree state from `AgentStarted` events
   27|       |//! - [`task_list_state`] - Task list state from `TaskCreated` events
   28|       |//!
   29|       |//! ## UI Components
   30|       |//! - [`layout`] - Panel layout calculations and screen partitioning
   31|       |//! - [`events`] - Event handling and input processing
   32|       |//! - [`widgets`] - Custom widgets for displaying agent output, tasks, etc.
   33|       |//!
   34|       |//! ## Application Loop
   35|       |//! - [`app`] - TUI application runner and render loop
   36|       |//!
   37|       |//! ## Model Configuration
   38|       |//! - [`ModelConfigUpdate`] - Message type for TUI -> App model configuration updates
   39|       |
   40|       |mod agent_node;
   41|       |mod agent_tree_state;
   42|       |mod app;
   43|       |mod events;
   44|       |mod layout;
   45|       |mod model_config_update;
   46|       |mod state;
   47|       |mod task_list_state;
   48|       |pub mod widgets;
   49|       |
   50|       |pub use app::run_tui_with_channels;
   51|       |
   52|       |// Re-exports for backward compatibility and convenient access
   53|       |// These are part of the public API but may not be used internally
   54|       |#[allow(unused_imports)]
   55|       |pub use agent_tree_state::{AgentNode, AgentStatus, AgentTreeState};
   56|       |#[allow(unused_imports)]
   57|       |pub use model_config_update::ModelConfigUpdate;
   58|       |#[allow(unused_imports)]
   59|       |pub use state::{FocusedPanel, TuiState};
   60|       |#[allow(unused_imports)]
   61|       |pub use task_list_state::{TaskDisplay, TaskListState};
   62|       |
   63|       |use crate::backend::BackendKind;
   64|       |use crate::models::ModelConfig;
   65|       |
   66|       |/// Channels for App -> TUI communication (model config)
   67|       |pub struct TuiConfigChannels {
   68|       |    /// Sender for initial model config (App sends once after discovery)
   69|       |    pub initial_config_tx: std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<ModelConfig>,
   70|       |    /// Receiver for model configuration updates from TUI (App reads)
   71|       |    pub config_update_rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<ModelConfigUpdate>,
   72|       |    /// Sender for available backends list (App sends if backend selection needed)
   73|       |    pub available_backends_tx: std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<Vec<BackendKind>>,
   74|       |    /// Receiver for selected backend from TUI (App reads)
   75|       |    pub selected_backend_rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<BackendKind>,
   76|       |}
   77|       |
   78|       |/// Channels for TUI thread (passed to `run_tui_with_channels`)
   79|       |pub struct TuiThreadChannels {
   80|       |    /// Receiver for log events
   81|       |    pub event_rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::logging::LogEvent>,
   82|       |    /// Receiver for initial model config (TUI reads once)
   83|       |    pub initial_config_rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<ModelConfig>,
   84|       |    /// Sender for model configuration updates (TUI sends to App)
   85|       |    pub config_update_tx: std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<ModelConfigUpdate>,
   86|       |    /// Receiver for available backends (TUI reads, shows popup if multiple)
   87|       |    pub available_backends_rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Vec<BackendKind>>,
   88|       |    /// Sender for selected backend (TUI sends after user selection)
   89|       |    pub selected_backend_tx: std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<BackendKind>,
   90|       |}
   91|       |
   92|       |/// Spawns a bridge task that forwards `LogEvent`s from a tokio broadcast channel
   93|       |/// to a `std::sync::mpsc` channel for consumption by the TUI thread.
   94|       |///
   95|       |/// This function handles the impedance mismatch between the async broadcast channel
   96|       |/// (used by the main application) and the sync mpsc channel (used by the TUI thread).
   97|       |///
   98|       |/// # Arguments
   99|       |///
  100|       |/// * `broadcast_rx` - A receiver from the `RunLogManager`'s broadcast channel
  101|       |///
  102|       |/// # Returns
  103|       |///
  104|       |/// Returns an `mpsc::Receiver<LogEvent>` that the TUI thread can use to receive events.
  105|       |///
  106|       |/// # Threading Model
  107|       |///
  108|       |/// - The bridge task runs as a tokio task
  109|       |/// - It receives events from the async broadcast channel
  110|       |/// - It forwards events to the sync mpsc channel
  111|       |/// - The mpsc channel has a bounded capacity of 1000 to prevent unbounded memory growth
  112|       |///
  113|       |/// # Error Handling
  114|       |///
  115|       |/// - If the broadcast channel lags (events are dropped), a warning is logged and the bridge continues
  116|       |/// - If the TUI thread exits (mpsc send fails), the bridge task terminates gracefully
  117|       |/// - If the broadcast channel is closed, the bridge task terminates gracefully
  118|       |///
  119|       |/// # Example
  120|       |///
  121|       |/// ```ignore
  122|       |/// let run_log_manager = RunLogManager::new(".paperboat/logs")?;
  123|       |/// let broadcast_rx = run_log_manager.subscribe();
  124|       |/// let event_rx = spawn_event_bridge(broadcast_rx);
  125|       |///
  126|       |/// // Spawn TUI thread
  127|       |/// std::thread::spawn(move || {
  128|       |///     run_tui(event_rx)
  129|       |/// });
  130|       |/// ```
  131|       |#[allow(dead_code)] // Public API for alternative event routing patterns
  132|      0|pub fn spawn_event_bridge(
  133|      0|    mut broadcast_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<crate::logging::LogEvent>,
  134|      0|) -> std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::logging::LogEvent> {
  135|       |    // Create a bounded sync mpsc channel for communicating with the TUI thread.
  136|       |    // Using sync_channel with a buffer to prevent blocking the bridge task while
  137|       |    // allowing backpressure if the TUI can't keep up.
  138|      0|    let (tui_tx, tui_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<crate::logging::LogEvent>(1000);
  139|       |
  140|       |    // Spawn the bridge task that runs for the lifetime of the application
  141|      0|    tokio::spawn(async move {
  142|       |        loop {
  143|      0|            match broadcast_rx.recv().await {
  144|      0|                Ok(event) => {
  145|       |                    // Forward the event to the TUI thread via mpsc
  146|       |                    // If send fails, the TUI thread has exited, so we should stop
  147|      0|                    if tui_tx.send(event).is_err() {
  148|      0|                        tracing::debug!("TUI thread has exited, stopping event bridge");
  149|      0|                        break;
  150|      0|                    }
  151|       |                }
  152|      0|                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(count)) => {
  153|       |                    // Some events were dropped due to the TUI not keeping up
  154|      0|                    tracing::warn!(
  155|       |                        "Event bridge lagged, {} events were dropped. TUI may be slow.",
  156|       |                        count
  157|       |                    );
  158|       |                    // Continue processing - don't break on lag
  159|       |                }
  160|       |                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => {
  161|       |                    // The broadcast channel was closed (all senders dropped)
  162|      0|                    tracing::debug!("Broadcast channel closed, stopping event bridge");
  163|      0|                    break;
  164|       |                }
  165|       |            }
  166|       |        }
  167|      0|    });
  168|       |
  169|      0|    tui_rx
  170|      0|}
  171|       |
  172|       |/// Spawns a bidirectional bridge for TUI communication with model configuration support.
  173|       |///
  174|       |/// This creates all the channels needed for bidirectional communication between
  175|       |/// the TUI thread and the main application:
  176|       |///
  177|       |/// - **App -> TUI**: Log events (broadcast -> sync mpsc via bridge task)
  178|       |/// - **App -> TUI**: Initial model config (sync mpsc, sent once after discovery)
  179|       |/// - **TUI -> App**: Model config updates (sync mpsc -> async mpsc via bridge task)
  180|       |///
  181|       |/// # Arguments
  182|       |///
  183|       |/// * `broadcast_rx` - A receiver from the `RunLogManager`'s broadcast channel
  184|       |///
  185|       |/// # Returns
  186|       |///
  187|       |/// Returns a tuple of:
  188|       |/// - [`TuiConfigChannels`]: Channels for the App to send config and receive updates
  189|       |/// - [`TuiThreadChannels`]: Channels to pass to the TUI thread
  190|       |///
  191|       |/// # Example
  192|       |///
  193|       |/// ```ignore
  194|       |/// let run_log_manager = RunLogManager::new(".paperboat/logs")?;
  195|       |/// let broadcast_rx = run_log_manager.subscribe();
  196|       |/// let (app_channels, tui_channels) = spawn_event_bridge_with_config(broadcast_rx);
  197|       |///
  198|       |/// // Spawn TUI thread
  199|       |/// std::thread::spawn(move || {
  200|       |///     run_tui_with_channels(tui_channels)
  201|       |/// });
  202|       |///
  203|       |/// // After model discovery, send initial config to TUI
  204|       |/// app_channels.initial_config_tx.send(model_config.clone()).ok();
  205|       |///
  206|       |/// // App can listen for config updates
  207|       |/// while let Some(update) = app_channels.config_update_rx.recv().await {
  208|       |///     app.apply_model_config_update(update);
  209|       |/// }
  210|       |/// ```
  211|      0|pub fn spawn_event_bridge_with_config(
  212|      0|    mut broadcast_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<crate::logging::LogEvent>,
  213|      0|) -> (TuiConfigChannels, TuiThreadChannels) {
  214|       |    // Create bounded sync channel for log events (TUI reads)
  215|      0|    let (event_tx, event_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<crate::logging::LogEvent>(1000);
  216|       |
  217|       |    // Create sync channel for initial model config (App sends once, TUI reads once)
  218|      0|    let (initial_config_tx, initial_config_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<ModelConfig>(1);
  219|       |
  220|       |    // Create sync channel for config updates (TUI writes, bridged to async)
  221|      0|    let (config_sync_tx, config_sync_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<ModelConfigUpdate>(100);
  222|       |
  223|       |    // Create async channel for config updates (App reads)
  224|      0|    let (config_async_tx, config_async_rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<ModelConfigUpdate>(100);
  225|       |
  226|       |    // Create channels for backend selection
  227|      0|    let (available_backends_tx, available_backends_rx) =
  228|      0|        std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<Vec<BackendKind>>(1);
  229|      0|    let (selected_backend_sync_tx, selected_backend_sync_rx) =
  230|      0|        std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<BackendKind>(1);
  231|      0|    let (selected_backend_async_tx, selected_backend_async_rx) =
  232|      0|        tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<BackendKind>(1);
  233|       |
  234|       |    // Spawn bridge task for log events (async broadcast -> sync mpsc)
  235|      0|    tokio::spawn(async move {
  236|       |        loop {
  237|      0|            match broadcast_rx.recv().await {
  238|      0|                Ok(event) => {
  239|      0|                    if event_tx.send(event).is_err() {
  240|      0|                        tracing::debug!("TUI thread has exited, stopping event bridge");
  241|      0|                        break;
  242|      0|                    }
  243|       |                }
  244|      0|                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(count)) => {
  245|      0|                    tracing::warn!(
  246|       |                        "Event bridge lagged, {} events were dropped. TUI may be slow.",
  247|       |                        count
  248|       |                    );
  249|       |                }
  250|       |                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => {
  251|      0|                    tracing::debug!("Broadcast channel closed, stopping event bridge");
  252|      0|                    break;
  253|       |                }
  254|       |            }
  255|       |        }
  256|      0|    });
  257|       |
  258|       |    // Spawn bridge task for config updates (sync mpsc -> async mpsc)
  259|       |    // This bridges the TUI's sync sends to the App's async receives
  260|      0|    tokio::spawn(async move {
  261|       |        loop {
  262|       |            // Use blocking task to receive from sync channel
  263|      0|            let update = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({
  264|      0|                let rx = config_sync_rx.try_recv();
  265|       |                move || rx
  266|       |            })
  267|      0|            .await;
  268|       |
  269|      0|            match update {
  270|      0|                Ok(Ok(config_update)) => {
  271|      0|                    if config_async_tx.send(config_update).await.is_err() {
  272|      0|                        tracing::debug!("Config update receiver dropped, stopping bridge");
  273|      0|                        break;
  274|      0|                    }
  275|       |                }
  276|       |                Ok(Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Empty)) => {
  277|       |                    // No message available, yield and try again
  278|      0|                    tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(50)).await;
  279|       |                }
  280|       |                Ok(Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected)) => {
  281|      0|                    tracing::debug!("TUI config sender dropped, stopping bridge");
  282|      0|                    break;
  283|       |                }
  284|       |                Err(_) => {
  285|       |                    // spawn_blocking failed, unlikely but handle gracefully
  286|      0|                    tracing::warn!("Config bridge spawn_blocking failed");
  287|      0|                    break;
  288|       |                }
  289|       |            }
  290|       |        }
  291|      0|    });
  292|       |
  293|       |    // Spawn bridge task for backend selection (sync mpsc -> async mpsc)
  294|      0|    tokio::spawn(async move {
  295|       |        // Only need to bridge one message
  296|      0|        let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || selected_backend_sync_rx.recv()).await;
  297|      0|        if let Ok(Ok(backend)) = result {
  298|      0|            let _ = selected_backend_async_tx.send(backend).await;
  299|      0|        }
  300|      0|    });
  301|       |
  302|      0|    let app_channels = TuiConfigChannels {
  303|      0|        initial_config_tx,
  304|      0|        config_update_rx: config_async_rx,
  305|      0|        available_backends_tx,
  306|      0|        selected_backend_rx: selected_backend_async_rx,
  307|      0|    };
  308|       |
  309|      0|    let tui_channels = TuiThreadChannels {
  310|      0|        event_rx,
  311|      0|        initial_config_rx,
  312|      0|        config_update_tx: config_sync_tx,
  313|      0|        available_backends_rx,
  314|      0|        selected_backend_tx: selected_backend_sync_tx,
  315|      0|    };
  316|       |
  317|      0|    (app_channels, tui_channels)
  318|      0|}

   12|       |/// Message type for sending model configuration updates from the TUI to the App.
   13|       |///
   14|       |/// This enables the TUI to modify the model configuration at runtime,
   15|       |/// with changes taking effect for newly spawned agents.

   18|       |pub struct ModelConfigUpdate {
   19|       |    /// New orchestrator model tier (if changed)
   20|       |    pub orchestrator_model: Option<ModelTier>,
   21|       |    /// New planner model tier (if changed)
   22|       |    pub planner_model: Option<ModelTier>,
   23|       |    /// New implementer model tier (if changed)
   24|       |    pub implementer_model: Option<ModelTier>,
   25|       |}
   26|       |

    1|       |//! TUI state management module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module contains the [`TuiState`] struct which holds all UI state,
    4|       |//! including agent tree navigation, agent output buffers, task lists,
    5|       |//! and panel focus management.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! # State Components
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! - [`TuiState`] - Main state container coordinating all UI state
   10|       |//! - [`AgentTreeState`] - Manages hierarchical agent navigation and metadata (see [`super::agent_tree_state`])
   11|       |//! - [`TaskListState`] - Manages task list from [`LogEvent`]s (see [`super::task_list_state`])
   12|       |//! - [`FocusedPanel`] - Tracks which panel has keyboard focus
   13|       |//! - [`ModelConfigUpdate`] - Message type for sending model configuration changes to the App (see [`super::model_config_update`])
   14|       |

   33|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   34|       |pub enum FocusedPanel {
   35|       |    /// Agent tree navigation panel (left)
   36|       |    #[default]
   37|       |    AgentTree,
   38|       |    /// Selected agent's output panel (center)
   39|       |    AgentOutput,
   40|       |    /// Task list panel (right)
   41|       |    TaskList,
   42|       |    /// Application logs panel (bottom)
   43|       |    AppLogs,
   44|       |}
   45|       |
   46|       |impl FocusedPanel {
   47|       |    /// Returns the next panel in the focus cycle (Tab key behavior).
   48|       |    #[must_use]
   49|     20|    pub const fn next(self) -> Self {
   50|     20|        match self {
   51|      7|            Self::AgentTree => Self::AgentOutput,
   52|      6|            Self::AgentOutput => Self::TaskList,
   53|      4|            Self::TaskList => Self::AppLogs,
   54|      3|            Self::AppLogs => Self::AgentTree,
   55|       |        }
   56|     20|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Returns the previous panel in the focus cycle (Shift+Tab behavior).
   59|       |    #[must_use]

   61|     12|        match self {
   62|      5|            Self::AgentTree => Self::AppLogs,
   63|      1|            Self::AgentOutput => Self::AgentTree,
   64|      2|            Self::TaskList => Self::AgentOutput,
   65|      4|            Self::AppLogs => Self::TaskList,
   66|       |        }
   67|     12|    }
   68|       |}

  170|       |    /// Creates a new TUI state with default values.

  187|    118|            last_message_count: 0,

  243|       |    /// Applies pending settings changes and prepares an update for the App.
  244|       |    ///
  245|       |    /// This method:
  246|       |    /// 1. Creates a `ModelConfigUpdate` from any pending changes in `SettingsState`
  247|       |    /// 2. Applies changes to the local `model_config` for immediate UI feedback
  248|       |    /// 3. Stores the update in `pending_config_update` for the event loop to send
  249|       |    /// 4. Clears the settings state pending changes
  250|       |    /// 5. Sets a status message indicating the changes were applied
  251|       |    ///
  252|       |    /// Returns `true` if any changes were applied, `false` if there were no pending changes.
  253|      4|    pub fn apply_settings_changes(&mut self) -> bool {
  254|      4|        if !self.settings_state.has_pending_changes() {
  255|      2|            return false;
  256|      2|        }
  257|       |
  258|       |        // Build the update from pending changes
  259|      2|        let update = ModelConfigUpdate {
  260|      2|            orchestrator_model: self.settings_state.pending_orchestrator,
  261|      2|            planner_model: self.settings_state.pending_planner,
  262|      2|            implementer_model: self.settings_state.pending_implementer,
  263|      2|        };
  264|       |
  265|       |        // Apply changes to local config for immediate UI feedback
  266|       |        // Convert single tier selections to single-element fallback chains
  267|       |        use crate::models::ModelFallbackChain;
  268|      2|        if let Some(tier) = update.orchestrator_model {
  269|      2|            self.model_config.orchestrator_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  270|      2|        }
                      ^0
  271|      2|        if let Some(tier) = update.planner_model {
                                  ^1
  272|      1|            self.model_config.planner_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  273|      1|        }
  274|      2|        if let Some(tier) = update.implementer_model {
                                  ^0
  275|      0|            self.model_config.implementer_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(tier);
  276|      2|        }
  277|       |
  278|       |        // Store update for the event loop to send to the App
  279|      2|        self.pending_config_update = Some(update);
  280|       |
  281|       |        // Clear pending changes in settings state
  282|      2|        self.settings_state.clear_pending();
  283|       |
  284|       |        // Provide user feedback
  285|      2|        self.status_message = Some("Model settings saved".to_string());
  286|       |
  287|      2|        true
  288|      4|    }
  289|       |
  290|       |    /// Takes the pending config update, if any, for sending to the App.
  291|       |    ///
  292|       |    /// This is called by the event loop to get updates to send via the config channel.
  293|       |    /// Returns `None` if there's no pending update.
  294|      2|    pub const fn take_pending_config_update(&mut self) -> Option<ModelConfigUpdate> {
  295|      2|        self.pending_config_update.take()
  296|      2|    }

  322|       |    /// Processes an incoming [`LogEvent`] and updates state accordingly.
  323|     62|    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: LogEvent) {
  324|     62|        match event {
  325|       |            LogEvent::AgentStarted {
  326|     33|                agent_type,
  327|     33|                session_id,
  328|     33|                depth,
  329|     33|                task,
  330|       |            } => {
  331|     33|                self.agent_tree_state.handle_agent_started(
  332|     33|                    agent_type,
  333|     33|                    session_id.clone(),
  334|     33|                    depth,
  335|     33|                    task,
  336|       |                );
  337|       |
  338|       |                // Auto-follow: select the new agent if enabled
  339|     33|                if self.auto_follow_enabled {
  340|     33|                    self.selected_agent_id = Some(session_id.clone());
  341|     33|                    self.agent_tree_state.select(&session_id);
  342|     33|                }
                              ^0
  343|       |            }
  344|       |
  345|       |            LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  346|      5|                session_id,
  347|      5|                content,
  348|       |                ..
  349|      5|            } => {
  350|      5|                self.agent_tree_state
  351|      5|                    .handle_agent_message(session_id.as_deref(), &content);
  352|      5|            }
  353|       |
  354|       |            LogEvent::AgentComplete {
  355|     10|                session_id,
  356|     10|                success,
  357|       |                ..
  358|     10|            } => {
  359|     10|                self.agent_tree_state
  360|     10|                    .handle_agent_complete(session_id.as_deref(), success);
  361|     10|            }
  362|       |
  363|       |            LogEvent::TaskCreated {
  364|     12|                task_id,
  365|     12|                name,
  366|     12|                description,
  367|     12|                dependencies,
  368|     12|                depth,
  369|     12|            } => {
  370|     12|                self.task_list_state.handle_task_created(
  371|     12|                    task_id,
  372|     12|                    name,
  373|     12|                    description,
  374|     12|                    dependencies,
  375|     12|                    depth,
  376|     12|                );
  377|     12|            }
  378|       |
  379|       |            LogEvent::TaskStateChanged {
  380|      2|                task_id,
  381|      2|                new_status,
  382|      2|                depth,
  383|       |                ..
  384|      2|            } => {
  385|      2|                self.task_list_state.handle_task_state_changed_at_depth(
  386|      2|                    &task_id,
  387|      2|                    &new_status,
  388|      2|                    depth,
  389|      2|                );
  390|      2|            }
  391|       |
  392|       |            // Tool events update the message buffer for the agent (standalone lines)
  393|       |            LogEvent::ToolCall {
  394|      0|                session_id,
  395|      0|                tool_name,
  396|       |                ..
  397|      0|            } => {
  398|      0|                let msg = format!("> Calling: {tool_name}");
  399|      0|                self.agent_tree_state
  400|      0|                    .handle_standalone_message(session_id.as_deref(), &msg);
  401|      0|            }
  402|       |
  403|       |            LogEvent::ToolProgress {
  404|      0|                session_id,
  405|      0|                progress_text,
  406|       |                ..
  407|      0|            } => {
  408|      0|                // Tool progress is streaming content, concatenate it
  409|      0|                self.agent_tree_state
  410|      0|                    .handle_agent_message(session_id.as_deref(), &progress_text);
  411|      0|            }
  412|       |
  413|       |            LogEvent::ToolResult {
  414|      0|                session_id,
  415|      0|                tool_name,
  416|      0|                is_error,
  417|       |                ..
  418|       |            } => {
  419|      0|                let icon = if is_error { "✗" } else { "✓" };
  420|      0|                let msg = format!("{icon} {tool_name} completed");
  421|      0|                self.agent_tree_state
  422|      0|                    .handle_standalone_message(session_id.as_deref(), &msg);
  423|       |            }
  424|       |
  425|       |            LogEvent::SubtaskCreated {
  426|      0|                parent_depth,
  427|      0|                new_depth,
  428|      0|                path,
  429|      0|                task_description,
  430|       |            } => {
  431|       |                // Notify the agent tree state about the subtask creation
  432|      0|                self.agent_tree_state
  433|      0|                    .handle_subtask_created(parent_depth, new_depth, &path);
  434|       |
  435|       |                // SubtaskCreated creates a new scope but the actual agent
  436|       |                // will be created via AgentStarted. We can log it.
  437|      0|                let msg = format!("+ Subtask: {task_description}");
  438|       |                // Add to the most recent agent's messages
  439|       |                // Clone the id to avoid borrow checker issues
  440|      0|                if let Some(id) = self
  441|      0|                    .agent_tree_state
  442|      0|                    .most_recent_session_id()
  443|      0|                    .map(str::to_string)
  444|      0|                {
  445|      0|                    self.agent_tree_state
  446|      0|                        .handle_standalone_message(Some(&id), &msg);
  447|      0|                }
  448|       |            }
  449|       |        }
  450|     62|    }

  452|       |    /// Cycles focus to the next panel (Tab key handler).

  462|       |    /// Handles a focus change to a new panel.
  463|       |    ///
  464|       |    /// This method centralizes focus change logic including:
  465|       |    /// - Setting the new focus
  466|       |    /// - Auto-selecting the first task when focusing the task list panel
  467|       |    /// - Clearing task selection when focusing agent tree or app logs panels
  468|       |    /// - Preserving task selection when focusing agent output panel
  469|     28|    pub fn on_focus_changed(&mut self, new_focus: FocusedPanel) {
  470|     28|        self.current_focus = new_focus;
  471|       |
  472|     28|        match new_focus {
  473|       |            FocusedPanel::TaskList => {
  474|       |                // Auto-select first task when focusing TaskList if tasks exist and none selected
  475|     13|                if !self.task_list_state.is_empty() && self.task_list_state.selected_index.is_none()
                                                                     ^6                                  ^6
  476|      2|                {
  477|      2|                    self.task_list_state.selected_index = Some(0);
  478|     11|                }
  479|       |            }
  480|      9|            FocusedPanel::AgentTree | FocusedPanel::AppLogs => {
  481|      9|                // Clear task selection when focusing agent-related panels
  482|      9|                self.task_list_state.selected_index = None;
  483|      9|            }
  484|      6|            FocusedPanel::AgentOutput => {
  485|      6|                // Keep task selection unchanged - allows scrolling agent output
  486|      6|                // while keeping task detail visible
  487|      6|            }
  488|       |        }
  489|     28|    }

  555|       |    /// Returns task progress as (completed, total) tuple.
  556|       |    ///
  557|       |    /// A task is considered "completed" if its status is "completed".
  558|       |    #[must_use]
  559|     22|    pub fn get_task_progress(&self) -> (usize, usize) {
  560|     22|        let tasks = self.task_list_state.tasks();
  561|     22|        let total = tasks.len();
  562|     22|        let completed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == "completed").count();
                                                              ^2          ^2
  563|     22|        (completed, total)
  564|     22|    }

  571|       |
  572|       |    /// Returns agent statistics: (succeeded, failed, in progress, total, active).
  573|       |    ///
  574|       |    /// - `succeeded`: Number of agents that completed successfully
  575|       |    /// - `failed`: Number of agents that failed
  576|       |    /// - `in progress`: Number of agents currently running
  577|       |    /// - `total`: Total number of agents (succeeded + failed + in progress)
  578|       |    /// - `active`: Same as in progress (number of currently running agents)
  579|       |    #[must_use]
  580|     26|    pub fn get_agent_stats(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize, usize, usize) {
  581|     26|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress) = self.agent_tree_state.count_agents_by_status();
  582|     26|        let total = succeeded + failed + in_progress;
  583|     26|        let active = in_progress;
  584|     26|        (succeeded, failed, in_progress, total, active)
  585|     26|    }

  621|      1|    fn test_focused_panel_cycle() {

  623|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.next(), FocusedPanel::AgentOutput);
  624|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.next().next(), FocusedPanel::TaskList);

  626|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.next().next().next().next(), FocusedPanel::AgentTree);
  627|      1|    }

  633|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.prev().prev(), FocusedPanel::TaskList);
  634|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.prev().prev().prev(), FocusedPanel::AgentOutput);
  635|      1|        assert_eq!(panel.prev().prev().prev().prev(), FocusedPanel::AgentTree);
  636|      1|    }

  672|       |    #[test]
  673|      1|    fn test_tui_state_handle_agent_started_event() {
  674|       |        use crate::logging::AgentType;
  675|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
  676|       |
  677|      1|        let event = LogEvent::AgentStarted {
  678|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  679|      1|            session_id: "sess-1".to_string(),
  680|      1|            depth: 0,
  681|      1|            task: "Test task".to_string(),
  682|      1|        };
  683|       |
  684|      1|        state.handle_event(event);
  685|       |
  686|       |        // Auto-follow should have selected the agent
  687|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_agent_id, Some("sess-1".to_string()));
  688|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_tree_state.agent_count(), 1);
  689|      1|    }

  729|      1|    fn test_tui_state_get_agent_stats() {
  730|       |        use crate::logging::AgentType;
  731|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
  732|       |
  733|       |        // Initially empty
  734|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress, total, active) = state.get_agent_stats();
  735|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 0);
  736|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
  737|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 0);
  738|      1|        assert_eq!(total, 0);
  739|      1|        assert_eq!(active, 0);
  740|       |
  741|       |        // Add running agents
  742|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentStarted {
  743|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  744|      1|            session_id: "sess-1".to_string(),
  745|      1|            depth: 0,
  746|      1|            task: "Task 1".to_string(),
  747|      1|        });
  748|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentStarted {
  749|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Planner,
  750|      1|            session_id: "sess-2".to_string(),
  751|      1|            depth: 0,
  752|      1|            task: "Task 2".to_string(),
  753|      1|        });
  754|       |
  755|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress, total, active) = state.get_agent_stats();
  756|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 0);
  757|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
  758|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 2);
  759|      1|        assert_eq!(total, 2);
  760|      1|        assert_eq!(active, 2);
  761|       |
  762|       |        // Complete one successfully
  763|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentComplete {
  764|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  765|      1|            session_id: Some("sess-1".to_string()),
  766|      1|            depth: 0,
  767|      1|            success: true,
  768|      1|        });
  769|       |
  770|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress, total, active) = state.get_agent_stats();
  771|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 1);
  772|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 0);
  773|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 1);
  774|      1|        assert_eq!(total, 2);
  775|      1|        assert_eq!(active, 1);
  776|       |
  777|       |        // Fail one
  778|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentComplete {
  779|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Planner,
  780|      1|            session_id: Some("sess-2".to_string()),
  781|      1|            depth: 0,
  782|      1|            success: false,
  783|      1|        });
  784|       |
  785|      1|        let (succeeded, failed, in_progress, total, active) = state.get_agent_stats();
  786|      1|        assert_eq!(succeeded, 1);
  787|      1|        assert_eq!(failed, 1);
  788|      1|        assert_eq!(in_progress, 0);
  789|      1|        assert_eq!(total, 2);
  790|      1|        assert_eq!(active, 0);
  791|      1|    }

  892|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
  893|       |
  894|       |        // Both should start false
  895|      1|        assert!(!state.help_visible);
  896|      1|        assert!(!state.settings_visible);
  897|       |
  898|       |        // Toggle settings
  899|      1|        state.toggle_settings();
  900|      1|        assert!(state.settings_visible);
  901|      1|        assert!(!state.help_visible);
  902|       |
  903|       |        // Toggle help (independent)
  904|      1|        state.toggle_help();
  905|      1|        assert!(state.settings_visible);
  906|      1|        assert!(state.help_visible);
  907|       |
  908|       |        // Toggle settings off
  909|      1|        state.toggle_settings();
  910|      1|        assert!(!state.settings_visible);
  911|      1|        assert!(state.help_visible);
  912|      1|    }

  922|       |    #[test]
  923|      1|    fn test_apply_settings_changes_no_pending() {
  924|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
  925|       |
  926|       |        // No pending changes should return false and not create an update
  927|      1|        let applied = state.apply_settings_changes();
  928|      1|        assert!(!applied);
  929|      1|        assert!(state.pending_config_update.is_none());
  930|      1|        assert!(state.status_message.is_none());
  931|      1|    }
  932|       |
  933|       |    #[test]
  934|      1|    fn test_apply_settings_changes_with_pending() {
  935|       |        use crate::models::{ModelConfig, ModelFallbackChain, ModelTier};
  936|       |
  937|      1|        let mut config = ModelConfig::default();
  938|      1|        config.orchestrator_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Haiku);
  939|      1|        config.planner_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Haiku);
  940|      1|        config.implementer_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Haiku);
  941|      1|        config.available_tiers = [ModelTier::Opus].into_iter().collect();
  942|       |
  943|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::with_model_config(config);
  944|       |
  945|       |        // Set pending changes
  946|      1|        state.settings_state.pending_orchestrator = Some(ModelTier::Opus);
  947|      1|        state.settings_state.pending_planner = Some(ModelTier::Sonnet);
  948|       |
  949|       |        // Apply changes
  950|      1|        let applied = state.apply_settings_changes();
  951|      1|        assert!(applied);
  952|       |
  953|       |        // Check local config was updated
  954|      1|        assert_eq!(
  955|      1|            state.model_config.orchestrator_model.primary(),
  956|       |            Some(ModelTier::Opus)
  957|       |        );
  958|      1|        assert_eq!(
  959|      1|            state.model_config.planner_model.primary(),
  960|       |            Some(ModelTier::Sonnet)
  961|       |        );
  962|      1|        assert_eq!(
  963|      1|            state.model_config.implementer_model.primary(),
  964|       |            Some(ModelTier::Haiku)
  965|       |        ); // Unchanged
  966|       |
  967|       |        // Check pending update was created
  968|      1|        let update = state.pending_config_update.unwrap();
  969|      1|        assert_eq!(update.orchestrator_model, Some(ModelTier::Opus));
  970|      1|        assert_eq!(update.planner_model, Some(ModelTier::Sonnet));
  971|      1|        assert!(update.implementer_model.is_none());
  972|       |
  973|       |        // Check settings state was cleared
  974|      1|        assert!(!state.settings_state.has_pending_changes());
  975|       |
  976|       |        // Check status message
  977|      1|        assert!(state.status_message.is_some());
  978|      1|    }
  979|       |
  980|       |    #[test]
  981|      1|    fn test_take_pending_config_update() {
  982|       |        use crate::models::ModelTier;
  983|       |
  984|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
  985|      1|        state.settings_state.pending_orchestrator = Some(ModelTier::Opus);
  986|       |
  987|       |        // Apply to create the update
  988|      1|        state.apply_settings_changes();
  989|      1|        assert!(state.pending_config_update.is_some());
  990|       |
  991|       |        // Take the update
  992|      1|        let update = state.take_pending_config_update();
  993|      1|        assert!(update.is_some());
  994|      1|        assert_eq!(update.unwrap().orchestrator_model, Some(ModelTier::Opus));
  995|       |
  996|       |        // Second take should return None
  997|      1|        let update2 = state.take_pending_config_update();
  998|      1|        assert!(update2.is_none());
  999|      1|    }

 1005|       |    #[test]
 1006|      1|    fn test_on_focus_changed_task_list_auto_selects_first_task() {
 1007|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
 1008|       |
 1009|       |        // Add some tasks
 1010|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::TaskCreated {
 1011|      1|            task_id: "task-1".to_string(),
 1012|      1|            name: "Task 1".to_string(),
 1013|      1|            description: "First task".to_string(),
 1014|      1|            dependencies: vec![],
 1015|      1|            depth: 0,
 1016|      1|        });
 1017|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::TaskCreated {
 1018|      1|            task_id: "task-2".to_string(),
 1019|      1|            name: "Task 2".to_string(),
 1020|      1|            description: "Second task".to_string(),
 1021|      1|            dependencies: vec![],
 1022|      1|            depth: 0,
 1023|      1|        });
 1024|       |
 1025|       |        // Initially no task selected
 1026|      1|        assert!(state.task_list_state.selected_index.is_none());
 1027|       |
 1028|       |        // Focus task list - should auto-select first task
 1029|      1|        state.on_focus_changed(FocusedPanel::TaskList);
 1030|      1|        assert_eq!(state.task_list_state.selected_index, Some(0));
 1031|      1|        assert_eq!(state.current_focus, FocusedPanel::TaskList);
 1032|      1|    }

 1047|      1|    fn test_on_focus_changed_task_list_keeps_existing_selection() {
 1048|      1|        let mut state = TuiState::new();
 1049|       |
 1050|       |        // Add tasks
 1051|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::TaskCreated {
 1052|      1|            task_id: "task-1".to_string(),
 1053|      1|            name: "Task 1".to_string(),
 1054|      1|            description: "First task".to_string(),
 1055|      1|            dependencies: vec![],
 1056|      1|            depth: 0,
 1057|      1|        });
 1058|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::TaskCreated {
 1059|      1|            task_id: "task-2".to_string(),
 1060|      1|            name: "Task 2".to_string(),
 1061|      1|            description: "Second task".to_string(),
 1062|      1|            dependencies: vec![],
 1063|      1|            depth: 0,
 1064|      1|        });
 1065|       |
 1066|       |        // Manually select second task
 1067|      1|        state.task_list_state.selected_index = Some(1);
 1068|       |
 1069|       |        // Focus task list - should keep existing selection
 1070|      1|        state.on_focus_changed(FocusedPanel::TaskList);

 1072|      1|    }

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tui/task_list_state.rs:
    1|       |//! Task list state management for the TUI.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides [`TaskListState`], which manages the task list display
    4|       |//! state extracted from `LogEvent`s. It tracks task creation, status changes,
    5|       |//! and provides navigation through the task list.
    6|       |
    7|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
    8|       |
    9|       |// ============================================================================
   10|       |// Task Display
   11|       |// ============================================================================
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Represents a task's display state.
   14|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   15|       |#[allow(dead_code)] // Some fields stored for future task detail view
   16|       |pub struct TaskDisplay {
   17|       |    /// Task identifier
   18|       |    pub task_id: String,
   19|       |    /// Task name
   20|       |    pub name: String,
   21|       |    /// Task description
   22|       |    pub description: String,
   23|       |    /// Task status string
   24|       |    pub status: String,
   25|       |    /// Task dependencies
   26|       |    pub dependencies: Vec<String>,
   27|       |    /// Depth in hierarchy
   28|       |    pub depth: u32,
   29|       |}
   30|       |
   31|       |// ============================================================================
   32|       |// Task List State
   33|       |// ============================================================================
   34|       |
   35|       |/// Manages the task list from `LogEvent`s.
   36|       |#[derive(Debug, Default)]
   37|       |pub struct TaskListState {
   38|       |    /// All tasks indexed by task ID.
