    1|       |//! Agent output widget module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module contains [`render_agent_output`] which displays
    4|       |//! real-time output from agents, including support for ANSI colors
    5|       |//! and scrolling.
    6|       |
    7|       |use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    8|       |use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};
    9|       |use ratatui::text::{Line, Span, Text};
   10|       |use ratatui::widgets::{
   11|       |    Block, Borders, Paragraph, Scrollbar, ScrollbarOrientation, ScrollbarState, Wrap,
   12|       |};
   13|       |use ratatui::Frame;
   14|       |
   15|       |use super::super::state::{FocusedPanel, TuiState};
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Renders the agent output panel.
   18|       |///
   19|       |/// This function displays streaming output from the selected agent,
   20|       |/// with support for scrollback and auto-scroll to bottom when new
   21|       |/// content arrives.
   22|       |///
   23|       |/// # Display Format
   24|       |///
   25|       |/// Messages are displayed with tool calls highlighted:
   26|       |/// ```text
   27|       |/// > view src/auth.rs
   28|       |/// Looking at the auth module...
   29|       |/// I see we need to add JWT...
   30|       |/// > str-replace-editor auth.rs
   31|       |/// Adding authenticate() function
   32|       |/// ```
   33|       |///
   34|       |/// # Arguments
   35|       |#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] // Terminal dimensions fit in u16
   36|       |///
   37|       |/// * `frame` - The ratatui frame to render into
   38|       |/// * `area` - The rectangular area to render into
   39|       |/// * `state` - The TUI state containing agent messages and scroll position
   40|       |/// * `focused` - Whether this panel currently has keyboard focus
   41|     20|pub fn render_agent_output(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, state: &mut TuiState, focused: bool) {
   42|     20|    let is_focused = focused || state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::AgentOutput;
                                              ^3
   43|       |
   44|       |    // Build the block with focus-dependent styling
   45|     20|    let border_style = if is_focused {
   46|     17|        Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)
   47|       |    } else {
   48|      3|        Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)
   49|       |    };
   50|       |
   51|     20|    let title = if let Some(agent) = state.selected_agent() {
                                          ^17
   52|     17|        format!(" {} Output ", agent.display_name(state.animation_frame))
   53|       |    } else {
   54|      3|        " Agent Output ".to_string()
   55|       |    };
   56|       |
   57|     20|    let block = Block::default()
   58|     20|        .title(title)
   59|     20|        .borders(Borders::ALL)
   60|     20|        .border_style(border_style);
   61|       |
   62|       |    // Get messages for the selected agent
   63|     20|    let messages = state.selected_agent_messages();
   64|       |
   65|       |    // Handle empty selection or no messages
   66|     20|    let content = if state.selected_agent_id.is_none() {
   67|      3|        Text::from(vec![
   68|      3|            Line::from(""),
   69|      3|            Line::from(Span::styled(
   70|       |                "No agent selected",
   71|      3|                Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray).italic(),
   72|       |            )),
   73|      3|            Line::from(""),
   74|      3|            Line::from(Span::styled(
   75|       |                "Select an agent from the tree on the left",
   76|      3|                Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
   77|       |            )),
   78|       |        ])
   79|     17|    } else if let Some(msgs) = messages.filter(|v| !v.is_empty()) {
                                     ^10
   80|       |        // Format messages with styling
   81|     10|        let lines = format_messages(msgs);
   82|     10|        Text::from(lines)
   83|       |    } else {
   84|       |        // Show animated "waiting" display with boat and waves
   85|       |        // Pass inner width (minus borders) for centering
   86|      7|        render_waiting_animation(state, area.width.saturating_sub(2))
   87|       |    };
   88|       |
   89|       |    // Calculate visible area (minus borders)
   90|     20|    let inner_height = area.height.saturating_sub(2) as usize;
   91|       |
   92|       |    // Create the paragraph with block and wrapping to calculate line count.
   93|       |    // This gives us the actual rendered line count, accounting for wrapped lines.
   94|     20|    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(content)
   95|     20|        .block(block)
   96|     20|        .wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
   97|       |
   98|       |    // Use line_count() to get actual rendered lines including wrapped lines.
   99|       |    // This accounts for the block borders internally.
  100|     20|    let total_lines = paragraph.line_count(area.width);
  101|       |
  102|       |    // Auto-scroll: if new messages arrived, scroll to bottom
  103|     20|    let current_message_count = messages.map_or(0, Vec::len);
  104|     20|    if current_message_count > state.last_message_count {
  105|       |        // New content arrived, auto-scroll to bottom
  106|      6|        if total_lines > inner_height {
  107|      4|            state.agent_output_scroll = (total_lines - inner_height) as u16;
  108|      4|        } else {
  109|      2|            state.agent_output_scroll = 0;
  110|      2|        }
  111|      6|        state.last_message_count = current_message_count;
  112|     14|    }
  113|       |
  114|       |    // Clamp scroll position to valid range
  115|     20|    let max_scroll = total_lines.saturating_sub(inner_height);
  116|     20|    if state.agent_output_scroll > max_scroll as u16 {
  117|      2|        state.agent_output_scroll = max_scroll as u16;
  118|     18|    }
  119|       |
  120|       |    // Add scroll offset to the paragraph and render
  121|     20|    let paragraph = paragraph.scroll((state.agent_output_scroll, 0));
  122|       |
  123|     20|    frame.render_widget(paragraph, area);
  124|       |
  125|       |    // Render scrollbar if content exceeds visible area
  126|     20|    if total_lines > inner_height {
  127|     11|        let scrollbar = Scrollbar::new(ScrollbarOrientation::VerticalRight)
  128|     11|            .begin_symbol(Some("▲"))
  129|     11|            .end_symbol(Some("▼"))
  130|     11|            .track_symbol(Some("│"))
  131|     11|            .thumb_symbol("█");
  132|     11|
  133|     11|        let mut scrollbar_state =
  134|     11|            ScrollbarState::new(max_scroll).position(state.agent_output_scroll as usize);
  135|     11|
  136|     11|        // Render scrollbar in the same area (it will appear in the border)
  137|     11|        frame.render_stateful_widget(scrollbar, area, &mut scrollbar_state);
  138|     11|    }
                  ^9
  139|     20|}
  140|       |
  141|       |/// Renders an animated waiting display with a paper boat sailing on waves.
  142|       |///
  143|       |/// The animation consists of:
  144|       |/// 1. A boat emoji (⛵) that moves forward across the screen, then "washes back" to start
  145|       |/// 2. Two rows of animated waves that shift horizontally
  146|       |/// 3. A status line showing "Agent working..." with elapsed time (if agent is running)
  147|       |///
  148|       |/// The boat moves forward over ~12 seconds, then quickly washes back over ~2 seconds.
  149|      7|fn render_waiting_animation(state: &TuiState, panel_width: u16) -> Text<'static> {
  150|      7|    let frame = state.animation_frame;
  151|       |
  152|       |    // Wave characters that create a flowing water effect
  153|       |    const WAVE_CHARS: [char; 4] = ['~', '·', '~', '·'];
  154|       |    const WAVE_WIDTH: usize = 24;
  155|       |
  156|       |    // Boat movement: moves forward over ~1800 frames (30s), washes back over ~300 frames (5s)
  157|       |    // Total cycle: 2100 frames (~35 seconds at 60fps)
  158|       |    const FORWARD_FRAMES: u32 = 1800;
  159|       |    const BACKWARD_FRAMES: u32 = 300;
  160|       |    const CYCLE_FRAMES: u32 = FORWARD_FRAMES + BACKWARD_FRAMES;
  161|       |
  162|      7|    let cycle_pos = frame % CYCLE_FRAMES;
  163|       |    // Boat position in visual columns (0 to WAVE_WIDTH - 2, since boat takes 2 columns)
  164|      7|    let max_boat_pos = WAVE_WIDTH - 2;
  165|      7|    let boat_pos = if cycle_pos < FORWARD_FRAMES {
  166|       |        // Moving forward: ease-out for smooth deceleration
  167|      7|        let progress = cycle_pos as f32 / FORWARD_FRAMES as f32;
  168|      7|        let eased = 1.0 - (1.0 - progress).powi(2); // ease-out quadratic
  169|      7|        (eased * max_boat_pos as f32) as usize
  170|       |    } else {
  171|       |        // Washing back: quick linear return
  172|      0|        let backward_progress = (cycle_pos - FORWARD_FRAMES) as f32 / BACKWARD_FRAMES as f32;
  173|      0|        ((1.0 - backward_progress) * max_boat_pos as f32) as usize
  174|       |    };
  175|       |
  176|       |    // Calculate centering padding
  177|      7|    let padding = (panel_width as usize).saturating_sub(WAVE_WIDTH) / 2;
  178|      7|    let pad_str = " ".repeat(padding);
  179|       |
  180|       |    // Wave offsets - different speeds for parallax depth effect
  181|       |    // Closer waves (bottom) move faster, distant waves (top) move slower
  182|      7|    let wave_offset_top = (frame / 24) as usize; // Slowest (horizon)
  183|      7|    let wave_offset_mid = (frame / 18) as usize; // Medium
  184|      7|    let wave_offset_bottom = (frame / 12) as usize; // Fastest (closest)
  185|      7|    let mut boat_line = String::with_capacity(WAVE_WIDTH + padding);
  186|      7|    let mut wave_line1 = String::with_capacity(WAVE_WIDTH + padding);
  187|      7|    let mut wave_line2 = String::with_capacity(WAVE_WIDTH + padding);
  188|       |
  189|       |    // Add centering padding
  190|      7|    boat_line.push_str(&pad_str);
  191|      7|    wave_line1.push_str(&pad_str);
  192|      7|    wave_line2.push_str(&pad_str);
  193|       |
  194|       |    // Build boat line (boat emoji is double-width, so we track visual position)
  195|      7|    let mut visual_col = 0;
  196|    168|    while visual_col < WAVE_WIDTH {
  197|    161|        if visual_col == boat_pos {
  198|      7|            boat_line.push('⛵');
  199|      7|            visual_col += 2; // Boat takes 2 visual columns
  200|    154|        } else {
  201|    154|            let wave_char = WAVE_CHARS[(visual_col + wave_offset_top) % WAVE_CHARS.len()];
  202|    154|            boat_line.push(wave_char);
  203|    154|            visual_col += 1;
  204|    154|        }
  205|       |    }
  206|       |
  207|       |    // Build wave lines with different offsets for parallax effect
  208|    168|    for i in 0..WAVE_WIDTH {
                           ^7
  209|    168|        let wave_char1 = WAVE_CHARS[(i + wave_offset_mid) % WAVE_CHARS.len()];
  210|    168|        let wave_char2 = WAVE_CHARS[(i + wave_offset_bottom) % WAVE_CHARS.len()];
  211|    168|        wave_line1.push(wave_char1);
  212|    168|        wave_line2.push(wave_char2);
  213|    168|    }
  214|       |
  215|       |    // Build the status message with elapsed time
  216|      7|    let status_message = if let Some(agent) = state.selected_agent() {
  217|       |        use super::super::agent_node::AgentStatus;
  218|      7|        match agent.status {
  219|       |            AgentStatus::Running => {
  220|      5|                let elapsed = agent.start_time.elapsed();
  221|      5|                let secs = elapsed.as_secs();
  222|      5|                let mins = secs / 60;
  223|      5|                let secs = secs % 60;
  224|      5|                if mins > 0 {
  225|      0|                    format!("Agent working... {}m {}s", mins, secs)
  226|       |                } else {
  227|      5|                    format!("Agent working... {}s", secs)
  228|       |                }
  229|       |            }
  230|      1|            AgentStatus::Completed => "Agent completed".to_string(),
  231|      1|            AgentStatus::Failed => "Agent failed".to_string(),
  232|       |        }
  233|       |    } else {
  234|      0|        "Waiting for output...".to_string()
  235|       |    };
  236|       |
  237|       |    // Center the status message
  238|      7|    let status_padding = (panel_width as usize).saturating_sub(status_message.len()) / 2;
  239|      7|    let centered_status = format!("{}{}", " ".repeat(status_padding), status_message);
  240|       |
  241|       |    // Use intensity modifiers instead of hardcoded colors for theme compatibility
  242|       |    // DIM = faint/far (horizon), normal = mid, BOLD = bright/close
  243|      7|    Text::from(vec![
  244|      7|        Line::from(""),
  245|      7|        Line::from(""),
  246|      7|        Line::from(Span::styled(
  247|      7|            boat_line,
  248|      7|            Style::default().add_modifier(Modifier::DIM),
  249|       |        )),
  250|      7|        Line::from(Span::styled(wave_line1, Style::default())),
  251|      7|        Line::from(Span::styled(
  252|      7|            wave_line2,
  253|      7|            Style::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  254|       |        )),
  255|      7|        Line::from(""),
  256|      7|        Line::from(Span::styled(
  257|      7|            centered_status,
  258|      7|            Style::default()
  259|      7|                .add_modifier(Modifier::DIM)
  260|      7|                .add_modifier(Modifier::ITALIC),
  261|       |        )),
  262|       |    ])
  263|      7|}
  264|       |
  265|       |/// Formats messages for display with styling.
  266|       |///
  267|       |/// Tool calls are highlighted with emoji indicators and different colors.
  268|       |///
  269|       |/// # Line Break Handling
  270|       |///
  271|       |/// - Single `\n` in content creates separate messages (via `handle_agent_message`)
  272|       |///   which display as consecutive lines (no blank line between)
  273|       |/// - Double `\n\n` creates an empty message between two content messages,
  274|       |///   displaying as content, blank line, content
  275|       |/// - Consecutive empty messages are collapsed to a single blank line
  276|       |/// - Messages with embedded newlines are split via `.lines()` for proper display
  277|     30|fn format_messages(messages: &[String]) -> Vec<Line<'static>> {
  278|     30|    let mut result: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
  279|     30|    let mut prev_was_empty = false;
  280|       |
  281|    135|    for msg in messages {
                             ^30
  282|    135|        if msg.is_empty() {
  283|       |            // For empty messages (line breaks), only add if previous wasn't empty
  284|       |            // This collapses consecutive line breaks into one
  285|      7|            if !prev_was_empty {
  286|      5|                result.push(Line::from(""));
  287|      5|            }
                          ^2
  288|      7|            prev_was_empty = true;
  289|       |        } else {
  290|       |            // For non-empty messages, split into lines and format each
  291|    129|            for line in msg.lines() {
                                      ^128
  292|    129|                result.push(format_line(line));
  293|    129|            }
  294|       |            // .lines() strips trailing newlines, so if the message ends with \n,
  295|       |            // add an empty line to preserve the line break in the display.
  296|       |            // This ensures single \n displays as a single line break.
  297|    128|            if msg.ends_with('\n') {
  298|      0|                result.push(Line::from(""));
  299|      0|                prev_was_empty = true;
  300|    128|            } else {
  301|    128|                prev_was_empty = false;
  302|    128|            }
  303|       |        }
  304|       |    }
  305|       |
  306|     30|    result
  307|     30|}
  308|       |
  309|       |/// Formats a single line with appropriate styling.
  310|    134|fn format_line(line: &str) -> Line<'static> {
  311|    134|    let line_owned = line.to_string();
  312|       |
  313|       |    // Tool call indicators (using simple text symbols, not image emoji)
  314|    134|    if line_owned.starts_with('>') {
  315|      1|        Line::from(Span::styled(
  316|      1|            line_owned,
  317|      1|            Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow).bold(),
  318|       |        ))
  319|    133|    } else if line_owned.starts_with('✓') {
  320|      1|        Line::from(Span::styled(line_owned, Style::default().fg(Color::Green)))
  321|    132|    } else if line_owned.starts_with('✗') {
  322|      1|        Line::from(Span::styled(line_owned, Style::default().fg(Color::Red)))
  323|    131|    } else if line_owned.starts_with('+') {
  324|      1|        Line::from(Span::styled(line_owned, Style::default().fg(Color::Blue)))
  325|       |    } else {
  326|    130|        Line::from(line_owned)
  327|       |    }
  328|    134|}
  329|       |
  330|       |/// Calculates the total rendered line count for agent messages, accounting for text wrapping.
  331|       |///
  332|       |/// This function creates a temporary Paragraph to get the accurate wrapped line count,
  333|       |/// matching the rendering logic in `render_agent_output`.
  334|       |///
  335|       |/// # Arguments
  336|       |///
  337|       |/// * `messages` - The agent messages to count lines for
  338|       |/// * `inner_width` - The inner width of the panel (excluding borders)
  339|       |///
  340|       |/// # Returns
  341|       |///
  342|       |/// The total number of rendered lines, including wrapped lines.
  343|       |#[must_use]
  344|      4|pub fn calculate_wrapped_line_count(messages: &[String], inner_width: u16) -> usize {
  345|      4|    let lines = format_messages(messages);
  346|      4|    let content = Text::from(lines);
  347|      4|    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(content).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
  348|      4|    paragraph.line_count(inner_width)
  349|      4|}
  350|       |
  351|       |/// Scrolls the agent output up by the specified number of lines.
  352|      4|const fn scroll_up(state: &mut TuiState, lines: u16) {
  353|      4|    state.agent_output_scroll = state.agent_output_scroll.saturating_sub(lines);
  354|      4|}
  355|       |
  356|       |/// Scrolls the agent output down by the specified number of lines.
  357|       |#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] // Terminal line counts fit in u16
  358|      3|fn scroll_down(state: &mut TuiState, lines: u16, max_lines: usize, visible_height: usize) {
  359|      3|    let max_scroll = max_lines.saturating_sub(visible_height) as u16;
  360|      3|    state.agent_output_scroll = (state.agent_output_scroll + lines).min(max_scroll);
  361|      3|}
  362|       |
  363|       |/// Scrolls to the top of the agent output.
  364|      3|const fn scroll_to_top(state: &mut TuiState) {
  365|      3|    state.agent_output_scroll = 0;
  366|      3|}
  367|       |
  368|       |/// Scrolls to the bottom of the agent output.
  369|       |#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] // Terminal line counts fit in u16
  370|       |#[allow(clippy::missing_const_for_fn)] // if-else prevents const
  371|      3|fn scroll_to_bottom(state: &mut TuiState, total_lines: usize, visible_height: usize) {
  372|      3|    if total_lines > visible_height {
  373|      2|        state.agent_output_scroll = (total_lines - visible_height) as u16;
  374|      2|    } else {
  375|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 0;
  376|      1|    }
  377|      3|}
  378|       |
  379|       |/// Handles keyboard events for the agent output panel.
  380|       |///
  381|       |/// This function processes keyboard events when the agent output panel
  382|       |/// has focus. Supported keys:
  383|       |///
  384|       |/// - `PageUp`: Scroll up by visible height
  385|       |/// - `PageDown`: Scroll down by visible height
  386|       |/// - `Up`/`k`: Scroll up by 1 line
  387|       |/// - `Down`/`j`: Scroll down by 1 line
  388|       |/// - `Home`/`g`: Scroll to top
  389|       |/// - `End`/`G`: Scroll to bottom
  390|       |///
  391|       |/// Returns `true` if the key was handled, `false` otherwise.
  392|      9|pub fn handle_agent_output_key(
  393|      9|    state: &mut TuiState,
  394|      9|    key_code: crossterm::event::KeyCode,
  395|      9|    visible_height: u16,
  396|      9|    visible_width: u16,
  397|      9|) -> bool {
  398|       |    use crossterm::event::KeyCode;
  399|       |
  400|       |    // Calculate total lines from current messages, accounting for text wrapping.
  401|       |    // We need to create a temporary Paragraph to get the accurate wrapped line count.
  402|       |    // Subtract 2 from width to account for left/right borders, matching render_agent_output
  403|       |    // which creates a paragraph with Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).
  404|      9|    let inner_width = visible_width.saturating_sub(2);
  405|      9|    let total_lines = state.selected_agent_messages().map_or(0, |msgs| {
  406|      9|        let lines = format_messages(msgs);
  407|      9|        let content = Text::from(lines);
  408|      9|        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(content).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
  409|      9|        paragraph.line_count(inner_width)
  410|      9|    });
  411|       |
  412|      9|    let page_size = visible_height.saturating_sub(2); // Account for borders
  413|       |
  414|      9|    match key_code {
  415|       |        KeyCode::PageUp => {
  416|      1|            scroll_up(state, page_size);
  417|      1|            true
  418|       |        }
  419|       |        KeyCode::PageDown => {
  420|      0|            scroll_down(state, page_size, total_lines, page_size as usize);
  421|      0|            true
  422|       |        }
  423|       |        KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => {
  424|      2|            scroll_up(state, 1);
  425|      2|            true
  426|       |        }
  427|       |        KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => {
  428|      2|            scroll_down(state, 1, total_lines, page_size as usize);
  429|      2|            true
  430|       |        }
  431|       |        KeyCode::Home | KeyCode::Char('g') => {
  432|      2|            scroll_to_top(state);
  433|      2|            true
  434|       |        }
  435|       |        KeyCode::End | KeyCode::Char('G') => {
  436|      1|            scroll_to_bottom(state, total_lines, page_size as usize);
  437|      1|            true
  438|       |        }
  439|      1|        _ => false,
  440|       |    }
  441|      9|}

  476|      1|    fn test_format_messages_single_line_break() {
  477|       |        // Simulates "Line 1\nLine 2" which gets split into ["Line 1", "Line 2"]
  478|      1|        let messages = vec!["Line 1".to_string(), "Line 2".to_string()];
  479|      1|        let lines = format_messages(&messages);
  480|       |
  481|       |        // Should produce two separate lines
  482|      1|        assert_eq!(lines.len(), 2);
  483|       |
  484|       |        // Verify the actual content
  485|      1|        let line1_spans = &lines[0].spans;
  486|      1|        let line2_spans = &lines[1].spans;
  487|      1|        assert_eq!(line1_spans.len(), 1);
  488|      1|        assert_eq!(line2_spans.len(), 1);
  489|      1|        assert_eq!(line1_spans[0].content.as_ref(), "Line 1");
  490|      1|        assert_eq!(line2_spans[0].content.as_ref(), "Line 2");

  546|       |    /// Test that verifies the exact scenario mentioned in the task:
  547|       |    /// "Line 1\nLine 2" should display as two separate lines
  548|       |    #[test]
  549|      1|    fn test_single_newline_splits_correctly() {
  550|       |        // Simulate the input as it would come from handle_agent_message
  551|       |        // with content "Line 1\nLine 2"
  552|       |        // split('\n') gives ["Line 1", "Line 2"]
  553|      1|        let messages = vec!["Line 1".to_string(), "Line 2".to_string()];
  554|      1|        let lines = format_messages(&messages);
  555|       |
  556|       |        // Should produce exactly 2 lines
  557|      1|        assert_eq!(lines.len(), 2, "Expected 2 lines, got {}", lines.len());
                                                                             ^0    ^0
  558|       |
  559|       |        // Verify content
  560|      1|        assert_eq!(lines[0].spans[0].content.as_ref(), "Line 1");
  561|      1|        assert_eq!(lines[1].spans[0].content.as_ref(), "Line 2");
  562|       |
  563|       |        // Ensure they are NOT merged into one line
  564|       |        // (if they were merged, we'd have 1 line with content "Line 1Line 2")
  565|      1|    }
  566|       |
  567|       |    #[test]
  568|      1|    fn test_format_messages_text_followed_by_empty() {
  569|       |        // Simulates streaming: "Hello" then "\n" arrives
  570|       |        // handle_agent_message would produce ["Hello", ""]
  571|      1|        let messages = vec!["Hello".to_string(), "".to_string()];
  572|      1|        let lines = format_messages(&messages);
  573|       |
  574|       |        // Should be: Hello, then a blank line = 2 lines
  575|      1|        assert_eq!(lines.len(), 2);
  576|      1|    }
  577|       |
  578|       |    #[test]
  579|      1|    fn test_format_messages_mixed_content() {
  580|       |        // Mixed: text, empty (newline), text, empty, empty (collapsed), text
  581|      1|        let messages = vec![
  582|      1|            "Line 1".to_string(),
  583|      1|            "".to_string(),
  584|      1|            "Line 2".to_string(),
  585|      1|            "".to_string(),
  586|      1|            "".to_string(),
  587|      1|            "Line 3".to_string(),
  588|       |        ];
  589|      1|        let lines = format_messages(&messages);
  590|       |
  591|       |        // Line 1 + blank + Line 2 + blank (collapsed from 2 empties) + Line 3 = 5 lines
  592|      1|        assert_eq!(lines.len(), 5);
  593|      1|    }

  595|       |    // ========================================================================
  596|       |    // Auto-Scroll Tests
  597|       |    // ========================================================================
  598|       |
  599|       |    #[test]
  600|      1|    fn test_render_agent_output_auto_scrolls_when_new_messages_arrive() {
  601|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  602|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 6);
  603|       |
  604|      8|        for idx in 0..8 {
                                 ^1
  605|      8|            state
  606|      8|                .agent_tree_state
  607|      8|                .handle_standalone_message(Some("agent-1"), &format!("line {idx}"));
  608|      8|        }
  609|       |
  610|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  611|       |
  612|      1|        let expected_scroll = Paragraph::new(Text::from(
  613|      1|            state
  614|      1|                .selected_agent_messages()
  615|      1|                .expect("messages should exist")
  616|      1|                .iter()
  617|      1|                .cloned()
  618|      8|                .map(|msg| Line::from(msg))
                               ^1
  619|      1|                .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
  620|       |        ))
  621|      1|        .block(Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL))
  622|      1|        .wrap(Wrap { trim: false })
  623|      1|        .line_count(area.width)
  624|      1|        .saturating_sub(area.height.saturating_sub(2) as usize) as u16;
  625|       |
  626|      1|        assert_eq!(state.last_message_count, 8);
  627|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, expected_scroll);
  628|      1|    }
  629|       |
  630|       |    #[test]
  631|      1|    fn test_auto_scroll_triggers_on_new_content() {
  632|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  633|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 6);
  634|       |
  635|       |        // Add initial messages using standalone messages (simpler counting)
  636|      3|        for idx in 0..3 {
                                 ^1
  637|      3|            state
  638|      3|                .agent_tree_state
  639|      3|                .handle_standalone_message(Some("agent-1"), &format!("message {idx}"));
  640|      3|        }
  641|       |
  642|       |        // Render to set baseline
  643|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  644|      1|        let initial_count = state.last_message_count;
  645|      1|        assert_eq!(initial_count, 3);
  646|       |
  647|       |        // User scrolls up manually
  648|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 0;
  649|       |
  650|       |        // New message arrives
  651|      1|        state
  652|      1|            .agent_tree_state
  653|      1|            .handle_standalone_message(Some("agent-1"), "new message");
  654|       |
  655|       |        // Render again - should auto-scroll
  656|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  657|      1|        assert!(state.last_message_count > initial_count,
  658|       |            "Message count should increase after new message");
  659|      1|    }
  660|       |
  661|       |    #[test]
  662|      1|    fn test_auto_scroll_stays_at_zero_when_content_fits() {
  663|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  664|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 20); // Large area
  665|       |
  666|       |        // Add a single short message
  667|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  668|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  669|      1|            session_id: Some("agent-1".to_string()),
  670|      1|            depth: 0,
  671|      1|            content: "short".to_string(),
  672|      1|        });
  673|       |
  674|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  675|       |
  676|       |        // Scroll should be 0 when content fits
  677|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 0);
  678|      1|    }

  684|       |    #[test]
  685|      1|    fn test_render_agent_output_clamps_scroll_after_content_shrinks() {
  686|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  687|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 6);
  688|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 99;
  689|       |
  690|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  691|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  692|      1|            session_id: Some("agent-1".to_string()),
  693|      1|            depth: 0,
  694|      1|            content: "short output".to_string(),
  695|      1|        });
  696|      1|        state.last_message_count = 1;
  697|       |
  698|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  699|       |
  700|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 0);
  701|      1|        assert_eq!(state.last_message_count, 1);
  702|      1|    }
  703|       |
  704|       |    #[test]
  705|      1|    fn test_scroll_clamp_to_valid_range() {
  706|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  707|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 6);
  708|       |
  709|       |        // Set an absurdly high scroll
  710|      1|        state.agent_output_scroll = 9999;
  711|       |
  712|       |        // Add minimal content
  713|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  714|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  715|      1|            session_id: Some("agent-1".to_string()),
  716|      1|            depth: 0,
  717|      1|            content: "line1".to_string(),
  718|      1|        });
  719|      1|        state.last_message_count = 1;
  720|       |
  721|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  722|       |
  723|       |        // Scroll should be clamped to valid range (0 since content fits)
  724|      1|        assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, 0);
  725|      1|    }
  726|       |
  727|       |    #[test]
  728|      1|    fn test_scroll_maintains_position_when_valid() {
  729|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  730|      1|        let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 6);
  731|       |
  732|       |        // Add enough content to scroll
  733|     15|        for idx in 0..15 {
                                 ^1
  734|     15|            state
  735|     15|                .agent_tree_state
  736|     15|                .handle_standalone_message(Some("agent-1"), &format!("line {idx}"));
  737|     15|        }
  738|       |
  739|       |        // Set initial render
  740|      1|        render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  741|      1|        let auto_scroll = state.agent_output_scroll;
  742|       |
  743|       |        // User scrolls up by 2
  744|      1|        if auto_scroll > 2 {
  745|      1|            state.agent_output_scroll = auto_scroll - 2;
  746|      1|            let manual_scroll = state.agent_output_scroll;
  747|       |
  748|       |            // Render again (no new messages)
  749|      1|            render_agent_output_to_string(&mut state, area);
  750|       |
  751|       |            // Scroll should stay at manual position (no new messages)
  752|      1|            assert_eq!(state.agent_output_scroll, manual_scroll);
  753|      0|        }
  754|      1|    }

  831|      1|        let mut state = state_with_selected_agent("agent-1");
  832|      1|        state.handle_event(LogEvent::AgentMessage {
  833|      1|            agent_type: AgentType::Orchestrator,
  834|      1|            session_id: Some("agent-1".to_string()),
  835|      1|            depth: 0,
  836|      1|            content: "this is a deliberately long line that wraps multiple times".to_string(),
  837|      1|        });
  838|       |
  839|      1|        let handled = handle_agent_output_key(
  840|      1|            &mut state,
  841|      1|            crossterm::event::KeyCode::End,
  842|       |            6,
  843|       |            18,
  844|       |        );
  845|       |
  846|      1|        let total_lines = calculate_wrapped_line_count(
  847|      1|            state.selected_agent_messages().expect("messages should exist"),
  848|       |            16,
  849|       |        );
  850|      1|        let visible_height = 4;
  851|       |
  852|      1|        assert!(handled);
  853|      1|        assert_eq!(
  854|       |            state.agent_output_scroll,
  855|      1|            total_lines.saturating_sub(visible_height) as u16
  856|       |        );
  857|      1|    }

 1060|       |    // ========================================================================
 1061|       |    // calculate_wrapped_line_count Tests
 1062|       |    // ========================================================================
 1063|       |
 1064|       |    #[test]
 1065|      1|    fn test_calculate_wrapped_line_count_empty() {

   39|       |/// Renders the application logs panel using tui-logger.
   40|       |///
   41|       |/// This function renders application logs using [`TuiLoggerWidget`] which provides:
   42|       |/// - Log level filtering per target
   43|       |/// - Scrollback through log history
   44|       |/// - Timestamp, level, target, and message display
   45|       |///
   46|       |/// Note: Uses `TuiLoggerWidget` directly instead of `TuiLoggerSmartWidget` to avoid
   47|       |/// the built-in speed indicator (e.g., `[log=0.3/s]`) that appears in the title.
   48|       |///
   49|       |/// # Arguments
   50|       |///
   51|       |/// * `frame` - The ratatui frame to render to
   52|       |/// * `area` - The rectangular area to render the widget in
   53|       |/// * `state` - The TUI state (contains logger state for filtering and navigation)
   54|       |/// * `focused` - Whether this panel currently has keyboard focus
   55|       |///
   56|       |/// # Example
   57|       |///
   58|       |/// ```ignore
   59|       |/// use ratatui::Frame;
   60|       |/// use ratatui::layout::Rect;
   61|       |/// use paperboat::tui::widgets::render_app_logs;
   62|       |/// use paperboat::tui::TuiState;
   63|       |///
   64|       |/// fn render(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, state: &TuiState) {
   65|       |///     let focused = state.current_focus == FocusedPanel::AppLogs;
   66|       |///     render_app_logs(frame, area, state, focused);
   67|       |/// }
   68|       |/// ```

   71|       |
   72|       |    // Create the border block with focus-dependent styling
   73|      2|    let border_color = if is_focused {
   74|      0|        colors::FOCUSED_BORDER
   75|       |    } else {
   76|      2|        colors::UNFOCUSED_BORDER
   77|       |    };
   78|       |
   79|       |    // Use TuiLoggerWidget directly instead of TuiLoggerSmartWidget to avoid the
   80|       |    // built-in speed indicator (e.g., [log=0.3/s]) in the title.
   81|      2|    let log_block = Block::default()
   82|      2|        .title(" App Logs ")
   83|      2|        .borders(Borders::ALL)
   84|      2|        .border_style(Style::default().fg(border_color));
   85|       |
   86|      2|    let logger_widget = TuiLoggerWidget::default()
   87|      2|        .block(log_block)
   88|      2|        .style_error(Style::default().fg(colors::ERROR))
   89|      2|        .style_warn(Style::default().fg(colors::WARN))
   90|      2|        .style_info(Style::default().fg(colors::INFO))
   91|      2|        .style_debug(Style::default().fg(colors::DEBUG))

   97|      2|        .output_file(false)
   98|      2|        .output_line(false)
   99|      2|        .state(&state.logger_state);
  100|       |

   58|      6|            self.selected_index = self
   59|      6|                .selected_index
   60|      6|                .checked_sub(1)
   61|      6|                .unwrap_or(self.available_backends.len() - 1);
   62|      6|        }
                      ^1
   63|      7|    }
   64|       |
   65|       |    /// Returns the currently selected backend, if any.
   66|       |    #[must_use]
   67|     20|    pub fn selected_backend(&self) -> Option<BackendKind> {
   68|     20|        self.available_backends.get(self.selected_index).copied()
   69|     20|    }
   70|       |
   71|       |    /// Confirms selection and hides the popup.
   72|       |    ///
   73|       |    /// Returns the selected backend.
   74|      5|    pub fn confirm_selection(&mut self) -> Option<BackendKind> {
   75|      5|        self.visible = false;
   76|      5|        self.selected_backend()
   77|      5|    }
   78|       |}
   79|       |
   80|       |// ============================================================================
   81|       |// Rendering
   82|       |// ============================================================================
   83|       |
   84|       |/// Renders the backend selection popup on top of the splash screen.
   85|       |///
   86|       |/// This function renders a centered popup for selecting between available
   87|       |/// AI backends. It uses the `Clear` widget to clear the area behind the
   88|       |/// popup, ensuring the selection is readable over the animated splash.
   89|       |///
   90|       |/// # Arguments
   91|       |///
   92|       |/// * `frame` - The ratatui frame to render into
   93|       |/// * `area` - The full terminal area to render the overlay on
   94|       |/// * `state` - The backend selection state
   95|      6|pub fn render_backend_selection_popup(
   96|      6|    frame: &mut Frame,
   97|      6|    area: Rect,
   98|      6|    state: &BackendSelectionState,
   99|      6|) {
  100|       |    // Calculate popup dimensions - small centered popup
  101|      6|    let popup_area = centered_rect(area, 50, 40);
  102|       |
  103|       |    // Clear the area behind the popup
  104|      6|    frame.render_widget(Clear, popup_area);
  105|       |
  106|       |    // Build the content
  107|      6|    let lines = build_selection_content(state);
  108|      6|    let content = Text::from(lines);
  109|       |
  110|       |    // Create the block with styled borders
  111|      6|    let block = Block::default()
  112|      6|        .title(" Select Backend ")
  113|      6|        .title_style(
  114|      6|            Style::default()
  115|      6|                .fg(Color::White)
  116|      6|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  117|       |        )
  118|      6|        .borders(Borders::ALL)
  119|      6|        .border_type(ratatui::widgets::BorderType::Rounded)
  120|      6|        .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan))
  121|      6|        .style(Style::default().bg(Color::Black));
  122|       |
  123|      6|    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(content)
  124|      6|        .block(block)
  125|      6|        .alignment(Alignment::Left);
  126|       |
  127|      6|    frame.render_widget(paragraph, popup_area);
  128|      6|}
  129|       |
  130|       |/// Builds the content lines for the backend selection popup.
  131|      8|fn build_selection_content(state: &BackendSelectionState) -> Vec<Line<'static>> {
  132|      8|    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
  133|       |
  134|       |    // Header
  135|      8|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  136|      8|    lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  137|       |        "Multiple AI backends are available:",
  138|      8|        Style::default().fg(Color::Gray),
  139|       |    )));
  140|      8|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  141|       |
  142|       |    // Backend options
  143|     14|    for (idx, &backend) in state.available_backends.iter().enumerate() {
                                         ^8                              ^8
  144|     14|        let is_selected = idx == state.selected_index;
  145|     14|        lines.push(build_backend_line(backend, is_selected, idx));
  146|     14|    }
  147|       |
  148|       |    // Instructions at the bottom
  149|      8|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  150|      8|    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
  151|      8|        Span::styled("↑/↓", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  152|      8|        Span::raw(" Navigate  "),
  153|      8|        Span::styled("Enter", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  154|      8|        Span::raw(" Select"),
  155|       |    ]));
  156|       |
  157|      8|    lines
  158|      8|}
  159|       |
  160|       |/// Builds a single backend option line with selection indicator.
  161|     16|fn build_backend_line(backend: BackendKind, is_selected: bool, index: usize) -> Line<'static> {
  162|     16|    let mut spans = Vec::new();
  163|       |
  164|       |    // Selection indicator (cursor)
  165|     16|    let cursor = if is_selected { "▶ " } else { "  " };
                                                ^8              ^8
  166|     16|    spans.push(Span::styled(
  167|     16|        cursor.to_string(),
  168|     16|        Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),
  169|       |    ));
  170|       |
  171|       |    // Number prefix
  172|     16|    spans.push(Span::styled(
  173|     16|        format!("{}. ", index + 1),
  174|     16|        Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
  175|       |    ));
  176|       |
  177|       |    // Backend name
  178|     16|    let name = backend.as_str();
  179|     16|    let name_style = if is_selected {
  180|      8|        Style::default()
  181|      8|            .fg(Color::White)
  182|      8|            .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
  183|       |    } else {
  184|      8|        Style::default().fg(Color::White)
  185|       |    };
  186|     16|    spans.push(Span::styled(name.to_string(), name_style));
  187|       |
  188|       |    // Description
  189|     16|    let description = match backend {
  190|      8|        BackendKind::Auggie => " - Augment CLI (auggie)",
  191|      8|        BackendKind::Cursor => " - Cursor Agent CLI",
  192|       |    };
  193|     16|    spans.push(Span::styled(
  194|     16|        description.to_string(),
  195|     16|        Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
  196|       |    ));
  197|       |
  198|     16|    Line::from(spans)
  199|     16|}
  200|       |
  201|       |/// Creates a centered rectangle within the given area.
  202|      7|fn centered_rect(area: Rect, percent_x: u16, percent_y: u16) -> Rect {
  203|      7|    let vertical = Layout::vertical([Constraint::Percentage(percent_y)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  204|      7|    let horizontal = Layout::horizontal([Constraint::Percentage(percent_x)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  205|      7|    let [area] = vertical.areas(area);
  206|      7|    let [area] = horizontal.areas(area);
  207|      7|    area
  208|      7|}

  216|      4|        let backend = TestBackend::new(80, 24);
  217|      4|        let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend).expect("test terminal should initialize");
  218|      4|        terminal
  219|      4|            .draw(|frame| render_backend_selection_popup(frame, frame.area(), state))
  220|      4|            .expect("popup should render");
  221|      4|        format!("{}", terminal.backend())
  222|      4|    }

  240|       |        // Zero backends: not visible

  249|       |

  309|      1|    fn test_navigation_cycle_through_all_backends() {
  310|      1|        let mut state = BackendSelectionState::with_backends(vec![
  311|      1|            BackendKind::Auggie,
  312|      1|            BackendKind::Cursor,
  313|       |        ]);
  314|       |
  315|       |        // Start at index 0
  316|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 0);
  317|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Auggie));
  318|       |
  319|       |        // Navigate forward through all items and wrap
  320|      1|        state.select_next();
  321|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 1);
  322|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Cursor));
  323|       |
  324|      1|        state.select_next();
  325|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 0); // wrapped
  326|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Auggie));
  327|       |
  328|       |        // Navigate backward through all items and wrap
  329|      1|        state.select_previous();
  330|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 1); // wrapped to end
  331|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Cursor));
  332|       |
  333|      1|        state.select_previous();
  334|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 0);
  335|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Auggie));
  336|      1|    }

  509|       |    #[test]
  510|      1|    fn test_full_selection_flow() {
  511|       |        // User opens backend selection with multiple backends
  512|      1|        let mut state = BackendSelectionState::with_backends(vec![
  513|      1|            BackendKind::Auggie,
  514|      1|            BackendKind::Cursor,
  515|       |        ]);
  516|      1|        assert!(state.visible);
  517|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_index, 0);
  518|       |
  519|       |        // User navigates down
  520|      1|        state.select_next();
  521|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Cursor));
  522|       |
  523|       |        // User navigates up (back to first)
  524|      1|        state.select_previous();
  525|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Auggie));
  526|       |
  527|       |        // User navigates up again (wraps to last)
  528|      1|        state.select_previous();
  529|      1|        assert_eq!(state.selected_backend(), Some(BackendKind::Cursor));
  530|       |
  531|       |        // User confirms selection
  532|      1|        let selected = state.confirm_selection();
  533|      1|        assert_eq!(selected, Some(BackendKind::Cursor));
  534|      1|        assert!(!state.visible);
  535|      1|    }

  537|       |    #[test]
  538|      1|    fn test_build_backend_line_selected_vs_unselected() {
  539|       |        // Test selected line
  540|      1|        let selected_line = build_backend_line(BackendKind::Auggie, true, 0);
  541|      1|        let selected_spans: Vec<_> = selected_line.spans.iter().collect();
  542|      1|        assert!(!selected_spans.is_empty());
  543|       |
  544|       |        // Test unselected line
  545|      1|        let unselected_line = build_backend_line(BackendKind::Cursor, false, 1);
  546|      1|        let unselected_spans: Vec<_> = unselected_line.spans.iter().collect();
  547|      1|        assert!(!unselected_spans.is_empty());
  548|       |
  549|       |        // Selected line should have cursor indicator
  550|      1|        let has_cursor = selected_line.spans.iter().any(|s| s.content.contains('▶'));
  551|      1|        assert!(has_cursor, "Selected line should have cursor indicator");
  552|      1|    }

  565|      1|        let state = BackendSelectionState::with_backends(vec![
  566|      1|            BackendKind::Auggie,

   67|       |/// Renders the help overlay on top of the main UI.
   68|       |///
   69|       |/// This function renders a centered popup with all keyboard shortcuts
   70|       |/// organized by context. It uses the `Clear` widget to clear the area
   71|       |/// behind the popup, ensuring the help text is readable.
   72|       |///
   73|       |/// # Arguments
   74|       |///
   75|       |/// * `frame` - The ratatui frame to render into
   76|       |/// * `area` - The full terminal area to render the overlay on
   77|       |///
   78|       |/// # Example
   79|       |///
   80|       |/// ```ignore
   81|       |/// use paperboat::tui::widgets::render_help_overlay;
   82|       |///
   83|       |/// fn render(frame: &mut Frame, state: &TuiState) {
   84|       |///     // Render main UI first...
   85|       |///     
   86|       |///     // Render help overlay last (on top of everything)
   87|       |///     if state.help_visible {
   88|       |///         render_help_overlay(frame, frame.area());
   89|       |///     }
   90|       |/// }
   91|       |/// ```
   92|     16|pub fn render_help_overlay(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {
   93|       |    // Calculate popup dimensions
   94|     16|    let popup_area = centered_rect(area, 60, 80);
   95|       |
   96|       |    // Clear the area behind the popup
   97|     16|    frame.render_widget(Clear, popup_area);
   98|       |
   99|       |    // Build the help content
  100|     16|    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
  101|       |
  102|     96|    for (idx, section) in HELP_SECTIONS.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^16           ^16    ^16
  103|       |        // Add blank line between sections (but not before the first)
  104|     96|        if idx > 0 {
  105|     80|            lines.push(Line::from(""));
  106|     80|        }
                      ^16
  107|       |
  108|       |        // Section title with styling
  109|     96|        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  110|     96|            section.title,
  111|     96|            Style::default()
  112|     96|                .fg(Color::Cyan)
  113|     96|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD | Modifier::UNDERLINED),
  114|       |        )));
  115|       |
  116|       |        // Shortcuts with aligned columns
  117|    320|        for (key, description) in section.shortcuts {
                                                ^96
  118|    320|            let key_span = Span::styled(
  119|    320|                format!("  {key:12}"),
  120|    320|                Style::default()
  121|    320|                    .fg(Color::Yellow)
  122|    320|                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  123|    320|            );
  124|    320|            let desc_span = Span::raw(*description);
  125|    320|            lines.push(Line::from(vec![key_span, desc_span]));
  126|    320|        }
  127|       |    }
  128|       |
  129|       |    // Add dismissal hint at the bottom
  130|     16|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  131|     16|    lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  132|       |        "Press ? or Esc to close",
  133|     16|        Style::default()
  134|     16|            .fg(Color::DarkGray)
  135|     16|            .add_modifier(Modifier::ITALIC),
  136|       |    )));
  137|       |
  138|     16|    let help_text = Text::from(lines);
  139|       |
  140|       |    // Create the block with styled borders
  141|     16|    let block = Block::default()
  142|     16|        .title(" Keyboard Shortcuts ")
  143|     16|        .title_style(
  144|     16|            Style::default()
  145|     16|                .fg(Color::White)
  146|     16|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  147|       |        )
  148|     16|        .borders(Borders::ALL)
  149|     16|        .border_type(ratatui::widgets::BorderType::Rounded)
  150|     16|        .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan))
  151|     16|        .style(Style::default().bg(Color::Black));
  152|       |
  153|     16|    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(help_text).block(block);
  154|       |
  155|     16|    frame.render_widget(paragraph, popup_area);

  158|       |/// Creates a centered rectangle within the given area.
  159|       |///
  160|       |/// # Arguments
  161|       |///
  162|       |/// * `area` - The area to center within
  163|       |/// * `percent_x` - Percentage of horizontal space to use
  164|       |/// * `percent_y` - Percentage of vertical space to use
  165|     19|fn centered_rect(area: Rect, percent_x: u16, percent_y: u16) -> Rect {
  166|     19|    let vertical = Layout::vertical([Constraint::Percentage(percent_y)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  167|     19|    let horizontal = Layout::horizontal([Constraint::Percentage(percent_x)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  168|     19|    let [area] = vertical.areas(area);
  169|     19|    let [area] = horizontal.areas(area);
  170|     19|    area
  171|     19|}

/Users/dbmrq/Documents/Programação/Misc/paperboat/src/tui/widgets/settings.rs:
    1|       |//! Settings overlay widget module.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the settings overlay UI for viewing and changing model
    4|       |//! configurations in the TUI. It allows users to:
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! - View current model assignments for each agent type (Orchestrator, Planner, Implementer)
    7|       |//! - Browse available models with descriptions
    8|       |//! - Select different models for each agent type
    9|       |//! - See pending changes before they are saved
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! # Usage
   12|       |//!
   13|       |//! Press `s` to open the settings overlay. Navigate with:
   14|       |//! - `←`/`→` or `Tab`: Switch between agent type tabs
   15|       |//! - `↑`/`↓`: Navigate the model list
   16|       |//! - `Enter`: Select the highlighted model
   17|       |//! - `Esc`: Close the overlay (pending changes are saved on selection)
   18|       |//!
   19|       |//! # Architecture
   20|       |//!
   21|       |//! The settings system has three components:
   22|       |//! 1. **UI Layer** (this module): Renders the overlay and manages UI state via [`SettingsState`]
   23|       |//! 2. **Config Loader** ([`crate::config::loader`]): Loads TOML configs from disk at startup
   24|       |//! 3. **Config Writer** ([`crate::config::writer`]): Persists changes to `.paperboat/agents/*.toml`
   25|       |//!
   26|       |//! Model changes are applied to the [`crate::models::ModelConfig`] but only affect
   27|       |//! newly spawned agents. Running agents continue with their original model assignment.
   28|       |
   29|       |use ratatui::layout::{Constraint, Flex, Layout, Rect};
   30|       |use ratatui::prelude::*;
   31|       |use ratatui::widgets::{Block, Borders, Clear, Paragraph};
   32|       |
   33|       |use crate::models::ModelTier;
   34|       |use crate::tui::TuiState;
   35|       |
   36|       |// ============================================================================
   37|       |// Agent Type Selection
   38|       |// ============================================================================
   39|       |
   40|       |/// The agent type currently selected in the settings panel.
   41|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   42|       |pub enum SelectedAgentType {
   43|       |    /// Orchestrator agent configuration
   44|       |    #[default]
   45|       |    Orchestrator,
   46|       |    /// Planner agent configuration
   47|       |    Planner,
   48|       |    /// Implementer agent configuration
   49|       |    Implementer,
   50|       |}
   51|       |
   52|       |impl SelectedAgentType {
   53|       |    /// Returns the display name for this agent type.
   54|      7|    pub const fn display_name(&self) -> &'static str {
   55|      7|        match self {
   56|      3|            Self::Orchestrator => "Orchestrator",
   57|      2|            Self::Planner => "Planner",
   58|      2|            Self::Implementer => "Implementer",
   59|       |        }
   60|      7|    }
   61|       |
   62|       |    /// Returns all agent types in order.
   63|      2|    pub const fn all() -> &'static [Self] {
   64|      2|        &[Self::Orchestrator, Self::Planner, Self::Implementer]
   65|      2|    }
   66|       |
   67|       |    /// Cycles to the next agent type.
   68|     11|    pub const fn next(&self) -> Self {
   69|     11|        match self {
   70|      4|            Self::Orchestrator => Self::Planner,
   71|      4|            Self::Planner => Self::Implementer,
   72|      3|            Self::Implementer => Self::Orchestrator,
   73|       |        }
   74|     11|    }
   75|       |
   76|       |    /// Cycles to the previous agent type.
   77|      8|    pub const fn prev(&self) -> Self {
   78|      8|        match self {
   79|      2|            Self::Orchestrator => Self::Implementer,
   80|      3|            Self::Planner => Self::Orchestrator,
   81|      3|            Self::Implementer => Self::Planner,
   82|       |        }
   83|      8|    }
   84|       |}
   85|       |
   86|       |// ============================================================================
   87|       |// Settings State
   88|       |// ============================================================================
   89|       |
   90|       |/// State for the settings overlay.
   91|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   92|       |pub struct SettingsState {
   93|       |    /// Currently selected agent type (tab)
   94|       |    pub selected_agent_type: SelectedAgentType,
   95|       |    /// Currently selected model index within the available tiers list
   96|       |    pub selected_model_index: usize,
   97|       |    /// Pending orchestrator model change (not yet applied)
   98|       |    pub pending_orchestrator: Option<ModelTier>,
   99|       |    /// Pending planner model change (not yet applied)
  100|       |    pub pending_planner: Option<ModelTier>,
  101|       |    /// Pending implementer model change (not yet applied)
  102|       |    pub pending_implementer: Option<ModelTier>,
  103|       |}
  104|       |
  105|       |impl SettingsState {
  106|       |    /// Creates a new settings state.
  107|       |    #[must_use]
  108|    137|    pub fn new() -> Self {
  109|    137|        Self::default()
  110|    137|    }
  111|       |
  112|       |    /// Selects the next model in the list.
  113|      8|    pub const fn select_next_model(&mut self, available_models_count: usize) {
  114|      8|        if available_models_count > 0 {
  115|      6|            self.selected_model_index = (self.selected_model_index + 1) % available_models_count;
  116|      6|        }
                      ^2
  117|      8|    }
  118|       |
  119|       |    /// Selects the previous model in the list.
  120|      5|    pub fn select_previous_model(&mut self, available_models_count: usize) {
  121|      5|        if available_models_count > 0 {
  122|      4|            self.selected_model_index = self
  123|      4|                .selected_model_index
  124|      4|                .checked_sub(1)
  125|      4|                .unwrap_or(available_models_count - 1);
  126|      4|        }
                      ^1
  127|      5|    }
  128|       |
  129|       |    /// Cycles to the next agent type tab.
  130|      8|    pub const fn next_agent_type(&mut self) {
  131|      8|        self.selected_agent_type = self.selected_agent_type.next();
  132|      8|        self.selected_model_index = 0; // Reset model selection when changing tabs
  133|      8|    }
  134|       |
  135|       |    /// Cycles to the previous agent type tab.
  136|      5|    pub const fn prev_agent_type(&mut self) {
  137|      5|        self.selected_agent_type = self.selected_agent_type.prev();
  138|      5|        self.selected_model_index = 0; // Reset model selection when changing tabs
  139|      5|    }
  140|       |
  141|       |    /// Gets the current model tier for the selected agent type.
  142|       |    ///
  143|       |    /// Returns the pending tier if set, otherwise the primary tier from config.
  144|      8|    pub fn get_current_model(&self, state: &TuiState) -> Option<ModelTier> {
  145|      8|        match self.selected_agent_type {
  146|      4|            SelectedAgentType::Orchestrator => self
  147|      4|                .pending_orchestrator
  148|      4|                .or_else(|| state.model_config.orchestrator_model.primary()),
                                          ^2                                    ^2
  149|      2|            SelectedAgentType::Planner => self
  150|      2|                .pending_planner
  151|      2|                .or_else(|| state.model_config.planner_model.primary()),
                                          ^1                               ^1
  152|      2|            SelectedAgentType::Implementer => self
  153|      2|                .pending_implementer
  154|      2|                .or_else(|| state.model_config.implementer_model.primary()),
  155|       |        }
  156|      8|    }
  157|       |
  158|       |    /// Sets the pending model tier for the selected agent type.
  159|      4|    pub const fn set_pending_model(&mut self, tier: ModelTier) {
  160|      4|        match self.selected_agent_type {
  161|      2|            SelectedAgentType::Orchestrator => self.pending_orchestrator = Some(tier),
  162|      1|            SelectedAgentType::Planner => self.pending_planner = Some(tier),
  163|      1|            SelectedAgentType::Implementer => self.pending_implementer = Some(tier),
  164|       |        }
  165|      4|    }
  166|       |
  167|       |    /// Returns true if there are any pending changes.
  168|     11|    pub const fn has_pending_changes(&self) -> bool {
  169|     11|        self.pending_orchestrator.is_some()
  170|      8|            || self.pending_planner.is_some()
  171|      7|            || self.pending_implementer.is_some()
  172|     11|    }
  173|       |
  174|       |    /// Clears all pending changes.
  175|      5|    pub const fn clear_pending(&mut self) {
  176|      5|        self.pending_orchestrator = None;
  177|      5|        self.pending_planner = None;
  178|      5|        self.pending_implementer = None;
  179|      5|    }

  186|       |/// Renders the settings overlay on top of the main UI.
  187|       |///
  188|       |/// This function renders a centered popup for viewing and changing model
  189|       |/// configurations. It uses the `Clear` widget to clear the area behind the
  190|       |/// popup, ensuring the settings are readable.
  191|       |///
  192|       |/// # Arguments
  193|       |///
  194|       |/// * `frame` - The ratatui frame to render into
  195|       |/// * `area` - The full terminal area to render the overlay on
  196|       |/// * `state` - The TUI state containing model config and settings state
  197|      1|pub fn render_settings_overlay(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, state: &TuiState) {
  198|       |    // Calculate popup dimensions
  199|      1|    let popup_area = centered_rect(area, 70, 85);
  200|       |
  201|       |    // Clear the area behind the popup
  202|      1|    frame.render_widget(Clear, popup_area);
  203|       |
  204|       |    // Build the settings content
  205|      1|    let lines = build_settings_content(state);
  206|       |
  207|      1|    let settings_text = Text::from(lines);
  208|       |
  209|       |    // Create the block with styled borders
  210|      1|    let block = Block::default()
  211|      1|        .title(" Model Settings ")
  212|      1|        .title_style(
  213|      1|            Style::default()
  214|      1|                .fg(Color::White)
  215|      1|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  216|       |        )
  217|      1|        .borders(Borders::ALL)
  218|      1|        .border_type(ratatui::widgets::BorderType::Rounded)
  219|      1|        .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Magenta))
  220|      1|        .style(Style::default().bg(Color::Black));
  221|       |
  222|      1|    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(settings_text).block(block);
  223|       |
  224|      1|    frame.render_widget(paragraph, popup_area);
  225|      1|}
  226|       |
  227|       |/// Builds the content lines for the settings overlay.
  228|      1|fn build_settings_content(state: &TuiState) -> Vec<Line<'static>> {
  229|      1|    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
  230|      1|    let settings = &state.settings_state;
  231|       |
  232|       |    // Tab bar for agent types
  233|      1|    lines.push(build_tab_bar(settings.selected_agent_type));
  234|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  235|       |
  236|       |    // Get current model tier for the selected agent type
  237|      1|    let current_tier = settings.get_current_model(state);
  238|      1|    let original_tier = get_original_model(state, settings.selected_agent_type);
  239|       |
  240|       |    // Section header
  241|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  242|      1|        format!("{} Model", settings.selected_agent_type.display_name()),
  243|      1|        Style::default()
  244|      1|            .fg(Color::Cyan)
  245|      1|            .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD | Modifier::UNDERLINED),
  246|       |    )));
  247|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  248|       |
  249|       |    // Available model tiers list
  250|      1|    let available_tiers = &state.available_tiers;
  251|      1|    if available_tiers.is_empty() {
  252|      1|        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  253|      1|            "  No models available",
  254|      1|            Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
  255|      1|        )));
  256|      1|    } else {
  257|      0|        for (idx, &tier) in available_tiers.iter().enumerate() {
  258|      0|            let is_selected = idx == settings.selected_model_index;
  259|      0|            let is_current = current_tier == Some(tier);
  260|      0|            let is_original = original_tier == Some(tier);
  261|      0|            lines.push(build_model_line(tier, is_selected, is_current, is_original));
  262|      0|        }
  263|       |    }
  264|       |
  265|       |    // Show pending changes indicator
  266|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  267|      1|    if settings.has_pending_changes() {
  268|      0|        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
  269|      0|            "  ● Unsaved changes",
  270|      0|            Style::default()
  271|      0|                .fg(Color::Yellow)
  272|      0|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
  273|      0|        )));
  274|      1|    }
  275|       |
  276|       |    // Instructions at the bottom
  277|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(""));
  278|      1|    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
  279|      1|        Span::styled("←/→/Tab", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  280|      1|        Span::raw(" Switch agent type  "),
  281|      1|        Span::styled("↑/↓", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  282|      1|        Span::raw(" Navigate  "),
  283|      1|        Span::styled("Enter", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  284|      1|        Span::raw(" Select  "),
  285|      1|        Span::styled("Esc", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
  286|      1|        Span::raw(" Close"),
  287|       |    ]));
  288|       |
  289|      1|    lines
  290|      1|}
  291|       |
  292|       |/// Builds the tab bar showing agent type selection.
  293|      1|fn build_tab_bar(selected: SelectedAgentType) -> Line<'static> {
  294|      1|    let mut spans = Vec::new();
  295|      1|    spans.push(Span::raw(" "));
  296|       |
  297|      3|    for agent_type in SelectedAgentType::all() {
                                    ^1
  298|      3|        let is_selected = *agent_type == selected;
  299|      3|        let style = if is_selected {
  300|      1|            Style::default()
  301|      1|                .fg(Color::Black)
  302|      1|                .bg(Color::Magenta)
  303|      1|                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
  304|       |        } else {
  305|      2|            Style::default().fg(Color::Gray)
  306|       |        };
  307|       |
  308|      3|        spans.push(Span::styled(
  309|      3|            format!(" {} ", agent_type.display_name()),
  310|      3|            style,
  311|       |        ));
  312|      3|        spans.push(Span::raw(" "));
  313|       |    }
  314|       |
  315|      1|    Line::from(spans)
  316|      1|}
  317|       |
  318|       |/// Builds a single model tier line with selection and status indicators.
  319|      0|fn build_model_line(
  320|      0|    tier: ModelTier,
  321|      0|    is_selected: bool,
  322|      0|    is_current: bool,
  323|      0|    is_original: bool,
  324|      0|) -> Line<'static> {
  325|      0|    let mut spans = Vec::new();
  326|       |
  327|       |    // Selection indicator (cursor)
  328|      0|    let cursor = if is_selected { "▶ " } else { "  " };
  329|      0|    spans.push(Span::styled(
  330|      0|        cursor.to_string(),
  331|      0|        Style::default().fg(Color::Magenta),
  332|       |    ));
  333|       |
  334|       |    // Radio button indicator
  335|      0|    let radio = if is_current { "◉" } else { "○" };
  336|      0|    let radio_color = if is_current {
  337|      0|        Color::Green
  338|       |    } else {
  339|      0|        Color::Gray
  340|       |    };
  341|      0|    spans.push(Span::styled(
  342|      0|        format!("{radio} "),
  343|      0|        Style::default().fg(radio_color),
  344|       |    ));
  345|       |
  346|       |    // Tier name (capitalize first letter)
  347|      0|    let name = tier.as_str();
  348|      0|    let display_name = name
  349|      0|        .chars()
  350|      0|        .enumerate()
  351|      0|        .map(|(i, c)| if i == 0 { c.to_ascii_uppercase() } else { c })
  352|      0|        .collect::<String>();
  353|      0|    let name_style = if is_selected {
  354|      0|        Style::default()
  355|      0|            .fg(Color::White)
  356|      0|            .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
  357|      0|    } else if is_current {
  358|      0|        Style::default().fg(Color::Green)
  359|       |    } else {
  360|      0|        Style::default().fg(Color::White)
  361|       |    };
  362|      0|    spans.push(Span::styled(display_name, name_style));
  363|       |
  364|       |    // Pending change indicator
  365|      0|    if is_current && !is_original {
  366|      0|        spans.push(Span::styled(
  367|      0|            " (pending)",
  368|      0|            Style::default()
  369|      0|                .fg(Color::Yellow)
  370|      0|                .add_modifier(Modifier::ITALIC),
  371|      0|        ));
  372|      0|    }
  373|       |
  374|      0|    Line::from(spans)
  375|      0|}
  376|       |
  377|       |/// Gets the original (non-pending) model tier for an agent type.
  378|      1|fn get_original_model(state: &TuiState, agent_type: SelectedAgentType) -> Option<ModelTier> {
  379|      1|    match agent_type {
  380|      1|        SelectedAgentType::Orchestrator => state.model_config.orchestrator_model.primary(),
  381|      0|        SelectedAgentType::Planner => state.model_config.planner_model.primary(),
  382|      0|        SelectedAgentType::Implementer => state.model_config.implementer_model.primary(),
  383|       |    }
  384|      1|}
  385|       |
  386|       |/// Creates a centered rectangle within the given area.
  387|       |///
  388|       |/// # Arguments
  389|       |///
  390|       |/// * `area` - The area to center within
  391|       |/// * `percent_x` - Percentage of horizontal space to use
  392|       |/// * `percent_y` - Percentage of vertical space to use
  393|      1|fn centered_rect(area: Rect, percent_x: u16, percent_y: u16) -> Rect {
  394|      1|    let vertical = Layout::vertical([Constraint::Percentage(percent_y)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  395|      1|    let horizontal = Layout::horizontal([Constraint::Percentage(percent_x)]).flex(Flex::Center);
  396|      1|    let [area] = vertical.areas(area);
  397|      1|    let [area] = horizontal.areas(area);
  398|      1|    area
  399|      1|}

  603|       |    #[test]
  604|      1|    fn test_get_current_model_returns_config_when_no_pending() {
  605|       |        use crate::models::{ModelConfig, ModelFallbackChain};
  606|       |
  607|      1|        let mut config = ModelConfig::default();
  608|      1|        config.orchestrator_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Opus);
  609|      1|        config.planner_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Sonnet);
  610|      1|        config.implementer_model = ModelFallbackChain::single(ModelTier::Haiku);
  611|       |
  612|      1|        let tui_state = TuiState::with_model_config(config);
  613|       |
  614|       |        // Test orchestrator
  615|      1|        let mut settings = SettingsState::new();
  616|      1|        settings.selected_agent_type = SelectedAgentType::Orchestrator;
  617|      1|        assert_eq!(
  618|      1|            settings.get_current_model(&tui_state),
  619|       |            Some(ModelTier::Opus)
  620|       |        );
  621|       |
  622|       |        // Test planner
  623|      1|        settings.selected_agent_type = SelectedAgentType::Planner;
  624|      1|        assert_eq!(
  625|      1|            settings.get_current_model(&tui_state),
  626|       |            Some(ModelTier::Sonnet)
  627|       |        );
  628|       |
  629|       |        // Test implementer
  630|      1|        settings.selected_agent_type = SelectedAgentType::Implementer;
  631|      1|        assert_eq!(
  632|      1|            settings.get_current_model(&tui_state),
  633|       |            Some(ModelTier::Haiku)
